These findings validate the effectiveness of the proposed transposition technique and its applicability to parallel conductor configurations with a broader range of strand numbers.

title: Fluid Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (FRIS) Enabling Secure Wireless Communications
authors: Xusheng Zhu, Kai-Kit Wong, Boyi Tang, Wen Chen, Chan-Byoung Chae
abstract: The fluid reconfigurable intelligent surface (FRIS) advances the conventional RIS paradigm by enabling spatial reconfigurability of its reflecting elements. This letter investigates FRIS-assisted physical-layer security in a MISO system where a multi-antenna access point (AP) communicates with a legitimate user in the presence of an eavesdropper. Unlike RIS with fixed elements, FRIS dynamically selects an optimal subset of elements from a set of candidate locations to exploit spatial diversity. We aim to maximize the secrecy rate by jointly optimizing the AP’s transmit beamforming, FRIS element selection, and discrete phase shifts. The resulting problem is a mixed-integer nonlinear program (MINLP), which is NP-hard. We propose an alternating optimization (AO) framework where the beamforming is solved via the generalized eigenvalue method and the combinatorial subproblem of joint selection and phase design is addressed through cross-entropy optimization (CEO). Numerical results demonstrate that FRIS significantly outperforms conventional RIS and other baselines, highlighting the substantial secrecy gains provided by element positioning as a pivotal new degree of freedom (DoF).

title: Reliable Multi-Modal Semantic Communication via AI-Oriented Residual Encoding
authors: Jinhyuk Choi, Seungeun Oh, Seong-Lyun Kim
abstract: This letter introduces a communication-efficient multi-modal semantic communication (MMSC) framework over wireless networks, termed Residual Hierarchical-Adaptive Modality Control (ReHi-AMC). Under an outage-based transmission without CSI feedback, ReHi-AMC preserves generative reconstruction quality. The framework assigns cross-modal transmission priorities and progressively encodes spatial sketches through a hierarchical multi-resolution representation. To further enhance payload efficiency, residual information between hierarchical levels is compressed via sparse residual encoding, reducing redundancy while maintaining essential structural cues. Numerical evaluations on a Stable Diffusion–based generative architecture demonstrate up to 46.47% improvement in CLIP score, with particularly pronounced gains in low-SNR regimes, confirming robustness against channel outages compared to single-scale baselines without residual encoding.

title: All-Fiber Optical Intensity Sensing Based on a PDMS/MWCNTs Composite Fabry–Pérot Probe
authors: Ying Wan, Sheng Zhang, Chen Jiang, Jianxin Ren, Jianxiang Wen
abstract: We demonstrated a compact and sensitive optical intensity sensor based on a Fabry–P´erot (FP) probe, which was fabricated by splicing a capillary fiber to a single-mode fiber and by filling the capillary with a Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) matrix containing dispersed multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs). The FP cavity filled with the PDMS/MWCNTs composite not only maintains the high temperature sensitivity of the sensing element but also enhances the photothermal conversion efficiency. When the focused light intensity increases from 0 to 49.8 mW, the sensitivity wavelength shift of the probe reaches 0.26 nm/mW. Compared with the FP probe filled with pure PDMS, the optical power sensitivity is improved by more than 8.6 times. The temporal response of the probe was measured, yielding heating and cooling times of 315 ms and 538 ms, respectively. Moreover, a reference cavity was introduced into the optical intensity detection system to generate the Vernier effect, which further enhanced the optical intensity sensitivity. As a result, the sensitivity increased to -1.78 nm/mW, representing an additional improvement of 6.8 times. These results demonstrate that the fabricated and low-cost FP probe offers a rapid response and high sensitivity, making it a promising candidate for practical optical intensity detection applications.

title: Spatiotemporal Coupling Analysis with Inertial Sensor in Cooperative Navigation
authors: Qingzhong Cai, Qian Yang, Jian Li, Ruomei Nie
abstract: Cooperative navigation is an enabling technique for various applications of the Internet of Robotic Things by exploiting both spatial and temporal information. Cooperation among robots can provide significant accuracy improvements for location-aware networks, while the position information inferred by each robot is a complex compound due to the correlation among its neighbors. Such spatiotemporal information coupling effect may result in performance loss of distributed algorithms and hinder the understanding of the cooperative navigation characteristics. In this paper, we develop a general approach to characterize the complex information dynamics induced by spatiotemporal cooperation. Specifically, we derive the probabilistic model of inertial error state process to characterize the position uncertainty. With this model, we further propose a coupling model in terms of Fisher information and characterize how spatiotemporal coupling information lead to network performance degradation in representative scenarios. The results of this paper can provide guidelines for the development of distributed algorithms.

title: Opticaly Transparent Wide-Angle Metasurface for 5G Outdoor-to-Indoor Signal Enhancement
authors: Shadi Danesh, Amirmasood Bagheri, Ali Araghi, Qu Luo, Pei Xiao, Mohsen Khalily
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This article presents an innovative approach to enhance fifth-generation (5 G) outdoor-to-indoor (O2I) wireless coverage by integrating an optically transparent metasurface directly onto standard window glass. The proposed symmetric unit cell, composed of hexagonal loops and patches with narrow copper lines, ensures polarization-tolerant and wide-angle transmission characteristics within a compact geometry. A copper micro-mesh metasurface laminated onto 3.8 mm glass achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\vert S\_{21}\vert > -3$</tex-math></inline-formula> dB up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$80^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> at 3.5 GHz, visible-band transmittance exceeding 80%, a unit-cell periodicity of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.25\lambda \_{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula> (at 3.5 GHz), and is demonstrated on a 750 mm × 750 mm fabricated prototype. To obtain a broad and stable angular response, an evolutionary optimization was employed to refine the unit-cell geometry. Experimental measurements demonstrate close agreement with simulations, confirming the feasibility of transparent transmission enhancement on window glass. The results verify that the proposed metasurface significantly improves outdoor-to-indoor propagation and reduces the bit error rate (BER) of an orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) link compared to conventional glass, particularly at oblique incidences of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$45^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$80^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>.

title: Improved Multiscale Networks and Collaborative Edge Computing for Precise and Real-Time Tool Fault Diagnosis in Industrial IoT
authors: Dongyang Li, Mingqiang Zhang, Na Li, Leiyu Wang, Shibao Li
abstract: With the rapid evolution of smart manufacturing, achieving high-precision and real-time tool condition monitoring has become increasingly crucial for maintaining production efficiency and system reliability. To address this challenge, this work proposes a tool wear fault diagnosis framework that integrates deep learning with collaborative edge computing. At the core of this framework, a physically motivated and hierarchically coupled spatial-temporal modeling network, namely Improved Multiscale Networks (IMSNet), is developed. Unlike conventional CNN-LSTM-attention architectures, IMSNet is designed according to the intrinsic degradation characteristics of tool wear vibration signals. Specifically, a structured multiscale convolutional module is employed to extract spatial representations that capture heterogeneous frequency-axis interactions, while an LSTM network simultaneously models the temporal evolution embedded in raw vibration signals to characterize cumulative degradation dynamics. The complementary spatial and temporal features are then fused through a multi-head attention mechanism for adaptive reliability-aware feature reweighting, enabling robust representation learning under non-stationary machining conditions. To meet stringent real-time requirements in Industrial IoT systems, we further design a cloud-edge-device collaboration (CEDC) framework for adaptive task offloading and low-latency inference. The framework decomposes diagnostic workloads across device, edge, and cloud layers and dynamically coordinates computation within scheduling windows, thereby improving system responsiveness. Experiments demonstrate that IMSNet achieves 97.81% accuracy on the self-collected industrial dataset and 98.93% on the public PHM benchmark, and showing stable and generalizable performance under varying operating conditions. Meanwhile, the CEDC framework reduces task off-loading latency by up to 17.4% compared with the advanced offloading strategies, while maintaining real-time responsiveness under varying workload conditions. To facilitate reproducibility and further research, the source code is publicly available at: https://github.com/lidongyang1/tool-fault-diagnosis-IIoT.

title: RAG-X: Density-Adaptive Path Sampling for Enhanced Knowledge Graph-Based Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: A. Akhil, Mudaliar Hariharan Ganeshan, Valiboyina Murali Sai, S. S. Abilaash, Sundar Srinivasan, Gomathi Sengodan, Praveen George, Ankit Pandey, C. Sherin Shibi, A. Alice Nithya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems face significant challenges in multi-hop reasoning scenarios where relevant information is distributed across multiple interconnected sources. Traditional vector-based retrieval methods struggle with graph-structured knowledge and suffer from hub over-exploration in dense regions of knowledge graphs. We introduce RAG-X, a novel framework that leverages density-adaptive path sampling and dual-phase optimization to address these limitations. The core innovation lies in a density-adaptive sampling algorithm that dynamically adjusts exploration strategies based on local graph topology, preventing excessive concentration on highly connected nodes while ensuring comprehensive coverage of relevant reasoning paths. Our dual-phase optimization approach combines efficient candidate generation using lightweight models with sophisticated reranking through cross-encoder architectures, achieving optimal balance between computational efficiency and answer quality. Experimental evaluation on custom web-scraped datasets demonstrates substantial improvements over baseline approaches: 92.4% factual accuracy versus 65.8% for traditional methods, 89.2% answer completeness compared to 71.3%, and 87.6% reasoning coherence against 69.7%. The system achieves 4.9x throughput improvement while maintaining superior answer quality, making it suitable for production deployment in enterprise environments. Ablation studies confirm the importance of each architectural component, with density adaptation contributing 12.3% accuracy improvement and dual-phase optimization adding 8.7% enhancement. RAG-X establishes a foundation for future research in graph-based retrieval systems and enables more reliable AI applications requiring factual accuracy and transparent reasoning processes.

title: Combined Heat and Power Dispatch Considering Flow Direction-Adaptive Heating Networks
authors: Xinyu Wang, Yixun Xue, Yuan Du, Xingtao Tian, Yingnan Liu, Hongbin Sun
abstract: Combined heat and power dispatch (CHPD) is regarded as a viable tool for enhancing overall energy efficiency and facilitating renewable integration. The district heating network (DHN) in traditional CHPD is modeled based on a fixed flow direction. However, the flow direction may changes according to the heat dispatch in multiple heat sources DHN. To address this issue, we propose a flow direction adaptive (FDA) DHN that the flow direction of each branch is represented by two binary indicators. Considering strong nonlinearities introduced by the bidirectional flow and hydraulic-thermal coupling constraints in the CHPD model with FDA-DHN, we employ a hierarchical linearization strategy, including piecewise McCormick envelopes and convex relaxations, to convert it into a tractable mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) problem. Finally,the effectiveness of the method was validated in a case study of CHPD.

title: Deep LSTM with Hybrid Feature Reduction for Cardiac Arrhythmia Classification Using Pyramid Learning
authors: A. Anitha, Anjana Nair
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Cardiac arrhythmias are significant health threats that need to be detected with high accuracy for timely intervention. This research introduces a new framework for classification which combines Deep Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with hybrid feature reduction and hierarchical learning. For dimension reduction and normalization of continuous features, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Rough Set on Fuzzy Approximation Space (RSFAS) were used to preprocess the original data to keep the most essential diagnostic knowledge intact. Further normalization of predictions was achieved using Pyramid Learning, which exploits hierarchical learning and incorporates higher dimensional combinations of features across levels of the pyramid. The proposed model achieves 96.42% accuracy with reduced computational complexity and improved robustness compared to existing deep-learning approaches.

title: Towards Role-Based Multi-Agent LLM Systems for Software Requirements Analysis
authors: Novica M. Trifković, Ilija D. Antović
abstract: This paper presents a structured overview of recent research on the application of Large Language Models (LLMs) and multi-agent systems in software requirements analysis and introduces a conceptual, role-based multi-agent architecture for addressing identified limitations. The proposed approach emphasizes hierarchical supervision, collaboration between Large and Small Language Models, and structured cross-checking based on report comparison and uncertaintyaware arbitration to improve robustness and reliability. The contribution of this work is theoretical and exploratory, providing a foundation for future research on multi-agent LLM systems aimed at improving the software requirements analysis process.

title: IoT-Machine Learning Based Predictive Maintenance Framework for Industrial Machinery
authors: Balamurugan R, K Navanithi, Koushik S V, Harine M, Ashwin M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Industrial equipment faces growing problem to unexpected breakdowns caused by problems including over-heating, overvoltage, and overcurrent, frequently leading to operational interruptions and production defects. This study presents a predictive maintenance solution using IoT technology and machine learning techniques for instantaneous malfunction predicting. IoT sensors are placed on vital machine parts to consistently gather operational metrics, which are processed to create extensive datasets. A machine learning model, trained on this data, examines these datasets to identify patterns signaling impending failures. The framework includes a live monitoring interface that emphasizes equipment under threat and associated irregular metrics, providing maintenance personnel with practical, preemptive alerts. This methodology efficiently reduces maintenance costs and avoids unscheduled machinery stoppages. The outcomes confirm that combining IoT-based sensing with machine learning analysis represents a powerful approach for transitioning industrial maintenance from reactive practices to predictive, condition-oriented strategies.

title: FibroLiteKD: A Novel Leak-Safe Knowledge Distillation Framework for Compact Ultrasound-based Liver Fibrosis Staging
authors: Md. Musfiqur Rahman Akib, Habibur Rahaman, Rosni Akter
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Reliable, noninvasive staging of liver fibrosis (F0–F4) is crucial for guiding therapy while minimizing biopsies. This study presents a compact, deployable ultrasound pipeline that-to our knowledge, the first to apply knowledge distillation (KD) to liver fibrosis staging-distills a high-capacity teacher (ResNet-50) into lightweight students (MobileNetV3-Small, ResNet-18, EfficientNet-B0) suitable for bedside inference. Credible estimation is enforced via a leak-safe split that groups images by file-stem to prevent near-duplicates from straddling train/validation partitions. Training couples class-weighted cross-entropy, label smoothing, MixUp, and modest geometric/photometric augmentation with AdamW, ReduceLROnPlateau, and early stopping; KD uses temperature T =3 and mixing α=0.6. Primary reporting excludes test-time augmentation (No-TTA) and emphasizes accuracy, balanced accuracy, and macro-F1 to align selection with deployment. On a 6,323-image dataset, the teacher attains 98.74% accuracy, 98.12% balanced accuracy, and 0.9812 macro-F1. The best student-KD(ResNet-50→EfficientNet-B0)-achieves 98.19%/97.41%/0.9733 with only 4.01M parameters and ≈1.87 ms per image, while KD(ResNet-50→MobileNetV3-Small) reaches 98.03%/97.07%/0.9705 at 1.52M parameters and ≈0.42 ms. Distillation therefore preserves high discrimination while substantially reducing size and latency. Error analyses show uniformly high per-class recall with residual confusions concentrated between adjacent stages, supporting clinical plausibility. Overall, the contributions are a simple yet rigorous KD framework tailored to ultrasound staging, a leak-aware validation protocol that curbs optimistic bias, and deployment-ready students that provide a strong basis for comparative studies and prospective multi-center validation toward trustworthy point-of-care decision support.

title: DRL-MPC: A Hybrid Control Strategy Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning and Model Predictive Control for Radiant Cooling Systems
authors: Yixiao Ma, Xueying Xia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: In response to the problems of high thermal inertia, easy condensation at the end of the radiation cooling fresh air system, and high energy consumption of traditional control strategies, this paper proposes a prediction method based on one-dimensional convolutional coupled long short-term memory neural network (CNN-LSTM) to achieve accurate prediction of indoor temperature, dew point temperature, and radiation end surface temperature; Based on this framework, a model predictive control (MPC) strategy is formulated, and deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is introduced to construct a DRL-MPC hybrid control strategy to further optimize control performance. The simulation experiment results show that the MPC strategy exhibits high stability in maintaining indoor thermal comfort, effectively reducing the risk of condensation, and can effectively reduce energy consumption during the cooling period. However, the MPC strategy has insufficient adaptability to dynamic environments and is prone to thermal comfort deviation during nighttime operation, which needs further optimization. The DRL-MPC strategy can effectively alleviate the above problems, reducing the proportion of uncomfortable time from 13.4% to 3.5%, and achieving an additional 8.6% reduction in system energy consumption while maintaining the thermal comfort index (PMV ∈ [-0.5,0.5]). This hybrid control strategy provides a reliable solution for the intelligent and efficient operation of radiative cooling systems.

title: Optimizing Load and Energy Efficiency in Cloud Computing with Bird Swarm Optimization: A Theoretical Approach Towards Green IT
authors: Ankit Anand, Manoj Kumar Shukla, Puneet Sharma, J Ajayan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The rapid growth in the cloud computing has added greatly to global power consumption and raised economic as well as environmental concerns. Data centers on its own contribute almost 1.5% of the global electricity consumption, a proportion expected to rise even more with growing digitalization. India's data centers are estimated to consume approximately 5 GW of power by the year 2025. Given India's pledge to net-zero emissions by 2070, followed by similar ambitions of the United States and the European Union to meet by 2050, environment-friendly solutions are imperative. Green IT is a solution that offers a path toward balancing increasing computational needs with greenness. Herein, we introduce a theoretical work using Bird Swarm Optimization (BSO) to achieve resource scheduling in cloud computing. Motivated by swarm foraging and watchfulness behaviors, BSO dynamically regulates workloads while reducing energy expenditure. Small-scale cloud simulations provide proof of concept for partial deployment. Initial results show that BSO performs better than conventional scheduling policies, including Round Robin and First-Come-First-Serve, in both load balancing and energy efficiency. This work provides the foundation for further large-scale dissertation studies with real-world cloud traces towards supporting sustainable cloud computing.

title: OSCAR: Operational System Context Acquisition and Representation
authors: Alexandre Disdier, Dimitri Masson
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: Sociotechnical systems are characterized by complex and emergent behaviors shaped by their interactions with the context in which they operate. Despite advances in Systems Engineering and Human Systems Integration, the concept of operational context remains underdeveloped and insufficiently integrated into modeling practices. This gap hinders the ability of designers to capture contextual dynamics of a system during early-stage design processes. To address this, we propose the OSCAR (Operational System Context Acquisition and Representation) framework, which formalizes the relationship between the four spaces inherent to complex sociotechnical systems: functions, structures, environment, and their evolution over time. By distinguishing and connecting these four spaces, OSCAR enables the systematic representation of how contextual elements interact with a system of interest during operations and how they evolve towards new situations. We illustrate applicability of the proposed framework through an air traffic control example and discuss how it could be harnessed within practical approaches to sociotechnical system design.

title: From Aerial Imagery to Label-Conditioned Disaster Reports
authors: Zeynep Karaca, Ilhan Aydın
abstract: This study proposes a hybrid approach for disaster damage detection and automated explanatory reporting using the LADI-v2 dataset, which comprises aerial imagery. The system is structured upon a two-stage architecture: in the first stage, a ResNet-18 model trained to identify 12 distinct damage categories exhibited high classification performance with an F1 score of 81.88%. In the second stage, Qwen2-VL and BLIP-2 Vision-Language Models (VLMs) were optimized through LoRA-based fine-tuning to translate detected findings into natural language. Performance analyses revealed that the BLIP-2 model outperformed in fidelity to reference texts and key concept extraction, achieving scores of 53.29 in sacreBLEU and 71.03 in ROUGE-1. Conversely, the Qwen2-VL model excelled in semantic flexibility and grammatical richness with a METEOR score of 0.81. Findings indicate that BLIP-2 excels in high-precision reporting, whereas Qwen2-VL provides superior contextual depth. This framework demonstrates significant potential as a rapid, interpretable, and collaborative decision-support tool for disaster management within experimental settings.

title: Evaluating Human AI Collaboration Through Survey Based Random Forest Approach
authors: Subir Gupta, Priyanka Roy, Sudipta Hazra, Bibhuti Bhusan Dash, Utpal Chandra De, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: It is proposed to address the identified gaps in the systematic consideration of human-AI collaboration in the field as a task complexity, human skills, AI confidence, and cost of decisions framework in this proposed study. A structured survey repository integrating subjective judgments with operational context information has been developed and evaluated with a Random Forest algorithm. This algorithm is well-informed, easily understandable, and capable of handling various forms of data irrespective of data type in a cross-sectional data set. Lastly, this algorithm has been validated with Stratified Cross Validation. This algorithm is also capable of producing results in terms of Accuracy (0.885) Precision (0.889), Recall (0.994), & F1 Score (0.939), indicating minimal error margins

title: Modeling User Intention in Mandatory Digital Systems: The Roles of Privacy, Security, and Trust
authors: Jenifir March, Tanty Oktavia
abstract: The deployment of mandatory digital government systems has reshaped user interaction with public service technologies, particularly in environments involving continuous usage and sensitive personal data. This study examines user intention in a mandatory digital taxation system, namely the Core Tax Administration System (Coretax), by extending the Technology Acceptance Model with data privacy, digital security, trust, and digital literacy. Data were collected from 404 individual taxpayers in Jakarta and analyzed using partial least squares structural equation modeling. The results show that data privacy and trust significantly strengthen digital security perceptions. Digital security has a significant positive effect on perceived usefulness but demonstrates a significant negative effect on perceived ease of use and intention to use. Consistent with technology acceptance theory, perceived usefulness remains a key determinant of user intention, while perceived ease of use shows a negative relationship with intention to use in the mandatory system context. Digital literacy also exhibits a significant negative relationship with intention to use, indicating more critical evaluations among digitally capable users in mandatory digital environments. The findings highlight the importance of balancing privacy protection, security assurance, and usability considerations in the design of mandatory digital government platforms and contribute to understanding technology acceptance in compulsory environments.

title: Leveraging Spatial Relationships for SelfSupervised Road Barrier Detection in StreetLevel Imagery
authors: Godson Michael Dsilva, Vinayak Ashok Bharadi, Amit Ankush Bhoir
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Street-level imagery plays a pivotal role in the development and maintenance of digital maps. This study introduces a novel approach for detecting road barriers using dashcam footage by explicitly integrating spatial domain knowledge into the learning process-a key advancement over existing self-supervised learning (SSL) methods that typically lack such spatial contextualization. Traditional supervised learning methods often struggle with generalizability across diverse data sources, especially when annotated datasets are limited. While current SSL approaches aim to overcome annotation scarcity, they generally focus on tasks unrelated to the target domain, resulting in learned features that do not effectively transfer or generalize. Our methodology addresses these limitations through a three-stage process-data curation, pretext model training, and fine-tuningthat leverages spatially related features from more accessible data. By employing foundational models to generate pseudo labels and training on this enriched data, the approach enhances feature relevance within the spatial domain. The final fine-tuning stage adapts the pretrained model for specific applications using minimal annotated data, thereby improving downstream task performance in data-scarce environments. This adaptable framework offers significant potential for various fields where spatial domain integration and limited annotation pose challenges.

title: Energy-Efficient Learning-Based Control of a Legged Robot in Multiple Gravity Environments
authors: Philip Arm, Oliver Fischer, Joseph Church, Adrian Fuhrer, Hendrik Kolvenbach, Marco Hutter
abstract: Legged robots are promising candidates for exploring challenging areas on low-gravity bodies such as the Moon, Mars, or asteroids, thanks to their advanced mobility on unstructured terrain. However, as planetary robots’ power and thermal budgets are highly restricted, these robots need energy-efficient control approaches that easily transfer to multiple gravity environments. In this work, we introduce a reinforcement learning-based control approach for legged robots with gravity-scaled power-optimized reward functions. We use our approach to develop and validate a locomotion controller and a base pose controller in gravity environments from lunar gravity ($1.62 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$) to a hypothetical super-Earth $\left(19.62 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}\right)$ in simulation. Our approach successfully scales across these gravity levels for locomotion and base pose control with the gravity-scaled reward functions. On the real system, the power-optimized locomotion controller reached a power consumption for locomotion of 23.4 W in Earth gravity on a 15.65 kg robot at $0.4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, a $23 \%$ improvement over a baseline policy. Additionally, we designed a constant-force spring offload system that allowed us to conduct real-world experiments on legged locomotion in lunar gravity. In this test setup, the power-optimized control policy reached $12.2 \mathrm{~W}, 36 \%$ less than a baseline controller which is not optimized for power efficiency. Our method provides a scalable approach to developing power-efficient locomotion controllers for legged robots across multiple gravity levels.

title: Design of an Improved Model for Financial Decision Transparency using D-BAFE FEA-BO and HBC-EC Techniques
authors: Sneha Sahare, Anupa Sinha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The growing incorporation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in financial decisions sparked urgent concerns regarding the transparency, trustworthiness, and regulatory compliance of automated systems. Current approaches to explainable AI (XAI) in financial systems are mainly faced with the issues of (a) static feature attribution, (b) centralized validation of explanations, (c) vulnerability to adversarial attack, (d) lack of autonomous auditing, and (e) lack of regulatory alignment. To this effect, the paper presents a new Blockchain-Enhanced Explainable AI (BE-XAI) framework that marries dynamic feature attribution, federated consensus mechanisms, adversarial resilience, autonomous auditing, and hierarchical compliance certification into a single iterative model. It has five modules, such as (1) Dynamic Blockchain Attributed Feature Extraction-D-BAFE-based real-time feature relevance tracing along with blockchain-backed immutability; (2) federated Explainability Aggregator using Blockchain Oracles-FEABO for decentralized, cross-validated explanation consensus; (3) immutable adversarial attack detection and logging-IADL; and (4) smart contracts-based real-time and autonomous anomaly detection through SCDEIM. Lastly, HBCEC- hierarchical blockchain consensus for explainability compliance will ensure multi-stakeholder regulatory certification of model outputs. Each module, thus, is specifically aimed at addressing a critical gap in the traditional XAI pipeline. Overall, by the proposed model, the stability of explanations is enhanced by 12%, trust scores in the explanation improved by 18%, exploited robustness by 94% in the true positive rate, precision in audit trigger to 92%, and consensus compliance success to 97%. Thus, this work builds for the financial decision systems a blockchain-enhanced, explainable, and regulatory-compliant AI paradigms. This sets new benchmarks for secure, transparent, and trustworthy AI deployment in finance across the board in the process.

title: Blockchain-IoT Model for Secure Data Management in Remote Physiotherapy Systems
authors: Ajitha Nancy Rani. R, Blessy Y M, L.N.Jayanthi, S Anand, R. Manojkumar, Sathishkumar V E
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Remote physiotherapy has garnered considerable interest due to its capacity to provide continuous rehabilitation and monitoring with Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled wearable devices. These technologies gather real-time mobility and health data, enabling physiotherapists to evaluate patient progress remotely. However, issues such as data manipulation, unauthorised access, and insufficient transparency hinder the dependability and uptake of existing cloud-based physiotherapy systems. This work presents a Blockchain-IoT architecture for safe data management in remote physiotherapy systems to overcome these concerns. The strategy incorporates IoT sensors for real-time patient monitoring with blockchain technology to provide data quality, authenticity, and traceability. Smart contracts are used to automate access control and provide safe data transfer among authorised parties. Experimental study reveals enhanced data security, transparency, and resistance to assaults, while ensuring low latency and efficient connectivity across IoT nodes. The proposed framework develops confidence among patients, therapists, and healthcare providers, facilitating the establishment of dependable and privacy-respecting tele-rehabilitation services within modern healthcare systems.

title: Auto Sentinel: Deep Learning System for Real Time Vehicle Fire Prediction
authors: D.Elakkiyappriyaa D. Elakkiyappriyaa, N.Chandrasekharan N. Chandrasekharan, S. Preethi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This paper presents the deep hybrid-based auto sentinel for fire detections in transports. It is highly essential to maintain reliable fire detection in modern transport systems to safeguard the life of humans and their assets. In modern vehicles for carrying Heavy loads and human commutations, the vehicle functionalities are highly sensitive to the temperature and environmental conditions. The Fire Detection Systems available in the current market often face a couple of challenges in the accuracy aspects, such as slow response and frequent false alarms. These inaccuracies lead us to take delayed decisions for these unforeseen circumstances. Thus, this study proposes an intelligent framework Auto Sentinel that leverages multi-modal sensors, including temperature, vibration, electrical load, and gas concentrations. The proposed auto sentinel deep hybrid architecture leverages us with well optimized solution for sequential anomaly detection in noisy sensor environments, enabling robust performance even in non-stationary and uncertain conditions. The proposed deep hybrid-based framework incorporates two techniques. The Adaptive Median Filtering for efficient noise removal by adjusting window size and Kalman based estimation technique for accurate removal of both impulsive and Gaussian noise. the results highlight statistically significant improvements in reducing false alarms, minimizing response time, and achieving zero missed detections. In general, this work illustrates the potential of deep learning-driven adaptive anomaly detection pipelines for practical fire monitoring and response systems in transport safety applications.

title: An Efficient Digital Prototyping with Thermal Comfort Model and Green Environmental Benefit Index for Urban Heat Application
authors: G. Ramkumar, Shabanabegum SK, Yousuf Hazaimeh, K Ramanjaneyulu, K Rajasri, N Krishna Chythanya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Efficient Digital Prototyping (EDP) means developing a digital model of the system that can be simulated, tested, and optimized before making it physical. The research introduces a novel Energy, Data, and Process-Driven Thermal and Green Utility (EDPTGU) model designed to enhance both the thermal management and environmental performance of complex systems, such as battery electric vehicles and hospital HVAC systems. The model is represented by a multi-layered digital prototype that integrates various frameworks for simulating system behavior under different scenarios. Some of the main features of this model are: defining performance standards at different layers, using real-time data for system evaluation, employing reinforcement learning for adaptive control strategies, and applying multi-objective optimization to achieve a trade-off between energy saving, comfort, and pollution reduction. Prediction of thermal comfort is based on a sophisticated modelling approach that looks at the trends and thus is able to provide quick and accurate evaluations. The performance analysis includes a confusion matrix, sensor analysis, accuracy, loss, and temperature calculations.

title: A Physics-Informed Learning Approach for Demand Response Potential Assessment of EV Charging Stations
authors: Jiachen Chen, Xiangyu Zhang
abstract: With the proliferation of electric vehicles (EVs), EV Charging Stations (EVCSs) become controllable loads that can provide demand response (DR) to distribution system operators (DSOs). Efficient DR potential assessment (DRPA) of EVCSs can help DSOs rationalize incentive pricing. Some studies leverage data-driven deep learning-based methods to conduct proper assessments while avoiding concerns like unrealistic assumptions. However, these methods suffer from limited interpretability, particularly in the economic correlation between incentive pricing and response potential. Therefore, this paper proposes a physics-informed learning (PIL) approach for DRPA of EVCSs. First, we develop a simulation module that integrates the EVCS operational model with predefined economic laws, such as the price elasticity mechanism, to generate sufficient samples. Furthermore, during training, the approach incorporates these economic laws into the loss functions of the physics-informed long short-term memory (PILSTM) networks to enhance interpretability. We generate 8,640 incentive price-response power data pairs using the proposed simulation method. Experiments show that PIL achieves accurate assessment performance and makes the model more interpretable.

title: Optimal Configuration of Electro-Hydrogen Storage for Voltage Stability and Cross-Cycle Balancing in Renewable-Rich Distribution Networks
authors: Zhipeng Li, Qifen Li, Fanyue Qian, Liting Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: High-penetration distributed PV significantly increases the operational complexity of distribution networks and intensifies conflicts between short-term voltage stability and long-term energy balance. Electro-hydrogen coupling has emerged as an effective pathway for flexible regulation and cross-cycle energy storage. This paper develops a multi-timescale optimal configuration method for electro-hydrogen-coupled distribution networks, integrating voltage stability and life-cycle economy into a unified multi-objective model. Two representative architectures—the Electro-Hydrogen Hybrid Storage (EHHS) and the Three-Level Pure Hydrogen Storage (TLPHS)—are compared through chronological simulations. Results show that the TLPHS achieves nearly complete local renewable consumption and reduces life-cycle cost by approximately 28.8% compared with EHHS, while maintaining adequate voltage quality. These findings provide theoretical and technical support for high-renewable distribution networks employing hydrogen-centric long-duration energy storage.

title: Optimized Capture-Per-Clock Logic Bist with Hybrid Test-Point Insertion for Efficient VLSI Testing
authors: M Kavitha, G Archana Devi, RadhaKrishna Karne, Niranjan Reddy Kallem, K Mounika, S Naga Jyothi
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: The continuous scaling of semiconductor technology and the emergence of safety-critical systems such as automotive and aerospace electronics have intensified the demand for high-quality, low-cost, and low-latency testing mechanisms. Logic Built-In Self-Test (LBIST) has become an effective design-for-testability (DFT) strategy, enabling onchip self-testing without extensive external equipment. However, the presence of random-resistant faults and long scan chain lengths still pose challenges to test efficiency. This paper presents an enhanced LBIST framework based on a Capture-per-Clock Hybrid Test-Point (CCHTP) architecture that integrates deterministic and pseudorandom testing to achieve improved fault coverage with minimal hardware overhead. The proposed method incorporates hybrid control and observation test points that simultaneously reduce Automatic Test Pattern Generation (ATPG) vector counts and enhance random testability. The Capture-per-Clock mechanism enables the system to apply and record test responses within each clock cycle, significantly reducing total test time. Experimental evaluation on industrial-scale circuits demonstrates substantial reductions in pattern counts, power consumption, and silicon area while maintaining high diagnostic precision. This integrated approach ensures compliance with modern functional safety standards such as ISO-26262 and provides a scalable testing methodology suitable for next-generation System-on-Chip (SoC) platforms.

title: Browser Extension for Secure Session Parameter Handling and Hyperlink Modification
authors: Rashmi Welekar, Charanjeet Dadiyala, Priyanshi Acharya, Saloni Vishwakarma, Sharvari Gohane, Samrudhhi Rathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This article describes a new browser extension that was created specifically to make authentication security at email and web login portals stronger. The extension provides a personalized solution that is capable of expertly pulling out HTTP/HTTPS session parameters like cookies and CSRF tokens from specific domains. The parameters are then sent securely to a server that has been specially designated to increase authentication security. A major point of this extension is its ability to find and alter particular links on an active webpage. The web traffic will then seamlessly flow through the testing domain which is a prerequisite for a proper evaluation of the authentication security protocols. Moreover, this extension will be made cross-lingual compatible so as to ensure continuity of operation in varied settings that are non-English website oriented. It has been built exclusively for Chromium-based browsers and follows the latest browser technologies and standards. In its testing, a lot of emphasis was given to reduction of browser warnings and permission requests, so a flexible and secure user experience was thus highlighted. The extension went through rigorous testing on top commercial web services like Gmail and it was found competent in taking care of authenticated sessions while also securely transmitting important session parameters. This study outlines a comprehensive strategy to improve authentication security, where the use of advanced browser extension technology not only reinforces the integrity of authentication processes but also makes them reliable in today’s digital environment.

title: Computationally Efficient Laplacian CL-colME
authors: Nikola Stankovic
abstract: Decentralized collaborative mean estimation (colME) is a fundamental task in heterogeneous networks. Its graph-based variants B-colME and C-colME achieve high scalability of the problem. This paper evaluates the consensus-based C-colME framework, which relies on doubly stochastic averaging matrices to ensure convergence to the oracle solution. We propose CL-colME, a novel variant utilizing Laplacian-based consensus to avoid the computationally expensive normalization processes. Simulation results show that the proposed CL-colME maintains the convergence behavior and accuracy of C-colME while improving computational efficiency.

title: Optimizing Microservices Performance with AI-Driven Load Balancing
authors: Ahmed Alic, Becir Isakovic
abstract: Microservices systems often face performance issues when workloads fluctuate, and services degrade over time. Traditional load balancing methods such as Round Robin or Latency-Aware routing do not adapt to changing conditions, which can lead to higher latency and increased error rates. This paper evaluates adaptive decision-making algorithms for request routing, including Deep Q-Network (DQN), Upper Confidence Bound (UCB), Thompson Sampling, and traditional heuristics. Experiments were executed on a production-scale cloud environment (Runpod, 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM) for 4 hours per algorithm with 50 concurrent users, generating more than 600,000 requests per experiment. Results show that contextual bandit algorithms significantly outperform deep reinforcement learning. UCB achieved a 0.097 % error rate and a median latency of 220 ms, compared to DQN which produced an 11.32 % error rate and instability during training. Latency-Aware routing performed well but could not match the adaptability of contextual bandits. These findings demonstrate that simpler learning algorithms such as UCB and Thompson Sampling provide faster adaptation, lower error rates, and better stability than deep RL approaches in microservices routing tasks.

title: Reducing Software's Carbon Footprint Through Performance Testing
authors: Sowmya Chintakindi
abstract: As technology continues to evolve, the demand for software usage and its reliability is rapidly growing. To meet this demand, the development of new software has also increased to support progress. These software systems need significant computational resources, such as CPU, memory, and network bandwidth. While performance testing is traditionally done to ensure system reliability, its role in reducing environmental impact has received little attention. This paper presents an industrial experience study on how performance testing can be systematically leveraged to lower the carbon footprint of software applications. Appropriate performance optimizations not only improve the performance of systems but also make them environmentally friendly. Our results show that performance testing is not only a tool for software quality assurance but also an effective mechanism for advancing green software engineering. We emphasize that our approach estimates carbon emissions using first-order analytical models rather than direct hardware power measurements. The goal is comparative analysis (before vs. after optimization), not absolute carbon accounting. We also discuss lessons learned from these performance tests, provide quantitative evidence of carbon reduction, and propose a framework for integrating sustainability metrics into performance testing practices.

title: A Zero-Shot Approach to Multilingual Spam Detection Using mBERT
authors: Srujana Inturi, Jasmine Mia, T.Suvarna Kumari, Guda Vanitha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The growing volume of spam messages poses a major challenge to secure online communication, especially in multilingual contexts. Traditional spam detection systems perform well in English but often fail to generalize to other languages due to limited labeled data, linguistic diversity, and frequent code-switching. This work presents a zero-shot multilingual spam detection system based on the multilingual BERT (mBERT) model. The system is trained on an English spam dataset and directly evaluated on Hindi, Telugu, and Bengali to assess cross-lingual performance without additional training. The model achieved 99.7% validation accuracy on English, with near-perfect precision and recall, confirming its effectiveness in the source language. In zero-shot testing, it generalized well, reaching F1-scores above 0.96 across all three target languages. High recall values 0.98 indicate reliable spam detection with minimal false negatives, while precision also remained consistently strong. Although a slight drop was observed for Bengali and Telugu compared to English, performance levels were still robust and suitable for deployment. These results show that mBERT provides an efficient and scalable approach to multilingual spam detection, reducing the need for language-specific datasets and supporting low-resource language settings.

title: A Rule-Based Decision-Support Prototype for Digital Maturity Assessment
authors: Dijar Mujalović, Nurudin Tivari, Lejla Gurbeta Pokvić, Sarah Spahić, Lemana Spahić, Almir Badnjević
abstract: Despite widespread discussion of digital transformation, many organizations struggle to assess their digital capability and define improvement priorities. We developed a transparent self-assessment tool, implemented as a Streamlit web application, based on 25 items grouped into five dimensions. Dimension averages are combined into a weighted overall score (1-5 scale) using explicit and visible scoring rules. The tool was evaluated through a single organizational case study (XL Labs) and a pilot expert review (N = 3), providing preliminary, non-generalizable evidence. In the case study, the organization achieved a score of 3.41 / 5.00, corresponding to the Intermediate maturity category, which remained stable under one-at-a-time sensitivity analysis (±0. 20 per dimension).

title: Quantitative Analysis and Intelligent Prediction of Defect Hazards in Power Transmission Equipment Health Management
authors: Shuai Guo, Xiangyu Zhao, Ruiye Zhou, Qinyu Feng, Shang Cao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To achieve precise quantification and intelligent prediction of defect hazards in power transmission equipment, this paper proposes an intelligent analysis framework that integrates multi-source data and domain knowledge. By constructing a RoBERTa-WDGCN dual-channel network to extract both textual and non-textual features, and utilizing a graph attention network (GAT) to embed equipment knowledge graphs for dynamic health status scoring, the framework also incorporates a TCN-Attention predictor to introduce environmental stress fields for defect evolution prediction. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves a health status classification accuracy of 94.2% on a dataset containing 85,000 text records and 52,000 infrared images. The root mean square error (RMSE) for a 30-day prediction range is 0.032, significantly outperforming baseline models. Ablation experiments reveal that the knowledge graph module contributes 4.1%, while the environmental stress field and attention mechanism reduce prediction errors by 23% and 15%, respectively. Typical case analysis confirms that the model can issue early warnings 20 days in advance, an improvement of 18 days over traditional methods. This study provides an effective technological approach for the health assessment of power transmission equipment and holds significant engineering application value.

title: Convergence Optimized Adaptive Multigrid Iterative Equalization for OTFS
authors: B M Bergyl Jeslin, T. Deepa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Modern wireless systems demand robust and stable data transmission that remains reliable under rapid time-frequency variations and effectively mitigates the delay-Doppler distortions. Under such challenging conditions, Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation emerges as a strong contender, demonstrating remarkable resilience to Doppler and delay spread effects. Yet, the robustness of the OTFS system hinges on the efficiency and effectiveness of the receiver's equalization process. Our work proposes a residual-based iterative equalization technique that combines Transpose-Free Quasi Minimal Residual (TFQMR) in combination with Algebraic Multigrid (AMG) preconditioner. This framework refines the Minimum Mean Squared Error (MMSE) by employing TFQMR in the residual domain, which avoids direct matrix inversion, while AMG provides accelerated convergence via a hierarchical multi-grid structure. Simulation results show that the proposed system achieves a better convergence rate with the condition number reducing from 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> to 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>, achieving a faster convergence, lower Bit Error Rate (BER) of approximately ten-fold error reduction at 12 dB, and less complexity overhead when compared to the conventional systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence Integrated Innovations in Surgery: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Nishant Rajendra Jumde, Harshraj N. Gadbail, Sahil Dhurve, Nakul Dube, Atharva Waghale, Rajendra Rewatkar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged across the surgical transformation through improved diagnostics, planning, and intraoperative performance. AI has appeared promising in surgical applications by employing ML and DL across cardiothoracic, vascular, and orthopaedic surgery. These developments have enhanced patient survival, reduced the incidence of complications, and improved the delivery of healthcare. Nevertheless, AI integration into surgical practice has certain limitations, including the issue of cost, where the implementation of new technologies requires extensive training and has certain ethical considerations. This review explores the current and future uses of AI in surgery to show how it can help transform surgical practice.

title: Design of Converter Load Test Energy Feedback System Based on STM32
authors: Mingsheng Yang, Quan Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address the energy dissipation issue in converter load testing, this paper designs a low-cost energy feedback system based on STM32G474RET6. The system takes SVPWM modulation and PR closed-loop control as the core, and realizes the "inversion-rectification-feedback" energy cycle through dual-converter topology reuse. Air switches and common-mode inductors are adopted to ensure stable operation. Experimental results show that the system's output voltage is stable within 32V±0.25V, with a total harmonic distortion (THD) of only 0.6% and an adjustable frequency range of 20Hz~100Hz. The energy feedback efficiency exceeds 90%. This scheme solves the energy consumption problem of traditional testing, featuring simplified hardware and controllable cost, and provides an efficient and energy-saving solution for medium and small power converter testing.

title: Continuous Inertia Estimation and Frequency Response Prediction Using Ambient Measurements
authors: Dmitry Shchetinin, Mats Larsson
abstract: The ongoing integration of converter-interfaced renewable energy sources into the grid is reducing the share of synchronous generators and thus diminishing grid inertia. In response, real-time inertia estimation has emerged as a key enabler for maintaining grid frequency stability. However, inertia alone may be insufficient as a direct indicator of grid resilience, since operators ultimately want to ensure that frequency deviations remain within secure operational limits. This paper proposes a data-driven approach capable of continuously estimating grid inertia and predicting the system frequency response to potential disturbances. It employs system identification techniques that leverage measurements of ambient load variations, eliminating the need for external perturbations. The proposed method is validated on the Nordic32 test grid in multiple scenarios, demonstrating both accuracy and robustness. The results suggest its suitability for practical deployment in real-world grid operation.

title: Multi-Scale Geographical Identification of Synchrophasor Sources for Enhancing Cyber-Physical Resilience in Distribution Networks
authors: Yi Cui, Ryan K L Ko, Tong Chen
abstract: Phasor measurement units (PMUs) play a critical role in wide-area power grid monitoring, which makes their cybersecurity vital to power grid resilience. While synchrophasor measurements recorded by PMUs have been widely used for state estimation and system dynamics monitoring, their potential for geographical source identification remains underexplored, particularly at fine spatial resolutions. This paper presents a novel study on the multi-scale geographical identification of synchrophasor sources, with a specific focus on distribution-level networks. By analyzing measurements from multiple locations, we reveal that synchrophasors exhibit location-specific features at multiple spatial scales. These findings enable enhanced detection of spoofing and anomalies, which can contribute to cyber-physical resilience in distribution networks. This work also supports the development of secure, location-aware monitoring strategies for smart grids using synchrophasors.

title: Enhancing Diabetic Retinopathy Diagnosis with Deep Neural Networks
authors: Thangamani R, Vimaladevi M, Prakasham V, Varshini S H, Mohammed Aadhil A, Sasvath K R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: DR is a critical microvascular secondary complication of diabetes, and if not identified in time, it often leads to irreversible blindness. Traditional screening techniques rely on manual fundus examination, which is not only time-consuming but mostly unavailable in under-resourced settings. This work, therefore, calls for the use of computer-aided detection and classification using fundus images of the retina. In this analysis, pre-trained Convolutional Neural Networks like ResNet50, VGG16, DenseNet, MobileNet, and EfficientNet have been fine-tuned, with image enhancement using contrast and data augmentation to emphasize subtle retinal lesions like microaneurysms and exudates. Experimental results indicated that ResNet performed best among the systems with an accuracy of 74%, thus outperforming others in classifying DR into multiple severity stages. For ease of access, this system is designed for deployment through a lightweight web-based application with real-time prediction, visualization of results, and personalized suggestions for treatment. Furthermore, this paper discusses the integration of Vision Transformers for improved lesion detection at the fine-grain level. This work contributes toward SDG 3 - Good Health and Well-being, as it proposes a scalable, accurate, affordable DR screening that would otherwise reduce clinical burden and improve healthcare equity.

title: Hybrid Neuro-Symbolic AI for Context-Aware Decision-Making Systems
authors: Sweta Chopdar, Soumadip Mondal, Sruthi Yapalaparvi, Rupam Bhagawati, Vengatesan.K, Mithun Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: More and more, context-aware decision-making systems are being used in smart environments, healthcare, autonomous systems, and intelligent industrial platforms. When it comes to perception and pattern recognition, deep learning models excel, but when it comes to logic, context, and transparency, they fall short. When it comes to ambiguity and high-dimensional data, symbolic AI falls short, but it excels when it comes to representing and reasoning about explicit knowledge. For robust, explainable, and context-aware decision-making, this study presents a hybrid neuro-symbolic AI system that combines deep neural learning with symbolic reasoning. The suggested architecture features a symbolic knowledge layer, neural sensing modules, and a context-driven inference engine that allow for dynamic adaptation to environmental, temporal, and semantic contexts. The hybrid method has superior accuracy, adaptability, and interpretability compared to neural and symbolic systems, according to the experimental data. Cognitive decision-making systems of the future may be based on neuro-symbolic integration, according to the results.

title: Design of a Golden Angle-inspired Multi-Element Yagi-Uda Antenna Array for Low-correlation 5G Lower band Applications
authors: Bandi Anusha Sumanthika, Bharath Reddy Gudibandi, Hanumantharao Bitra, Vunnava Chaitanya Kumari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The current work presents a golden-angle–inspired MIMO antenna design for 5G lower-band applications in the frequency range of 5.1–5.8 GHz. Three Yagi-Uda antenna elements are arranged on a circular substrate following the golden-angle principle to minimize spatial overlap among radiation fields, there by reducing mutual coupling and channel correlation. Simulations in ANSYS HFSS confirm good impedance matching across the operating band and an envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) consistently below 0.01. The proposed configuration demonstrates the effectiveness of golden-angle placement in achieving compact, low-correlation antenna arrays suitable for massive MIMO and high-capacity 5G communication systems.

title: Optimal Coordination of Hydrogen-enabled Integrated Energy Systems Considering Dynamic Zoning Operation of Ground Heat Exchangers
authors: Shuobin Wang, Zhanbo Xu, Jian Qiu, Kun Liu, Jiang Wu
abstract: Hydrogen-enabled integrated energy systems (HIES) provide a promising approach for sustainable, low- carbon energy supply in buildings. However, excessive use of ground-source heat pumps (GSHPs) can lead to cold accumulation in the central region of ground heat exchangers (GHEs), causing soil temperature attenuation and reduced system efficiency. Zoning operation of GHEs is considered a promising strategy to alleviate these issues, but it requires coordinated scheduling due to the complex coupling between energy supply, storage devices, and GHEs. This paper investigates the optimal coordination of HIES considering the dynamic zoning operation of GHEs. A decomposition-based iterative algorithm is developed to address the computational challenge. Numerical results show that the developed algorithm achieves near-optimal solutions with less than 0.01% performance degradation and 1.6~13.7 times speedup compared to the centralized solution. Moreover, the dynamic zoning operation of GHEs effectively mitigates soil temperature attenuation and maintains system efficiency.

title: A Hybrid Framework for the Design and Pricing of Weather Options for Photovoltaic Risk Management
authors: Haozhe Lu, Xiangrui Meng, Huanxin Liao, Wenxuan Liu, Junhua Zhao
abstract: Renewable energy, particularly photovoltaic (PV) generation, faces significant uncertainty from weather variability. Traditional risk management approaches lack tailored financial instruments specifically for solar producers. This paper proposes a weather derivative based on a Temperature-Adjusted Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI) index, termed Photovoltaic Meteorological Index (PVMI), which compensates PV operators when monthly PVMI falls below a defined threshold. A hybrid pricing framework integrating Ornstein–Uhlenbeck modeling, machine learning-based residual adjustments, and Esscher transform for risk-neutral calibration via Monte Carlo simulation is presented. Empirical evaluation using a 50 MW photovoltaic plant confirms notable improvements in revenue stability and risk mitigation.

title: Impact of Different Baffle Configurations on Natural Convection in a Square Enclosure
authors: Tejas K Raysad, Saddala Reddy Rahul, D.Arumuga Perumal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This research offers a numerical investigation of natural convection in a square enclosure equipped with different baffle configurations. Basic case used over here for natural convection in a simple square enclosure filled with air, and the initial results obtained are used to compare for validating against prior benchmark results to predict whether the applied numerical method can produce accurate metrics. So, through that validation, the impacts of internal baffles on flow and heat transfer have been studied in detail by varying the length, position, and configurations of the baffles in the analysis. A case of a central obstruction has also been analyzed. The governing equations for laminar, 2-D, incompressible flow are solved using ANSYS Fluent. The results show that baffle geometry and placement play an important role in thermal stratification, and circulation patterns in the cavity. These results can help us to get an insight into optimizing passive thermal management systems with geometry.

title: Multi-Modal Data Integrity Using the Selfattention Based Ensemble GNN for IIot
authors: L. Natrayan
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Industrial IoT systems depend on the various sectors of the industrial sites where numerous sensors are involved in the collection of data, which needs to be processed for decision-making based on its application. The large volume of real-time data generated reduces system performance, particularly in preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification. Data integrity issues are increasing in the IIoT system as its applications grow. Based on this issue, this work proposes a feature-level fusion model for the IIoT system that uses a self-attention mechanism for feature extraction and an ensemble of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for data fusion. The proposed work focuses on various modals and combines them by averaging the nodes and edges of the two modals. Thus, the proposed work was evaluated using a numeric and imagebased dataset from the IIoT system, achieving an accuracy of 97.51 % and a data fusion percentage of 96.7%.

title: Exploiting Voltage Variations in Load Redistribution Attacks to Compromise Power System Stability
authors: Zelin Liu, Yongheng Wang, Tao Liu
abstract: Conventional small-signal stability (SSS) analysis in power systems often relies on the constant voltage model (CVM), which simplifies system dynamics by assuming fixed voltage magnitudes and neglecting reactive power. This simplification can result in overly optimistic assessments of system stability. In this paper, we propose a novel load redistribution attack (LRA) that specifically targets voltage variations to degrade system SSS property, while remaining undetected by traditional operator tools based on CVM. The attack is executed in two stages: first, a vulnerable operating point with degraded SSS is identified using the full AC power flow model; second, a bi-level optimization strategy is developed to manipulate load measurements and drive the system toward this point without triggering alarms. By exploiting the mismatch between actual and perceived stability under CVM, the proposed voltage-based LRA can cause severe degradation in system stability while appearing benign to operators. Case studies on the IEEE 30-bus system demonstrate that this method can significantly reduce stability margins and even induce instability, exposing critical blind spots in existing SSS monitoring frameworks.

title: Identification of Diabetic Retinopathy Stages using Swin Transformers
authors: Toleti Sai Shanmukh Kailash, N Koteswara Rao, V Venkatareddy, Yedukondala Rao Veeranki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is one of the leading causes of vision loss globally, and early detection using retinal imaging plays a crucial role in preventing blindness. This work presents an automated DR classification framework using a fine-tuned SwinV2 Small Transformer architecture. The proposed system integrates a comprehensive preprocessing pipeline involving contrast enhancement (CLAHE), black-border cropping, and data augmentation to improve image quality and diversity. A Focal Loss function with class-balanced weights is employed to handle severe class imbalance, while mixed-precision training and adaptive learning-rate scheduling enhance convergence efficiency. Experimental results on the APTOS 2019 Blindness Detection dataset show that the proposed model effectively captures both local and global retinal features, achieving high accuracy in grading diabetic retinopathy severity. These results underscore the potential of transformer-based models as a robust alternative to conventional convolutional approaches for medical image analysis..

title: Socio-Economic Co-Driven Post-Hurricane Resilient Restoration of Urban Distribution Networks
authors: Shuo Li, Dong Liu, Chi K. Tse, Shouxiang Wang, Qianyu Zhao
abstract: Urban distribution networks (UDNs) are increasingly vulnerable to severe hurricanes, resulting in prolonged power outages that disrupt critical social services and place a heavy burden on socially vulnerable communities. Conventional restoration strategies primarily focus on economic efficiency, often neglecting social vulnerability and leading to inequitable recovery. This paper proposes a two-stage resilient restoration framework that jointly optimizes economic and social objectives by incorporating a social vulnerability index into post-disaster repair scheduling. Case studies on real city-scale networks show that, compared with approaches that consider only economic costs, the proposed method significantly accelerates service restoration in high-vulnerability areas, reducing the cumulative outage impact on critical social services by approximately 18%. The results underscore the importance of integrating social vulnerability metrics into UDN resilience planning to enable more equitable and effective recovery outcomes.

title: Research on Multi-Agent Guidance Control Strategy in EMS High Speed Maglev Vehicle Based on Virtual Leader-Follower Communication Topology
authors: Yang Feng, Chunfa Zhao, Bingzheng Chen, Zhiqiang Zhang, Xin Liang
abstract: With the high speed maglev train operation speed raising to 600 km/h, the dynamic regulation of guidance electromagnetic force also plays a critical role to ensure the running safety and ride comfort. However, researches about guidance control system remains limited. In this paper, a control strategy adopting multi-agent algorithm and virtual leader-follower communication topology (MA-VLF) is proposed innovatively. The single guidance unit system dynamic model including accurate guidance electromagnetic force calculation model is developed. The virtual leader-follower communication topology is then designed for a three-agent system. Finally, the high speed maglev vehicle system dynamics simulation is conducted in three operation conditions to compare the proposed guidance control algorithm against the traditional PID algorithm. The results demonstrate that the proposed MA-VLF guidance control algorithm significantly improves the lateral dynamic performance of the guidance system, particularly can effectively reduce the fluctuations in both of guidance gap and coil current when the vehicle is operating at speed of 600km/h. The proposed guidance control algorithm reduces guidance gap fluctuation by 46.71% and coil current fluctuation by 13.23% under crosswind disturbance compared to the traditional PID algorithm. Furthermore, when the maglev vehicle passing through a R9000m curve line, MA-VLF guidance algorithm can reduce guidance gap fluctuation with reduction of 53.5%.

title: Acoustic Noise Rejection Mode of Atomic Force Microscope Imaging: A Data-Driven Control Approach
authors: Jiarong Chen, Qingze Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: In this article, we propose an imaging mode of atomic force microscope (AFM) that is robust to environmental disturbances such as acoustic noise. AFM operation is sensitive to external disturbances such as acoustic noise, as disturbances to the probe-sample interaction directly result in distortions in the images obtained. Although conventional passive noise cancellation has been employed, the passive-noise apparatus limits the function of AFM and its use in emerging applications. Moreover, residual noise still persists. In this work, a noise rejection mode (NRM) is proposed to combat the external disturbance effect during the imaging process—regardless of the disturbance location and without identifying the disturbance dynamics. Specifically, the set point of the feedback control loop for tracking the sample topography is adaptively adjusted online such that the AFM system is insensitive and thereby, robust to the environmental noise. Then, an adaptive feedforward controller is augmented to counteract the noise-caused probe vibration. Moreover, the proposed NRM can be integrated to high-speed imaging. Experimental implementation is presented and discussed to illustrate the proposed technique.

title: Real-Time Remote Monitoring of Correlated Markovian Sources
authors: Mehrdad Salimnejad, Marios Kountouris, Nikolaos Pappas
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: We investigate real-time tracking of two correlated stochastic processes over a shared wireless channel. The joint evolution of the processes is modeled as a two-dimensional discrete-time Markov chain. Each process is observed by a dedicated sampler and independently reconstructed at a remote monitor according to a task-specific objective. Although both processes originate from a common underlying phenomenon (e.g., distinct features of the same source), each monitor is interested only in its corresponding feature. A reconstruction error is incurred when the true and reconstructed states mismatch at one or both monitors. To address this problem, we propose an error-aware joint sampling and transmission policy, under which each sampler probabilistically generates samples only when the current process state differs from the most recently reconstructed state at its corresponding monitor. We adopt the time-averaged reconstruction error and the average cost of actuation error as the primary performance metrics and benchmark the proposed policy against state-of-the-art joint sampling and transmission schemes. For each policy, we derive closed-form expressions for the corresponding time-averaged reconstruction error and average cost of actuation error. We further formulate and solve an optimization problem that minimizes the time-averaged reconstruction error subject to an average sampling cost constraint. Analytical and numerical results demonstrate that the proposed error-aware policy achieves the minimum time-averaged reconstruction error among the considered schemes while efficiently utilizing the sampling budget. The performance gains are particularly pronounced in regimes with strong inter-process correlation and stringent tracking requirements, where frequent sampling by both samplers is necessary.

title: Camera Trajectory Planning in Immersive 3-D Human Motion-Based Entertainment Contents
authors: Kyungjune Lee, Beom Kwon, Sanghoon Lee
abstract: Finding the preferred camera trajectory is an important sequence for making three-dimensional (3-D) human music videos, which is a very challenging task. To achieve this, several studies have attempted to combine human visual information with 3-D video camera trajectories. However, previous studies have been limited to specific situations, and their applications have been limited to certain scenarios. To address this limitation, we present a novel method for camera trajectory planning. Toward this goal, we first developed a perceptual metric to quantify satisfaction with the trajectory of a camera capturing a moving 3-D human model. We introduce new metrics designed to derive three features: 1) the normalized limb length sum incorporating part attention, 2) the normalized human gaze vector, and 3) the normalized stability viewpoint. Along with these features, we inherit a commonly used feature: 4) normalized projected area. By maximizing these four features, we obtain an intermediate camera trajectory. Furthermore, we introduce new metrics to obtain two features that potentially disrupt visual immersion: 1) boredom resulting from repetitive or stationary trajectories, and 2) visual discomfort caused by promiscuous camera movements.With intermediate camera trajectory, we can find an optimal camera trajectory by minimizing these two adverse features. To encapsulate these metrics, we propose a novel camera trajectory planning system (CTPS). By conducting rigorous experiments, we demonstrate that a camera trajectory from the proposed algorithm is preferred and exhibits a 22.1% higher similarity to the camera trajectory of professionals compared to existing solutions.

title: DFCNet: A Dynamic Cross-Modal Transformer for Fashion Recommendation
authors: Xin Lu, Jian-Hong Wang, Kuo-Chun Hsu
abstract: The rapid development of mobile networks and e-commerce has driven remarkable progress in fashion recommendation technologies. Existing methods have achieved substantial progress in multimodal feature fusion and graph neural network-based architectures. However, this field still faces two fundamental challenges: the information entropy dilution problem within single-modal features and the modality bias issue during cross-modal fusion.
