To address these problems, this study proposes a novel Dynamic Fashion Cross-Modal Transformer (DFCNet). The core of DFCNet lies in the introduction of the Modality-Adaptive Core Information Focusing mechanism (MACIF). By employing a feature representation purification paradigm, MACIF pre-distills visual and semantic information, effectively extracting core attributes from high-dimensional noise. Furthermore, we propose a Dual-Layer Dynamic Contribution Regulation Framework (DCRF) to achieve fine-grained semantic alignment and adaptive modality weight balancing via cross-modal tensor projection and a modality contribution meta-controller. Compared to existing methods, the DFCNet model demonstrates superior single-modal representation discriminative ability and deeper cross-modal semantic associations. Experiments conducted on the Polyvore and Polyvore-D datasets show that DFCNet outperforms state-of-the-art methods across tasks such as compatibility prediction, fill-in-the-blank, and complementary item retrieval. Ablation studies validate the effectiveness of each mechanism, while visualization results highlight its precise perception of style consistency and attribute correlation.

title: NEXA-NET: Advancing Knowledge-Driven Autonomy in AI-Native 6G and Beyond Wireless Networks
authors: Marco Sousa, Thaína Saraiva, Luís Mata, Madalena Cilínio, Pedro Vieira
abstract: The evolution toward artificial intelligence (AI)-native networks demands architectures that move beyond statistical learning to deliver transparent, policy-aligned, and knowledge-driven autonomy. Existing frameworks from the International Telecommunication Union and 3<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rd</sup> Generation Partnership Project provide a foundation for integrating AI/machine learning into 5G and beyond, but remain correlation-based, lacking the causal and semantic depth required for explainable and trustworthy autonomy. This article proposes Neuro-symbolic EXplainable Autonomy for Next-gEn Telecom systems (NEXA-NET), a neuro-symbolic framework unifying causal inference, knowledge representation, and generative AI to enable knowledge-driven autonomy in wireless networks, supported by an operational case study in a live private 5G O-RAN deployment. NEXA-NET’s five interoperable modules interpret natural language intent, perform causal reasoning, audit compliance, orchestrate execution, and maintain a semantic knowledge base. Key research challenges are outlined, presenting a vision for future AI-native networks that embed cognitive intelligence grounded in causality and semantics, advancing toward fully cognitive-native autonomy.

title: No Evidence of Phase-Amplitude Coupling in Auditory Steady-State Responses
authors: Aurimas Mockevičius, Inga GrišKova-Bulanova
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Phase-amplitude coupling (PAC), reflecting the modulation of high-frequency amplitude by the phase of lower-frequency oscillations, is increasingly recognized as a key mechanism underlying neural information processing. While PAC is typically associated with higher-order perceptual and cognitive processes, some studies explored PAC in relation to auditory steady-state responses (ASSR), a paradigm commonly used to assess gamma-band synchronization. However, findings from these studies remain inconclusive due to methodological variability and challenges in PAC analysis. In this study, we systematically investigated PAC in the EEG signal recorded during 40 Hz auditory steady-state stimulation using a rigorous analysis pipeline with three established PAC estimation methods: Mean Vector Length, Kullback-Leibler Modulation Index, and Phase-Locking Value. Our approach was validated on simulated EEG-like signals and applied to scalp EEG data from 12 participants (26.7±3.6 years, 5 females) in 40 Hz ASSR and resting-state (rsEEG) conditions. We found no significant differences in PAC between ASSR and rsEEG conditions in the ASSR-associated frontocentral region, regardless of PAC estimation methods. Furthermore, individual-specific peak PAC values and their associated frequencies showed no consistent patterns across conditions. These results suggest that PAC is not reliably elicited by auditory steady-state stimulation in EEG, challenging the utility of the ASSR paradigm for assessing PAC.

title: Decoding taVNS Effects via Neural and Autonomic Biomarker Sensing for Stress, Depression, Anxiety, and Emotion Regulation: A Review_supp1-3673727.doc
authors: Hasan Al-Nashash

title: Spatiotemporal Feature Learning for Real-Time Human Fall Detection System using TCNs and FMCW Radar Point Clouds
authors: Garry Nelson, Istiqomah, Fiky Y. Suratman
abstract: Falls among elderly individuals remain a major health concern, often leading to serious injuries or fatalities if not detected promptly. Existing vision and wearable-based systems face privacy, comfort, and reliability issues, while most radar-based methods are limited to offline analysis. Efficient modeling of long-term dependencies is crucial for real-time radar applications. Recurrent models often face gradient and latency issues, whereas Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs) enable stable, parallel, and low-latency processing through dilated causal convolutions. This study presents a real-time human fall detection system that integrates Frequency-Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radar with TCN for accurate and privacy-preserving monitoring. The FMCW radar (TI IWR6843) captures three-dimensional point cloud data representing human motion, which are segmented and normalized before TCN-based spatiotemporal modeling using dilated causal convolutions. The optimized model achieved 99.82% accuracy, 99.71% precision, 99.59% recall, and an F1-score of 99.65%. A multithreaded architecture enables parallel radar acquisition, preprocessing, and inference, reducing latency and ensuring continuous real-time operation. Experimental results demonstrate detection accuracy ranging from 70% to 100% depending on the target’s location, with an average latency of 1.9867 seconds and an overall detection accuracy of 87.7%, confirming the system’s effectiveness and reliability for real-world applications.

title: Sub-terahertz scattering-type scanning near-field optical microscopy for biological cell imaging
authors: Zhaomin Peng, Jun Shi, Wei Mao, Mingyue Liu, Yang Yu, Dehai Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Biological cell imaging in the sub-terahertz (sub-THz) regime is exceptionally challenging due to the intrinsically low dielectric contrast of weakly scattering specimens. Here, we present a sub-THz scattering-type scanning near-field optical microscopy (s-SNOM) system, specifically engineered to overcome this limitation. Superior near-field sensitivity is achieved through a strategically designed zero-intermediate-frequency (Zero-IF) architecture, featuring high transmitter-receiver isolation (39.95 dB at 105.87 GHz), and a custom-designed probe with a 100 nm tip radius. This probe design deliberately maximizes the near-field scattering efficiency for low-dielectric materials. We validated the system's performance by imaging ovarian cancer cells in three distinct preparations: cell-suspension, thick-sliced (5 μm), and ultrathin-sliced (100-200 nm) samples. The system successfully generated high-fidelity, nanoscale images with signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) up to 50.3 dB, clearly resolving subcellular structures. This success demonstrates the potential of the s-SNOM platform to serve as a powerful label-free, non-invasive imaging tool for cellular biology and clinical diagnostic research.

title: Dual-Mode Time-Sharing-Based Buck and Boost Inverter With Reactive Power Injection Capability
authors: Yunfeng Xu, Weimin Wu, Mohamed Orabi, Eftichios Koutroulis, Henry Shu-Hung Chung, Frede Blaabjerg
abstract: Among the numerous single-phase nonisolated single-stage buck and boost inverters (SS-BBIs), dual-mode time-sharing-based SS-BBIs offer significant advantages in terms of high efficiency, buck and boost operation, and low voltage stress. SS-BBIs with the neutral-point clamped structure can suppress leakage current. However, the main drawback of SS-BBIs combining dual-mode time-sharing technology and the neutral-point clamped structure is the inability to inject reactive power and achieve bidirectional energy flow. This article provides a single-phase nonisolated SS-BBI with a multiplexed buck and boost inductor and six insulated gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs) to meet the aforementioned requirements. In this article, the proposed scheme is justified from the perspectives of operating principles, mathematical modeling, design guidelines, control strategy, experimental verification, and comparison analysis. Finally, an experimental prototype with 500 W nominal power is employed in the experimental section to verify performance under the 110~V and 50 Hz AC grid.

title: Fingerprint Presentation Attack Detection with Fixed Prototype Contrastive Learning and Anomaly Detection
authors: Chuanwei Huang, Hongyan Fei, Pengcheng Luo, Jingwei Li, Jufu Feng
abstract: Fingerprint Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) is crucial for ensuring the reliability of fingerprint recognition systems. Mainstream methods typically frame PAD as a binary classification task. Although they achieve commendable results, these approaches often fail on unseen spoofs. This is because their decision boundaries rely heavily on trainset spoofs, leading to poor generalization. To address this issue, we reformulate PAD as an anomaly detection problem and propose the Fixed-Prototype PAD method. First, we introduce Fixed-Prototype Contrastive Learning (FPCL) for extracting PAD features. FPCL clusters the features of live fingerprints near a fixed prototype while pushing the features of spoof fingerprints away. We then design a Fixed-Prototype Anomaly Detection method to replace binary classification for decision-making. Specifically, this involves comparing the distance between test fingerprint features and the fixed live prototype to detect spoofs. Our method eliminates the dependency on the trainset spoofs during decision-making, thereby enhancing generalization capability. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on the LivDet 2019 and LivDet 2021 benchmarks.

title: ICIPCN 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)

title: Machine Learning-Enhanced Optimization for Resilient PV Placement Under Multiple Distribution Network Hazards
authors: Mahdi Azimian, Xinwei Shen, Umar Farooq
abstract: This paper presents a machine learning-enhanced optimization framework for optimal photovoltaic (PV) placement in distribution networks under multiple hazard scenarios. The proposed method integrates neural network-based forecasting with enhanced particle swarm optimization to address equipment failures, extreme weather events, and cyber-security threats simultaneously. The framework employs a multi-objective approach that balances technical performance, economic viability, and system resilience. Neural networks predict load demands and PV generation patterns under dynamic conditions. Comprehensive testing on the IEEE 69-bus system demonstrates significant improvements across key metrics: 25.8% renewable penetration, 1.62% voltage profile enhancement, 12.5% SAIDI reliability improvement, and a 6.8-year payback period. The neural network achieves 95.2% prediction accuracy with 7.32-second convergence time, supporting real-time applications. The framework maintains IEEE voltage standards while reducing power losses and enhancing system resilience against multiple hazards.

title: A Multi-Hazard Resilient Power Distribution System Planning with Enhanced Column-and-Constraint Generation Implementation
authors: Umar Farooq, Xinwei Shen, Mahdi Azimian
abstract: Modern power distribution systems face increasing challenges from renewable energy integration, extreme weather events, and dynamic load patterns. These challenges necessitate resilient infrastructure design. This research presents a robust multi-hazard optimization framework for distributed generation (DG) and energy storage system (ESS) placement using enhanced column-and-constraint generation (CCG) methodology with min-max-min trilevel structure. The framework incorporates detailed AC power flow constraints and polyhedral uncertainty sets. It addresses multiple hazard scenarios including equipment failures, cyberattacks, and extreme weather events. Investment costs, operational constraints, voltage quality criteria, and reliability metrics under uncertainty are all considered in the model. Computational experiments on IEEE 33-bus and 123-bus systems with real-world data demonstrate substantial improvements in cost reduction, computational efficiency, and solution reliability compared to traditional robust optimization approaches. Statistical validation confirms the significance of observed performance improvements. The enhanced CCG algorithm achieves faster convergence while ensuring optimal voltage profiles, ensuring robust operational compliance and enabling proactive smart grid planning.

title: An Innovative Design of Energy-Efficient Digital Twin Model for Enhanced Wind Farming and Multimodal Behaviour Recognition using Deep Learning
authors: Lingala Venkateswarlu, Omar Mustafa Ahmad Al-Omari, S Kondala Rao, S. Agnes Shifani, Vinay Kulkarni, Rajini Parimala
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: A Digital Twin (DT) model is a virtual representation of a physical system that uses real-time data to simulate performance and aid operators in decision-making for real-world scenarios. This paper presents the Integrated Encoder–Digital Twin Wind Monitoring (IEDTWM) model, merging Lidar data, turbine parameters, and Navier-Stokes equations in a deep learning framework to enhance flow estimation and behaviour recognition in wind farms. Utilising a physics-informed neural network with a composite loss function ensures physical consistency while inferring flow variables. The DT component leverages real-time and historical data to simulate wind farm behaviour and adjust predictions dynamically. A ResNet-18 visual encoder and MLP-based sensor encoder for stable feature extraction, which includes a cross-modal mapping network to maintain data representation in case of missing inputs. The performance analysis is calculated at boiler efficiency of 92% to 96%, gas consumption of 30 to 85 kWh, seasonal efficiency of 92.38%, seasonal gas consumption of 252.75 MWh, and throughput of 0.45 KB/s with 10 requests.

title: CNN Based Optimization for High Gain Converter for Renewable Applications
authors: Ms S Gomathi, Mouseena Banu M, Oviyavarshini R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The increasing demand for efficient integration of dc sources with DC microgrids mirrors the limitations imposed by the classical DC–DC converters that use Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithm and inverter stages, leading to high cost, complexity, and conversion losses. In response, the present study puts forth an interleaved quartic high-gain DC–DC converter that directly interfaces with solar panels, energy storages, and DC loads without using an inverter stage. The proposed technique involves a hybrid control scheme that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Genetic Algorithm (GA) optimization for adaptive and precise voltage and current control according to dynamic variations in solar irradiance and loads. The proposed system provides enhanced energy flow control, grid stability, and reliability for both standalone and DC-grid mode of operations. The novelty entails the combination of an interleaved quartic converter topology with a CNN–GA hybrid control scheme that provides enhanced power density, reduced current ripple, minimized thermal stress, and maximum energy utilization efficiency. The aim is a scalable, high-performance platform for sustainable dc-based energy systems.

title: Impact of GRI-linked ESG Requirements on Energy Strategy of Listed Companies
authors: Ling Wang, Jue Wang, Liang Gao, Dongqun Xiao, Yaozhou Ma
abstract: The topic of Environment, Social and Governance (ESG) is involved in the information disclosure of listed companies. Global Report Initiative (GRI) is a widely adopted ESG report standard. This paper examines the energy information disclosure requirements of GRI (GRI-302) and makes a comparison of energy disclosures among Hong Kong, the US, and China, which adopt GRI-linked ESG Reporting Guides, revealing distinct focuses and practices. Further analysis on the vehicle industry, based on the reports of an HKEX-listed vehicle company, is performed for GRI-oriented optimization of energy strategy. The analysis compared the measures under every disclosure item of GRI-302, which are evaluated on their contribution to an optimized energy strategy. Finally, the author applies the tool of SWOT analysis to build a framework that helps to reshape the energy strategy of a publicly listed company complying with GRI-linked ESG Requirements, mitigating energy impacts from ESG requirements.

title: Decentralized and Intelligent Hybrid Cognitive Academic Systems: Blockchain and AI-Driven Approaches to Personalized Learning
authors: Raunak Singh, Prikshat Kumar Angra, Shashi Kant Gupta, Pritpal Singh, Ashwani Kumar, Gagandeep Singh Cheema
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The rapid evolution of digital education requires safe, transparent and adaptive frameworks to aid in personalization. Classical academic systems frequently entail problems including privacy issues, centralized control, opaqueness and lack of flexibility towards different learners. In this paper, a Decentralized and Intelligent Hybrid Cognitive Academic System (DI-HCAS) based on the integration of Blockchain and Artificial Intelligence (AI) is introduced to enhance secure personalization and scalability in learning environments. Blockchain enables permanent record retention, transparency, and decentralized access to educational data; AI cognitive models deliver adaptive learning paths, predictive analytics (student success and behavior), and personalized advice. Institutions automatically verify credentials and manage trust using smart contracts incorporated into the blockchain system for data sharing, while models (e.g. machine learning) provide real-time decision-making and learning analytics. A hybrid AI architecture is proposed, which integrates on-chain security and off-chain AI processing to improve the scalability and efficiency. Case studies and simulations indicate that DI-HCAS leads to better user engagement, greater confidence in academic records, and imparts adaptive cognitive support based on student personality. Through the integration of blockchain and AI, it forms an independent and reliable paradigm for a new generation academic ecosystem, guaranteeing security, personalization, interoperability and sustainability in digital education.

title: Urban Digital Twins for Adaptive Water Quality Monitoring in Smart Cities: A Robotics-Based Approach
authors: Umar Alfa, Auwal Shehu Tijjani, Luc Fabresse, Eric Duviella, Raymond Houe Ngouna
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: Urban water resources face increasing threats from pollution and climate change. These challenges necessitate adaptive management strategies that traditional monitoring methods cannot adequately provide. This paper introduces a system architecture for the closed-loop integration of autonomous Surface Aquatic Drones (SADs) with an Urban Digital Twin (UDT) to facilitate real-time, adaptive water quality monitoring. The framework consists of three core layers: a physical layer incorporating sensor-equipped SADs, a communication interface that ensures robust data transmission, and a digital layer dedicated to analytics and visualization. A simulation-based validation environment was developed using ROS 2 and Gazebo to implement and test the critical feedback loop. Preliminary results indicate successful autonomous navigation, real-time sensor data assimilation into a Cellular Automata (CA)-based spatiotemporal model, and the operation of an adaptive response mechanism. This study validates a foundational architecture that cyber-physical systems in environmental monitoring. The resulting framework provides a scalable and sustainable testbed for the future integration of advanced AI/ML models into environmental monitoring. The demonstrated integration of robotics with digital twins through a systems engineering approach represents a significant advancement in intelligent and responsive urban water management.

title: Review of Frequency Control Strategies for Renewable Power Systems under Extreme Weather Conditions
authors: Ming Pei, Lin Ye, Ruqing Gong, Yinzhe Wang, Licheng Sha, Yong Tang
abstract: The increasing frequency of extreme weather events poses significant challenges to the power and energy balance of new energy power systems, making it difficult to ensure the safe and stable operation of the power grid. Focusing on the impact of extreme weather on the source-grid-load elements of new energy power systems, this paper explores methods and strategies for maintaining power balance under extreme weather conditions, with frequency security as the main concern. It analyzes measures and strategies for power balance under extreme weather events across different time scales and proposes specific approaches from multiple perspectives—generation side, grid side, and load side—to ensure power balance. Additionally, the paper discusses an optimized dispatch framework for new energy power systems under extreme weather conditions and provides an outlook on future research directions for maintaining power and energy balance in such systems.

title: Resource Optimization in Edge Networks with Game Theory Model
authors: Sandhya Thakur, Sumit Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The Rapid development of edge computing provides decentralized computational platform which minimize delays and improve bandwidth efficiency. Edge computing is increasingly important in today’s dynamic application ecosystem, where edge servers are deployed by firms to deliver a smoother experience to users. To stay competitive, application providers aim to use edge servers as cost effectively as possible. Application providers require a cost effective solution for employing edge infrastructure. This paper introduces a Multi Agent Edge Selection Game (MAES Game), game theory based solution that presents the resource optimization problem at the network edge as a potential game. The MAES Game provides a stable solution, as the application provider will necessarily converge to at least one Pure Nash equilibrium (PNE). Furthermore, the analysis shows that the Price of Stability (PoS) of the MAES Game is bounded by O(log m), ensuring near-optimal cost efficiency in equilibrium.

title: Bachelors Buddy
authors: Omkar Mallikarjun Gopchade, Yash Ravindra Jadhav, Sandesh Anandrao Jadhav, Varsha Y Bhole
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: This paper re-presents Bachelor’s Buddy, a combined digital platform which addresses daily living problems of bachelor students and bachelor working profession. This system compiles essential services including mess services, paying guest and flat services, iron shops, and barber shops. It’s a One stop solution to the bachelors they actually suffer in the new cities those are shifted First time its easy to the bachelors they can find the require services initially they are actually wanted. Frontend is developed using React and Tailwind CSS, where middleware is developed using Node.js with express.js routing and in the backend used firebase a real time database for storing real time data.

title: Development of a Rapidly Frequency-Agile Microwave Source for Nitrogen-Vacancy Center-Based Quantum Current Sensors
authors: Chen Hu, Aichao Yang, Liang Zhu, Min Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Quantum NV center-based current transformers enable high-precision current measurement, relying primarily on optically detected magnetic resonance (ODMR) technology and resonant frequency tracking methods. However, conventional microwave sources currently face issues such as slow frequency switching speed, limited dynamic range, lack of coordinated control, and insufficient stability. To address these challenges, this paper investigates the development of a rapidly frequency-agile microwave source for quantum NV center-based current transformers. Based on frequency synthesis technology, a DDS frequency source module was designed, utilizing the AD9914BCP to generate a digitally programmable high-frequency synthesizer. A 10 MHz oven-controlled crystal oscillator (OCXO) provides the reference signal for the DDS. The local oscillator (LO) unit, employing the LMX2572, generates the LO signal required for upconversion, while the mixer unit, featuring the LTC5510 IQ, upconverts the intermediate frequency (IF) signal from the DDS to the microwave frequency required by the system. Performance test results demonstrate that the developed frequency-agile microwave source achieves an output power of 21.44 dBm, a flatness of 2.41 dB, and a frequency stability of 0.0127 ppm.

title: Multi-Criteria Evaluation of Mobile Navigation Applications for Non-SOLAS Vessels in Confined Coastal Waters
authors: Ivan Mraković, Igor Stanovčić, Igor Petrović, Rino Bošnjak, Božo Krstajić
abstract: The increasing reliance on mobile navigation applications by non-SOLAS vessels introduces safety challenges in confined coastal waters, where traditional Aids to Navigation (AtoN) are increasingly complemented by virtual solutions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V$</tex> AtoN). This paper presents a structured multi-criteria evaluation of four commercial navigation applications and one locally developed solution, BokaSafe, using the Boka Bay as a case study. An expert-based SMARTER method combined with Rank Order Centroid (ROC) weighting was applied to ten evaluation criteria derived from established literature. Results indicate that while commercial applications perform well in general usability, they often lack critical V-AtoN integration and safety alerting capabilities. Applications incorporating context-aware and V-AtoN-related functionalities demonstrate measurable advantages in safety-critical categories; quantitatively, the locally developed BokaSafe achieved the highest aggregated score of 0.979, compared to 0.74 for the best-performing commercial application (ORCA).

title: Ensemble Learning Method for Brain Tumor Classification: Techniques, Applications, and Frontiers in Glioma Analysis
authors: Arvind Singh, Puneet Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The diagnosis and categorization of cancerous and non-cancerous brain tumors imposed a major challenge in medical diagnosis. It has non-negligible importance for treatment strategy and outcome. Recent improvements in brain tumor classification have been observed using an ensemble learning technique. This approach integrates multiple models for enhancing the accuracy of prediction. This study gives an exhaustive summary of an ensemble learning approach in tumor categorization. The survey covers an extensive set of ensemble techniques, such as bagging, boosting, and stacking. The main results obtained from this study show that: (i) the proposed ensemble model always outperforms isolated classifiers, with general increases of 3-8% in classification accuracy; (ii) integration of different modalities such as T1 image, T2 image, T2 CE and FLAIR MR sequences is an important factor for improving model performance; and (iii) addressing class imbalance through data-level methods is essential for reliable clinical use. Discussion demonstrates/illustrates how various feature-picking strategies, pre-processing steps, and including multi-modal data influence the efficiency of the models. This research identifies the potential drawbacks, including feature sizes and imbalanced class sizes, among others, and provides possible workarounds. The survey further discusses recent findings and possible paths for future scope to do extensive research in brain tumor categorization with the help of an ensemble approach. Research shows promising avenues of using hybrid models, optimal use of data from diverse sources, and several state-of-the-art techniques, including transfer learning, to improve accuracy. A multi-model dataset is a contribution to an open challenge for researchers and doctors to compile more accurate and reproducible models of brain classification. This is done by means of a comprehensive review of the current practices and pinpoints gaps that need to be studied.

title: Generalized Inertia:An Index to Evaluate the Small Signals Stability in Droop Controlled Microgrids
authors: Haochen Zhang, Yu Wang
abstract: The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources intensifies stability challenges in droop controlled microgrids, where conventional small-signal models struggle to quantify effective inertia. This paper proposes a characteristic root index-based framework that reformulates the coupling between power and frequency/voltage into a compact mapping matrix. Building on this, a novel generalized inertia index is introduced, defined via eigenvalue analysis, to extend the inertia concept to inverter-dominated systems. The index quantitatively measures inertia levels and reveals the sensitivity of dynamic stability to droop parameters. For representative AC microgrids, both time-domain and frequency-domain simulations confirm that the proposed index tracks inertia reduction, identifies critical stability margins, and provides clear guidance for droop coefficient tuning. Compared with conventional approaches, the generalized inertia index offers a concise, accurate, and computationally efficient tool for inertia assessment, making it a practical methodology for evaluating the stability level of droop controlled microgrids.

title: A novel fast-turn-on DTSCR ESD protection device for low-voltage applications
authors: Gongtang Yu, Shanglin Yang, Juin Jei Liou
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel diode-triggered silicon-controlled rectifier (NTDTSCR) for electrostatic discharge (ESD) protection, designed specifically for 1.8 V input/output interfaces. By adopting an embedded diode-triggered structure and metal wiring to inject current from the conducting diode into the base of the internal NPN transistor of the SCR, the trigger current is lowered, enabling the device to realize bidirectional conduction capability during both positive and negative ESD events. The design separates the diode-triggering path from the full-turn-on SCR path, resulting in excellent current discharge capacity and rapid device activation. This decoupling of the triggering path and the fully conducting SCR path reduces the trigger voltage, increases the holding voltage, and shortens the turn-on time.

title: Energy Management for Renewable-Colocated Artificial Intelligence Data Centers
authors: Siying Li, Lang Tong, Timothy D. Mount
abstract: We develop an energy management system (EMS) for artificial intelligence (AI) data centers with colocated renewable generation. Under a cost-minimizing framework, the EMS of renewable-colocated data center (RCDC) co-optimizes AI workload scheduling, on-site renewable utilization, and electricity market participation. Within both wholesale and retail market participation models, the economic benefit of the RCDC operation is maximized. Empirical evaluations using real-world traces of electricity prices, data center power consumption, and renewable generation demonstrate significant electricity cost reduction from renewable and AI data center colocations.

title: Epilepsy Detection Using an Adaptive Network-Fuzzy Inference System
authors: Muzaffar Naveed, Krishna Tejus E M, Alivelu M Parimi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Epileptic patients suffer random physical symptoms during a seizure event, as these occur randomly without any obviously noticeable pattern. This makes it challenging to monitor and detect the health condition of a patient. Therefore, epilepsy type detection becomes important in the medical field because of its accurate and efficient classification requirements. Earlier studies used single signals and complex computational methods to identify the type classification of epilepsy. However, these studies did not take into consideration the categorization of Non-Epileptic Seizure (NES) from general seizure types. Thereby increasing the false alarms and inaccuracies of epilepsy type classification. In the study, a multi-signal analysis method is used to identify the type of epilepsy using an Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS). The results validate a significant categorization of NES in other types of epilepsy with an accuracy of 99.09% and offer an optimized framework for epilepsy diagnostics.

title: A Study on Battery SOC Estimation Method Based on TAM-PSO Optimised UKF
authors: Zhixiang Wang, Baigong Wu, Keyuan Su, Wenqiang Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: State-of-charge estimation is a critical component of battery management systems amid the rapid development of energy storage technologies and new energy vehicles. However, strong nonlinearity, temperature sensitivity, and ageing effects make it difficult for conventional SOC estimation methods to achieve both robustness and accuracy. In addition, the dependence of the UKF measurement noise covariance matrix R on empirical settings may degrade filtering stability and accuracy. This paper proposes an enhanced Unscented Kalman Filter incorporating trend-aware particle swarm optimisation. The Trend-Aware Mechanism-PSO (TAM-PSO) algorithm exploits global search capability to optimise R by minimising SOC estimation RMSE. By integrating trend vectors with adaptive random vectors, the method mitigates premature convergence and balances dynamic responsiveness with noise suppression. Compared with the conventional UKF, the proposed approach reduces RMSE, MAE, and maximum error by 65.6%, 66.2%, and 70%, respectively.

title: NETex-CAD: A Novel Neural Essence Transfer Explainable Framework via Knowledge Distillation for Lightweight Coronary Artery Disease Prediction
authors: Pial Paul, Rosni Akter, Md. Musfiqur Rahman Akib, Habibur Rahaman
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Coronary artery disease (CAD) is the leading cause of death worldwide, however current diagnostic methods are invasive and resource heavy. While deep learning and ensemble approaches have shown good predictive accuracy for CAD classification, their computing requirements significantly limit real-time implementation in clinical settings with restricted resources. This paper proposes a lightweight knowledge distillation system to bridge the crucial gap between high accuracy and clinical deployability from a pure CatBoost teacher model to a shallow three-layer deep neural network student. We use temperature-scaled soft targets (T=5) in combination with a weighted hybrid loss function (α = 0.3) to transfer the teacher’s decision knowledge to the student and ensure model interpretability. In efficacy, evaluated on new 952 merged patient records from 5 public CAD cohorts, the distilled student obtains an accuracy of 0.90 and AUC 0.95 which is recovering over 96% performance of teacher but with model size costed only at 50KB in a sub-millisecond inference time. More importantly, the student maintains probabilistic calibration to teacher and inherits feature-based interpretability as confirmed by SHAP analysis (age, cholesterol and ST depression remain clinically coherent predictors). Our results show that knowledge distillation is an effective way to close the gap between accuracy and efficiency in CAD prediction, allowing high-fidelity models to be put on edge devices and resource starved deployments without losing clinical transparency or decision reliability.

title: Deep Learning Ensemble Framework for Real-Time Traffic Accident Detection in Smart Cities with AMSE - ADM Data Augmentation
authors: Porika Himabindhu, Kumbala Pradeep Reddy, Mateti Rajkumar, Ganga Sanuvala, Amgoth Narendra
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The fast growing and very variable nature of road traffic presents professional challenges to public safety and effective traffic management in smart city surroundings. Frequent traffic accidents not only result in loss of life but also break urban motility systems, featuring the need for intelligent and automated accident detection explanations. This work presents a complete experimental study of existing traffic accident detection techniques, highlighting the current limitations of the state of the art paths while offering an in-depth analysis of common accident types such as crashes of rear-end, crashes at T-bone, & front accident simpact. To address these objections, the proposed work introduces a novel and lightweight deep learning architecture named AMSE-ADM, specifically designed for traffic accident detection of vision-based in smart city surveillance systems. The model effectively involves RGB video frames with flow of optical information to capture both spatial and temporal motion features of traffic scenes. The architecture influences advanced spatio temporal feature extraction mechanisms along with enhanced temporal dependency modeling across video sequences, enabling accurate detection of accident events. Extensive experimental evaluation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach, showing that the AMSE-ADM model outperforms other configurations. Furthermore, this study discusses critical challenges such as data imbalance, limited datasets, and constrained road and traffic scenarios, which significantly impact model generalization. Overall, the proposed framework paves the way for an efficient accident detection model in real-time and which is suitable for deployment on edge devices within smart city traffic infrastructures.

title: Approach Guidance and Control for Tumbling Uncooperative Target Using Adaptive Artificial Potential Field
authors: Satoshi Ohya, Toshinori Kuwahara
abstract: Approaching an uncooperative spacecraft (target) is a critical technique for on-orbit servicing (OOS) missions. In active debris removal (ADR) missions in particular, the target’s motion cannot be estimated precisely until the service spacecraft (chaser) approaches and observe it directly. Therefore, the guidance and control system must be capable of approaching a tumbling target. This study proposes a guidance and control method using an adaptive artificial potential function (AAPF) to approach a tumbling, uncooperative target. In this study, a new guidance technique is proposed that introduces a rotational vector field alongside AAPF, allowing the chaser to account for the rotational motion of the target. The guidance sequence is divided into three phases by comparing the chaser’s acceleration constraint and the acceleration required for tumbling synchronization; (1) approach to a sphere of radius A within which synchronization is feasible; (2) synchronize with the target’s tumbling; and (3) approach the desired point. In the final phase, the chaser synchronizes with the target’s tumbling and approaches the desired point using the proposed guidance method which combines AAPF and RVF. The findings contribute to approach and capture technologies for OOS missions.

title: An Efficient Privacy-Preserving Authentication Scheme for VANETs Using Bilinear Pairing
authors: Shiva Kumar Bejjenki, Arun Sekar Rajasekaran, Ramkumar Natarajan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) require efficient, secure, and privacy-preserving communication among vehicles and roadside infrastructure to have message integrity and maintain vehicle privacy. Existing authentication schemes provide security and privacy; however, they are unfit for real-time applications due to their high computational overhead and high communication overheads. In this work, an efficient, anonymous authentication method with bilinear pairing concept is used. The proposed scheme optimizes cryptographic operations to reduce signing and verification times on both the sender and receiver sides, thereby lowering overall communication and communication costs. Specifically, the scheme reduces the message signing and verification times by 62.54% and 16.42%, respectively, in the computational analysis, and reduces the communication cost by 56.25% in the communication cost analysis. Furthermore, the suggested work provides conditional privacy, resistance to common attacks, and traceability for misbehaving vehicles. Compared with existing schemes, it achieves significant improvements in authentication delay, verification speed, and overall resource consumption, making it suitable for large-scale VANET deployments.

title: Research Status, Challenges, and Future Trends of Deep Reinforcement Learning in Microgrid Optimization Scheduling
authors: Xuanyuan Dong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Against the backdrop of global energy transition and large-scale integration of distributed renewable energy, microgrids (MG) are crucial for enhancing renewable energy absorption and ensuring energy supply resilience, yet their optimal dispatch faces severe source-load uncertainties. Traditional methods like linear programming are constrained by reliance on precise models and poor dynamic adaptability. Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) provides an innovative solution with its strengths in high-dimensional state processing, dynamic sequential decision-making, and data-driven learning. By customizing state, action, and reward functions for MG scenarios, algorithms such as DQN, DDPG, and TD3 enable real-time optimization for single MG, while MA-SAC and other multi-agent DRL algorithms break through the bottleneck of multi-MG collaborative dispatch. However, DRL applications still suffer from limitations including low training sample efficiency, hyperparameter sensitivity, poor interpretability, and weak generalization in practical scenarios. Future research will focus on optimizing algorithms via integration of model predictive control, meta-learning, and transfer learning, accelerating technology deployment through digital twins and edge computing, and extending applications to integrated energy systems and electric vehicle fleet interactions. This review systematically elaborates on MG scheduling requirements, DRL adaptability, algorithm classifications, application progress, technical challenges, and future directions, providing a comprehensive reference for the field.

title: Real-Time Identification of Laryngeal Carcinoma Using Enhanced Endoscopic Imaging and Convolutional Models
authors: Pavithra Mani, Gunasundari A, Mouna S, Ilakiya Thangavelu, Haritha Shanmugam, Kavyasree N V
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Accurate and early identification of laryngeal squamous cell carcinoma (LSCC) is vital for optimizing patient outcomes, yet diagnosis through video laryngoscopy remains highly dependent on clinical expertise. This study introduces a real-time detection framework for LSCC by employing a convolutional neural network trained on both white light (WL) and narrow-band imaging (NBI) endoscopic footage. A total of 675 video frames, derived from 219 histologically confirmed cases, were utilized to develop and evaluate several models based on the YOLOv5 architecture. Image enhancement methods, such as contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization and robust data augmentation, were applied to improve detection performance. The most effective model—an ensemble of YOLOv5s and YOLOv5m incorporating test time augmentation—achieved a precision of 66%, recall of 62% and a mean average precision (mAP) of 63% at a 0.5 IoU threshold, with an average inference time of 0.026 seconds per frame, the system demonstrates strong potential for integration into real- time clinical workflows, particularly in non-special environments.

title: Automated Real-Time Epileptic Seizure Detection and Alert System
authors: G. Valarmathi, Reena Pagare, Nandana Shibu, Renju P Pittapillil, Parthiv Roshan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Epilepsy is a neurological disorder associated with recurrent seizures. Traditional detection methods often rely on manual EEG analysis, which can be time-consuming and subjective. This paper presents an automated deep learning framework using 1D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to classify EEG signals from the Bonn University dataset into Normal, Abnormal, and Epileptic categories. To enhance patient safety, the system integrates Twilio to send real-time SMS alerts for abnormal or epileptic predictions. The proposed model attained an accuracy of approximately 88% on the test dataset. Its performance is further evaluated and compared with existing models, including ResNet, SVM, LSTM, and MLP, using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The results demonstrate that the CNN-based approach offers improved execution speed, making it suitable for real-time deployment. Future extensions include evaluation on large EEG dataset such as CHB-MIT and TUH EEG to improve clinical generalization

title:
Multivariate Forecasting of Co
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Emissions Using Hybrid Machine Learning Models Based on Energy Consumption and Renewable Adoption
authors: Elluri Sri Lakshmi, Kavitha R, Dalvin Vinoth
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The study presents a machine learning approach to predict carbon dioxide (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) emissions by analysing key factors such as energy consumption, renewable energy adoption, and economic growth (GDP). Traditional forecasting methods struggle to capture the complex and nonlinear patterns of emissions. To overcome the limitations and improve the accuracy, research combines classical statistical models like ARIMA and VAR with advance techniques, including deep learning (LSTM) and ensemble methods (XGBoost, stacking). The models are trained on a global dataset of energy and economic records. The results shows that the hybrid models, particularly the LSTM + XGBoost and stacked approaches, have outperformed the traditional methods by obtaining a lower Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and a higher coefficient of determination (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>). Apart from advancing environmental data science, the research offers a solid predictive framework to support policy initiatives related to the Sustainable Development Goals, specifically SDG 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy) and SDG 13 (Climate Action).

title: LightViT-Eye: Lightweight Vision Transformer for Eye Diseases Classification
authors: Jayasree P, A.Shajin Nargunam, Ajay Sajan B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: LightViT-Eye, a lightweight Vision Transformer for automatic classification of common eye diseases, including diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, age-related macular degeneration, and cataract, using retinal fundus and OCT images. This model replaces conventional patch embedding with efficient convolutional tokenizers and employs compact transformer blocks with reduced heads and dimensions to minimize computational cost. A hybrid local–global feature extraction strategy, combining inverted residual blocks and small transformer modules, enhances visual representation for maintaining efficiency. Targeted data augmentations optimized for retinal imaging further improve model generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that LightViT-Eye achieves competitive accuracy with substantially fewer parameters and FLOPs, enabling reliable deployment on clinical edge and mobile devices.

title: Single-Sensor Sampling Current Control Strategy of Multiphase Interleaved DC-DC Converters
authors: Di Shen, Fuyuan Zhuang, Yiming Jia, Kejie Zhou, Jie Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address the practical needs of emergency response to power supply failures in urban rail transit, this paper proposes a design scheme for an emergency traction power supply system. Subsequently, to tackle the current imbalance issue in parallel topologies, a dual-closed-loop control strategy employing anti-saturation PID controllers and an improved feedforward-dual-closed-loop cooperative current sharing control strategy were designed to achieve system current balancing. Dynamic current sharing in a nine-phase parallel system was effectively realized through a nine-branch current detection mechanism. Experimental verification was conducted based on the proposed approach. The reliability of the control scheme is demonstrated.

title: Autision: An AI-Based Hybrid System for Early Autism Risk Prediction
authors: Leela Sowmya D
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Timely intervention and improved developmental outcomes for children depend on early detection of autism spectrum disorder (ASD). Conventional diagnostic techniques are frequently unreliable, subjective, and time-consuming in environments with limited resources. In this paper, we introduce Autision, a hybrid screening system based on artificial intelligence that uses facial and behavioral features to predict the likelihood of ASD. Our method uses a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) based on ResNet18 for facial image analysis, Support Vector Machines (SVM) for behavioral data classification, and a fusion layer to produce reliable predictions. To improve accessibility and user engagement, a chatbot driven by OpenAI is also included. As a potential pre-screening tool for early ASD risk detection, Autision exhibits high accuracy and scalability, making it a promising AI-assisted tool for early autism risk detection in both clinical and non-clinical settings.

title: Optimal Frequency Trajectory-Based Tuning Strategy for Frequency Regulation Gains of Wind Farms Considering Rotor Dynamics Constraints
authors: Bangkun Liao, Yunfeng Wen, Mingrui Sun, Jingwen Wang
abstract: As a prevalent renewable energy source, wind power enhances grid frequency dynamics through the utilization of wind turbine rotor kinetic energy. To effectively enhance the utilization efficiency of rotor kinetic energy in wind farms (WFs), a frequency and power linearization method based on optimal frequency trajectory (OFT) is proposed, and the corresponding differential equation of rotor motion of wind turbine is established. Furthermore, a frequency regulation gain tuning strategy considering rotor dynamics constraints for WFs will be proposed, aiming to ensure stable operation of WFs in the low and medium rotor speed range while maximizing the utilization of rotor kinetic energy of WFs in high and constant rotor speed range. The effectiveness and accuracy of the proposed method will be validated using the modified IEEE 39-bus system.

title: 3D Mapping Using a Lightweight and Low-Power Monocular Camera Embedded inside a Gripper of Limbed Climbing Robots
authors: Taku Okawara, Ryo Nishibe, Mao Kasano, Kentaro Uno, Kazuya Yoshida
abstract: Limbed climbing robots are designed to explore challenging vertical walls, such as the skylights of the Moon and Mars. In such robots, the primary role of a hand-eye camera is to accurately estimate 3D positions of graspable points (i.e., convex terrain surfaces) thanks to its close-up views. While conventional climbing robots often employ RGB-D cameras as hand-eye cameras to facilitate straightforward 3D terrain mapping and graspable point detection, RGB-D cameras are large and consume considerable power. This work presents a 3D terrain mapping system designed for space exploration using limbed climbing robots equipped with a monocular hand-eye camera. Compared to RGB-D cameras, monocular cameras are more lightweight, compact structures, and have lower power consumption. Although monocular SLAM can be used to construct 3D maps, it suffers from scale ambiguity. To address this limitation, we propose a SLAM method that fuses monocular visual constraints with limb forward kinematics. The proposed method jointly estimates time-series gripper poses and the global metric scale of the 3D map based on factor graph optimization. We validate the proposed framework through both physics-based simulations and real-world experiments. The results demonstrate that our framework constructs a metrically scaled 3D terrain map in real-time and enables autonomous grasping of convex terrain surfaces using a monocular handeye camera, without relying on RGB-D cameras. Our method contributes to scalable and energy-efficient perception for future space missions involving limbed climbing robots.

title: Evaluating Fuzzy Logic in Migraine Prediction
authors: K. Rajeswari, Mandip Bhattarai, Ranjeet Nath Chaudhary, Nishant Bhakar, Atharv Bhore
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The accurate identification of migraines in modern times remains difficult because symptoms vary widely between patients along with the absence of definite diagnostic markers which creates hurdles for healthcare professionals to deliver immediate appropriate diagnosis. Standard machine learning systems face limitations in dealing with uncertain data which leads to classifications that are less accurate. Our research uses fuzzy logic together with machine learning methods to enhance migraine diagnosis through more effective treatment of ambiguous symptom information. An evaluation between Neural Networks, Decision Trees, SVM, KNN, and Random Forest with traditional and fuzzy implementation was performed. The evaluation demonstrated that fuzzy models outperform standard models in every aspect while achieving maximum accuracy of 96.25% with the fuzzy neural network. The conducted research shows that fuzzy classification enables better medical diagnostics through a dependable system for migraine prediction. The future development requires using extensive clinical datasets and hybrid modeling approaches in combination with real-time data collection for enhancing hospital applications.

title: PTRD: A Physical-Temporal Real-Time Detector Against False Data Injection Attacks on Wind Turbines
authors: Yitian Zhou, Shiyi Zhao, Ruilong Deng
abstract: The rapid growth of the wind power industry has further solidified its role in modern energy systems. However, the remote location and communication vulnerabilities of wind turbines increase their exposure to cyber threats. Among these, false data injection (FDI) attacks present a significant risk by manipulating system parameters, potentially leading to performance degradation or even system failure. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a physical-temporal real-time detector (PTRD) for identifying FDI attacks on variable-speed wind turbines. By leveraging temporal coherence and inherent physical constraints in the system model, the proposed detector performs consistency checks across consecutive sampling points to detect attack behavior. In contrast to conventional observer-based approaches, PTRD eliminates the need for convergence time, enabling precise and real-time detection. Despite its lightweight design, the detector achieves high accuracy and supports continuous monitoring and timely alerting. Extensive simulations on a 5 MW doubly-fed induction generator-based wind turbine system validate the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed PTRD.

title: Low-Complexity Federated Learning for Decentralized Air Quality Monitoring on 5G/6G Edge
authors: Sahar Ebadinezhad, Eric Domah, Olusegun Emmanuel Adeshina, Comfort M Korlewala, Fitzgerald Emmanuel Bowier
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Air pollution monitoring is essential for public health, yet common centralized machine learning methods have serious issues, such as data privacy risk, excessive communication burden, and single points of failure. In this paper, a low-complexity federated learning (FL)-based framework for decentralized PM2.5 prediction is suggested for optimized 5G/6G edge infrastructure. Using the Beijing Multi-Site Air Quality Dataset, consisting of 420,768 hourly observations at 12 monitoring stations, we write a lightweight LSTM model having 5,921 parameters (0.0226 MB), which is trained on five edge clients around the globe. Our FL approach provides a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of 0.9573, achieving 52.02% bandwidth saving from centralized training (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.9515). Moreover, a 5G/6G edge simulation with high fidelity shows that, compared with 5G (76.92 ms vs. 5.61 ms/round), next-generation 6G networks achieve a communication latency reduction of 92.7%, allowing for real-time low-latency prediction. The generalization of the model is good for heterogeneous (non-IID) data, the result being a per-client R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score that never drops below 0.94. This framework offers a scalable, privacy-preserving solution that is in line with United Nations Sustainable Development and provides a practical map for the smart city environmental intelligence.

title: Creation and Evaluation of a Kannada Digit Speech Dataset using Crowdsourced Voice Samples
authors: Bhargavi K, Mahadevaswamy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: A critical requirement to build precise Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems involves creating excellent datasets. Learning systems require the step-by-step process of recording audio along with transcription work and annotation processes. This paper presents the creation of a Kannada digit speech dataset using a crowdsourced method. Voice recordings of digits 0 to 9 were collected from 50 native Kannada speakers using a mobile ASR app. Each speaker recorded multiple samples, resulting in a total of 5000 high-quality audio files. The dataset was carefully checked for background noise, consistency, and clarity. A Mean Opinion Score (MOS) test was conducted with listeners to assess the quality of the recordings, and the results showed excellent clarity and naturalness. This dataset fills an important gap for Kannada speech recognition research and can be used to train, test, and improve ASR systems. The dataset will be made available to researchers upon request to support future work in regional language technologies.

title: Adaptive Perception Driven Traffic Sign Detection using Dynamic ROI Extraction and Hybrid Shape Intelligence
authors: A. Mary Jenifer, R. Balamanigandan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Detection of traffic signs is important to the autonomous driving system and advanced driver assistance system but the real world condition presents many challenges in the form of different backgrounds, illumination, occlusions, and shapes and color of the signs. In this paper, a hybrid image processing system as well as dynamic ROI extraction is provided to improve the sign detection strength of complex scenes. The system combines color-based segmentation, which makes use of Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM), Canny edge detection, which enhances the edges, and a Hybrid Shape Detection Network (HSD-Net) that accurately analyses shapes. In order to enhance the localization, candidate regions are adaptively scaled by using a Dynamic ROI Scaling and Edge-Preserving Refinement (DRS-EPR) module, without sacrificing structures. The algorithm is tested on a set of more than 12,000 images, and it has high precision, recall, and F1-score and low processing time per frame. The experimental findings prove the effectiveness of the framework in overcoming the challenges of illumination variation, background clutter, and partial occlusions, which is proposed to use a framework in real-time traffic sign recognition and intelligent transportation systems.

title: Design and Implementation of a Containerized Multi-Platform Monitoring Framework Using Open-Source Technologies
authors: Siddhant Jadhav, Harsh Agarwal, Pranav Kumar, Sarin Deore
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: In the contemporary landscape of distributed computing, infrastructure observability has become a critical requirement for maintaining system reliability. Traditional monitoring solutions often suffer from high resource overhead, vendor lockin, and an inability to scale effectively in hybrid environments consisting of both Windows and Linux subsystems. This research proposes a novel, lightweight, and containerized monitoring framework that orchestrates VictoriaMetrics, OpenTelemetry, and Grafana to address these limitations. The system leverages the OpenTelemetry Collector to aggregate metrics from heterogeneous sources, utilizing a push-based architecture that decouples ingestion from storage. Empirical analysis was conducted on a testbed simulating high-cardinality production workloads. The results demonstrate that the proposed architecture achieves a 30% reduction in query latency and a 20.7% improvement in storage compression compared to standard Prometheus deployments. Furthermore, the system exhibited linear scalability up to 15,000 active time series while maintaining a memory footprint under 600 MB. This paper details the complete implementation strategy, configuration logic, and performance benchmarks, offering a reproducible model for cost-effective, enterprise-grade observability.

title: Smoke Quit APP PuffFree QS
authors: Sruthi R, K. Durga Devi, Sreenidhi A, Sowdeswari S, Srihari S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: PuffFree QS is a supportive companion on the journey to quit smoking. It helps users to monitor their progress in a simple, visual way showing how their lungs and overall health improve day by day. Through daily challenges, rewards, and helpful tips, PuffFree QS motivates users to build healthy habits that stick. The app highlights important milestones of the users on the recovery timeline; for example, when a user completes a goal in addition to the suggested one, it is recorded as a milestone to motivate them. When cravings hit, the app provides quick coping tips and a timer to guide through those tough moments. Additionally, the users can connect with friends and share their progress, which enables motivation and social support among them. PuffFree QS helps the users to lead a healthier and smoke- free life. By combining behavioral psychology principles with modern web technologies, Puffree-QS provides an interactive, user-centered solution for smoking cessation, aiming to improve both health outcomes and long-term adherence.

title: Solar PV-Fed Multilevel DC–DC Converter with Generalized TZCM based ZVS for DC Microgrid Applications
authors: S Gomathi, Keerthana M, Nilophine B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The growing need for the efficient integration of renewable energy sources into DC microgrids encounters various challenges, including switching losses, diminished reliability, and unstable power management in response to variable solar irradiance. To tackle these concerns, this study introduces a Solar PV-fed multilevel DC–DC converter that utilizes a generalized Triangular Zero-Current Modulation (TZCM) based Zero-Voltage Switching (ZVS) technique. A Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) controller is employed to guarantee optimal energy harvesting, while the proposed converter reduces switching losses and improve conversion efficiency. The system integrates protection and control strategies designed to uphold stability amid voltage and current fluctuations. A high-voltage DC bus facilitates dependable interconnection with both residential and industrial loads, where as a bidirectional battery bank is utilized to balance storage and supply thus ensuring smooth operation during periods of peak demand or backup scenarios. Simulation outcomes validate enhancements in efficiency, power quality, and operational flexibility. The innovation is characterized by the application of the generalized TZCM based ZVS control in multilevel DC–DC conversion, which promotes highly reliable, loss-reducing, and adaptable DC microgrid applications.

title: Machine Learning based Prediction of Coronary Heart Disease using Clinical Data
authors: Nupur G. Kamdi, Sachin Dagadkar, Chinmaya Khadse
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This investigation outlines a robust machine learning strategy for anticipating coronary heart disease (CHD) early on, leveraging patient clinical and demographic information. The utilized dataset covers essential risk elements like age, gender, chest pain category, resting blood pressure, cholesterol levels, fasting blood sugar, ECG outcomes, peak heart rate, angina triggered by exercise, oldpeak, and ST slope. Post-preprocessing actions such as scaling, categorical conversion, anomaly correction, and attribute prioritization, diverse ML classifiers underwent assessment: Logistic Regression, SVM, Random Forest, and KNN. Evaluation criteria encompassed accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The Random Forest model stood out with an accuracy of 0.95 and AUC of 0.97, excelling in managing intricate non-linear associations. Attribute significance pinpointed chest pain category, oldpeak, ST slope, peak heart rate, and cholesterol as primary indicators. Such outcomes validate the potency of combined machine learning strategies in aiding prompt CHD identification and bolstering medical judgments.

title: Adaptive Residual Learning for Real-Time Hyperspectral Anomaly Detection
authors: Shalini M, Naveen C, Karthick R, Manojkumar P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Hyperspectral anomaly detection is important for finding unusual objects in complex scenes, but it is still challenging due to high spectral dimensionality, varying backgrounds, and the real-time demand of deployment. This article suggests a refined solution based on an existing lightweight residual convolutional neural network that features adaptive multiscale residual learning and live statistical modeling for higher robustness and scalability. The enhanced model produces pseudo-background representations by mitigating the central-pixel bias through context-aware non-central convolution, with the assistance of multiscale residual feature extraction to be able to capture anomalies with different spatial scales. To handle non-stationary background data, the anomaly scoring step is based on a Mahalanobis distance, and an online covariance update method is used to adapt the model without retraining. Experimental results over three standard hyperspectral datasets, Pavia University, Texas Coast (AVIRIS), and San Diego I (AVIRIS), demonstrate performance advantages compared to the baseline model and classical detectors. Methodologies The proposed method achieves AUC scores of 98.61%, 97.08%, and 96.32% in the three datasets, reduces false alarm rates to less than 1.6%, and still guarantees real-time inference speeds of ~34-~35 ms per picture. The results validate the effectiveness of the proposed adaptive adaptations and illustrate the suitability of this framework for real-time resource-constrained remote sensing systems based on hyperspectral anomaly detection.

title: Cognition-Guided Coupled Learning for Camouflaged Object Detection
authors: Hanru Zhou, Dongjun Liu, Gang Xie, Tianyang Qin, Kehan Wang, Wanzeng Kong
abstract: Camouflaged object detection (COD) aims to identify targets that visually blend into their surroundings, posing significant challenges due to low contrast between targets and backgrounds, diverse target appearances, and limited annotated training samples. A critical yet often overlooked subtask in COD is object presence detection, which determines whether a camouflaged object actually exists in an image. This step is essential as it underpins accurate localization and segmentation and enables timely decisions in practical applications such as disaster rescue and wildlife monitoring. However, most existing methods assume the target is always present and focus mainly on localization, limiting their robustness and reliability in complex scenarios. Inspired by the human brain's remarkable ability to perceive camouflaged objects by integrating edge and contextual cues, we propose a cognitive-visual coupling learning framework to enhance object presence detection. Specifically, electroencephalogram (EEG) signals are incorporated during training to embed human cognitive patterns into the model's visual feature learning through coupled representation learning. Notably, during inference, our framework relies solely on visual input, effectively leveraging cognitive insights acquired during training to improve detection performance. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves superior accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods and exhibits strong generalization across diverse visual backbone architectures and unseen cross-dataset scenarios.

title: Federated Aggregation of Polyhedral Demand Flexibility Sets
authors: Yifan Dong, Ge Chen, Junjie Qin
abstract: This paper proposes a federated framework for demand flexibility aggregation to support grid operations. Unlike existing geometric methods that rely on a static, pre-defined base set as the geometric template for aggregation, our framework establishes a true federated process by enabling the collaborative optimization of this base set without requiring the participants to share sensitive data with the aggregator. Specifically, we first formulate the base set optimization problem as a bilevel program. Using optimal solution functions, we then reformulate the bilevel program into a single-level, unconstrained learning task. By exploiting the decomposable structure of the overall gradient, we further design a decentralized gradient-based algorithm to solve this learning task. The entire framework, encompassing base set optimization, aggregation, and disaggregation, operates by design without exchanging raw user data. Numerical results demonstrate that our proposed framework unlocks substantially more flexibility than the approaches with static base sets, thus providing a promising framework for efficient and privacy-enhanced approaches to coordinate demand flexibility at scale.

title: LUT-Based Convolutional Tsetlin Machine Accelerator with Dynamic Clause Scaling for Resources Constrained FPGAs
authors: Rashed Al Amin, Roman Obermaisser
publish: IEEE Journal on Exploratory Solid-State Computational Devices and Circuits
abstract: The rapid growth of machine learning (ML) workloads, particularly in computer vision applications, has significantly increased computational and energy demands in modern electronic systems, motivating the use of hardware accelerators to offload processing from general-purpose processors. Despite advances in computationally efficient ML models, achieving energy-efficient inference on resource-constrained edge devices remains a significant challenge. The Tsetlin Machine (TM) has emerged as an attractive alternative for image classification due to its high throughput and inherently energy-efficient learning paradigm. However, existing TM-based hardware accelerators struggle to balance classification accuracy and energy efficiency, limiting their practical deployment at the edge. This paper presents a resource- and energy-efficient Convolutional Tsetlin Machine (CTM) accelerator with dynamic clause scaling, optimized explicitly for edge Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) platforms. The proposed architecture employs LUT-based pipelining and targeted resource-optimization techniques to minimize FPGA resource utilization while maintaining high energy efficiency and performance. The accelerator is implemented on a Xilinx Zybo-Z20 FPGA and evaluated using the MNIST, Fashion-MNIST (FMNIST), and Kuzushiji-MNIST (KMNIST) datasets, achieving classification accuracies of 97.78%, 85.53%, and 88.54%, respectively, with an energy consumption of up to 0.3 μJ per image classification. Compared with state-of-the-art CTM accelerators, the proposed design achieves up to 40× improvements in resource and energy efficiency, demonstrating its suitability for real-time image and pattern classification on edge FPGA-based systems.

title: Fault Estimation and Isolation for Nonlinear Multi-Agent Systems Based on a Mixed Performance Observer: A Zonotopic Approach
authors: Lulin Zhang, Yi Li, Jiuxiang Dong
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of fault estimation and isolation for nonlinear discrete-time multi-agent systems subject to unknown but bounded disturbances. A fault diagnosis framework is developed by combining an intermediate observer with L2-L∞/H∞ mixed performance and a zonotopic interval estimation approach. Compared with existing observer-based fault estimation methods, the proposed observer does not require the conventional observer matching condition, while two auxiliary variables are introduced to increase the design freedom and improve interval estimation performance. In addition, the proposed mixed-performance index enables a unified treatment of the L2-L∞ and H∞ performance requirements through a tunable parameter. To handle the effects of disturbances and nonlinearities, the Lipschitz nonlinear term is reconstructed and incorporated into the interval estimation framework in a zonotopic form, which reduces the conservatism of the obtained state and fault intervals while maintaining set containment. Based on these interval estimates, an interval-based fault detection and isolation strategy is established to identify the occurrence instants and locations of faults. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Aero-engine Life Prediction Based on ARIMA and LSTM with Multi-Head Attention Mechanism
authors: Shiju Li, Hongjie Lan, Ruizhi Ouyang, Ning Shen, Tingting Wang, Kaiye Gao
abstract: Remaining useful life (RUL) prediction plays a crucial role in prognostics and health management of aero-engines, as it directly supports condition-based maintenance and reliability-oriented decision-making. Although accurate RUL prediction is of great im-portance, existing approaches often struggle to simultaneously capture linear degradation trends and complex nonlinear degradation behaviors under varying operating conditions. To address this issue, this study proposes a hybrid ARIMA–Attention– LSTM framework for aero-engine RUL prediction, in which linear temporal characteristics and nonlinear residual dynamics are modeled in a decomposed manner. Specifically, an ARIMA model is employed to extract linear degradation trends, while an LSTM network with a multi-head attention mechanism is used to learn nonlinear residual dependencies, followed by a fusion strategy to integrate both predictions. The proposed method is evaluated on two public C-MAPSS subsets with different complexity levels and a private industrial dataset collected from in-service aero-engines. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently outperforms conventional statistical methods and representative deep learning models in terms of accuracy, robustness, and stability. The findings indicate that integrating linear trend modeling with attention-based nonlinear learning provides an effective and reliable solution for aero-engine RUL prediction and offers practical value for real-world PHM applications.

title: Analysis and Modeling of Frequency-Domain Compensation Techniques for High Bandwidth Shunt Resistors
authors: Maciej Chojowski, Robert Sosnowski, Marcin Baszyński, Szczepan Milewski
abstract: Bandwidth is a critical performance parameter for current measurement systems using shunt resistors. To accurately reproduce measured currents, especially nonsinusoidal waveforms rich in harmonic content, shunt-based sensors must maintain sufficient bandwidth. This paper presents an analytical frequency-domain model of a current shunt resistor circuit with compensation circuit, including the effects of parasitic elements such as inductance and parasitic capacitance. Based on the derived transimpedance expressions, the influence of parasitics on the frequency response is analyzed and used to identify limitations and requirements for frequency-domain compensation techniques aimed at extending the usable bandwidth of shunt resistors. The approximate transfer function formula and overall bandwidth limit are obtained from the equivalent circuit model. The analytical results are validated experimentally using a surface-mounted metal-film shunt resistor, and the impact of key parasitic components—such as package inductance and parasitic capacitance— on compensation effectiveness and frequency response is discussed.

title: A Wireless Charging Alignment Strategy Under Complex Working Conditions Using Voltage Fingerprints and a Residual-Calibrated Ensemble Network
