abstract: Institutions of higher education are exposed to different types of risks that can be internal, which the institution can directly influence, but also external, which it has no direct influence on. The lack of a systematic and integrated approach to risk management can significantly reduce the effectiveness of the educational process and negatively affect the overall functioning of the institution. The aim of this paper is to present some identified risks in a higher education institution and to contribute to the development of an innovative approach to risk management through the application of a digitized risk database. The proposed approach is based on the use of a risk matrix and enables structured recording, assessment and monitoring of risks in accordance with the principles of the quality management system. The proposed model is a practical and easy-to-apply solution that, with appropriate adaptation, can be applied in other higher education institutions with the aim of improving the entire system of risk management, and thus the entire system of work and operations of the institution.

title: Dynamic Photovoltaic Accommodation Capacity Assessment of Distribution Network Considering the Regulatory of Electric Vehicles
authors: Yuan Zhuang, Nian Liu, Lu Tan
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) power generation can significantly reduce the carbon emission intensity of the power system through zero-carbon emission operation. In order to enhance the ability of distribution networks to accommodate distributed PV, this paper proposes the concept of Dynamic PV Accommodation Capacity (DPVAC) for the first time and introduces a method to enhance DPVAC. Distinguishing from previous studies that mainly focus on the maximum carrying capacity (MHC) at the planning stage, the DPVAC proposed in this paper aims to maximize the dynamic PV consumption of the system at different operating moments. The proposed method for optimizing DPVAC incorporates the regulation of electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage (ES), building upon conventional enhancement approaches. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method in enhancing DPVAC is quantitatively analyzed through three case study scenarios, and the results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively alleviates the midday PV abandonment phenomenon and significantly enhances the system's DPVAC.

title: Classification and Detection of Skin Cancer using YOLOv11 and SHAP AI
authors: R. Kowsalya, S. Ravi, Saranya. S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Traditionally, a dermatologist performs a visual dermoscopic examination to diagnose skin cancer, which is temporally inefficient and potentially subject to human error, especially for early-stage or ambiguous-looking lesions. Incorporating recent advances in deep learning and computer vision, a new hybrid algorithm called Skin Cancer Analysis through Deep Learning and Explainability (SCADLE) is proposed as an advanced object detection method that is accurate and computationally fast. Yolov11 trains the dataset to detect and locate skin lesions in dermoscopic images based on morphological and textural characteristics. Shapley Analysis Explanations (SHAP) are also incorporated into the AI model to boost transparency and trustworthiness. These show how a given image has contributed to a given decision. The model provides 99.7% accuracy and 99.7% precision. The results guarantee that the SHAP implementation is beneficial to the performance of the YOLOv11 detection, but also works toward providing explainable AI to clinical practice.

title: Poultry Disease Classification Using Transfer Learning for Improved Farm Management
authors: Tippireddy Palli Thanusma, Sura Ashwini, Shaik Mastan Vali, Thota Anjali Sri, Ajith Jubilson E
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Poultry farming is an essential marker in the production of food security globally, but it is susceptible to infectious diseases that consistently cause significant economic and animal losses. This study introduces an intelligent signifying-based web-based automated model to recognize disease in poultry based on deep learning and metadata provided by users. The aim of the system is to categorize four conditions, including Salmonella, Newcastle Disease, Coccidiosis, and Healthy based on the images of symptoms such as droppings through transfer learning models, such as VGG16 and MobileNetV2. The application will also include the uses of environmental data and clinical observations to supplement the diagnostic accuracy via a hybrid decision model in addition to imaging input. Compared to the previous works, which require hardware-heavy solutions, such as thermal cameras or web applications, the solution is far less demanding in terms of the deployed devices; the resource-poor environments can easily integrate it through the browser and use it by utilizing unlimited scalability. The practical easily understandable interface enables farmers and field workers to submit images and provide a description of the symptoms and the suggestions are given instantly on the process of treatment and control. The system has addressed the gap between the academic research and implementation of the technology with an approach of lightweight, reliable, and user focused platform. Finally, the method supports the early identification of the disease through early intervention, and enhanced management of poultry health, thus serving as an adequate decision support technique in precision animal farming and animal diagnostics.

title: Multi-Type Energy Storage Capacity Optimization for Power Grids Under High-Proportion Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Zhanxing Xu, Qiang Liu, Na Zhong, Lingjun Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This study optimizes multi-type energy storage capacity in power grids to meet peak-shaving demands of high-proportion renewable energy integration, enhance grid flexibility, and minimize system costs; a dual-objective optimization model (minimizing total economic cost, maximizing renewable energy consumption) with constraints on grid energy balance, energy storage state, and power capacity was built, solved via the robust, global-search Cat Swarm Optimization (CSO) algorithm, and verified by a case study using a province’s 2035 projected data, 8760-hour annual simulation, and NPV-based analysis of 12 pumped/electrochemical energy storage schemes. Key findings show the province’s 2035 energy storage demand is 25,500 MW (21,710 MW gap vs. existing 3,790 MW), with Scheme 1 (10,000 MW new pumped storage, 11,710 MW new electrochemical storage) achieving the lowest total cost (~2.8241 trillion yuan) by reducing coal-fired power costs despite higher energy storage NPV. This research provides a practical grid-side hybrid energy storage optimization framework, where a reasonable pumped/electrochemical storage ratio cuts system costs and guides energy storage planning for regions with high renewable energy integration needs.

title: Towards Self-Adaptive Cloud Security: Deep Learning Models for Privilege Escalation Detection
authors: Sheema Fatima, Rubeena Afsar, Samreen Sultana, Mohammad Pasha, Mohd Umar Farooq, Fahmina Taranum
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: As cloud adoption and clever device usage growth, privilege escalation attacks-specifically those executed by malicious insiders-have emerged as an increasing cybersecurity risk. This study proposes a framework founded on self-adaptive machine learning to recognize and stop such attacks in real period. Four models-Random Forest, AdaBoost, XGBoost, and TabNet-are qualified and assessed using a modified version of the Kaggle Privacy Data dataset. Of them, TabNet outclassed conventional models with the uppermost accuracy of 85%. A soft voting classifier that joint predictions from numerous models were used to additional enhance classification presentation. This method recovers the system's flexibility and aptitude to identify different insider actions, such as attempts at privilege escalation. The system delivers a state-of-the-art (SOTA) solution for defensive cloud environments after insider threats and displays robust, accurate, and scalable performance.

title: Topological Deep Learning for Hierarchical Asset Manifold Embedding in Non-Euclidean Financial Market
authors: Yogesh Kumar Jain, Manish Didwania, Saurabh Sharma, Prachi Beriwala, Mini Srivastava, Samiya Mubeen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This paper had focused on making a new model that can keep both hierarchy and topology of financial graphs which are often very complex. The study had used hyperbolic graph neural network combined with topological regularization so the embedding space was more close to real market like structure. Synthetic graphs had been generated with stochastic block models and stress conditions had been applied to test stability. The result had shown that the proposed framework had preserved persistence diagram structure better and also kept sectors and sub sectors separated clearly in latent space. When compared with normal Euclidean GNN the method had worked more strong even under noise. So this study had give a possible path for future financial network modeling.

title: Dispatch-Embedded Long-Term Tail Risk Assessment and Mitigation via CVaR for Renewable Power Systems
authors: Kai Kang, Feng Liu
abstract: Renewable energy (RE) generation exhibits pronounced seasonality and variability, and neglecting these features can lead to significant underestimation of long-term power system risks in power supply. While long-term dispatch strategies are essential for evaluating and mitigating tail risks, they are often excluded from existing models due to their complexity. This paper proposes a long-term tail risk assessment and mitigation framework for renewable power systems, explicitly embedding dispatch strategies. A representative scenario generation method is designed, combining multi-timescale Copula modeling to capture RE’s long-range variability and correlation. Building on these scenarios, an evolution-based risk assessment model is established, where Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) is employed as a robust metric to quantify tail risks. Finally, a controlled evolution-based risk mitigation scheme is introduced to refine long-term dispatch strategies for mitigating tail risks. Case studies on a modified IEEE-39 bus system incorporating real-world data substantiate the efficacy of the proposed method.

title: Stress Detection Using Physiological Signals with CNN, LSTM and Attention on WESAD Dataset
authors: Sadasivuni Sai Rahul, Mohammad Farukh Hashmi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: In affective computing, mental health evaluation, and workplace well-being tracking, stress recognition is a crucial problem. Wearable technology has made it feasible to continuously track multimodal physiological signals that have a strong correlation with stress reactions. In order to classify stress on the WESAD dataset, we systemically assess deep learning models based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), CNN in addition with Long Short-Term Memory (CNN+LSTM), and CNN+LSTM enhanced with an Attention mechanism. We plan experiments on individual physiological signals (ECG, EDA, EMG, temperature, and respiration) as well as multimodal inputs. According to our findings, CNNs are proved to be effective at capturing local dependencies, but the addition of LSTMs enhances temporal modeling, and the inclusion of attention greatly improves feature selection across time steps. We also provide detailed comparisons of training/inference times, Accuracy, F1-Score, Recall, Precision and confusion matrices. The results achieved show that multimodal fusion produces the best overall performance and that ECG and EDA are the most informative modalities.

title: Short-term power load forecasting model of Power stations based on federated learning
authors: Bin Qu, Wei Gao, Xiaosong Hou, Haoming Ma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Accurate short-term power load forecasting is crucial for the secure operation and efficient scheduling of modern power systems. However, practical forecasting tasks are often challenged by missing data, privacy constraints, and heterogeneous regional load patterns. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Federated Intelligent Repair and Prediction Framework (FedPLF), which integrates intelligent data repair and federated learning into a unified workflow. An improved sparrow search algorithm optimized backpropagation neural network (ISSA-BP) is employed to repair missing and abnormal load data, improving data quality prior to model training. In the federated prediction stage, a dynamic contribution weighting mechanism is introduced to alleviate data imbalance among clients, while a geographic clustering strategy is adopted to capture regional load characteristics. Experimental results on multi-region power station datasets show that ISSA-BP achieves a mean square error of 25.6 kW<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> under a 30% missing rate, and FedPLF reduces the mean absolute percentage error to 8.9% under extreme weather conditions, outperforming the conventional FedAvg method.

title: Transitioning from a Single-Model Baseline to a Multi-Stage Deep Learning Pipeline: A Head-to-Head Evaluation for Tooth Numbering in Intraoral Photographs
authors: Ehsan Shirdel, Saeideh Azizipour, Mary Magdalyanova, Igor Ashurko
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Despite the growing use of deep learning (DL) in dental imaging, no study has compared the relative effectiveness of single-stage and multi-stage DL architectures for World Dental Federation (FDI)-based tooth numbering. Therefore, in this paper, we present a head-to-head benchmarking study comparing a single-stage baseline against a multi-stage pipeline for automated tooth detection and the FDI numbering system. The study was conducted on a dataset of 3,600 intraoral occlusal photographs. The baseline method uses a 28-class You Only Look Once version 8 medium (YOLOv8m) object detector to directly predict per tooth labels from annotated images. On the other hand, the pipeline method leverages a multi-stage process in which each image is first classified by a Residual Neural Network classifier (ResNet-18) to identify the arch as maxillary or mandibular. As the second a YOLOv8m is applied to localize the teeth, where each resulting bounding box is forwarded to the appropriate ResNet-101 classifiers to assign an anatomical class. Finally, a deterministic mapping function is used for integrating the anatomical label, tooth position, and the arch type to generate the corresponding FDI number. Both approaches are trained on identical data splits and evaluated under the same matching protocol and decision thresholds finetuned on a held-out validation set. Evaluation at Intersection over Union (IoU) ≥ 0.5 and confidence ≥ 0.7 resulted in the following: accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score values of 0.871, 0.879, 0.873, and 0.875 for the baseline, and 0.948, 0.950, 0.948, and 0.949 for the pipeline, respectively. McNemar’s test yielded p=0.0002. Beyond empirical benchmarking, our study addresses a fundamental question in the literature, can a streamlined baseline achieve higher accuracy than a staged pipeline, or whether data simplification inherently enhance or compromise performance? The results provide an evidence-based and reproducible template for future dental artificial intelligence (AI) research.

title: Reliability Analysis and Optimal Redundancy Configuration Scheme of All-DC Offshore Wind Power Centralized Booster Station
authors: Anran Hua, Yanbo Che, Mengxiang Zheng, Lei Wang
abstract: Offshore wind farms, as a key component of future renewable energy systems, require reliable power collection and transmission to ensure grid resilience. This paper focuses on the reliability analysis and optimal redundancy configuration of a hybrid centralized booster station based on modular multilevel converters (MMCs). First, the operational principle of a face-to-face hybrid MMC-based DC/DC converter is analyzed to determine the appropriate submodule (SM) mixing ratio that ensures DC fault isolation capability. Second, a hierarchical reliability model of the MMC is developed using fault tree analysis (FTA), addressing redundancy strategies for submodules, control and protection systems, along with valve cooling systems. On this basis, a method for computing the optimal redundancy configuration is proposed, which considers various MMC operating conditions and optimizes the configuration with respect to reliability, economic performance, and power losses. Finally, a case study is conducted to determine the optimal redundancy configuration scheme for the hybrid MMC.

title: Design and Development of a Magnetorheological (MR) Damper for Smart Resistance Training Equipment
authors: Shivam Satpute, Parth Sawant, Sudipti Sonawane, Shreya Navale, Rohan Shinde, Siddhant Kakad, Nitin Satpute
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Magnetorheological fluids are intelligent materials whose rheological characteristics can be changed quickly and reversibly in the presence of a magnetic field. The presented work discusses the design, manufacturing and validation of a magnetorheological damper that is modified to fit in smart resistance training devices. The damper is designed with lowcurrent magnetic circuit and 0.5 mm annular fluid gap in order to attain effective resistance force. Experimental analysis is carried out to scale the resistance force versus coil current. Further damping coefficient is determined by measuring piston velocity for the applied force. The force exhibits approximately linear variation with respect to the coil current. (< 3%). The findings suggest that the proposed damper has the potential to offer electronically controlled, safe and compact solutions to traditional weight stacks and hydraulic systems. This system will overcome limitations and has the potential to implement a programmable and fatigue adaptive resistance in fitness and rehabilitative settings.

title: Voltage and Frequency Analysis of Offshore Wind Farms Following MMC DC-side Fault
authors: Jiaqi Xu, Michael Merlin, Stephen Finney, Asif Khan
abstract: DC short-circuit faults represent a primary challenge to the development and deployment of offshore wind energy using modular multilevel converter (MMC) based high voltage direct current technology. The core difficulty in using alternating current circuit breaker (ACCB) to address such faults lies in maintaining the AC-side voltage and frequency stability after offshore MMC blocking. This paper establishes mathematical expressions for the offshore AC-side frequency and voltage before and after ACCB activation, identifying the related system parameters. The findings are validated by MATLAB/Simulink simulations, showing that the proposed frequency expressions have a prediction error of only 3.5% over 200 ms. Based on the theoretical analysis, the paper demonstrates that the stability of the voltage and frequency can be effectively achieved by tuning the output voltage limiter and current references of the wind turbine’s grids-side converter without altering its operating mode.

title: Enhancing On-Board Orbit Prediction with Machine Learning
authors: Benedikt Aigner, Thomas Andert, Fabian Dallinger, Benjamin Haser, Johannes Bachmann, Francesco Porcelli, Ernesto Rama Novo
abstract: In recent years, the demand for autonomous spacecraft operations has grown significantly, becoming a critical factor across all areas of space engineering. Accurate onboard orbit determination (OD) and orbit prediction (OP) are essential for reliable autonomous mission planning. However, onboard orbit modeling remains challenging due to inherent uncertainties and environmental complexity. This paper presents a hybrid approach combining traditional physics-based OD with a machine learning (ML) method inspired by Peng and Bai [1]. A lightweight Artificial Neural Network (ANN) is implemented for real-time onboard OP error correction. Simulations demonstrate the method’s effectiveness using realistic GPS tracking data generated by a high-fidelity model. Results show significant improvements in OP accuracy, reducing maximum along-track errors from approximately 5 km to about 1.2 km over a three-day OP horizon. The hybrid method is planned to be experimentally validated onboard the ATHENE-1 satellite [2], scheduled for launch in 2026, demonstrating enhanced spacecraft autonomy.

title: A Siamese Network-based Approach for Robust Offline Signature Verification Under Data Security
authors: Dalbir Singh, Tejinder Kaur
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Traditional handcrafted feature-based approaches often fail to generalize well under such constraints. leveraging extensive preprocessing and data augmentation strategies. we created our own dataset having 1000 genuine and 1000 forged signatures by individuals. The findings indicate that Siamese-based metric learning is a promising solution for practical offline signature verification systems operating under constrained data conditions. However, the automatic verification of handwritten signatures, particularly in offline scenarios where only static images are available, still presents significant research challenges.

title: A Machine Learning-based System for Predicting Monogenic Genetic Disorders
authors: Sonia Maria D’Souza, Mohammed Faisal, Kusha Karur, Nazia Zehra, Srihaasa Vinnakota, P. Mohith Eswar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Genetic conditions are medical conditions caused by irregularities in an individual's DNA. It is often inherited from one or both parents. These conditions that are caused by variation in genetic material can be inherited or arise from mutations. Monogenic disorders happen to be significant due to their predictable nature through Mendelian Inheritance patterns. This research demonstrates a Genetic Disorder Prediction System that applies machine learning techniques to predict the presence of such disorders either in a single person or using the genetic characteristics of parents can predict the genetic condition of the child, this system uses a synthetic dataset and uses different classifiers like Decision Trees, Naive Bayes and Random Forest Classifier. Streamlit is used to create a user-friendly user interface. The experimental evaluation demonstrates that Random Forest Classifier categorizes individuals as normal, carrier or affected. When compared to currently existing manual counselling and costly laboratory genetic testing our system provides and cost-effective, accessible and scalable tool. The result highlights the use of machine learning and bioinformatics in early diagnosis.

title: Schema-Agnostic Fraud Detection System Using LLM-Based Schema Alignment and Explainability
authors: Varsha Bhole, Prashant Kudtarkar, Brijesh Maurya, Malhar Nikam
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Detecting financial fraud is hard because it involves data from many different sources, which makes collecting and processing it complicated. Most current systems rely on manually built ETL pipelines that often break and are hard to expand. In this work, we introduce FraudGuard AI, a web-based platform that can handle data without needing a fixed structure. The system uses Google’s Gemini Large Language Model (LLM) to match columns from uploaded CSV files to a standard set of features, so there’s no need for strict ETL rules and the system becomes more flexible. After this, a hybrid rule-based engine spots suspicious transactions. For each case it flags, LLM writes a short explanation to make it easier for analysts to understand. Tests show that this step adds only about 0.85 seconds per schema alignment. This is a small delay compared to how much better the system handles different types of data. Overall, FraudGuard AI provides a practical and transparent way to monitor financial activity automatically.

title: Semantic Segmentation of Dog Hearts Using Vision Transformers for Accurate Vertebral Heart Score Computation
authors: Lokavya PolamReddy, Sravani Kumari K, M. Muthulakshmi, R. Vaisshale, P. Selvaraj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Automating dog heart segmentation helps veterinarians work more accurately and efficiently by saving time and reducing manual errors. It facilitates early detection of heart problems, enables better tracking of treatment progress, and ensures more reliable use of tools like the Vertebral Heart Score (VHS), ultimately improving care and making advanced diagnostics more accessible. The proposed work introduces Segmenter, a transformer- based model for semantic segmentation. The capabilities of the Vision Transformer (ViT) are enhanced by adapting it for semantic segmentation, which supports accurate calculation of VHS for diagnosing heart enlargement. In the proposed model, the sequence of patches is encoded by a transformer encoder and projected through point-wise linear mapping. The decoder employs a mask transformer and skip connections, similar to the U-Net model, to extract intermediate feature maps from the transformer encoder and predict heart segmentation masks. A dedicated VHS measurement module estimates heart size by incorporating an orthogonal layer to ensure precise alignment of the heart’s long and short axes, using an ellipse contour-fitting method. Since manual VHS estimation is inconsistent and lacks precision, this approach combines traditional and deep learning techniques for improved accuracy and reliability

title: Monitoring algorithm of dust movement in valve hall based on improved particle swarm optimization
authors: Zhouyang Tan, Guangze Zhu, Sheng Hua, Jianwu Zhu, Wangzhun Chu, Helong Li, Boming Li, Yisheng Hu, Nan Hong, Qi Gao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address insulation flashover risks caused by dust pollution in HVDC transmission valve halls, this study proposes an online dust monitoring and pollution source localization method based on a mobile monitoring platform and an improved particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm. The system employs drones equipped with miniature dust sensors for adaptive path cruising, real-time spatial concentration data collection, and dynamic dust diffusion modeling. To overcome the positioning accuracy limitations of traditional methods in complex flow fields, an enhanced PSO algorithm is introduced, integrating adaptive inertia weighting, chaotic perturbation, and local gradient search. This approach transforms pollution source parameter inversion into a multidimensional constrained optimization problem, enabling rapid and precise identification of concealed pollution sources. The solution provides an effective technical means for intelligent operation and maintenance of valve halls.

title: Integration Strategy of Sensorless and Current Reconstruction for PMSG in Offshore Wind Power Field
authors: Ruiliang Ren, Qiu Xie, Hao Shi, Hongzhao Wang, Jiwei Peng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: In the offshore wind power field, Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator (PMSG) are extensively used due to their high efficiency and low maintenance needs. However, the sensorless control strategies of PMSG may suffer from performance degradation in system stability and transient response when facing current sensor failures. Research shows that there are mutual coupling effects between the sensorless control strategy and the current fault reconstruction strategy of PMSGs, which can cause system performance degradation. To solve this problem, a feedforward-based strategy is proposed to eliminate the coupling effects caused by rotor position angle deviation and current reconstruction delay. Simulation results indicate that the proposed hybrid algorithm can effectively eliminate low-frequency oscillations and enhance the system's dynamic performance during current sensor faults.

title: Forecasting Urban Cellular Traffic with Higher-Order Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Deepa A, Pravati Swain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The increasing demand for mobile data services has intensified the need for accurate cellular traffic prediction to ensure efficient resource provisioning and quality of service in next-generation networks. This paper presents a deep learning-based approach using Higher-Order Convolutional Neural Networks (HO-CNNs) to forecast cellular traffic patterns by capturing complex spatiotemporal dependencies and higher-order feature interactions. Unlike conventional CNNs that process linear feature relationships, HO-CNNs incorporate multi-dimensional convolutions and advanced feature transformations to learn non-linear correlations across spatial grid cells and temporal intervals. The model is trained and evaluated on the open and publicly available NetMob23 dataset, which offers recent, high-resolution mobile traffic data collected over 2-4 weeks across multiple cities—including app usage statistics, call durations, and data transfer volumes—captured with fine spatial and temporal granularity. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed HO-CNN significantly outperforms standard CNN and LSTM baselines in terms of prediction accuracy, measured by Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), and the Coefficient of Determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). This work highlights the potential of higher-order deep learning models in enabling proactive and intelligent traffic management in contemporary urban cellular networks

title: An Integrated Identification–Analysis Workflow for Ultra-Low Frequency Oscillation Mechanism Elucidation in Power Systems
authors: Tao Zhou, Huawei Xu, Qiang Fu, Siqi Bu
abstract: This paper proposes an integrated identification– analysis workflow to comprehensively address the mechanisms of ultra-low frequency oscillations (ULFOs) in power systems. A dynamic model of multi-area automatic generation control (AGC) systems is established, and a damping torque index (DTI) is derived based on the damping torque transmission mechanism, enabling quantification of damping contributions from AGC and primary frequency regulation (PFR) loops to realize unambiguous identification of oscillation modes without empirical thresholds. On this basis, the workflow then conducts targeted analysis of oscillation characteristics for each mode and in-depth mechanism studies on how key parameters respectively influence the damping and oscillatory behavior of their corresponding modes. Case studies validate the method’s effectiveness in both mode identification and mechanism analysis, which reveal the key AGC and PFR parameters respectively weaken the damping of their oscillatory modes. The proposed integrated identification–analysis workflow provides a theoretical basis for mechanism analysis and suppression strategies in future renewable-dominant grids.

title: PQD Recording Triggered by the Composite Criterion
authors: Yuhao Jia, Lin Zhu, Ruochen Zhang, Yixin Liu, Li Guo, Chengshan Wang
abstract: With the emergence of new power systems, new Power Quality (PQ) problems have also arisen, characterized by strong randomness, rapid changes and shorter durations. To improve the reliability and sensitivity of Power Quality Disturbance (PQD) recording, this paper proposes a PQD recording technology based on a composite criterion trigger. Firstly, wavelet threshold denoising is applied to the collected signals to suppress noise interference. Then, the denoised signals are respectively recorded based on time-domain and frequency-domain criteria triggers. Finally, the recording results of the two criteria are combined to obtain the recording result based on the composite criterion trigger. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can reliably record waveforms of new PQDs.

title: Performance Evaluation of Jakarta EE-Based Applications in Containerized Environments
authors: Miljana Stanković, Miloš Milić, Siniša Vlajić
abstract: This paper introduces software quality concepts related to performance and portability and evaluates the performance of Jakarta EE-based applications. For evaluation purposes, Spring Boot-based and Quarkus-based applications are developed and compared by applying performance metrics. Although the experiment was limited, the results indicate that natively compiled Quarkus-based application achieves better performance compared to Spring Boot-based application. On the other hand, Spring Boot-based application benefits from a more mature ecosystem and stability. The research suggests that framework selection should be guided by the specific requirements of the software system under development.

title: On Inductive Characterization for Divergence-sensitive Probabilistic Branching Bisimilarity
authors: Hao Wu, Yuxi Fu, Huan Long, Xian Xu, Wenbo Zhang
abstract: None

title: Efficient Hardware Realization of Distributed Arithmetic-Based LMS Adaptive Filters Using VLSI Techniques
authors: Akhil Ratnapuram, Krishnaveni B V, RadhaKrishna Karne, Niranjan Reddy Kallem, Pradeep Kumar, Shivaprasad A
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: This work presents an area- and power-efficient VLSI architecture for a least-mean-square (LMS) adaptive filter based on distributed arithmetic (DA). Conventional DA-based LMS adaptive filters rely on look-up tables (LUTs) for both filtering and weight adaptation, but the LUT size increases exponentially with filter order, leading to substantial hardware overhead. To address this limitation, the proposed approach stores offset-binary-coded (OBC) combinations of the input samples and filter coefficients, which significantly reduces the required LUT size. As a result, the architecture avoids the need to decompose a large LUT into multiple smaller LUTs to achieve improvements in area and power efficiency. By retaining a non-decomposed LUT structure, the proposed design achieves notable reductions in both area and power compared with conventional implementations. Moreover, for an equivalent LUT size, the architecture exhibits lower hardware complexity. Synthesis results demonstrate that the proposed LMS filter architecture uses <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6. 6 7 \%}$</tex> fewer LUTs and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9. 0 4 \%}$</tex> fewer flip-flops than the best reported existing design, while achieving reductions of 19.83 % in area and 20.54 % in power consumption.

title: Instance Prompt Meets Dynamic Adapter: Continual Test-Time Adaptation for Urban Scene Images Segmentation
authors: Kuiliang Gao, Anzhu Yu, Xiong You, Yongqi Sun, Yu Su, Yujun Quan, Danyang Hong, Yanfei Zhong
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Recently, to enhance the ability of deployed models to adapt to continually changing environments, the continual test time adaptation (CTTA) for urban scene images (USIs) segmentation has been preliminarily studied. CTTA aims to improve the performance of models while continually adapting to multiple different USIs domains that arrive online in sequence. However, due to the lack of effective solutions for error accumulation and catastrophic forgetting, existing methods fail to achieve satisfactory performance. To this end, this paper proposes a novel CTTA method featuring instance prompts and dynamic adapters. The key innovations include: (1) An instance-wise prompt generator based on the depth anything model is proposed, which can provided more precise prompt information at different levels and effectively alleviate error accumulation. (2) A dynamic adapter module is proposed, where the short-term and long-term adapters learn domain-specific and domain-invariant knowledge respectively, to quickly adapt to new domains while avoiding catastrophic forgetting. (3) An uncertainty estimation strategy with multiple prediction is designed to further optimize the CTTA of USIs. Two typical CTTA scenarios, the continual discrepancies in geographical regions and weather conditions, are established using unmanned aerial vehicle images and street-view images respectively. Extensive experiments indicate that the proposed method outperforms existing methods by a sizeable margin, with an improvement of at least 1.8%∼2.1%.

title: Mapping Present-Day Vertical Land Motion in the Alpine–Apennine System from GNSS Time Series: Uncertainty Reduction Using Hydrological Loading Corrections
authors: Meilin He, Yuanjin Pan, Lv Zhou, Jiashuang Jiao, Tao Chen, Yixin Xiao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Understanding vertical land motion (VLM) in the Alpine-Apennine Orogenic System requires disentangling tectonic drivers from surface mass redistribution. This study presents a multi-geodetic analysis of contemporary VLM in the Alpine-Apennine orogenic system by integrating Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) (2002–2021), Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment and Follow-On (GRACE/GRACE-FO), and hydrological models, covering the time frame from 2002 to 2021. We resolve critical uncertainties through three corrections: (1) Common mode error (CME), reduced by ∼30% in velocity uncertainty via principal component analysis, primarily associated with 2-7 year hydrological interannual signals; (2) Hydrology-induced elastic deformation, contributing an average of -0.3 mm/yr to GNSS-derived VLM, with GRACE/GRACE-FO corrections suppressing uplift rates by 0.3 mm/yr in the Alps; (3) Glacial isostatic adjustment (GIA), exhibiting a southward-decreasing trend (-0.1 to -0.5 mm/yr) from the Alps to Apennines. Beyond a multi-dataset comparison, this study provides a reproducible framework to reduce the uncertainty of GNSS-derived VLM by integrating physically based surface-mass-loading corrections with a consistent noise aware uncertainty assessment. We quantify how much of the apparent VLM scatter can be explained by hydrological loading and identify the residual components that remain dominated by colored noise and non-loading local effects. The VLM imaging reveals coherent regional-scale deformation domains across the Alpine–Apennine system (Alpine uplift, Po Plain subsidence, and alternating uplift/subsidence along the Apennines), enabling a spatially interpretable characterization of contemporary VLM rather than site-by-site reporting. The proposed workflow is transferable to other regions and provides practical guidance on when loading corrections are expected to be most effective and where limitations persist.

title: SSFDiffusion: A Degradation-Aware Diffusion Model for Ocean Sound Speed Field Super-Resolution
authors: Yi Liu, Xinhai Chen, Gen Zhang, Qingyang Zhang, Qinglin Wang, Jie Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ocean sound speed field (SSF) is a fundamental geophysical parameter in underwater acoustics and ocean observation, as it plays a critical role in applications such as sonar imaging, underwater communication, and navigation. However, its low spatial resolution limits the performance of these applications in practical scenarios. Super-resolution (SR) techniques provide an effective means to reconstruct high-resolution (HR) SSFs from low-resolution (LR) observations. Nevertheless, most existing SR methods assume a simple, static degradation process, which overlooks the complex and unknown degradation and noise patterns inherent in real marine environments. This oversimplification often results in limited generalization and suboptimal performance in real-world applications. To overcome this limitation, we propose SSFDiffusion, a degradation-aware diffusion model that explicitly models the coupled effects of global degradation and local noise through three core components: (1) a dynamic denoising network adaptive to varying noise levels, (2) a directional multi-scale Transformer for modeling global degradation and local noise, and (3) an alternating optimization strategy that balances degradation correction and noise suppression to prevent artifact accumulation. Extensive experiments on the South China Sea and GDCSM Argo datasets demonstrate that SSFDiffusion consistently outperforms seven state-of-the-art super-resolution methods across five key quantitative metrics. Notably, our method achieves up to 48.52 dB PSNR and an order-of-magnitude RMSE reduction. These experimental results indicate that SSFDiffusion enhances both the spatial fidelity and practical applicability of reconstructed SSF data, benefiting oceanographic research and underwater acoustic applications.

title: Aggregating DER Uncertainty: Minkowski Sum Under Joint Chance Constraints
authors: Chuyi Li, Kedi Zheng, Pedro P. Vergara, Hongye Guo, Mohammad Shahidehpour, Ning Zhang
abstract: Addressing uncertainty is essential in power systems with high levels of renewable energy penetration. Distributed energy resources (DERs), due to their partially controllable nature, are a major source of uncertainty. However, due to their large numbers and complex correlations, their aggregated uncertainty is highly complex. This paper aims to track how the uncertainty is modeled from individual DER prediction errors to the aggregated-level. By enforcing a specified confidence level, the aggregated-level probabilistic flexibility boundary is formulated as a Minkowski sum under joint chance constraints (JCCs). Despite the inherent intractability of this problem, we establish an equivalent representation that allows for an effective approximation using the proposed quantile cube approximation method. An iterative algorithm is also developed to enhance computational efficiency in implementing the method. Numerical tests demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces the conservativeness of the aggregated confidence boundary and the computation time at the same time.

title: One-Step Image-Function Generation via Consistency Training_supp1-3676205.pdf
authors: Ke Liu
abstract: Diffusion models achieve remarkable image generation quality but suffer from slow inference due to hundreds of denoising steps. Consistency models alleviate this and enable one-step sampling by directly mapping noise to images with a U-Net generator, but they remain unstable and resource-hungry under small-batch training and can only generate images at fixed resolutions. These limitations motivate us to explore more efficient and flexible architectures. To this end, we propose a novel framework for efficient one-step flexible-resolution image generation. First, we represent images as continuous image functions based on implicit neural representations (INRs), which decouple image resolution from the total amount of the generated parameters and can be differentiably rendered at arbitrary resolutions. Then, we design a Transformer-based generator that directly outputs such continuous functions, providing an end-to-end architecture tailored for consistency training and stabilizing training under limited resources. To further improve convergence, we introduce an image reconstruction warm-up task that strengthens the function prediction module. Extensive experiments on one-step image generation demonstrate that our method significantly improves the stability and performance of consistency models under low-resource settings, while also enabling efficient any-resolution sampling.

title: Improving Transferability of Data-Free Universal Adversarial Perturbations via Auxiliary Ensembles
authors: Lifeng Huang, Han Wang, Chen Wan, Zusheng Zhang, Shaojian Qiu, Qiong Huang
abstract: Unlike most adversarial attacks that generate perturbations tailored to specific inputs, Universal Adversarial Perturbations (UAPs) are designed to mislead the model across a wide range of data. This characteristic makes UAPs particularly effective for evaluating the robustness of deep models in the real world. To simulate practical scenarios where the attacker lacks prior knowledge of the data, data-free methods can generate UAPs without requiring any training data from the target model. However, we observe that existing data-free UAP attacks often struggle with severe overfitting dilemmas, resulting in poor transferability and limiting their ability in evaluations for black-box models. To overcome this issue, we propose a novel data-free UAP attack, namely <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>uxiliary <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">E</u>nsemble for transferable <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">U</u>niversal <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>dversarial <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">P</u>erturbation (AE-UAP). Specifically, we prune the forward propagation paths of the existing surrogate model to construct auxiliary models, which are then fine-tuned using artificially generated synthetic datasets. Both the surrogate and auxiliary models undergo multi-layer ensemble optimization by maximizing the feature differences between adversarial and clean samples at the intermediate layers of each model. To encourage the UAP to learn more generalizable low-level features, we prune the attack procedure on the surrogate and auxiliary models, directing the optimization focus toward the shallow layers. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AE-UAP outperforms state-of-the-art UAP methods in both white-box and black-box settings across various models and defense mechanisms, providing a strong benchmark for future research. The codes and models will be released publicly.

title: BASL-IoD:A Blockchain-Assisted Secure and Lightweight Authentication Scheme for Internet of Drone
authors: Hanyu Chen, Dongyan Huang
abstract: To address the challenges of low efficiency, centralization, and weak security in existing Internet of Drone authentication, this article proposes BASL-IoD, a blockchain-assisted lightweight scheme. The proposed system consists of a trusted authority, end users, unmanned aerial vehicles, and a blockchain-based distributed ground control station network. Smart contract is employed to enable dynamic access control. The authentication protocol is built upon bitwise XOR and one-way hash operations, while incorporating fuzzy extractor and physical unclonable function to enhance entity security. The scheme is formally proven secure under the Real-or-Random model, ensuring the semantic security of the session key, and is further validated using the AVISPA tool to confirms its resistance to MITM, replay, and impersonation attacks. Additional informal analysis demonstrates its resilience against various common attacks. BASL-IoD was experimentally tested in a wireless network environment, and its smart contract functionality was further verified through deployment in the Truffle and Ganache framework. Comparative evaluations with other competing schemes show that BASL-IoD provides stronger security, more comprehensive functionality, and significantly reduced computational, communication, and storage overhead.

title: A Parasitic-Tolerated Design of Compact Inverse Class-F GaN HEMT Power Amplifier With Integrated Matching Network
authors: Dang-An Nguyen, Dat Vuong, Danh Cuong Do, Van-Dinh Nguyen
abstract: A novel load structure is proposed for designing an inverse class-F (class F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>) power amplifier (PA) with compact size and high power added efficiency (PAE). The proposed load network is implemented entirely with six transmission lines (TLs) and integrates all of main functions: class F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> harmonic control, biasing, and fundamental output matching in one which typically operate in separate networks in the conventional topologies. The TL parameters of constituent elements are extracted based on derived analytical equations aided by load-pull measurements which tolerate the parasitic effects of a packaged transistor, and then neither extra parasitic compensator nor time-inefficient tuning mechanism is required while both optimal harmonic and fundamental conditions are met. As a result, compact and low insertion loss class <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">F−1</sup> PAis realized.With this design methodology, the implemented PAcan exhibits a state-of-the-art performance at 920 MHz with a measured peak PAE of 85.6%, corresponding to gain of 14.7 dB, and output power of 39.7 dBm, and a miniature size of 0.21λ×0.20λ.

title: Adaptive Cooperative Intra–Inter Domain Adversarial Network With Stage Division for RUL Prediction of Ship Electric Propulsion System
authors: Yuanjie Ren, Lanyong Zhang, Peng Shi
abstract: The remaining useful life (RUL) prediction of ship electric propulsion system faces severe challenges such as multicondition switching and complex degradation modes. Under complex coupling conditions involving significant distribution differences across multisource domains and quantitative requirements for prediction uncertainties, conventional multisource methods struggle to resolve feature shifts caused by coupled condition-mode differences, as they primarily focus on single-aspect solutions. To this end, an RUL prediction method that integrates the entropic Gromov–Wasserstein (EGW) distance and adaptive cooperative intra–inter domain adversarial network (ACIDAN) is proposed in this article. First, a divergence measurement model for the EGW distance distribution is constructed, and the first predicted time is accurately identified through adaptive parameter optimization. Then, the ACIDAN is designed. Intradomain adversarial training is employed to eliminate feature-distribution shifts under multiple working conditions. The outputs of multisource regressors are coordinated through an interdomain kernel-density-estimation weighting strategy to ensure consistency. A multisource predictive variance method for the residual connected regressor is introduced in the degraded perception stage to generate confidence intervals containing uncertainty quantification. Finally, the RUL prediction accuracy and uncertainty quantification of the algorithm proposed in this article are verified.

title: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
authors: N/A

title: ICAIQSA 2025 Preface

title: A Simple Analytical Design Approach for Small-Frequency-Ratio Dual-Band Rat-Race Couplers
authors: Brij Kumar Bharti, Jagannath Malik
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a simple analytical approach for designing small-frequency-ratio dual-band rat-race couplers (RRCs) without using stubs, complex designs, or multilayer structures. The approach utilizes the odd-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pi $</tex-math> </inline-formula> phase condition at the mid/null frequency, which forces a transmission null and creates the coupler response into two distinct passbands. Using this principle, two dual-band RRCs are developed for frequency ratios of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{2}/f_{1} =1.5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and 1.27, both centered at a mid/null frequency of 5 GHz. Closed-form line-length equations are proposed to determine the electrical lengths required for multiple small ratios, and the transmission-line impedance is tuned in each design to ensure the equal power division. The designs are analyzed theoretically, then simulated, and finally validated through fabricated prototypes. The measured results confirm good agreement with theory and simulations.

title: Exploiting GANs Against IDSs: A Systematic Review, Meta-Analysis, and Case Study Evaluation
authors: Yasmeen Alslman, Mouhammd Alkasassbeh, Mohammad J. Abdel-Rahman
abstract:
With the rapid improvement of generative adversarial networks (GANs) in the landscape of data augmentation and generation, researchers have started to explore the use of GANs in other fields, such as adversarial attack generation. Using GANs has improved the field of adversarial attacks, particularly against intrusion detection systems (IDSs). GAN-based adversarial attacks are considered sophisticated attacks that can significantly compromise the security of IDSs. Addressing GAN-based adversarial challenges can ensure the effectiveness and severity of such attacks, which, in turn, lead to the development of strong defense strategies. Few surveys covered the effects and challenges of adversarial attacks against IDSs, focusing on gradient-based adversarial attacks. Recently, many research papers have examined GAN-based adversarial attacks.

This paper provides a systematic and comprehensive review of recent research on GAN-based adversarial attacks targeting IDSs, complemented by a meta-analysis guided by critical review questions. Through case studies, we empirically demonstrate the vulnerabilities of binary and multi-class IDS models under GAN-based attacks, examining the influence of feature selection. We are the first to employ advanced GAN variants to craft novel adversarial attacks. Using Fréchet inception distance and Hellinger distance, we assess IDS performance and the realism of generated samples, revealing key system weaknesses. The paper concludes by summarizing the current state of knowledge, outlining major challenges, proposing future research directions, and identifying the most severe GAN architecture and the one that generates the most realistic adversarial samples.

title: A Two-Stage Data-Driven Topology Identification in Three-Phase Distribution Networks
authors: Wenjie Xiong, Zhiyuan Tang, Hongjun Gao, Youbo Liu, Ao Qiao, Junyong Liu
abstract: Topology identification lays out the essential foundation for the operation monitoring and management of distribution networks. In this article, a novel two-stage data-driven topology identification approach is proposed for unbalanced three-phase distribution networks utilizing the measurements of smart meters. In the first stage, the phase sequence of each bus is recovered sequentially using the proposed phase identification method, where the similarity criteria are employed to reduce the influence of line impedance on voltage correlation. In the second stage, based on the phase identification results, the buses with relatively low active power injections are grouped into different clusters. By regarding each cluster as an aggregated node, the simplified system topology and the local topology of each cluster (i.e., each aggregated node) are identified sequentially using the ridge regression method. The full-scale topology of system is obtained by integrating the simplified system topology and the local topology of each cluster. Moreover, to further improve the identification efficiency, a novel optimal input design approach is proposed to select rich-information data from historical records. Various case studies are conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed topology identification approach.

title: Research on Failure Determination of Switching Devices Coupling Indicators Based on Vibration Parameters and ESNIM
authors: Yankai Li, Shuxin Liu, Chaojian Xing
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: The degradation process of switching devices with contact structures is characterized by diversification and multi-attributes, which makes it difficult to determine the real state of the switching devices through a single indicator, but the existing methods neglect the definition and determination process of the coupling indicators, so this paper combines the advantages of vibration signal acquisition with low cost and convenient practical installation, proposes a method for failure determination of switching devices coupling indicators based on vibration parameters and Echo State Network Integration Model (ESNIM). Firstly, failure indicators and vibration signal datasets under different coupling states are constructed using CJX2-5011 switching devices as experimental objects. Then, 26-dimensional vibration parameters are extracted from the switching device vibration signals, and strongly correlated vibration parameters for the determination of each indicator are constructed using the Spearman correlation coefficient. Finally, the ESNIM switching device coupling indicator failure determination model is designed to achieve high-precision detection of switching device states. Compared to other methods, the ESNIM model's average evaluation metrics are higher by at least 0.006, 0.002, 0.011, and 0.006, respectively, effectively illustrating the superiority of the ESNIM model in the process of coupling indicator failure determination. It provides a new technical solution for prognostics health management (PHM) of switching devices with contact structures.

title: Robust Generalized Partial-to-Full Point Set Registration with Overlap-Guided Bidirectional Hybrid Mixture Models for Computer-Assisted Orthopedic Surgery
authors: Xinzhe Du, Zhengyan Zhang, Ang Zhang, Rui Song, Yibin Li, Max Q.-H. Meng, Zhe Min
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In computer-assisted orthopedic surgery (CAOS), robust and accurate registration of the preoperative full bone model and the intraoperative partial point set is a prerequisite for reliable surgical navigation, yet remains highly challenging due to partial overlap, noise, and outliers. We propose the Overlap-Guided Bidirectional Hybrid Mixture Registration (OBHMR) framework for robust and accurate partial-to-full registration. First, geometric features (i.e., surface normals) extracted from raw point sets are incorporated in both correspondence estimation and transform computation. Meanwhile, we formulate a hybrid mixture model that jointly represents positions with Gaussian mixtures (GMMs) and normals with von Mises–Fisher (vMF) mixtures across the two generalized point sets. Second, a dual-branch overlap prediction network leverages feature similarity and geometric structure to provide accurate point-wise overlap scores that guide hybrid-mixture construction under partial overlap. Third, a correspondence module integrates rotation-invariant features, multi-level self-attention, and clustering-based refinement to enhance reliability under noise and misalignment. Finally, a bidirectional objective jointly aligns source-to-target and target-to-source mixtures, explicitly accounting for discrepancies induced by noise and outliers in both the preoperative and intraoperative point sets to achieve robust optimization. Extensive experiments on 1,399 femur and 1,301 hip models demonstrate superior performance over state-of-the-art methods across overlap ratios from 5% to 70%, under both isotropic and anisotropic noise and outlier ratios up to 100%, achieving errors as low as 1.27° rotation and 1.18 mm translation at 50% overlap with 2.5mm noise. Additional tests on liver and ModelNet40 confirm strong generalization across medical and non-medical data. Ablation studies further validate the contributions of normals, overlap estimation, and the bidirectional formulation.

title: Adaptive Token Merging for Efficient Transformer Semantic Communication at the Edge
authors: Omar Erak, Omar Alhussein, Hatem Abou-Zeid, Mehdi Bennis, Sami Muhaidat
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Large-scale transformers are central to modern semantic communication, yet their high computational and communication costs hinder deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. This paper introduces a training-free framework for adaptive token merging, a novel mechanism that compresses transformer representations at runtime by selectively merging semantically redundant tokens under perlayer similarity thresholds. Unlike prior fixed-ratio reduction, our approach couples merging directly to input redundancy, enabling data-dependent adaptation that balances efficiency and task relevance without retraining. We cast the discovery of merging strategies as a multi-objective optimization problem and leverage Bayesian optimization to approximate the Pareto front trade-offs between accuracy, inference cost, and communication cost. On ImageNet classification, we match the accuracy of the unmodified transformer with 30% fewer floating-point operations per second and under 20% of the original communication cost, while for visual question answering our method achieves performance competitive with the full LLaVA model at less than one-third of the compute and one-tenth of the bandwidth. Finally, we show that our adaptive merging is robust across varying channel conditions and provides inherent privacy benefits, substantially degrading the efficacy of model inversion attacks. Our framework provides a practical and versatile solution for deploying powerful transformer models in resource-limited edge intelligence scenarios.

title: Hierarchical Federated Learning in Device-to-Device Networks with Learning-Topology Co-Optimization_supp1-3673393.pdf
authors: Mengfan Wu
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training across distributed devices while preserving privacy. However, growing heterogeneity in device resources and communication links challenges conventional FL, especially when relying on a single central server. Hierarchical federated learning (HFL) mitigates these issues by organizing devices into clusters coordinated through intermediate aggregators. Yet, the effectiveness of HFL critically depends on how clusters are formed: intra-cluster communication must be efficient, device computational capacities should be balanced to reduce stragglers, and data heterogeneity must be managed to ensure stable convergence. In this work, we propose a learning-topology co-optimization framework for HFL in networks where nodes communicate with each other with links of varying quality (e.g., device-to-device (D2D) or mesh networks). Our method jointly optimizes device connection topology and learning directions, leading to communication-efficient clusters that remain well aligned in optimization space. We provide a convergence analysis under mild assumptions, showing how inter- and intra-cluster divergence affect learning stability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach consistently improves HFL performance, yielding at least a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6 \%$</tex-math></inline-formula> accuracy gain under unbalanced data distributions and over <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> reduction in training time for regression tasks compared with existing clustering algorithms.

title: Benchmarking the Time Cost of Adversarial Attacks on GANs
authors: Khushi Sayani, Het Gohil, Hussain Degani, Hezal Lopes
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are now a foundation of contemporary generative AI, driving everything from art to synthetic data generation. Yet, their susceptibility to adversarial attacks, as well as the rapidity with which these attacks can compromise them, is still a vital area of research. Although extensive research has been conducted on the success of attacks, there has not been a great deal of emphasis on the time it takes for an attack to be effective, a very important consideration in practical environments where prompt detection and prevention are absolutely essential. This paper introduces a benchmarking platform intended to quantify the time expense of GAN adversarial attacks. By considering three predominant attack categories, latent-space perturbations, training-time poisoning (backdoors), and membership inference, we not only consider their efficacy but also whether they can attack a model quickly. Our results indicate a speed vs. impact trade-off: the Latent Perturbation attack is quick to perform but infects individual outputs, whereas the time poisoning attack, take longer to seed but can infect models entirely or spill training data and finally Membership Inference attacks are quick to perform and can reveal if an individual’s data was used in a model’s training set. This benchmark seeks to offer a down-to-earth, time-savvy perspective for researchers and practitioners alike to more effectively evaluate GAN vulnerabilities and design timely defensive measures.

title: Knowledge Graph for Assessing Flooding Risks to Electricity Distribution Substations
authors: Simeng Li, Xinyuan He, Chenghong Gu
abstract: Flooding poses increasing risks to power system infrastructures due to climate change, with distribution substations being particularly vulnerable. This paper presents a knowledge graph-based framework for assessing flood risks to distribution substations in England, United Kingdom. Knowledge graph system is capable of managing complex data and supporting efficient decision-making. The proposed approach incorporates substation attributes relevant to flood exposure together with multiple floods forecast scenarios. It leverages Quantum Geographic Information Systems (QGIS) for spatial analysis and Neo4j for graph database management. The framework generates two main outputs: i) a potential flood risk for each substation and ii) an impact on substations under the assumption of flooding. In addition, targeted hardening and emergency response measures based on risk and impact analysis are also provided, offering a practical tool to improve resilience planning in power distribution networks.

title: Smart-Edge RF Harvesting and Reconfigurable Antenna Array for Sustainable IoT Communication
authors: Vijaya Raghavan N, Rajganesh A, G.Sugathi, Jothilakshmi U, R.Thiagarajan, R.Krishnamoorthy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The viral increase in the Internet of Things (IoT) has caused previously unimaginable difficulties with ensuring the sustainability of the energy supply of sensor nodes particularly within remote and energy-limited conditions. This paper describes a new Smart-Edge RF Harvesting and Reconfigurable Antenna Array system that is proposed to provide energy-independent and context-aware IoT communications. The proposed system combines a wideband RF energy harvester circuit that has been optimized using ADS, a reconfigurable MEMS-based antenna array that is simulated in HFSS, and a low-power microcontroller platform that includes TinyML-based situational adaptation intelligence. This system is able to capture ambient RF energy at frequencies between 900 MHz, 2.4 GHz and 5.8 GHz with a highest power conversion efficiency of 78.3% at +5 dBm input. The antenna module showed accurate steering of the beam with the maximum directional gain of 7.9 dBi and the mean return loss of -14 dB. Transmission protocols that are energy-aware adjust the duty cycle and power level to real-time residual energy and can achieve more than 95% packet delivery even at low energy thresholds. The inference of TinyML was able to attain an environmental RF zone classification with 96.4% accuracy and a latency of sub-15 ms. Comparative benchmarking shows that there are great performance gains in the energy sustainability, communication range and smart adaptability against the conventional systems. The integrated solution is a new move towards self-sustaining, smart IoT infrastructure, which is applicable in smart agriculture, city-wide surveillance, and disaster management. RF energy harvesting, an adaptive antenna system, and edge AI, combine to provide a powerful platform of next-generation IoT deployments in the power-scarce environments.

title: Research on Dynamic Regulation and Control Methods of Energy-Saving Speed Control Technology for Pumping Units During High Water Cut Period
authors: Zhi Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: The pumping unit system in oil wells during the high water cut period faces technical challenges of severe load fluctuations and persistently high energy consumption. To address this problem, an intelligent speed control strategy based on multi-parameter collaborative optimization is proposed. The key novelty lies in the integration of fractional-order sliding mode surface design with adaptive fuzzy inference mechanisms, enabling simultaneous optimization of energy consumption, stability, and vibration control through a weighted multi-objective function with dynamically adjusted parameters. By establishing a dynamic model of the pumping unit system and analyzing the time-varying load characteristics under high water cut conditions, an adaptive fuzzy sliding mode controller is designed to achieve real-time load identification and dynamic speed matching. Field tests show that this method reduces system energy consumption by 23.7%, improves motor operating efficiency by 18.3%, and ensures stability of pumping parameters, providing a new technical approach for energy saving and efficiency improvement in oil fields during the high water cut period.

title: An Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Breast Cancer Diagnosis using GAN-based Augmentation
authors: T. A. Karthikeyan, V. Arun Antony, N. Renuka, R. Jayadhanush, A. Jesinthwilson, T. Dileep
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Patient survival depends on the early and precise detection of breast cancer, but the clinical applicability of deep learning models is frequently constrained by the notable class imbalance in medical image datasets. Given the rarity of malignant cases in breast ultrasound imaging, this issue is especially noticeable. In order to rectify this imbalance in the publicly available Breast Ultrasound Images (BUSI) dataset, this study suggests a deep learning framework. We present a novel hybrid augmentation approach, where standard geometric augmentations are applied to the ’normal’ class and a Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Network (DCGAN) is used to generate high-quality, synthetic images specifically for the underrepresented ‘malignant’ class. This focused method avoids the computational burden of producing every class while producing a balanced dataset. This rebalanced dataset is then used to fine-tune a pre-trained EfficientNet-B7 model for three-class classification (benign, normal, and malignant). We incorporate Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) to give visual explanations for model predictions in order to promote clinical trust. For the critical malignant class, our framework achieves a strong F1-score of 0.89 and a classification accuracy of 84.8%. Interestingly, it lowers the False Negative Rate to 12.1%, which is medically acceptable. This study offers a solid, comprehensible, and practical method for creating computer-aided breast cancer diagnostic systems.

title: Voltage Control Based on Load Partitioning and Virtual Active Power Injection in Low-Voltage Distribution Network
authors: Benjian Jiang, Yinchao Wang, Xin Liu, Wen Gu, Zhipeng Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address the voltage violation problem in low-voltage distribution networks caused by large-scale residential photovoltaic integration, a photovoltaic inverter grid-connection control strategy based on load partitioning is proposed, with the reactive power control of inverters as the core means. Based on load data, the K-means++ clustering algorithm is used to analyze the load data, and subsequently, the loads are partitioned into different zones according to their characteristics. Since the active power fluctuations within the same load zone are basically consistent, the difficulty of control is thus reduced. By introducing the concept of virtual active power, the load reactive power is converted into active power that reflects its impact on voltage. Thus, the two-dimensional variables affecting the voltage increment can be transformed into a one-dimensional variable, reducing the control complexity. Subsequently, the reactive power that the inverters should inject is derived based on the virtual active power to implement voltage control.

title: Multi-Disease AI-Bridging Diagnostics, Doctors, and Donors
authors: Nirmala M, Chandrashekar R, Nalagala Suala Charan Kumar, Narsepalli Adarsh, Rajesh R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: More than 2 billion people of the world are suffering from diseases because the affordable and reliable healthcare facilities are unavailable . This is very challenging and especially for elderly people as well as low-income people. Although there are digital platforms available like disease detection systems, they do not have the doctor verification, telemedicine, and direct fundraising. This paper introduces Multi-Disease AI (a unique three-component platform). It tackles these issues in the simplest way. It has a patient login, whereby a chatbot (which is integrated to Gemini API) can chat with them and provide preventive measures as well as telemedicine. Doctors who offer free service can log in to the portal of doctors and check the AI- developed medicines to prevent the mis-medication and cause harm to the patients. What makes it different from other platforms is the open source platform for fundraising that it provides. It gives donors the option of donating money to the targeted patients in need without the intermediaries and thus develops a culture of trust and accountability. This paper merges the AI-augmented diagnostic process with key management by medical experts with a direct funding mechanism. Multi-Disease AI shall offer a unique, integrated solution that not only makes healthcare accessible and efficient but also offers a secure and open support system for patients. It was tested on 500 anonymized patient cases where the AI diagnosis module had a Precision of 0.94, Recall of 0.91, and F1- Score of 0.92 with the Gemini API inference engine. Human-in- the-loop verification of AI-generated recommendations demonstrated the need of human verification, which classified about 8% of the recommendations as being incorrect.

title: LunaPolaris: A stereo camera, point cloud and IMU dataset for future lunar exploration in polar regions
authors: Dave van der Meer, Uland Wong, Miguel Olivares-Mendez
abstract: Space missions shift their focus to the lunar surface, for which they require autonomous robots that work in extreme lighting conditions. Most existing datasets used for developing advanced Simultaneous Localisation And Mapping (SLAM) algorithms focus on urban environments like office buildings or cities. Only a limited number of datasets show deserted landscapes without vegetation, and they are usually captured during daylight. Simulations are frequently used in the absence of real or analogue data, but they have fidelity limitations compared to complex planetary environments. The LunaPolaris dataset addresses this issue by providing twelve sequences recorded in a lunar analogue facility using a small rover with a stereo camera, a solid-state LiDAR, and an IMU. These datasets can be used for validating the robustness of visual SLAM or Light Detecting And Ranging (LiDAR)-SLAM in challenging, lunar-like environments, highlighting the challenges around the extreme lighting conditions on the Moon. In this paper, we assess current gaps in existing datasets, present an overview of LunaPolaris, elaborate on the environment and technical details of how the data collection was conducted, and provide initial validation results using stereo and LiDAR SLAM.

title: A Transformer-Enhanced Neural Network for Multimodal Feature Fusion in AC Fault Line Selection
authors: Yuming Zhao, Mingquan Xu, Junyao Gao, Jingbing Zhao, Lijun Sun, Jiangtao Zhu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Electrical fire incidents caused by arc faults in low-voltage distribution systems have been increasing annually, posing serious threats to life and property in recent years. With the widespread adoption of smart household appliances, the diversity of system load types and the complexity of line topologies present significant challenges for arc-fault detection and feeder selection. Therefore, rapidly and accurately detecting arc faults in complex scenarios and promptly identifying the faulted circuit are crucial for the safe and stable operation of the system. This study addresses the difficulty of feeder selection under complex line topologies by extracting various time–frequency features of current as one-dimensional feeder-selection features, and simultaneously generating voltage–arc effect current trajectory images as two-dimensional features. These are combined to construct a multi-modal fused feature set, which is then used to train a multi-modal fusion neural network for arc-fault feeder selection. This approach enables the identification of load types directly connected in series with the arc fault, thereby determining the faulted circuit.
