The results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a feeder-selection accuracy of 96.4% under four different multi-load operating scenarios, effectively realizing arc-fault localization and providing new insights for both theoretical research and engineering applications in AC arc-fault detection and feeder selection.

title: Two-layer Reactive Power Optimization Scheme for Power Grids Considering Reactive Potential of Wind Cluster
authors: Xiping Ma, Wenxi Zhen, Chen Liang, Xiaoyang Dong, Yaxin Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Given the increasing need for reactive power challenges due to the grid integration of large-scale wind clusters, a two-layer optimization scheme is developed in this paper. The upper-level model aims to minimize active power losses and voltage deviations during grid operation, thereby enhancing grid efficiency and security. At the lower level, the reactive power potential of the wind cluster is evaluated. The optimization objective focuses on utilizing the available wind farm’s reactive power potential by coordinating the outputs of individual wind turbine units within the cluster. A hybrid multi-objective algorithm integrating particle swarm optimization and the whale optimization scheme is introduced to solve this optimization model. Simulation results confirm that the strategy and algorithm not only substantially reduce system losses and voltage deviations but also make effective use of the reactive power potential of wind power generation.

title: Automating P-Card Reconciliation with UiPath and Microsoft Power Automate: A Transformational Approach to Streamlining Financial Operations
authors: Pullaiah Babu Alla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Modern business operations handle more financial transactions while facing stricter regulatory standards and higher performance demands. The process of P-card reconciliation stands as a vital organizational function that validates all company expenses for accuracy and compliance. The process of manual reconciliation requires excessive time and produces multiple errors while needing substantial resources. The paper introduces a combined intelligent automation system that uses UiPath Robotic Process Automation (RPA) together with Microsoft Power Automate and AI-based intelligent document processing for P-card reconciliation. The proposed system performs complete P-card reconciliation through automated steps, which start with transaction data extraction, followed by receipt acquisition and classification and GL code assignment and exception management, and audit record maintenance. The system architecture unites rule-based bots with machine learning (ML) for adaptive decision-making to achieve both operational efficiency and system resilience. The experimental testing shows that the system achieves better results in terms of processing speed and accuracy, and compliance preparedness. The paper demonstrates results while discussing practical applications and assessment methods, and potential development paths for automated financial operations.

title: Semi-Automatic Solar Panels Cleaning Rover
authors: Bhoomi Adke, Yuvraj Bhambare, Purva Atale, Atharva Kadu, Ansh Rathod, Nitin Borse
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Solar panel efficiency can drop drastically due to dust accumulation, especially in regions with dry climates or high pollution levels. This decreases not only limits power output but also leads to long-term energy and financial loss. Our project introduces a remote- controlled cleaning system that ensures consistent energy production by removing surface dust regularly and effectively. The system helps automates daily cleaning, maintaining optimal panel performance with minimal human involvement. By offering a low-cost, practical solution, this technology supports more sustainable and reliable solar power generation over time.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classroom Distraction Detection and Monitoring System
authors: Abhijith P, Nitish R, Nithin S, Ganesan M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The pervasive use of smartphones in classrooms increasingly undermines student concentration and disrupts the learning environments. This paper presents an intelligent monitoring system leveraging advanced computer vision to perform real-time facial recognition for attendance and to detect behaviors such as use of an unauthorized device and inattention of student. Upon detection, the system delivers immediate alerts with visual evidence to educators. Designed to operate securely within institutional networks and upholds robust privacy standards, this solution exemplifies thoughtful integration of technology to mitigate digital distraction and foster more effective, focused learning. A custom YOLO model achieved mAP@0.5 of 0.943 and a peak F1 of 0.89, with strong precision and recall for "read" and "sleep" actions. Despite minor confusion between "write" and background, the results show high robustness and reliability in real-world scenarios.

title: Stability Analysis and Suppression Strategy of Grid-Forming MMC-HVDC Systems with Dual-Loop Vector Voltage and Current Control
authors: Peiwen Yao, Jianyu Zhou, Shilin Gao, Yuhong Wang
abstract: This paper focuses on the stability issue of grid-forming (GFM) MMC-HVDC system with dual-loop vector voltage and current control. First, a single-input-single-output small signal model of GFM MMC-HVDC systems is developed. By theoretical analysis, the equivalence of two common inner control structures, i.e., the dual-loop vector voltage and current control and virtual resistance control, in the low-frequency range is revealed. Therefore, they possess similar subsynchronous oscillation characteristics. Furthermore, since the inner loop of dual-loop vector voltage and current control can counteract the arm impedance of MMC, it is more sensitive to changes in the grid strength. A virtual inductance based control strategy is proposed to suppress the subsynchronous oscillations. Finally, the validity of the stability analysis and the proposed suppression strategy are verified through simulations in MATLAB/Simulink.

title: Quantitative Synthesis of Large Language Model Performance in Medical Reasoning Tasks
authors: Pavankumar D. Paikrao, Meenal Wagh, Sayali Chavan, Sukumar Chougule, Rasika Vishal Wattamwar, Farhina Anjum Sayyad, Daulappa Bhalke, Chaitali Deshpande
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of AI diagnostic models across diverse clinical cases sourced from Thieme (77 cases) and Elsevier (48 cases). The dataset spans frequent (42 cases), less frequent (44 cases), and rare (39 cases) conditions, ensuring balanced assessment. Diagnostic performance, treatment recommendation accuracy, and linguistic reliability were compared across multiple state-of-the-art AI systems. Results highlight strong overall diagnostic capabilities, with notable variations across specialties and disease frequencies. While Pediatrics consistently demonstrated the highest performance, Surgery emerged as the most challenging specialty. Among models, GPT-4o achieved superior diagnostic consensus, treatment recommendation accuracy, and linguistic precision, underscoring its clinical utility. The findings provide empirical benchmarks for advancing AI-based medical decision support systems.

title: Histology-Aware Texture Encoding with Multi-Scale Attention for Uvular SCC and Dysplasia Classification
authors: Padma Perala, Venkatesan M, Prabhavathy P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: In order to enhance the treatment outcomes and curb morbidity, the earliest identification of squamous cell carcinoma (SCC) and dysplasia in the uvula is crucial. But, the diagnosis is difficult because of the variability of the stains, the similarity between benign, precancerous and malignant lesions in uvular tissues and the rarity of uvular lesions. Here, we suggest HistUvuNet, a hybrid deep learning architecture, which combines Histology-Aware Texture Encoding (HTE) with a Multi-Scale Attention Fusion (MAF) mechanism that is capable of classifying uvular SCC and dysplasia. HTE module balances the change in the stain and strengthen the texture patterns induced by epithelial area, whereas the MAF block combines CNN-induced nuclear features with global context representations, obtained through a Transformer. A design that is designed with a segmentation method employs a predicted epithelial mask to gates the transformer attention. The tests on OCDC database reveal high performance particularly on differentiating between dysplasia and SCC which offers a new and clinically useful method of screening uvular cancer.

title: A Raspberry Pi-based Intelligent System for Detecting Vitamin Deficiencies using Image Analysis and Lightweight Deep Learning Models
authors: S. Kaviya, M. Kirthika, S. Senthilkumar, P. Arunkumar, R. Ramanan, M. Kavitha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Vitamin deficiencies contribute to millions of preventable health complications worldwide, ranging from vision loss and neurological dysfunction to reduced immunity and skeletal weakness. In low-resource communities, laboratory testing is often inaccessible or costly, resulting in undiagnosed and untreated deficiencies. Many deficiencies, however, leave identifiable visual traces on anatomical regions such as the eyes, tongue, nails, and skin. This research presents a fully offline, low-cost, and portable diagnostic device built using Raspberry Pi, Pi Camera, TensorFlow Lite deep learning, and fuzzy logic reasoning. The system takes pictures of certain parts of the body, processes them, and then uses a MobileNet-based model that is optimized for embedded inference to sort them. Fuzzy logic refines borderline predictions for improved diagnostic stability. The device displays deficiency results, provides nutritional recommendations, and generates structured PDF reports. Comprehensive evaluation demonstrates that the proposed system is an effective preliminary screening tool suitable for rural health screening, portable clinics, and educational institutions.

title: A Deep Neural Network Based Filtered BiFPN object Detector using Scale Integrated Channel Attention
authors: J.Thilagavathy, S.Ebenezer Juliet, D. Kesavaraja
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Multi-scale object detection is one of the most algorithmically complex and computationally demanding problems in intelligent machine vision. Typical object detection techniques address memory optimization but compromise model accuracy and performance gain in terms of model complexity. Most of the existing methods provide complex architecture with a greater number of edges in convolutional neural network (CNN), which increases computation cost and fails to extract significant features effectively. To overcome this complexity, it has been proposed several key optimization techniques that improves the accuracy for the detector called as Scale Integrated Attention and Filtered BiFPN (SICA-FBiFPN) framework. First, it has been proved that improvement in accuracy has been done by replacing the existing Feature pyramid network (FPN) with a Filtered Bi-directional Feature Pyramid Network (FBiFPN) technique. FBiFPN effectively generates multi-scale feature with reduced number of edges in FPN. Secondly, the performance gain has been improved using proposed Scale Integrated Channel Attention (SICA) module. It also extracts significant features effectively and improves accuracy of detector of multiple spatial scales. It has been experimented on the MS COCO and PASCAL VOC that our proposed method outperforms the current state-of-the-art research. The performance metrics such as accuracy and parameters were evaluated. The experimental results on SICA-FBiFPN model achieves 76.7% mAP@0.5 Intersection over Union, 58.8 mAP on the MS COCO dataset. It achieves 85.5% mAP on Pascal VOC dataset.

title: Improving Elbow Movements in Spinal Cord Injury Patients using Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) Through Deep Reinforcement Learning Control
authors: Prudhvi Panuganti, Vimala Kumari Jonnalagadda, Pavani Alla, Varaprasad Rao Bommidi, Abu Saleem Mohammad
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Assisting individuals with spinal cord injuries (SCI) in regaining functional elbow movement is a major difficulty in neurorehabilitation. Basic daily functions including clothing, feeding, and self-care are greatly impacted by upper limb motor deficits, particularly those involving the elbow. Two examples of conventional rehabilitation methods that typically lack adaptability and necessitate manual stimulation parameter adjustment are repetitive physical therapy and traditional Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES). This can result in muscle fatigue, ineffective contractions, and poor functional outcomes. This paper provides an intelligent rehabilitation system that combines FES and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to address these issues. The suggested system functions in a closed-loop manner, dynamically modifying stimulation parameters including pulse amplitude, width, and frequency based on continuous feedback from sensor-driven evaluations of elbow joint motion and muscle reaction. A comparison research used dataset-based evaluations and experimental simulations to evaluate DRL's performance with Q-Learning and Support Vector Machine (SVM) controllers. The findings show that DRL consistently performed better than traditional techniques over a range of target angles (30°, 60°, 90°, and 120°), leading to smoother joint trajectories, higher accuracy, lower error, and improved fatigue management. The results demonstrate that by improving rehabilitation's effectiveness, customization, and adaptability, the DRL-FES framework improves SCI patients' functional recovery and independence. This work shows how combining FES with AI-driven adaptive control might enhance quality of life and result in the creation of next-generation neurorehabilitation systems.

title: Exploring Asymmetrical Relationships in Predicting Postpartum Depression Using Theil's-U
authors: N Banupriya, T Sethukarasi, A Jasmine Gilda, Kalaiselvi S, Jayabharathi S, Navdha S P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Postpartum depression (PPD) affects many women and can have long-term impacts on mothers and infants. Early diagnosis and intervention require a better understanding of risk factors. Cramér's-V and Theil's-U measures are used to quantitatively examine and display asymmetry correlations among PPD-related psychological and behavioral components. There were modest to moderate relationships, with "feeling anxious" and "feeling guilty" having the strongest asymmetry. Logistic Regression was used to verify these dependencies' predictive power. This study shows that Theil's-U can reveal directional and non-linear connections among PPD risk factors, providing a more interpretable and data-driven foundation for maternal mental health predictive modeling.

title: Semi-Automated Brain Tumor Segmentation Using Thresholding and Morphological Processing with Real-Time OpenCV GUI Control
authors: Surajit Das, Priyadarshini Chatterjee, Ausali Harini, P. Kruthi Hamsika, Sargam Harini Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: It is important to be able to accurately separate brain tumors from MRI images in order to make a diagnosis, plan therapy, and keep an eye on the condition. However, manual segmentation requires a significant amount of time and effort, and distinct individuals may obtain different results. Deep learning techniques may be quite powerful, but they frequently need enormous annotated datasets and a lot of computing power and are difficult to understand. In this study, we suggest a semi-automated segmentation framework that combines traditional image processing methods with an OpenCV-based graphical interface that enables users to engage with the system in real time. The suggested technique employs global intensity thresholding, followed by morphological procedures, to create more accurate binary masks. A user-adjustable threshold slider enables dynamic control and visual feedback during segmentation. To identify the tumor area, the greatest connected contour is taken out. This is then utilized to make a tumor mask. The method also allows us to estimate the area of the tumor in physical units (mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) using pixel spacing information and saves the results in a CSV file with the image name, threshold value, and tumor area. We use conventional measures, such as the dice coefficient, IoU, precision, and recall, to compare the segmented masks to the ground truth annotations. The proposed framework works well in tests, with excellent segmentation accuracy, is open and adaptable, and doesn’t need a lot of resources to execute. This makes it a useful tool for research, annotation, and preliminary healthcare evaluations, particularly in resource-limited settings.

title: A Wearable Maternal Monitoring System for Fetal Kick Detection and Abdominal Temperature Tracking Using Accelerometer and Thermal Sensor
authors: Banupriya N, S Garvitha, Sangeetha Sri S, Ramya S, Sannareddy Ramakeeerthi, Ramireddy Nandu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This paper outlines the design and development of a wearable system for maternal health monitoring system, enabling continuous tracking of fetal movements and abdominal temperature. The system employs an MPU6050 accelerometer for fetal kick detection and a DS18B20 digital temperature sensor for skin temperature monitoring. An ESP32 microcontroller is utilized for sensor data acquisition and wireless transmission to a web dashboard through Wifi . The wearable setup is developed to be lightweight, battery-powered, and comfortable for routine use throughout pregnancy. The system aims to offer an accessible, affordable tool for early detection of fetal distress and maternal fever conditions.

title: RICEVISION: An AI-Enabled Visual Intelligence-Driven Classification for Rice Varieties using Deep Learning and Computer Vision Approach
authors: Sharra Mae B. Fernandez
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The Philippines, home to a diverse array of rice types, produces a significant amount of rice—one of the world's most extensively cultivated cereal crops—particularly in Asia. The purpose of this research was to develop and evaluate an AI system that could use image analysis to detect and categorize different types of rice grown in the Philippines. The system extracts features using computer vision. It recognizes patterns and classifies them using deep learning methods, particularly CNN and SVM. The researcher narrowed the 2,400 photos to 600 each of jasmine rice, glutinous rice, black rice, and red rice. Testing and training sets were created from the same datasets. The CNN achieved 93.74% accuracy, whereas the SVM achieved 87.54%. As part of the digital revolution in Philippine agriculture, this project seeks to improve the efficiency, reliability, and scalability of agricultural quality evaluation by using artificial intelligence to identify different types of rice.

title: Real-Time Sky Image-Based Solar Power Forecasting for Ramp Rate Control in Solar Farms
authors: L.S.V. Jayathissa, S.U. Dewasurendra, G.L.D. Anjana, W.D.A.S. Rodrigo
abstract: Fast-moving clouds present the challenge of intermittency in solar power integration, which leads to abrupt ramp events that can cause grid instability. This paper outlines a real-time forecasting and ramp control system that uses deep learning and computer vision techniques. The project was conducted at the 10 MW Vydexa Solar Power Plant in Vavuniya, Sri Lanka, where a 4K fisheye camera was installed to capture sky images at 10-second intervals. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks are employed to forecast cloud movement, while a hybrid LSTM model using features such as estimated irradiance, cloud cover, sun brightness, and solar angle predicts short-term solar power output. Forecasted power output is controlled by rule-based ramp control algorithms, implemented with and without a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS), to minimize losses and prevent future power ramp violations, enabling more stable solar integration.

title: Influence of Electric Vehicle's Participation in Power Grid Peak Regulation on Battery Health
authors: Haoteng Li, Shuaibang Liu, Aina Tian, Xiaoguang Yang, Jiuchun Jiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: With the increasingly serious problems of energy shortage and environmental pollution, people pay more and more attention to the development of new energy vehicles, energy storage power stations, power grids and other fields. Due to their excellent portability, high energy density, long service life and low self-discharge rate, lithium-ion batteries have become an important choice for ensuring energy security in various fields. Due to their energy storage function and strong mobility, electric vehicles can be used for peak shaving and valley filling in the current power grid. The health status of lithium batteries is very important for judging the aging of lithium batteries, and the health status of lithium-ion batteries of electric vehicles will also change in the process of participating in power grid interconnection. Therefore, accurately and quickly assessing their health condition has important practical significance. In this paper, a peak-shaving model based on Monte Carlo is established to demonstrate the feasibility of electric vehicles participating in the peak-shaving of power grid. 18650 lithium battery is selected as the research object, and a direct measurement method, namely aging cycle experiment, is used to analyze the aging index of lithium battery to get the real data, and then SOH estimation method based on fusion model is used to predict. Finally, the experimental results and errors are analyzed to draw the corresponding conclusions.

title: Adaptive Hyper-Heuristic Svm Optimization for Scalable Intrusion Detection in Big Data
authors: Alisha Qizra Khan, I.Samuel Peter James, Samreen Sultana, Mohammad Pasha, Mohd Umar Farooq, Safia Khanam
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Cybersecurity in the era of big data poses significant challenges, particularly in intrusion detection, where identifying malicious activities amidst vast and complex data is critical. This work presents a bi-objective hyper-heuristic approach for optimizing Support Vector Machine (SVM) hyperparameters, balancing two conflicting objectives: maximizing accuracy and minimizing model complexity. Our proposed method incorporates a high-level strategy and diverse low-level heuristics to effectively explore the SVM configuration space, enabling adaptive selection of heuristics based on search performance. We validate our framework using the NSL-KDD dataset, a well-regarded benchmark for network intrusion detection, achieving approximately 87% accuracy alongside balanced precision and recall metrics. The results demonstrate the potential of our hyper-heuristic SVM framework to enhance intrusion detection performance on big data while maintaining manageable model complexity, offering a promising direction for scalable and effective cybersecurity solutions.

title: Enhancing Federated Learning Efficiency Through Minimal-Cluster Kubernetes Orchestration
authors: D. Nandhini, M. Brindha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) protects data privacy while facilitating cooperative model training among dispersed clients. However, convergence and accuracy may be hampered by lowering the number of participating clients, as required in small-scale or resource-constrained deployments. In order to maintain FL efficacy with just 19 clients on the CIFAR-10 dataset, this study examines the use of FedProx, an optimization technique intended for heterogeneous environments, in conjunction with Kubernetes Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA). This method matches test accuracy while lowering communication rounds by 40% and total communication volume by 45% when compared to a baseline FedAvg setup with 30 clients. The findings show that moderate cluster reduction is possible without sacrificing performance when algorithmic regularization and adaptive resource scaling are combined.

title: Hybrid LLM-Vision Pipeline for Detection of Malicious QR Codes in Social Engineering Attacks
authors: Konda Hari Krishna, Patnam Ravi Kumar, V Herah, Ponnolu Vishnu Vardhan Reddy, Kopparthi Jwalapati Suhas Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Quick Response (QR) codes work well as a convenient information sharing option but are being more commonly used in social engineering attacks to provide phishing links, malware, and other malicious payloads. Hybrid LLM Vision Pipeline This paper suggests a hybrid model that combines computer vision to detect structural anomalies and LLMs to detect semantic threats. The visualization module utilizes convolutional neural networks to detect the violation, absence and abnormalities in the QR code picture and the LLM checks the deciphered message about suspicious URL structure, domain names, and phrases denoting intent to action. A fusion layer network is a multimodal network that jointly infers the two modalities and improves accuracy and reliability. According to experiments, its accuracy in detecting objects with 98.4% wins over single-modality baselines and is also robust to adversarial distortion. The proposed framework offers a scalable and flexible defense against the attacks exploiting the QR codes, it is part of the expansive defense against the AI-motivated attack on societal engineering.

title: A GAN-Augmented Multi-Scale Feature Fusion Framework for Explainable Crop Disease Detection
authors: Gurram Sunitha, Basi Reddy.A, Gopisetty Pooja Harshitha, Manda Likitha, Kotte Usha Rani, Muddigari Dinesh Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The identification of crop diseases plays a crucial role in safeguarding agricultural productivity and ensuring global food security by enabling timely intervention and minimizing yield losses. Early diagnosis mitigates economic repercussions, optimizes pesticide application, and promotes sustainable agricultural practices. Automated detection systems enhance effective crop management and facilitate informed decision-making through increased accuracy and scalability. This research introduces a GAN-augmented multi-scale feature fusion network as an innovative methodology for crop disease detection and interpretability. To address class imbalance and improve generalization in real-world agricultural contexts, we employ generative adversarial networks (GANs) to enrich training datasets with diverse, high-quality synthetic crop images. The proposed framework utilizes a deep convolutional architecture with multi-scale feature fusion to extract hierarchical disease characteristics from leaf images, thereby enhancing recognition of subtle symptomatology across varying conditions. Our solution achieves cutting-edge detection accuracy (99.95% validation accuracy, AUC = 1.0), robust performance with limited data, and enhanced explainability through SHAP and LIME techniques. Comprehensive experiments conducted on publicly available crop disease datasets demonstrate superior performance compared to traditional methods including VGG16, ResNet50, and conventional augmentation approaches. This research advances precision agriculture and supports informed strategies for crop disease management by offering a scalable, interpretable deep learning instrument.

title: A Deep Learning based Medical Image Analysis Framework for AIDS/HIV Prevention and Clinical Decision Support
authors: Chala Mitafa, Tiruveedula Gopi Krishna, N.Sateesh Kumar, S.Zahoor Ul Huq, Teklu Urgessa, Mesfin Dagne
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: In the current research investigation, we created and deployed advanced chatbots for HIV/AIDS communication in Afaan Oromo. We constructed a comprehensive dataset by curating question-and-answer pairs national HIV prevention guidelines and healthcare organization websites, were formatted as JSON. After thorough preparation, we used the word2vec word embedding method for feature extraction in model training. Our design incorporated Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), We achieved impressive accuracy rates of 92.11%, 93.8%, 95.27%, and 94.4% respectively. To reduce overfitting during model training, stratified k-fold cross-validation was employed. Human evaluation showed an average performance score of 80.5% and user acceptance of 84.12%, indicating effective system performance and strong acceptance for addressing HIV/AIDS queries in Afaan Oromo.

title: Research on the Prediction of Separation and Purification Performance of Differential Pressure Thermally Coupled Distillation Process Based on LSTM-DQN Hybrid Model
authors: Fang He
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: The differential pressure thermally coupled distillation (DPDiC) process is widely used in the field of chemical separation due to its low energy consumption advantage. Its separation and purification effects are affected by the dynamic coupling of multiple parameters such as operating pressure, reflux ratio, and feed flow rate. Traditional mechanism models have the problems of complex parameter fitting in the separation process and insufficient generalization ability of single deep learning models for different separation systems. To improve the prediction accuracy, a prediction model integrating the long short - term memory network (LSTM) and the deep Q-network (DQN) is proposed. This model uses LSTM to capture the time-series correlation and dynamic change characteristics of process parameters, and utilizes DQN to optimize the feature weight distribution through reinforcement learning, thereby enhancing the adaptability to complex working conditions. The Aspen Dynamics software is used to conduct dynamic simulation of the DPDiC process for the methanol-water system, and the large amount of data generated is used as samples to compare and verify the hybrid model with the single deep learning model LSTM. The results show that the mean absolute error (MAE) of the proposed LSTM-DQN model is 0.0371, and the root mean square error (RMSE) is 0.0504, which are 57.40% and 47.39% lower than those of the single LSTM model respectively. The coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) is as high as 0.9377. This model can accurately capture the dynamic laws of the process, providing a theoretical basis for purification performance prediction and parameter optimization. It also offers reliable technical support for real-time monitoring and optimized control of the purification performance of the DPDiC process in the industrial chemistry field, maximization of the energy-saving potential of thermal coupling, and guarantee of operational safety.

title: Adaptive Speech Transcription for Medical Environments using Deep Neural Networks
authors: P Sushma, Bhawna Khokher, Subhash K M, Veerendra Kumar G S, Somesh, Madhu M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: In today’s healthcare environment, accurate and efficient transcription of medical speech plays a vital role in ensuring quality patient care, improving communication, and reducing administrative burdens on clinicians. Manual transcription processes are not only time-consuming but also prone to errors, especially in busy hospital settings where speed and precision are critical. This paper introduces a deep learningdriven solution designed specifically for biomedical speech transcription. Our system combines the capabilities of OpenAI’s Whisper model for speech recognition with SciSpacy’s biomedical named entity recognition (NER) and multilingual translation models from Hugging Face, creating a comprehensive and versatile pipeline. A user-friendly interface built using Gradio allows healthcare professionals to interact with the system easily. Users can provide live speech input, view transcriptions in real time, review detected medical terms, and select target languages for translation. The system’s performance has been assessed using standard metrics, including word error rate (WER) for transcription accuracy and precision, recall, and F1-score for NER. The results demonstrate the system’s ability to produce reliable and meaningful outputs that could assist in clinical documentation, patient communication, and healthcare analytics. The modular design also means that components can be upgraded or expanded as technology advances. This work illustrates how deep learning and natural language processing can come together to simplify medical transcription and improve the efficiency of healthcare delivery in multilingual environments.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Student-Centric Course Recommendations
authors: Sumit Gupta, Prikshat Kumar Angra, Shashi Kant Gupta, Pritpal Singh, Ashwani Kumar, Neeraj Mathur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Choosing the elective courses is one of the most crucial and complex problems for a student as it can affect his/her academic performance, career path and skill development. Conventional course selection approaches may not provide personalization which can result in sub-ideal decisions. The proposed study seeks to design a student-centered elective course recommender system, which is hybrid in nature leveraging collaborative filtering and content-based filtering for accurate and personalized course recommendations. Recommendations are personalized for individual students using their academic record, preferences and the attributes of courses. Machine learning techniques (i.e. clustering, classification) are embedded to improve prediction accuracy and overcome single-method shortcomings. In addition, the performance of the proposed model is evaluated by some standard metrics (i.e., Precision, Recall and F1-score), illustrating that our model can enhance recommendation relevance and student satisfaction. The hybrid methodology provides a scalable and flexible solution that seeks to recommend electives for students according to the interest, ability, and future direction of the student.

title: Overcoming Barriers to Health Informatics Adoption: A Systematic Literature Review of Resistance and Integration in Telemedicine
authors: Loso Judijanto, Sri Nurhayati
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The rapid expansion of telemedicine has highlighted both the potential and the persistent challenges of health informatics adoption. Despite significant global investments in digital health, implementation remains uneven, constrained by technical limitations, human resistance, organizational barriers, and fragmented regulatory frameworks. This study was conducted to systematically identify and synthesize the key barriers and integration strategies in telemedicine, aiming to provide a comprehensive understanding that supports sustainable adoption. A qualitative research design was employed through a Systematic Literature Review (SLR), guided by the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) protocol. Data were collected exclusively from peer-reviewed articles indexed in Scopus, beginning with 4,365 records identified through an initial keyword search. After sequential screening based on thematic relevance, publication year (2020–2025), and accessibility criteria, 43 articles met the inclusion requirements and were analyzed. Data analysis involved thematic synthesis to extract dominant patterns, recurring barriers, and effective integration strategies across diverse healthcare contexts. The results reveal five overarching themes: technical and infrastructural limitations, human and professional resistance, organizational and financial constraints, policy and legal challenges, and integrative strategies for overcoming adoption barriers. The synthesis further indicates that upstream technical and regulatory constraints often intensify organizational costs and financial risk, which in turn amplify professional and patient resistance. Evidence indicates that successful telemedicine adoption requires coordinated interventions that combine infrastructure investment, professional and patient training, supportive policies, and financial incentives. In conclusion, telemedicine adoption is shaped not only by technological readiness but also by social, economic, and regulatory dynamics.

title: Parameter Identification Method of Grid-Forming Wind Turbine for Wideband Oscillation Analysis
authors: Wendi Jiang, Yinhan Zhang, Wenjian Chen, Shuang Feng, Jiaxing Lei
abstract: Grid-forming wind turbines enhance power system stability through virtual synchronous generator control, which mitigates the risks associated with high penetration of renewable energy integration. However, wind power grid connections frequently trigger wideband oscillations, threatening grid safety. Therefore, it is necessary to establish an equivalent wind turbine model suitable for wideband oscillation analysis. However, the unknown and dynamically changing parameters of wind turbines make model establishment challenging. To address this, this paper proposes a combined model-based and data-driven parameter identification method to enhance accuracy and efficiency while ensuring applicability in wideband oscillation analysis. Firstly, under the virtual synchronous generator strategy, mathematical models for each module of the grid side converter are presented. Then, a structured sparse regression algorithm is employed for parameter identification. Finally, time domain simulations and impedance scans are conducted to verify the accuracy of the grid-forming wind turbine parameter identification and its applicability in wideband oscillations.

title: LightAttention-UNet: A Lightweight Model for Efficient Brain Tumor Segmentation on the BraTS 2024 Dataset
authors: Tirth H Vijekar, Mohammad Farukh Hashmi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Multimodal magnetic resonance imaging (MRI)-based automated brain tumour segmentation has been a major research topic and is essential to the advancement of neurooncology. The BraTS 2024 dataset, a sizable, professionally annotated collection of multimodal MRI scans, is made publicly available in this paper to help with this task. To create and evaluate a lightweight model that produces better segmentation results with a substantially lower computational cost, even though many state-of-the-art systems rely on computationally demanding structures. An overview of the BraTS 2024 dataset and the common evaluation metrics applied to this issue are given in this paper.The methodological approach is next presented, with an emphasis on striking a cutting-edge balance between model efficiency and prediction accuracy. The possible implications of the results are then examined for both future research and actual clinical practice.

title: ForensicFaceGen: AI-Powered Suspect Sketch Generation Using Stable Diffusion for Criminal Investigations
authors: Rahul Suryawanshi, Ayush Jadhao, Bhuvneshwar Ghate, Dhananjay Parbat, Kanishq Birole, Omprakash Yadav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This paper demonstrates the entire process to construct forensic face composites from witness statements through advanced AI and image processing techniques. The system begins with text input from witnesses. This input is subjected to natural language processing (NLP) for extracting primary features. It achieves this by disassembling words to determine their base forms and comparing similar words. The system then converts the processed data into an organized prompt. It improves this question more with a large language model(LLM) to ensure that it's precise and comprehensive.The improved prompt guides an AI image generation tool (like Stable Diffusion or GANs) to make an initial face composite. This picture gets better through techniques to boost resolution and cut down noise making it clearer. A user screen lets people give feedback and make changes over and over so the result matches what the witness said. Law enforcement checks the final face before it goes into a safe database (PostgreSQL or Firebase) and is put on cloud platforms (AWS or Google Cloud) for approved users to access.This method speeds up the old forensic sketch process. It uses AI to be more accurate work faster, and grow , while still letting humans step in to make it better. The system is built in parts, so it can change as NLP and AI that makes new things get better in the future.

title: See Where You Read: Reading Tracking via Eye Gaze Tracking and Large Language Model
authors: Sikai Yang, Gang Yan, Wan Du
publish: ACM Transactions on Internet of Things
abstract: None

title: Centralized Control for Paralleled VSCs with Grid-forming and Oscillation Suppression Capability
authors: Yangjian Ling, Meng Huang, Pan Feng, Zhen Tian, Yushuang Liu, Xiaoming Zha
abstract: Paralleled multiple voltage source converters (VSCs) are adopted in photovoltaic (PV) or energy storage systems, and may suffer from oscillation and fault disturbance issues under various grid conditions. In this paper, a centralized control frame is proposed to suppress the active power oscillation and support the grid voltage/frequency via grid-forming (GFM) control. A virtual synchronous generator (VSG) control is applied as the centralized controller, which communicates with local controllers. The mutual damping and power angle feedback are adopted in the local controller to achieve an accurate active power sharing. The influences of key parameters such as the communication delay and mutual damping are analyzed, Finally, the proposed control is verified through the simulation and hierarchical controller-hardware-in-loop (CHIL) experiments.

title: Prioritizing Gradient Sign Over Modulus: An Importance-Aware Framework for Wireless Federated Learning
authors: Yiyang Yue, Jiacheng Yao, Wei Xu, Zhaohui Yang, George K. Karagiannidis, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Wireless federated learning (FL) facilitates collaborative training of artificial intelligence (AI) models to support ubiquitous intelligent applications at the wireless edge. However, the inherent constraints of limited wireless resources inevitably lead to unreliable communication, which poses a significant challenge to wireless FL. To overcome this challenge, we propose Sign-Prioritized FL (SP-FL), a novel framework that improves wireless FL by prioritizing the transmission of important gradient information through uneven resource allocation. Specifically, recognizing the importance of descent direction in model updating, we transmit gradient signs in individual packets and allow their reuse for gradient descent if the remaining gradient modulus cannot be correctly recovered. To further improve the reliability of transmission of important information, we formulate a hierarchical resource allocation problem based on the importance disparity at both the packet and device levels, optimizing bandwidth allocation across multiple devices and power allocation between sign and modulus packets. To make the problem tractable, the one-step convergence behavior of SP-FL, which characterizes data importance at both levels in an explicit form, is analyzed. We then propose an alternating optimization algorithm to solve this problem using the Newton-Raphson method and successive convex approximation (SCA). Simulation results confirm the superiority of SP-FL, especially in resource-constrained scenarios, demonstrating up to 9.96% higher testing accuracy on the CIFAR-10 dataset compared to existing methods.

title: Boosting Spectral Efficiency via Spatial Path Index Modulation in RIS-Aided mMIMO
authors: Ahmet M. Elbir, Abdulkadir Celik, Asmaa Abdallah, Ahmed M. Eltawil
abstract: Next generation wireless networks focus on improving spectral efficiency (SE) while reducing power consumption and hardware cost. Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) offer a viable solution to meet these requirements. In order to enhance the SE, index modulation (IM) has been regarded as one of the enabling technologies via the transmission of additional information bits over the transmission media such as subcarriers, antennas and spatial paths. In this work, we explore the usage of spatial paths and introduce spatial path IM (SPIM) for RIS-aided massive multiple-input multiple-output (mMIMO) systems. Thus, the proposed framework improves the network efficiency and the coverage with the use of RIS while SPIM provides SE improvement. In order to perform SPIM, we exploit the spatial diversity of the millimeter wave channel and assign the index bits to the spatial patterns of the channel between the base station and the users through RIS. We introduce a low complexity approach for the design of hybrid beamformers, which are constructed by the steering vectors corresponding to the selected spatial path indices for SPIM-mMIMO. Furthermore, we conduct a theoretical analysis on the SE of the proposed SPIM approach, and derive the SE relationship between the SPIM-based hybrid beamforming and fully digital (FD) beamforming. Via numerical simulations, we validate our theoretical results and show that the proposed SPIM approach presents an improved SE performance, even higher than that of the use of FD beamformers while using a few RF chains.

title: Diffractive Neural Network-Based Intelligent Multiband Metasurface Antennas for Gain-Bandwidth Synthesis
authors: Hongjun Chu, Dandan Zhang, Hancong Sun, Wenzhi Li, Zhaohui Song
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The growing demand for high-capacity wireless connectivity in industrial systems has driven the adoption of millimeter-wave and multi-standard networks, necessitating intelligent multiband antennas for adaptive spectrum use. However, conventional designs rely on expert-driven optimization, limiting agility and scalability. Here, we present a physics-informed, deep learning-enabled framework for automated antenna synthesis using diffractive neural networks (DNNs). By employing cascaded metasurfaces composed of Huygens unit-cells to emulate multilayer neural networks, this framework enables parametrical mapping of learned weights to spatial amplitude and phase modulation of electromagnetic waves, allowing the automated generation of customized antenna gain-bandwidth profiles across operating bands without manual tuning. For demonstration, we design three DNN-based antennas that establish single gain band with a peak at 60 GHz, double gain bands with peaks at 50 GHz and 70 GHz, and triple gain bands with peaks at 50 GHz, 60 GHz, and 70 GHz, respectively. The out-of-band rejection is larger than 20dB. Measurement results demonstrate the gain-bandwidth shaping capability in good agreement with simulations. This work offers a pathway for intelligent antenna design in industrial wireless networks.

title: Contact Plan Design for Cross-Linked GNSSs: An ILP Approach for Extended Applications
authors: Huan Yan, Juan A. Fraire, Ziqi Yang, Kanglian Zhao, Wenfeng Li, Xiyun Hou, Haohan Li, Yuxuan Miao, Jinjun Zheng, Chengbin Kang, Huichao Zhou, Xinuo Chang, Lu Wang, Linshan Xue
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSSs) employ inter-satellite links (ISLs) to reduce dependency on ground stations (GSs), enabling precise ranging and communication across satellites. Beyond their traditional role, ISLs can support extended applications, including providing navigation and communication services to external entities. However, designing effective contact plan design (CPD) schemes for these multifaceted ISLs, operating under a polling time-division duplex framework, remains a critical challenge. Existing CPD approaches focus solely on meeting GNSS satellites' internal ranging and communication demands, neglecting their extended applications. This paper introduces a CPD scheme capable of supporting extended GNSS ISLs. By modeling GNSS requirements and designing a tailored service process, our approach ensures the allocation of essential resources for internal operations while accommodating external user demands. Based on the BeiDou constellation, simulation results demonstrate the proposed scheme's efficacy in maintaining core GNSS functionality while providing extended ISLs on a best-effort basis. Additionally, the results highlight the significant impact of GNSS ISLs in enhancing orbit determination (OD) and clock synchronization for the Earth-Moon libration point (LP) constellation, underscoring the importance of extended GNSS ISL applications.

title: Hybrid Model Predictive Control with Adaptive Modulation for Voltage Balancing in Three-Phase Three-Level NPC Rectifiers for Green Hydrogen Production
authors: Tayebeh Faghihi, Pavol Bauer, Hani Vahedi
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid model predictive control (HMPC) strategy for a three-level neutral-point-clamped (3L NPC) rectifier to optimize the operation of electrolyzers for green hydrogen production. Unlike conventional approaches that focus primarily on inverter applications, the proposed method directly addresses the critical issue of neutral-point voltage imbalance in NPC rectifiers. The control framework combines discrete switching-state selection with adaptive objective prioritization, utilizing real-time capacitor voltage measurements to exploit switching-state redundancy. A multi-objective cost function balances current tracking, DC-link voltage regulation, neutral-point voltage balancing, and switching-loss minimization. An adaptive weighting mechanism dynamically prioritizes control objectives in response to system conditions such as renewable intermittency and grid disturbances. To improve long-term reliability, the strategy introduces state diversity enforcement and inactivity penalties, reducing capacitor stress without compromising harmonic performance. The effectiveness of the proposed method is evaluated using a dynamic equivalent model of a proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolyzer, enabling a realistic assessment of green hydrogen production. Validation through hardware in the loop (HIL) test demonstrates robust current control and stable DC-link voltages in varying operating scenarios.

title: Event-Triggered Platooning Control for Nonlinear Vehicles With Switching Communication Topologies and External Disturbances
authors: Lei Zuo, Maode Yan, Mengmeng Jun, Jinqi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The vehicle platoon tracking problems with Markov switching communication topologies and external disturbances are significant for intelligent connected vehicles. In this paper, we present an event-triggered vehicle platooning control protocol, in which both the switching communication topologies and external disturbances are taken into consideration. To deal with the switching communication topologies, an event-triggered mechanism is developed based on the communication switching mechanism and the vehicle state estimation errors. Depending on this proposed event-triggered mechanism and vehicle dynamic models, the vehicles can always obtain the states of leader vehicle and follow the desired platoon with any communication topology. Particularly, this event-triggered mechanism extends the traditional communication assumption and only needs a uniform communication topology with leader rooted spanning tree in an average dwell time (ADT). For the external disturbances in this proposed vehicle system, the strictly <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(Q, R, S)-\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula> dissipative theory is employed to analyze the system stability and robustness. The string stability of vehicle platoon with switching topologies is also recursively demonstrated by using the Lyapunov string stability. We further present the formation of the ADT and the parameters in proposed control protocol. To obtain these parameters, the linear matrix inequalities (LMIs) are used to improve the computation efficiency. Finally, numerical simulations are provided to validate the feasibility and effectiveness of proposed approaches.

title: Wavelength-tunable all-PM all-fiber femtosecond laser based on intracavity loss tuning
authors: Shuo Zhang, Xinyuan Zhao, Kui Jiang, Luming Song, Yikun Bu, Zhengqian Luo
abstract: We reported a C+L-band wavelength-tunable femtosecond Er-doped fiber laser with the all-polarization-maintaining (all-PM) all-fiber structure. The figure-9 laser is stably mode-locked by combining phase-biased nonlinear amplifying loop mirror and a home-made PM-fiber end-face dielectric mirror. Continuous tuning of the mode-locking wavelength from 1559.09 to 1602.46 nm is achieved, which is attributed to the 1500-1650 nm broadband reflection of the PM-fiber end-face dielectric mirror and the intracavity loss control using a variable optical attenuator. The laser delivers femtosecond pulses with a minimum pulse duration of 331 fs and a maximum 3-dB spectral bandwidth of 10.38 nm. To the best of our knowledge, this is the widest wavelength tuning range achieved in strictly all-PM all-fiber Er-doped fiber lasers.

title: Search Multilayer Perceptron-Based Fusion for Efficient and Accurate Siamese Tracking
authors: Tianqi Shen, Huakao Lin, Ning An
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems
abstract: Siamese visual trackers have recently advanced through increasingly sophisticated fusion mechanisms built on convolutional or Transformer architectures. However, both struggle to deliver pixel-level interactions efficiently on resource-constrained hardware, leading to a persistent accuracy-efficiency imbalance. Motivated by this limitation, we redesign the Siamese neck with a simple yet effective Multilayer Perception (MLP)- based fusion module that enables pixel-level interaction with minimal structural overhead. Nevertheless, naïvely stacking MLP blocks introduces a new challenge: computational cost can scale quadratically with channel width. To overcome this, we construct a hierarchical search space of carefully designed MLP modules and introduce a customized relaxation strategy that enables differentiable neural architecture search (DNAS) to decouple channel-width optimization from other architectural choices. This targeted decoupling automatically balances channel width and depth, yielding a low-complexity architecture. The resulting tracker achieves state-of-the-art accuracy-efficiency trade-offs. It ranks among the top performers on four general-purpose and three aerial tracking benchmarks, while maintaining real-time performance on both resource-constrained Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) and Neural Processing Units (NPUs).

title: Variance-Corrected Wavelet Diffusion for Industrial Sensor Signal Augmentation and Denoising
authors: Bohan Wang, Zhiyang Jia, Zhao Xu, Hongtao Zhou, Hongyan Cui, Yi Hu
abstract: Multivariate time series play a critical role in industrial production for monitoring, anomaly detection, and failure prediction, yet real-world scenarios often suffer from data scarcity and class imbalance, limiting the effectiveness of deep learning models. Existing generative approaches such as GANs and VAEs still face challenges in complex time series generation, including training instability, mode collapse, and limited ability to capture intricate temporal dynamics, making reliable deployment in industrial applications difficult. These limitations highlight the need for a more stable, controllable, and high-fidelity generative framework. To address the aforementioned challenges, we propose VC-WaveDiff, a diffusion-based generative framework specifically designed for time-series data. It tightly integrates our newly developed WaveTS-UNet with a denoising diffusion model to enable multi-scale structural modeling and stable high-quality synthesis. WaveTS-UNet incorporates a wavelet-based decomposition–reconstruction module tailored for time-series characteristics, which uses fixed wavelet filters to explicitly separate trend and rapidly varying components, achieving multi-scale representation learning with linear computational complexity. Within the diffusion framework, VC-WaveDiff analytically derives the optimal variance of the reverse process, allowing the model to follow a standard diffusion training procedure and generate high-quality samples adaptively at inference without additional training. Furthermore, a continuous noise scheduling function is introduced to unify discrete and continuous diffusion processes, enabling the generative trajectory to better reflect the inherent continuity of time series. Experiments on two public sensor datasets show that VC-WaveDiff generates diverse and high-fidelity time series under conditions of data scarcity or class imbalance, significantly improving downstream task performance and demonstrating its practical value for industrial AI applications.

title: Markov Chain-Driven Asymmetric Dual-Beta Region Reconstruction Random SVPWM for High-Frequency Sideband Harmonic Suppression and Peak Frequency Band Control in PMSM Systems
authors: Wenjun Ruan, Yong Chen, Ruiping Wang, Shuangxia Niu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: To effectively suppress high-frequency sideband voltage harmonics (HFSVH) in space vector pulse width modulation (SVPWM)-driven permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) systems and enable controllable regulation of the HFSVH peak (HFSVHP) frequency band, this paper proposes a Markov chain-driven asymmetric dual-Beta overlapped random switching period SVPWM (MDBORSP). First, the analytical expression for the output voltage power spectral density (PSD) of RSP-SVPWM in a two-level PMSM system is derived, and the impact of different carrier switching frequency (CSF) probability density functions (PDFs) on the distribution of HFSVH is clarified. Second, to address the limitation that under uniform distribution, HFSVH energy concentrates around the CSF and a single Beta distribution cannot effectively suppress the HFSVHP while adjusting the frequency band, MDBORSP constructs a dual-Beta distribution with a region overlap mechanism. This approach effectively reconstructs the HFSVH spectrum, significantly enhancing harmonic suppression performance. Furthermore, a three-state Markov chain is introduced to establish a harmonic distribution control mechanism, using asymmetric CSF PDFs to regulate the HFSVHP frequency band while maintaining strong harmonic suppression. Simulation and experimental results show that, compared to traditional SVPWM, MDBORSP improves HFSVH suppression by 21.25 dB and 19.83 dB, respectively, and achieves controllable migration of the harmonic peak frequency band. Additionally, the algorithm requires only 14.79 μs per switching cycle, providing ample real-time implementation margin. This study provides novel theoretical insights and practical implementation approaches for harmonic reconstruction and modulation strategy optimization in low-noise PMSM systems.

title: Iterative Learning Security Control under Bit-Rate Constraints and Attacks
authors: Yue Huang, Michael Shi, Jun Cheng, Mei Yan, Leszek Rutkowski
abstract: This paper addresses the iterative learning control (ILC) problem for linear time-invariant systems operating under bit-rate constraints and denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. An adaptive encoding–decoding mechanism is proposed, where the encoder dynamically adjusts its quantization range according to the allocated bit rate of each sensor node, thereby ensuring reliable decoding under bandwidth limitations. To minimize the tracking error bound, a constrained optimization problem is formulated and solved using a particle swarm optimization (PSO)-based allocation scheme, which achieves superior resource utilization than conventional Lagrange multiplier methods. Furthermore, a novel compensation strategy based on single exponential smoothing is introduced into the ILC framework, providing accurate prediction of lost data and strong resilience against both isolated and successive DoS attacks. Theoretical analysis establishes boundedness of the tracking error, and simulation studies demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves trajectory tracking accuracy and robustness relative to existing methodologies.

title: SS-CoSaMP-Based Channel Estimation for RIS-Assisted Multi-User Systems with Multi-Region User Distribution
authors: Bin Qiu, Yang Xu, Shuqi Xu, Xiao Chang, Zhongshan Zhang, Jianqing Li, Zhen Chen
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) is a pivotal enabling technology for sixth-generation (6G) wireless systems, yet the massive number of passive reflection elements in RIS introduces prohibitive pilot overhead during channel estimation—especially in multi-user scenarios where users are clustered in multiple angular regions. To address this challenge, this paper exploits the underutilized column structural sparsity induced by multi-region user distribution, a feature that prior works have not fully leveraged. First, a multi-region channel model is established to characterize the inherent correlation of user channels within the same angular region. Then, we propose a Structure-Sparse Compressive Sampling Matching Pursuit (SS-CoSaMP) algorithm, which decomposes cascaded channel estimation into two sequential stages: estimating the positions of sparse elements and recovering the channel matrix using the CoSaMP framework. Simulation results validate that the proposed SS-CoSaMP algorithm outperforms the exsiting methods in terms of Normalized Mean Squared Error (NMSE). Specifically, it achieves a 1–2 dB NMSE reduction compared to OMP and DS-OMP, and a 0.25 dB reduction compared to SS-OMP under the same signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), while significantly reducing pilot overhead.

title: Unsupervised Incremental Class Discovery Via Exploring Self-Distillation Reciprocity Knowledge
authors: Yanhua Yang, Jie Yin, Xi Wang, Cheng Deng
abstract: Unsupervised incremental class discovery (UICD) is a novel scenario in which a model must continuously learn new categories from a stream of unlabeled data while retaining previously acquired knowledge. Existing methods primarily leverage the representation capabilities of pre-trained models (PTMs) but fail to explore underlying feature spaces and neglect data relationships, limiting the PTMs' effectiveness. To address these challenges, we propose the self-distillation reciprocity knowledge discovery (SDRKD) framework to unlock the significant PTMs' potential. Specifically, we introduce the Reciprocity Nearest-Neighbor Feature Fusion (R2NF) method to generate reciprocally structured samples, optimizing the updates to the teacher-student self-distillation network by maximizing mutual information, thereby obtaining a more discriminative model for the novel class. Moreover, we propose a reciprocity classifier calibration (R2C) method, and utilize the correlation between classifiers to calibrate the new classifiers, reducing the overlap in the feature space during the cascading process, thus minimizing the forgetting of previously learned knowledge. Furthermore, our method reduces memory loss of previously learned knowledge while enhancing the model's ability to discover new classes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on various benchmarks.

title: An improved Preisach-based Hysteresis Modeling of the Temperature Dependence for Soft Magnetic Materials
authors: Wei Li, Shiyi Liu, Lixun Zhu, Tianran He
abstract: The magnetic properties and iron losses of the soft magnetic materials are susceptible to temperature increase, and are difficult to be accurately simulated and calculated. In this paper, an improved temperature dependence Preisach hysteresis model for soft magnetic materials is proposed, which can be used to describe the hysteresis behavior of materials at various ambient temperatures. The nonlinear influence of temperature is introduced by establishing the relationship between magnetic field strength and temperature. The hysteresis loops at various different temperatures are measured by using ring specimens, to construct and identify the Everett function database. The iron loss of soft magnetic materials at different temperatures can be calculated using the proposed model. The validity and accuracy of the proposed model are verified by comparing the calculated results with the experimental measurement results.

title: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
authors: N/A

title: Automated spatiotemporal response identification and separation for averaged and single-trial EEG and MEG data_supp1-3676122.pdf
authors: Oskari Ahola
abstract: Objective: Conventional analysis approaches of evoked EEG and MEG typically rely on assumptions of independence or uncorrelatedness, fixed temporal windows, and predefined regions of interest to extract neural responses. However, cortical activity is spatially and temporally overlapping and interactive, meaning that independence or uncorrelatedness cannot be assumed. As a result, these methods often fail to account for overlapping cortical activity and individual variability, limiting the accuracy and interpretability of the results. To enhance the validity of research and clinical inferences, we aimed to reliably isolate spatiotemporally localized neural evoked responses. Methods: We developed Spatiotemporal Event Response ENcoding (”SEREN”), an algorithm that automatically identifies spatiotemporally localized evoked response components by leveraging the spatiotemporal density properties of post-synaptic currents using Gaussian kernels in time and space. SEREN can extract individual evoked response components for both averaged and single-trial data and operates in both sensor and source space. Results: We demonstrate SEREN's effectiveness on auditory and visual-evoked MEG data as well as on simulated datasets. Additionally, we show that SEREN can be calibrated for robust single trial monitoring in noisy EEG systems using transcranial magnetic stimulation-evoked potentials, including simulated in real-time applications. Conclusion: SEREN reliably isolates cortical evoked responses, overcoming limitations of conventional analysis approaches that do not account for inter-response overlaps or individualization. Significance: By improving the precision of neural response extraction, SEREN provides a powerful tool for advancing the analysis of neural dynamics and improving the validity of research and clinical applications.

title: Multi-UAV Cooperative Trajectory Planning with Dynamic Programming
authors: Jiaxin Yan, Zebo Zhou, Bei Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper proposed a cooperative trajectory planning method with Dynamic Programming (DP) to address the real-time generation of safe trajectories for Multi-UAV in complex environments. The DP comprises three key components: (1) adaptive batch sampling of the state space to efficiently reduce computational complexity; (2) application of DP principles to compute feasible trajectories from the sampled states; (3) formulation of the trajectory optimization problem as a quadratic programming (QP) problem through spline curve parameterization, yielding optimal smooth trajectories. Extending the single-UAV planning paradigm, we introduce a distance-constrained information exchange for multi-UAV coordination. Through collaborative environmental sampling, each UAV gains access to extended spatial awareness beyond its individual sensor range, thereby expanding the solution space and enhancing the reliability and robustness of the planning outcomes. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework achieves real-time performance with computation times in the order of milliseconds. Comparative evaluations against state-of-the-art methods reveal superior trajectory quality, making it suitable for multi-UAV applications in complex environments.

title: Superconducting Through-Silicon Coplanar Waveguide Interconnects for High-Bandwidth Three-Dimensional Integration
authors: Yang Huang, Gaowei Xu, Bojun Chen, Chunyu Lan, Kelaiti Xiao, Xiaoming Xie
abstract: Superconducting digital integrated circuits (ICs) are driving increasing demand for low-loss, high-bandwidth vertical interconnects to support advanced cryogenic systems. In this work, broadband superconducting through-silicon coplanar waveguides (CPWs) are fabricated within wet-etched vias by sputtering and patterning niobium (Nb) on the via sidewalls, establishing an effective architecture for high-frequency vertical signal transmission. By systematically optimizing the CPW geometry and introducing sidewall transition compensation within the via, the proposed structure mitigates impedance discontinuities and enhances transmission performance. Experimental measurements demonstrate stable broadband operation, with S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sub> ≤ −15 dB and S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</sub> ≥ −3.5 dB from 0.01 to 48 GHz. De-embedded results further indicate that a single through-silicon CPW achieves S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sub>​ ≤ −15 dB and S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</sub> ≥ −1 dB up to 37 GHz, confirming low reflection and reduced transmission loss in the via region. These results demonstrate the feasibility of superconducting through-silicon CPWs as high-bandwidth vertical interconnects and provide a scalable pathway toward three-dimensional integration in superconducting computing and cryogenic microwave systems.

title: Monocular Ground Normal Prediction for the Road Ahead
authors: NORBERT MARKÓ, ZOLTÁN RÓZSA, ÁRON BALLAGI, TAMÁS SZIRÁNYI
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Robust fusion of monocular and inertial data has the potential to offer a low-cost alternative for ground surface normal prediction ahead, compared to more expensive sensors, such as LiDAR. Yet robust camera-based prediction remains challenging, particularly for steep grades and texture-poor, homogeneous road surfaces. To address these issues, we propose an enhanced monocular camera-IMU fusion pipeline incorporating a lightweight transformer-based feature matcher for improved correspondence accuracy, and robust temporal filtering, using a spherical linear interpolation (SLERP) filter, to enhance consistency and reduce drift. To enable rigorous benchmarking and reproducibility, we also standardize the evaluation protocol and release a novel dataset containing synchronized camera, LiDAR, and IMU-derived pose data, specifically captured across diverse incline and decline scenarios. Extensive continuous validation demonstrates that our method significantly improves both accuracy and temporal stability over existing approaches, setting a new state of the art for robust, continuous ground normal estimation ahead. The proposed pipeline and dataset are publicly available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://norbertmarko.github.io/imu_cam_normal_prediction/</uri> <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://norbertmarko.github.io/imu_cam_normal_prediction/</uri>.

title: FedDOT: Defending Federated Learning Against Overwhelming Targeted Attacks
authors: Priyesh Ranjan, Ashish Gupta, Federico Corò, Sajal K. Das
abstract: Federated Learning (FL), which facilitates collaborative model training and protects users’ privacy, has drawn great interest from the research community. With FL, participants train their models on local data and submit the corresponding updates for aggregation to a server. While concealing the identities of the participants, FL may attract adversaries in order to hamper the underlying model. In this paper, we propose an FL framework, FedDOT, to defend against adversaries performing targeted attacks. FedDOT incorporates two powerful defense algorithms, Maximum Spanning Tree based attacker detection (MSTAD) and Densest graph based attacker detection (Density-AD), which leverage correlation between weight updates and graph theory concepts, maximum spanning tree, and densest graph. With a goal to withstand an overwhelming number of attackers, our algorithms provide strong solutions to aid an FL server, even in overwhelming scenarios where adversaries constitute more than half of the participants. Along with theoretical bounds in correlation space, a rigorous experimental analysis using image classification datasets is carried out to validate the robustness of the FedDOT framework in non-iid settings, which ascertains the superiority of the models against the state-of-the-art methods using a variety of metrics evaluating the accuracy and attack detection rate. With an attack success rate of < 10% for targeted attacks like single-label flipping, multi-label flipping, and backdoor, FedDOT successfully defends against overwhelming adversaries with a marginal accuracy drop of less than 2%.

title: Comparison of Event-Driven and Batch Processing Architectures for Fraud Detection and User Profiling in Banking
authors: Elma Midžić, Bećir Isaković
abstract: Banking systems nowadays handle millions of transactions every day, where speed matters most when the system must detect fraud. Traditional batch-processing systems introduce delays because data is being processed at scheduled intervals. Event-driven architecture handles each transaction at the moment it appears; therefore, the system can react almost immediately. This paper compares event-driven and batchprocessing architectures using simulated banking transactions. The results show that event-driven processing significantly reduces latency and enables earlier fraud detection, while batch processing still works well for non-critical jobs, such as periodic user profiling.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Architectures for Image Classification
authors: Disha Moharle, Vrushali Mahalle, Rutuja Ingole, Achal Bangde, K.T.V.Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Deep learning has been beneficial to many visual recognition tasks through enabling automated extraction of features from unprocessed image data and as part of this work the authors have investigated performance of DenseNet121, ResNet50, and InceptionV3 on the Intel Image Classification Dataset which contains 25,000 images of natural scene type images. The results from the experiments confirm that DenseNet121 achieves the best classification accuracy of the three models due to its mechanism of reusing dense features and that InceptionV3 follows; however, ResNet50 has a lower performance than the others. The difficulties of within class visual similarity and imbalanced data that exists within the dataset limit the ability to discriminate between classes. Therefore, the results indicate that the model architecture used, training strategy adopted, and management practices applied by the researcher will be significant factors to improve classification accuracy. Future work should also investigate techniques such as data augmentation techniques, class balancing, and use of attention-based mechanisms for improving the reliability of the classification.

title: A Development of Clustered Edge Subjected Trust Assessment in Federated Learning (FL) Assisted Digital Twin based Internet of Things Environment
authors: T P Ramachandran, Abdullah Khatib, V Syambabu, Yashashwini S, Geeta Padole, Ramya M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is a form of decentralized machine learning that allows multiple devices to collaborate on creating a single overarching model while keeping their respective data completely private. CETFDI outlines the architecture of advanced IoT networks in B5G areas. The main components of CETFDI are: Clustered Network Formation (CNF), Edge Subject Learning (ESL), Secured Blockchain Model (SBM) and FL. ESL employs the use of GAN to evaluate the trust levels between different devices within an edge network and this helps to strengthen the access control mechanisms. The use of OFDMA within the blockchain component provides secure data transmission and allows for fast, reliable transactions that are conducted using smart contracts.

title: Cuffless Multi-Parameter Cardiac Monitoring: A Portable ECG-PPG-SpO2 Integration System
authors: Sahana M. Kulkarni, Jamuna S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Cardiovascular illnesses remain a top global health problem, highlighting the need of precise, practical, and user-friendly monitoring technologies. On a small, power-efficient platform, the device can record ECG, PPG, and SpO₂ data all at once.
