Even in common, real-world settings, Pulse-Edge guarantees steady, high-quality data capture by including sophisticated preprocessing methods. After the preprocessing step, more than 95% of the acquired signals met diagnostic criteria, according to the first study with 50 participants. The results of the test demonstrated that the gadget accurately measures HRV and blood pressure while also picking up on short cardiac events that are missed by traditional cuff-based monitors. The participants said the device was great for everyday tasks since it was easy to use and comfortable. This gadget has a lot of great features that make it a great tool for telemedicine, chronic illness management, and preventative healthcare. Pulse-Edge combines clinical precision with user-friendly design for continuous monitoring, according to the study, making it a key step in individualized heart health monitoring.

title: Voltage Optimization Control in Distribution Networks via Graph Attention Reinforcement Learning with Coordinated Inverter and Energy Storage Systems
authors: Shengyuan Wang, Fengzhang Luo, Zhengyang Xu, Lukun Ge, Chao Pang
abstract: To address the limitations of existing deep learning-based voltage optimization control methods, particularly the insufficient modeling of inter-agent coupling, this paper proposes a multi-agent deep reinforcement learning approach based on a graph attention mechanism for voltage control tasks involving the coordinated operation of inverters and distributed energy storage systems. First, the voltage control problem is formulated as a decentralized partially observable Markov decision process, with each inverter and energy storage unit modeled as an independent agent. A centralized training and decentralized execution framework is adopted to enable collaborative optimization among agents. Second, graph-structured data is constructed based on electrical distances between nodes to characterize the coupling relationships among agents. Then, graph attention module is integrated into the critic network to adaptively learn the importance weights of neighboring node features, enabling each agent to generate differentiated spatial aggregation features and thereby improve the quality of policy learning. Finally, control strategies are continuously optimized using a policy gradient mechanism, achieving efficient and distributed voltage regulation. Simulation results show that the proposed method exhibits superior voltage control performance and strong generalization capability on both the IEEE 33-bus and 141-bus systems.

title: Charging Infrastructure Planning for Promoting Vehicle to Grid: A Two-Stage Framework Considering Willingness of Electric Vehicle Owners
authors: Xuewei Zhang, Qianqian Yan, Zhenhong Lin, Haobo Dong
abstract: The development of vehicle to grid (V2G) technology and distributed generation resources (DGRs) is driving the transformation of power distribution network (PDN) toward greater decarbonization and operational flexibility. This phenomenon poses challenges to the coordinated deployment of both. Hence, this paper proposes a two-stage planning framework to achieve the coordinated deployment of V2G charging piles (V2GCPs) an DGRs. Stage 1 integrates real GPS travel data with V2G willingness model to predict charging demand and V2G capacity of electric vehicles (EVs). Stage 2 optimizes location and capacity of V2GCPs and DGRs aiming to minimize the social cost impacted by V2G. Tianhe District, Guangzhou, China fed by a 21-bus PDN is selected as test system to verify effectiveness of proposed approach. Research indicates that V2GCPs tends to be concentrated in areas where EVs have prolonged parking durations. In design of V2G contracts, annual revenue of V2G, battery degradation warranty period, and battery capacity under warranty should be set as high as possible. For guaranteed state of charge and daily plugged-in hours, values of 0.3 and 5 hour are respectively found to be optimal.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Tiered Cyberbullying Severity Assessment and Escalation
authors: Cinthuja K, Preetha D, M Rekha, Kavitha Subramani, Deepika J., Abinaya S.
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Cyberbullying has become amajor issue, particularly for youth. students and young people. It makes people depressed, anxious,. and won’t do very well in school. Most AI tools that are in use right now. They just look for harmful content and do nothing further have to report abuse themselves. Cyberbullying has emerged as a major societal issue, particularly among youth and students, leading to significant psychological consequences such as depression, anxiety, and poor academic performance. While current AI tools can detect harmful content, they often lack integrated reporting mechanisms, placing the burden on victims to report abuse. To address these limitations, this paper proposes BullyBlock AI, a comprehensive system that not only detects cyberbullying but also assesses its severity and automates the reporting process.Leveraging advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques, including BERT and spaCy, the system analyzes online communications to identify abusive content and classifies its severity into Low, Medium, or High categories. In cases of severe or repeated bullying, the system automatically generates and sends email alerts to relevant authorities via SMTP, ensuring immediate intervention. Furthermore, the platform features a sentiment analysis and visualization module, which distinguishes between positive and negative messages and displays their distribution through interactive graphs. This allows stakeholders to monitor communication trends over time and gain insights into the overall online climate.By integrating precise detection, intelligent severity classification, proactive reporting, and intuitive visualization, BullyBlock AI provides an effective early-warning system designed to create a safer digital environment for all users.

title: Improving Epileptic Seizure Recognition with GAN-Augmented Spectrograms and Hybrid AI Models
authors: Rohit Kandpal, Akansha Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Epilepsy is a complex neurological condition characterised by sudden and recurrent seizures, but it afflicts millions of people worldwide. EEG(Electroencephalogram) has been de-scribed as an effective non-invasive technique for recording brain activity and detecting seizures. The lack of seizure samples and the extreme class imbalance make it difficult to diagnose epileptic seizures using EEG signals. In order to improve robustness and clinical reliability, this study suggests a spectrogram-based architecture that combines hybrid deep learning models with GAN-augmented data. Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) was used to convert EEG signals from the UCI Epileptic Seizure Recognition dataset into spectrograms. A GAN was then trained to produce synthetic seizure spectrograms, balancing the dataset. Four models were tested on both original and supplemented datasets: CNN-BiGRU, 2D-CNN, Vision Transformer (ViT), and Swin Transformer. On the original dataset, ViT obtained the best recall (98.89 %) and F1-score (96.02 %), while the Swin Transformer obtained the maximum accuracy (98.43 %) and precision (98.16 %). With the Swin Transformer achieving an accuracy of 99.02 %, precision of 99.39 %, and F1-score of 99.00, and ViT achieving the highest recall (99.23 %), all models showed improvement following GAN augmentation. According to these results, sensitivity and generalisation are greatly enhanced by GAN-based augmentation, and Transformer-based models provide the best overall performance for EEG-based seizure recognition.

title: Intrusion Detection in Cloud Systems Using a Deep Attention Pyramid Network
authors: Shivani Yadao, G Sreelatha, Srinidhi Samboju, Pitani Mounika
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: With increasing threats in cloud environments, intrusion detection is becoming more challenging as attackers exploit diverse protocols. This work proposes a deep self-attention pyramid network combined with horse herd optimization (HOA) to enhance security, accuracy, and efficiency. HOA selects the optimal features from datasets. The method is evaluated using DARPA IDS and CSE-CIC-IDS2018 datasets, achieving accuracies of 99.67 % and 99.45%, respectively, with loss reduced below 5%.

title: A Multi-Terminal Hybrid DC System for Offshore Wind Power Transmission with Auxiliary Contact Line
authors: Yucheng Wu, Qiang Song, Zhengxuan Li
abstract: In offshore wind DC transmission systems, diode rectifiers (DRs) offer advantages over modular multilevel converters (MMCs) in terms of lower cost, reduced size, and lighter weight. However, DRs lack power reversal and grid-forming capability. To leverage the benefits of DRs while enabling gigawatt-scale offshore wind power delivery, this paper proposes a multi-terminal hybrid DC transmission topology with auxiliary DC contact line (MTH-CL). The scheme implements hybrid DR-MMC topologies at respective offshore terminals, interconnected via low-capacity contact lines. These connections facilitate low-capacity power transfer from MMC windfarms to DR windfarms, thereby resolving critical challenges including black-start and near-zero power (NZP) condition in DR windfarms. Furthermore, this work introduces a reactive power grid-forming control and contact line power control strategy for DR windfarms, enabling stable operation across multiple operational modes including black-start, steady-state, and NLP condition. Simulation results demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed topology and control strategy.

title: An Interpretable Machine Learning Framework for ICU Mortality Risk Prediction in COVID-19 Patients
authors: Rutuja Ingole, Disha Moharle, Vrushali Mahalle, Achal Bangade, Rajendra Rewatkar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: It is critically important to have dependable tools available to help identify inpatients during admission for those patients at highest risk for in-hospital death due to COVID19. The COVID19 Pandemic has created an unprecedented amount of demand on intensive care units (ICUs) around the world; consequently, it is vital to initiate clinical intervention and allocate limited intensive care unit (ICU) resources as quickly as possible by early risk stratification among patients, particularly in an intensive care unit (ICU) setting. This article combines routinely-collected clinical data routinely available at the time of admission to the intensive care unit (ICU) in order to provide a predictive analytic framework for evaluating the mortality of patients who have been admitted into an intensive care unit (ICU) due to COVID19 infection. The final data set consisted of 1,250 patients admitted into the intensive care unit (ICU) from a tertiary care medical center, of these patients, approximately 28% had died as a direct result of their COVID infection. A variety of supervised learning techniques were tested including, Logistic Regression, Gradient Boosting Machines, Extra Trees, XGboost, and Random Forests; Frequently loaded datasets from Methodological Evaluation were used as the source for developing and validating the supervised machine learning models listed above. The Random Forest Model selected as the Optimal Classifier Model had excellent classification capabilities and inherent interpretable features. The Random Forest classifier model had an ROC-AUC of 0.866, an F1 of 0.566, and a Recall of 0.439 when evaluated on a holdout Test Dataset; Features considered important for predicting clinical outcomes consistent with adapting clinical management based on current clinical knowledge were: Age, Oxygen Saturation Levels, Inflammatory Markers, and Comorbidities. In light of the fact that moderate recall highlights the potential for false negative results, the framework should be viewed as a means to aid clinical judgement rather than replace it. Overall, the proposed strategy provides an open, evidence-based decision support system for use in ICU triage and resource allocation during pandemics.

title: Efficient Secure Aggregation in Federated Learning for Edge Computing
authors: Sindhu A, Suresh A, Prapti Bhattacharjee, Megha Chandel, Pooja Shree H R, Esakkiammal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The deployment of Federated Learning (FL) in edge environments faces three major obstacles because it requires high communication expenses and device failure events and privacy threats from gradient information disclosure. The paper presents an efficient secure aggregation system which protects privacy through multi-layer masking and additive secret sharing and topology-based communication optimization and differential privacy options. It enables efficient client participation through gradient compression and energy-based scheduling and fault-tolerant mask reconstruction methods. The system reduces communication expenses by 42% while maintaining accuracy levels that match or surpass current secure aggregation methods according to experiments conducted on cloud servers and smartphones and Jetson devices and IoT-class hardware using MNIST and CIFAR-10 and HAR datasets. The proposed model proves suitable for real-world deployment on edge networks because it provides both privacy protection and reliable operation across various edge network conditions.

title: AI-Powered Lung Cancer Screening: A Vision Transformer Optimization Approach
authors: P.Kalyanasundaram, S.Senthilkumar, S.Rajalakshmi, R.Vijayarangan, V. Thirumurugan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Aim: Lung cancer is the top leading cause death globally, and early and precise detection is required to achieve better survival rates. This research tends to create an optimized Vision Transformer (ViT) model that can be used for real-time lung cancer detection with better generalization and efficiency. Leveraging deep learning and transformer-based models, the proposed model endeavors to realize high diagnostic accuracy along with computational efficiency for real-world usage. Materials and Methods: This research consists of two groups. Group 1: Recently, several start-ups explore the adoption of Vision Transformers (ViTs) to overcome some of the limitations CNNs exhibited. Group 2: Hybrid approaches that leverage the advantages of both ViTs and convolutional architectures, as well as advanced attention mechanisms, represent a strong opportunity to enhance lung cancer detection performance. Results: The Hybrid Attention Vision Transformer (HViT) model classification shows a 96.7% accuracy of 96.7%, , Recall of 96.63%, Precision of 94.8%, F1-score of 96.1% and AUC of 0.97. The model exhibits a substantial inference time reduction, processing images at 0.32 seconds per scan, and is most applicable for hence well-suite for real-time scenario. The model also generalizes well across datasets, with a 5.6% performance gain over conventional CNN-based models. Conclusion: The optimized Vision Transformer model proposed here effectively improves lung cancer detection with enhanced generalization and efficiency. The real-time processing capabilities with improved accuracy of this work make it a potential tool for early diagnosis, which can assist healthcare professionals in making timely decisions and enhancing patient outcomes.

title: An Evolutionary Deep Learning Framework for Early Liver Cancer Detection via Genetic Algorithm–Driven Feature and Hyperparameter Optimization
authors: M. Kalaivani, S. Rajaram, S. Punitha, J.P. Srividhya, P. Vidhya, V. Brinda
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Computed tomography images are still difficult to use to identify liver cancer at its early stages because small visualization patterns are displayed and variations are high in patients. The current paper introduces an evolutionary deep learning architecture which combines convolutional neural networks with evolutionary algorithms based optimization to improve the detection. Initial pre-processing of CT images in a publicly available Kaggle dataset is aimed at denoising noises and equalizing the intensity difference. Deep convolutional network is used to develop hierarchical features representation of liver regions. A genetic algorithm is proposed instead of utilizing all extracted features as a way of selecting discriminative feature subsets and tuning important network hyperparameters at the same time. This joint optimization scheme helps to decrease redundancy, enhance generalization, and decrease manual parameter selection. The experimental findings prove that the developed solution ensures better classification accuracy and sensitivity than a base deep learning model with no evolutionary optimization. The framework provides a trustworthy and versatile method of early detection of liver cancer based on medical imaging data.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Real-Time Misinformation Detection in Social IoT
authors: Praveen Gopal Gaonkar, Manas Singha, Rupam Sah, Rupam Bhagawati, Vengatesan.K, Bikramjit Saha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: A convergence of social networks and Internet of Things ecosystems, the idea of the Social Internet of Things (Social IoT) has arisen. One defining feature of this idea is the social interaction-based autonomous interaction of smart devices, users, and services. This paradigm improves knowledge sharing and intelligent collaboration but propagates misinformation at unparalleled speed and scale. Social IoT environments are dynamic, relational, and heterogeneous, therefore content analysis and static network features fail to detect misleading. This study introduces a GNN-based framework for real-time disinformation detection in Social IoT systems. The suggested technique learns structural, temporal, and semantic patterns of misinformation dissemination by modelling users, IoT devices, content, and interactions as a dynamic heterogeneous graph. Experimental results show that the suggested technique has high detection accuracy, low latency, and robust performance under changing network conditions. The results show that graph-based learning protects trust and dependability in next-generation Social IoT platforms.

title: Real-Time Cyber Threat Prediction for Supply Chain Security: A Machine Learning-Based Framework
authors: Baddam Srilatha, K. Vijaya Babu
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Advanced cyber-attacks on supply chainintegrated vendor networks are raising the visibility of supply chain cybersecurity. Conventional reactive security controls are not effective as threats are always evolving, particularly with supply chains becoming increasingly digital and geographically dispersed. In this paper, a novel machine learning paradigm for real-time cyber threat forecasting in supply chains with proactive security controls for multivendor environments is presented. Our framework integrates ensemble learning techniques with threat intelligence analysis through Random Forest, Gradient Boosting Machine, and Support Vector Machine algorithms to evaluate attack history trends, network traffic patterns, vendor security scans, and real-time threat intelligence feeds. The framework implements a multi-layered ensemble strategy that achieves 94.3% threat detection accuracy while reducing false positives by 67 % compared to the best current methods. The system processes data in real-time with 1.8 seconds average p50 latency and 10,000 events per second throughput. Pilot deployment in an 87-vendor logistics firm over three months tested 91.7 % detection rate on verified attacks. Comprehensive assessment of edge deployment optimization (93.1 % accuracy on resource-constrained hardware), privacy-preserving federated learning (1.6 % accuracy compromise), and horizontal scaling analysis (linear efficiency up to 16 nodes) establishes production-readiness of the framework. Empirical research demonstrates how learning by ensembles significantly outperforms stand-alone algorithms while maintaining real-time guidance, resulting in much more robust and resilient supply chains.

title: Correction of the Eyring Reverberation Time Formula Using an Artificial Neural Network: A One-Solution Approach
authors: Zoran N. Milivojević, Violeta Stojanović, Bojan Prlinčević, Dijana Kostić
abstract: The first part of this paper presents the classical Eyring formula used for estimating the reverberation time T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">60</inf> of a room. An experimental study to assess T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">60</inf> using the classical Eyring formula was performed. The Schroeder algorithm to estimate T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">60</inf> from the Room Impulse Response (RIR) was also applied. The second part of the paper introduces a corrected Eyring formula obtained by multiplying the original Eyring formula by a correction coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k_{\text{Eyr}}$</tex>, parameterized by the coefficients <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta, \gamma$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\delta}$</tex>. Using an Artificial Neural Network (ANN), the parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\beta}, {\gamma}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\delta}$</tex> were computed. An ANN training procedure on the dataset <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{D}_{\text {train }}$</tex>, consisting of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=25401$</tex> RIRs, was performed. The corrected Eyring formula to test on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M=10887$</tex> RIRs was then applied. The mean absolute error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\overline{{e}}$</tex> and the mean squared error MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{\text {Eyr }}$</tex> as evaluation metrics for reverberation time estimation were used. The experimental results with graphs and a table are presented. A detailed comparative analysis shows that the corrected Eyring formula improves the precision of reverberation time estimation by 51.38 % compared to the classical Eyring formula was performed.

title: Diffusion-Augmented Medical Image Interpreters for Clinical Reliability
authors: S. P. Santhoshkumar, Ashish Kumar, Syed Karimulla
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: By learning a forward noise-perturbation process and its corresponding reverse denoising trajectory, diffusion models have become potent generative frameworks that can produce high-fidelity images. Medical image generation, reconstruction, translation, and noise-robust analysis have all advanced more quickly thanks to their strong representational ability. However, unpredictable noise, covariate shifts, and label imperfections are common challenges for medical image interpretation systems in real-world clinical settings, which reduce diagnostic confidence and predictive reliability. To present Diffusion-Augmented Medical Image Interpreters for Clinical Reliability, a unified framework that improves robustness, calibration, and decision quality across various medical imaging tasks by utilizing diffusion-based generative modeling. In order to achieve stable classification under adversarial perturbations, unseen noise, and resolution degradation, the framework first combines transformer-based feature extraction with diffusion-driven density estimation. In order to effectively mitigate noisy-label effects, it also introduces diffusion-enabled answer refinement for Medical Visual Question Answering (Med-VQA) using a task-aware conditional generator and coarse-to-fine Answer Diffuser. Furthermore, UniMIE, a training-free universal enhancement model, uses a single ImageNet-pretrained model without fine-tuning to synthesize high-quality images across 13 imaging modalities. Comprehensive tests on multi-modality, X-ray, dermoscopy, and Med-VQA datasets show excellent calibration, accuracy, and dependability. All things considered, diffusion models have a great deal of promise to make medical image interpretation systems more reliable and broadly applicable.

title: Model-Free Learning Compensation of Robotic Arm Manoeuvres for a Free-Flying Base*
authors: Adam Vigneron, Steve Ulrich
abstract: This study addresses the dynamic coupling between a servicer spacecraft and its robotic arm in which routine arm manoeuvres degrade the attitude performance of its base. The industrial robotic technique of iterative learning control is applied to base attitude control during repeated arm manoeuvres, compensating the corresponding disturbance torques without the need for complex dynamic models. Experiments carried out using Carleton University’s Spacecraft Proximity Operations Testbed demonstrate improved settling behaviour for the arm-base system as a whole, with base attitude control error reducing five-fold from 5.05° RMS in the first iteration to 0.81° RMS in the fourth iteration. Such improvements can reduce the keep-out volume needed by the servicer in spacecraft proximity operations, enabling transitions between standard arm configurations without endangering other spacecraft nearby.

title: Quantum-Classical Weighted Clique Detection for Microservice Deployment in Cloud Systems
authors: Seda Nur Güngör, Mehmet Karaköse
abstract: The widespread adoption of microservice architectures in large-scale cloud systems introduces challenges related to the optimal deployment of service components and the optimization of inter-service communication. This study proposes a quantum-classical hybrid approach to address this problem, which can be considered an NP-hard problem at large scale, with the aim of achieving an optimal deployment of microservices that exhibit intensive interactions. In the proposed method, microservice interactions are modeled as a weighted graph and transferred to a quantum environment for the purpose of maximum clique detection. Modeling the problem using a weighted graph, where microservice interaction parameters such as latency, communication frequency, and data volume are evaluated as composite edge weights, enables the deployment problem to be grounded in system-oriented criteria and treated as a multi-dimensional optimization task. In this study, all valid clique structures are correctly identified, thereby enabling optimization strategies to be executed within the quantum computing environment.

title: Explaining User Intentions to Use AI-Generated Image Tools Through Trust, Risk, and Service Quality
authors: Calvin Andrew Widjaja, Tanty Oktavia
abstract: This study examined the factors influencing users' behavioral intention to use AI-generated image (AIGI) tools in Indonesia by extending the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT) through service quality, trust in AI, and perceived risk. We conducted a quantitative analysis of six variables based on survey data from 100 Indonesian AIGI users: service quality, trust in AI, perceived risk, performance expectancy, effort expectancy, and behavioral intention. Moreover, it has been examined that performance expectancy and effort expectancy significantly affect behavioral intention, signaling that perceived ease of use and usefulness continue to be key determinants of behavioral intention. Level of service quality has a major influence on effort expectancy; however, it does not directly impact performance expectancy. AI trust increases performance expectancy and decreases perceived risk whereas perceived risk reduces behavioral intention, while it does not impact performance expectancy. These results contribute to demonstrating how usability, trust, and risk mitigation can positively reinforce AIGI adoption.

title: A Hybrid Adaptive Visual Servoing Approach for Robust Robotic Manipulation in Space
authors: Lina María Amaya-Mejía, Kuldeep Barad, Miguel Olivares-Mendez, Carol Martinez
abstract: Visual servoing is a core capability for autonomous robotic manipulation in space applications like in on-orbit servicing (OOS) operations, yet traditional methods degrade under visibility changes, motion constraints, or unreliable depth. We propose a Hybrid Adaptive Visual Servoing (HAVS), combining coarse eye-to-hand (ETH) position-based with fine eye-in-hand (EIH) 2.5D servoing. A blending strategy is used to ensure that transitions avoid discontinuities, and pose estimation selectively fuses localized depth when reliable. HAVS is validated in a high-fidelity dynamic simulation of Redwire Space’s STAARK manipulator for space operations. Results show that HAVS achieves smoother control, lower energy consumption, and faster convergence compared to classical hybrid-switching, while maintaining accuracy and feature visibility. Its depth strategy improves behavior over pure 2D and is more efficient than full 3D, making HAVS well suited for precision OOS.

title: Cloud Detection in Remote Sensing Images Using Deep Learning Models: GAN, FCN, and CloudFormer
authors: Roshini Fathima S., B. Surendiran, Dhivya Bharathi, Chandra Mouli P.V.S.S.R.
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: For satellite-based applications like weather forecasting, climate monitoring, environmental tracking, and agricultural planning to be more accurate, cloud detection in remote sensing imagery is essential. Spectral confusion is a common problem for traditional methods that rely on simple thresholds, particularly when clouds mimic snow or bright land surfaces. Deep learning models are being used for more precise and adaptable cloud detection as a result of these challenges. Three cutting-edge deep learning models are examined in this work: the Generative Adversarial Network (GAN), the Fully Convolutional Network (FCN), and CloudFormer, a transformer-based model. A synthetic dataset of Maxar satellite photos that have been enhanced with randomly generated cloud masks to replicate real-world cloud conditions is used to train these models. Key performance metrics such as accuracy, the confusion matrix, and ROC-AUC scores are the main focus of our evaluation.With a high AUC score and over 97% accuracy, CloudFormer outperformed the GAN and FCN models by a significant margin. Its transformer architecture improves segmentation accuracy by capturing both local and global patterns. The advantages of transformer-based architectures in handling challenging pixel-wise segmentation tasks, such as cloud detection, are demonstrated by this comparative analysis. According to the findings, CloudFormer is a superb fit for operational remote sensing systems.

title: Transforming Agricultural Market Access Through Predictive Intelligence And Multimodal Voice Interaction
authors: Kewal Dawada, Neel Gangar, Harsh Dabhi, S.Theres Beimila Jenet
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Agriculture is still the backbone of most of the world’s developing countries, but in many cases, farmers still have to deal with high costs of mediators, uncertain market prices, and no access to market information that is reliable and real-time at the same time. Recent machine learning techniques, especially LSTM and Prophet models, have been able to predict price trends very effectively. Moreover, recommender systems and voice-based interfaces can assist farmers in making better decisions and also help in lessening the negative effects caused by language or literacy barriers.The KrushiConnect framework is set to solve these problems by providing farmers with the power of predictive insights, facilitating the communication between farmers and buyers directly, and introducing multilingual, voice-based features making it a user-friendly system. The framework combines datasets on crops, climate, and prices and adopts various models like Random Forest, XGBoost, LSTM, and Prophet. These models are then made available via a cloud-based microservices architecture that is highly scalable and is augmented by a voice-enabled Natural Language Processing interface.In our studies, we found that LSTM was the most accurate pricing prediction model (with MAE values ranging between 77 and 89). Random Forest achieved approximately 88 percent accuracy.

title: Efficient Decomposition Algorithm for Coordinated Component Repair and Load Restoration under Multiple Failures in Distribution Systems
authors: Siyuan Sun, Gengfeng Li, Yiheng Bian, Yanzhe Ren, Zhaohong Bie
abstract: To address the conflict between solution optimality and computational efficiency of the coordinated component repair and load restoration problem in distribution systems, this paper proposes an exact and computationally efficient decomposition algorithm. The integer L-shaped method is leveraged as the basic framework, and several improved techniques are employed to facilitate convergence and avoid duplicate computation. Numerical results on the modified 33-node test system and IEEE 8500-node test feeder validate that the proposed method can achieve computational efficiency while ensuring solution optimality.

title: FreshNet: A Lightweight Computer Vision System for Real-Time Freshness Assessment of Vegetables
authors: K S Shashikala, Sandyarani Vadlamudi, Shreyash B Kone, Sai Yeshwanth, Harshit Gajendran, Thiyagarajan V. S
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: According to the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), nearly 40% of fruits and vegetables produced globally are lost before reaching consumers, primarily due to spoilage and improper post-harvest handling. In India alone, the post-harvest loss of vegetables is estimated at over ₹13,000 crore annually, largely because of the absence of affordable freshness monitoring tools. While high-end solutions such as hyperspectral imaging or chemical analysis exist, they are inaccessible to small-scale farmers, street vendors, or low-income households due to their cost and technical complexity. To address this gap, we propose FreshNet, a lightweight, computer vision-based system that can assess the freshness of vegetables in real time using basic RGB imagery. The system uses classical techniques such as colour histograms, brightness thresholding, and texture variation detection to classify vegetables as fresh, moderately stale, or rotten — without relying on cloud connectivity, expensive hardware, or high-performance GPUs. Designed to run on laptops, mobile phones, or single-board computers like Raspberry Pi, FreshNet integrates offline voice feedback, multilingual support, and a low-memory footprint, making it ideal for deployment in rural areas, local marketplaces, and smart kitchens. By enabling accessible and cost-free freshness evaluation, FreshNet aims to reduce food waste, enhance food safety, and empower underrepresented communities with practical AI-driven tools.

title: An AI-Driven Real-Time Toxicity Detection System for Voice Chat in Online Gaming Environments
authors: Soha Ahmed Ehssan Aly Mohamed, Mirna Sherif, Mirvt Mohammed, Nada Salah, Roaa Waleed, Mohammed Ismail, Saddam Bekhet
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Science and Applications in Industry and Society (CAISAIS)
abstract: Online gaming, while offering rich social experiences, increasingly exposes vulnerable populations, particularly children aged 8-16, to various forms of harmful communication, including toxic speech, identity-based hate, and inappropriate language. This study proposes an innovative, AI-driven real-time in-game toxicity detection system designed specifically for voice chat interactions to significantly enhance child safety in online gaming environments. The system leverages a multi-component pipeline: first, it employs Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) to accurately transcribe live voice communications. Second, the transcribed text is subjected to a sophisticated multi-label hate speech classification model, alongside a parallel tone analysis module that assesses the emotional intensity and intent from the raw audio. A critical innovation is the integration of game-aware contextual filtering, which intelligently differentiates between genuinely toxic speech and benign, action-based in-game banter, thereby substantially reducing false positives. Furthermore, the system incorporates robust parental control customization, enabling caregivers to define sensitivity levels and specify trigger alerts for various harmful content types, such as threats or offensive jokes. Upon detection of elevated toxicity, real-time notifications are dispatched via email, providing parents with immediate insight into their child’s exposure to or engagement in harmful interactions. Seamlessly integrated with a dedicated mobile application, the system processes model outputs and determines appropriate, timely interventions, granting parents unprecedented transparency and control over their child’s online gaming experiences. This comprehensive solution is engineered to cultivate a safer, more positive, and respectful gaming ecosystem.

title: AI-Driven Micro-Doppler Classification: Inverted Residual Network with Linear Bottlenecks for Small Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Sujata Patil, Madan Mali, Supriya Rajankar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Small unmanned aerial vehicles have become increasingly prevalent in both civilian and defense settings, but their misuse poses significant risks to security. Their small size, low altitude flight, and ability to blend with environmental clutter make them difficult to detect using traditional radar techniques. A promising solution lies in analysing micro-Doppler signatures, which capture the fine motion patterns caused by rotor blades and body movements. These signatures carry unique information that can help separate small unmanned aerial vehicles from birds, insects, or other aerial objects. In this study, we present a deep learning-based methodology for UAV classification using DIAT-uSAT dataset. The raw radar signals were preprocessed and converted into time-frequency spectrograms, which were then used as input to a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and transfer learning models. Four architectures—MobileNetV2, VGG16, ResNet50, and EfficientNetB0—were evaluated to compare classification performance. The experiments were conducted on simulated data, and the results demonstrate that MobileNetV2 outperformed the other models, achieving a training accuracy of 98. 78%, a validation accuracy of 99.14%, and a minimal validation loss of 0.0014. These results confirm the effectiveness of lightweight CNN architectures for UAV detection in real-time environments. The proposed framework offers a reliable solution to improve situational awareness in civilian and defense applications.

title: Contextual Cyberbullying Detection Using Emotion Enhanced Bystander Modeling
authors: K. Nirmala Devi, T. Vanaja, S. Kavitha, Chandru A, Arvind M, Dharani M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Early intervention of cyberbullying in social networking sites (SNSs) is very essential to remain safe online and avoid negative contacts. The conventional methods usually pay attention to the primary post without considering the role of bystander responses in determining the extent and situation of cyberbullying. In this work, the article proposes a chained deep-learning architecture, which incorporates the roles of bystanders as well as labels of cyberbullying to allow fine-grained detection. The model uses pretrained transformer embeddings (DistilBERT and RoBERTa) with LSTM and BiLSTM networks to learn the semantic and sequential relationships on social media text threads. In order to deal with the issue of class imbalance, oversampling measures were employed to provide sufficient coverage of the bystander roles and the levels of cyberbullying severity. The experimental results have proved that the DistilBERT and BiLSTM model was the most accurate with a 97.1% accuracy and a 97.3% F1-score, compared to DistilBERT and LSTM, RoBERTa and LSTM, and RoBERTa and BiLSTM variants. The findings substantiate the significance of embedded context, sequential relationships, and bystander roles in the robust recognition of subtle online aggression, and provide a scalable route to safer and more reliable online relationships.

title: Optimizing Feeder Selection Across Underfrequency Load Shedding Scheme Steps
authors: Oswaldo Arenas, Mohammad Rajabdorri, Lukas Sigrist, Enrique Lobato, Luis Rouco, Víctor Sordo, Abel Santamaría, Jesús Gutiérrez
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of optimal feeder selection in underfrequency load shedding (UFLS) schemes. Conventional UFLS schemes, which remain the predominant approach in power systems, operate in predefined steps, with each step intended to disconnect a specific percentage of the total load. However, the increasing variability in load profiles, driven largely by the growing penetration of distributed generation, makes it increasingly challenging to consistently achieve the targeted shedding levels. To overcome these challenges, we formulate the feeder selection task as a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) problem, enabling the optimal assignment of feeders to each UFLS stage. Given the computational burden of the full MILP model, especially in large-scale systems with multiple stages and many feeders, we propose a sequential optimization approach that solves each UFLS step independently. Both models are tested using real historical hourly data from a region in Spain. Results show that the sequential model achieves nearly the same solution quality as the full MILP formulation, with significantly lower computational effort, making it suitable for practical deployment in operational settings.

title: NeuroASD-Net: A Computationally Efficient 2D CNN Framework for Autism Spectrum Disorder Classification from Structural MRI Slices
authors: Vignesh E, Gobika S, Mahalakshmi P, Beno Ranjana J
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is usually diagnosed through behavioral observation, which often takes time and may delay early treatment. To support faster and more objective diagnosis, this paper presents NeuroASD-Net, a lightweight deep learning model for ASD classification using structural MRI images. Instead of analyzing full 3D scans, the proposed method extracts a small number of meaningful 2D slices, reducing computational cost while preserving important brain information. Each slice is preprocessed through normalization and contrast enhancement before being processed by a custom 2D Convolutional Neural Network. The model applies regularization techniques and class-weighted training to improve reliability. Experimental results indicate strong classification performance, highlighting the usefulness of the proposed approach for automated ASD screening.

title: Complementarity Analysis of Hydro-Wind-Photovoltaic Hybrid Systems and Research on the Optimal Installed Capacity Ratio
authors: Yongpei Li, Siyang Liao, Jian Xu, Youhan Deng, Yushu Li, Fengming Shi
abstract: Under the impetus of the "carbon peak and carbon neutrality" goals, the large-scale integration of wind and photovoltaic (PV) renewable energy has raised challenges for the grid's absorption and stable operation. Meanwhile, hydropower, with its rapid start-up and shutdown as well as wide-range regulation characteristics, has become the core support for stabilizing fluctuations. This paper addresses the optimal capacity allocation problem for hybrid hydro-wind-PV systems by proposing a multi-dimensional complementarity assessment framework and a volatility minimization allocation method. An empirical study taking the Wudongde Hydropower Station as an example shows that there is significant temporal complementarity between wind power and photovoltaic power, and the optimal installed capacity ratio is 7:3. When the proportion of hydropower is 90% (hydropower: renewable energy =9:1), the fluctuation of the hydro-wind-PV hybrid system is the smallest, verifying the core role of the wide-range regulation capability of hydropower in suppressing the fluctuations of wind and photovoltaic power. The proposed method provides a universal capacity allocation tool for the large-scale development of wind and photovoltaic in water-rich areas, supporting the safe and stable operation of the new power system.

title: Optimizing Artificial Pollination in Indian Agriculture through Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning and Generative AI for Precision Pollen Distribution
authors: P Charanya, V Kalaivani, D Vimal Kumar, S Akila, A Praveena, M Dhurgadevi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: India’s agriculture, supporting 58% of its population, faces threats from declining natural pollinators, impacting key crops like mango and cotton. Traditional artificial pollination methods are labor-intensive, while robotic solutions are costly and impractical for smallholder farmers. This paper proposes PollenNet, an AI-only framework using multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) and generative adversarial networks (GANs) to optimize pollen distribution via affordable tools (e.g., hand-held blowers). Simulations and trials in Maharashtra demonstrate a 91% pollination success rate, 35% pollen reduction, and costs of ₹5000/hectare, tailored to Indian conditions. PollenNet offers a sustainable, scalable solution for Indian farmers.

title: A Reliability Optimization Method for Medium-Voltage Distribution Network Structure Based on SAIDI Data and Load Moment Analysis
authors: Yuting Li, Peng Xie, Lu Gao, Tao Jiang, Ding Hu, Bo Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional medium-voltage distribution network reliability analysis methods, such as decoupled computation of power (P) and distance (L), poor adaptability to complex regions, and inaccurate reflection of actual reliability goals, a reliability optimization method for medium-voltage distribution network structures based on SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index) data and load moment analysis is proposed. This method innovatively adopts load moment (MW∙km) as the measurement dimension to linearly solve the nonlinear reliability problem. By calculating the load distribution skewness coefficient, regional total load moment, unit load moment outage risk probability, and SAIDI level, the actual load moment level of each segment and branch is compared with the threshold to determine optimization objects and strategies. Case studies show that the method effectively overcomes the contradictions of traditional decoupled solutions, improves computation accuracy by 15%-20%, and has strong adaptability in new urbanization areas, suburbs, and rural power grids.

title: Early Detection and Categorization of Cervical Cancer Cells Using Deep Learning Technique
authors: Akalya K, Prathisha S, Sri Ganga Harini K, Chidambaram S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Cervical cancer is one of the highly fatal cancers affecting women, and its early detection is crucial for effective treatment. In this paper, we present an automated diagnostic system that employs a cross-entropy dependent multi-deep transfer learning approach for the early detection and classification of cervical cancer cells. The proposed model consists of four key stages: preprocessing, feature extraction and fusion, feature reduction, and feature classification. In the feature extraction and fusion stage, features are derived using multiple deep transfer learning models, including MobileNet, DenseNet, EfficientNet, Xception, RegNet, and ResNet-50, after which the extracted features are fused. The performance of the proposed model is validated using benchmark datasets such as SIPaKMeD. Experimental results demonstrate that the model attains an accuracy of 98% on the SIPaKMeD dataset utilizing 676 features.

title: Optimal Capacity Mix to Meet Incremental Peak including Green Data Center Loads
authors: Emon Chatterji, Tony Ahfock, Morgan D. Bazilian
abstract: This paper proposes a stylized model to determine the capacity mix to meet an incremental GW peak in China and Australia. A stochastic linear programming (SLP) model is used to evaluate how differences in thermal and renewable energy capital and operating costs, load profiles, interannual variability of solar/wind, frequency control ancillary services requirements, emissions constraints, and demand flexibility influence technology selection and costs. While low coal capital costs in China allow coal to remain competitive, Australia's higher coal costs and stronger wind resource shift the optimal mix toward renewables and storage. In both countries, carbon constraints raise capital costs significantly and the implied cost of carbon is significantly higher than simpler models predict. The analysis demonstrates how a stylized SLP model can be a useful aid in a more robust assessment of load growth on costs, technology mix and carbon constraints compared to simplistic policy models.

title: A Survey on Platform-Level Data Quality: From Visibility to Controllability
authors: Tony Rosset, Lukasz Wisniewski, Stefano Scanzio
abstract: Modern data platforms integrate heterogeneous data sources—ranging from IoT sensors and APIs to databases and event streams—and deliver information to diverse analytical and operational applications. Ensuring data quality in such environments is increasingly complex, as platforms often possess only partial visibility into both data origins and usage contexts. Most traditional data quality frameworks implicitly assume full knowledge of the source schema or consumer requirements— assumptions that rarely hold in large-scale, multi-tenant ecosystems. This survey consolidates three decades of research on data quality and reinterprets it through the constraint of partial visibility. Over seventy academic and industrial sources are organized into ten thematic clusters encompassing foundations, governance, streaming, metadata, ML/AI, profiling, integration, storage, economics, and trust. By analyzing these clusters through the lens of platform capabilities, the paper exposes a systematic gap between what platforms can observe and what they can control. The surveyed literature is synthesized into a visibility–controllability taxonomy that distinguishes four regimes of platform-level quality management: inferential, interface, adaptive, and operational. This taxonomy explains why many existing approaches fail to scale to platform settings and identifies the conditions under which quality guarantees remain feasible despite incomplete information. The study highlights open challenges, including quality estimation without ground truth, integration of observability and governance, adaptive thresholds, and distributed accountability. The results establish a foundation for inference-driven, context-aware data quality management—enabling platforms to ensure trustworthy Quality of Service (QoS) even when full transparency of sources and consumers is unattainable.

title: Energy-Efficient User Grouping-Based Federated Learning with UAV Assistance
authors: Chien-Wei Fu, Meng-Lin Ku, Keshav Singh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: This paper proposes an energy-efficient user grouping (UG)-based federated learning (FL) framework with the assistance of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) to overcome transmission rate constraints that hinder model dissemination. By dividing users into groups, the proposed method enhances the effective transmission rate, ensuring feasibility and improving system scalability under rate-limited conditions.We propose a novel joint design framework to optimize UAV trajectory, FL participation, power allocation, and data volume, in which participation control, data volume allocation and UG are simultaneously incorporated and co-designed. A convergence analysis is conducted to characterize how user participation and data volume affect the expected global loss gap of the FL model. The resulting non-convex problem is addressed using successive convex approximation (SCA), forming a UG-SCA algorithm which integrates UG with SCA techniques. Simulations show that UG-SCA significantly outperforms benchmark schemes in terms of both energy efficiency and convergence performance. Increasing the number of groups supports larger model transmissions and better scalability. The results reveal a trade-off between transmission feasibility, model size, and learning performance in UAV-assisted FL.

title: ThermalTexture: Temperature-Integrated Surface Rendering Using Thermal Referral Induced by Texture Slip-Feedback
authors: Donghyeon Kim, Chungman Lim, Gunhyuk Park
abstract: Thermal–vibration feedback has been reported to elicit the thermal referral phenomenon, offering new possibilities for enhancing user experience. Yet, little research has explored whether thermal referral occurs in the presence of texture-slip feedback. To address this, we developed a custom haptic device capable of delivering integrated thermal and slip feedback to the middle and distal phalanges of an index finger. We primarily estimated the thermal referral occurrence under passive and active touch conditions across two different perceptual experiments, while each further examined its impact on texture perceptions and user experiences (UX). In Perceptual Experiment 1 (passive), we observed the highest occurrence of thermal referral at 40°C (0.86 on a 0–1 scale), and found that referral increased the median of perceived roughness from 43.5 to 60 on a 0—100 scale. In Perceptual Experiment 2 (active), the kinesthetic cues significantly enhanced thermal referral, with the occurrence probability at 24°C increasing to 0.86 compared to 0.51 (0—1 scale) in the first study. In addition, high UX ratings demon strated the applicability of our device to temperature-integrated virtual texture interactions. Our findings expand the scope of thermal referral and highlight the potential for enabling VR users to interact with virtual surfaces that provide temperature cues.

title: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems publication information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems

title: Predefined-time convergence terminal sliding mode guidance laws against angle constraints
authors: Feng Yang, Chengyu Zhao, Ziheng Cheng
abstract: This paper proposes three-dimensional impact angle control guidance laws based on a predefined-time convergence sliding mode control scheme. Unlike the usual decoupling design approach, the guidance laws are derived from strong coupled engagement dynamics, and the guidance commands are formulated in terms of the missile’s lateral accelerations rather than the tangential accelerations of the lineof-sight. A predefined-time convergence terminal sliding mode is presented to enforce the desired impact angle precisely at the time of interception. Since the traditional reaching control is prone to arouse actuator saturation despite small initial errors, a double surfaces approach is proposed to manage the reaching phase. The corresponding guidance law realizes a whole global sliding mode, and enforces that the original sliding variable converge to zero within a predefined time. The stability of the guided system is analyzed with Lyapunov stability theory. The property of predefined-time convergence is demonstrated via analytic solutions. Numerical simulation results are presented to validate the proposed guidance laws for a constant, as well as realistic missile model.

title: Observer Sequence Analysis for High-Speed IPMSMs Sensorless Drive
authors: Xingyang Guo, Wei Sun, Dawei Li, Ronghai Qu
abstract: Modern digital control systems are currently discrete. The execution sequence of several sensorless control segments (Sampling, Clark transformation, Park transformation, current loop, inverse Park transformation and sensorless algorithm), particularly the execution sequence of the observer relative to other segments (i.e., observer sequence), significantly impacts coordinate transformation angle and system performance, especially in the case of low-pulsewidth modulation (PWM) to operating fundamental frequency ratio. This manuscript analyses sequence of discrete-time full-order observer for high-speed internal permanent magnet synchronous motors (IPMSMs) sensorless drive and proposes three control sequences that can operate in steady state. The efficacy of the analysis is validated through simulations and experimental.

title: A Middle-Capacitor-free Two-Stage 48V to 1-1.2V Converter using Fixed 0.5 Duty-Cycle Switched-Capacitor
authors: Chan-Ho Lee, Hyun-Woo Jeong, Jeeyoung Shin, Woong Choi, Junwon Jeong, Sung-Wan Hong
abstract: This paper proposes a 48V to 1-1.2V two-stage converter, consisting of a high-voltage (HV) switched-capacitor (SC) stage and a low-voltage (LV) buck converter stage. By fixing the duty-cycle (D) of the HV SC at 0.5, peak current in HV SC is optimized, minimizing conduction loss (PCON) in the HV SC. In addition, the continuous capacitor-current (IC) path in the LV stage enables removing a middle-capacitor and improves transient response. The proposed converter supports up to a 4A maximum load current and achieves peak efficiencies of 90.9% at output voltage (VO) of 1V and 92.1% at VO of 1.2V.

title: Probabilistic Disaggregation of Behind-the-Meter PV Systems Using Conformal Prediction
authors: Jialin He, Tarek Alskaif
abstract: Disaggregating solar photovoltaics (PV) profiles from smart electricity meter data has attracted attention, as Distribution System Operators (DSOs) need street-level PV generation profiles to improve grid operations and planning. Given the importance of reliability in operational decisions, probabilistic results are preferred to avoid overlooking potential violations. This paper proposes a probabilistic disaggregation framework based on Conformal Prediction (CP), a cutting-edge uncertainty quantification methodology. This framework trains a deterministic regressor to estimate normalized PV generation profiles and proposes an efficient capacity estimation algorithm to help compute the full PV generation profiles. To obtain probabilistic results, the framework applied CP with different variants, such as Mondrian Binning (MB) and Conformal Predictive System (CPS), to enhance the reliability of prediction intervals. To address the arbitrary bin count in CP with MB, the paper proposes a novel CP variant, namely: Adaptive Mondrian Binning (AMB). Its performance, along with other CP methods, is evaluated and benchmarked against quantile regression methods on two actual datasets from the region of Amsterdam, the Netherlands, and Sydney, Australia. Results show that using LightGBM as the deterministic regressor, AMB outperforms quantile regression and other CP variants. The generalizability of the proposed framework is analysed for both probabilistic outputs and key sub-processes, such as deterministic disaggregation and capacity estimation.

title: Suppressing Mobility Overestimation in Electrolyte-Gated Transistors Using an AC Small-Signal Method
authors: Xiaoci Liang, Jiawei Zhang, Li Luo, Shaocong Lv, Chenglin Li, Yongliang Zhao, Zixin Wang, Mengye Wang, Chuan Liu
abstract: Mobility extraction in electrolyte-gated transistors is complicated by ionic migration and electrochemical adsorption, which introduce frequency-dependent gate capacitance and lead to pronounced mobility overestimation in conventional DC measurements. Here, we develop an AC small-signal test methodology that enables simultaneous extraction of gate capacitance, transconductance, and channel mobility under transistor operation. The method is validated through circuit simulations and experiments on SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/IGZO TFTs. When applied to AlOx/InOx electrolyte-gated transistors, the AC-extracted mobility remains stable at approximately 8 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, whereas DC extraction yields values exceeding 70 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>. The results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively suppresses ionic-induced mobility overestimation, providing a practical approach for mobility evaluation in electrolyte-gated transistors.

title: AI-Empowered Performance Optimization of Cooperative Perception for Connected and Automated Vehicles
authors: Xumin Huang, Zuhang He, Yating Lin, Weifeng Zhong, Jiawen Kang, Xiaohuan Li, Yuan Wu
abstract: To overcome the limitations of the single-vehicle perception, cooperative perception has been proposed as a promising approach to enable the spatially separated connected and automated vehicles (CAVs) and roadside infrastructures to share and make full use of the perceptual information. However, the current works tend to perform the redundant feature interaction, neglect the spatio-temporal modeling, and lack the incentive-aware CAV selection. To overcome the drawbacks, we study the AI-empowered performance optimization of cooperative perception to achieve the high-accuracy and computation-efficient feature fusion, and utility-aware CAV selection. A novel cooperative perception framework based on the enhanced spatiotemporal interactive and sparsity-aware cooperative perception is elaborately designed to tackle the critical challenges of communication overhead, computational efficiency, and dynamic scene understanding. In addition, we present a greedy-based CAV selection approach to tackle the performance-cost tradeoff in the cooperative perception service, i.e., the overall perception performance and total incentive cost. Experimental results on the OPV2V and V2XSet datasets are presented to verify the advantages of our scheme in comparison with the benchmark schemes. Finally, we discuss the open challenges and future research directions of cooperative perception.

title: Dark-EvGS: Event Camera as an Eye for Radiance Field in the Dark_supp1-3674360.pdf
authors: Jingqian Wu
abstract: In low-light environments, conventional cameras often struggle to capture clear multi-view images of objects due to dynamic range limitations and motion blur caused by long exposure. Event cameras, with their high-dynamic range and high-speed properties, have the potential to mitigate these issues. Additionally, 3D Gaussian Splatting (GS) enables radiance field reconstruction, facilitating bright frame synthesis from multiple viewpoints in low-light conditions. However, naively using an event-assisted 3D GS approach still faced challenges because, in low lights, events are noisy, frames lack quality, and the color tone may be inconsistent. To address these issues, we propose Dark-EvGS, the first event-assisted 3D GS framework that enables the reconstruction of bright frames from arbitrary viewpoints along the camera trajectory. Triplet-level supervision is proposed to gain holistic knowledge, granular details, and sharp scene rendering. The color tone matching block is proposed to guarantee the color consistency of the rendered frames. Furthermore, we introduce the first real-captured dataset for the event-guided bright frame synthesis task via 3D GS-based radiance field reconstruction. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves better results than existing methods, conquering radiance field reconstruction under challenging low-light conditions. The code and sample data are included in the supplementary material.

title: Real-Time Rail Potential Regulation in Flexible DC Traction Power Systems Based on a Physical-Meaning-Inspired Optimization Algorithm
authors: Zhanhe Li, Xiaoqian Li, Weihan Bao, Chao Lu, Chi Yung Chung, Kechun Ma, Shiyun Li
abstract: Flexible DC traction power systems (TPSs) based on voltage sourced converters (VSCs) enable real-time optimal operation for energy conservation. However, excessive rail potential remains a critical safety concern. Although flexible DC TPSs can mitigate rail potential through VSC voltage control, existing methods either compromise other operational objectives, such as energy saving, or fail to meet the sub-second computational demands imposed by highly volatile train loads. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a novel physical-meaning-inspired optimization algorithm that adjusts VSC voltage reference orders to constrain rail potential. This study first establishes an analytical relationship between VSC voltages and rail potential, followed by the derivation of a simplified but explicit relationship to provide physical insights. Then, the underlying physical meanings of rail potential regulation are interpreted and translated into a mathematical mapping. The mapping is essentially a quadratic programming problem that directly relates excessive rail potential to the required VSC voltage adjustments while retaining other operational performance. This fast-to-solve mapping constitutes the proposed algorithm, guaranteeing high computational efficiency. Case studies based on a real-world metro project demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, achieving a 39.9× speedup compared to the primal-dual interior point method (PDIPM) while maintaining near-optimal performance.

title: A Multi-Device Ground-Air Collaborative Path Planning Method with Hierarchical Architecture Based on Search-Enhanced Walrus Optimizer
authors: Junwei Sun, Ruohan Li, Yingcong Wang, Yanfeng Wang
abstract: As the demand for multi-dimensional ground and air information acquisition increases on modern battlefields, ground-air cooperation has become a crucial method to enhance operational efficiency and decision-making. In the complex and changeable battlefield environment, unmanned ground vehicles (UGVs) and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) can leverage their strengths to maximize exploration efficiency. The multi-level collaborative path planning method for UGV-UAV systems (MLCPP-UUs) is proposed in this paper, comprising three layers: single-UAV, multi-UGV and interactive planning. To optimize the total cost of the model, multiple strategies are used to improve the global exploration and local search of the walrus optimizer (WO). In search-enhanced walrus optimizer (SEWO), a dynamic step size adjustment is introduced during migration based on terrain steepness to avoid blind random search and improve search space coverage. In the later iteration, a nonlinear decreasing search factor is used to accelerate the convergence speed. For high-quality solutions, the simplex method is used to complete the “secondary exploitation" by efficiently searching the neighborhood of the solution space. To evaluate the performance of SEWO, three reference terrains from real digital elevation models (DEM) are generated with obstacle scenarios and interaction modes. The results show that the proposed algorithm can plan the collaborative paths satisfying the constraints efficiently, proving its effectiveness in the ground-air cooperative planning problem.

title: Fog-Enabled IoT for Biomedical Imaging: A Comprehensive Review with Deep Learning Perspectives
authors: P. Venkata Sateesh Kumar, Nagarajan Velmurugan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Biomedical imaging creates huge, complex datasets that are very important for keeping an eye on patients, making treatment plans, and finding diseases. However, traditional cloud-based image analysis methods don’t work well in healthcare settings that need real-time data and have limited resources because of issues like high latency, limited bandwidth, and privacy concerns. The Internet of Things (IoT) and the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) are growing quickly, which has raised the need for distributed, intelligent, and secure computing frame- works. The review examines how fog-enabled systems facilitate prompt and precise diagnostics by minimizing latency, enhancing bandwidth efficiency, and bolstering data security. It also talks about how fog computing can be used with the Internet of Things (IoT) to analyze biomedical images. We look at the current IoT- fog frameworks, look at their architectures, and compare how well they work in terms of security, energy efficiency, and latency. Significant research deficiencies are recognized, notably the lack of standardized datasets, the need for explainable deep learning integration at the fog layer, and issues related to scalability. Finally, we present a conceptual IoT–Fog– Cloud framework that combines explainable AI and federated learning. This framework points the way to safe, scalable, and scalable biomedical imaging solutions in the future.

title: ReplayOpt: Optimizer-State Replay to Resolve Critical-Path Bottlenecks in Offloaded Training
authors: Seongtae Bang, Gyeongseo Park, Kyeonghyeon Ryu, Daehoon Kim
abstract: CPU-offloaded training enables billion-scale model training on modest GPU clusters by storing high-precision optimizer state in host memory while keeping low-precision weights on the GPU. We find that CPU-offloaded training is often bottlenecked not by PCIe transfers but by storing high precision optimizer-state to host memory, which delays the next iteration and stalls the GPU. In our measurements, these non overlapped stores account for up to 28% of CPU-side optimizer time and delay the start of the next iteration, leaving the GPU idle. We propose ReplayOpt, an optimizer design that reorders the CPU–GPU update schedule to remove host memory state stores from the critical path. ReplayOpt produces and transfers the next-iteration low-precision weights first and replays high precision state updates in the background to remove host memory writebacks from the critical path. Across LLM workloads, ReplayOpt reduces CPU-side optimizer time by up to 55.2% and step time by up to 21.7% without accuracy loss.

title: Research on high-order Vese Osher model based on partial differential equation and its application in planetary gearbox fault diagnosis
authors: Zhile Wang, Xiaoli Yu, Yu Guo, Xin Chen, Wei Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Planetary gearbox is a crucial variable-speed transmission component in wind turbines, and the solar wheel is prone to local faults under harsh environmental conditions. Firstly, to avoid the influence of time-varying transmission paths, this paper preprocesses the fault vibration signal of solar wheel using window function and further transforms it to the angular domain.. Then, a high-order Vese-Osher model based on partial differential equations is subsequently developed to effectively identify the fault features of solar wheel. By introducing the mathematical expression of second derivative, this approach avoids the over-smoothing problem associated with the first derivative. While the Euler equation is commonly used to solve the total variation model, it cannot handle the G-norm, therefore, the divergence norm is considered to reformulate the model. Additionally, two weighting parameters are introduced between different regularization terms to optimize the solution, enhancing the ability of model to suppress noise in the fault-windowed vibration signal of solar wheel in the angular domain and thereby making the fault spectral lines more distinguishable. The Split Bregman algorithm is adopted to avoid directly solving high-order nonlinear partial differential equations. Furthermore, the method leverages the fast Fourier transform and generalized soft-thresholding equation to decompose the global optimization problem into several subproblems. Since higher-order cumulant exhibit superior noise suppression, the diagonal slices of third-order cumulant for the model output are extracted, and further obtain the 1.5-dimensional demodulation spectrum. This makes the fault feature of solar wheel completely prominent. Finally, The robustness of the above method in fault diagnosis of solar wheel is verified by fault test data.

title: Surface estimation of translucent materials: an application to fabric digitization_supp1-3676259.pdf
authors: Diego Sagredo

title: Severity of Diabetic Foot Ulcer Patients Prediction using a Hybrid and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Rapolu Satheesh, C Balarengadurai, Boddu Ravi Prasad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The Diabetic Foot Ulcers (DFUs) are one of the most disabling complications of diabetes mellitus conditions which most of the times result in the course of infection, amputation, and fatality in case of failure management. Accurate forecasting of the severity of DFU in a patient at their initial phases is a key milestone in ensuring a timely response on part of the clinical fraternity and improved patient only outcomes. DF competed with DL and ML methods have become an effective measure of DFU risk assessment, but some limitation remain with the use of the two methods in their interpretability, generalizability, and accuracy. Survey in this review is accompanied by the systematic review of the current trends in DFU severity prediction, especially with the focus on hybrid and novel ML algorithms. We explore the data sets, data pre processing methods, data features selection methods, machine learning techniques and measurement boxes. Further, new advances are also critically evaluated in terms of hybrid models that integrate classical ML with the deep learning, fuzzy systems or optimization algorithms. The present limitations, clinical applicability and future research direction form the conclusion of the review to make attempts to bridge the opportunity between intelligent systems and personalized DFU management.

title: Advancements in Design and Testing of IDRA, a Modular Inflatable Robotic Arm for Space Applications
authors: P. Palmieri, M. Gaidano, M. Melchiorre, L. Salamina, M. Troise, S. Mauro
abstract: This paper presents the design, implementation, and preliminary testing of IDRA, a modular inflatable robotic arm for In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) applications. The system leverages inflatable link technology to achieve a high packing ratio and large operational workspace, enabling functionalities beyond the capabilities of conventional rigid manipulators. A planar prototype was developed for functional validation under laboratory conditions using visual servoing control. Experimental trials assessed positioning accuracy and repeatability across varying internal pressures, demonstrating reliable convergence to the target despite the inherent compliance of the inflatable structure. The results confirm the robustness of the control strategy. Building on these outcomes, future work will focus on integrating the latest chamber design with a deployment-retraction mechanism, validating it in thermal-vacuum conditions, and advancing toward a fully operational multi-DOF robotic system suitable for space environments.

title: Neural Network-Based Prediction of Inter-Area Mode Parameters from Bus Voltage Phasors
authors: Lazar Šćekić, Danilo Planinić, Vesna Popović-Bugarin
abstract: This paper presents a data-driven approach for predicting inter-area oscillation mode frequency and damping ratio directly from bus voltage magnitudes and phase angles. Unlike most machine learning-based stability assessment methods that frame the problem as binary classification, the proposed approach addresses a regression task to estimate the exact numerical values of modal parameters, providing system operators with quantitative stability margins. A feedforward neural network is developed and benchmarked against Least Squares Boosting (LSBoost) and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithms. The training dataset is generated through Monte Carlo simulations of the Kundur two-area system, incorporating load demand variations, generation redispatch, N-K contingencies, and measurement noise. Results on the test set show that the neural network achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> values of 0.9691 and 0.9690 for frequency and damping ratio prediction, respectively, with a mean absolute percentage error of 0.85 % for frequency and 5.80 % for damping ratio.

title: Comparison of Active Frequency and Voltage Support Capabilities of Grid-Forming and Grid-Following Converters
authors: Qihang Zong, Wei Yao, Hongyu Zhou, Jinyu Wen, Yongxin Xiong, Jun Liang
abstract: Active frequency and voltage support capabilities of power converters are critical for ensuring the stability of renewable-integrated power systems. Grid-forming (GFM) and grid-following (GFL) converters represent the two fundamental controls. The relationship between the two converters in terms of support capabilities is unclear.
