This paper comprehensively reveals the uniformity and differences between GFM and GFL units in terms of frequency and voltage support, contributing to clarifying the relationship. For uniformity, the frequency and voltage support characteristics of the two converters are the same in case the control parameters are dual. For the differences in specific scenarios, the GFM converter has better support of the frequency nadir at near full support. The GFL converter is potentially more favorable for the voltage of the early period due to the error component of reactive power. Validation studies performed on a modified IEEE two-area system confirm the accuracy of the analytical findings.

title: Enhancing Machining Performance of AISI 1018 Steel in EDM Using Axial Magnetic Field Assistance: A Taguchi-Based Experimental Study
authors: Shivam Satpute, Parth Sawant, Sameer Sake, Pavitra Sardesai, Ganesh Dongre, Ravi Raut
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The research paper explores how an axial magnetic field affects machining of the AISI 1018 steel in die-sinking Electrical Discharge Machining (EDM). An oxygen-free copper electrode in the form of a rectangle and permanent magnets of NdFeB were utilized to create a controlled magnetic field that was perpendicular to the discharge axis to provide better evacuation of the debris by the Lorentz force action. An orthogonal array of Taguchi (L9) was used to consider the effect of discharge current (20-30 A), pulse-on period (50-90 0s), pulse-off period (40-80 0s) with three magnetic field conditions (0,2, and 4 magnets). It is found that magnetic field aid has a significant positive effect on machining productivity, as it has a better material removal rate (MRR), lower roughness (Ra) of the surface, and lower tool wear rate (TWR). The best performance was obtained with the use of 30 A, $70 \mu \mathrm{s}$ pulse-on time, and 4-magnet configuration, which gave the MRR 91.5 mg/s and Ra 2.4 0m. The results confirm the usefulness of magnetic-assisted EDM and show that it can be applied practically to enhance the quality of machining mild steel when permanent magnets are used at relatively low costs.

title: Security Defense of False Data Injection Attacks for Frequency Stability of Wind Farms Based on Tube Model Predictive Control
authors: Dongliang Jiang, Zhenqian Wang, Yuchen Zeng, Min Gao, Xiang Xu, Kaishun Xiahou
abstract: As a typical cyber-physical power system (CPPS), the wind farm relies heavily on its communication system for operation and supervision, which also introduces cybersecurity risks. The frequency stability of wind power systems has attracted increasing attention. In this paper, a tube-based model predictive control (TMPC) controller is designed for security defense of wind farm. The controller consists of a nominal model predictive controller (MPC) and an ancillary MPC. The nominal MPC solves the primary optimization problem and obtains the nominal state. The ancillary MPC guides the disturbed state of actual system towards the nominal trajectory to counteract the impact of false data injection attacks (FDIA) on frequency stability. Finally, simulation studies were conducted under three attack scenarios. The simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed method in enhancing the resilience of wind farms against cyber attacks.

title: A Flash of Insight: Real-Time Meteor Shower Classification with Machine Learning
authors: Youssef Maaod, Mohamed Abd Elaziz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Science and Applications in Industry and Society (CAISAIS)
abstract: Meteor shower classification plays a crucial role in understanding the origins and compositions of meteoroids, aiding both planetary science and space mission planning. Traditional classification methods rely on computationally intensive orbit determination, rendering them unsuitable for real-time applications. In this study, we propose a machine learning approach that enables accurate meteor shower classification using only early-stage geocentric and topocentric observations, bypassing the need for full orbital calculations. Leveraging a dataset of over 2 million meteor events from the Global Meteor Network, we trained and evaluated four models spanning different architectural classes—Logistic Regression, LightGBM, XGBoost, and a deep learning-based TabTransformer—on features including geocentric radiant position, entry velocity, and solar longitude. The gradient boosting models achieved exceptional performance, with XGBoost reaching 98.36% accuracy. The fully-trained TabTransformer also demonstrated strong competitive performance, proving the viability of deep learning on this task, albeit at a higher computational cost. Feature importance analysis confirmed that the GBDT model's predictions align with known physical properties of meteor showers. Our findings demonstrate that machine learning can provide a fast, accurate, and physically interpretable alternative for real-time meteor classification, paving the way for scalable meteor data triage and discovery.

title: Analysis of Models for Building Dependency Parsing in Agglutinative Languages
authors: Abdullayeva Oqila Xolmo‘minovna, Norkuziev Doniyorbek Bakhtiyorovich, Bayjonov Furqat Baxramovich, O‘tkirova Fotima Baxtiyorovna
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: In the domain of computational linguistics, syntactic analysis that is, the comprehension of the interrelationship between words-plays a pivotal role in natural language processing (NLP). Dependency parsing, a fundamental component of computational linguistics, offers a methodical approach to syntactic analysis. On a global scale, one of the most advanced and vital branches of NLP is dependency parsing, which focuses on detecting dependency relations between words. This technique is of particular significance in applications such as machine translation and sentiment analysis. The present article discusses the construction of dependency parsing models for agglutinative languages, their application to Uzbek sentence structures, the differences from other language types, and an analysis of foundational data resources and models for the Uzbek language. In this study, a BERT-based dependency parsing model is employed to analyze Uzbek sentence structures. The article discusses the construction of dependency parsing models for agglutinative languages and their application to the Uzbek language, highlighting differences from other language types. Furthermore, it provides an analysis of foundational linguistic resources and pretrained language models relevant to Uzbek. The paper delineates the distinguishing characteristics of grammatical dependencies in Uzbek sentences by leveraging contextual embeddings generated by the BERT model and juxtaposes these findings with traditional syntactic parsing approaches. Finally, the study evaluates the advantages and future potential of BERT-based dependency parsing for low-resource and agglutinative languages such as Uzbek.

title: Research on cross-vendor interoperable three-terminal differential protection synchronization technology
authors: Wei Liu, Feng Wang, Jiangang Lu, Jia Zhu, Tianyu Liang, Jinsong Li
abstract: Aiming at the interoperability challenges among three-terminal differential protection devices caused by proprietary protocols and non-unified synchronization technologies, this paper conducts research on synchronization techniques enabling cross-manufacturer interoperability. Based on the IEC61850-9-2 protocol framework, an open and transparent communication protocol for differential protection is designed, which standardizes the data frame format and status quantity definitions, thereby resolving the issue of transparent data transmission between devices from different manufacturers. A unified synchronization technology based on the sampling time adjustment method is proposed, clarifying the calculation of channel delays, steps for sampling time adjustment, and criteria for synchronization flag judgment to achieve precise alignment of sampling instants among three-terminal devices. Experimental verification shows that the communication protocol demonstrates good interoperability across manufacturers, with high synchronization accuracy and minimal error in differential current calculation, meeting the requirements of differential protection. The research results provide a feasible solution to break down manufacturer technical barriers and enhance the flexibility of power grid protection systems, holding significant implications for the standardized development of protection equipment in new-type power systems.

title: Blockchain-Enabled AI Architecture for Secure Patient Data Management and Trustworthy Healthcare Analytics
authors: Pattlola Srinivas, Addul Rahmath Mohammad, Abdul Aziz, Shiza Arshad, Mohammed Shafi Kundiladi, Siva Koteswara Rao Katta
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This paper presents a Blockchain-Enabled AI Architecture designed to enhance secure patient data management and trustworthy healthcare analytics. By integrating blockchain’s immutable and decentralized storage with AI-driven data processing, the framework ensures privacy-preserving data sharing, improved integrity, and reliable clinical insights. Smart contracts enable automated access control and compliance, while machine learning models operate on encrypted or anonymized data to support accurate decision-making. The proposed architecture strengthens data security and builds trust in digital healthcare systems.

title: Renewable Energy Resource Management Using Deep Learning in Smart Grids
authors: Jia Qi Zhu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: As the penetration rate of renewable energy in smart grids continues to increase, how to achieve efficient energy resource management has become a critical issue that needs to be addressed urgently. This paper proposes a deep learning-based renewable energy resource management framework that achieves accurate prediction and intelligent scheduling of renewable energy sources such as wind and solar power through the construction of multi-level prediction models and optimization scheduling strategies. The framework adopts a hybrid architecture of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), combined with attention mechanism to extract spatiotemporal features, and optimizes energy scheduling decisions through deep reinforcement learning. Experimental results show that the proposed method reduces the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of renewable energy power prediction to 4.237%, representing a 32.5% improvement over traditional methods, and increases energy utilization efficiency by 28.9%, significantly reducing grid operation costs. This research provides a new technical approach for efficient management of renewable energy in smart grids and has important theoretical value and practical significance for promoting energy transformation.

title: NFT Based Loyalty Program framework using Polygon CDK
authors: Mohammed Ghouse, Aaryan Malik, Gandhi Kishor Addanki, Swarnalatha P, Niha K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: With the growing demand for decentralized, user-centric digital ecosystems, businesses are increasingly exploring blockchain-powered loyalty solutions that overcome the limitations of traditional systems. We have developed a application that tackles these issues head-on using an NFT-based loyalty system built on Polygon CDK essentially a toolkit that lets you create fast, secure blockchain networks that still connect to Ethereum. What makes this approach special is how it bridges the gap between traditional payment systems and blockchain technology. Customers can pay with regular credit cards through Stripe or similar payment system, but the loyalty rewards automatically get created as NFTs and distributed through smart contracts. We present a modular framework that can be seamlessly integrated into enterprise platforms such as e-commerce, healthcare, and retail systems.

title: AI-Powered System for Evaluating Water Potability and Air Quality Index Using Environmental Datasets
authors: Krishnendu Nair, Atharva Aniruddha Jukar, Sahil Bhausaheb Ghuge, Soham Kalpak Kulkarni, Amey Ashok Sangale
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: This project presents an AI-powered framework for integrated evaluation of air quality and water potability using environmental datasets. It utilizes Random Forest for forecasting AQI levels based on pollutants like PM2.5, PM10, NO2, CO, and O3, and XGBoost for assessing water safety using parameters such as $\mathbf{p H}$, hardness, turbidity, and total dissolved solids. The system incorporates data preprocessing, feature selection, and model optimization to ensure accuracy. Additionally, an AI-driven chatbot is integrated to enhance user interaction. The framework provides actionable insights to support environmental decision-making and promote sustainable practices.

title: Trajectory Optimisation Solver for Active Trajectory Control of Model Rocket
authors: Yusuf Kalsekar, Varad Deshpande
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)

title: Research on Data Processing Methods for Transmission Line Icing Monitoring
authors: Hongfei Wang, Zepeng Sun, Yuzhi Shi, Kunpeng Ji, Peng Li, Jingshan Hang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue of insufficient quality in current transmission line icing monitoring data, which makes it difficult to directly use such data for constructing icing prediction models. It conducts research on preprocessing methods based on the actual measured icing data from a certain transmission line. Firstly, monitoring data is obtained, and grey relational coefficients are employed to analyze icing factors, identifying five factors with a correlation degree higher than 0.6 as input variables for the subsequent icing dataset. Subsequently, the dataset undergoes nearest-neighbor interpolation and normalization processing, further maintaining the continuity, completeness, and stability of the data, thereby achieving the goal of ensuring the dataset's accuracy and usability. This lays the foundation for the future construction of transmission line icing prediction models.

title: Infrared Eye Tracking: Unlocking Communication Pathways for Coma Patients
authors: Pranith Bhaskar P, Somnath Sinha, Binayak Dutta
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Eye tracking technologies have emerged as a groundbreaking technology in assessing and facilitating communication in patients with disorders of consciousness, including patients in a coma. Traditional methods of diagnosis rely on behaviour responses, which are non-existent or very minimal, thereby resulting in misdiagnosis or delayed intervention. Eye tracking provides an objective, non-intrusive means to measure ocular movement, visual interest, and response patterns, which may enable clinicians to make inferences regarding cognitive processing and residual consciousness. Furthermore, these devices provide a window of opportunity for the creation of minimum communication, enabling patients to communicate needs or preferences utilizing gaze-supported interfaces. This paper discusses current eye tracking systems, their application in the clinical field, and the prospect of applying them in the integration of these devices into conventional diagnostic and therapeutic routines. The findings stress that eye tracking not only enhances diagnostic accuracy but offers a platform for patient-centred communication, which eventually contributes to improved clinical outcomes and quality of life. Of the methods discussed (EOG, VOG, and infrared), infrared eye-tracking system had the best overall balance of spatial precision, responsiveness, and patient comfort and thus functioned best for diagnostic detection and communication based on gaze in this population.

title: A Smooth Mode Switching Strategy Between Grid-forming and Grid-following Controls
authors: Bo Yang, Xu Zhou, Zhanhao Zhao, Yulin Zhang, Naihu Li
abstract: Conventional switching strategies between grid-following (GFL) and grid-forming (GFM) modes often face challenges such as transient current variations and power fluctuations. This paper proposes a smooth switching strategy based on a backup operation for a single converter. The GFM control loop maintains integrator assigned during GFL mode. Similarly, the GFL control loop preserves integrator assigned during GFM mode. And the mode switching is allowed at any time when the instruction arrive. The hardware-in-loop simulation results indicate that the maximum power fluctuation during mode switching is only 2% under Short-Circuit Ratio (SCR) of 10, and remains below 2% even under power step changes. Under weak grid conditions (SCR = 2), the power fluctuation remains below 2%, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed backup-based smooth switching strategy.

title: Detecting DDPM-Manipulated Medical Images Using Contrastive Learning-based Pre-Training
authors: Turan Goktug Altundogan, Mehmet Karakose
abstract: Deep learning models can detect AI-generated images with high accuracy; however, classifying locally altered fake images is more difficult due to the low manipulation rate and the preservation of original patterns. This study proposes a novel CNN architecture for detecting images manipulated with tumor in-painting based on DDPM, which are difficult to distinguish by both the human eye and neural networks. The proposed architecture was trained with a two-stage training strategy that enhances performance, and a contrastive learning approach was used in the pre-training process. The default and two-stage training performances of the presented model were compared with pre-trained neural networks such as ResNet and MobileNet under the same conditions. In addition, comprehensive performance comparisons were carried out with existing deepfake production and manipulation detection methods in the literature. As a result of the evaluations, the proposed model demonstrated competitive performance, achieving an F1 score exceeding 99% under the evaluated DDPM-based manipulation setting.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Early Dysgraphia Screening and Support Through Handwriting
authors: Sonali Ridhorkar, Deep K Sonar, Kanak U Nigote, Gauri M Ganthade, Dhammdip Ganvir, Dhanshree A Tale
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Dysgraphia is a learning difficulty that significantly affects handwriting, spelling, and fine motor control, often leading to academic struggles and reduced confidence in children. Early identification is essential, yet current diagnostic practices are time- consuming, costly, and dependent on specialist availability, leaving many cases undetected, especially in under-resourced settings.This paper proposes a technology-assisted framework for early screening and support of dysgraphia using machine learning– driven handwriting analysis combined with real-time classroom observations. The system leverages mobile-based tools employing techniques such as edge detection and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to analyse handwriting patterns, while integrating insights from teachers and parents for a holistic evaluation.Beyond detection, the framework provides actionable, real- time feedback to support educators, families, and clinicians in developing personalized learning strategies, classroom adjustments, and ongoing progress tracking. By making screening accessible, cost- effective, and scalable, the approach reduces reliance on clinical resources and promotes inclusive learning environments. Ultimately, it empowers children with dysgraphia to receive timely interventions, fostering both academic achievement and social confidence from the earliest stages of education.

title: The Evolution of Text Summarization: Advancements and Challenges in Low-Resource Languages
authors: Asma Begum, D. Lakshmi Padmaja
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: The rapid development of digital content in Hindi has intensified the need for efficient Automatic Text Summarization (ATS) systems. However, the development of such tools for Hindi remains substantially underdeveloped compared to English, primarily because of the language's linguistic complexity and the critical scarcity of resources. This comprehensive survey synthesizes existing research to map the evolution of Hindi text summarization from early extractive methods employing statistical and graph-based techniques to contemporary neural approaches leveraging fine-tuned multilingual Large Language Models (LLMs) such as mBART, mT5, and IndicBART. Our analysis reveals that while these advanced models show significant promise, their performance is consistently constrained by fundamental challenges, including the lack of high-quality training datasets, limited availability of specialized NLP tools, and the absence of Hindi-specific pretrained models. The paper systematically identifies these research gaps. We conclude that future progress in the field is contingent upon a concerted effort to build large-scale benchmarks, develop optimized models for Hindi's unique grammatical structure, and create more robust evaluation frameworks, thereby enabling the creation of reliable summarization tools for the most regularly spoken languages in the world.

title: Multi-parameter Fusion Diagnosis and Maintenance Optimization for Electricity Metering Terminals Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Jin Li, Shaolei Zhai, Yong Chen, Shaohua Jiang, Shijun Xiong, Zhiming Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Intelligent inspection of low-voltage distribution networks (IVDN) imposes extremely high demands on the real-time performance and reliability of fault detection. Addressing the pain points such as the difficulty in assessing the quality of electricity metering terminal network construction and low operational efficiency, this paper proposes an on-site fault detection and maintenance framework that integrates multi-parameter semantic communication and deep reinforcement learning. A portable maintenance device is developed, integrating HPLC carrier, RS485 wired, and GPRS wireless communication interfaces to achieve multi-dimensional status perception of the terminal group. A health scoring model based on multi-parameter fusion and a hierarchical diagnostic strategy are constructed, and an Optimized Reinforcement Q-Network (ORQN) algorithm is adopted to dynamically coordinate the allocation of diagnostic resources. Furthermore, terminal multi-parameter iteration technology is studied, realizing one-click device configuration through protocol adaptation and rapid import methods. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme can significantly reduce detection delay, with a delay improvement percentage of 98.5%.

title: Image Feature Extraction for Sensory Pathway Analysis in AI-Generated Virtual Influencer Content
authors: Nikita R Nadkarni, Shashikant Patil
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Virtual influencers (VIs) do increasingly shape digital consumer behaviour, yet not much is known about the translation of their sensory characteristics into credibility and purchase intention. This research presents a two-phases approach of mixed-methods research whereby human sensory perception is linked to computational multimodal analysis to identify the drivers of persuasion through proximal (PSC), distal (DSC), and synesthetic (SYN). In Phase 1 (N = 250 Gen Z; 848 observations), survey-based evaluations revealed that PSC and SYN significantly increased credibility which emerged as a dominant predictor of purchase intention in support of the S-O- R pathway shown in digital persuasion research [42], [3]. In Phase 2, a multimodal pipeline extracted ~110 visual, motion, facial and audio features from these same VI posts, showing that human sensory judgments are represented by objective cues including motion coherence, luminance stability, spectral brightness, and color warmth. Integrating both phases, it can be seen that credibility is the psychological medium through which the psychological impact of sensory realism on behavioural intent is assumed to be stronger, with DSC making the small contribution.The novelty of this study is to empirically link subjective sensory processing and objective multimodal signals, providing a unified framework for assessing the efficacy of the VI and developing the theories of virtual persuasion through credibility, multimodal integration and sensory realism.

title: Carbon Emission Allocation Strategy for Power System Source-Load Based on System Dynamics
authors: Chunxia Wei, Fan Ye, Lu Xie, Ye Li, Feng Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Inefficient allocation of carbon responsibility in power systems impedes effective decarbonization. To address this, this paper proposes a source-load carbon emission allocation strategy based on System Dynamics (SD). First, a causal loop diagram is constructed to model the interactions within the system. Next, an incentive-based allocation model is formulated with distinct objective functions, where the generation side minimizes generation, investment, and incentive costs, while the load side minimizes electricity procurement and carbon trading costs. This model explicitly addresses the conflicts regarding carbon allowances between the source and load sides. Case studies on a modified IEEE 9-bus system validate the approach. The results demonstrate that the proposed strategy effectively coordinates source-load interactions to promote system-wide carbon emission reduction.

title: Sliding-Window Spectrum Interpolation FFT for Real-Time Harmonic Estimation in Smart Metering Applications
authors: Muhammed Rafi C K, Jiju K, Aby Joseph
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The rapid growth in the penetration of renewable energy sources and prosumer-based distributed generation has led to heightened harmonic distortion and wide off-nominal frequency variations, posing challenges to conventional power quality monitoring techniques. This paper presents a real-time harmonic estimation algorithm based on a 4096-point sliding-window Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), sampled at 20.4 kHz, utilizing a Hamming window with double-point interpolation. The proposed approach provides reasonable frequency resolution and accurately estimates harmonic components up to the 50th order, withstanding variations in grid frequency. Comparative analysis with other FFT windowing and interpolation methods including Hann, Blackman, Kaiser, and Flat Top--demonstrates that the proposed Hamming-based approach offers superior trade-offs between frequency accuracy, spectral leakage reduction, and computational efficiency. Sliding-window averaging further enhances temporal stability and noise robustness. The method complies with IEC 61000-4-7 for harmonic measurement and supports long-term harmonic monitoring as outlined in IEEE 519:2014. Simulation results in MATLAB Simulink validate the algorithm’s accuracy and demonstrate its potential for implementation in real-time embedded platforms such as DSPs and FPGAs for power quality analysis in dynamic, renewable-rich grid environments.

title: Autonomous Legged Mobile Manipulation for Lunar Surface Operations via Constrained Reinforcement Learning*
authors: Alvaro Belmonte-Baeza, Miguel Cazorla, Gabriel J. García, Carlos J. Pérez-Del-Pulgar, Jorge Pomares
abstract: Robotics plays a pivotal role in planetary science and exploration, where autonomous and reliable systems are crucial due to the risks and challenges inherent to space environments. The establishment of permanent lunar bases demands robotic platforms capable of navigating and manipulating in the harsh lunar terrain. While wheeled rovers have been the mainstay for planetary exploration, their limitations in unstructured and steep terrains motivate the adoption of legged robots, which offer superior mobility and adaptability. This paper introduces a constrained reinforcement learning framework designed for autonomous quadrupedal mobile manipulators operating in lunar environments. The proposed framework integrates whole-body locomotion and manipulation capabilities while explicitly addressing critical safety constraints, including collision avoidance, dynamic stability, and power efficiency, in order to ensure robust performance under lunar-specific conditions, such as reduced gravity and irregular terrain. Experimental results demonstrate the framework’s effectiveness in achieving precise 6D task-space end-effector pose tracking, achieving an average positional accuracy of 4 cm and orientation accuracy of 8.1 degrees. The system consistently respects both soft and hard constraints, exhibiting adaptive behaviors optimized for lunar gravity conditions. This work effectively bridges adaptive learning with essential mission-critical safety requirements, paving the way for advanced autonomous robotic explorers for future lunar missions.

title: Electromechanical Transient Modeling of Grid-Forming Converters with Fault Current Limiting Strategy
authors: Yujun Zhou, Jiawen Cao, Dongbao Lv, Changgang Li, Meng Liu
abstract: This paper proposes techniques for modeling grid-forming (GFM) converters in the electromechanical transient mode. Through mathematical transformation and structural reformulation, outer loop control strategies, including droop control and virtual synchronous generator (VSG) control, are integrated into a generic model, enabling a flexible transition between control modes. To simplify modeling complexity, the high-frequency dynamics of the current and voltage control loops in the electromagnetic transient (EMT) model are neglected, leading to a grid interface structure with an internal voltage source in series with a virtual impedance. To enhance the applicability of the model under fault conditions, an internal volt-age reconstruction-based current limiting method is proposed, enabling fast output current limitation. The proposed model has been implemented in open-source simulation toolkit STEPS and validated against the phasor model in PowerWorld (PW) Simulator and the EMT model in Simulink. Finally, two cases are tested to confirm the dynamic accuracy and computational efficiency of the proposed model and the impact of the current limiting strategy on the system.

title: Predicting Caloric Intake based on Rice Consumption and Age Demographics using Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Rahunathan L, Arun G, Suhana Nafais A, Monika Sri M, Sreenithi G, Subash Chandra Bose A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Estimation of the daily caloric intake of a person, which plays a very significant role in the context of nutrition and health, particularly in the region where rice acts as the primary food material. By understanding the pattern of consumption of rice, this research relies on the use of Machine Learning models in order to develop an accurate estimate of a person’s daily caloric intake.A real-time dataset with 385 individual records was collected from the participants, with some key features such as the age group, gender, rice consumption frequency, how many cups of rice they typically eat on average, how each individual described their rice consumption, and other lifestyle variables. The impact of the staple food rice that is consumed by the people on the daily calorie intake is an important factor to enhance nutrition and human health, especially in the socio-economic population where rice forms a significant staple food. The overall performance of the models were assessed using Standard (Accuracy, F1-Score) metrics. The paper discusses the application of machine learning in predicting the individual caloric intake using the patterns of rice consumption and the age demographic. Six algorithms Logistic Regression, Decision tree, random forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost and Ensemble Methods were trained and compared based on conventional evaluation metrics. Among all of the six machine learning models XGBoost (accuracy = 0.96, F1- score = 0.9613, precision = 0.9648) provided the most accurate predictions, and in the next place, the Random Forest (accuracy = 0.95, F1-score = 0.9555). The findings of the caloric intake indicate that ensemble models of learning have the potential of capturing the multifactorial correlation between rice consumption, age as well as calorie consumption, physical activity, quantity of rice etc., In general, the paper presents the role of machine learning in assisting in smarter and data-driven nutrition tracking and custom dietary planning.

title: A One-Stage CNN Object Detection Framework for Automated Classification of Otoscopic Ear Diseases
authors: Karen Althea Aquino, Philip Reginson F. Chavez, Patricia Mae G. Dela Cruz, Lysa V. Comia
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of ear diseases is essential for preventing complications such as hearing loss and chronic infection, yet traditional otoscopic examination is subjective and prone to variability, particularly in resource-limited settings. This study presents an automated diagnostic system using a one-stage convolutional object detection model trained on an augmented Otoscopy Image Dataset covering five categories: Acute Otitis Media, Chronic Otitis Media, Cerumen Impaction, Myringosclerosis, and Normal. The model achieved strong performance (mAP@0.5 = 0.95, precision = 0.95, recall = 0.93) with near-perfect precision–recall characteristics and stable confidence-based metrics. Qualitative evaluation showed highly reliable detection of visually distinct conditions such as Acute Otitis Media and Cerumen Impaction, while subtle pathologies like Myringosclerosis remained challenging. Deployed via a web-based interface on Hugging Face Spaces, the system enabled real-time classification of both internal and external otoscopic images. These results demonstrate the feasibility of one-stage object detection for automated otoscopic disease diagnosis and highlight its potential to improve diagnostic accuracy, consistency, and accessibility in clinical and remote healthcare environments.

title: Research and Application of Early Warning Method for Jamming Fault of Steam Turbine Inlet Valves in New Power System
authors: Yakui Li, Junfeng Qi, Penghui Jia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: In the context of high proportion of renewable energy integration, the Jamming fault of steam turbine inlet valves (main steam valve, main regulating valve, etc.) is difficult to be identified early due to the deep peak shaving operation of coal-fired units. This paper proposes a force-displacement collaborative perception early warning method that integrates the valve stem-liner contact dynamics mechanism and 1D-CNN data-driven model. By non-intrusively deploying high-precision force and displacement sensors on the valve stem surface, the synchronous online monitoring of "axial force-valve stem displacement" throughout the entire process of the main valve operation of the steam turbine is achieved. Based on the multi-physical field coupling mechanism of heat, force and chemistry, a valve stem-liner contact dynamics model is established to extract a four-dimensional sensitive feature set covering force-displacement time domain, frequency domain, time-frequency domain and coupling features. A lightweight 1D-CNN network is designed to achieve precise early fault identification. The engineering verification results on a 660 MW supercritical unit show that this method can issue high-accuracy Jamming early warnings, which is 3.2 times more effective than the traditional opening deviation method, providing a new technical path for valve health management in the context of deep peak shaving units

title: ResUNet-X: Next-Generation Residual U-Net for Accurate Cardiac Imaging
authors: T Rajani, Kishor Kumar Gajula, Mohammed Ali Shaik, Yerram Sneha, Ashok Kumar Vootla, Kokkula Shiva Prasad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Cine MRI is capable of quantitative evaluation of ventricular anatomy and, therefore, cardiac findings rely upon adequate separation of cardiac structures. We present a slightly adapted architecture Residual U-Net (ResUNet-X) in this text, which contains a ResNet-34 encoder and attention-sensitive skip layer in the question of improving convergence together with boundary interpretation. The proposed model on the ACDC benchmark using splits by patient yields a mean Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) of 0.896, Hausdorff Distance (HD95) of 1.8 mm and Intersection-over-Union (IoU) of 0.85, a 3.0 percent higher mean DSC and 36 percent smaller Hausdorff distance than a baseline U-Net. Having 9.1M parameters and 23.4 GFLOPs, this lightweight architecture gives the highest segmentation accuracy and can be inferred in less time, which makes it appropriate to be used in clinical settings with limited resources.

title: Spiking Neural Networks Outperform Convolutional Models in Voice-Based Heart Failure Detection
authors: T.Jothilakshmi, K.Sathiyamurthy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Heart failure (HF) continues to be one of the world’s most prevalent causes of hospitalization and premature death, highlighting the urgent need for diagnostic approaches that are accurate, non-invasive, and cost-efficient. Recent advances suggest that speech carries physiological signatures of cardiopulmonary function, enabling voice-based biomarkers to act as indicators of disease progression. This study presents a comparative framework that evaluates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) for detecting HF from vocal acoustics. A curated dataset of patient speech samples was pre-processed to extract Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), log-Mel spectrograms, and prosodic features capturing rhythm and intensity variations. Both CNN and SNN models were trained under identical conditions with ten-fold cross-validation to ensure fairness and reproducibility. While CNN achieved 91.2 % accuracy, 89.5 % sensitivity, and ROC-AUC = 0.93, the SNN consistently surpassed it with 94.6 % accuracy, 93.8 % sensitivity, and ROC-AUC = 0.96, alongside a 43 % reduction in inference latency and nearly 40 % lower energy consumption. SHAP-based explainability revealed that spectral and prosodic cues jointly influenced model predictions, and ablation experiments confirmed the advantage of multimodal feature fusion. These findings demonstrate that SNNs combine clinical interpretability with energy-aware computation, making them ideal for edge-AI deployment in low-resource healthcare environments. The work establishes SNNs as a biologically inspired and computationally efficient paradigm for real-time voice-based cardiovascular screening.

title: HygioFog: A Fog Based Hand Sanitization System with Hand Surface Contamination Detection
authors: Anisha Nimbalkar, Tejal Tale, Atharva Tayde, Rahul Agrawal, Nekita Chavhan Morris, Chetan Dhule
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: One of the best and most reliable ways to hinder the transmission of infectious diseases is the use of proper hand hygiene. Conventional hand sanitizer dispensers of either manual pumps or touchless automatic sensors, however, have no means of checking if the user’s hands are genuinely contaminated or adequately cleaned. This makes the system less reliable and less inspiring for the user, increasing the potential for hygiene errors. To solve the problem, we have developed HygioFog, an intelligent hand sanitization system that incorporates a fog based automatic sanitizer delivery system and ultraviolet (UV-A) detection of contamination on the surface of the hand. HygioFog significantly minimized sanitizer wastage, boosted user confidence by providing visual proof of hygiene, and showed outstanding accuracy in contaminant detection

title: Performance Analysis of Incremental Relaying based Underlay Cognitive NOMA Networks with Partial Relay Selection
authors: Archana P, V P Harigovindan, Babu A V
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This research work analyzes the throughput performance of the incremental relaying (IR) based cognitive non-orthogonal multiple access (i.e., CNOMA) networks with partial relay selection (PRS) scheme, namely IR-CNOMA with PRS. We consider secondary base station to transfer information to the secondary users by using N decode and forward, half duplex (HD) user-assisted relay. We derive analytical expression for the system throughput and outage probability of the IR-CNOMA with PRS network, considering the interference threshold of the primary receiver (PR), imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC), and the maximum transmit power of the secondary nodes. Through simulation and numerical results, the effectiveness of the proposed scheme is assessed with traditional cooperative relaying CNOMA (CR-CNOMA) network with PRS and stochastic relay selection (SRS) scheme in terms of throughput and outage.

title: A Novel Hybrid Graph-Attention Summarization with Adaptive Ranking
authors: P.Neelakantan, N.Kaushik Rishi, Y.Chandhra Banu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Although automatic text summarization is one of the most difficult areas of natural language processing, current techniques are ineffective in handling long texts, and as such fail to consistently retain the information from longer documents while at the same time finding the right balance between extractive accuracy and abstractive readability. To address this problem, this research provides a new framework called Hybrid Graph-Attention Summarization (HGAS), which combines multi-scale feature extraction with adaptive sentence ordering to find the best solution to both issues. The HGAS framework integrates, under a unified theoretical model, graph-based representations of sentences, multi-head attention, and dynamically optimized thresholds. The HGAS framework has three stages: feature extraction of sentences using a modified version of TextRank with semantic embedding, sentence weighting based on attention and scale, and adaptive abstractive refinement through a transformer-based generator. Experiments on the standard datasets have shown a large improvement over the other state of the art methods, reaching ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, and ROUGE-L scores of.487, .234, and.441 respectively, surpassing those of other methods by 8.3%, 12.7% and 9.8%. The HGAS framework reduces redundancy, maintains the semantic coherence of the document, and is computationally efficient, making it suitable for use in many practical applications.

title: Study on Foam Jet Range of Compressed Air Foam Monitors Under Wind Effects for Power System Fire Protection Scenarios
authors: Tiantian Tan, Yi Guo, Jiaqing Zhang, Gexin Wu, Bo Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This study integrates field-scale jet experiments with numerical simulations to investigate the discharge performance of compressed air foam monitors under varying wind speeds, focusing on the application demands of ultra-high voltage (UHV) substation fire protection. A full-scale 1:1 simulation model was established using Fluent software, and the optimal mesh strategy was determined through mesh independence analysis. The simulation results indicate that under both full headwind (0°) and 30° crosswind conditions, the foam jet distance decreases linearly with increasing wind speed. Additionally, the peak ground impact pressure and foam volume fraction both decline as wind speed increases. By comparing the simulation results with experimental data at wind speeds of 0 m/s and 17.3 m/s, the relative errors were found to be within the ranges of 0.44%–4.97% and 2.84%–9.16%, respectively, validating the reliability of the simulation method. Furthermore, numerical simulation extended the wind speed range beyond the limitations of experiments, enabling a systematic evaluation of the variation law of foam jet range. Finally, a jet distance prediction model tailored for power system complex wind field environments was established based on the simulation data, providing theoretical support for the engineering design and application of compressed air foam fire-fighting systems in complex wind field environments.

title: Robotic push-based self-assembly of hexagonal tiles for space applications
authors: Chayada Thidrasamee, Sabine Hauert, Lucy Berthoud
abstract: Assembling large-scale infrastructure in space presents significant technical and logistical challenges. Traditionally, this process involves dividing complex systems into smaller modules, launching them separately, and assembling them in orbit often relying on sophisticated and costly robotic systems. This paper introduces a robotic push-based self-assembly approach to create arrays of hexagonal tiles. Such tiles and arrays are prevalent in space structures, and in particular in the upcoming space-based solar power stations (SBSP). This design reduces mechanical complexity by employing a passive “pusher” mechanism. The system enables autonomous alignment and propulsion of hexagonal tiles into desired configurations without the use of advanced robotics, offering a simple yet scalable geometric arrangement suitable for large-scale space structures. Experimental validation was performed using a low-friction test-bed (an air hockey table) to approximate microgravity conditions. Results indicate that the pusher mechanism successfully enables directional self-assembly of hexagonal tiles in a linear configuration. Building on this outcome, future work will focus on combining multiple linear arrays to form larger, scalable hexagonal assemblies for space infrastructure applications.

title: Prediction Model of Energy Economic Operation Based on Multi-source Big Data Fusion and Deep Learning
authors: Xiaoyu Shao, Mao Zhang, Jianxiong Jia, Xue Li, Jie Ding
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: With the increasing importance of energy economy in the global economic structure, accurate prediction of its operation situation has become the key. The purpose of this article is to build an efficient and accurate energy economic operation prediction model. Based on multi-source big data fusion technology, this article integrates multi-dimensional data such as energy production, consumption, price and macro-economy, and uses the LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory Network) model in DL (Deep Learning) to deeply explore the complex laws of energy economic system. By collecting the data related to energy economy from 2010 to 2025, the experiment is divided into training set, verification set and test set. The results show that the model is excellent in predicting energy consumption, energy price index and other indicators, with MAE (Mean Absolute Error) of 0.35, MAPE (Mean Absolute Percentage Error) of 4.8%, and the determination coefficient R2 of 0.92. Compared with the traditional ARIMA model (Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average Model) and SVM (Support Vector Machine) model, this model has obvious advantages in various performance indexes, which provides strong theoretical support and technical guarantee for decision-making and development planning in the field of energy economy.

title: Searching Voltage Security Region Boundary of Power Systems Utilizing Holomorphic Embedding Method
authors: Menglei Zhi, Xue Li, Tao Jiang, Rufeng Zhang
abstract: Power system voltage security region (VSR) is an important tool for analyzing and evaluating the voltage security of power systems with the influence of randomness and uncertain factors. In order to realize the accurate and fast construction of VSR, a searching method for power system voltage security region boundary (VSR boundary, VSRB) based on Holomorphic Embedding Method (HEM) is proposed. The proposed method is analyzed and verified by IEEE-14 and IEEE-118 test systems. The results show that the proposed method can realize the accurate and fast construction of VSRB and enhance the online assessment and monitoring capability of static security of power system.

title: A View-Specific Few-Shot Comparison of Capsule Networks and Standard CNN Backbones on INbreast Mammography
authors: Khaldoon Alhusari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This work compares a basic Capsule Network (CapsNet) with three convolutional baselines (a compact CNN, VGG16, and ResNet50) for few-shot binary classification of INbreast mammograms. Experiments were conducted separately for craniocaudal (CC) and mediolateral-oblique (MLO) views. Few-shot setups with 10, 20, and 31 labeled samples per class were constructed and repeated across five stratified folds. The convolutional models were trained in TensorFlow/Keras (with transfer learning for VGG16 and ResNet50 using frozen backbones in the reported runs), while the CapsNet implementation was trained in PyTorch following the original routing-by-agreement formulation. Evaluation used per-fold validation and a single held-out test set, and all results are reported as mean ± standard deviation across folds. Across all views and shot regimes, the compact baseline CNN achieved the highest mean held-out test accuracy (CC: 0.702, 0.654, 0.707 for 10, 20, 31 shots; MLO: 0.571, 0.662, 0.648). CapsNet reached mid-range performance in several settings but did not surpass the compact CNN. ResNet50 (with a frozen backbone) showed lower accuracy and greater inter-fold variability. Overall, these findings suggest that in highly label-limited mammography tasks (under the specific configurations tested here) a well-regularized compact CNN or a lightweight classifier atop pretrained features remains a strong and dependable baseline. The capsule architecture merits further investigation with training strategies tuned specifically for low-data regimes. Code will be publicly released upon acceptance.

title: Deep Learning Warm Starts for Trajectory Optimization on the International Space Station
authors: Somrita Banerjee, Abhishek Cauligi, Marco Pavone
abstract: Trajectory optimization is a cornerstone of modern robot autonomy, enabling systems to compute trajectories and controls in real-time while respecting safety and physical constraints. However, it has seen limited usage in spaceflight applications due to its heavy computational demands that exceed the capability of most flight computers. In this work, we provide results on the first in-space demonstration of using machine learning-based warm starts for accelerating trajectory optimization for the Astrobee free-flying robot onboard the International Space Station (ISS). We formulate a data-driven optimal control approach that trains a neural network to learn the structure of the trajectory generation problem being solved using sequential convex programming (SCP). Onboard, this trained neural network predicts solutions for the trajectory generation problem and relies on using the SCP solver to enforce safety constraints for the system. Our trained network reduces the number of solver iterations required for convergence in cases including rotational dynamics by $60 \%$ and in cases with obstacles drawn from the training distribution of the warm start model by $50 \%$. This work represents a significant milestone in the use of learning-based control for spaceflight applications and a stepping stone for future advances in the use of machine learning for autonomous guidance, navigation, & control.

title: Deep Learning Architecture for Land-Cover Satellite Image Classification using Transfer Learning
authors: Prathmesh Kulkarni, Ojas Geet, Rachit Garg, Satishkumar Varma, Vinaya Sawant
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Satellite imagery-based automatic land-cover classification is critical to a range of applications from precision agriculture and environmental monitoring to urban planning. Conventional machine learning methods are frequently hindered by remote sensing data’s high dimensionality, heterogeneity, and noise. In this paper, an end-to-end, modular image classification system is introduced based on the EuroSAT dataset. It is hand-designed deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with residual connections and transfer learning from pre-trained models (VGG16, ResNet50). The proposed pipeline features include robust data ingestion, preprocessing, and corruption handling for faulty imagery and scales to model-comparison capabilities. The residual-augmented custom CNN is superior in classification accuracy to a straight CNN baseline, while transfer-learning models realize fast convergence and good generalization with minimal training data. The Streamlit-based web interface, coupled with Google Earth Engine, supports real-time classification, time-series analysis, and interactive visualizations, allowing non-expert users to study land-cover dynamics without a hitch. Through modular code design, model versioning at scale, and thorough evaluation metrics, our system tackles essential reproducibility, model selection, and deployment readiness issues in satellite image analysis.

title: From Decarbonization to Health Risks: Linking Renewable Energy Transition, Heatwave and Urban Health Resilience
authors: Zhixue Yang, Hongcai Zhang
abstract: Climate change is driving more frequent extreme heatwaves, which strain urban power grids and increase the risk of blackouts—exposing vulnerable populations to dangerous temperatures and heightened health risks. However, the link between urban health resilience and power grid operation, especially under growing renewable penetration, remains unclear. To address this, we develop a blackout-induced urban health risk assessment method that captures the interactions between heatwaves, power systems, and urban health. This approach combines a heat exposure–mortality model, a detailed power grid, and hourly simulations. Our findings suggest that, driven by grid decarbonization and population growth, blackout-related excess deaths could increase by a factor of 3.15 from 2030 to 2050, potentially contributing to over a 7% rise in annual mortality.

title: Hybrid STAR-RIS-Assisted Short Packet ISAC Systems: Transmission Paradigm and Resource Optimization
authors: Shuang Zhang, Wanming Hao, Gangcan Sun, Xingwang Li, Ning Wang, Bo Ai
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a key technology for improving spectrum efficiency and enabling intelligent wireless networks, yet its deployment in short-packet transmission scenarios faces significant challenges such as finite block-length (FBL) effects, channel estimation uncertainty, and limited coverage. To address these issues, this paper investigates a short-packet ISAC system assisted by a hybrid simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) and proposes a two-stage ISAC transmission paradigm. In Stage I, the hybrid STAR-RIS performs target direction-of-arrival estimation, and a closed-form expression of Cramér–Rao Bound (CRB) is derived to establish channel state information (CSI) uncertainty model based on CRB. Meanwhile, each user performs channel estimation locally and feeds results back to DFBS. In Stage II, the estimated CSI is utilized to jointly design resource allocation, and an optimization problem is formulated to maximize target illumination power under FBL and CSI uncertainty constraints. To tackle this strongly coupled non-convex problem, we develop a hierarchical solution strategy: the sensing duration is first determined via one-dimensional search, and then, an alternating optimization framework is employed to decouple the problem into DFBS beamforming and hybrid STAR-RIS coefficient optimization, where iterative algorithms based on semi-definite relaxation, semi-definite programming, and singular value decomposition are proposed to ultimately obtain a convergent optimal solution. Simulation results validate the fast convergence and superior performance of our proposed algorithm, reveal the inherent trade-off between the two stages under constrained resources, and demonstrate the importance of joint two-stage resource design assisted by hybrid STAR-RIS in enhancing short-packet ISAC system performance.

title: Ontology Engineering Framework for Space Systems Data Repository
authors: Guilherme Venticinque, Geilson Loureiro
abstract: This work proposes an Ontology Engineering Framework (OEF) for the development and exploitation of Space System Ontology (SSO), aimed at enabling information sharing among stakeholders throughout the entire system lifecycle. Over the past decade, Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) has been increasingly adopted in space projects; however, interoperability, particularly at the system level throughout the lifecycle, remains a major challenge due to the lack of a shared vocabulary. Data reuse is also affected by the lack of semantic compatibility, especially in customer–supplier relationships during product transition processes. Consequently, the definition of a common conceptual data model for space systems and systems engineering is essential to support machine-readable and processable data exchange. Recently, ontologies have gained attention due to their ability to capture formalized definitions in a tool-independent logical language, enabling consistency checking, automated reasoning, and knowledge inference beyond what is explicitly stated. What differentiates an ontology from an application-specific data-model is that it requires explicit semantics to be understood and used across various applications, and it is shareable among all stakeholders. This capability enables the interoperability among software applications. In addition, Semantic Web technologies provide an open infrastructure for intelligent agents, allowing distributed data to be interconnected through reference ontologies. The proposed OEF provides high-level guidance, including principles, best practices, and scope boundaries, to cover all shareable aspects of space systems. It supports data integration from heterogeneous sources across the system lifecycle through ontologies aligned with Semantic Web technologies. To demonstrate the proposed OEF and validate the feasibility of ontology-based data sharing, a module of the SSO - the wiring harness ontology (WHO) - was developed and validated using real satellite project data. In conclusion, the proposed OEF can serve the basis for a formal representation of space systems, promoting the interoperability among tools and promoting the reuse of engineering information

title: Evaluating the Effectiveness of Q-Learning in Ant Colony Optimization for the Traveling Salesman Problem
authors: Ameer Mohammed, Reham Kablaoui, Yousef Alfezea
abstract: The Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) is a fundamental combinatorial optimization challenge where exact solvers face exponential time complexity. Consequently, metaheuristics are widely employed to identify high-quality solutions within feasible timeframes. In this work, we investigate the added utility of integrating Reinforcement Learning into swarm intelligence by proposing QL-ACO, a hybrid framework that naturally embeds Q-learning into the Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) metaheuristic. The primary objective is to assess how Q-learning affects the trade-off between exploration and exploitation by simultaneously updating Q-values and pheromone trails. This hybrid search is further refined using a 2-Opt local search mechanism. We evaluate the proposed approach on standard TSPLIB benchmark instances to analyze both solution optimality and algorithmic efficiency. The results demonstrate an average optimality gap of 2.19%, which is comparable to state-of-the-art hybrid algorithms. Moreover, to verify the specific added value of the reinforcement learning component, we conduct a comprehensive ablation study. This analysis reveals that the inclusion of Q-learning significantly accelerates convergence compared to the baseline ACO (by up to 12%), confirming its effectiveness in guiding the search process toward high-quality solutions more efficiently.

title: Unilateral Assistance Using a Lightweight and Back-drivable Hip Exoskeleton
authors: Sue Min Park, Suncheol Kwon, Youngjin Na
abstract: While lightweight and highly back-drivable exoskeletons enable natural interaction by minimizing resistance to voluntary motion, few studies have investigated the resulting combined physiological and biomechanical effects of unilateral hip torque assistance. This paper presents a hip exoskeleton designed to provide unilateral torque assistance during walking to investigate the effects of unilateral actuation on lower-limb muscle responses. The device employs a quasi-direct-drive actuator and a simplified angle-based control scheme to deliver torque in phase with the hip flexion. Although the mechanical frame was bilaterally symmetrical, only the right hip joint was actuated to evaluate the effects of unilateral assistance while maintaining inertial balance and low mechanical impedance on the unpowered side. Three healthy participants walked on a treadmill under different load (0, 1, and 2 kg) and assistive torque (0, 6, and 9 N·m) conditions. Subsequently, surface electromyography and motion capture data were recorded simultaneously. The activity of the rectus femoris muscle consistently decreased, showing over 30% reduction compared to that observed during unassisted walking under loaded conditions. Furthermore, postural deviation was measurably reduced by up to 46.8% during assisted conditions without hindering the natural swing. These findings demonstrate that a lightweight unilaterally actuated exoskeleton can provide evident physiological benefits even with a minimal control strategy. Notably, moderate torque (6 N·m) proved to be sufficient for yielding significant benefits, in some cases outperforming higher torque levels (9 N·m). This suggests that optimizing assistance levels based on user-specific needs is crucial for maximizing walking comfort and supporting rehabilitation in future designs.

title: Contrastive Learning for Modeling Sensitive Attributes in Fairness-Aware Recommendation
authors: Guoyang Wu, Jiayang Sun, Shenghao Liu, Xianjun Deng, Yuanyuan He, Jing Wang, Laurence T. Yang, Philip S. Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Recently, the research on fairness in recommendation systems has garnered widespread attention. Moreover, numerous fair recommendation models have been developed for scenarios with limited sensitive information, thereby alleviating the issue of missing sensitive information. However, the performance of these methods still tends to decline significantly when sensitive attributes are extremely scarce. In this paper, we propose FairCL, a novel fair recommendation framework designed to perform effectively under limited sensitive attribute information. FairCL features a contrastive learning-based sensitive attribute encoder that can be integrated with existing fair recommendation algorithms. By leveraging both collaborative information and item side information, we predict unknown sensitive attributes and apply contrastive learning for sensitive attribute modeling. Furthermore, we theoretically demonstrate how FairCL can be integrated with mutual information-based and adversarial learning-based fairness algorithms. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets show that FairCL significantly enhances fairness, even when only a small portion of users' sensitive attributes are known. The code and data are at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CL-for-FairRec-000A/.

title:
Non-Contact Enhanced Photoacoustic C
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    H
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Gas Sensor Based On Multiple excitation
authors: Xuan Liu, Zhonghai Zhang, Xinru Zhang, Kai Cui, Chenggang Liu, Xiaoqian Li, Zongliang Wang
abstract: This paper develops a non-contact enhanced photoacoustic C₂H₂ gas sensor based on repeated laser excitation for trace gas analysis. Two high-reflectivity mirrors are placed on both sides of the H-shaped photoacoustic cavity, with a layer of polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) film (10μm thick) coupled at the center of the resonant cavity, thereby constructing a non-contact enhanced photoacoustic cavity. This design effectively overcomes the limitations associated with detection elements being exposed to corrosive gases and particulate matter over extended periods. For the non-contact C₂H₂ detection, a microphone-PDMS film combination is employed, where the film acts as a physical barrier isolating acetylene from the microphone. This approach enhances detection safety while extending the detection element's lifespan. The photoacoustic signal is amplified fivefold compared to single excitation. Measurements indicate a minimum detection limit of 183.89 ppbv with an acquisition time of 172 seconds.

title: Power Quality Improvement in Nonlinear Lighting Load Applications Using Shunt Active Power Filter Control
authors: Mohamed Hajjej, Lassaad Sbita, Kuntal Mandal, Abdelali El Aroudi
abstract: This paper develops an enhanced current control approach for a Three-Phase Four-Wire (3P4W) split-capacitor Shunt Active Power Filter (SAPF) to mitigate current harmonics produced by Compact Fluorescent Lamps (CFLs). A Fuzzy Hysteresis Current Controller (FHCC) and a Modulated Hysteresis Current Controller (MHCC) are proposed to provide the triggering signals for the Insulated-Gate Bipolar Transistors (IGBTs) of the SAPF to ensure improved switching performance and reduced steady-state error near the Shifted Zero-Crossing Area (S-ZCA). The Synchronous Reference Frame (SRF) power theory is adopted for reference current generation. The effectiveness of both techniques, FHCC and MHCC, is evaluated through simulations inMATLAB<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup>/Simulink and validated in real time using the Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) platform, especially with the Typhoon HIL-402 device. As both techniques act exclusively for switching gate signal generation techniques and not reference-current extraction methods, the structures are general in nature and are not load-dependent. Comparative analysis with the conventional hysteresis current controller (HCC) demonstrates significant improvements in the reduction of Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) to 1.08% and the overall improvement of power quality without notches in waveforms near zero crossings. The results confirm the robustness and efficiency of the proposed MHCC strategy in the HIL implementation.

title: Fingerprinting Localization based on Super Resolution CSI Features
authors: Zekeriya Uykan, Hanan Al-Tous, Olav Tirkkonen, Riku Jäntti
abstract: We consider fingerprint localization based on super-resolution features for single point massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. We consider k-nearest neighbours (kNN), weighted k-nearest neighbors (W-kNN) as well as neural network based localization. The radio environment is characterized by several multipath components. We investigate several channel state information (CSI) features. We study the localization performance when the CSI feature includes absolute delays, relative delays, and no-delay information of multipath components. Simulation results based on the DeepMIMO data-set for a challenging non line-of-sight environment show that a) treating the angle and delay separately and formulating the normalized polar feature outperforms other CSI features from the literature, b) the neural network based fingerprint localization is remarkably superior to the W-kNN based fingerprinting localization using the same features, and c) the best localization performance is obtained when absolute delay information is available. Localization accuracy with few centimeters resolution is achieved by the proposed feature and based on neural network localization, less than 30 cm average localization error can be achieved based on relative delays with the angles and powers of multipath components, and less than 60 cm average localization error can be achieved for the no-delay case (i.e., power and angle of arrival of MPCs).

title: Multi-Source Assessment of Machine Learning Models and Atmospheric Correction Products for Chlorophyll- Retrieval in Meso&#x2011;Eutrophic Waters: Application to Hamilton Harbour, an Area of Concern in Lake Ontario, Canada_supp1-3676135.docx
authors: Ali Reza Shahvaran

title: Agile Flight Through Tilted Spaces Using a Single-Servo Tilt-Rotor Quadrotor
authors: Yi Luo, Xiao Cao, Minghao Lu, Nan Chen, Peng Lu
abstract: Agile flight through collapsed buildings primarily demands robust and independent control of attitude and position under unpredictable environmental disturbances and complex aerodynamic interactions. Prior studies improve underactuated quadrotors by adding actuators to enable independent pitch or roll control, but introduce control complexity and redundant mechanical load. To overcome these limitations, we achieve five-dimensional (x, y, z, pitch, and yaw) agile flight through a unified tilt mechanism codesigned with an adaptive physics-informed neural network (PINN)-based disturbance rejection controller for aggressive and precise maneuvers. The developed methodology achieves analytical pitch-axis decoupling and incorporates differential flatness-based trajectory planning to guarantee precise and agile flight despite actuator coupling and environmental disturbances. Furthermore, a robust PINN observer enables real-time estimation of external and internal torques, significantly enhancing disturbance rejection in the presence of model uncertainties. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves high tracking accuracy and robust performance under aggressive maneuvers, successfully traversing a 1.2-m-wide tunnel tilted at 60<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{\circ}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> with an overall position root-mean-square error of 0.26 m, which highlights its strong applicability to inspection tasks in complex environments.

title: Research on Human Action Recognition Based on WiFi CSI Using Improved Parallel Neural Network
authors: Guoqing Chen, Lixing Wang, Qingxu Deng, Wei Fang, Daqing Chen, Chao Shi, Jingyu Dong
abstract: Device-free human activity recognition (HAR) using WiFi Channel State Information (CSI) offers a low-cost and privacy-preserving sensing solution. However, existing deep learning approaches often rely on generic architectures that underutilize multi-domain physical features and incur prohibitive computational overhead for edge devices. In this paper, we propose a lightweight, task-oriented parallel neural network tailored for WiFi sensing. Specifically, high-resolution Doppler Frequency Shift (DFS) features are explicitly extracted to capture fine-grained human kinematics. These are jointly modeled alongside temporal and spatial dynamics using a three-branch architecture comprising attention-enhanced bidirectional GRUs and a channel-attention CNN module. Furthermore, an adaptive attention-gated mechanism is introduced for optimal multidomain feature fusion. Extensive experiments on the public UT-HAR dataset yield a state-of-the-art accuracy of 98.49%. Additional evaluations on the highly challenging, cross-domain Widar dataset demonstrate an accuracy of 83.33%, substantially outperforming classical and heavy-weight vision baselines (e.g., ResNet18 and ViT). Requiring only 4.92 M parameters and 98.72 M FLOPs, the proposed framework achieves a superior trade-off between recognition precision and computational efficiency, validating its immense potential for practical, resource-constrained WiFi sensing applications.

title: TalkingEyes: Pluralistic Speech-Driven 3D Eye Gaze Animation_supp2-3674699.mp4
authors: Xuan Cheng
abstract: Although significant progress has been made in the field of speech-driven 3D facial animation recently, the speech-driven animation of an indispensable facial component, eye gaze, has been overlooked by recent research. This is primarily due to the weak correlation between speech and eye gaze, as well as the scarcity of audio-gaze data, making it very challenging to generate 3D eye gaze motion from speech alone. In this paper, we propose a novel data-driven method which can generate diverse 3D eye gaze motions in harmony with the speech. To achieve this, we firstly construct an audio-gaze dataset that contains about 14 hours of audio-mesh sequences featuring high-quality eye gaze motion, head motion and facial motion simultaneously. The motion data is acquired by performing lightweight eye gaze fitting and face reconstruction on videos from existing audio-visual datasets. We then tailor a novel speech-to-motion translation framework in which the head motions and eye gaze motions are jointly generated from speech but are modeled in two separate latent spaces. This design stems from the physiological knowledge that the rotation range of eyeballs is less than that of head. Through mapping the speech embedding into the two latent spaces, the difficulty in modeling the weak correlation between speech and non-verbal motion is thus attenuated. Finally, our TalkingEyes, integrated with a speech-driven 3D facial motion generator, can synthesize eye gaze motion, eye blinks, head motion and facial motion collectively from speech. Qualitative and quantitative evaluations, along with a perceptual user study, demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method in generating diverse and natural 3D eye gaze motions from speech. The project page of this paper is: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://lkjkjoiuiu.github.io/TalkingEyes_Home/</uri>.

title: Surface Offsetting: A Survey from Geometric Construction to Neural Implicit Representations
authors: Xuyi Zhao, Yuanrui Yang, Jianmin Wang, Enya Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Surface offsetting is a fundamental geometric operation in computer-aided design, manufacturing, robotics, and computational physics. Despite its conceptual simplicity, generating offset surfaces robustly and efficiently for complex and irregular geometries remains a persistent challenge, hindered by self-intersections, topological inconsistencies, and feature degradation. This paper reviews recent advances in the field of surface offsetting, offering a structured overview of the evolution of techniques from classical constructive methods to contemporary neural implicit representations. Our survey addresses a gap in the literature, as prior foundational reviews over the past two decades in this field focused primarily on parametric methods. We introduce a taxonomy that organizes existing algorithms into five principal classes: Constructive, Spatial Discretization, Optimization-based, Field-based, and Learning-based approaches. An analysis of 46 representative algorithms reveals trade-offs in algorithm design: achieving both geometric accuracy and topological correctness proves difficult, and representation choices introduce inherent complexity constraints. We identify open problems including offset generation for surfaces with open boundaries, preservation of thin features, and resolution of self-intersections in concave regions. These challenges point toward promising research directions, including extending classical offset theory to non-manifold and open-boundary domains, developing scalable geometric predicates, and designing hybrid neural representations that disentangle distance-field smoothness from geometric sharpness.

title: Are They All Good? Evaluating the Quality of CoTs in LLM-based Code Generation
authors: Binquan Zhang, Li Zhang, Zhiwen Luo, Yuxin Du, Fang Liu, Song Wang, Lin Shi
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive performance in code generation, particularly when augmented with chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting techniques. They break down requirements into intermediate reasoning steps, which act as design rationales to guide LLMs in writing code like human programmers. Thus, the quality of these steps is crucial for ensuring the correctness and reliability of the generated code. However, the specific factors influencing the quality of CoT generated by LLMs remain largely unexplored. To what extent can we trust the thoughts generated by LLMs? How good are they? This paper empirically explores the external and internal factors of why LLMs generate unsatisfactory CoTs by analyzing 1,023 failed code samples on two widely used code generation benchmarks. We also evaluate their impact on code generation performance by analyzing 210 CoT-code pairs and refining the unsatisfied CoTs by prompting LLMs. Our study yields the following findings: 1) Among the factors affecting CoT quality, external factors account for 53.60%, primarily including unclear requirements and lack of contextual information. Internal factors make up 40.10%, mainly due to inconsistencies between CoT and prompts caused by LLMs’ misunderstanding of the instructions. 2) Despite CoT being correct, 18.5% of the generated code still contains errors. This is primarily due to LLMs failing to follow instructions, leading to inconsistencies between CoT and the code. Additionally, we found that even when the code is correct, there is an 11.90% chance that the CoT contains errors. 3) Our further research on refining the low-quality CoTs reveals that LLMs can improve CoT, especially when providing detailed CoT problem information.
