Our findings shed light on the underlying issues that hinder the effectiveness of CoT in LLM-based code generation, offering valuable insights for enhancing both the reasoning process and the overall reliability of code generation.

title: Optimal Power Allocation Strategy for Hydrogen Production System of ALK-PEM Electrolyzer Considering Second-level Response Characteristics
authors: Yumin Zhang, Nianshang Zhang, Xingquan Ji, Zhaoyang Dong, Xiaoming Dong, Fushuan Wen
abstract: Most electrolyzer scheduling models operate at minute-to-hour resolutions and thus overlook the second-level dynamics required to track fast renewable fluctuations, leading to avoidable wind curtailment and revenue loss for off-grid wind-to-hydrogen systems. This paper proposes an optimal power allocation framework for a hybrid electrolyzer system composed of alkaline (ALK) and proton exchange membrane (PEM). ALK provides low-cost, large-capacity baseline production but responds slowly, whereas PEM delivers second-level agility to absorb high-frequency power variations. A two-stage strategy is designed to couple efficiency-oriented power allocation with refined power dynamic regulation under explicit start/stop, ramp-rate, and capacity constraints. Moreover, a dual-layer array-rotation mechanism is incorporated to balance operating stress across stacks and prevent unit-specific overuse. Case studies using measured data from a practical project in Northeast China show that, compared with traditional power allocation methods, the proposed method increases hydrogen production by 3.3%-5.6%, reduces wind curtailment by 70.6%-94.7%, and improves hydrogen revenue by 21.9%-32.0%, enhancing renewable utilization and profitability.

title: Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Remote Detection of Early Parkinson's Disease by Mobile Phone Digital Biomarkers
authors: Tongyue He, Chi Lin, Qiang He, Yongfei Wu, Junxin Chen
abstract: As a ubiquitous portable device, mobile phones play an important role in large-scale data collection and remote health detection. Parkinson's disease (PD), a typical movement disorder, can be detected by capturing digital biomarkers using mobile phone sensors. Nevertheless, it is difficult to obtain reliable label information in large-scale remote data collection, especially for time-series digital biomarkers. Based on this, we develop a novel multi-dimensional self-supervised contrastive learning framework for remote detection of early PD by mobile phone time-series digital biomarkers. Specifically, depending on two different augmentation views, the proposed framework considers temporal contrasting, spatial contrasting, contextual contrasting, and inter-modal contrasting to enable the model to learn more discriminative features. For temporal and spatial contrasting, certain time steps (channels) of one view are used to predict the next time steps (channels) of the other view, thereby constructing a cross-view prediction task. Meanwhile, contextual contrasting is introduced into the contrast framework to consider temporal prediction and spatial prediction context representation, respectively, further increasing similarity between positive pairs and decreasing it between negative pairs. In addition, inter-modal contrasting is used to force the model to capture the potential relationship between different modal data. Experimental results show that fine-tuning with only 10% of the labeled data can outperform supervised learning and generally surpass state-of the-art algorithms.

title: PTAN: Probability-Aware Topological Adaptive Navigation for Mobile Robots in Human-Dense Dynamic Environments
authors: Wenzheng Chi, Jiajie Yu, Chenyang Cao, Lining Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Operating in inherently dynamic and unstructured environments, mobile robots must routinely navigate substantial uncertainties—particularly in crowded spaces or multi-door configurations. Yet mainstream navigation approaches often disregard such environmental unpredictability and underutilize historical task experience, leading to recurrent obstacle blockages that degrade both efficiency and safety. This work proposes an adaptive navigation method for uncertain environments using probabilistic topological mapping (PTM). We develop a Gaussian mixture model (GMM)-based PTM construction approach that extracts environmental feature nodes through path similarity and node density filtering, clusters nodes via GMM, and establishes topological connections to efficiently encode environmental characteristics. For adaptive navigation, we introduce: 1) a posterior heuristic algorithm for planning under uncertainty; 2) a topological edge analysis with re-planning and escape mechanisms for robustness; and 3) a dynamic particle filter for online probability updates. This framework enables robots to autonomously analyze environmental information and progressively optimize navigation strategies through probabilistic self-updating. Simulations and real-world experimental studies reveal that our method exhibits higher efficiency and safety compared to current mainstream navigation approaches.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Diffusion and Aggregation in Wireless Federated Learning
authors: Yunli Ji, Jie Zheng, Hongyang Du, Jiawen Kang, Haijun Zhang, Dusit Niyato, Shiwen Mao
abstract: User devices (UDs) with non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data will worsen accuracy performance of the global model in federated learning (FL). Therefore, the implementation of diffusion strategies in machine learning (ML) models can enhance the effectiveness of federated learning with non-IID data. However, in a device-to-device (D2D) wireless federated learning (WFL) system, limited wireless resources and severe wireless channel interference become the important bottleneck to restrict the diffusion performance and model aggregation so as the global model of WFL with non-IID suffers from the weight divergence challenge. Thus, we propose a novel joint over-the-air computation (OAC) aggregation and diffusion framework by using a graph neural network (GNN) for WFL, termed an OAC-GNN-Dif framework. By integrating the OAC with message passing neural network (MPNN) of GNN, we further develop the OAC-MPNN-Dif algorithm based on the OAC-GNN-Dif framework. To further reduce communication costs, we designed an OAC message recurrent neural network (OAC-MPRNN-Dif) algorithm, where each UD propagates local models via D2D communications to refresh the graph embedding in the current frame based on the graph feature extraction and localization state of the previous frame to reduce communication costs. Additionally, we introduce dynamic time-varying MPNN for federated diffusion within evolving D2D network topologies. The experimental results indicate that our approach significantly performs well in communication overhead, with a 30%-60% decreasing in wireless resources overhead and 1.2-3.5 times decreasing in the number of model transfers compared to the FedDif methods. Moreover, our approach also improves the global model test accuracy, which is about 2.7% higher than the existing communication diffusion FL with non-IID characteristics.

title: IConSCEPT 2025 Breaker Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)

title: Future Directions of CPR Training: Synergy between Markerless AR and Medical Education
authors: Jahnavi Gundakaram, Niha Kamal Basha, Surendiran B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Applications of Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) facilitate complex interventions that aid emergency medical treatment and can significantly improve survival rates in cases of sudden cardiac arrest (SCA). If left untreated, SCA causes irreversible brain death within minutes. As technological advancements continue to reshape the field of medical education, this paper describes the evolving landscape of CPR training by exploring the potential of immersive technology to build powerful tools. This study aims to present a novel Markerless AR application developed to train both healthcare providers and the public on how to administer CPR to an individual experiencing sudden cardiopulmonary arrest with a primary objective to enhance knowledge retention, understanding and the learning experience using Creative Cloud and Adobe Aero. This application provides simulation-based training with realistic practice scenarios and integrated text-to-speech standard CPR instructions to guide users at each step. Through this innovative approach, traditional learning methods are transformed by providing an interactive and immersive learning environment and maximizing learners' outcomes with personalized feedback.

title: On the Role of Aggregation Operators in Deployment of Federated Learning
authors: Matea D. Lukić, Tamara D. Drobnjaković, Ana M. Poledica
abstract: Federated learning trains models across distributed clients without moving raw data, but its effectiveness depends on how the server aggregates client updates. We present an operator-centric overview of aggregation mechanisms, organized by the practical problems they are designed to handle. Within this view, we frame client selection and weighting as two sides of the same aggregation design space and summarize representative operator families. We then motivate interpretability-oriented and security-aware aggregation as increasingly important for real deployments, emphasizing that under privacy mechanisms, the server may not access per-client signals, limiting which interpretable solutions can be implemented.

title: Multi-Agent Video Intelligent System for Educational and Archival Video Analysis
authors: Gnanakumar D, Ratnakaram Shiva Sai Raju, T. Mahaveer, T. Sai Karthick, P. Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The research presented a Tri-Modal Timestamp- Driven Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) system to support intelligent and efficient Video Understanding. Using a combination of textual, visual and audio modalities, we proved that our framework is able to significantly reduce the time to find relevant content in video data (52%), while retrieving items with an accuracy of 93.6% (retrieval accuracy) and pinpointing the time to within almost perfectly matched timestamps (95.1%). The proposed approach is capable of returning time-stamped responses that can be directly linked to both visual frames and transcribed speech, enabling the ability of an individual to ask questions in their native language and receive responses that are time-stamped and correlated with both a visual and text representation. The objective and logical nature of this framework make it applicable to many different domains where both accuracy and interpretability of the content are important, which is not necessarily true for other systems developed for the purposes of video summarization. Thus, this tool will ultimately enable digital archives, e-learning platforms and educational institutions to provide users with context-specific, verifiable information related to video content.

title: Curriculum-based Reinforcement Learning for Flexibility Evaluation in Buildings
authors: Lorenz Ray Payonga, Hatef Madani, Mark Stefan
abstract: This paper presents a reinforcement learning (RL) approach for evaluating and controlling residential building flexibility in response to dynamic electricity pricing and intermit-tent grid requests. To address the challenge of sparse rewards and fluctuating states, a curriculum learning (CL) strategy is integrated with reward shaping, enabling a model-free RL agent to learn complex, multi-objective control behavior. A custom Gymnasium-EnergyPlus environment is developed to simulate thermal dynamics, price signals, and USEF-aligned flexibility requests. The agent is trained through a three-stage curriculum, progressing from comfort maintenance to cost minimization, and finally, to flexibility response. Results show that CL significantly improves training stability and responsiveness compared to a non-curriculum approach, with agents able to balance comfort, cost efficiency, and flexibility provision under varying conditions.

title: Simulating Quantum Teleportation Circuits with RXX(π/2) Gates for Efficient Qubit Transfer
authors: Siam Uddin, Shaharier Kabir, Shihab Islam, Joheb Muhammad Tanzeer Sayeed, Riad Rashed, Abirul Islam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The article suggests a novel quantum teleportation program, which involves the combination of RXX (π/2) entangling gates, QFT and Y-basis entanglement to increase the precision, stage steadiness, and stability of quantum states. Unlike in the past where Bell state measurements with real-valued entangling interaction have been employed, controlled rotation gates, i.e. CRX and CRZ are employed here as well as a complex-phase entangling interaction. A numerical analysis is done as well as an end-to-end analysis. Better qubit purity and phase control, as well as teleportation fidelity, are achieved and it is extended to Bloch sphere representations and measurement probability distributions. This extends to QFT which can be used to implement far more effective multi-qubit operations. Besides, a six-state security measure is applied, which is needed to make the device more resistant to noise and eavesdropping. This incorporates a scalable, secure, and noise-tolerant architecture which can be used in high-fidelity quantum communication, distributed quantum networks, and resilient quantum information processing system.

title: A Fuzzy Chance Constrained Feasible Region Projection Method for Distribution Systems
authors: Shirui Zhang, Peng Hao, Chunyi Huang, Kangping Li
abstract: Accurately characterizing the external features of the distribution network feasible region (FR) enables the non-iterative transmission-distribution coordinated dispatch. To this end, this paper proposes an uncertain FR characterization method for topology-aware distribution systems based on a fuzzy chance constrained projection algorithm. Firstly, the non-iterative coordinated dispatch framework and the problem statement of feasible region projection are given. Secondly, a fuzzy chance constrained energy management model of the distribution system is built to optimize the dispatch strategy of distributed energy resources and reserves, where the uncertain outputs of distributed generations and load demands are uniformly modeled as trapezoidal fuzzy sets under predefined dynamic confidence levels. Thirdly, an improved vertex search algorithm is proposed to perform the accurate projection of the spatial-temporally coupled high-dimensional FR of the distribution system. Case study demonstrates that the proposed method can achieve a vertex coincidence rate of 100% and zero boundary error at confidence levels between 0.90 and 0.95, providing accurate boundary mapping to support secure and economical transmission-distribution coordinated dispatch.

title: A Deep Learning Approach Using ConvNeXt-Tiny and EfficientNetV2-B0 for Multi-Fruit Quality Prediction
authors: Abburi Ramesh, Kambhampati John Wesly, Vinnakota Manoj Kumar, Bokkisam Anil Kumar, Shaikthettu Sharif, R.S. Gangadharan, Moturi Sireesha
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Automating fruit sorting is difficult because produce varies so much in appearance and datasets are rarely balanced. Existing solutions tend to focus on solving each of these problems, which affects the overall efficiency of the system. This paper proposes a dual-stage framework based deep learning system for the integration of these two tasks. For classification of six fruit categories in the FruitNet dataset, a ConvNeXt-Tiny model pretrained on ImageNet is used, and an EfficientNetV2-B0 model that has undergone same-domain transfer learning is used for quality level grading (good, average, poor). Standard geometric augmentations and class-weighted loss functions are applied to mitigate class imbalance and enhance robustness. With a solid F1-score of 95.6%, the system proves reliable enough for realworld use on edge devices, the proposed system is superior to the existing approaches. Mostly, the suggested modular and lightweight architecture showcases the practicality of instant fruit evaluation on underpowered gadgets as a first important move for extending agricultural automation.

title: A Deep Residual Attention U-Net For Robust Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Nagaraj R, Nandhini S, Harisshani Venkatesan, Kiruthika J
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation is crucial process for diagnosing and monitoring various retinal and systemic diseases, such as diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma and hypertension. However, factors like differences in vessel width, low image contrast, and pathological abnormalities make segmentation difficult. To overcome these challenges, we present a Deep Residual Attention U-Net that combines residual connections and attention mechanisms with the U-Net architecture. The Residual blocks help spread features and reduce vanishing gradients, while attention modules help the network focus more effectively on vessel-related features. Testing on benchmark datasets shows that Residual Attention U-net achieves better segmentation accuracy than standard U-Net and other recent models, demonstrating its effectiveness and clinical usefulness.

title: Mobility test means of the MMX Rover IDEFIX at DLR – Prototypix
authors: Fabian Buse, Lukas Burkhard, Nico Borgsmüller, Erich Krämer, Marie Kretschmer, Henrik Muders, Juliane Skibbe, Roman Weinberger
abstract: The IDEFIX rover, developed by CNES and DLR for JAXA’s MMX mission, is designed to explore Phobos’ surface to investigate the origins of the Martian moons. Key to achieving its scientific goals is reliable mobility on Phobos’ low-gravity, regolith-covered terrain. To support operations and validate critical flight software, the Prototypix mobility test platform was developed. Prototypix replicates the rover’s key systems, including flight-representative software, locomotion hardware, navigation, and camera systems. It is located in the Planetary Exploration Lab (PEL) at DLR-RMC. While not designed to replicate Phobos’ mechanical environment, Prototypix enables robust mobility operations training, system validation, and software integration. Testing has shown the platform’s effectiveness in simulating mobility scenarios and software testing, confirming the function of the rover and its ground support hardware, such as the gravity offloading mechanism.

title: Left atrial appendage Enhancement in Transoesophageal echocardiography images through Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs)
authors: S.Jayakrishnan, J.Anish Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Molecular ultrasound imaging is a targeted contrast image enhancement method that integrates molecular contrast agents with advanced ultrasound technology. In this study, the Left Atrial Appendage (LAA) is identified as the targeted region for enhancement using proposed methods Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs), which enforce physical principles of ultrasound wave propagation and bioacoustic interactions directly into the learning process. Simulation results demonstrate significant enhancement in the visualization of LAA shapes and sizes, enabling better distinction of the Left Ventricle (LV) and LAA from surrounding cardiac tissue and muscle regions. To further improve interpretability and model robustness, this work incorporates PINNs.This hybrid approach reduces overfitting, enhances generalization across patients, and improves reliability in clinical deployment. The integration of molecular contrast agents, wavelet-based deep networks, and physics-informed learning results in clinically relevant contrast-specific enhancement in TEE imaging. Experimental evaluations report an overall accuracy of about 98%, highlighting the potential of the proposed framework for precise detection and visualization of LAA, which is crucial for stroke risk assessment and atrial fibrillation management.

title: Virtual Production for Hardware-in-the-Loop Vision-based Orbital Navigation Experiments
authors: Mohsi Jawaid, Marcus Märtens, Owen Claxton, Justin Du Plessis, Tat-Jun Chin
abstract: Hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing is essential to validate vision-based orbital navigation algorithms. Increasingly, these algorithms leverage deep learning, which offers superior performance when trained on large datasets. However, discrepancies between the training data and the real in-orbit environment (e.g. geometry, color, textures and lighting), can cause the deep learning algorithm’s performance to degrade, affecting navigational accuracy. For robust validation of these algorithms, a testbed capable of realistically simulating both the foreground satellite and the complex space background –including stars, Earth, the Sun and other in-orbit elements (e.g. space stations) - is paramount. Existing testbeds have employed chroma-keying and CAD-model-based masking to composite such backgrounds. However, these techniques introduce limitations such as imperfect masking artifacts, inaccurate background integration (e.g. not being observed in reflections) and challenges of masking in the presence of optical lighting artifacts like lens flares. To address these issues and enable accurate HIL validation, we explore the application of virtual production for background simulation. Our analysis shows that this is a viable direction and offers several advantages for simulating in-orbit environments over existing techniques. Virtual production for HIL validation of vision-based orbital navigation algorithms has been underexplored, and our aim is to advance adoption of this technology in orbital testbeds.

title: Spatial Flood Risk Assessment for EV Charging Infrastructure in England
authors: Xiaoyu Wang, Chenghong Gu
abstract: There is a growing penetration of electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure in the UK, but the increasing risk of flooding caused by climate change has not been sufficiently considered during the planning and operation stages. This paper develops a new spatial risk assessment framework to evaluate the flood exposure and spatial clustering of EV charging stations across England. A composite scoring system, integrating the geographic location of charging stations, elevation, river proximity, and government-defined flood zone classification, is built to rank individual chargers into six exposure levels. Local Moran’s I is applied to identify high-risk spatial clusters. The method: 1) categories Local Authority Districts (LADs) into four spatial risk types by using a quadrant-based framework and 2) identifies critical "blind spots" where flood vulnerability is high, but infrastructure coverage remains insufficient. This study reveals risk aggregation in major UK cities, and proposes resilient charging station deployment strategies, considering typologies and localized risk characteristics. The findings highlight the urgent need for flood-adaptive planning in future EV infrastructure development.

title: Grid-Forming Control for Multiterminal MMC-MTDC Systems Integrating Hydropower
authors: Junjie Lin, Wang Xiang, Haobo Zhang, Shaopeng Zhang, Jinyu Wen
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources (RESs) in power systems, the receiving-end grids connected to modular multilevel converter-based multiterminal high voltage direct current (MMC-MTDC) systems are experiencing a decline in grid strength. This necessitates adopting grid-forming (GFM) control at receiving-end MMCs of the MTDC system to enhance overall system stability. However, existing studies are primarily focused on GFM strategies for single or two-terminal systems, lacking consideration for multiterminal configurations. This paper proposes the GFM control strategy for the MMC-MTDC system integrating hydropower and weak grids. Firstly, common GFM control methods are reviewed, and several feasible control combinations are identified. A comparative analysis of system performance under different control combinations is then carried out, demonstrating that the selection of dual-port GFM control and power synchronization loop (PSL) based GFM control at receiving ends offers superior performance. Finally, a three-terminal MTDC system connected to a four-machine two-area AC grid is modeled in PSCAD/EMTDC to validate the proposed strategy through simulation.

title: Harmonic Source Localization for Distribution Networks with Network Parameter Uncertainty
authors: Weihao Shuai, Junjie Sun, Hao Bai, Yawen Zheng, Yelun Peng, Zhikang Shuai
abstract: Accurate localization of harmonic sources is essential for determining harmonic responsibility and mitigating harmonic distortions in distribution networks. However, existing localization methods face difficulties in achieving accurate results without considering uncertainties in network parameter values. To achieve accurate harmonic source localization, this paper proposes a method based on a reconstructed dynamic graph convolutional recurrent network (DGCRN). First, an input feature matrix is constructed by leveraging the temporal response and spatial distribution characteristics of harmonic voltages and currents, together with the network topology and node measurement data, effectively capturing the spatiotemporal evolution of harmonics. Then, an improved DGCRN incorporating a self-attention mechanism is developed to extract spatiotemporal features under network uncertainty, enabling precise mapping to harmonic source locations. The modified IEEE-33 test system is tested to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: 3D Manifestation of the Positions for a Cricketer with Pose Matrix
authors: Sarvesh Bhoyar, Vedashree Takle, T. N. Shankar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Transforming a 2D RGB image into a 3D model for the precise representation of any pictorial object, using the image-to-lixel regression policy. This converts a 2-D object into a 3-D framework and addresses the ambiguity of the intended position by elevating it to a higher dimension, thereby forming the expected with the better manifestation of the indiscernible positions. A Convolutional Neural Network is useful for extracting features of body position in preparation for the Skinned Multi-Person Linear model to create accurate physical meshes in the standard RGB format, by neglecting depth sensors. This paper focuses on the PoseMatrix, which is appropriate for fitness coaching, virtual avatars, capturing real-time motion, telehealth, sportsman and cricketer position detection for fair decision-making as a durable and expected real-time solution.

title: Classification of Obfuscated Privacy Malware using Deep Learning and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: K.Saranya, Veeramalai Sankaradass, V.Antony Suresh, G. Manimozhi, Rajarajeswari R, Sunitha.T
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Malware detection and classification in cyber security is a major challenge because of the rising methods of adversarial evasion. This paper suggests a multi-level categorization scheme including: (1) a binary content classifier that separates malware and benign files, (2) a category classifier that locates malware types (benign, rebounder, and Trojans), and (3) a family classifier that assigned 16 malware strains to a family. Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE) approach was utilized to solve the problem of class imbalance, increasing the model robustness. As a reference, the models were trained based on traditional deep learning (DL) (e.g., CNNs, LSTMs) as well as Random Forest (RF). The experiments have verified the fact that Random Forest has significantly better results than other deep learning models when applied in multiclass classification in terms of accuracy, precision and recall. In particular, RF performed better in terms of generalization when it comes to detecting obfuscated and polymorphic malware variants whereas DL models performed poorly in response to sparseness of features. The study brings to the fore the possible uses of hybrid ML-DL systems and feature engineering in the fight against the emerging forms of malware. In future, the work will look into optimizations to real-time detection as well as adversarial training, and seek greater resilience to the zero-day attacks.

title: Simulating Interconnected Electricity and Carbon Markets: A CVAE-Based Multi-Agent Framework
authors: Ruihua Yu, Yuxie Zhou, Junhua Zhao
abstract: In alignment with the critical climate targets outlined by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), the carbon emission market has emerged as a key instrument for imposing emission constraints on electricity companies. This paper introduces an innovative simulation and analytical approach for the integrated electricity and carbon market, utilizing multi-agent-based modeling to capture the complex interplay between trading behaviors across these markets. By incorporating Conditional Variational Autoencoders (CVAEs) to synthesize bidding strategies for generators active in both markets, our method offers a holistic perspective on unified market dynamics. The framework is validated using the IEEE-30 bus system, where diverse participant types and behavioral patterns are analyzed to assess their influence on market outcomes. Our findings reveal that the diversity in participant behaviors substantially impacts their profitability in these interconnected markets.

title: Study on optimised energy storage allocation strategy based on improved VMD decomposition with multi-objective grey wolf optimisation
authors: Hao Cheng, Shuaizhuang Li, Xinhang Lu, Xingchao Zhang, Xiaodong Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid energy storage system (HESS) capacity allocation method for wind farm power fluctuation smoothing, integrating frost-ice optimised variational modal decomposition (RIME-VMD) and multi-objective grey wolf optimisation (MOGWO). The method comprises three parts: adaptive signal decomposition (separating power signal high-frequency components via VMD for supercapacitor and battery bearing), dynamic power allocation (establishing a multi-objective model focusing on economy, lifetime loss and levelling effect, solved by improved MOGWO to obtain Pareto optimal solutions), and the state of charge (SOC) optimal control of HESS via fuzzy control. Simulation results show that under multi-objective synergy, the method reduces the fluctuation standard deviation by 0.11 MW compared with the lowest-cost solution, while controlling HESS SOC fluctuation within the 20%-80% safety zone.

title: Distribution Power Network Reconfiguration with Queued EV Support: Deep Reinforcement Learning for V2G Scheduling under Uncertainty
authors: Shangyang He, Jinpeng Tian, Ziyu Cui, Guokai Hao, Zipeng Liang, Yingping Cao, Chi Yung Chung
abstract: Power outages due to extreme weather induce economic losses and critical infrastructure disruption in the distribution power network (DPN). To enhance DPN resilience, this paper investigates electric vehicles (EVs) as grid-support assets via vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology. A reinforcement learning (RL) framework is developed to manage real-time EV charging and parking scheduling under V2G, which enables charging stations (CSs) equipped with energy storage systems (ESS) to address behavioral uncertainties and queuing dynamics. These V2G-enabled CSs are then deployed in the DPN. We propose a novel co-optimization framework integrating EV charging and parking management at the CS with emergency power support for DPN restoration under extreme weather. Experimental results demonstrate: (1) the effectiveness of coordinated V2G support and RL scheduling in enhancing grid resilience; (2) the significant impact of CS queueing processes on power support capabilities during DPN restoration.

title: BiLSTM-Based Reconstruction of ECG Waveforms from 24 GHz Continuous-Wave Radar Signal
authors: Peda Babu G, Lakhan Dev Sharma
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Radar sensing provides a fully contactless means of monitoring cardiac activity by detecting subtle chest-wall movements that occur with each heartbeat. This study presents a compact Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) model designed to reconstruct electrocardiogram (ECG) waveforms from 24 GHz continuous-wave (CW) radar I/Q signals. The radar data are preprocessed to extract phase-based features through standardization, quadrature-imbalance correction, phase unwrapping, and band-pass filtering. Time-synchronized radar and ECG signals are divided into 12 -second overlapping segments (50 % overlap), and the Bi-LSTM model is trained using a mean squared error (MSE) loss function. Model performance is evaluated under both subject-specific and crosssubject protocols. In subject-specific testing, the reconstructed ECG waveforms exhibit strong morphological similarity to the reference recordings, achieving a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.32 % and a Pearson correlation coefficient of 96.61 %. These results demonstrate that radar-based ECG reconstruction is a practical and reliable contact-free approach for continuous cardiac monitoring, highlighting the importance of improving cross-subject generalization and motion robustness for real-world applications.

title: Real Face Foundation Models for Generalized Synthetic Media Detection
authors: Gurram Sunitha, Basi Reddy. A, K Sree Sahithya, K S Vignesh, Mayana Faizan Ahamed Khan, Kaspa Vishvas Naidu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The quick development and diversification of deepfake technologies present serious obstacles to efficient and broadly applicable detection techniques. Because traditional methods rely on particular examples of fake faces, they frequently fail to generalize across the wide range of face manipulation algorithms. In order to overcome this, we suggest a brand-new framework called Real Face Foundation Representation Learning (RFFR), which radically changes the detection paradigm from identifying patterns in fake faces to simulating the intrinsic distribution of real faces. Using large-scale authentic face datasets and self-supervised masked image modeling, RFFR learns deep, fundamental facial representations that naturally capture the essential characteristics of real faces. This foundation facilitates robust detection across various manipulation types and invisible attacks by identifying subtle deepfake artifacts as deviations from real face reconstruction. To improve discriminative power and embedding space separation, we combine the RFFR with auxiliary training objectives such as triplet loss and attribution-aware techniques. Our approach greatly outperforms state-of-the-art models in generalization performance, providing a scalable, dependable, and useful solution to deepfake detection in real-world applications, according to extensive evaluations on a variety of difficult deepfake benchmarks, including cross-dataset and in-the-wild scenarios. Furthermore, we extend RFFR with an open-set manifold-based scoring mechanism and adversarial manifold smoothing, making the detector not only capable of differentiating between real and known deepfake types but also of explicitly indicating unknown manipulation styles and resisting invisible adversarial perturbations. The proposed Open-set Adversarially-Robust RFFR (OAR-RFFR) demonstrates superior unknown-attack detection rates and stable performance under gradient-based adversarial attacks over state-of-the-art baselines.

title: Design of a High-Performance Iterative Barrett Modular Multiplier for Cryptographic Applications
authors: Pavani Jetamoni, RadhaKrishna Karne, Niranjan Reddy Kallem, Shivaprasad A, Venkataswamy G, Kishore B
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Modular multiplication (MM) is a pivotal arithmetic operation in modern cryptographic accelerators such as RSA, ECC, and Diffie-Hellman protocols. This paper presents an optimized iterative Barrett Modular Multiplier (BMM) architecture realized on an FPGA platform to enhance speed and area efficiency in residue-number-system (RNS)based crypto systems. The proposed architecture replaces conventional longword divisions with parameterized quotient estimation and precomputed constants, thereby minimizing hardware complexity. Iterative refinement and pipe lined control reduce latency while sustaining precision at large word lengths (up to 1024 bits). The design integrates efficient addition and compression logic with bounded quotient estimation, ensuring deterministic range compliance across iterations. Implementation in Xilinx ISE 13.2 using a Spartan3E FPGA demonstrates a critical-path delay of 18 ns and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40^{\sim}$</tex> % resource reduction compared with classical BMM and Montgomery designs. The results validate the suitability of the proposed method for high-performance crypto processors and embedded security cores where throughput and resource tradeoffs are critical.

title: Engagement-Aware Churn Prediction for OTT Platforms: A Comparative Analysis of ML and DL Approaches
authors: Sayeda Asfiya Afreen, Sara Fatima, Samreen Sultana, Mohammad Pasha, Mohd Umar Farooq, Farheen Fatima
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: For subscription-based OTT platform services to keep users, user churn prediction is crucial. This study uses subscription and behavioral data from a Netflix-like streaming platform to create a strong and essential machine learning framework for churn prediction. To record users' activity profile, we design essential elements like engagement score, login rate, watch time and premium content interaction. Performance metrics (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score, and ROC-AUC) are used to assess four tuned models: Logistic Regression, XGBoost, Neural Networks, and LSTM. According to our findings, XGBoost has the best predictive power (ROC-AUC: 0.919), which is closely followed by LSTM (0.917). Feature significance analysis identifies the main churn factors as being watch time, subscription tenure, and streaming quality. While logistic regression offers explainable risk metrics, the optimized LSTM model captures behavioral trends in a unique way. In addition to highlighting the trade-offs between model complexity and effectiveness in practical churn prediction, this work gives clear advice for keeping users from leaving.

title: A Framework for Extending SysMLv2 Views and Viewpoints to Non-Modelers in Agile System Developments
authors: Irfan Javid, Jean-Michel Bruel, Mohammad Chami
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: The primary stakeholders for the system models are often systems engineers (SE) in a dynamic and increasingly changing environment, due to the continual improvement in the development of complex systems. To comprehend a system’s requirements and behavior, an SE team creates models utilizing Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) tools. SysMLv2 View and Viewpoint concept, based on an independent metamodel, for robust, flexible, adaptable, and integrated system model representations. However, several limitations still need to be addressed, particularly when non-modelers are considered model stakeholders in an agile working environment. Consequently, the adoption of SysMLv2 remains challenging and requires expertise due to increased complexity and a steep learning curve, whether in textual or visual representation.Our research aims to expand the views and viewpoints of non-modelers in SysMLv2. We propose a concurrent agile modeling framework for explaining and modeling, with a particular emphasis on user-specific views, extended to non-modeler stakeholders based on SysMLv2 viewpoints. The proposed framework will comprise guidelines, best practices, and recommendations for the effective use of Views and Viewpoints in SysMLv2 by both modelers and non-modelers.

title: A Hardware-Realistic Quantum Web Protocol with Blockchain-Mediated Trust and Verifiable Security Metrics
authors: R. Dhanalakshmi, Kunal Siyag, Sahaya Beni Prathiba B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The development of a Quantum Internet that can scale needs to establish secure inter-domain communication methods which operate without needing any prior established trust. The majority of current proposals depend on either perfect quantum channels or a centralized system which quantum attackers can exploit. The paper presents a Quantum Web Protocol (QWP) stack which includes a hardware-based foundation for its development and a blockchain system which enables trustless decentralized access control. We developed four new standardized indices which connect quantum physics to network governance and successfully validated them through testing. Our system uses post-quantum CRYSTALS-Dilithium signatures from on-chain verification to establish trustless coordination which prevents unauthorized node access. The researchers evaluated system performance through a large-scale simulation which tested 100,000 trials under standard hardware noise parameters of T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> = 100 µs, 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> gate error. The system achieved a Route Success Rate of 72.0%, with an average end-to-end fidelity of 0.6137. The anomaly detector’s built-in machine learning features demonstrated strong performance capabilities, achieving F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>-Score = 0.7350 (σ = 0.0932) and AUC-ROC = 0.9795 (σ = 0.0096). The blockchain control plane processed over 1.5 million simulated transactions, which produced a cryptographic audit trail that could be digitally verified. The framework underwent comprehensive evaluation together with its measurement system, which will establish essential standards for developing future Quantum Internet systems.

title: Multi energy flow comprehensive collaborative optimization scheme for building energy microgrid
authors: Wenyong Wang, Xiaolong Xu, Jun Hao, Benqiang Li, Xingyang Li, Jingyuan Yin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Challenges faced by microgrids in building parks, such as customized requirements, multi energy flow collaboration, and adaptability upgrades, a multi energy flow collaborative optimization method for building energy microgrids is proposed. Firstly, a low-carbon building energy system structure is constructed, by connecting to a multi port flexible interconnection device, the flow coordination between different regions is achieved. Secondly, regional carbon emission quantification factor is established to dynamically regulate the load, and the demand response considering carbon emissions has been realized. On the basis of power reciprocity characteristics, a multi-objective optimization scheduling model is constructed by fully considering carbon emissions, operating costs, and system voltage stability, and the regulation of power flow in architectural park is realized. Finally, based on the human evolutionary optimization algorithm mechanism, the grey wolf optimizer (GWO) was improved to achieve global fast and high-precision optimization. The simulation verified that the proposed scheme can accurately guide the building energy microgrid to achieve low-carbon power flow mutual benefit, ensure system power quality, the low-carbon economic operation can be achieved.

title: Integrated Wind-PV-Thermal-Storage Energy System: Synergistic Operation and Benefit Evaluation with EC-BEST Ultra-Supercritical Units
authors: Bin Zhang, Guangjie Zhou, Chaonan Tan, Linxuan Li, Haoyu Liu, Shijia Xu, Dongliang Wang, Yan Gao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: The global energy transition necessitates innovative solutions to integrate variable renewable energy into power systems while ensuring grid stability and economic viability. This study investigates the synergistic operation and economic benefits of an integrated wind-photovoltaic-thermal-storage energy system, utilizing a 1000 MW EC-BEST ultra-supercritical unit as its stable power core. Results demonstrate that the energy storage system effectively performs price arbitrage, charging during periods of low electricity prices coinciding with high renewable output and discharging during high-price, peak-demand periods. This operational flexibility significantly enhances the system's economic performance. The integrated system achieved total revenues of 8.9424 million CNY (winter) and 10.0642 million CNY (summer). The system's comprehensive benefit increased by 10.33% in winter and 15.47% in summer compared to a configuration without energy storage. This research validates the integrated system as a promising model for improving the flexibility and economic competitiveness of thermal power plants in a high-renewable-penetration period.

title: The DLR Autonomous Navigation for the MMX Rover Under Phobos Environmental Influences: Illumination, Temperature, and Radiation
authors: Lukas Burkhard, Jitao Zheng, Hans-Jürgen Sedlmayr
abstract: The Martian Moons eXploration (MMX) is set to examine the Martian Moons Phobos and Deimos. As part of this mission, a small rover named IDEFIX will explore the surface of Phobos as the first in situ system. The German Aerospace Center (DLR) develops the onboard autonomous navigation solution NAVDLR as a technology demonstration and also to increase the safety of rover driving operations. The harsh Phobos environment challenges the autonomous navigation due to its illumination conditions, the temperatures, and the radiation environment, especially as there remains a high uncertainty about the environmental parameters. This paper presents our approach to simulate varying and challenging environment effects on existing, representative data sets. We analyze the robustness of our autonomous navigation solution in relation to these conditions. Our results demonstrate that a loss of tracking can occur for challenging scenarios, but NAVDLR generally provides accurate and robust results. Finally, we propose mitigation techniques to reduce loss of tracking risk and improve navigation robustness.

title: Coordinated Control of Onshore Green Hydrogen Hubs for Grid Frequency and Voltage Support
authors: Meng Liu, Moudud Ahmed, Lasantha Meegahapola, Siqi Bu
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy reduces system inertia and reactive power support, posing significant challenges to grid stability. The canonical Green Hydrogen Hub (GHH) integrates electrolyzers, fuel cells, and solar generation to form a multi-energy system, uniquely providing more adaptive ancillary services through the dual-mode energy conversion process. This paper proposes a coordinated control strategy to achieve simultaneous frequency and voltage support from an onshore GHH. The proposed strategy is validated through time-domain simulations under three disturbance scenarios, where the electrolyzer, fuel cell and solar-farm operate at varying power levels. Results confirm that GHH delivers coordinated frequency and voltage support services with fast response, especially driven by the fast response of the electrolyzer. Furthermore, a simulated comparison of a battery energy storage system (BESS)-based GHH against a canonical setup demonstrates the superior dynamic frequency support capability. This enhancement reinforces the role of GHH in promoting renewable energy integration and supporting decarbonization objectives.

title: Frequency Security-Enhanced Day-Ahead Dispatching Method for Networked Microgrids with Mobile Energy Storage Systems
authors: Hui Li, Chengkai Nie, Qiuwei Wu, Yunpeng Jiang
abstract: Networked microgrids offer superior reliability, renewable energy accommodation, and primary frequency regulation (PFR) ability compared to isolated microgrids. To enhance frequency security and economic performance, this paper proposes a frequency security-enhanced day-ahead scheduling approach for networked microgrids incorporating mobile energy storage systems (MESSs). We first formulate the PFR problem for networked microgrids and model the spatio-temporal dynamics of MESSs scheduling. Then, a frequency security-enhanced dispatching model is developed to optimize the coordinated operation of MESSs and networked microgrids. A robust optimization is established to tackle the uncertain wind generation in day-ahead scheduling. Case studies validate the proposed methods, with simulation results highlighting significant improvements in frequency security and cost-effectiveness through MESS integration.

title: A Power Regulation Method for High-Energy-Consuming Electrolytic Lead Loads Participating in Power Grid Demand Response
authors: Tianfang Si, Siyang Liao, Deping Ke, Huiji Wang, Yongkang Li, Lingfang Li
abstract: Some high-energy-consuming loads are high-quality flexible adjustable resources. However, existing studies generally focus on typical industrial loads such as electrolytic aluminum and steel. Electrolytic lead loads consume a large amount of electricity and account for a relatively high proportion in some coastal provinces rich in lead-zinc ores. To address the issue that the characteristics and control methods of high-energy-consuming electrolytic lead loads are unclear, this paper proposes a power regulation method for high-energy-consuming electrolytic lead loads to participate in power grid demand response. The method first analyzes the electrical load characteristics of the electrolytic lead production process in combination with its production process flow, establishes electrical and thermodynamic models, and clarifies the relationship between regulatory variables and power. Then, from the perspective of power grid demand, it constructs a demand response model, establishes a multi-objective function that integrates power grid demand and load loss, and solves the optimal regulatory variables with production and equipment safety as constraints. Verified by field experiments, this method can accurately respond to power grid instructions with little impact on production. It helps tap the regulation potential of electrolytic lead loads, and improves the stability of the power grid and the absorption capacity of renewable energy.

title: TriMiX: An Interpretable Machine Learning Pipeline for Tri-Class Motor Imagery EEG Classification for Clinical Brain Computer Interfaces
authors: Shankar J., Gayatri Kiran Kadlag, Sathidevi P. S., Sreelekha G.
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Motor imagery-driven Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) offer significant potential for neurorehabilitation and assistive device control. However, most systems use binary classification and without explicit rest-state modeling, limiting asynchronous control and increasing the risk of unintended device activations. Consequently, a prosthetic limb may move unexpectedly during idle periods, undermining user safety and trust. In this work, we present TriMiX, a novel and interpretable machine learning pipeline for tri-class motor imagery EEG classification (left-hand, right-hand, and rest). TriMiX combines neurophysiological Filter Bank Common Spatial Pattern features, bandpower analysis, ANOVA F-test-based feature selection, and XGBoost-based classification. When tested on the PhysioNet EEG Dataset with 109 subjects, TriMiX achieves 96.26% subject-wise accuracy and 77.9% leave-one-subject-out cross-validation accuracy with Cohen’s Kappa 0.9392. Critical to clinical deployment, TriMiX demonstrates: (1) asynchronous control capability with a false positive rate of only 1.77% during rest-state detection, significantly outperforming the clinical benchmark of 13.7%, (2) real-time CPU-only inference at 0.0488 ms per sample on 12th Gen Intel i9 hardware, enabling bedside deployment without GPU infrastructure, and (3) intrinsic neurophysiological interpretability enabling clinician validation. Cross-dataset validation on BCI Competition IV-2a achieves 87.26% subject-wise and 80.04% cross-subject accuracy, demonstrating robust generalisation across different electrode configurations and sampling rates.

title: Designing an application for Chronic kidney disease (CKD) prediction
authors: Prasanna Lohe, Khushi Mishra, Adiba Sheikh, Samruddhi Bichpuriya, Keyuri Bhuptani, Aditya Parate
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) has emerged as a major global health challenge, contributing significantly to illness, mortality, and healthcare costs. Early-stage CKD often remains undiagnosed because symptoms appear late, increasing the likelihood of patients progressing to end-stage renal failure. To address this issue, we propose a machine-learning-based CKD prediction system built using a hybrid stacking ensemble. The approach integrates Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Logistic Regression models with an effective feature-selection mechanism to highlight essential clinical parameters such as serum creatinine, glomerular filtration rate (GFR), and protein levels in urine.Using a comprehensive dataset containing demographic and clinical records, the model demonstrates high prediction performance. A user-friendly web interface further enables accessible, real-time CKD risk evaluation for healthcare practitioners and patients. Achieving 92% accuracy and a 95% ROC-AUC score, the proposed framework shows significant promise as a supportive tool in modern healthcare, contributing to precision-based diagnosis and early intervention.

title: Few-Shot Battery State-of-Health Estimation via Meta-Learning and Cross-Domain Adaptation
authors: Song Zhang, Weixiang Shen
abstract: Accurate state-of-health (SOH) estimation is critical for the reliability and safety of batteries in electric vehicles and energy storage systems. However, conventional supervised and transfer learning methods face limitations due to their reliance on extensive labelled data and poor generalisation across different operating conditions caused by significant domain shifts and labelled data scarcity. In this paper, we present a meta-learning framework for few-shot cross-domain SOH estimation, integrating a domain-adaptive encoder and a frozen predictor within the model-agnostic meta-learning paradigm. With only a few early-cycle labels from previously unseen batteries, the framework enables robust SOH estimation through rapid model adaptation. Validation on public datasets demonstrates that the proposed approach consistently outperforms traditional baselines, particularly under label-scarce conditions. The effectiveness of encoder adaptation for fast domain transfer is confirmed by ablation studies. The results show that the proposed approach achieves a 60% reduction in root mean squire error compared to the baseline, highlighting its practical value for data-scarce senarios.

title: Electric load forecasting using PCC-RFE-GCRA-LSTM model
authors: Haowei Gong, Chunsheng Hu, Liang Ma, Yuan Liu
abstract: Accurate load forecasting is crucial for ensuring the stable operation of power grids. However, the complex dynamic characteristics of electricity demand time series make load forecasting a challenging task. To address these challenges, this study proposes an advanced model integrating feature selection and hyperparameter optimization, termed PCC-RFE-GCRA-LSTM. The model combines pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) and recursive feature elimination (RFE) for feature selection, and utilizes the greater cane rat algorithm (GCRA) to optimize the hyperparameters of the long short-term memory network (LSTM). To validate the model's performance, comparative experiments are conducted against four other models. The results demonstrate that, compared to the TPE-LSTM model which employs tree-structured parzen estimator (TPE) for LSTM hyperparameter optimization, this model reduced RMSE by 29.85%.

title: Stochastic Damping Optimization for MTDC Systems Considering Wind Power Uncertainty
authors: Xinyao Wang, Yuhong Wang, Jianquan Liao
abstract: In wind-integrated voltage source converter-based multi-terminal high voltage DC transmission (VSC-MTDC) systems, the weak grid and stochastic power fluctuations increase the risk of instability. Traditional damping controllers, designed for fixed equilibrium points, fail to maintain effective performance under such uncertain and varying conditions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a distributionally robust damping optimization control framework based on Gaussian Mixture Modeling (GMM) and Distributionally Robust Optimization (DRO). Firstly, damping characteristics are quantified using second-order sensitivity of the damping ratio with respect to control parameters and wind power. Secondly, GMM is employed to capture the statistical features of wind power fluctuations. Then, an ambiguity set is defined based on the GMM to describe forecast error of wind power fluctuation. Finally, the stochastic damping optimization problem is formulated as a distributionally robust optimization (DRO) problem and efficiently solved to obtain robust yet non-conservative control parameters. Simulation results on a modified IEEE 39 bus with three-terminal VSC-MTDC system validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: A Multi-Metric Trade-Off Framework for AI Model Selection for Diagnosis and Prognosis in Predictive Maintenance
authors: Andrés Barrantes Boza, Juan José Montero Jiménez, Rob Vingerhoeds
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: Predictive maintenance is a strategy designed to trigger maintenance actions only when necessary for human-made assets. It relies on advanced systems capable of detecting early-stage faults and assessing degradation to estimate the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of the component or system under consideration. Given its importance for both academia and industry, predictive maintenance has attracted increasing attention over the past decade. However, developing effective predictive maintenance systems remains a major challenge. Although numerous models support diagnostic and prognostic tasks, their selection is often guided by practitioner experience and evaluated primarily through precision. Relying on a single performance metric is insufficient for a comprehensive tradeoff analysis. This study proposes a holistic trade-off evaluation procedure for predictive maintenance models based on a scoring system that incorporates multiple performance indicators. This evaluation focuses on the four most relevant metrics in the literature: precision, computational efficiency, generalization, and robustness. To validate the proposed trade-off methodology, a case study on Li-Ion Battery RUL estimation is considered in which two deep learning models are compared: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) and Attention-based Convolutional Neural Networks. The results highlight the decisive value of the proposed evaluation framework, as it accounts for a wider range of performance criteria beyond precision alone. This work provides a solid foundation for advancing future research on the selection of predictive maintenance models.

title: An Efficient VLSI Design Model using Alpha-Beta Filtering and RTree-based Fast Preprocessing with Deep Reinforcement Learning Algorithm
authors: S. Agnes Shifani, Ashraf Shaqadan, V Syambabu, Verma Verma, D. Siri, S. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: A VLSI Design Model is an organised system for creating, simulating, and refining integrated circuits that have millions of transistors packed into a single chip. The Enhanced VLSI Automated Block Routing and Design (EVABRD) model constructs a system aimed at increasing the effectiveness of floor planning and routing in VLSI design. It entails three computational techniques: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for floor planning, R-Tree-based preprocessing for routing efficiency, and Alpha-Beta filtering for dynamic parameter management. The DRL unit models the design as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), which facilitates policy optimisation by successive interaction with the environment and receipt of rewards. The R-Tree method is used to be less redundant in localising the connections, while depth-first search and overlap checking are used to improve scheduling and routing methods. Alpha-Beta filtering facilitates the enhancement of tracking and the implementation of changes in design parameters dynamically. The performance analysis is cumulative probability, runtime, wirelength, Vout Vs Vout_Bar, and sensing delay.

title: Tapered Slot Loaded Patch Antenna for Superficial Skin Cancer Treatment
authors: Kiran Kumari, Nitika Sharma, Rajesh Khanna, Mayank Kumar Rai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance evaluation of a compact tapered slot loaded patch antenna for microwave hyperthermia applications, specifically targeting superficial skin cancer treatment at 915 MHz. A multilayer heterogeneous phantom, with and without tumor, is modeled in CST Microwave Studio to assess the antenna’s electromagnetic and thermal behavior. Four configurations were analyzed: phantom without tumor, phantom with tumor, phantom without tumor with water bolus, and phantom with tumor with water bolus. The simulated results indicate that the antenna maintains stable impedance matching near 915 MHz across all cases, with minimal frequency shift due to phantom loading. SAR analysis revealed enhanced energy localization in tumor regions owing to dielectric contrast, while the addition of a 5 mm water bolus reduced surface SAR peaks and improved energy penetration depth. Temperature simulations, performed with an input power of 3 W, demonstrated that without a water bolus, heating is concentrated at the skin surface, suitable for superficial cancer treatment. In contrast, with a water bolus, peak heating shifted deeper, effectively protecting the skin and maintaining therapeutic temperatures (42–45 °C) within the tumor region.

title: Design of AI-Based Integrated Swarm Robotics for Autonomous Farming Operations
authors: Sushil Tirpude, Kapil Jajulwar, Abhijit Titarmare
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Agriculture is moving toward automation to improve efficiency, productivity, and sustainability. This paper presents the design of an AI-based integrated swarm robotic system for autonomous farming operations. The proposed approach combines Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) for navigation in unstructured farm environments with swarm intelligence to enable coordinated actions among multiple robots. Artificial Intelligence techniques are integrated to support decision-making in tasks such as crop monitoring, pesticide spraying, soil analysis, and precision harvesting. Unlike conventional single-robot systems, the swarm approach provides scalability, robustness, and fault tolerance, making it suitable for large-scale farming. The paper also highlights the role of optimization and learning methods in improving path planning, task allocation, and energy efficiency. Potential applications in precision agriculture and the challenges of real-world deployment are discussed. This work aims to contribute toward the development of smart and sustainable farming systems for the future.

title: AuditGPT: Automated Financial Auditing and Regulatory Compliance Checks using LLMs
authors: Abhijeet Moghe, Rutuj Langde, Mufaddal Bohra, Ayush Chaware, Anirudh Bhagwat, Ashish Talekar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Financial auditing and regulatory compliance are essential for maintaining transparency, accountability, and adherence to legal standards. However, traditional auditing processes often rely heavily on manual effort and lack the scalability and explainability required in complex financial environments. This paper introduces AuditGPT, a novel framework that leverages Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and large language models (LLMs) to automate and enhance financial auditing and compliance verification. AuditGPT processes a wide range of financial documents including audit reports, sales records, and regulatory texts by embedding them into a vector store for efficient semantic retrieval. When queried, the system generates evidence backed assessments with structured verdicts: Compliant, Non Compliant, or Requires Review. These outputs are supported by traceable references to the original source documents, ensuring transparency and verifiability. An experimental evaluation on real world audit and sales datasets demonstrates that AuditGPT outperforms standard LLM based approaches across multiple metrics, including compliance accuracy, factual consistency, and retrieval relevance. The results highlight AuditGPT’s potential to significantly reduce manual workload, improve auditing accuracy, and offer greater explainability in decision-making. By addressing the limitations of conventional tools, AuditGPT establishes a scalable and trustworthy foundation for intelligent financial compliance monitoring.

title: Multi-Period Interaction Networks: Enhancing Deep Time Series Forecasting Under Complex Seasonality
authors: Nguyen Thi Ngoc Anh, Bui Hoang Tien Dat, Nguyen The Phong
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Forecasting time series with multiple overlapping cycles, such as daily, monthly, and yearly patterns, is a significant challenge in many fields. Current deep learning models often only capture short-term or long-term dependencies, overlooking multi-cyclical interactions - a key factor affecting forecasting accuracy. This paper proposes a multi-cycle separation model based on a Multi-Layer Perceptron architecture capable of simultaneously modeling relationships within each cycle (intraperiod) and between cycles (cross-period). The proposed model allows for the parallel extraction of two types of information: intra-period variation and cross-period variation. To achieve this, the model decomposes the signal into Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs) based on their characteristic frequencies. High IMFs are selected and combined with weights to create an information-rich representation for the forecasting step. The model is validated on various datasets and shows superior results compared to models that do not account for multi-series characteristics.

title: A Multi-Scenario Prediction Study on Data Center Energy Consumption Integrating Dual-Path Modeling and Regional Decomposition Mechanisms
authors: Qi Wei, Min Liu, Mengxi Zhang, Yufei Shi, Na Du, Zhihong Xiao, Guangxiang Jin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Motivated by the structural transformation of data center energy consumption triggered by the large-scale deployment of artificial intelligence (AI), this paper constructs a dual-path forecasting framework that integrates total computing power and intelligent computing power. Using a multi-scenario approach, we systematically assess China's data center electricity demand and its regional disparities from 2025 to 2030. A bottom- up model aggregates parameters such as rack quantity and load rate to estimate the energy consumption of total computing power, while a independent physical model quantifies the energy consumption of intelligent computing driven by factors such as the scale of AI and computing efficiency. Three development scenarios (low, medium, high) are further defined, with key parameter evolution paths fitted using Logistic, saturation growth, and compound exponential functions. Combined with Monte Carlo simulation and truncated normal disturbances to quantify uncertainty, the national trends are adapted to typical regions such as the Yangtze River Delta (YRD), Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei (BTH), and Guizhou province. The results show that by 2030, the total electricity consumption of data centers nationwide is expected to reach 170-400 billion kWh, with the share of intelligent computing power increasing from less than 20% to 50%-60%. Regionally, the YRD region will become the largest electricity consumption hub due to the high concentration of AI computing clusters; the BTH region will maintain rapid growth supported by policy and research advantages; Guizhou, with its low PUE and clean energy endowment, will evolve into an important area for the growth of green computing power. This study provides a scientific support for optimizing the layout of the "East Data, West Computing" initiative and formulating differentiated energy policies.

title: Detection of Cloud-Based Intrusions Using Optimized Neural Networks for Data Transmission
authors: Kalyankumar Dasari, Nagasai Tadikonda
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: The exponential growth of cloud computing has resulted in the generation and exchange of massive volumes of data, making security a major concern for both users and service providers. To overcome these limitations, the proposed methodology introduces an intelligent Intrusion Detection System (IDS) for secure data transmission in cloud environments using the KDD dataset. The process begins with Dataset Preprocessing, which involves cleaning, normalization, and feature selection to eliminate redundant or noisy attributes, ensuring improved learning efficiency. The refined data is categorized into five distinct classes: DoS, Normal, Probe, R2L, and U2R attacks. The classification phase employs an Artificial Neural Network (ANN), which effectively models complex, nonlinear relationships between input features and attack categories. To further enhance the ANN's learning performance, the Artificial Bee Colony (ABC) optimization algorithm is integrated for fine-tuning network weights and biases, achieving faster convergence and better generalization. This hybrid approach significantly improves detection accuracy, minimizes computational overhead, and reduces the false positive rate compared to conventional IDS models. As a result, the proposed IDS ensures robust data protection and reliable intrusion detection, making it highly suitable for dynamic cloud computing environments.

title: A Deduction Method for the Peak Shaving Market Price Considering the Interaction of Industrial Users
authors: Tianyu Yang, Xingying Chen, Lei Gan, Lin Zhu, Zhou Yan, Kun Yu, Le Bu, Xuesong Shao, Can Wang
abstract: With industrial users participating in peak shaving ancillary services market transactions, the market price may exhibit complex evolution trends. To analyze and reveal the possible evolution trends of market price, an evolutionary game approach considering the interaction of industrial users is proposed. Firstly, an industrial chain framework considering product transactions and an industrial user production-transaction optimization model for production-market optimal scheduling are constructed. And then, based on the uniform pricing market clearing mechanism and the model proposed, an evolutionary game approach supporting interaction among users is proposed. Finally, the payoff of industrial users and the dynamic learning strategy considering interaction fraud and trust degree are designed. Simulation results show that the evolution trend of market price is closely related to the adjustment costs of each industrial consumer and the system peak shaving requirements.

title: Automated Retinal Image Analysis for Early Detection of Eye Disorders
authors: Srilakshmi V, Uday Kiran G, Akhila P, Ashritha Reddy K, Abhinav Reddy K, Varun Reddy E
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Retinal diseases, including Choroidal Neovascularization (CNV), Diabetic Macular Edema (DME), and Drusen, rank among the highest in causing vision loss and blindness globally. This paper presents a deep learning-based fully automated retinal image analysis system that classifies OCT images into four categories: CNV, DME, Drusen, and Normal. The system is based on the ResNet-18 architecture, modified with layers pretrained on the OCT2017 dataset, with 300 images per class allocated for training and 180 images per class used for testing. The dataset was preprocessed by normalizing and resizing images to maintain uniformity, ensuring optimal model performance. The primary objective is to achieve high performance in class discrimination using diagnostic metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and others. The model is capable of predicting visual outcomes in real time, facilitating its use in open-access clinics and improving clinical workflow. The system demonstrates the capacity of AI embedded in medical devices to automate tasks, reduce the likelihood of errors, and enable timely intervention to prevent vision loss. Future work includes integrating attention mechanisms to highlight disease-specific regions and expanding the dataset for multi-center validation to enhance model robustness. This system has the potential to serve as a decision-support tool, bridging gaps in ophthalmic care and improving patient outcomes globally. Its deployment in routine clinical practice could significantly accelerate early diagnosis and personalized treatment planning for retinal diseases.

title: AI Transformer based Lung Cancer Detection and Malignancy Prediction Using CT Scan
authors: Kaavya S, Rajalakshmi M, Sadeesh Kumar A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Lung cancer is among the important causes of death due to cancer worldwide, and early detection is the key to any improvement in patient survival. Moreover, manual interpretations of CT scans by radiologists are cumbersome, subjective, and prone to errors, hence possibly delaying diagnosis. This project covers the use of deep learning by developing a Vision Transformer framework for the automatic detection of lung cancer and the prediction of malignancy, abolishing the need for manual feature extraction. The system utilizes readily available public CT scan datasets from Kaggle for model training and evaluation. It divides images into patches, adds positional encoding, and relies on multi-head self-attention to model both local and global features. In this way, the model detects lung nodules, classifies them into benign and malignant, and predicts the malignancy severity. Presenting an accurate and fast diagnostic tool will support the radiologist in early detection and treatment planning. Performance evaluation is performed in terms of performance metrics. The proposed system represents a novelty because of its Transformer-based architecture.

title: A Study on the Constant-Current/Constant-Voltage Output Characteristics of a Lithium Battery Wireless Charging System Based on the LCC-S Compensation Topology
authors: Weidong Lang, Fei Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This paper investigates a wireless power transfer (WPT) system based on the LCC-S compensation topology and proposes a natural implementation method for achieving constant-current (CC) and constant-voltage (CV) outputs without requiring additional control mechanisms. A system mathematical model is established through fundamental harmonic approximation, and analytical expressions for the output current and voltage are derived. The conditions for realizing constant-current operation (B = 0) and constant-voltage operation (satisfying specific resonance constraints) are clarified. Furthermore, the zero-phase-angle (ZPA) characteristics are analyzed to ensure unity power factor operation in both modes.Based on the theoretical analysis, key system parameters are designed and validated through simulation. At 100 kHz, stable constant-voltage output is achieved (output voltage of 15.95 V with a voltage gain of 1.0638). At 118 kHz, constant-current operation is obtained, and the output characteristics remain unaffected by load variations while still satisfying the ZPA condition. An experimental prototype is constructed for testing, yielding a measured receiver-side voltage of 15.16 V, which aligns closely with the simulation results. The observed deviation primarily arises from component tolerances, parasitic parameters, and measurement accuracy.The results demonstrate that the LCC-S topology offers excellent output stability and high efficiency potential in lithium-battery wireless charging applications, providing both theoretical guidance and practical reference for achieving efficient and reliable CC/CV charging.

title: Hybrid Legal BERT and Rule Engine Model for Compliance Monitoring in Financial Regulatory Frameworks
authors: Joel Osei-Asiamah, Narinder Kumar Bhasin, Ramanjeet Kaur, Iskandar Muda, Sajiv G, Alamelu Alias Rajasree S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The financial industry becomes more and more complicated in meeting regulatory requirements because of the change of legal standards and vagueness of the language used in the legislation. The classical rule-based approaches do not have the capability to accommodate linguistic variation, and standalone machine learners are incapable of imposing rigid legal rules. The proposed research is a Hybrid Legal BERT and Rule Engine Framework of clause-level compliance monitoring integrating semantic interpretation and deterministic logic validation.
