The Legal BERT model of fine-tuning performs the classification of contextual clauses, and the rule engine checks the compliance with KYC, AML, and Basel frameworks. This two-facet mechanism makes it more precise, easy to interpret, and has a broad coverage to regulate, reaching 98 % accuracy in financial documents. The framework identifies minor and significant policy violations and provides a transparent, dynamic, and interpretable solution, which is an intelligent audit assistant, allowing institutions to predict risks, keep compliance, and ensure ongoing regulatory transparency in volatile financial conditions.

title: A Modular AI-Powered CCTV Surveillance Architecture for Action Recognition and Decision Support
authors: M. Sathiya, S Girija Lakshmi, Bala Murgan S, Mounish S, Soundarya S, Ziya Sherzeen S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive AI-powered intelligent CCTV surveillance system integrating real-time computer vision, multi-object tracking, rule-based safety monitoring, and natural language query capabilities. The proposed system comprises four integrated subsystems: Vision (YOLOv11 detection, pose estimation, action recognition, attribute detection), Tracking (ByteTrack, Re-ID embedding, Kalman filtering), Intelligence (polygon-zone monitoring, JSON rules engine, automated alert dispatch), and Chatbot (intent classification, database queries, evidence retrieval). The system achieves 95%+ overall detection accuracy, 77.3% MOTA tracking performance, and processes surveillance feeds at 30 frames per second with 35-40ms end-to-end latency. Experimental validation demonstrates the system's capability to answer natural language queries such as "Did I lock the doorƒ" within 3 seconds while providing video evidence with 96% confidence. The architecture supports real-time safety compliance monitoring in industrial environments, automated threat detection in security applications, and instant evidence retrieval for household scenarios. Performance evaluation on standard benchmarks confirms the system's suitability for deployment on RTX 3080-class hardware supporting 4-camera configurations. The integrated approach reduces manual video review time from 15 minutes to 3 seconds and enables proactive safety interventions through automated alerts with severity-based escalation protocols.

title: Two-Stage CNN-Based Pose Estimation for Uncooperative Axisymmetric Spacecraft
authors: Toma Takahashi, Yudai Furuta, Toshinori Kuwahara
abstract: Pose estimation of uncooperative spacecraft, which is essential for on-orbit servicing, is challenging for highly axisymmetric targets such as rocket bodies. For these targets, accuracy is degraded because the rotation around the symmetric axis is difficult to observe. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a new CNN-based estimation framework that divides the problem into two stages. In the first stage, the framework estimates the rotation around the camera’s line-of-sight to normalize the input image. In the second stage, it estimates the remaining pose components from this simplified image, thereby reducing the overall estimation complexity. Through evaluation experiments using custom datasets of both axisymmetric (H-IIA R/B) and nonaxisymmetric (SOHO satellite) spacecraft, we demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms the accuracy of conventional single-stage estimation. Especially, for the axisymmetric target, the proposed method achieved a median absolute angular error of 0.59°, compared to the best-performing single-stage method result of 1.89°. These results indicate that an approach that progressively simplifies the estimation process is highly effective for the pose estimation of axisymmetric spacecraft, which includes the rotation about the symmetric axis.

title: High Isolation Based 2-Port MIMO Antenna for X, K,Ku, and Ka bands for 5G and mm-wave Application
authors: Dipanjan Dutta, T. Shanmuganantham
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: We propose a high-gain, multi-wideband Dielectric Resonator Antenna (DRA) utilizing an orthogonality technique to minimize interference and noise, achieving a low correlation coefficient. Operating across X, Ku, K, and Ka bands, the design incorporates a Defected Ground Structure (DGS) with two cylindrical and two semi-circular cuts, alongside a hybrid DRA with horizontal and vertical cuts. A microstrip feed enhances impedance matching, controls electric field distribution, and increases bandwidth, ensuring optimal performance across the targeted frequency bands. The antenna operates at four resonant frequencies: 12.6 GHz, 18.1 GHz, 30.5 GHz, and 40.4 GHz, with good impedance matching and bandwidths of 11.11%, 42.92%, 18.42%, and 14.18%, respectively. Transmission coefficients are below -30.41 dB, - 33.74 dB, -26.74 dB, and -29.66 dB. The achieved gains are 5.78 dB, 6.23 dB, 7.51 dB, and 8.19 dB, with radiation efficiencies exceeding 97% across all bands. Axial ratios remain below 3 dB, with values of 1.652 dB at 18.62 GHz and 1.746 dB at 40.4 GHz. Low Envelope Correlation Coefficients (ECC) of 0.0004 to 0.002 ensure minimal interference. This antenna is ideal for satellite communications, military aircraft, defense tracking, and radar systems.

title: Enhancing Data Privacy in Financial Fraud Detection based on Encryption Techniques for Securing Credit Card Transactions
authors: Suchismita Chatterjee
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Financial technology (Fintech) is on a constant rise, thus, security requirements on financial data have taken a high level. Sensitive data integrity protection is increasingly becoming complex due to the daily growth of digital networks and the continued growth of cloud technology. Encryption techniques are emerging as a meaningful instrument to these purposes, transforming plaintext into ciphertext that is resistant to unauthorized usage and probability attacks. The security of audited financial data is a crucial consideration because more organizations are subject to cybercrimes and focus on compliance. The security has to be high so that the sensitive information is not stolen in the continuously increasing crime of financial fraud committed through credit cards. This paper enhances the privacy of data during fraud detection by utilizing AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) with key sizes of 128-bit, 192-bit, and 256-bit. The method involves data preprocessing of credit card fraud detection information, encryption and decryption and reporting the time spent in the entire process of the operation to ascertain the level of efficiency of the encryption and decryption process. The encryption level is determined by a variety of performance measures such as encryption overhead and throughput. This test indicates that the throughput of AES- 256 is the highest (3,685,374.87 bytes/sec) with acceptable encryption overhead (850157642 bytes/sec) on all the key sizes. The nodes comparison and timing analysis of various encryption schemes also indicate AES-192 has a better encryption/throughput ratio compared to others. The findings highlight the benefits of applying AES in securing financial transactions, and the computational cost as to the applicability of encryption-based solutions in financial fraud prevention.

title: Automated Ocular Disease Screening using Machine Learning and Deep CNN Architectures: Implications for U.S. Ophthalmic Care
authors: Dipta Paul, Md Mehedi Hasan, Sujoy Saha, Md Ashraful Islam Nayem
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The increasing prevalence of ocular diseases such as diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and cataract highlights the urgent need for accurate and scalable automated screening systems to support early diagnosis and prevent vision loss. In the United States recent advances in medical image analysis and deep learning have enabled the development of intelligent frameworks for retinal disease detection using fundus images. In this study, an automated ocular disease screening framework is proposed using deep learning architectures, including a conventional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), VGG16, MobileNetV2, and ResNet-15 V2. The models are trained and evaluated on a curated dataset of 4,217 retinal fundus images categorized into four classes: Normal, Cataract, Diabetic Retinopathy, and Glaucoma. Experimental results demonstrate that ResNet-15 V2 achieves the highest classification accuracy of 90.52%, while MobileNetV2 provides competitive performance with lower computational complexity, making it suitable for resource-constrained environments. The findings confirm that deep learning-based screening systems can effectively enhance early ocular disease detection and support practical deployment in modern ophthalmic care systems.

title: Distributed Online Constrained Convex Optimization With Compressed Communication_supp1-3676217.pdf
authors: Jiaxu Liu
abstract: We consider a distributed online convex optimization problem with inequality constraints, where streaming data is distributed among computing agents over a peer-to-peer network. A key challenge in such settings is the high communication load, which has become a bottleneck for the efficiency of distributed algorithms. To address this issue, we propose a consensus-based primal-dual gradient algorithm with compressed communication (DC-DOPDGD) that employs carefully designed compression operators to significantly reduce the amount of transmitted data, along with an error-compensation technique effectively eliminates compression errors. Both dynamic and static network regret bounds are analyzed for the proposed algorithm, and network constraint violation is used to measure constraint violation. Particularly, we prove that the proposed algorithm achieves a static regret bound of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(T^{\max \lbrace \kappa, 1-\kappa \rbrace })$</tex-math></inline-formula>, while the violation of constraints is bounded by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\mathit {T}^{1-\kappa /2})$</tex-math></inline-formula> for convex losses, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathit {T}$</tex-math></inline-formula> is the time horizon and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa \in (0,1)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, which generalize those of centralized algorithms. For strongly convex objectives, our algorithm achieves a static regret bound of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\log (\mathit {T}))$</tex-math></inline-formula> and a constraint violation bound of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\sqrt{\log (\mathit {T})\mathit {T}})$</tex-math></inline-formula>, which also matches that of centralized algorithms and achieves the state of the art in distributed scenarios. Numerical simulations validate our theoretical findings and demonstrate significant reductions in the number of transmitted bits compared to uncompressed distributed counterparts with inequality constraints.

title: Black-Box Attacks on DOA Estimation
authors: Shilian Zheng, Xiaoxiang Wu, Luxin Zhang, Peihan Qi, Weiguo Shen, Keqiang Yue, Zhijin Zhao, Xiaoniu Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Despite significant advancements in deep learning-based DOA estimation, such models exhibit critical vulnerabilities to adversarial attacks. Previous studies of attacks on DOA estimation models typically require full knowledge of the target model, which is impractical in real-world black-box scenarios. In this paper, we explore two black-box adversarial attack strategies for deep learning-based DOA models: transfer-based and decision-based attacks. Decision-based attacks iteratively generate adversarial samples by querying the model’s hard-label predictions, while transfer-based attacks craft perturbations using surrogate models with accessible architectures and data. We systematically investigate how the architecture, depth, and input of the surrogate model affect attack transferability. Under an 8-element uniform linear array setting, experimental results reveal that both strategies can substantially degrade the performance of deep learning-based DOA models and reach attack success rates above 88% in black-box settings. Crucially, we further demonstrate that adversarial samples designed for deep learning models also impair classical subspace-based DOA estimation methods, yielding RMSE values that are 1.39 to 50.77 times higher than those under AWGN, depending on the attack type.

title: Stability Degradation Induced by Angle–Voltage Coupling in Power Systems: Bode-Type Fundamental Performance Limitation Analysis
authors: Xiaoyu Peng, Zhongze Li, Cong Fu, Peng Yang, Zhaojian Wang, Feng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a novel perspective on understanding how the interaction between phase-angle and voltage dynamics affects the stability of inverter-dominated power systems. To explore this, we analyze Bode’s integral of frequency-domain sensitivity functions, which reveals the underlying law that limits the system’s ability to reject disturbances. Our finding indicates that, although the “total” sensitivity integrals remain conservative in angle-voltage coupled systems, the coupling reshapes their distribution between phase-angle and voltage dynamics, potentially worsening system stability. This finding is generalized into fundamental laws named sensitivity conservation and its breaking in this paper. In addition to the mechanism, our work also suggests that the stability could be improved by appropriately redistributing sensitivity across different frequency bands, diverse devices, or between phase-angle and voltage dynamics. However, this redistribution must be conducted carefully, as the sensitivity breaking law indicates that improving the performance of one aspect may lead to instability of another. This law cannot be bypassed directly by any controllers with a generic feedback structure, underscoring the fundamental limit of angle-voltage coupling dynamics and fostering a broader understanding of its nature. The proposed mechanism provides a core principle for control design in angle-voltage coupled power systems.

title: Analyzing of Stable Operating Regions and Adaptive Suppression Strategy for Low-Frequency Oscillations in PMSG-Based Wind Power Systems
authors: Gangui Yan, Fu Guo, Cheng Yang, Yupeng Wang
abstract: Low-frequency oscillation (LFO) in grid-connected permanent magnet synchronous generator (PMSG)-based wind power systems is influenced by multi-factor coupling effects. To reveal the oscillation mechanisms, to characterize the stability region, and to develop mitigation methods are critical for ensuring stable operation. This paper constructs a multiloop damping torque analysis model for the reduced-order gridconnected wind farm system, thoroughly analyzes the complex interactions and mechanisms between grid strength, active power output levels, control parameters, and damping paths from a damping perspective, and accurately identifies the dominant factors of LFO. Furthermore, this study precisely characterizes the stability region by quantitatively analyzing the applicability of dominant control parameters under different active power output levels and grid strengths, and proposes an adaptive lowfrequency oscillation suppression strategy from the perspective of parameter optimization. Simulation results fully validate the accuracy of the proposed stability region and the effectiveness of the adaptive oscillation suppression strategy.

title: Semi-analytical Homogenized Model of AC Loss in HTS Stacks under External Field
authors: Liufei Shen, Cheng Zhang, Shoudao Huang, Yujia Zhai
abstract: Accurately calculating the AC losses of DC-carrying coils under external fields is essential for assessing the electromagnetic performance of HTS devices. However, due to the significantly non-uniform current density distribution at the stack ends, calculating magnetic AC loss in large-scale superconducting systems remains challenging. In this work, we propose a semi-analytical homogenized modeling method to enhance computational efficiency while improving model accuracy. The proposed method consists of two steps. First, the integral method submodel, containing only superconducting tapes, calculates the current density distribution of the whole stack and defines the parabolic critical boundary function. Second, the coil submodel in COMSOL application introduces a gradient auxiliary function of the parabolic function, allowing effective homogenized division. The results show that the relative accuracy of magnetic AC loss calculation in our proposed model is improved by several times compared to the previous lognormal model. This indicates that the model enables more accurate division of stack models with non-uniform current density distribution, thereby effectively calculating the AC loss in large-scale HTS systems under external magnetic fields.

title: Coverage Analysis for Integrated Satellite-Terrestrial Downlink Networks
authors: Jungbin Yim, Jeonghun Park, Namyoon Lee
abstract: Integrated satellite–terrestrial networks (ISTNs) of fer a compelling architecture for extending coverage beyond the limits of terrestrial infrastructure, Yet, despite their growing practical relevance, a unified and tractable mathematical frame work for analyzing their performance remains underdeveloped. In this paper, we develop a stochastic-geometry-based frame work for analyzing the downlink coverage of multi-tier ISTNs operating over orthogonal frequency bands. We model terrestrial and satellite base stations as independent homogeneous Poisson point processes deployed on concentric spheres of different radii, capturing the inherent three-dimensional geometry of integrated networks. A key technical step is a displacement principle that projects base stations from distinct spherical layers onto equivalent rings while preserving the distance distribution seen by a typical user. This transformation enables a tractable characterization of interference and coverage. Incorporating Shadowed-Rician fading for satellite links and orthogonal spectrum allocation across tiers, we derive closed-form expressions for downlink coverage probability under general system parameters. The analysis reveals a fundamental design insight: network performance is maximized when the user association density across tiers is optimally balanced according to the spatial densities and channel statistics of each layer. Numerical simulations confirm the accuracy of the analytical results and illustrate the interplay between geometry, fading, and user distribution in determining ISTN performance.

title: Torque improvement with Flask-shaped Magnet for Spoke-type IPM Motor
authors: Ryonosuke Otsuka, Yuki Hidaka
abstract: Spoke-type interior permanent magnet motors (IPM motors) have merit of their high torque density; however, their advantage is limited by the leakage flux occurring in the outer bridges. In this study, a novel rotor structure was proposed to address this issue by utilizing flask-shaped magnets instead of conventional rectangular magnets. By adopting the flask-shaped magnet, a structure was achieved in which the magnet engaged with the rotor core in the centrifugal direction. Furthermore, by contacting the rotor core with a large surface, a structure was realized in which even the thin ribs on the inner bridge could sufficiently hold the magnet. This design enabled the complete elimination of the outer bridges, thereby reducing the leakage flux. The novelty of the proposed structure lies in its ability to completely eliminate leakage flux paths, and ensure centrifugal force resistance, which is unattainable with conventional designs. The effectiveness of this structure was confirmed through numerical and experimental verifications. The obtained results revealed a significant reduction in leakage flux and a 6.6% improvement in the maximum torque. Moreover, efficiency map of the proposed motor showed that higher efficiency can be obtained on low rotational speed conditions. In the high-speed range, the efficiency of the proposed motor was lower than the conventional method which is due to the larger weakening flux current caused by the increased rotor flux. This study demonstrated that the proposed bridgeless structure is highly effective for applications that require high torque and efficiency at low-to-medium rotational speeds.

title: BRep-GD : a Graph Diffusion Model for CAD Boundary Representation Generation
authors: Feiwei Qin, Chenqi Luo, Junhao Hou, Meie Fang, Ligang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: In modern computer-aided design (CAD), Boundary Representation (B-rep) is a widely used geometric modeling technique in industrial design and manufacturing. However, existing B-rep generation methods, which rely on tree-based hierarchies to represent and generate B-reps, fail to fully exploit the inherent graph structure of B-reps, resulting in suboptimal efficiency and model quality. To address this issue, we propose BRep GD, a graph diffusion-based model specifically designed for B rep generation. Unlike prior methods, BRep-GD treats B-reps as graphs, where nodes represent face elements, and edges represent boundary and vertex elements. By utilizing a continuous topological graph data structure, BRep-GD overcomes the challenges associated with directly applying graph diffusion models to B-rep generation. Specifically, BRep-GD introduces a graph diffusion method tailored to the features of CAD data, generating faces and edges sequentially. During edge generation, continuous topology decoupling is employed to avoid the need for global attention calculations, reducing computational complexity while ensuring geometric consistency and high-quality results. Experimental results demonstrate that BRep-GD outperforms existing state of-the-art methods in both unconditional and class-conditional generation tasks, particularly in generating watertight solids and handling complex geometries. It significantly reduces isolated or inconsistent geometric components and improves generation efficiency. The related code, pre-trained models, and dataset are available at https://github.com/lcqii/BRep-GD.

title: Compression in 3D Gaussian Splatting: A Survey of Methods, Trends, and Future Directions_supp1-3676048.pdf
authors: Muhammad Ali
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has recently emerged as a pioneering approach in explicit scene rendering and computer graphics. Unlike traditional neural radiance field (NeRF) methods, which typically rely on implicit, coordinate-based models to map spatial coordinates to pixel values, 3DGS utilizes millions of learnable 3D Gaussians. Its differentiable rendering technique and inherent capability for explicit scene representation and manipulation positions 3DGS as a potential game-changer for the next generation of 3D reconstruction and representation technologies. This enables 3DGS to deliver real-time rendering speeds while offering unparalleled editability levels. However, despite its advantages, 3DGS suffers from substantial memory and storage requirements, posing challenges for deployment on resource-constrained devices. In this survey, we provide a comprehensive overview focusing on the scalability and compression of 3DGS. We begin with a detailed background overview of 3DGS, followed by a structured taxonomy of existing compression methods. Additionally, we analyze and compare current methods from the topological perspective, evaluating their strengths and limitations in terms of fidelity, compression ratios, and computational efficiency. Furthermore, we explore how advancements in efficient NeRF representations can inspire future developments in 3DGS optimization. Finally, we conclude with current research challenges and highlight key directions for future exploration.

title: ICAIQSA 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)

title: Welcome
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)

title: Bargaining-based Approach for Dynamic Operating Envelope Allocation in Distribution Networks
authors: Zhisen Jiang, Ye Guo
abstract: In this paper, we develop a novel bargaining-based approach for the allocation of Dynamic Operating Envelopes (DOEs) in distribution networks (DNs). Addressing challenges of voltage and line power flow overlimits resulting from reverse power flow, DOEs are allocated by Distribution System Operators (DSOs) to aggregators, imposing time-variant export power limits at various nodes. The proposed method integrates the upper and lower DOE constraints requested by aggregators into the DOE allocation model. Consequently, the DOEs assigned to aggregators adhere to their respective requirements, ensuring a more efficient network capacity allocation. As a result of the Nash Bargaining-based solution, the allocation of DOE is more fair. A test on a 4-bus system geometrically interprets the principle for our DOE allocation methods, and a 141-bus system test validates the effectiveness of the proposed DOE allocation method.

title: Influence of Shielding on Conducted EMI in High-Power Wireless Power Transfer Systems
authors: Mattia Simonazzi, Vincenzo Cirimele, Riccardo Mandrioli, Leonardo Sandrolini
publish: IEEE Journal on Wireless Power Technologies
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) systems are widely used in automotive and consumer electronics due to the many benefits they can offer. However, according to the inherent presence of power electronics and extended electromagnetic parts, they can be the source of significant electromagnetic interference (EMI). This paper investigates the conducted emissions (CEs) of a 1 kW WPT system, assessing the effect of the conductive shields of the coil assemblies on the CEs through a purely experimental approach. The study also considers the effect of power modulation implemented by a phase-shift control, and highlights its impact on the overall CEs behaviour. The objective is to provide a quantitative insight into the role of the shielding in WPT CEs, considering both the conditions of grounded and ungrounded shields. The measurements are conducted over the DC bus of a GaN-based commercially available evaluation board in order to guarantee the highest level of reproducibility of the results. The CEs are measured with an EMI receiver and the impedance seen from the system under test to the power supply is stabilized with a line impedance stabilization network (LISN).

title: Research on Multi-time-scale Voltage-reactive Power Cooperative Optimization Model and Algorithm for Distribution Networks Incorporating Flexible On-load Tap-changing Transformers
authors: Chang Cheng, Jiangang Dai, Lei Wu, Yue Guan, Linxiang Gao, Haiyang Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: In response to voltage fluctuation issues in distribution networks caused by high penetration of renewable energy sources, this paper presents a coordinated control method based on the Flexible On-load Voltage Regulating Transformer (FOVRT). Firstly, a mathematical model of the FOVRT is established, and its operational characteristics are analyzed. Subsequently, a day-ahead and real-time multi-timescale dynamic optimization control framework is proposed. The day-ahead optimization utilizes forecasted data and aims to minimize both power losses and voltage deviations, thereby formulating the daily operation plan for discrete reactive power compensation devices. The intraday real-time rolling optimization fully leverages the fast and precise regulation capability of the FOVRT to perform minute-level compensation for voltage deviations resulting from renewable energy fluctuations. Simulations are conducted on a modified IEEE 33-node distribution system. The results demonstrate that the proposed control framework effectively improves the system voltage profile and reduces network losses, verifying its feasibility and superiority.

title: Cross-Domain Transfer Learning Framework for Personalized Recommendation Systems Leveraging Heterogeneous Big Data Streams
authors: Joel Osei-Asiamah, Malik Bader Alazzam, Ajmeera Kiran, Kamila Ibragimova, Sajiv G, N Dharani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Heterogeneous big data streams are used to engineer an effective cross-domain transfer learning framework for personalized recommendation systems that effectively leverages. Existing recommendation models have challenges with dealing with data sparsity, weak domain adaptability, and a lack of the capability to handle multi-format real-time data, thus not performing as effectively in dynamic environments. The suggested DANN-CF framework integrates Domain-Adversarial Neural Networks (DANN) and Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF) to allow the model to learn domain-invariant user tastes from diverse data sources such as ratings, reviews, and item features. The Douban Dataset (Ratings, Reviews, Side Information) validates the system's performance across different domains such as movies, books, and music. Implemented on the Python platform with Apache Flink and TensorFlow on simulated data streams, DANN-CF improves precision while promoting scalability and flexibility. It greatly enhances recommendation precision over traditional single-domain models with an RMSE value of 0.1687, personalized content presentation, and smart education through accurate, real-time, cross-context recommendations.

title: A Deep Learning–based Text-to-Braille Translation Framework: A Case Study on an Indic Script
authors: Navaneeth Bhaskar, Ashritha K P, Priyanka Tupe-Waghmare, Varadaraj K S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Access to braille material for low-resource and morphologically complex scripts is limited, which is problematic for the inclusive education of visually impaired learners. This paper presents a deep learning-based text-to-Braille translation framework by taking Kannada language as a case study, as it has a rich character structure that consists of vowels, consonants, compound characters, numerals, and special symbols. A unique dataset containing 734 characters of Kannada Scripts that represent vowels, consonants, complex characters, numbers, and special symbols was prepared and further augmented to create more than 49,912 images to overcome variation such as handwriting, lighting, and noise so that the system becomes more robust. The proposed system uses a MobileNetV2-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model, using transfer learning, which allows the model to quickly and accurately recognize the characters while keeping the computational complexity low. The recognized character is then translated to the corresponding Unicode and numerical representation in Braille, to allow compatibility with both digital Braille displays and printed Braille sheets. Experimental evaluation proves that the proposed framework achieves a classification accuracy of 99.49%. Comparative analysis is conducted against several other baseline deep learning architectures to ensure the effectiveness of the proposed deep learning architecture. Precision, recall and F1-score values provide further evidence of the robustness and reliability of the system. The proposed solution is scalable, language-independent and open to extension for other scripts, providing a good basis for multilingual and assistive Braille translation systems.

title: Exploring Nature-Inspired Metaheuristics for Intelligent and AI-Based Optimization Techniques
authors: Manickavasagam Suruthi, Narayanan Ganesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Optimization problems and AI-assisted systems face significant key challenges when addressing complex optimization problems. Conventional optimization methods often show typically perform poorly when applied to nonlinear or high-dimensional search spaces, leading to increased interest in nature-based metaheuristic algorithms. This study discusses five recently advanced metaheuristic algorithms: the Gazelle Optimization Algorithm (GOA), Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm (HOA), Prairie Dog Optimization Algorithm (PDO), Egret Swarm Optimization Algorithm (ESOA), and Enhanced African Vulture Optimization Algorithm (EAVOA). Their performances are compared based on 23 test functions commonly used in the literature. In addition, all five algorithms will be compared based on their ability to converge quickly, overall accuracy, robustness, and stability. The results of this experiment show that EAVOA provided the best overall performance with the lowest average fitness value on 78% of the test functions, along with rapid convergence rates for both HOA and PDO for the unimodal functions, whereas GOA demonstrated superior global exploration. The ESOA demonstrated stability through the balance of exploration versus exploitation. The results should help inform the selection of optimization techniques to support the real-world applications of intelligent computing processes.

title: A Self-Supervised Learning Framework for Low-Resource Computer Vision Applications
authors: Rupam Sah, Praveen Gopal Gaonkar, Manas Singha, Rupam Bhagawati, Vengatesan.K, Aman Aditya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The application of deep learning has resulted in significant advancements in computer vision systems; nonetheless, extensive labelled datasets and processing resources are required. In a lot of different domains, particularly emerging countries and edge-based deployments, there is a lack of labelled data, annotation is expensive, and computational resources is limited. An innovative Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) architecture has been developed as a result of this research. It is designed for low-resource computer vision applications that have limited labelled data and computational resources. Using unlabelled data, pretext tasks, representation consistency learning, and lightweight feature encoders, the proposed method is able to learn strong visual representations without the need for manual annotation. Under low-label regimes, the suggested method achieves better results than the usual supervised and transfer learning baselines when applied to benchmark datasets. The use of self-supervised learning makes it possible to create reliable computer vision systems in situations where resources are limited, and it is also scalable.

title: Universal Structural-Semantic Model of Design Patterns
authors: Sinisa Vlajic, Ana Trujic, Milos Milic, Dusan Savic, Ana Poledica
abstract: This paper introduces the Universal StructuralSemantic Model of Design Patterns (USSMDP), a minimal and formal framework that explicitly separates a pattern's structural skeleton from its semantic and behavioral specification. USSMDP represents structure using Basic Design Pattern Structure of the Solution (BDPSS) and captures semantic intent, responsibilities, and operational relations through Basic Design Pattern Semantic Module (BDPSM). This separation enables unambiguous specification of pattern participants and their roles while remaining independent of implementation details and modeling notation. The approach is demonstrated through full structural-semantic formalizations of three representative GoF patterns - Abstract Factory, Composite, and Observer-covering creation, composition, and behavioral dependency management, respectively. The results show that USSMDP provides concise, machine-readable yet human-interpretable pattern specifications, suitable for systematic analysis, education, and further automation.

title: Hybrid Blockchain-Based Multi-Factor Authentication Online Voting System
authors: Sanika Kathe, Sakshi Hatmode, Manas Gotarne, Mansi Deore
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The Hybrid Blockchain-Based Multi-Factor Authentication Online Voting System is a new online voting platform that uses blockchain technology, face/biometric authentication, and strong encryption methods to ensure that this system is secure, transparent, and tamper-proof elections. This system uses a custom and private blockchain to store each vote as an irreversible transaction, as opposed to conventional or typical electronic voting systems that rely on centralized databases and could be vulnerable to insider threats or data manipulation. It also has multi-factor authentication with passwords, facial scanning, and OTP verification for the validation of the voter’s identity. This paper outlines the system’s design, workflow, key algorithms, and module design, along with details on how it is implemented using modern web frameworks and cryptographic methods. The evaluation shows the platform’s ability to prevent duplicate voting, protect voter privacy, and enable clear result generation. The study ends by discussing the system’s potential for large-scale use and future improvements, such as offline voting and mobile integration.

title: Coupled Modeling and Optimization of Heterogeneous Mobile Energy Storage Units in Power Systems Considering Variable Transportation Delays
authors: Xuanqi Wang, Qingchun Hou, Yi Ding
abstract: Mobile Energy Storage Units (MESUs) enhance grid flexibility by combining battery storage with spatial mobility. We develop a coupled power–transportation model with variable travel delays, embedding a heterogeneous time–space network in a mixed-integer unit-commitment formulation that co-optimizes MESU routing/charging and thermal generation while respecting generator, network, and mobility limits. This framework represents heterogeneous fleets with type-specific capacities, power ratings, and travel speeds, capturing diverse mobility–energy characteristics in a unified decision space. Tests on modified IEEE 6- and 30-bus systems show that MESUs smooth peaks and reduce operating costs, with net benefit governed by a trade-off among fleet size, travel speed, and energy capacity—underscoring the economic promise of mobility-aware storage.

title: Low-Power Priority-Based Arbiter for Optimal On-Chip Resource Allocation in SoC Architectures
authors: Ragapreeti D, Rajesh Kumar R, Padma Priya M, Jeevitha K, Karthikeyan C, R Babuji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Effective allocation of resources is an important necessity in today's digital systems, primarily in power-sensitive platforms like embedded systems, multi-core processors, and Network-on-Chip (NoC) interconnects. Standard arbiters, though popular, typically exhibit high dynamic power dissipation, latency, and biased resource allocation based on static priority policies [1]. This paper introduces Low-Power Priority-Based Arbiter design realized on FPGA platforms that incorporates energy-efficient mechanisms like clock gating, reduced signal transitions, and dynamic scheduling of priorities [2]. The design switches off unnecessary clocking during idle periods, adjusts the priority levels reactively to avoid starvations of requests that hold low ranks, and reduces unnecessary signal toggle rates to minimize switch transition power [3]. Experimental results thus obtained through Xilinx ISE prove that the Low-Power Priority-Based Arbiter realizes appreciable power reduction over state-of-the-art arbitration techniques, without trading off latency and fairness [4]. Comparative evaluation verifies improvement in power efficiency, throughput, and resource utilization [5]. The proposed solution is a scalable and reliable solution for modern-day digital architecture designs, opening up suitability for energy-sensitive application domains and paving the way for future works through adaptive workload modeling and machine learning–based arbitration policies [6].

title: STRIDE Framework: An AI-Driven Multidimensional Clustering Approach to Assess G20 Economic Readiness
authors: Agnus Infanta B, M. Jayanthi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Inclusion, Wellbeing and Sustainability are often looked as multi-dimensional aspect of development but traditional country classifications usually neglect and ignores these like World Bank income groups depends on Gross national income (GNI) per capita). This study introduces AI data driven approach that clusters G20 economies based on STRIDE framework. The six pillars of STRIDE are: Startups & Entrepreneurship, Technology & Digitalization, Research & Innovation, Infrastructure, Deployment Capacity and Enhancing Skills & Education. Data were collected from World Bank, National Statistical reports and ILO. Unsupervised machine learning techniques like including k-means, hierarchical clustering and Principal Component Analysis were used to test the data and the results show three varied clusters among G20 countries with significant differences confirmed by Chi Square test and PCA explained 66.9 % of variance (first two components highlighting structural disparity). The findings highlight the limitation of GDP centric metric and offers holistic perspective for policy making aligning with SDG. This work provides a replicable methodology to educate capability based economic classifications.

title: Explainable Reinforcement Learning Based Real-Time P2P Energy Trading for Electro-Thermal Microgrids
authors: Rongqi Guo, Hong Liu, Junkai Li, Zipeng Liang, Chi Yung Chung, Baijie Yang
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy sources into distribution networks in recent years, the role of users is gradually transitioning from that of mere consumers to "prosumers". To address the issue of low computational efficiency associated with traditional mathematical optimization methods for solving multi-microgrid P2P energy trading, this paper proposes a real-time market clearing method based on reinforcement learning. First, a non-cooperative game model for microgrids is formulated, incorporating electro-thermal coupling characteristics. The existence and uniqueness of the market’s Nash Equilibrium are then theoretically proven. Subsequently, a solution framework based on the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) algorithm is designed. By learning the market equilibrium patterns through offline training, the framework can rapidly determine the clearing price during online execution based on the real-time state of each microgrid. This method satisfies the real-time requirements of the electricity market, and its reward function, designed based on the mathematical features of market equilibrium, ensures strong algorithmic explainability. Case studies validate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method.

title: AI Integration in E-Commerce: Transforming Customer Interactions and Operational Strategies
authors: Amit Kumar Jaiswal, Tanmay Jain, Ajit Sourav, Abhinav Kumar
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: In the e-commerce sector, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a disruptive force that is changing how companies engage with clients and streamline operations. An AI-integrated e-commerce system utilizing machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and BERT-based recommendation models is presented in this paper. The suggested system was assessed using precision, recall, $\mathbf{F 1}$-score, and AUC metrics on a dataset of more than $\mathbf{1. 2}$ million product reviews. The results show that the optimized BERT model outperformed traditional recommendation models like Logistic Regression (0.76) and LSTM (0.83) with an F1-score of 0.90. The results demonstrate how AI can improve customer happiness, boost suggestion accuracy by 15-20%, and boost operational efficiency through automation and predictive analytics. But issues like algorithmic prejudice, moral dilemmas, and data privacy continue to exist. Overall, this study provides quantitative proof of AI’s efficacy and future potential while highlighting its dual function in improving customer engagement and operational optimization in e-commerce.

title: Alternative TSO-DSO Cooperation Scheme based on Imbalance Band Market
authors: Yong Soon Kim, Yong Hyun Song, Seung Wan Kim
abstract: With increased proliferation of distributed renewable energy sources (DRESs), net load variability and uncertainty in distribution systems increase significantly, causing imbalances at TSO-DSO interfaces. It has become inefficient for the transmission system operator (TSO) to independently manage all resources and handle imbalance management. To address this, this paper proposes a decentralized day-ahead operation method where the TSO and regional distribution system operators (DSOs) share balancing responsibilities. The proposed method employs Interval Unit Commitment (IUC) to determine imbalance generation scheduling, reserve procurement and set marginal prices for energy and imbalance bands. By leveraging imbalance correlation, the TSO reduces total reserve capacity costs while maintaining reliability. This study further examines how imbalance bands affect reserve pricing and congestion costs, ensuring these factors are properly reflected in energy and band prices. Numerical results demonstrate that cost allocation proportional to imbalance can incentivize DSOs locally management imbalances, relieving TSO responsibilities.

title: NutriTrack: Mobile Nutrition-Label Scanner & Dietary Insight Application
authors: Shrishail Rugge, Trunal Wagh, Parth Mishra, Ravindra Rajhans, Kavitha S
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: NutriTrack is a Flutter-based mobile application that enables users to scan packaged food labels and instantly receive nutrition information and health insights. Unlike traditional OCR-based nutrition apps that rely heavily on barcode lookups or generic text extraction, NutriTrack integrates a hybrid pipeline that combines OpenCV-based preprocessing, structured OCR field validation, and a domain-specific interpretation engine to achieve reliable nutrient-level extraction across diverse label formats. The system incorporates a Flask-based OCR service, a Node.js/MongoDB interpretation backend, and a clean Flutter interface for real-time feedback. The app evaluates key factors including calories, macronutrients, sugar/sodium content, serving inconsistencies, and overall nutritional impact. This paper describes the system design, dataset used for benchmarking, technology stack, and real-world deployment insights, highlighting NutriTrack as a more accurate and accessible alternative for nutrition-label understanding.

title: Leveraging Domain Models and Metamodels to Build Ontologies for Knowledge Capitalization
authors: Gregoire Blanc, Romain Delabeye, Regis Plateaux, Olivia Penas
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: It is acknowledged that expert knowledge is frequently embedded within expert domain models. However, it is only accessible and intelligible to specialists. Exploitation is thus limited, as this knowledge is not extracted into a unique, structured knowledge base to allow its usage by the other members of the organization. As a result, knowledge capitalization and exploitation across heterogeneous models remain a persistent model-based systems engineering (MBSE) challenge, notably in multi-site companies. Existing approaches, such as models transformation or interfacing aim at model interoperability or standardization. They are not focused on a global knowledge enrichment to enable the knowledge extracted from these models to be exploitable by multi-organization experts and non-experts. A way to tackle this issue is to enrich a structured knowledge base from the expert domain models. A multi-layered ontological approach is hence introduced, extracting knowledge from models and storing it in a centralized knowledge base through extension mechanisms. This approach builds upon upper ontologies specialized with core domain ontologies. These ontologies can then be dynamically extended through metamodel- and model-specific layers that capture both modeling expertise and domain-specific knowledge. To validate this approach, a case study demonstrates the enrichment of the Industrial Ontology Foundry core (IOF-core ontology) by two expert domain models from distinct modeling environments (a product-process model and a parametric model). Qualitative results show that the case study capitalizes knowledge, enhances semantic alignment, and enables structured reuse of modeling-based knowledge assets.

title: Crop Vitality: IoT Based Solution for Modern Farming
authors: Swayash Gaikwad, Om Malunjkar, Rohit Mandve, Vaibhavi Mhatre, Mini Namboothiripad
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Modern agriculture faces challenges in ensuring sustainable practices and maximizing crop yields. This paper presents an IoT-based system for efficient plant health monitoring and disease management. The system utilizes Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) values, processed via a Raspberry Pi, to assess plant health and distinguish healthy plants from unhealthy ones. Camera sensors capture real-time, high-resolution data. An image processing algorithm helps to classify the type of disease along with the possible nutrient deficiency, the plant might be facing through the leaf images. Additionally, a stationary system is employed which collects real time data of external environmental parameters around the crop to predict if the conditions are optimal or Suboptimal for the healthy growth of plant. The proposed system delivers actionable insights for timely interventions, optimizing resource utilization and reducing environmental impact. Scalable and adaptable, this solution promotes sustainable agriculture across diverse farming environments, contributing to the resilience of the agricultural sector. To narrow down the study, all the considerations in this paper are done by taking potato plant as the reference plant.

title: Factors Influencing Generation Z's Intention to Use Generative AI: An Acceptance and Threat Avoidance Perspective in Indonesia
authors: Juan Vaughn Verdene Parengkuan, Elfindah Princes
abstract: This preliminary research examines the determinants of Generation Z's intention to use GenAI by integrating the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT) and Technology Threat Avoidance Theory (TTAT), along with trust and perceived risk constructs. Empirical evidence was obtained through the dissemination of an online survey spread through social media channels, yielding 100 valid responses, and analyzed using Structural Equation Modeling (SEM). The results show that effort expectancy and facilitating conditions significantly increase usage intention, while performance expectancy and perceived avoidability strengthen trust. However, performance expectancy does not directly influence intention. Perceived risk is not a significant predictor of trust, and neither social influence nor trust directly affects intention. In contrast, perceived threat shows an unexpected positive association with trust. These findings highlight the dominant role of usability and supportive resources over social or purely psychological factors in shaping generative AI adoption.

title: Heat Pump Capacity Configuration Method Based on the High-Efficiency Interval of the Unit Performance Curve
authors: Kui Wang, Chupeng Xiao, Xiaoming Wang, Wenguang Zhao, Xiaonan Chen, Caihua Liang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address the problems of insufficient theoretical support and unclear selection principles in unit capacity configuration, as well as operation strategies that fail to simultaneously consider high efficiency and service life of units, a mathematical model of heat pump units was established based on a mechanism method. Based on the high-efficiency interval of the unit performance curve, a general capacity configuration method and corresponding operation strategy for heat pump units were proposed. The results indicate that, under heating conditions, the optimal capacity configuration scheme corresponds to a demand satisfaction ratio of 1.09 to 1.10, a capacity ratio greater than or equal to 2.5, and a unit number ratio of 8:4.

title: Deep Learning Approach for Detecting Kidney Abnormalities from Medical Imaging
authors: Jackulin T, Harini K R, Gopika L
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Medical imaging is computed tomography (CT), is the instrumental in detection and diagnosis of the kidney disease. Manual analysis of CT scans, however, is, in most cases, it consumes large amount of time and exposed to inconsistency based on error done by the human. In an effort to replace this manual process, we created a deep learning–based classification system that would automatically analyze the CT kidney images. A publicly accessible dataset was utilized, and appropriate preprocessing measures including balancing, resizing, and augmentation were conducted to provide stable training. The model proposed relies on InceptionV3 architecture using transfer learning and fine-tuning so that the network can leverage pretrained ImageNet features for medical imaging tasks. The system was trained for classifying the CT scans into four classes: Normal, Tumor, Cyst and Stone. Performance based on test , set validated that such model could classify between these classes efficiently and effectively, its ability as a supportive tool for computer- aided diagnosis thus demonstrated. This research is an addition to the utilization of the Deep Learning in the medical imaging and attests to the utility of the same in enhancing diagnostic efficiency in kidney diseases.

title: Harnessing the Power of CNNs for Urban Water Prediction in the Metaverse: Revolutionizing River Waterflow and Water Level Forecasting
authors: Ambhika C, Kamalika M, Monica G K, Gandhavalli Kalyani, Mahadiya Maheen K F
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Urban population growth has led to a substantial increase in water demand worldwide over the past decades. Innovative predictive techniques have been developed using long-term variation analysis to address water scarcity. These techniques enhance the monitoring of river water flow and maintain water levels within specified limits. This study introduces a machine learning algorithm based on convolution neural networks(CNN) designed to optimize the structure and parameters of water resource allocation, thereby preventing scarcity in targeted areas. The proposed method demonstrates high accuracy by collecting sample data from water stations along the Shannon River and incorporating climatic conditions, runoff timing, and specific area feature extraction as parameters. The Water Prediction Deep Learning Neural Network (WPDLNN) aids in analyzing data for specific regions. Furthermore, this research explores the integration of water level prediction within the Metaverse, enabling virtual simulations and AI-driven digital twins for real-time urban water management. By leveraging metaverse-based visualization, policymakers and researchers can simulate different scenarios of water distribution and climate impact in a virtual space. Additionally, our paper compares the accuracy of the proposed approach with other algorithms, such as ANN and SVM

title: Interpretable ML for Diabetes and Prediabetes Screening Using Self-Reported Health Indicators
authors: Sanja Lazic, Stevan Cakic, Isidora Rubezic Lukic, Natasa Popovic, Tomo Popovic
abstract: Early identification of type 2 diabetes (T2D) and prediabetes enables timely interventions, yet screening often relies on self-reported data rather than laboratory testing. This work compares lightweight Machine Learning (ML) models: Logistic Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM), and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) trained on 21 self-reported indicators from the 2015 Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) dataset for threeclass classification (no diabetes, prediabetes, diabetes). We propose a screening-oriented evaluation where a probability threshold is selected to achieve a target sensitivity (recall) of 0.80. LightGBM achieves balanced accuracy of 0.52 and precision of 0.33 at the target sensitivity, with 38 % of cases flagged. Tree SHapley Additive exPlanations (TreeSHAP) highlight general health status, age category, body mass index (BMI), and hypertension as dominant predictors. A FastAPI web application provides individual risk estimates and instance-level explanations. The pipeline demonstrates feasibility of interpretable, calibrated screening from nonlaboratory data.

title: Prospect-Theory-Based Modeling of GENCO Strategies in Electricity–Carbon Coupled Markets
authors: Yuqi Fan, Zhaoyuan Chai, Chen Lv, Rong Pan, Baodi Ding, Xiaohui Qin
abstract: Driven by carbon neutrality goals and carbon pricing reforms, power generation companies (GENCOs) are facing increasingly complex decision-making challenges. This paper first develops a bilevel modeling framework to describe the participation of multiple GENCOs in the electricity–carbon market. Then, the upper-level model is established to capture the optimal decision-making behavior of GENCOs based on prospect theory (PT) theory, and the lower-level model represents the day-ahead (DA) market clearing process. The Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions are applied to reformulate the bilevel structure into a single-level MPEC. Finally, based on a modified IEEE 30-bus system, the equilibrium of the electricity–carbon market is solved and GENCO strategies are analyzed. Simulation results show that increasing carbon preference significantly reduces system emissions and raises carbon prices, while high-emission enterprises exhibit more pronounced strategic shifts, validating the effectiveness of the proposed behavioral model.

title: Deep Unrolled Optimization Networks for Solving High-Dimensional Inverse Problems in Risk-Sensitive Portfolio Engineering
authors: Yogesh Kumar Jain, Rajashri Chaudhari, Anshu Kumar Tripathi, Prachi Beriwala, Vinayak Dagadu Shinde, Sheetal Dayanand Shirke
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The authors proposed a new framework using Deep Unrolled Optimization Networks to solve high-dimensional inverse problems for Risk Sensitive Portfolio Engineering. Classical optimization methods and most Deep Learning Methods are limited as they either lack scalability or interpretability. The proposed framework unrolls an iterative solver into a trainable network thereby providing faster convergence rates, better risk-return trade-offs, and greater robustness with respect to noisy covariance estimates than traditional methods. Simulations were used to demonstrate superior performance of the proposed framework relative to well-established benchmarks (Markowitz, Black-Litterman) in terms of cumulative wealth growth, Sharpe Ratio, Value-at-Risk and drawdown reductions. The simulations suggest that Deep Unrolled Optimization has potential as a means to provide interpretable and computationally efficient tools for practical portfolio management in dynamic and uncertain financial markets.

title: MRI-Guided Brain Tumor Detection UsingYOLOv8 and Adaptive Hybrid Transfer Learning
authors: A.Sangavi, R.Suganya, L.Srinivasan, R.P. Shermy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Brain tumor detection from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans is a critical clinical diagnosis application in which timely and accurate detection directly influences treatment planning and patient survival. Traditional machine learning algorithms and deep learning algorithms, although effective in classification, fall short in precise tumor location identification and real-time appropriateness. This study proposes an improved detection framework on YOLOv8 with Adaptive Hybrid Transfer Learning (AHTL) for multiclass identification of brain tumors, including glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and normal conditions. The Kaggle and Hayatabad Medical Complex dataset was preprocessed with normalization, bounding box annotation, and augmentation for improving the dataset's robustness. YOLOv8 model architecture, promoted by AHTL, is based on leveraging pretrained information from COCO with adaptive finetuning on MRI features to yield improved generalization across variation in tumor shape, size, and location. 50 epochs were used for training with optimization using momentum and early stopping to avoid overfitting. Experimental performance demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed system with precision of 0.93, recall of 0.91, F1-score of 0.92, and mAP@0.5 of 0.94, outperforming standard CNN-based classifiers, segmentation models, and YOLO frameworks. Visual output with bounding boxes also enhanced interpretability to enable radiologists to validate AI-based predictions. Briefly, the novel YOLOv8 + AHTL approach provides a computationally effective, accurate, and clinically interpretable brain tumor detection method that has filled the gap between research experimentation and actual clinical practice. This performance serves as good support for decision making in health care.

title: Trust-Aware Human-in-the-Loop Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Systems
authors: Nagalakshmi Vallabhaneni, Prabhavathy Panneer, Nutalapati Sai Kalyan Ram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The growing use of autonomous systems in the safety-critical areas reveals the value of striking a balance between machine intelligence and human control. Although human in-the-loop reinforcement learning (HRL) has become a promising paradigm toward the realization of this balance, existing methods tend to believe that human feedback is fixed and always absolute. Practically, though, autonomous agents are not so trusted by humanity and may vary depending on the situation, the difficulty of the task, or the previous work of the system. This paper proposes a framework of making training and decision-making processes more human center, which is titled Trust-Aware Human-in-the-Loop Reinforcement Learning (TA-HRL). Including trust estimation in the reward shaping and adjusting of the policy, the framework enables the agent to tune the extent of the dependence on human input as opposed to an autonomous policy. The domain-neutral validation for autonomous driving and human-robot collaboration shows that TA-HRL can not only increase the safety of high-uncertainty states, but also better the efficiency of learning by reducing the over-reliance of any decision model on another, be it human or machine.

title: Gesture-Controlled Mobile Robotic Arm with Vision-Based Object Retrieval for Differently Abled
authors: Rahul R V, Nunna Venkata Sai, Sita Devi Bharatula, Vuppuloori Ravi Sekhara Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of an autonomous robotic vehicle controlled by gestures equipped with a vision-based object detection and retrieval system. The proposed model integrates a Pi as the central processing unit, coordinates motor control through an L298N driver module, and uses a USB camera for real-time image acquisition and path recognition. A one-degree-of-freedom (1-DOF) gripper is mounted on the front end to perform object pickup operations. Each of the five color-coded boxes is preassigned to a unique hand gesture (1–6), allowing the robot to interpret user intent, locate the corresponding box, and navigate autonomously towards it. Once the object is collected, the robot returns to its original position using visual feedback and motion control. The entire system is powered by a Li-Po battery, ensuring efficient and portable operation. The proposed approach demonstrates an effective combination of computer vision, embedded control, and human–robot interaction, offering a cost-effective and adaptable solution for smart automation and assistive robotics.

title: Integration of IOT-Based Remote Monitoring with Cloud-Assisted Patient Database Management Framework Incorporating Historical Health Records
authors: Shilpa Devram Pawar, Ashish Rout, Ratan Singh, Rohit Singh, Sharayu Ashish Kumar Lokhande, Laboneey Sudhir Dhanure
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This paper presents a framework for a cloud-based medical system designed to securely store and share physician and patient information to facilitate the delivery of online medical services. By embracing cloud computing technology, the proposed framework supports a large patient database containing personal information, medical histories, and past prescriptions, as well as an equivalent physician database containing their credentials, specialties, and history of consultations. By centralizing data in the cloud platform, the system allows hospitals to access and exchange patient records with ease, thereby facilitating healthcare providers to view a patient's health history during treatment and make sound medical decisions. In order to ensure the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive information, the platform supports encryption mechanisms and stringent access controls. To protect sensitive records, the platform pairs the JSON Web Token (JWT) login with fine-grained Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) so that only authorized users can view or modify information. Its layered design delivers near-instant access to patient data while complying with HIPAA regulations through end-to-end AES-256 encryption and an optional biometric sign in. Moreover, the implementation of this framework reduced the system's response time by approximately 56 percent, further enhancing its efficiency. Patients and doctors are each provided with a dedicated dashboard. Patients can check their history and prescriptions, while doctors can search, review, and update records after passing the required security checks. In addition, the use of an independent patient health card facilitates the identification and retrieval of patient information among various medical centers. This framework increases efficiency and continuity of care and provides a scalable framework for the management of critical health information among numerous institutions.

title: Incorporating Long-Term Costs of Renewable Energy Generation into Electricity Pricing
authors: Yanning Zhang, Jin Zhong
abstract: Renewable energy generation has almost no variable costs and is no longer suitable for marginal cost pricing. New renewable power-generating facilities have a large amount of fixed costs, which can be determined by long-term average cost to join the final electricity price setting in the electricity market. This paper designs a price offer method that let renewable energy generators use their corresponding Levelized Cost of Electricity (LCOE) as their price offer benchmarks to participate electricity price setting. It uses the Multi-Period Mixed-Integer Nonlinear Programming (MINLP) to conduct the uniform pricing model to study the effect. Results indicate that the proposed price offer benchmark can help maintain reasonable wholesale electricity price in high renewable energy penetration and create incentives for renewable energy development. It provides a way to price the electricity of future green energy system.

title: Optimal Dispatch Strategy for Marine Energy System With Submarine Compressed Air Energy Storage
authors: Xinyu Wang, Hanchen Liu, Sen Cui, Laijun Chen
abstract: With the ongoing transition toward low-carbon energy systems, clean energy sources such as offshore wind power have experienced rapid development. However, due to their inherent randomness and uncertainty, offshore wind power generation are facing challenges in providing comprehensive support to the power grid. Submarine compressed air energy storage (CAES), which involves constructing rigid caverns under seawater for gas storage, offers an effective solution for integrating and utilizing offshore wind energy. A mathematical model for submarine CAES is firstly established. Subsequently, a two-stage robust optimization scheduling model is developed. Simulations are conducted using data from an constructing submarine CAES plant currently, which verifies the correctness of the proposed model and demonstrates the effectiveness of submarine CAES in enhancing the utilization rate of offshore wind power.

title: Adaptive Charging Management of Electric Vehicles with Embedded Operation Regions
authors: Jianshu Zhou, Yue Xiang, Pingliang Zeng
abstract: To address the security risks caused by the disorderly charging of large-scale electric vehicles to the power grid and the delayed handling of power grid security risks due to the slow response and complex calculation of traditional scheduling methods, an EV adaptive charging management with embedded operation regions is proposed. The load of electric vehicles and the output of distributed power generation are considered state variables for security analysis, and an operation regions model is established, taking into account capacity and voltage constraints. The state variables of electric vehicles and other loads are monitored in real-time, and the method of power flow simulation calculation is used to determine whether there is an over-limit situation. According to the over-limit situation of the operation regions, the event-triggered alternating direction method of multipliers decomposes the optimization problem and distributes it to the EV terminals within their regions, achieving local and rapid handling of power grid security risks. An example simulation using the IEEE 33-node system is conducted, and the results verify that the proposed method for electric vehicles can achieve local and rapid accurate handling of power grid security risks.

title: Navigating Digital Transformation in Healthcare: Challenges and Opportunities
authors: El Asli Neila, BoukherroubTasseda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Science and Applications in Industry and Society (CAISAIS)
abstract: This paper explores the recent digital transformation (DT) in healthcare, referred here to as Healthcare 4.0. It examines current literature to understand how healthcare organizations can effectively implement and manage their digital initiatives to maximize the benefits of emerging technologies. Key success factors and challenges are identified, highlighting the potential impacts of DT on performance and service delivery. Our findings suggest that a collaborative approach is essential, emphasizing the importance of multidisciplinary teams, teamwork among medical and IT professionals, and ongoing skills development. Addressing challenges such as regulatory compliance and technical barriers, including data sharing, interoperability, and privacy concerns, is crucial for a successful DT in healthcare organizations.

title: Frequency Estimation for IBR-Integrated Power Systems Using Limited PMU Measurements
authors: Yongxin Xiong, Siqi Bu
abstract: Accurate bus frequency estimation is essential for frequency support and control, particularly with the integration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) increasing. This paper proposes a frequency estimation method for IBR-based power systems using limited information from phasor measurement units (PMUs) installed at source-connected buses. First, the impact of voltage variations on frequency estimation during transient processes is illustrated. Subsequently, the voltages vector at non-source-connected buses are estimated using voltage measurements from source-connected buses. Then, by utilizing frequency and voltage vectors measurements at source-connected buses and the network admittance matrix, a frequency estimation method for IBR-based systems is proposed. As the number of source-connected buses is limited, the proposed method significantly reduces PMU deployment requirements. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with the standard Frequency Divider Formula (FDF), the proposed method improves frequency estimation accuracy, especially under large voltage disturbances.

title: Research on the Topology Structure of Embedded DC Transmission System
authors: Shangan Du, Xiaolin Shen, Yiqi Liu, Xingtong Mao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: In areas with tight transmission corridors, converting AC lines to DC systems can significantly increase the transmission capacity of the transmission channel, greatly improve the utilization efficiency of the transmission corridor, and alleviate the problem of tight transmission corridors. This paper studies the topology of the embedded DC system that converts AC transmission to DC transmission, compares different topology structures such as bipolar metallic neutral line, bipolar ground neutral line, and symmetric monopole, and conducts a comprehensive technical and economic comparison considering equipment selection, project cost, and system feasibility. Finally, a recommended topology structure is proposed, which is suitable for the DC transmission system embedded in the regional power grid.

title: Neuro-Symbolic Reinforcement Learning for Strategic Brand Positioning in Competitive Markets
authors: Praveen Kumar Mannepalli, Ayesha Khan, Amreen Habibullah Khan, Tabassum H Khan, Nadagundla Pavan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This paper had explored the use of neuro-symbolic reinforcement learning (NSRL) for strategic brand positioning in competitive market enviroments. The approach had combined neural networks for perception with symbolic reasoning for constraint based decision making, enabling the agent to adapt with both flexibility and interpretability. A simulated environment had been constructed to model competing brand-agents, consumer sentiment dynamics, and market shocks. The results had shown that NSRL achieved faster convergence, more stable policies, and higher long-term brand equity compared to traditional reinforcement learning baselines. Ablation studies had further highlighted the necessity of both symbolic and neural components. Although synthetic in nature, the findings pointed toward a promising paradigm for marketing strategy, where AI could balance short-term gain with sustainable positioning, avoiding greedy tactics often seen in practice.

title: CARESUM: A Verification-Driven Framework for Clinical Dialogue Summarization with an Intelligent Chatbot Interface
authors: Ivan Dominic, Monish S, Rishi Gattu Nandakumar, Rajeshkannan Regunathan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Clinical dialogue summarization is the automated process of converting multi-turn doctor-patient conversations into concise, structured summaries that preserve essential medical details. Its primary objective is to reduce the documentation burden on clinicians while maintaining the accuracy of critical information. However, despite recent advances in large language models (LLMs), existing systems often suffer from hallucination-producing fluent but factually incorrect statements. In healthcare, such inaccuracies can negatively impact patient outcomes and clinical decisions. To address these limitations, this study presents CARESUM (Clinical AI for Reliable and Enhanced Summarization), a multi-stage Generator-Verifier-Judge framework designed to enhance factual consistency and interpretability in clinical summaries. The framework first transcribes the conversation using an Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) module. It then uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) layer to pull in relevant context, and finally creates an initial summary through the Generator model. A Verifier then classifies each statement as supported, contradicted, or uncertain based on the source dialogue, while a Judge module determines whether the summary meets predefined factual accuracy thresholds. Approved summaries are stored in a PostgreSQL database for downstream clinical integration. Experiments on a synthetic clinical dataset show that CARESUM reaches higher F1 scores in factual accuracy compared to baseline summarization models. These findings demonstrate CARESUM’s effectiveness in generating reliable, interpretable, and verifiable clinical summaries, advancing the deployment of trustworthy AI solutions in real-world healthcare environments.

title: Amygdala-Inspired Deep Learning Architecture for Fear-Driven Obstacle Avoidance in Autonomous Navigation
authors: Shivani J, Sandhya P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: We suggest a deep learning architecture for obstacle avoidance in mobile robots that is biologically inspired and modeled after the amygdala's function in threat perception. After integrating a Self-Organizing Map (SOM) for unsupervised feature projection, the system uses a Feedforward Convolutional Neural Network (FCNN) to classify objects as either "regular" or "fear." Movement orders are sent over MQTT for low-latency control, and a Python-based pipeline processes real-time video input from a mobile camera.
