According to experimental data, when compared to a traditional CNN–HTTP robot, the suggested SOM–FCNN–MQTT model achieves an overall classification accuracy of 83.2%, reduces detection latency from 21 s to 10 s, and lowers collision frequency by 65%. When it detects fear-inducing things, the robot stops and reroutes, exhibiting reflex-like behavior; for ordinary objects, it navigates normally. The potential of lightweight communication protocols and neuro-inspired learning for emotion-aware, real-time autonomous navigation is demonstrated by this work.

title: Insights into the Performance of 5GOpen@TheBeacon: A Flexible and Scalable 5G Testbed Based on Open Source Solutions
authors: João Francisco Nunes Pinheiro, Andreas Gavrielides, Miguel Camelo, Johann Marquez-Barja
abstract: As 5G rollout keeps progressing, the need for experimentation platforms with different functionalities, like MEC deployments, flexible Core functions and multi-vendor compatibility, keeps growing. Existing testbeds have some commercial/closed source solutions integrated to a degree (in the Core, Radio Access Network (RAN) or both), limiting development of new and innovative functionalities, and experimentation that might require more fine grain control over the network. This paper provides valuable insights into the current 5G performance by using the 5GOpen@TheBeacon testbed, a flexible solution that utilizes multiple open-source solutions in both indoor and outdoor environments, working in real-world conditions. We leverage two architectures and combine the different software available which later can enable research across different use cases, such as eMBB, URLLC and V2X.

title: A VIKOR-Based Electricity Package Recommendation Method Integrating Spectral Clustering and Trapezoidal Fuzzy Numbers
authors: Yiying Zhao, Yuxin Yan, Yuanqian Ma
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Existing methods for Electricity Package Recommendation often treat consumers as a homogeneous group, ignoring specific load characteristics and the uncertainty in evaluating complex tariff structures. To address this, this paper proposes a targeted recommendation framework. First, heterogeneous evaluation data regarding package attributes is unified into Trapezoidal Fuzzy Numbers (TrFNs). Spectral Clustering is then employed to segment consumers into distinct categories. Second, focusing on a specific target cluster, a proximity-based weighting method aggregates individual evaluations into a representative group consensus matrix. Third, attribute weights are derived to reflect the specific cost-sensitivity of the target user type. Finally, a Dynamic VIseKriterijumska Optimizacija I Kompromisno Resenje (VIKOR) method, integrating time weights, ranks the packages. method is utilized to rank the packages. An empirical study on 500 users in North China validates the framework. By isolating the "Industrial User" cluster, the model identifies a stable, fixed-rate package (A1) as the optimal solution (Q=0.085), effectively capturing the group's risk-averse preference for cost certainty during peak seasons.

title: Explainable AI Based Machine Learning Approaches for Power Transformer Fault Diagnosis Methods
authors: Vijay Shivaji Chavan, Mithilesh Singh, P. William
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Power transformers are critical and costly components of electrical power systems; their unexpected failure can cause prolonged outages, significant economic loss, and safety hazards. Accurate and timely diagnosis of transformer faults—such as winding short-circuits, inter-turn faults, insulation degradation, core issues, and overheating—is therefore essential for reliable grid operation. Traditional diagnostic methods (Dissolved Gas Analysis, dissolved gas-in-oil ratios, frequency response analysis, thermal imaging, partial discharge measurement) are effective but often require expert interpretation and may not provide actionable insight into evolving failure modes. Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) augments machine learning (ML) strategies for transformer fault diagnosis by combining predictive power with human-interpretable explanations, thereby increasing operator trust and enabling informed maintenance decisions. This paper surveys XAI-based ML approaches applied to transformer diagnostics, covering feature engineering from multimodal sensor inputs (IEC 60599 DGA metrics, vibration, acoustic, temperature, bushing and tap-changer signals), supervised and semi-supervised models (SVM, random forests, gradient boosting, deep neural nets), and post-hoc and intrinsic explainability techniques (SHAP, LIME, attention visualization, rule extraction, surrogate models). We analyze how XAI helps identify root causes, prioritize alarms, and supports condition-based maintenance while highlighting challenges: label scarcity, class imbalance, noisy sensor data, regulatory requirements, and the trade-off between interpretability and accuracy. Finally, recommendations are provided for integrating XAI into transformer asset-management workflows, dataset standardization, and future research directions including causal inference and real-time explainable monitoring.

title: Grid-Interactive Buildings as Virtual Storage Systems to Support Flexible Grid Operations
authors: Chandra Prakash Barala, Aaquib Firdous, Parul Mathuria, Rohit Bhakar
abstract: Leveraging buildings as virtual energy storage by exploiting their internal thermal inertia offers a promising solution for enhancing grid flexibility. There has been a multidomain expansion in this field of study in recent years. However, several challenges, such as regulatory and interoperability hurdles, unclear roles and obligations of stakeholders, high implementation costs, and the need for advanced technologies, must be addressed to unlock their full potential. This study examines how buildings’ heating and cooling systems, traditionally designed for comfort, can now be used as distributed energy storage devices. A systematic literature review curated to investigate the benefits and barriers of using buildings as virtual storage is presented. Building standards, dynamic pricing, demand response programs, and appliance standards policies now in place serve as a foundation and must be integrated with new policies and standards to fully utilize virtual storage potential. Given the complexity of this transformation and the number of stakeholders involved, the paper highlights need for well-structured solutions and technological innovations to guide the successful integration of efficient buildings into the grid.

title: Text-to-Visual: Generating Images from Descriptions Using Deep Learning
authors: Kambham Pratap Joshi, Boggarapu Srinivasulu, S. Annapoorna, B Venkatesh, Chunduru Anilkumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This research explores a lightweight and efficient way for text-to-image generation by fine-tuning the Stable Diffusion v1.5 model with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). Diffusion models, particularly, have gained attention for producing high-quality images from text input. Complete fine-tuning of these models is computationally expensive and even not always possible for particular application areas. Previous work has demonstrated the potential of models such as DALL.E, Imagen, and baseline Stable Diffusion in producing general-purpose images but tend to be imprecise in semantic matching and fine detail creation. Our focus is on improving fidelity and prompt accuracy for specific facial image creation using a high-resolution subset of CelebA-HQ. By incorporating LoRA into the attention layers of the UNet, we drastically reduce the number of trainable parameters while not compromising output quality. The method includes a strong preprocessing pipeline for text inputs, CLIP-based semantic embedding, latent space manipulation through a diffusion process, and reconstruction using a denoising UNet and VAE decoder. The model is also fine-tuned on various epochs with a truncated noise schedule and evaluated by both qualitative visual checks and quantitative assessments such as Inception score (IS), Contrastive Language–Image Pre-training (CLIP) Score. Our results indicate that models fine tuned with LoRA score higher than the baseline Stable Diffusion in terms of realism, structure preservation, and semantic consistency, particularly on face synthesis tasks. Specifically, the model achieved a CLIP Score of 0.35, indicating strong semantic alignment with text prompts, and an Inception Score of 6.25, reflecting high image realism and diversity. The proposed system effectively balances computation against generative performance, validating LoRA as a viable approach to fine-tuning diffusion models for specialized domains.

title: Smart Drone System for Detecting Ripe and Unripe Chillies based on Their Ripeness
authors: Thirumoorthy P, Vijay Franklin J, Karthick Kumar N, Pavithra S, Suvedha T, Swathi M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Identifying the ripeness of chilli fruits at the correct harvesting stage is a critical task in precision agriculture, as it directly affects crop quality, yield, and market value. Manual inspection of chilli plants is time-consuming, labor-intensive, and often inaccurate, especially in large-scale farms. To address these challenges, this paper presents a smart drone-based system for automatic detection and classification of ripe and unripe chillies using deep learning techniques. A camera-equipped unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) captures aerial images of chilli plants under real-field conditions. These images are analyzed using the YOLOv8.5 object detection model to classify chillies based on color characteristics, where red indicates ripeness and green indicates unripens. The proposed system achieves an overall accuracy of 92% and a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 94%, demonstrating robust performance even under varying lighting conditions and partial occlusion by leaves. Experimental results show that the system significantly reduces manual effort while providing reliable and fast ripeness estimation. The proposed approach offers a scalable and efficient solution for smart agriculture and supports data-driven harvesting decisions for farmers.

title: Economic Decarbonization with Battery Energy Storage System Degradation in Integrated Agricultural Energy System
authors: Yuhang Wu, Canbing Li, Xinxi Li, Hong Chang, Jun Chen
abstract: The pursuit of decarbonization in integrated agriculture energy system (IAES) leads to increased operational costs, particularly as the electricity generation from carbon-emission sources diminishes. Battery energy storage system (BESS) plays a vital role in decarbonization due to its fast response to the power fluctuation of renewable energy, the clean energy. However, BESS degradation impacts the battery performance and increases the aging costs, consequently affecting the balance between the carbon emissions and operation costs. This paper integrates BESS degradation models into the IAES and compares the performances of varying models. The results demonstrate that incorporating the degradation models facilitates a crucial trade-off between carbon emissions and operational costs, thereby contributing to a more sustainable operational strategy for IAES.

title: Low Illumination Enhancement and Flaw Location Technique for Night Inspection of Transmission Lines
authors: Yong Zhu, Yang Teng, Lei Fan, Mingwei Wang, Yuxuan Lei, Benjun Xu, Yunjiang Lei
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: The identification distance of infrared inspection of transmission lines at night was often compressed to less than 40 meters. The lack of defect samples and the dark image quality deterioration mechanism became the bottleneck restricting all-weather operation and maintenance. In this paper, an optical-algorithm-data closed-loop was built with the 220-kilo-voltage line fitting's overheating defect as an incision. First, the temperature-radiation-noise coupled degeneration model and the pitch-roll-zoom three-level decomposition equation were used to describe the infrared low-light image quality decay. Then, the blackbody radiation correction and the Cycle-GAN self-monitoring branch were embedded to enhance the physical-data coordination. Together with the 5g carbon fiber coax dual-light head and the improved YoLOv5n-AP-FPM network, the 640×512 infrared and 2K visible light outputs were realized in the same frame. From September 2023 to September 2024, 120,000 images were collected at night on ten routes, and a four-level annotation library was established. Flight verification was completed in three types of scenes: plain, mountainous, and urban under 0.1 Lux illumination, rain and fog, and extreme environments of-20~40℃. The actual measurement recognition distance doubled to 80m, the defect recall rate was 92%, the false alarm rate was <3%, the power consumption of the whole machine was 18W, the battery life was 42 minutes, and the energy efficiency was more than 50% higher than that of the commercial platform. It provided a replicated lightweight technical path for the continuous operation and maintenance of transmission lines at night.

title: Say It Better by Rewriting Harmful Prompts for Responsible LLM Use
authors: V. Purushothama Raju, Smita Nirkhi, Priti Kulkarni, Vijay Kumar Joshi, Sabnam Kumari, Manish Motghare
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in natural language processing and conversational applications. However, user inputs may sometimes contain harmful or biased language, leading to toxic or inappropriate model outputs. We propose LLM-ReFrame, an automated system that rewrites such inputs into neutral, non-offensive queries while preserving their original intent. LLM-ReFrame first detects potentially harmful content, then applies a sequence-to-sequence rewriting model to generate safe alternatives. We evaluate our approach on both synthetic and real-world datasets of toxic prompts. Results demonstrate that LLM-ReFrame significantly reduces output toxicity and bias, while maintaining semantic fidelity. This work represents a step toward safer and more responsible use of LLM- based systems.

title: Explainable AI for Transparent Legal Decision Support in Judicial Systems Using Hybrid LegalTech Framework
authors: Srigouri Kosuri, Meenakshi Sharma, Guru Basava Aradhya S, V. Selvi, S.Suma Christal Mary, Alamelu Alias Rajasree S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This study introduces a hybrid Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) framework to improve transparency, accountability, and reliability in legal decision support systems. The framework combines rule-based legal reasoning with data-driven models, integrating Random Forest classifiers with Legal-BERT embeddings to capture both structured statutory signals and nuanced semantic patterns in judicial texts. Evaluated on a curated dataset of 35,000 Indian High Court judgments, the proposed approach achieved 94.8% accuracy, 93.5% precision, and 95.1% recall, representing an 11% relative improvement over standalone baselines. The dataset was collected from publicly available Indian High Court digital repositories and underwent extensive preprocessing including anonymization, text normalization, and removal of redundant metadata to ensure ethical use and consistency in analysis. Explainability is provided through SHAP value analysis and attention visualizations that expose feature contributions, legal citations, and contextual phrases that influence predictions. An ablation study demonstrates the contribution of the rule-based layer to interpretability with minimal performance trade-offs. A user evaluation with legal professionals yielded an average interpretability score of 4.5/5, indicating practical acceptability. The framework aims to bridge data-driven inference and principled legal reasoning, supporting judicial workflows without supplanting human judgment. Future study will extend evaluation across multi-jurisdictional datasets and implement privacy-aware data governance to ensure ethical deployment in operational settings. Comprehensive documentation, reproducible code, and deployment guidelines are provided to facilitate transparent adoption widely by practitioners and stakeholders.

title: Compact and Efficient Circular Patch Antenna Incorporating Slots and Parasitic Elements for 6G Communications at 90 GHz
authors: Yashika, Joel Saxena, Vaishali Kikan, Ashwni Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: A high-efficiency circular microstrip patch antenna designed for W-band millimeter-wave 6G applications is presented. The antenna integrates parasitic elements along with rectangular slots on the patch and a circular slot on the ground plane to achieve enhanced impedance matching and improved radiation performance. Implemented on a low-loss Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate (ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> = 2.2), the optimized design resonates precisely at 90 GHz, yielding a minimum return loss of –47.53 dB and a VSWR of 0.073, indicating excellent impedance conformity. It delivers a peak gain of 11.257 dBi and maintains highly stable impedance characteristics (49.98 Ω real, –0.42 Ω imaginary), enabling efficient power transfer. HFSS simulations validate consistent radiation characteristics in both the E- and Hplanes, along with strong directivity. The optimized antenna demonstrates enhanced gain, minimized return loss, and broadened bandwidth, making it an excellent candidate for 6G wireless systems, millimeter-wave sensing, and other advanced high-frequency technologies.

title: AI-Enabled real-time speech-to-sign language conversion: enhancing accessibility and inclusivity with animated avatars in the Metaverse
authors: Ambhika C, Annamalai K, Girija S D, Hasika R, Hindhuja R, Kumari Selvi C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This study introduces an intelligent real-time system that converts speech into sign language to enhance accessibility and inclusivity in the Metaverse. The proposed system combines voice recognition technology, language understanding algorithms, and 3D animated avatars to provide seamless, real-time translations for individuals with hearing and speech impairments. Unlike existing solutions, our approach ensures low-latency performance, scalability across multiple sign languages, and integration into virtual environments. A comparative analysis highlights the system’s advantages over traditional speech-to-text tools and sign language translation models. Additionally, user feedback from the deaf community validates its usability, emphasizing improvements in real-time communication and engagement in digital spaces. This research also discusses challenges such as latency, scalability, and bias mitigation while proposing future enhancements, including gesture customization, multi-user support, and AR/VR adaptation. The results indicate that the system can serve as a reliable accessibility tool for the Metaverse, fostering inclusive communication in virtual and digital ecosystems.

title: Immersive Analytics for Business Intelligence: Leveraging Augmented and Virtual Reality for Enhanced Data Visualization and Decision Support
authors: Chandra Prakash Gupta, V. V. Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: One of the main aspects of the future technologies is the immersive analytics made possible by augmented and virtual reality, and they are on the verge of changing our perceptions of Business Intelligence data. The emergence of new technology has paved the way for immersive analysis tools. Still, there is a lack of empirical studies that explore the organizational adoption and the value that comes with Immersive Analytics. This research investigates through the lens of research papers, industry case studies, and field reports how AR/VR can be integrated into Business Intelligence Systems. It analyzes academic studies and case reports. It seeks to identify universal themes, reasons behind their adoption by organizations, user interaction patterns, and the impact such technologies have on the organizations. One of the focal points of the study is the integration of immersive environments as a means for data scientists to quickly and easily understand complicated datasets, engage in collective discussions over analysis, and eliminate the drawbacks associated with conventional 2D dashboards. Besides, one of the major obstacles to organizations’ use of such tools is the high cost of hardware, integration issues, and the learning curve for users. This research aggregates and interprets prior studies’ findings to develop a theme-based framework. This framework delineates the primary benefits, limitations, and design considerations of immersive business intelligence visualization. The outcomes not only enhance comprehension by contextualizing immersive analytics in actual business scenarios but also indicate the directions for the future research concerning the large-scale implementation of these systems, their user-friendliness, and user experience in corporate environments.

title: Design and Deployment of a Smart Digital Notice Board Using Raspberry Pi for Real-Time Campus Communication
authors: Pranali Choudhari, Siddhanta Dolui, Smit Mhadgut, Yash Mitkari, Harsh Jaiswal
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The growing needs for automation and eco-friendly digital infrastructure in academic settings has made the move from traditional paper-based systems towards intelligent, networked solutions. This article addresses the design and development of an intelligent, web-based Digital Notice Board system using the computational power of Raspberry Pi 4 and the versatility of contemporary web technologies. Conventional notice boards tend to become cluttered, with stale notices hanging around and priority notices are hard to spot, creating inefficiencies. The proposed work provides a solution to these problems by providing real-time updates to notices, visual cues based on priorities, automatic expiry, and deadline-sensitive alarms. Powered by a safe, faculty-managed admin portal and an interactive, color-coded public display window, this system makes sure only important, current information is presented, enhancing readability and accessibility. By applying Internet of Things (IoT) concepts through lightweight server-side logic and modular frontend, the system offers a cost-effective, environmentally friendly, and scalable solution to conventional notice boards. This work adds to the progressive field of smart campuses by providing an affordable, remotely controlled, clutter free and environmentally friendly platform for real-time information dissemination.

title: Model-Based Virtual Sensing for High-Accuracy Cooperative Control: A Dual-Robot Drilling and Riveting System for Aircraft Assembly
authors: Bo Li, Jiamei Lin, Zhen Fan, Sunyu You, Wei Tian
abstract: In high-value manufacturing, dual-robot systems face a fundamental observability paradox: the very scenarios demanding precise cooperation are often those where physical occlusion prevents the dual-sided sensing required to achieve it. This bottleneck severely limits the application of flexible automation in complex tasks such as aircraft assembly. To address this challenge, this study proposes a cooperative control paradigm centered on model-based virtual sensing. This approach leverages a high-fidelity and precalibrated kinematic model of the dual-robot system as a virtual sensor, enabling the precise inference of the occluded robot’s target pose using only single-sided physical measurements. We designed, integrated, and validated this paradigm on an industrial-scale dual-robot system for cooperative drilling and electromagnetic riveting (EMR). The experimental results demonstrate that the framework successfully constrains the cooperative attitude error to below 0.4°. Compared to noncooperative methods, it reduces the average height of drilling exit burrs by 69.3% and improves the quality of EMR joints by decreasing the average perpendicularity error of the driven head by 34% and the coaxiality error by 64% . This work presents a robust and cost-effective solution that replaces infeasible or costly physical sensors with model-driven intelligence, advancing the frontier of flexible automation in aerospace manufacturing.

title: Understanding Teleoperation in Robotics: A Systematic Review of Device Types and Main Performance
authors: John W. Castro, Victor Ariza, Vladimir Riffo
abstract: Teleoperation of robotic manipulators has advanced significantly in recent years, serving as a critical solution for high-risk applications in medicine, industry, and space exploration, where direct human intervention is impractical or hazardous. Despite these advances, persistent technical and operational challenges, such as latency-induced instability and limited real-world validation, restrict broader adoption, necessitating a systematic evaluation of emerging technologies and their integrations to bridge the gap between laboratory prototypes and field-deployable systems. To this end, we conducted a Systematic Mapping Study across Scopus, IEEE Xplore, and Web of Science, screening 3,957 records and selecting 339 primary studies from 2019 to mid-2024 for in-depth analysis. The results categorize advancements into eight key groups: exoskeletons, haptic devices, immersive technologies, neural interfaces, motion and biometric sensing systems, direct input and control interfaces, advanced identification and communication systems, and combinations. The findings reveal a pronounced shift toward multimodal hybrids (e.g., haptic-immersive fusions) that enhance operator precision, situational awareness, and ergonomic usability across domains. However, communication latency remains the predominant barrier, with delays over 200 ms compromising stability and safety irrespective of interface complexity. These findings underscore that while hybrid integrations mitigate isolated limitations, domain-specific tolerances —such as near-zero in medicine versus moderate in industry— demand tailored optimizations for effective deployment. Future research should prioritize rigorous field trials with standardized benchmarks (e.g., NASA-TLX for workload and FMEA for safety) for combined technologies, alongside the development of 6G-enabled predictive algorithms to preempt latency issues, ensuring scalable, resilient teleoperation in critical scenarios.

title: Coordinated Operation of Multiple Distribution Systems using Adjusted Cost-influencing Participation Factor based on a Distributionally Robust Chance-Constrained Model
authors: Dongwon Lee, Changhee Han, Sungwoo Kang, Sungchul Hwang, Gilsoo Jang
abstract: The integration of photovoltaic generators introduces significant uncertainties in distribution network (DN) operations. This study presents a novel distributionally robust energy management strategy for multiple DNs, addressing inherent variabilities in renewable energy generation and load demands. To achieve the optimal energy management across the entire system under uncertainty, we introduce a novel concept: the adjusted cost-influencing participation factor (ACPF). The distribution system operator incorporates critical components such as the net power of the entire system, the locational marginal prices of the DNs, and the rated capacities of the generation units to compute the ACPF. We further reformulate the proposed distributionally robust chance constraint into a deterministic linear constraint by employing duality theory and the nested bisection search method. Comprehensive case studies on modified IEEE test systems validate the proposed methodology, demonstrating enhanced operational efficiency and reduced generation costs under various parameters and scenarios. The approach offers a robust framework for optimizing energy transactions across multiple DNs.

title: Swin-TUNA : A Novel PEFT Approach for Accurate Food Image Segmentation
authors: Haotian Chen, Zhiyong Xiao
abstract: In food image processing, parameter-efficient semantic segmentation is important for high-performance applications under constrained training resources. Existing large-scale transformer-based models, such as FoodSAM, still require substantial computational resources during full fine-tuning. This study introduces the TUNable Adapter module (Swin-TUNA), a Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) method that integrates multiscale trainable adapters into the Swin Transformer architecture. Swin-TUNA achieves strong food image segmentation performance while updating only 4% of the total parameters. The core innovation is a hierarchical feature adaptation mechanism that uses depth-wise separable convolutions and stage-dependent dimensional mappings to reduce feature mismatch between shallow and deep layers, together with a dynamic balancing strategy for task-agnostic and task-specific features. Experiments show that the proposed method reaches mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) scores of 50.56% and 74.94% on FoodSeg103 and UECFoodPix Complete, respectively, surpassing full fine-tuning of FoodSAM while tuning only 8.13M parameters. In addition, Swin-TUNAdemonstrates faster convergence and stronger discriminative capability, providing an efficient solution for high-precision food image segmentation. The codes are available at https://github.com/chtzs/swin-tuna.

title: Multi-Objective Distributed Economic Dispatch for Multi-Microgrid with Hydrogen Energy Storage Considering Communication Delay
authors: Yisha Wang, Yulian Jiang, Yang Li, Shenquan Wang, Yogendra Arya
abstract: In a multi-microgrid (MMG) system, flexible loads (FLs) and hydrogen energy storage systems (HESS) are crucial for demand response, significantly improving system reliability. However, communication delays may hinder the timely scheduling of FLs, HESS, and generators, potentially reducing operational efficiency. To address this challenge, a multi-objective distributed economic dispatch (MODED) strategy is proposed, considering both communication delay and FLs. The strategy employs a unified framework that ensures comprehensive optimization by transforming the economic dispatch problem (EDP) of MMG into a multi-agent consensus problem. The MMG model is constructed with multiple microgrids (MGs), each comprising diesel generators (DGs), renewable energy (RE), and FLs. Meanwhile, it improves environmental sustainability that considering both the environmental pollution caused by fossil fuels and inclusion of HESS in economic scheduling improves environmental sustainability. Firstly, the incremental costs of generators (DGs and RE) and benefits of FLs are marginal costs by leveraging multi-agent consensus theory, ensuring optimal power generation and maintaining the supply-demand balance within each MG. Then the maximum allowable communication delay bound is determined by Z-transform and generalized Nyquist. Furthermore, an improved Metropolis method is proposed to solve the weight matrix in the consensus algorithm, facilitating accurate power flow interactions between the MGs and accelerating system convergence. Stability analysis of the proposed method is also conducted. Finally, the effectiveness and economic benefits of the proposed strategy are demonstrated through experiments.

title: Improvement of Reactive Power Absorption Capacity for Synchronous Condenser
authors: Guorui Xu, Junxian Li, Haipeng Shen, Guangliang Yang
abstract: The reactive power absorption capacity of synchronous condensers (SC) plays a crucial role in suppressing transient overvoltage and enhancing the voltage stability of new energy power grids. To enhance the reactive power absorption capacity of the SC, the reactive power characteristics and stable operation capability under loss-of-excitation condition are analyzed and it is obtained that the air-gap length is a critical factor. The reactive power absorption of the SC is increased as the increase of the air-gap length, however, the temperature rise in the end region can restrict its reactive power absorption capability. A three-dimensional magnetic-fluid-thermal model in the end region of the SC is established to investigate the loss and temperature in the stator end structural components under different air-gap lengths. The temperature distribution in the stator end region is analyzed and the variation of the maximum temperature with the increase of the air-gap length is revealed. the maximum reactive power absorption capacity of the SC is obtained and the maximum air-gap length is determined. The experimental results on two 300-MVar SC with different air-gap length validate the accuracy of the calculations. This research provides a theoretical foundation and technical support for enhancing the reactive power absorption capacity of SC.

title: Autonomous SSV With YOLOv8-Powered Vision Robotical Arm for Plastic Collection and Oil Separation
authors: S. Ohmshankar, Maheswari D, KL Varshaa, Karunya R K, R Vaishnavi Devi, S. Sivakesava
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: The novel framework features an autonomous Surface Surveillance Vehicle (SSV) coupled with a YOLOv8-based vision system, a robotic arm as well as the original idea to solve the dual problems of plastic collecting and oil separation in water bodies. Through the use of on-the-spot object detection along with accurate robotic manipulation, the system makes sure proper identification, fetching, and even sorting of wastes from water surfaces is accomplished. One of the major aspects of the vision module based on YOLOv8 is that it improves detection accuracy, so the robotic arm can adjust its operation according to different environmental conditions. The main emphasis of the proposed framework as compared to the existing traditional manual or semi-automated methods/disadvantages is its potential to develop further, maintain continuous functionality, and reduce the need for human intervention, thereby contributing to the overall sustainability of environmental monitoring and waste management. Furthermore, the combination of smart decision-making within the autonomous platform is a great step forward in the use of machine learning-based robotic systems for the purpose of environmental restoration. This effort points out that the framework may be a dependable and flexible solution allowing for extensive application in various water bodies of the earth.

title: GAP-Net-Based Real-Time Phase Reconstruction for Dynamically Evolving Holographic Scenes in Bioprinting
authors: Kaijun Lin, Xinyi Dong, Yaozhen Hou, Yuji Liu, Shanming Bai, Juan Cui, Toshio Fukuda, Huaping Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Holographic phase enables quantitative observation of the morphology of bioprinted structures and holds strong potential in bio-fabrication. However, achieving accurate and rapid holographic phase reconstruction for real-time monitoring during 3D bioprinting remains a challenge. In this paper, we propose a novel Global Attention Phase Network (GAP-Net) that focuses on real-time and accurate phase reconstruction for holographic phase monitoring in bioprinting. The GAP-Net leverages lightweight feature extraction module and global attention for semantic segmentation of wrapped phases, enhancing reconstruction precision. In addition, by predicting the first six Zernike coefficients to capture the dominant background aberrations, the method effectively mitigates phase distortions and further improves reconstruction accuracy. The approach achieves real-time phase reconstruction at 22.8 FPS with a 1.11-fold accuracy improvement over existing deep learning methods. The proposed method also enables successful real-time visualization of the curing dynamics of biological samples during bioprinting, further demonstrating its practical effectiveness. This work not only provides real-time observational data for bioprinting but also shows promising potential for broader applications in fields such as optics and biomedical imaging.

title: LocAPS: Adaptive Positive Sampling for Network Embedding
authors: Anbiao Wu, Ye Yuan, Changsheng Li, Yuliang Ma, Yishu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Network embedding (NE) aims to learn low dimensional node representations, wherein both neural-based (NNE) and factorization-based (FNE) methods commonly employ negative sampling (NS) as an essential component. However, the role of NS differs markedly between these two paradigms: in NNE, negative samples are randomly chosen to facilitate efficient training, while in FNE, the distribution of negative samples plays a pivotal role in deriving the factorized matrix. In this work, we propose LocAPS (Loccal cluster-based Adaptive Positive Sampling), a novel sampling strategy that adaptively determines positive samples for each node based on local clustering. Building on LocAPS, we develop an enhanced NNE method, VERSE+, which achieves both sampling and training in linear time. For FNE, we introduce an adaptive negative sampling distribution derived from LocAPS, which tailors the sampling probability for each node. This distribution informs the construction of a factorized matrix that adaptively retains information from the similarity matrix. Moreover, its node-wise nature enables the development of FREDE+, an efficient streaming-style NE method with linear time and space complexity. We conduct extensive experiments on multiple real-world datasets, evaluating our methods on node classification and link prediction tasks, demonstrating their effectiveness and superior performance.

title: Temporal-Convolutional Adversarial Autoencoding with Channel-Wise Attention for Intrusion Detection in The Internet of Vehicles
authors: Arash Heidari, Abdulnasir Hossen, Rami Al-Hmouz, Majdi Mansouri
abstract: The Internet of Vehicles (IoV) lets cars talk to one another in smart ways by sharing data in real time between vehicles, infrastructure, and edge nodes. But as more and more parts become linked, the danger of advanced attacks rises. At the same time, traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) typically cannot keep up with IoV's changing, low-latency, and resource-limited needs. Deep learning has become a viable alternative, but it needs a lot of labeled data and architectures that are hard to compute, which makes it less useful in real-world vehicle situations. To solve this problem, we suggest Vehicular Intrusion Detection by Temporal-aware Attention (VITA), a lightweight, self-supervised, and label-free IDS framework made just for IoV. VITA uses an Adversarial Autoencoder (AAE) with Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) to pick out important spatial characteristics and residual Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs) to simulate long-range temporal relationships in a way that is efficient way. A latent-space smoothing technique is added to stabilize adversarial learning, and a log-cosh reconstruction loss is added to make the system more resistant to noisy vehicle telemetry. VITA works in real time and does not need a lot of processing power, so it can be used on edge devices in vehicles. Our proposed VITA framework shows superior performance across several critical criteria, including an average 30% reduction in latency compared to state-of-the-art models. VITA achieves an average 8% improvement in detection accuracy, effectively identifying a broad spectrum of vehicular attacks, such as replay, Global Positioning System (GPS) spoofing, and Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks. Furthermore, VITA outperforms existing systems with an average 5x reduction in false positive rate, and shows robust performance under varying noise levels, with only a modest 4.7% drop in accuracy under high jitter conditions. Additionally, it excels in computational efficiency, requiring 50% less memory and processing power on average, making it highly suitable for real-time, edge-based IoV deployments.

title: Convergence Theory of Sharpness-Aware Minimization with Interpolating Neural Networks
authors: Tan Sun, Yusu Hong, Junhong Lin
abstract: We present theoretical analysis of Sharpness-Aware Minimization (SAM) applied to training loss minimization with neural networks and smooth activations. Unlike prior works on stationary points measure in standard non-convex smooth optimization settings with noise assumptions, we leverage intrinsic properties of neural network loss landscapes to establish a convergence rate of Õ$(1/T )$ for the average of training loss, under the smooth loss function and interpolating neural networks. Here, T is the total number of iterations. Our analysis lies on the properties of self-bounded-like gradients and Hessians, distinguishing neural network optimization from general nonconvex smooth optimization. Our results require that the width $m \ge \tilde \Omega ( T )$ for shallow neural networks while $m \ge \tilde \Omega (\sqrt T )$ for deep neural networks (though with a minor different scaling architecture), both with a constant learning rate and a small perturbation parameter. The width requirement can be further reduced to $m ≥$Õ(1) for the logistic loss function. To our knowledge, our results are the first of its kinds for SAM in neural networks on training loss bounds.

title: Automatic Psychophysiological Stress Detection Through Infrared Thermography and Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Irving A. Cruz-Albarran, Karla Muñoz-Esquivel, Salvador Calderon-Uribe, Luis A. Morales-Hernanez, Benjamin Dominguez-Trejo
abstract: Stress is the body's response to an unusual situation. While it can be beneficial to a certain extent, in large amounts, it can be adverse. This paper proposes a methodology for detecting psychophysiological stress using infrared thermography, deep learning and explainable artificial intelligence. The Trier Social Stress Test protocol was used to induce psychophysiological stress in 58 university students, and thermographic images were collected during this time. Subsequently, the facial and hand areas were automatically segmented. This information was used to train a convolutional neural network to detect psychophysiological stress. Similarly, the Grad-CAM method was implemented to identify which areas of the face or hands contributed to the classification. Finally, the computational complexity of the proposal was evaluated. The results demonstrate that stress detection can be achieved with 99.5% accuracy, 98.3% precision, 98% recall and 98% F1-Score. Regarding the Grad-CAM results, it was observed that both areas, the hand and facial areas, tend to contribute more to classification in women than in men. The computational complexity analysis of the model indicates that it used 480.371MFLOPS, 11.569 MPARAMS and an average training time of 150.82 seconds. Based on this, it is concluded that infrared thermography is a technology that can aid in the detection of stress, and when combined with explainable artificial intelligence techniques, it enables users to understand the basis for their decisions, specifically which areas of the face and hands are affected.

title: Feature Space-Preserving Machine Unlearning for Robust Image Classification with Noisy Labels
authors: Shohei Yamamoto, Soh Yoshida, Mitsuji Muneyasu
abstract: Deep neural networks excel in image classification with clean data, but their performance deteriorates significantly when trained with noisy labels. In contrast to conventional approaches, which address this challenge through sample selection or loss correction, this study proposes a fundamentally different paradigm in which machine unlearning is employed to selectively forget noisy samples. Existing unlearning methods such as scalable remembering and unlearning unbound cause detrimental feature space dispersion, which creates ambiguous decision boundaries. Thus, this paper introduces a feature space-preserving unlearning framework that maintains discriminative structures while removing the influence of noisy labels. The proposed approach computes class centroids from reliable samples and implements a dual optimization strategy in which noisy samples are pushed away from all centroids while clean samples are simultaneously pulled toward their appropriate class centers. Comprehensive experiments on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets with symmetric and asymmetric noise (ranging from 10%–90%) show that our method consistently outperforms standard unlearning techniques, achieving accuracy improvements of up to 27.61 percentage points on the more complex CIFAR-100 dataset under high-noise conditions. Furthermore, we confirm that our method remains effective even when applied to models trained with state-of-the-art noisy label learning methods. Visualization analysis demonstrates that our approach preserves compact class clusters with clear boundaries, despite aggressive unlearning. The reported implementation is available at https://github.com/meruemon/FSPMU.

title: Wideband High-Gain Circularly Polarized Substrate Integrated Cavity Antenna with RCS Reduction
authors: Zheng-Gang Yan, Peng Lei, Zhang-Qi Liao, Ya-Wen Liu, Wen-Zhong Lu, Xiao-Chuan Wang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A circularly polarized (CP) substrate integrated cavity (SIC) antenna array with radar cross section (RCS) reduction is proposed. The two-layer element consists of two suspended metal vias, two semicircular patches, and a metasurface, which introduced three CP resonance points to enhance the axial ratio (AR) bandwidth while the surrounding SIC structure is employed to improve gain and isolation. On this basis, the 4 × 4 antenna array is constructed from subarrays in a sequentially rotated configuration, which not only maintains wideband high-gain CP radiation performance but also achieves RCS reduction. Furthermore, an analytical method based on the Pancharatnam-Berry phase theory is developed to clarify the RCS reduction mechanism. The measured results demonstrate that the proposed array achieves an overlapping bandwidth of 34.97% from 7.88 to 11.22 GHz, while considering the −10-dB reflection coefficient, 3-dB AR, and 3-dB gain bandwidths properties, with a peak gain of 18.68 dBic, a maximum aperture efficiency of 87.75%, and an in-band RCS reduction more than 7 dB under normal incidence of x- and y-polarized waves.

title: Exposing Go’s Hidden Bugs: A Novel Concolic Framework
authors: Karolina Gorna, Nicolas Iooss, Yannick Seurin, Rida Khatoun
publish: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 23rd International Conference on Software Engineering Research, Management and Applications (SERA)
abstract: The widespread adoption of the Go programming language [1] in infrastructure backends and blockchain projects has heightened the need for improved security measures. Established techniques such as unit testing, static analysis, and program fuzzing provide foundational protection mechanisms. Although symbolic execution tools have made significant contributions, opportunities remain to address the complexities of Go’s runtime and concurrency model. In this work, we present Zorya, a novel methodology leveraging concrete and symbolic (concolic) execution to evaluate Go programs comprehensively. By systematically exploring execution paths to uncover vulnerabilities beyond conventional testing, symbolic execution offers distinct advantages, and coupling it with concrete execution mitigates the path explosion problem. Our solution employs Ghidra’s PCode [2] as an intermediate representation (IR). This implementation detects runtime panics in the TinyGo compiler [3] and supports both generic and custom invariants. Furthermore, $\mathbf{P}$ Code’s generic IR nature enables analysis of programs written in other languages such as $\mathbf{C}$. Future enhancements may include intelligent classification of concolic execution logs to identify vulnerability patterns.

title: Real-Time American Sign Language (ASL) Translator using OpenCV and MediaPipe
authors: Phanendra T, Harsha Vardhan G V, Karthikeya Chowdary D V, Amutha S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Sign language is crucial for the hearing impaired, but there is a communication gap between signers and non-signers. This paper introduces an ASL Translator based on OpenCV and MediaPipe that identifies predefined static hand signs in real-time. Utilizing MediaPipe’s 21 keypoint tracking, the system identifies gestures such as" Hello" (raised pinky)," What is your name?" (three raised fingers), and "Stop" (closed fist) without relying on deep learning models. A rule based classification framework ensures efficient processing at 30 FPS, making it suitable for real-time use on consumer grade hardware. Tested under various conditions, it is accurate at over X percentage. In comparison to deep learning models, the methodology is light and inexpensive. The ASL Translator has potential use in assistive communication, education, and accessibility software. Potential future enhancements may be added to support dynamic gestures, machine learning to achieve greater accuracy (up to 95%), and mobile implementation. This study contributes towards accessible technology by offering a cost effective, scalable solution to close the communication gap.

title: Key Technologies of IGCT-based Converters for HVDC Transmission Application
authors: Yong Xia, Jianxiang Xie, Jiaqi Wu, Dongxu Li
abstract: This paper analyzes the technical challenges in high-proportion renewable energy transmission systems, and examines integrated gate-commutated thyristor (IGCT) technology that combines the high-power capability of thyristors with the controllability of fully controlled devices. The research explores two key IGCT-based approaches: hybrid commutated converters for improved stability and IGCT-based modular multilevel converters for enhanced efficiency. By comparing different system configurations suitable for various grid conditions, the study demonstrates IGCT's potential to address both technical and economic challenges in renewable energy integration. The findings provide valuable insights for developing next-generation HVDC systems optimized for high penetration renewable energy transmission.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework LesionFuseNet for Accurate Skin Lesion Segmentation in Dermoscopic Images
authors: Sarvesh Prabhu, S.S. Sivaraju, Subramaniam Ganesan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of skin lesions from dermoscopic images is a critical step in automated melanoma detection and dermatological disease diagnosis. However, this task remains challenging due to irregular lesion shapes, varying scales, low contrast between lesions and surrounding skin, and interference from artifacts such as hair and illumination noise. To overcome these limitations, this research proposes LesionFuseNet, a robust deep learning–based framework for skin lesion segmentation. LesionFuseNet employs a hybrid encoder–decoder architecture integrating residual learning for stable deep feature extraction, hybrid dilated convolutions for capturing multi-scale contextual information, attention mechanisms for enhanced lesion localization, and dense feature fusion to improve information flow between encoder and decoder stages. The model is trained on publicly available ISIC dermoscopic datasets with expert-annotated masks, utilizing extensive preprocessing and data augmentation techniques to enhance generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that LesionFuseNet achieves superior segmentation performance, evaluated using segmentation metrics including Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) (98%), Intersection over Union (IoU) (97%), Accuracy (98%), and Precision (97%). The proposed framework provides reliable and precise lesion delineation, making it suitable for clinical decision support and automated skin lesion analysis.

title: Machine Learning Approach for Tracking and Predicting Student Performance
authors: T C Swetha Priya, Tanusha Gorak, Saba Fatima
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Accurately forecasting future performance of the students from their ongoing academic records is essential in implementing timely and effective pedagogical interventions, ultimately supporting ontime and satisfactory graduation [1], [5]. Although huge study was done on predicting student performance using data-driven methods, in solving particular problem or mastering individual courses, the task of predicting degree completion performance (for example, in college programs) is still relatively understudied and poses unique challenges: (1) Students backgrounds and course choices differ greatly; (2) Courses predictive value varies;(3) Effective modeling of students changing academic paths is necessary. To overcome these obstacles, we offer a unique machine learning framework in this study for forecasting degree program student success [2], [8]. Two significant advancements are incorporated into the approach. To provide reliable predictions based on the changing performance levels of the students, it first uses a bilayered architecture that consists of cascade of ensemble predictors and many base predictors. Secondly, it presents a data-driven methodology that uses probabilistic matrix factorisation and latent factor models to determine course relevance, which is essential in building effective base predictors. Comprehensive tests on a UCLA three-year undergraduate dataset show that our strategy performs noticeably better than well-known benchmark approaches, providing a more precise and flexible way to forecast academic achievement over the long run.

title: Feature-Enriched Deep Neural Networks for Early Parkinson’s Disease Prediction
authors: Jayakeerthi M, P. Saravanabhava, V. Jyothi, Dhruva R. Rinku, S. P. Santhoshkumar, Gajalakshmi S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease stands as a pervasive global malady, comprising approximately one-third of all diagnoses, yet the current diagnostic process is burdensome and laborious. Machine-assisted methodologies offer heightened efficiency, with deep learning, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs), mirroring intricate human brain functions for data organization, pattern recognition, and decision-making. Our proposed system endeavors to harness an efficient CNN model for Parkinson's disease detection through meticulous image analysis, leveraging a dataset culled from the Disease Image Database hosted on the UCI Machine Learning Repository. Upon user upload of a test image, the process commences with meticulous image segmentation for nuanced feature isolation, followed by seamless conversion from RGB to grayscale to facilitate streamlined processing. Subsequently, segmentation algorithms are applied, and the neural network proactively predicts and classifies whether the image manifests discernible markers indicative of Parkinson's disease. The exhaustive training dataset encompasses a diverse array of images depicting both Parkinson's-afflicted subjects and healthy controls, thereby fostering heightened accuracy and expediency in diagnosis, thereby fortifying the ongoing battle against Parkinson's disease proliferation.

title: Data-driven Resonance Area Prediction Model based on Generalized Regression Neural Network
authors: Yueling Sun, Xinwei Li, Wentao Sun, Jiebei Zhu
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven Resonance Area (RA) prediction scheme based on historical scenarios information and wind turbine generators (WTGs) controller parameters. Firstly, small-signal stability model (SSSM) is introduced to acquire resonance characteristics to establish RA. Then, the RA prediction model is created based on artificial intelligence algorithm: generalized regression neural network (GRNN). And the model is trained and tested by accuracy assessment index to verify its accuracy. Finally, the scheme is verified by an IEEE 39-bus benchmark model with a WTG integrated. The proposed scheme can accurately predict RA under new operating scenarios.

title: Robust Evaluation of Weighted EV Charging Loads Hosting Capacity in Flexible Distribution Network Considering Renewable Energy Uncertainty
authors: Jinlong Xu, Chengcheng Shao, Puheng Wang, Lyuzerui Yuan, Lei Yu, Qiuming Xu
abstract: Evaluation of electric vehicle (EV) charging loads hosting capacity (HC) in distribution network (DN) is crucial for guiding their rational integration and maintaining network security. This paper proposes a robust evaluation model for weighted EV charging loads HC in flexible distribution network (FDN) considering renewable energy uncertainty. First, a HC evaluation model with node-specific spatio-temporal weights derived from EV mobility and user charging behavior is established. Second, the model is reformulated within the robust optimization framework considering renewable energy uncertainty. Then, a solution methodology is developed by integrating second-order cone relaxation (SOCR), polygonal approximation and a nested column-and-constraint generation (NC&CG)-based iterative algorithm. Finally, the case studies on the modified IEEE 33-bus DN have been conducted to verify the effectiveness of the model. The impacts of uncertainty and flexible resources on the HC results are also analyzed.

title: Leveraging SPDM for Secure In-Band and Out-of-Band Communication in Data Center Platforms
authors: Raman Sharma, Shivakumar Udkar, Udaya Krishnan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Data centers of our modern era are repositories of enormous ecosystems of related hardware and software which engage in exchange of control, management, and telemetry information always. The responsibility of end-to-end trust in such communication paths is very important with increasing number of devices, firmware modules and interfaces. Old-fashioned security systems tend to be independent and not based on a common trust system and thus there are often weak points that can be used by spoofing, malicious firmware injection or tampering. This paper will discuss how the Data Model and Security Protocol (SPDM) which is established by the DMTF can be integrated to develop a unified security architecture of both in-band and out-of-band communication channels. This method provides a secure device identity validation, message confidentiality, and integrity assurance by enabling PCIe devices, Baseboard Management Controllers (BMCs) and CPUs to perform authentication and encryption of the device identities, message confidentiality, and integrity respectively. Experimental findings about a prototype on multi-node demonstrate that SPDM is able to improve platform trust without significantly affecting performance. This paper shows that SPDM is capable of being a scalable, hardware-based framework of security of next-generation data centers.

title: Quality of Service Aware Reliable Resource Allocation with Energy Efficient Comprehensive Learning Strategy for Smart Internet Communication
authors: G. Ramkumar, Abdullah Khatib, T. Vijetha, Chrispin Jiji, Ramya M, Bharathi Panduri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Quality of Service (QoS) Aware Reliable Resource Allocation efficiently manages network resources for specific service demands. To overcome this, the QoS-aware Reliable Resource and Energy-efficient Comprehensive System (QRRECS) system is an integrated framework of the Internet of Things (IoT), fog, and cloud computing that aims at resource efficiency, energy savings, and reliable data transmission. It makes use of a three-tier architecture comprising IoT devices that collect commands to start local processing at fog nodes, through a Decentralized Decision System (DDS). Cloud acts as a central data storage for heavy post-processing. The Energy Efficient Comprehensive Learning Strategy (EECLS) extends learning capacity and intentionally keeps a premature convergence from happening by enabling learning rates to be adaptively evaluated. The system tracks QoS parameters like latency and reliability to facilitate smooth communication in the entire network. The evaluation parameter is used to calculate network lifetime, alive sensors, dead sensors, energy consumption, and compare through calculations.

title: Analysis and Suppression of Post-fault Overvoltage Considering Electromagnetic Dynamics
authors: Zhipeng Bao, Xinyu Liu, Tianshu Bi, Xiuting Rong, Yunlong Chu
abstract: post-fault overvoltage arises in renewable-integrated power systems due to the insufficient transient voltage support ability (TVSA) of Voltage Source Converters (VSCs). To solve this problem, this paper investigates the postfault overvoltage mechanism and proposes a coordinated parameter optimization method for overvoltage suppression. First, the VSC is modeled as a constant current source and frequency-dependent impedance, where the dynamics of the inner-loop current controller is considered. Based on this model, the overvoltage mechanism is attributed to excessive equivalent impedance and the impact of weak grid conditions, and its evolution is quantitatively evaluated. Subsequently, a coordinated control parameter optimization strategy is proposed to reshape the equivalent impedance, thereby suppressing the overvoltage. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Hybrid CNN–SVM Model for Intelligent Early Detection of Pest Infestation in Crops
authors: Swetha B.M., Sugunadevi C, P.Thiruselvan, Ashwini A Rao, Saranya S., K.Manikandan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Pest infestation is one of the primary causes of reduced crop productivity worldwide, resulting in up to 40% yield losses annually. Traditional pest detection methods depend on manual inspection, which is inefficient and error-prone. To address this, we propose a Hybrid Convolutional Neural Network–Support Vector Machine (CNN–SVM) model for intelligent early detection of pest infestation in crops. The CNN automatically extracts spatial and texture features from leaf images, while the SVM classifier enhances decision boundaries for accurate classification. The framework was evaluated on a dataset of 12,000 images comprising five major pest categories and healthy crop samples. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid model achieves an overall accuracy of 96.8%, outperforming standalone CNN (94.2%) and conventional SVM (89.5%). The hybrid model also reports improvements in precision (96.3%), recall (97.1%), and F1-score (96.7%). These findings highlight the potential of the CNN–SVM hybrid approach in supporting smart agriculture by enabling early detection, reducing pesticide misuse, and improving crop yield.

title: Dispatch-Aware Deep Neural Network for Optimal Transmission Switching: Toward Real-Time and Feasibility Guaranteed Operation
authors: Minsoo Kim, Jip Kim
abstract: Optimal transmission switching (OTS) improves optimal power flow (OPF) by selectively opening transmission lines, but its mixed-integer formulation increases computational complexity, especially on large grids. To deal with this, we propose a dispatch-aware deep neural network (DA-DNN) that accelerates DC-OTS without relying on pre-solved labels. DA-DNN predicts line states and passes them through a differentiable DC-OPF layer, using the resulting generation cost as the loss function so that all physical network constraints are enforced throughout training and inference. In addition, we adopt a customized weight-bias initialization that keeps every forward pass feasible from the first iteration, which allows stable learning on large grids. Once trained, the proposed DA-DNN produces a provably feasible topology and dispatch pair in the same time as solving the DCOPF, whereas conventional mixed-integer solvers become intractable. As a result, the proposed method successfully captures the economic advantages of OTS while maintaining scalability.

title: Consortium Hyperledger blockchain security for secure health care transaction using block based chain-link rail fence cryptography algorithm in cloud
authors: B.Sharmila, A.V.Santhosh Babu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Security is the important aspect to predict the data in decentralized cloud data centres. Because they connect all the communication medium, network, storage and processing unit in all over the services consortium. The healthcare sectors maintain the patient data centrally to main the sensitive information. The data need to security to protect the personalized information. The communication overhead in security principles due to various attacks, hacking principle, data breaches which is knowledge by the hackers. The data protection and security need advanced security standard to protect the data and higher authentication schemas to improve the security. Prevailing intentions doesn’t meet the higher security standards publicly verified by cryptographic principles leads key leakages, authentication failures, data losses and soon. To resolve this problem to propose a advanced Consortium Hyperledger blockchain security (ACHBS) for secure health care transaction using block based chain-link rail fence cryptography algorithm in cloud.the block chain ensure the peer end verification policy to handover the key and verfy the authentication to improve the securityi. The proposed system improves the decentralized data securityi communications as well in authenticity proof of verification to enrich trust under key principles and handover principles compared to the other system.

title: An Integrated Deep Learning Approach for Multi-Class Detection of Neurological Diseases Using Brain Imaging
authors: Someshwaran. G, S. Srinivasan, A. Pavithra, S. M. Lekha Sri, M. Kanimozhi, C. U. Keziah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Advancements in medical imaging, coupled with deep learning techniques, has substantially enhanced the early diagnosis of brain-related disorders such as brain neoplasms and cerebral ischemia, achieving high validity rates. Among data-driven learning approaches, algorithms driven by neural networks have proven particularly effective in the analysis of medical imaging tasks, including identification, delineation, and categorization of these conditions. However, most existing models are limited to addressing a single task at a time, a constraint often referred to as narrow AI. To advance toward strong AI, there is a pressing need for methodologies capable of performing concurrent tasks. Herein, this work proposes a combined framework CNN-driven capable of detecting and classifying two brain disorders—tumors and ischemic stroke—at the same time. Unlike existing single-disease methods, it bridges cerebrovascular and oncological imaging domains through combined 2015 BRATS and 2015 ISLES datasets. The proposed architecture enhances feature fusion and promotes generalizable AI-based neurodiagnosis. To achieve this, we developed a new dataset by combining two publicly available datasets. The proposed architecture is an improved version of the UNET encoder–decoder model, where the feature maps of given block encoder are integrated with the subsequent block’s outputs. This design helps preserve detailed low-level information and better separate superimposed features throughout the encoding process, while also maintaining traditional UNET shortcut connections. The data were divided as training and test subsets using an 80:20 split, with each training batch constructed to maintain balanced image samples, thereby reducing class imbalance. The proposed model attained a mean accuracy of 99.66%, along with a specificity of 99.99%, precision of 99.69%, and an F1-score of 99.67%. These results show the efficacy of the proposed feature fusion strategy for multi-condition diagnosis in medical imaging.

title: Value-Based Industrial Load Shedding with Supply Chain Coordination Reduces Electricity Cost
authors: Jiarui Li, Ruike Lyu, Yingrui Zhang, Xicheng Cha, Kedi Zheng, Hongye Guo
abstract: Reducing industrial electricity consumption during power shortages can significantly reduce the needed investment for reliable power supply. Compared to indiscriminate load shedding, reducing the production of industrial users with lower value-added per kWh first can minimize the socioeconomic impact of load shedding. Furthermore, the coupling relationships between upstream and downstream industries should be considered, as production curtailment in a low-value industry may impede production in downstream high-value industries. However, to our knowledge, the system-level benefits of these two measures have not been modeled and evaluated in existing research. In this paper, we model and evaluate the benefits of value-based optimized load reduction considering supply chain coupling relationships using real-world socioeconomic data of various industrial sectors in Anhui Province, China. Results demonstrate that under a net-zero carbon emission scenario, optimized load shedding strategies can save approximately 22.1 billion CNY in annualized investment for carbon capture coal-fired power plants and about 1.44 billion CNY in annualized long-duration storage investment. Our research provides a preliminary assessment of the benefits of supply chain coordinated load shedding and its role in decarbonizing grids.

title: Analyzing Urban Sprawl Through Aerial Views of Satellite Imagery using Geospatial Analysis with Generative AI
authors: L.Manjula, Prabhavathi K, Sakthidevi I, S.Sujatha, R. Divya, Sangeetha S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Urbanization has drastically altered the landscape, impacting the built environment, vegetation, and water supply. This paper compares satellite imagery from March 2020 and March 2025 to provide a comprehensive analysis of urban expansion in Hyderabad. In this work, Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI), Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI), Normalized Difference Built-up Index (NDBI), and Urban Sprawl Index (USI) were used to quantify changes in building density, vegetation loss, and water resource depletion. The images are processed using Python libraries such as Rasterio, NumPy, and Matplotlib to gain valuable insights using thresholding and index-based classification methods. The results highlight the need for sustainable urban planning by showing a noticeable increase in built-up areas and a significant decrease in vegetation and water coverage. A unique feature of the proposed approach is the inclusion of ChatGPT to understand and categorize these changes in natural language. ChatGPT helps both experts and non-experts better understand the environmental consequences by providing easy-to-understand summaries of NDVI, NDWI, and density variations over time. The proposed work demonstrates how generative AI and geospatial analysis can be combined to enable well-informed decision-making in environmental management and urban development.

title: LLM Based Software Test Scenario Generation System From Bug Descriptions
authors: Merve Yilmazer Gundem, Mehmet Karakose
abstract: Large, user-interactive software systems require extensive software testing for safe deployment. Manually generated test cases by software testers require extensive time and effort, and their scope remains limited. In this study, a system based on LLM is proposed for automatic test cases and test steps generation from bug descriptions. In the proposed system, bug descriptions were first collected from the Defects4J Dataset. Keywords relevant to the bug scope were extracted from the descriptions. Structured automatic prompts were generated from the extracted keywords. The generated prompts were fed into the LLM as input, and software test cases appropriate for the bugs were generated. The method was evaluated on actual bug descriptions by comparing it to a general (baseline) prompt. Experimental results show that the lengths of the generated scenarios are similar, but the proposed method increases the keyword match rate with error descriptions to an average of 89 %. The findings reveal that keyword-driven prompts are effective in generating more reliable and error-specific test scenarios without requiring additional data.

title: Integrating MBSE and MDAO through SysML v2
authors: Jean-Marie Gauthier, Jean-Christophe Giret, Sylvain Béchet, Francesco Torrigiani, Sparsh Garg, Jasper Bussemaker
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: The integration of Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) and Multidisciplinary Design Analysis and Optimization (MDAO) remains fragmented, limiting traceability and automation across tools. This paper shows how SysML v2 can support end-to-end digital continuity between MBSE and MDAO. A SysML v2 metamodel structured around the Problem–Strategy–Solution concepts captures variables, objectives, constraints, strategies, and solutions while maintaining explicit links to requirements and architecture. A SysML v2–GEMSEO plug-in then instantiates executable optimization workflows from these models. The approach is evaluated on a Thermal Management System case study, where optimization artefacts were systematically derived from SysML v2 and propagated back for requirement verification. The workflow minimized system mass while satisfying performance constraints and revealed inconsistencies in units and parameter bounds. These results demonstrate that SysML v2 provides sufficient semantic expressiveness to connect MBSE and MDAO, enabling closed-loop requirements verification and improving traceability.

title: Deep Learning Based Transient Voltage Stability Assessment for Large Scale Hybrid Power Grid
authors: Xiyang Liu, Kunpeng Zhang, Yutian Liu
abstract: Based on deep learning technologies, a two-stage transient voltage stability assessment (TVSA) method is proposed for large scale hybrid power grid. Firstly, the power grid is partitioned into a primary sub-area and secondary subareas by agglomerative hierarchical clustering (AHC). In the first stage, the cluster graph convolutional network (Cluster-GCN) is employed to extract localized features of each sub-area independently. These features are then integrated into a feature matrix. The features of primary sub-area are retained intact, and the features of secondary sub-areas are simplified by average pooling. In the second stage, convolutional neural network (CNN) is employed to capture both interactive influence between sub-areas and critical localized information within the primary sub-area in the integrated feature matrix. Squeeze-and-Excitation Network (SE-Net) is incorporated to concentrating on key features by attention mechanism. The case study of Shandong provincial power grid in China demonstrates that the proposed model enhances TVSA accuracy compared to conventional methods.

title: The Impact of Popularity Bias and Perceived Fairness on Intention to Use Recommender System in Indonesian E-Commerce
authors: Vaniecia C. Dewi, Nilo Legowo
abstract: This study examines factors influencing users' Behavioral Intention (BI) to use Artificial Intelligence (AI)based recommender systems in Indonesian B2C e-commerce by integrating Popularity Bias (PB) and Perceived Fairness (PF) into the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). The purpose of this study is to investigate how PB shapes PF and how fairnessrelated perceptions subsequently influence Trust (T), Perceived Usefulness (PU), and users' Behavioral Intention (BI) to use recommender systems. The proposed research model analyzes the relationships among these key behavioral factors, including Cognitive Absorption (CA). A quantitative survey was conducted with 400 active users of major Indonesian ecommerce platforms, and the data were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The results show that PB affects PF, which plays a central role in shaping T, PU, and BI. CA enhances PU, while T and PU significantly drive users' BI to use recommender systems. These findings emphasize the importance of fairness-aware recommendation mechanisms and extend TAM in the context of AI-based recommender systems.

title: Evaluation of the Models for Abstract Generation
authors: Dragica S. Ljubisavljević, Milica J. Bačić, Dušan S. Savić, Siniša S. Vlajić
abstract: This paper investigates the application of prompt engineering for scientific abstract generation using large language models. The study analyzes how different prompt formulations, ranging from general zero-shot prompts to domain-specific and structured instructions, influence the quality of generated abstracts. Two large language models are evaluated by comparing their generated outputs to the original human-written abstracts using standard reference-based metrics, including ROUGE-1, ROUGE-2, ROUGE-L, and BLEU. The experimental analysis demonstrates that increasingly specific and structured prompts lead to improved content coverage, coherence, and alignment with the reference abstracts, without any model fine-tuning. Furthermore, the results indicate consistent performance differences between the evaluated models, highlighting the combined impact of prompt design and model choice. These findings emphasize the effectiveness of structured prompt engineering as a practical approach for scientific abstract generation in zero-shot settings.

title: AutoOnboard Pro: An Intelligent Client Onboarding Automation System Using Workflow Orchestration and Machine Learning
authors: Trupti Lotlikar, Lakshmi Gadhikar, Akshayya Sawant, Anushka Sawant, Anu Sophiya, Rohan Bhagwat
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Organizational productivity often depends on how smoothly tasks and client onboarding is executed. Many traditional systems, however, still rely on scattered tools and manual steps, which can slow processes and create friction.
