This work presents AutoOnboard Pro, an intelligent workflow automation platform that combines AI-enhanced onboarding with the no-code n8n orchestration framework. n8n provides a flexible way to automate processes across multiple applications without writing custom code. It allows triggers, actions, and conditional logic to be linked across tools like Trello, Slack, MicrosoftTeams, GoogleSheets, and Gmail by creating workflows. This improves project coordination, eliminates redundancy and creates consistent task execution, all across disparate tools. Additionally, by integrating machine learning models with n8n’s workflow capabilities, the system is able to create custom welcome messages, develop weekly activity summaries, assign project tasks dynamically, and execute several other routine tasks. The platform’s adaptive architecture supports parallel onboarding and real-time cross-platform collaboration, providing actionable insights to optimize workflow efficiency.

title: A Stackelberg Game Framework for EV Dynamic Pricing with Prospect Theory-Based Bounded Rationality
authors: Tianwei Jiang, Shan Gao, Yuxuan Huang, Xin Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To mitigate grid fluctuations caused by uncoordinated electric vehicle (EV) charging and address the limitations of traditional rational-choice models, this paper proposes a dynamic pricing optimization strategy based on a Stackelberg game framework. In the follower stage (user side), the model departs from the assumption of perfect rationality by incorporating prospect theory and quantal response equilibrium. Using the average static time-of-use (TOU) price as a psychological reference point, the model quantifies users' loss aversion and waiting anxiety. Users are segmented into Ideal, Swing, Helpless, and Rigid types based on parking flexibility and anxiety sensitivity, forming a discrete choice probabilistic model to predict aggregated demand. In the leader stage (grid side), the operator optimizes time-series tariffs to smooth load fluctuations, secure revenue, and ensure price stability. To guarantee social equity, Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR) constraints are integrated to protect vulnerable user groups from excessive costs. The resulting bi-level, non-convex optimization problem is solved using a Primal-Dual iterative algorithm. Case studies from an industrial park in Nanjing demonstrate that, compared to static TOU pricing, this strategy effectively shifts load to off-peak periods and significantly reduces grid volatility. Furthermore, it achieves a Pareto improvement, lowering costs across all user segments while maintaining grid operational safety.

title: Towards an Explainable Reinforcement Learning Agent for Resource-Constrained Robots
authors: Giorgio Clivio, Luigi La Spada, Brian Davison
abstract: This paper considers the problem of how a robot during a planetary exploration mission can operate under strict resource constraints, requiring high transparency in decision-making: a context where failure is not an option. Standard reinforcement learning methods are often unsuitable for such environments due to their lack of transparency and high data requirements. In this work, we introduce SPARCbandit, a lightweight, explainable reinforcement learning agent that integrates linear Q-learning with tile coding, adaptive action cost tracking, and behavioural latching. We benchmark SPARCbandit in both standard control (LunarLander-v3) and simulated planetary locomotion (a quadruped lunar rover in PyBullet), demonstrating improved learning speed, stability, and transparency.

title: Toward Learning-Based Power Subsystem Simulation for Scalable Digital Twins in Distributed Space Systems
authors: Angel Pan Du, Andreas M. Hein
abstract: Simulating the full set of satellite subsystems with high fidelity is essential for both mission design and operational planning. However, conventional simulators are often computationally intensive and difficult to scale, particularly in the context of distributed space systems (DSS) such as constellations, swarms, or formation-flying architectures. This work explores the use of machine learning (ML) as a lightweight, data-driven alternative for modeling the power subsystem, specifically, the prediction of solar array power output, battery input power, and battery output power. Using open-access telemetry from the BEESAT-4 CubeSat mission, Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) models were trained to estimate these quantities based on a wide range of onboard sensor data. The models achieved high accuracy, with mean absolute errors below 2% of the respective power ranges. Permutation Feature Importance analysis revealed that subsystem activity indicators, such as charger currents, sun vector orientation, and communication system states, play a critical role in power behavior. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of using ML to approximate subsystem dynamics with minimal computational overhead, and provide insights into sensor prioritization for future Digital Twin (DT) implementations in space systems.

title: Data-Driven Terramechanics Approach Towards a Realistic Real-Time Simulator for Lunar Rovers
authors: Jakob M. Kern, James M. Hurrell, Shreya Santra, Keisuke Takehana, Kentaro Uno, Kazuya Yoshida
abstract: High-fidelity simulators for the lunar surface provide a digital environment for extensive testing of rover operations and mission planning. However, current simulators focus on either visual realism or physical accuracy, which limits their capability to replicate lunar conditions comprehensively. This work addresses that gap by combining high visual fidelity with realistic terrain interaction for a realistic representation of rovers on the lunar surface. Because direct simulation of wheel-soil interactions is computationally expensive, a data-driven approach was adopted, using regression models for slip and sinkage from data collected in both full-rover and single-wheel experiments and simulations. The resulting regression-based terramechanics model accurately reproduced steady-state and dynamic slip, as well as sinkage behavior, on flat terrain and slopes up to 20°, with validation against field test results. Additionally, improvements were made to enhance the realism of terrain deformation and wheel trace visualization. This method supports real-time applications that require physically plausible terrain response alongside high visual fidelity.

title: The Transparency Paradox: Information Asymmetry and Concentration in Builder Markets
authors: Mohammad Pasha, Mohammed Ali Shaik
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: This paper investigates the quantitative impact of information asymmetry, specifically private order flow, on market concentration and proposer revenue in a simulated block building market. Using an agent-based model, we compare a scenario with symmetric information (public mempool) to one with asymmetric information (private order flow assigned to builders) under varying proposer selection policies (epsilon-greedy). Our results reveal a counterintuitive outcome relative to empirical observations from real-world builder markets. While prior studies often associate private order flow with greater concentration and inequality among builders, our simulation, built on the assumption that proposers retain verifiable value transparency, enabled by mechanisms that bound builders' ability to misrepresent block value and allow proposers to reliably rank bids in expectation, suggests the opposite. Specifically, we observe lower market concentration <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 2 0 - 2 8 0}$</tex> across all epsilon policies (as measured by the HHI) under the private order flow setting. MEV extraction rates also show notable differences: symmetric information settings plateau at around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28-32 \%$</tex>, while the private flow regime with verifiable value transparency allows MEV extraction rates to reach up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2 \%}$</tex> for low epsilon, indicating enhanced efficiency in capturing MEV value. These findings highlight that it is not private order flow itself that drives centralization, but rather the opacity surrounding it. Thus, strengthening transparency between builders and proposers emerges as a critical lever for reducing concentration risks and promoting fairness in block building markets.

title: Optimized Detection of Small Objects in Aerial Imagery
authors: Bhavya Zaveri, Tejas Gaikwad, Hetansh Sanghavi, Richa Sharma
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: It is still a challenging task to detect minute objects in the imagery of drones because of poor resolution, changes in scale of objects, occlusion, and background clutters. Our hypothesis in this paper is to present a sophisticated detection framework by using a blend of superior deep learning techniques, such as Slicing Aided Hyper Inference (SAHI), Weighted Box Fusion (WBF), and a hybrid of YOLOv8 and Faster R-CNN. The framework also improves the capability of detecting small and partially obscured targets by using the image slicing method and aggregating the outputs of multiple detectors. The conducted experiments on the VisDrone benchmark have shown that the accuracy, precision and recall of these models are significantly higher than those of regular detection models. The modular structure of the framework also enables easy flexibility to various situations, including city protection, environmental monitoring, and crisis control. On the whole, the current work presents a viable and efficient strategy in solving the long-standing problem of small-object detection in the complicated aerial scenes.

title: Brain MRI Age Estimation Using Convolutional Neural Networks and Genetic Algorithm-Based Feature Selection
authors: Jangam Subbarayudu, G. Michael, V. Sheeja Kumari
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Accurate estimation of chronological age from structural brain MRI scans has wide-ranging uses in neuroscience research, clinical diagnosis, and the study of neurodegenerative mechanisms. In this article, a hybrid method integrating genetic algorithm-based feature selection and convolutional neural networks is presented for estimating brain age from T1-weighted MRI. We define age estimation as aclass classification problem for human subjects in the age range of 18 to 92 years from 486 multi-modal features (volumetric, morphometric, texture, and shape) derived from preprocessed MRI scans. Genetic algorithm optimization provided 142 best features (71% reduction in dimensionality), which were used to train an in-house CNN classifier. The publicly released PAC 2019 database (2,638 healthy subjects) was used to evaluate the system. Mean absolute error was 4.82 years, classification accuracy was 87.3%, and Pearson correlation coefficient was 0.924. Ablation studies show a 19% improvement over CNN methods that do not use feature selection. The features selected correspond to established neurobiological markers of aging such as hippocampal atrophy, ventricular enlargement, and cortical thinning and hence have biological interpretability. The hybrid approach unifies the representational capabilities of deep learning with evolutionary optimization for making brain age estimation robust, efficient, and interpretable for datasets that are not excessively large.

title: Age-Specific Autism Spectrum Disorder Prediction Using Optimized Machine Learning Classifiers with Advanced Feature Transformation Techniques
authors: Pavuluri Jhansi Rani, Vijaya Chandra Jadala
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Accurate and early prediction of autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is a critical step toward enabling timely clinical intervention and personalized therapeutic planning. The complexity and variability of ASD manifestations across different age groups-Toddlers, Children, Adolescents, and Adults-necessitate robust data-driven models capable of generalizing across diverse behavioural and developmental profiles. This paper presents a comparative machine learning framework for ASD prediction that systematically evaluates the performance of three classification algorithms-Logistic Regression (LR), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA)-under four advanced feature scaling and transformation techniques: Quantile Transformer (QT), Power Transformer (PT), Normalizer, and Min-Average Scaling (MAS). Each preprocessing approach is applied to behavioural and questionnaire-based ASD datasets segmented by age category to analyse its impact on classification accuracy, sensitivity, and model stability. Experimental results reveal that appropriate feature transformation significantly influences the learning dynamics and generalization ability of the classifiers. Among all tested configurations, SVM combined with Quantile Transformation and LDA with Power Transformation yielded the most consistent and accurate predictions across all age groups. The findings confirm that the integration of optimized feature scaling with robust classifiers enhances ASD detection reliability and provides a scalable computational framework for age-adaptive autism prediction and screening systems.

title: An IoT-Driven Machine Learning Approach for Predictive Maintenance of CNC Machines
authors: Ganesh Dattatraya Korwar, Sanket More, Mayuresh Ware, Om Nimbalkar, Divesh Vishwakarma, Vedant Korke, Nitin Satpute
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: In modern manufacturing environments, minimizing unplanned machine downtime is critical for ensuring productivity and cost efficiency. This paper presents a cost-effective predictive maintenance system for CNC machines using IoT and machine learning. Real-time sensor data, including vibration from the MPU6050 and temperature from the DHT11, was collected from the spindle and transmitted via an ESP8266 microcontroller to Firebase Realtime Database. The data was analyzed using machine learning algorithms such as Random Forest, SVM, KNN, and Gradient Boosting, with Random Forest achieving the highest fault prediction accuracy of 93.2%. A Telegram bot was integrated to send real-time alerts to operators upon anomaly detection. The proposed system offers an affordable, scalable solution suitable for small and medium enterprises, supporting the transition toward Industry 4.0 by enhancing machine reliability and reducing unplanned downtime.

title: Robust State Evaluation and Adaptive Fault Warning for Heat Exchangers: An Uncertainty-Aware Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Yuzhen Dang, Fuliang Ma, Gong Lu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Chemical heat exchangers often face complex dynamic environments characterized by strong coupling and nonlinear disturbances. Traditional threshold-based methods often struggle with these variable operating conditions, leading to high false alarm rates and delayed responses. To address this, this paper proposes a robust Reinforcement Learning-based State Evaluation and Adaptive Warning Strategy (RL-SEAWS). Unlike static classifiers, the proposed agent dynamically optimizes its warning policy by interacting with a tailored reward mechanism that balances sensitivity and reliability. The model improves upon the Deep Q-Network (DQN) by integrating a temporal dual-attention encoder to capture local-global dependencies and an uncertainty-aware Q-value regularization term to penalize risky decisions in unseen scenarios. Experimental results on the Tennessee Eastman Process (TEP) show that RL-SEAWS improves fault identification accuracy by 17.8% while maintaining low false positive rates, demonstrating superior adaptability compared to conventional methods.

title: Real-Time Image Inpainting: A GAN-Based Approach for User-Specified Object Removal
authors: Savaliya Nirav Jayanthibhai, Unagar Amish Manubhai, Rampriya R S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) provide realistic and context-sensitive reconstructions, and have been proven to be able to accomplish challenging tasks such as image inpainting. In this work, we propose an enhanced GAN-based inpainting framework that combines Mask-RCNN with an Intersection over Union (IoU)-based mask refinement strategy. Mask-RCNN and IoU strategy are used for object localization and enabling accurate user-directed object removal, respectively. Compared to existing methods, the proposed work ignores post-inpainting operations such as noise reduction, color correction, and image enhancement, followed by DeepFillv2 method. Instead, we considered these steps as a separate post-processing phase. This approach aids in maintaining the image structure properly and provides an even better outcome. The proposed model is trained using the Places365 dataset. Metrics such as PSNR, FID, SSIM, and LPIPS are used to evaluate quality of image reconstruction and realistic image appearance. The results show the proposed work performs well compared to latest inpainting models. Also, the examples of failure cases are included in this paper to improve the future real-time inpainting systems.

title: Quantum-Inspired Metaheuristic Optimization for Dynamic Stand-Alone PV DC-DC Converter Sizing in Smart Grids
authors: Dr.V.Jayalakshmi, Ravikumar Kenuva, Venkata Rao Chilukuri, Santhosha B M, Aied Eqab Muraay Majed, K. Selvakumarasamy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Smart grids Photovoltaic (PV) systems need an efficient conversion of various powers when the environment varies. The available approaches, e.g. traditional PSO and GA-based optimizations, are slow to converge, traps in local optima, and are not very adaptable to various irradiance and temperature. This paper will present a quantum-inspired meta-physics optimization algorithm of dynamic DC-DC buck-boost converter sizing, combining quantum bits in the probabilistic search of the search problem with adaptive rotation gates in the global convergence of the problem. It consists of the methodology of PV parameters correction, computing the power, converter modeling, and an iterative optimization based on the Quantum-Inspired Population Optimization (QIPO) algorithm. The concept was applied to MATLAB/Simulink with Kaggle Solar Panel PV System dataset. It has been demonstrated that performance appraisal indicates high efficiency of converters (96.7%), low power losses (3.2 W), and low voltage ripple (0.45 V) and quick convergence. The suggested solution has exhibited improved energy conversion, dependable voltage regulation, and low-cost operation of PV systems, which is a viable solution in the context of smart grid.

title: Enhancing DevSecOps with Generative AI for Secure Code Review and Threat Modeling
authors: Avdhesh Kumar Bhardwaj
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: As development pipelines grow increasingly automated, ensuring that security keeps pace with speed remains a major challenge. Conventional static and dynamic analysis tools flag common coding weaknesses but rarely understand the developer’s intent or the broader design context. This paper introduces a framework that embeds Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) into the DevSecOps workflow to support contextual and explainable security decisions. The proposed approach uses large language models to perform real-time reasoning during code review and threat modeling, reducing false positives while improving insight into potential risks. Experiments show that combining generative analysis with existing CI/CD tooling produces measurable gains in accuracy, review efficiency, and developer confidence, while also enabling teams to detect subtle design flaws earlier, streamline collaboration across security and engineering groups, and maintain stronger governance without slowing delivery cycles. Additionally, the framework enhances traceability, supports more adaptive threat modeling practices, and provides actionable recommendations tailored to project-specific architectures, thereby strengthening the overall resilience of modern software ecosystems.

title: Plant Irrigation Water Robot
authors: Mihir Talele, Eshaan Kadav, Aqleem Siddiqui, Mayank Patil, Praseed Kumar, Bipin Mashilkar
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The project involves the design and development of an autonomous irrigation sprinkler robot to optimize water usage in agriculture. This robot employs advanced sensors to continuously monitor soil moisture and environmental conditions, ensuring plants receive water only when necessary. Mounted on a mobile platform and equipped with a precision sprinkler system, it can traverse different terrains while distributing water efficiently based on real-time sensor data. A built-in control system analyzes this data to determine the optimal timing and duration of irrigation, significantly reducing water wastage and promoting healthier plant growth. Initial field tests have shown a marked decrease in water consumption compared to conventional irrigation techniques, underlining the robot’s potential in promoting sustainable farming. Future enhancements will focus on improving navigation algorithms and integrating features such as nutrient delivery and pest detection. Although the present prototype is optimized for smaller plots, the movement strategy is inherently scalable and can be extended to longer rows or larger land blocks with minor modifications.

title: Touchless Computer Control Through Hand Gesture Recognition and Speech Modeling
authors: Balamurgan K S, Navabharathi J, Preethi S, Sarawin R, K.V. Nithya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: In today's world of technology, users no longer need to touch anything to interact with their computers. Touch-free interfaces are becoming one of the most interesting areas of Human–Computer Interaction because they offer many benefits over traditional methods of controlling the computer (i.e., using a mouse or keyboard). A big disadvantage of using mice and keyboards is that is do not allow for people who have disabilities (e.g., blindness and paralysis) to control their computers, which means it cannot participate fully in society. Therefore, hand gestures and voice commands have become popular examples of how can control the computer in a hands-free environment. This study proposes the development of a multimodal user interface that allows the user to control a computer using both hand gestures and voice commands simultaneously. Hand gestures will be captured by a webcam and processed using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to perform actions such as moving the cursor, clicking, scrolling, or dragging and dropping items on a screen. On the other hand, voice commands will be processed using Hidden Markov Models (HMMs) to identify specific spoken commands that tell the computer what to do (e.g., open a specific application or perform a function on a computer). Using both visual (gesture) and audio (voice command) inputs will provide real-time feedback and allow for a robust system that works in different environments with varying levels of light and user conditions while maintaining a high quality user experience.

title: Transfer Learning-Based Computer Vision Framework for Automated Detection and Localization of Lung Ultrasound Vertical Artifacts
authors: Myriam C. Jumila-Polinar
abstract: Lung ultrasound (LUS) is widely used for pulmonary assessment due to its portability, safety, and realtime imaging capability, with vertical artifacts (B-lines) serving as key indicators of lung pathology. However, manual identification of these artifacts is operator-dependent and subject to inter-observer variability. This study presents a transfer learning-based computer vision framework for automated detection and localization of LUS vertical artifacts using clinically acquired ultrasound images. The dataset consists of polygon-annotated LUS images collected from patients with pulmonary diseases at national referral hospitals, enabling detailed spatial modeling of artifact morphology. The proposed approach leverages multi-scale feature extraction to capture the elongated and directionally consistent characteristics of vertical artifacts. Experimental results demonstrate stable convergence and strong detection performance within the evaluated dataset, with qualitative analyses confirming accurate localization on unseen samples. These findings illustrate the feasibility of applying deep learning-based methods to LUS artifact analysis and motivate further validation across more diverse clinical settings.

title: A Novel Hybrid Deep Learning Framework with Multi-Modal Feature Fusion and Temporal Analysis for Enhanced Heart Disease Prediction
authors: Giri Pooja, M. Prashanthi
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases remain the leading cause of mortality worldwide, necessitating advanced predictive systems for early detection and intervention. While existing machine learning approaches have shown promise, they suffer from several limitations including reliance on small datasets, lack of temporal analysis, insufficient feature engineering, and absence of multi-modal data integration. This paper proposes a novel Hybrid Deep Learning Framework (HDLF) that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, and Transformer architectures for comprehensive heart disease prediction. Our approach integrates structured clinical data, temporal vital sign patterns, ECG signal analysis, and demographic information through an innovative attention-based fusion mechanism. The proposed HDLF-ATT (Hybrid Deep Learning Framework with Attention) achieves 97.3% accuracy, 96.8% sensitivity, and 97.9% specificity on a comprehensive dataset of 15,000 patients, significantly outperforming existing approaches while providing interpretable predictions through attention visualization.

title: Image Detection Using Federated Learning
authors: Kalyani. Priyanka, M Swapna, M Sowmya
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Image detection is essential in applications like autonomous vehicles, smart surveillance, and medical imaging. Traditional centralized approaches face challenges with privacy, security, and regulatory compliance, making Federated Learning (FL) a promising alternative. On edge devices, FL allows decentralised model training while preserving raw data local while sharing encrypted updates for aggregation. Techniques like Federated Averaging (FedAvg), clustered FL, and personalized FL address challenges like non-IID data and limitations on resources. Differential privacy, safe privacy, and other privacy measures aggregation ensure data security. Despite limitations like communication overhead and computational costs, innovations in model compression and efficient protocols enhance FL's potential for scalable, privacy-preserving image detection systems.

title: Highly-Sensitive Cholesterol Detection Using β-Cyclodextrin and Graphene Oxide Enhanced Surface Plasmon Resonance Sensor
authors: Xuegang Li, Jiaqi Zhang, Dongqi Yan, Xue Zhou, Fang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This article researched a novel cholesterol sensor that leverages the power of reflective surface plasmon resonance (SPR). The sensor's core principle relies on the highly specific interaction between cholesterol and β-cyclodextrin (β-CD). A complex is formed when cholesterol adsorbs onto the β-CD molecules on the sensor surface. The measurable refractive index change on the surface of optical fibers is caused by this complex formation, which translates into a detectable shift in the wavelength of the SPR spectrum. The sensor's effectiveness is further amplified by incorporating two-dimensional graphene oxide as a sensitizing material. This addition significantly enhances the sensor's sensitivity, reaching an impressive 2910 nm/RIU(Refractive Index Unit), representing a remarkable 75% improvement compared to its unsensitized counterpart. Meanwhile, the abundant functional groups on the graphene oxide surface provide a natural and efficient binding platform for the cholesterol-selective β-CD probes. This sensor can detect cholesterol with a concentration of 0.1 μM, a response time of about 30 seconds, and a detection limit of 2 nM. In addition, the sensor achieves excellent selectivity for cholesterol in solution, minimizing interference from other biomolecules in solution. The compact size, plug and play, and excellent stability of this sensor enable it to perform high-sensitivity cholesterol detection, and it is expected to be used in the future to detect human cholesterol levels.

title: A High-Speed and Low-Power 1.2-kV SiC ASG-MOSFET With Enhanced Short-Circuit Robustness
authors: Yibo Zhang, Xiaoyan Tang, Hao Yuan, Jingkai Guo, Chenyu Wang, Zhiwen Zhang, Haohang Yang, Keyu Liu, Yu Zhou, Fengyu Du, Zhaojun Li, Yuming Zhang, Qingwen Song
abstract: In this work, a 1.2-kV high-speed, low-power SiC ASG-MOS was successfully fabricated and its excellent dynamic performance and enhanced SC ruggedness were experimentally demonstrated. Various electrical characteristics were measured and compared with those of the conventional MOSFET (C-MOS) fabricated on the same wafer and state-of-the-art SiC MOSFETs. Results show that ASG-MOS significantly improves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${C}_{\text {iss}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${C}_{\text {rss}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> while achieving low parasitic capacitance and effectively achieving high switching speed and low switching losses. Parameters such as <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${C}_{\text {iss}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${C}_{\text {rss}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and high-frequency figure of merit [HF-FOM(<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R}_{\text {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${C}_{\text {rss}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>)] reached the highest levels reported to date, while short-circuit withstand time (SCWT) was improved by 60%. ASG-MOS demonstrates excellent tradeoffs in switch, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R}_{\text {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and SC robustness, highlighting its tremendous potential for high-frequency, high-reliability power electronics applications.

publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)

title: Literature Review on AI-Based Real-Time Scene understanding and Guidance for Visually Impaired Users
authors: Sudha P, Sharan S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: By providing real-time object recognition, traffic signal awareness, indoor/outdoor navigation, money recognition, medical label reading, and voice-guided interaction, AI-Vision is a smartphone-based assistive technology that enables people with visual impairments achieve independent mobility. By combining on-device computer vision, spatial inference for directional hints, OCR for text extraction, and text-to-speech feedback, the system offers rapid, context-aware help with user-friendly controls and haptics in a range of operating modes. AI-Vision focuses on offline-first operation and privacy-preserving design, with the goal of providing dependable, low-latency help on commodity smartphones while remaining extensible for cloud-augmented features. This article outlines the system overview, summary of Model Selection Trends, Workflow, Latency and Real-Time Performance, and conclusion section to benchmark assistive systems.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A

title: Boundary stabilization for uncertain delay Markovian reaction-diffusion neural networks with partially unknown transition rates via sliding mode approach
authors: Wei-Jie Zhou, Xiao-Zhen Liu, Kai-Ning Wu, Yong-Xin Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper investigates the sliding mode boundary stabilization for uncertain delay Markovian reaction-diffusion neural networks with partially unknown transition rates. First, a mode-dependent sliding mode surface (SMS) and a mode-dependent sliding mode boundary controller are designed, offering enhanced adaptability to different modes. Next, the almost sure finite-time reachability of the proposed SMS is derived, despite the challenges posed by Markovian switching with partially unknown transition rates. Then, by employing the Lyapunov stability method, inequality analysis techniques, and the free-weighting matrices approach, a criterion is established to guarantee the mean-square robust exponential stability of the closed-loop system. The proposed approach effectively overcomes the challenges arising from the coexistence of partially unknown transition rates, diffusion dynamics, and boundary disturbances under Markovian switching. Finally, the proposed theoretical results are validated via application to temperature control in lithium-ion battery packs for electric vehicles, demonstrating their effectiveness.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Modular Mobile Robot for Autonomous Hospital Logistics
authors: Neri Niccolò Dei, Simona Gandah, Giorgia Spreafico, Andrea Firrincieli, Raquel Juliá Ros, Víctor Solaz Estevan, Ángel Soriano, Francesco Scotto Di Luzio, Nevio Luigi Tagliamonte, Lampis Papakostas, Michalis Karamousadakis, Alejandro Medrano-Gil, Javier Del Río-Martín, Vasileios Lolis, Konstantinos Votis, Pilar Sala Soriano, Jordi Rovira Simón, Przemysław Kardas, Paweł Lewek, Dariusz Timler, Laura Llorente Sanz, Jorge Nieto De Vicente-Arche, Saskia Haitjema, Imo Hoefer, Sergio Guillen, German Gutierrez, Leandro Pecchia, Loredana Zollo, Giuseppe Fico, Marcello Chiurazzi, Gastone Ciuti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Hospital logistics can significantly benefit from autonomous robotic technologies capable of alleviating personnel workload, reducing operational costs, and streamlining internal workflows. This paper introduces HOSBOT (HOSpital roBOT), a modular and cost-effective robotic system designed to enhance hospital logistics and workflow management through autonomous missions and real-time monitoring of transported items. HOSBOT combines a commercially available autonomous mobile robot with a customisable non-motorised cart, the SmartRack, which accommodates standalone, sensorised SmartBoxes equipped with Radio Frequency IDentification technology. This paper describes the overall design of HOSBOT, including its mechanical, electrical, and software interfaces. The system has been validated in three pilot experiments in real hospital environments across Europe, demonstrating good levels of acceptability, usability and efficacy. This innovative robotic solution highlights the potential for scalable integration of autonomous mobile robots in healthcare and other indoor logistics settings.

title: Movable Antennas-Assisted Secure Transmission for Cooperative Wireless Communication
authors: Zhihui Shang, Tao Zhang, Ruoyu Zhang, Weiwei Yang, Hao Wu, Xiaoqiang Qiao
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Wireless communication is vulnerable to deep fading and eavesdropping, especially in long-distance double-hop scenarios. To address these issues, this paper proposes the movable antenna (MA-assisted secure transmission scheme. This scheme is designed for a cooperative wireless communication system in the presence of multiple single-antenna eavesdroppers. Our objective is to maximize the achievable secrecy rate, by jointly optimizing the transmit beamforming and antenna positions vectors. To solve the highly non-convex problem due to the strongly coupled variables, the alternating optimization and projected gradient ascent (PGA) methods are designed to the joint update of MA position and beamforming. Furthermore, an artificial noise (AN) interference signal is introduced. Specifically, the legitimate link actively transmits jamming noise to eavesdroppers during signal transmission, thereby enhancing the channel capacity of the secure communication system. Finally, the simulation results show that the proposed MA-assisted secure transmission for the cooperative wireless communication system significantly outperforms conventional fixed-position antenna (FPA)-assisted systems by up to 70% and existing benchmarks in terms of secrecy rate.

title: A High-quality and Robust Intravascular Electromyography (iEMG) Acquisition Method for Locomotor Tasks
authors: Ying Du, Gan Liu, Yudong Ma, Sining Li, Yahan Duan, Wenzhi Wang, Feng Duan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Electromyography (EMG) is essential in medical and rehabilitation fields for assessing neuromuscular functions. However, mainstream traditional surface EMG (sEMG) is susceptible to electrode displacement or noise interference during walking, leading to lower signal quality and limited long-term stability, which constrains its broader application. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel intravascular electromyography (iEMG) acquisition method. By a minimally invasive surgery, a self-expanding stent sensor with microelectrode and lead was implanted into the femoral vein adjacent to the tibialis anterior muscle to record deep muscle activity. In this study, sEMG and iEMG were simultaneously acquired from sheep hindlimb muscle in standing state and walking state for comparative analysis of their electrophysiological properties. Level walking served as the dynamic locomotor task in this work. Totally, this acquisition experiment lasted for three days. In standing state, both EMG exhibit a high correlation (Spearman’s ρ = 0.9018, p < 0.001). In walking state, iEMG demonstrates a 10.04% higher signal-to-noise ratio compared to sEMG. Additionally, iEMG shows a 33.82% lower coefficient of variation in power spectral density than sEMG, indicating a 1.51-fold improvement in signal stability. These results demonstrate that our iEMG acquisition method enables high-quality and robust recordings for long-term monitoring during walking, which provides a reliable foundation for clinical rehabilitation applications requiring precise, long-term muscle activity tracking.

title: Automatic Monitoring and Controlling of Substation using LabVIEW
authors: Thangam E, Anandhakumar C, Balaji Vignesh L K, Ganesh N, T.Sudhakar, Senthil Kumar A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Substation automation is critical for enhancing operational reliability, minimizing response to faults, and protecting assets. Substation control currently relies heavily upon the conventional use of multiple Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs) connected to a SCADA architecture. This has resulted in increased cost and complexity of system design. This paper describes a LabVIEW-based monitoring and control system, which allows for a centralized, software-based replacement of traditional IED functionalities. Continuous collection of electrical parameters (voltage, current, power, and power factor) as well as transformer health indicators (temperature and oil level) is done using sensors that are interfaced with a Data Acquisition system and Shielded Connector Box. Acquired analog signals from these sensors are digitized, processed, and analyzed in real-time within the LabVIEW environment allowing for automated fault detection, relay operation, alarm generation, and logging of data. The use of a prototype with radial substation demonstrates that effective supervision, protection, and visualization of the radial secured through the use of an interactive graphical interface. The proposed solution will reduce dependence on hardware, simplify the design of automation, and provide a cost-effective and flexible alternative for substations using only limited SCADA.

title: Smart Service Bot
authors: Varsha Y. Bhole, Sandhya S. Dongre, Vinay T. Patil, Pornima V. Elyekar, Vedika P. Gawand
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Routine transportation of documents and lightweight materials within academic institutions is still largely performed manually, often interrupting faculty and administrative activities. This paper presents the design, development, and experimental evaluation of a voice-controlled Smart Service Bot intended for indoor delivery tasks in structured academic environments. The robot is built around an ESP32 microcontroller and operates using Bluetooth-based voice commands that allow users to record and autonomously replay delivery routes using a Teach-and-Repeat navigation strategy. A 3D-printed chassis provides mechanical stability and supports payloads of up to 2 kg. Obstacle detection is implemented using an ultrasonic sensor mounted on a servo motor to ensure safe navigation at low speeds. Experimental trials conducted in classrooms and corridors demonstrate reliable route repetition, stable payload handling, and effective obstacle response. The proposed system demonstrates that simple embedded hardware and basic navigation logic are sufficient for routine indoor delivery tasks in academic environments.

title: Modified Low Light Image Enhancement using Retinex Decomposition combined with Zero-DCE and its variant
authors: Puppala Sai Ratna, Chandra Mouli P.V.S.S.R.
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Zero-Reference Deep Curve Estimation (Zero-DCE) is one of the most effective methods for enhancing low-light images, improving overall brightness, contrast, and visibility without relying on reference or paired data. A simple and efficient lighter version of Zero-DCE called Zero-DCE++ has been introduced, which reduces the number of parameters, computation time, and improves overall performance. Despite their effectiveness, both variants still face challenges such as noise amplification, color distortion, and poor preservation of structural and textural details. In this paper, a pre-processing step is introduced to Zero-DCE method using multi-scale retinex decomposition (MSRD). The architecture of Zero-DCE is also modified. The MSRD ensures the preservation of essential details while enhancing illumination before feeding the image into the Zero-DCE network. Additionally, the Modified Zero-DCE architecture has been optimized by reducing the number of layers for computational efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a PSNR improvement of 4.96 dB and an SSIM gain of 0.33 over the original Zero-DCE on the LOL dataset. These results confirm that the proposed approach effectively balances enhancement quality, structural preservation, and efficiency in low-light image enhancement tasks.

title: Bi-Objective Optimal Mission Planning for Active Debris Removal with Refueling
authors: Vivid Chutivikai, Ryo Iijima, Toshinori Kuwahara
abstract: Active Debris Removal (ADR) is critical to prevent the continued growth of debris, with an annual removal rate of at least five debris to stabilize the debris population in Low Earth Orbit (LEO). This study addresses the removal of rocket bodies in Sun Synchronous Orbit (SSO) regions using the orbital transfer vehicle (OTV) equipped with de-orbit kits, and supported by a space logistics mission including on-orbit refueling and resupply to prolong OTV’s lifetime. The ant colony optimization is employed to optimize the removal sequence for a removal mission of 25 and 35 debris. The sequences are evaluated across multiple OTV designs, the resulting Paretooptimal solutions in mission time and propellant mass reveal the performance and efficiencies in propellant consumption of each design. The results provide valuable insights for mission designs to balance the fuel capacity and the servicing frequency, guiding more efficient and sustainable ADR mission planning.

title: Research on Intelligent Detection of Remote Power Grid Outage Status under Multi source Data Fusion
authors: Xingyuan Fan, Hui Li, Yin Zheng, Shaoliang Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: With the rapid development of smart grids, remote power outage status detection is of great significance for the reliability of power grids and rapid response to faults. Therefore, this study proposes an intelligent detection method for remote power grid outage status based on multi-source data fusion. Research on the design of multi-source data fusion perception algorithm based on DBN-DS architecture, using deep belief network to extract features from heterogeneous data such as power grid current, voltage, power, and meteorological geography. Comparative divergence algorithm is used to complete network pre training and combined with gradient descent method to optimize parameters. Simultaneously introducing the DS evidence theory to integrate multidimensional feature probability allocation, constructing a recognition framework and power set mapping relationship. An improved Adaboost algorithm is proposed for power outage detection tasks, which establishes a multi-stage error feedback system by dynamically adjusting the sample weight mechanism. In iterative training, the cumulative effect of weak classifiers is comprehensively evaluated, the global error distribution characteristics are reconstructed, and the transient signal identification ability is optimized. The test results show that the method proposed in this paper achieves an accuracy of over 95% and a response time of 0.89-1.78 seconds in power outage detection.

title: A Compact TDAC-Integrated SAR ADC for Low-Voltage Biomedical Applications
authors: Arra Neeraja, G. Snehalatha, Kunapuli Sai Tanuja, Maale Nitin Goud, Potharpalli Sharmista
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: In this paper, a time-domain DAC (TDAC) integrated with a low-power and area-efficient SAR ADC for low-voltage biomedical signal capture in 45-nm CMOS technology is presented. When compared to traditional DAC implementations, the suggested TDAC dramatically reduces dynamic switching power by using time-sequenced weighted current activation. Low-amplitude physiological signals, such as ECG, EEG, and neural signals, can be converted quickly and accurately using an optimised SAR control logic and a high-speed comparator. Reliable functioning across process and temperature fluctuations is ensured by strong reference and biassing mechanisms. The suggested architecture's appropriateness for energy-constrained wearable and implantable biomedical applications is confirmed by simulation results that show good linearity, resolution, and conversion speed.

title: Error Accumulation using Linearized Models for Aggregating Flexibility in Distribution Systems
authors: Yanlin Jiang, Xinliang Dai, Frederik Zahn, Yi Guo, Veit Hagenmeyer
abstract: This paper investigates flexibility aggregation approaches based on linear models. We begin by examining the theoretical foundations of linear AC power flow, two variants of so-called DC power flow, and the LinDistFlow model, along with their underlying assumptions. The discussion covers key system details, including network topology, voltage constraints, and line losses. Simulations are conducted on the KIT Campus Nord network with real demand and solar data. Results show that, in the absence of negative losses, line losses are generally underestimated by linear models. Furthermore, line losses errors tend to accumulate both at the point of common coupling (PCC) and over extended time horizons.

title: Online Inertia Parameter Estimation for Unknown Objects Grasped by a Manipulator Towards Space Applications
authors: Akiyoshi Uchida, Antonine Richard, Kentaro Uno, Miguel Olivares-Mendez, Kazuya Yoshida
abstract: Knowing the inertia parameters of a grasped object is crucial for dynamics-aware manipulation, especially in space robotics with free-floating bases. This work addresses the problem of estimating the inertia parameters of an unknown target object during manipulation. We apply and extend an existing online identification method by incorporating momentum conservation, enabling its use for the floating-base robots. The proposed method is validated through numerical simulations, and the estimated parameters are compared with ground-truth values. Results demonstrate accurate identification in the scenarios, highlighting the method’s applicability to on-orbit servicing and other space missions.

title: A Comprehensive Survey on AI-Enabled Physiotherapy: Advances in Human Pose Estimation, Exercise Assessment, and Personalized Tele-Rehabilitation
authors: Ujjwal Patil, Aditya Acharya, Shruti Mary Varughese, Vineet Iyer, Vrushali Bahurupi
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in physiotherapy represents a paradigm shift toward accessible, scalable, and personalized rehabilitation care. This comprehensive survey systematically reviews the current state-of-the-art in AI-driven physiotherapy systems, focusing on human pose estimation, exercise recognition, movement quality assessment, and tele-rehabilitation platforms. We conducted a systematic literature review of 62 peer-reviewed studies published between 2019-2024, examining datasets, methodologies, evaluation metrics, and clinical validation approaches. Our analysis reveals significant advances in pose estimation accuracy (PCKh scores exceeding 97%), exercise recognition (F1-scores $\boldsymbol{i} 0.91$), and quality assessment ($R^{2} \boldsymbol{i} \mathbf{0. 9 2}$). However, critical gaps persist in clinical validation, dataset diversity, real-world deployment, and explainable AI integration. We propose a novel taxonomy of AI physiotherapy systems categorizing approaches by sensing modality, processing architecture, and clinical application. Additionally, we present a comprehensive framework integrating multi-modal sensing, edge-cloud computing, and clinical decision support. Key research directions identified include federated learning for privacy preservation, multimodal foundation models, and clinically validated assessment frameworks. This survey provides researchers and practitioners with a comprehensive roadmap for developing next-generation AI physiotherapy solutions that bridge the gap between technical innovation and clinical implementation.

title: Optimal Switching State, Sequence and Modulation Technology for a Family of Multi-level Cascaded Noninverting Buck-Boost DC/DC Converter
authors: Zhen Zhu, Manchung Wong, Weng Chi Ao Ieong
abstract: Firstly, A family of multi-level cascaded noninverting buck-boost dc-dc converter topology is proposed to adapt the wide range of input or output voltage. Next, based on the proposed topology, the concepts of "Nearest Two-Point Criterion" and "Nearest Triangle Criterion" is firstly defined to get the optimal states on different operations. And based on the mathematical induction method, the optimal switching states of two-level, three-level and multi-level cascaded noninverting buck-boost converter (ML-CNIBB) are analyzed, respectively. Furthermore, to get this optimal switching sequence, a hybrid modulation strategy is designed to realize the optimal state and sequence by two popular modulation techniques, phase-shifted modulation and phase-disposition modulation. Finally, a power prototype of ML-CNIBB system is constructed and the experiments are carried out to prove the performance of the proposed system.

title: CUTE-Planner: Confidence-aware Uneven Terrain Exploration Planner
authors: Miryeong Park, Dongjin Cho, Sanghyun Kim, Younggun Cho
abstract: Planetary exploration robots must navigate uneven terrain while building reliable maps for space missions. However, most existing methods incorporate traversability constraints but may not handle high uncertainty in elevation estimates near complex features like craters, do not consider exploration strategies for uncertainty reduction, and typically fail to address how elevation uncertainty affects navigation safety and map quality. To address the problems, we propose a framework integrating safe path generation, adaptive confidence updates, and confidence-aware exploration strategies. Using Kalman-based elevation estimation, our approach generates terrain traversability and confidence scores, then incorporates them into Graph-Based exploration Planner (GBP) to prioritize exploration of traversable low-confidence regions. We evaluate our framework through simulated lunar experiments using a novel low-confidence region ratio metric, achieving 69% uncertainty reduction compared to baseline GBP. In terms of mission success rate, our method achieves 100% while baseline GBP achieves 0%, demonstrating improvements in exploration safety and map reliability.

title: Attention Guided Dense U-Net for Enhanced Change Detection in Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Parthasaradhi Ganta, Siddardha S, Sujith B, Surendiran B, Dhivya Bharathi P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Detecting structural change from satellite imagery is important for urban monitoring and disaster response. We propose a novel bi-temporal building change detection model combining dual DenseNet121 encoders with a U-Net decoder enhanced by Convolutional Block Attention Modules (CBAM). Each temporal image is processed independently, and multi-scale features are combined in the decoder, where CBAM enhances spatial and channel-wise attention to localize changes better. The model is trained on the LEVIR-CD+ dataset and evaluated cross-domain on WHU-CD, demonstrating strong generalization without finetuning. The model achieved an accuracy of 98% along with an IoU of 0.68 and F1-score of 81%, where these metrics outperformed the baseline U-Net and ResNet-based models. The DenseNet backbone also allows for a reduction in model complexity by reusing features, which contributes to making the architecture computationally efficient for real-time remote sensing use cases. This work illustrates the effectiveness of attention applied on the decoder-side, as the proposed method is capable of achieving robust cross-domain performance. The proposed method is a practical solution for spatially precise and scalable building change detection.

title: Early Diabetes Risk Assessment using an Integrated Machine Learning Model based on Random Forest and Logistic Regression
authors: Chitra K, T.Kalpana., Dhanushya V, Karthiksanthosh S, Kiruthiga M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Timely intervention occurs as a result of determining diabetes at the earliest opportunity. Thus, helping to minimize longer-term complications associated with diabetes. This study compared and evaluated two different supervised machine learning techniques, Logistic Regression and Random Forest Classifier, using data based on both clinical and nonclinical/lifestyle characteristics to detect diabetes. Our experimental findings show that Random Forest produced the highest accuracy of 96.99%, while Logistic Regression produced 95.91% accuracy. To improve predictive ability, we created an additional Soft Voting Ensemble that combines both techniques, which produced the most accurate prediction, at 97.04%, of the three studied techniques. Our analysis of feature importance identifies three main factors that contribute to significantly higher likelihood of predicting future occurrences of diabetes: 1) HbA1c Levels; 2) Blood Glucose Levels; 3) Age. These results support the notion that developing an ensemble-based approach to assist with early diagnosis and an additional source for decision-making in a healthcare environment is effective.

title: A Novel 3D Line-of-Sight Guidance Law for Path Following of AUV Subject to Ocean Currents
authors: Lazar Ašanin, Luka Martinović, Žarko Zečević
abstract: This paper presents an improved threedimensional line-of-sight (LOS) guidance law for path following of underactuated autonomous underwater vehicles. The proposed approach employs a Luenberger observer to estimate body-frame velocities from position measurements, enabling direct computation of the crab angles. A corrected desired pitch angle formulation ensures convergence of the vertical-track error to zero without requiring zero horizontal crab angle or constant depth assumptions. Simulation results demonstrate improved performance compared to existing adaptive LOS methods.

title: Error Components Of Traveling Wave Fault Location Complex In Branched Distribution Networks
authors: Rustem G. Khuziashev, Ildar A. Minaev, Dmitriy V. Sarychev
abstract: The main sources of error in the multilateral traveling wave fault location algorithm in branched medium-voltage distribution networks are analyzed. The procedure for determining the onset of a transient signal is shown using experimentally recorded oscillograms of emergency transient processes as an example. It is shown that redundant information due to signal recording at many network points allows determining the traveling wave propagation velocity. A model algorithm for wave fault location is used that involves finding the minimum of the objective function. The objective function consists of sums of absolute values of the difference between two values. The number of terms is equal to the number of unique pairs of sensors of the traveling wave fault location complex. The first value is equal to the experimentally determined difference in the signal onset in the sensor relative to the reference sensor. The second value is equal to the similar calculated difference. The minimum of the objective function is sought for by varying the calculated fault location and the traveling wave velocity. The determining effect of the time sampling of transient signals on the error in wave fault location is shown.

title:
Real-Time Indoor CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Forecasting via Occupancy-Aware Long Short-Term Memory Models
authors: Yashika R S, Madhumitha M, K. Venkatasubramanian, S. Shriram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: A two-layer Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) net- work is proposed for forecasting indoor CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration. The model was trained on four weeks of time-series data containing occupancy information and CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> measurements, structured with a 60-step sliding window and normalized via MinMax scaling. The LSTM achieved high predictive accuracy (MAE = 6.59 ppm, RMSE = 20.06 ppm, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9989) across varying occupancy levels, including peak conditions. Reliable occupancy inputs were found critical, as noisy or lagged signals reduced accuracy. Evaluation with a rolling-origin split confirmed robust generalization, and inference latency tests (< 20 ms) demonstrated feasibility for edge deployment in smart ventilation and building energy management applications.

title: Data-Driven Forward-Looking Risk Assessment for Regional Energy Security Planning
authors: Ethan Cantor, Lauren Eckert, Jie Li
abstract: The energy systems are undergoing a major transition while facing elevated cyber and physical risks. This work investigates a data-driven risk assessment for regional energy security planning. The proposed approach is implemented for electricity security evaluation of a region in the Northeast United States, but is adaptable to other energy sectors, such as natural gas and liquid fuel, and other regions. Three major metrics generally used for characterizing any system risks, i.e., threat likelihood, system vulnerabilities, and impact consequences, are comprehensively defined with customized electricity sector measures. Furthermore, considering the ongoing and rapid energy system evolution, as well as the evolving cyber-physical threat profile, another metric, future outlook, is proposed and integrated into the risk assessment, fostering a forward-looking approach. Publicly available data sources, predominantly those available at the county level, are explored to estimate these metrics, facilitating aggregations at the regional level. Assessment results are expected to provide data-informed guidance to infrastructure stakeholders in support of risk mitigation decision-making. Some low-frequency high-impact threats, such as electromagnetic pulses, exhibit higher risk levels than commonly aware threats, such as hurricanes and winter storms, underscoring the need for effective risk mitigation strategies for these black-swan events that are currently less considered by stakeholders.

title: Learning Decentralized Routing Policies via Graph Attention-based Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning in Lunar Delay-Tolerant Networks
authors: Federico Lozano-Cuadra, Beatriz Soret, Marc Sanchez Net, Abhishek Cauligi, Federico Rossi
abstract: We present a fully decentralized routing framework for multi-robot exploration missions operating under the constraints of a Lunar Delay-Tolerant Network (LDTN). In this setting, autonomous rovers must relay collected data to a lander under intermittent connectivity and unknown mobility patterns. We formulate the problem as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Problem (POMDP) and propose a Graph Attention-based Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (GATMARL) policy that performs Centralized Training, Decentralized Execution (CTDE). Our method relies only on local observations and does not require global topology updates or packet replication, unlike classical approaches such as shortest path and controlled flooding-based algorithms. Through Monte Carlo simulations in randomized exploration environments, GAT-MARL provides higher delivery rates, no duplications, and fewer packet losses, and is able to leverage short-term mobility forecasts; offering a scalable solution for future space robotic systems for planetary exploration, as demonstrated by successful generalization to larger rover teams.

title: Explainable Federated Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Lung Disease Detection Using Chest X-Ray Images
authors: Abhay Yeole, Rajesh Dharmik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Chest X-ray imaging serves as a diagnostic method for identifying lung and chest conditions although its analysis can be subjective, laborious and susceptible to differences between observers. Lately artificial intelligence (AI) has demonstrated potential in supporting radiologists by providing automated, reliable and swift image evaluation. This research introduces an AI-driven Chest X-ray Disease Detection System that combines the YOLOv8 learning architecture with an intuitive interface, for clinical assistance. The suggested system autonomously detects pulmonary disorders including Atelectasis, Consolidation, Interstitial Lung Disease, Pleural Effusion, Pneumothorax, Pulmonary Fibrosis and Nodule-Mass while excluding conditions, outside the lungs to guarantee focused lung disease evaluation. Identified abnormalities are pinpointed with bounding box labels, colour-marked for visualization and include severity categorization according to model confidence levels (mild moderate severe). More over the system delivers context- insights, such, as probable causes recommended treatments and preventive guidance effectively connecting raw model results with practical clinical understanding. Besides identification the framework features interactive visualizations enabling healthcare professionals to engage directly with model outcomes. An exclusive report generation component also improves utility by automatically producing organized PDF diagnostic reports that encapsulate findings, severity assessments and labelled X-ray images. These reports enhance documentation while also minimizing the time spent on note-taking and recordkeeping. Combining deep learning algorithms with a user-friendly graphical interface guarantees usability for medical specialists and practitioners in settings, with limited resources [2]. Providing real-time analysis, transparent annotations and organized decision support the system is designed to assist radiologists of substituting them keeping human expertise at the core of the diagnostic workflow. In summary this study emphasizes the promise of AI-powered decision support tools in healthcare. The suggested model illustrates how merging computer vision with expertise can boost diagnostic accuracy support early identification of lung conditions lessen mental strain on medical professionals and ultimately enhance patient health results. Potential future developments might involve linking with hospital information systems broadening to multimodal imaging modalities (like CT scans) and ongoing model updates, with datasets to enhance applicability across different populations [7].

title: Identification and Optimal Output Regulation of Linear Systems Using Integral Reinforcement Learning
authors: Žarko Zečević, Luka Martinović, Božo Krstajić
abstract: This paper addresses the optimal output regulation problem for continuous-time linear systems with unknown dynamics and external disturbances. A data-driven approach based on integral reinforcement learning is employed to identify the system matrices and the optimal state-feedback gain using measured input-state data. The feedforward control gain is subsequently obtained by solving the regulator equations based on the identified model, enabling reference tracking and disturbance rejection. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: LungScan-KD: An Energy-Efficient Distilled Deep Learning Model for Web-based Lung Cancer Diagnosis using CT Imaging
authors: Tanveer Saad Sinan, Habibur Rahaman, Md. Musfiqur Rahman Akib
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Lung cancer remains a leading cause of mortality globally, with early detection substantially improving patient prognosis. However, the computational complexity of deep neural networks presents significant barriers to deployment in resource-constrained clinical environments. This paper presents a rigorous framework that addresses this challenge through knowledge distillation applied to Computed Tomography (CT) imaging. Specifically, we distill a deep Xception teacher network into a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) student architecture, achieving 99.51% classification accuracy on a four-class histological dataset while substantially reducing computational requirements. The framework incorporates hybrid loss function optimization combining Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence, categorical cross-entropy, and Mean Squared Error (MSE) components to maximize knowledge transfer efficiency. Comprehensive evaluation demonstrates superior performance compared to baseline architectures (Inception V3: 99.23%, ResNet-50: 82.18%, EfficientNet: 97.23%), with inference time reduced by 47.3% relative to the teacher model. Practical deployment via Django-based web application enables real-time prediction in both urban clinical and resource-limited remote healthcare settings. This work advances the practical applicability of deep learning for medical image analysis in low-resource regions.

title: Multimodal Explainable AI for Stress Detection in Emotion-Aware Systems
authors: Sai Prajwal H, Sofia Singh, Dipti Theng, Dipali Dakhole
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: We propose a continuous stress-detection pipeline that integrates wavelet-derived heart-rate-variability (HRV) features with behavioural signals in a staged ensemble. The method computes time-resolved HRV spectral measures (VLF/LF/HF) via wavelet decomposition and fuses them with behavioural descriptors, then trains a Random-Forest based ensemble on the combined feature set. Post-hoc SHAP analysis provides dataset-level importance rankings as well as instance-level attributions, allowing clinicians and end users to examine the rationale behind each prediction. On WESAD (N=15; ECG at 700 Hz; sessions ≈35–40 min), the system achieved 83.0% accuracy on a held-out test split. SHAP reliably emphasized LF and HF approximate power as superior predictors, and the wavelet plots showed short-term spectral variations associated with recognized striking stress periods confirming the physiological plausibility of the reports.

title: AI Ethics Framework for Detecting Manipulative AI in Social Media
authors: Trilochan Ojha P, L Srinivasan, Jayapriya P, R Suganya, Selvaraj R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The autonomy of users and educated public conversation are seriously threatened by the proliferation of deceptive content on social media platforms. By developing the "AI Manipulation Detector," a web-based program that can differentiate and reveal persuasive and deceitful language, our project will address this conundrum. Our system uses a hybrid architecture that combines three analytical pillars in a unique way: a revolutionary contextual source analysis module, an adaptive Naive Bayes classifier selected for its interpretability, and a transparent rule-based engine. This integration of text analysis with an author-level "Source Trust Score" is the main contribution’. The first layer of the system is a rule-based engine that looks for verbal cues that point to the possibility of manipulation. This entails recognizing ethically produced manipulative techniques, such as creating a sense of urgency, employing divisive "us versus them" rhetoric, and asserting irrational authority. This gives the analysis a solid and intelligible basis. In order to do contextual source analysis, the second layer communicates with the Reddit API. It determines an author's age and karma to provide a Source Trust Score, which is a crucial component that goes beyond simple text analysis. An adaptive machine learning component makes up the third layer. A Naive Bayes classifier is constructed using a labelled set that can be created with a user feedback system. Model predictions and rule-based scoring are used in the integrated methodology to produce a system that improves its performance over time in an iterative manner

title: Conversational AI Semantic Search for E-commerce using Elasticsearch + RAG: A Scalable Hybrid Framework
authors: Karthik Govardhanan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This work provides a modular hybrid information retrieval architecture that combines Elasticsearch with RAG to send context-aware, accurate, and low-latency replies to user queries. The system has four primary modules: transformer-based reformulation for query comprehension, BM25 and k -NN embeddings for lexical and dense retrieval, transformer models for generative answer synthesis, and a ranking-feedback loop for adaptive optimisation In a simulated retail environment with synthetic product data and queries, the hybrid system outperforms standalone Elasticsearch and RAG models in Precision@5 (0.81), Recall@10 (0.88), and Mean Reciprocal Rank (0.77) with moderate latency (310 ms). Results show that real-time search applications benefit from lexical accuracy and semantic richness. Scaling multimodal queries, deploying efficient tiny language models, and incorporating real-time user input for learning and personalisation are future directions.

title: An Adaptive Cut Selection Method for Fast MILP Solution with Reinforcement Learning
authors: Rouyu Lin, Qian Ma, Ran Zhang, Jixiang Ren, Yilin Zhou, Lei Yan
abstract: Branch and bound is a classic algorithm solving mixed integer linear programming (MILP) problems such as security constrained unit commitment, and cuts are used to accelerate it. Existing researches on the selection of cuts rarely consider the impact of weight allocation of scoring rules, which makes the selected cuts unable to effectively improve the problem- solving performance. Therefore, this paper proposes an adaptive cut selection method with reinforcement learning aimed at selecting better cuts to accelerate the solution of MILP problems. To efficiently characterize MILP problems, a graph convolutional neural network with self-attention mechanism is developed to represent the bipartite graph of MILP problem as a single vector, which, combined with custom cut features, forms a mixed state tensor for sequential agents. The sequential agents learn the characteristics of specific MILP problems to output adaptive score weights for cut evaluation, select and rank cuts, ultimately choosing an effective cut set. A case study illustrates that this method can decrease the average solution time by 18.1% to 85.5% compared to other approaches, which underscore its effectiveness.

title: Stability Analysis of Grid-following and Grid-forming Hybrid System with Synchronized Parameters
authors: Yan Mei, Xia Yuan, XiangYi Chen, Shun Zhou, Rui Yang, Jiaji Huang, Fei Tang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: With the rapid increase in renewable energy penetration, the structure of power systems is gradually transitioning from being dominated by synchronous generators to hybrid systems composed of both grid-forming and grid-following renewable energy sources. The complex dynamic interactions between different generation units exhibit strong nonlinear coupling characteristics, which present new challenges to the transient synchronization stability of the system. To address these issues, this paper employs the phase plane method to analyze the impact of grid-forming virtual synchronous control parameters and grid-following phase-locked loop control parameters on the system’s transient stability. Based on this analysis, a parameter design principle for controllers suitable for the stable operation of hybrid systems is proposed. Finally, simulation results under fault scenarios validate the effectiveness of the proposed model and the correctness of the parameter design method, providing a theoretical foundation for the stable operation of power systems with high renewable energy penetration.

title: Whole-Volume Vision Transformer for Dense Breast Lesion Detection in Digital Breast Tomosynthesis
authors: D.E. Martina Jaincy, P. Shanthakumar, N. Vijayakumar, D. Karthikeswaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Breast cancer detection in women with dense breast tissue is challenging due to tissue overlap that can obscure lesions in conventional mammography. Digital Breast Tomosynthesis (DBT) provides volumetric slice imaging that improves lesion visibility, but manual interpretation of full DBT volumes is time consuming. This paper presents a transformer based framework for whole volume DBT analysis, designed to capture long range contextual information across slices for occult lesion detection in dense breasts. Using the publicly available Breast Cancer Screening DBT Dataset (22,000+ volumes from 5,060 patients), the proposed method incorporates whole volume positional embeddings and self-attention to model inter slice dependencies. Experimental results show up to a 12% increase in sensitivity and a 6% gain in AUC over convolutional neural network (CNN) baselines, while reducing false positives. Attention-based visualizations confirm improved lesion localization in dense tissue. These findings demonstrate that whole volume transformer architectures can enhance radiologist performance in dense breast cancer screening.

title: Diffusion-Driven Synthetic Tabular Data Generation for Enhanced DoS/DDoS Attack Classification
authors: Aravind B, Anirud R.S., Sai Surya Teja N, Bala Subrahmanya Sriranga Navaneeth A, Karthika R, Mohankumar N
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Class imbalance refers to a situation where certain classes in a dataset have significantly fewer samples than others, leading to biased model performance. Class imbalance in network intrusion detection using Tabular Denoising Diffusion Probability Models (TabDDPM) for data augmentation is addressed in this paper. Our approach synthesizes high-fidelity minority-class samples from the CIC-IDS2017 dataset through iterative denoising processes. For the minority classes that have smaller samples, synthetic samples were generated and merged with the original dataset. The augmented training data enables an ANN classifier to achieve near-perfect recall on previously underrepresented attack classes.
