These results establish diffusion models as an effective solution for tabular data imbalance in security domains, with potential applications in fraud detection and medical diagnostics.

title: Development of a Collaborative Transport Platform for Substations
authors: Yong Zhang, Duanwei Ling, Chan Pei, Jie Chen, Jin Qian, Tao Zhu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address the inefficiencies and safety risks associated with the manual transport of safety tools in substations, this paper proposes an engineering solution by integrating mature technologies into a collaborative transport platform. The platform combines a brushless DC motor, an electronic reduction system, RTK positioning, and LiDAR obstacle avoidance. Field deployment and testing at a 500kV substation demonstrated that the average transport time for a 400m/50kg task was reduced from 50 minutes to 16.765 minutes, achieving a 66.5% efficiency improvement. A process capability index (Cpk) of 1.55 confirmed stable system performance under test conditions. This work provides an engineering implementation and field validation case for substation environments, offering a practical reference for mobile robotics applications in similar industrial settings.

title: Research on Adaptive Protection Scheme for Low Voltage Flexible DC Distribution Network under Multiple Operation Modes
authors: Yitian Cai, Wenping Qin, Zhilong Zhu, Ruipeng Lu, Kai Zhao, Zheng Yan
abstract: The magnitude of fault current varies greatly under various operating modes of low-voltage flexible DC distribution network, which makes the existing protection principle invalid. The general protection scheme under different modes has become an urgent need for the current DC system protection research. In this paper, based on the traditional inverse time overcurrent protection idea, an adaptive protection scheme based on current transient quantity is proposed by using the current change rate and its derivative to form a double independent variable, which unifies the problem of internal and external fault identification and the action delay setting of the upper and lower circuit breakers. It ensures the selectivity under serious faults while taking into account the quickness. It does not need data synchronization, and can adaptively identify faults in different operating modes, which meets the protection requirements of low-voltage DC distribution network. The feasibility of the proposed protection scheme is verified in real time digital simulator (RTDS).

title: Research on Power Fluctuation Suppression of Photovoltaic Microgrid Based on Hybrid Energy Storage
authors: Junda Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: With a high proportion of renewable energy connected to the power system, the power fluctuation problem of photovoltaic microgrid is becoming increasingly prominent. It is difficult for a single energy storage technology to meet the requirements of high power density and high energy density at the same time. Hybrid energy storage system (HESS) has become an effective solution to stabilize power fluctuation through the cooperative work of energy and power energy storage equipment. In this paper, a collaborative control strategy of "system-level frequency division correction-equipment-level energy shaping" is proposed. In the system level, Moby Dick optimization algorithm (BWO) is used to optimize the parameters of Variational Modal Decomposition (VMD) to achieve accurate frequency division of power signals, and in the equipment level, an energy shaping (ES) controller is designed based on port-controlled Hamiltonian (PCH) model. Through Matlab/Simulink simulation verification, the power deficit range of the system before suppression was -863.31W to 1182.25W. After adopting the control strategy proposed in this paper, the power deficit was suppressed to - 47.12W to 81.87W, reducing the fluctuation amplitude by 93.78%. Compared to traditional PI control, this strategy lowers DC bus voltage fluctuations by 45.3% to 65.8% and shortens suppression time by 24.7% to 33.3%. It significantly enhances the microgrid's stability and dynamic response performance under sudden power changes.

title: Day ahead prediction of flexible load demand response behavior based on small sample data augmentation
authors: Yiheng Zhang, Ke Wang, Siyu Lu, JiaYang Wang, Yingjun Zhuo
abstract: With the advancement of China's electricity marketization construction, the way load resources participate in the operation of the power grid is gradually becoming market-oriented. However, it is difficult to obtain large-scale and high-quality flexible load electricity price response data in practical scenarios. This article proposes a data enhanced method for predicting electricity price response behavior based on deep learning hybrid networks, which enhances demand response (DR) samples by taking into account electricity price characteristics. Firstly, this article selects historical electricity price response data for training, forming a neural network benchmark model. Next, K-means clustering is used to partition the historical electricity price response data, and the clustering labels are used as conditional features. Combined with Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (CGAN), virtual samples are generated to enhance sample diversity. Based on the enhanced samples, a neural network enhancement model is trained to form a neural network enhancement model. Finally, validation was conducted on the same test set to compare the accuracy of response potential prediction. The method was used to conduct experiments on data from a building in a certain province, and the results showed that the method proposed in this paper can achieve higher accuracy compared to other prediction models.

title: A Technological Shifting Paradigm in Detection and Classification of Cardiopulmonary Disorders
authors: N Shivaanivarsha, G Sai Santhiya, S Pavita, A Akash, K Rajalakshmi, M Neha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Some of the CRDs (chronic obstructive diseases) like tuberculosis (TB), pneumonia, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) continuously causing considerable morbidity and deaths globally. Diagnosis should be done quickly and correctly, usually delayed by a lack of resources and being manual and the need to interpret radiographs. In the proposed work, a new, multi-stage machine learning pipeline is proposed to detect pneumonia in chest X-ray images. It will deal with a major limitation of the few pixel-level annotations through innovative combination of a deep learning model to extract features and an optimized classifier to make the final diagnosis. To perform the role of a highly effective feature extractor with the ability to produce so-called pseudo-masks of pneumonia images, a deep U-Net architecture was pre-trained on a public dataset of tuberculosis abnormality masks. A rich collection of numerical features (including geometric and intensity measures) was derived out of these pseudo-masks. An XGBoost classifier was then trained using these features with its hyperparameters intelligently optimized using a new Whale Optimization and Moth-Flame Optimization (WOA+MFO) hybrid algorithm. A last model that was trained on a class-balanced dataset by applying SMOTE had better performance on an unknown test set with an accuracy of 85.6 percent and an AUC of 92.8 percent. These findings show that this hybrid solution is an effective and robust solution to automated diagnosis of medical images and is superior to the simpler Decision Tree baselines and supports a novel model of using unlabeled data. Making AI-driven diagnostics accessible to a wider audience in the underserved areas in particular, the work helps decrease the presence of healthcare disparity and enhance the rates of respiratory health improvement.

title: A Blockchain-Enabled Deception and Behavioural Monitoring Framework for Ransomware Protection
authors: Shamayita Moitra, Avik Kumar Das
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Ransomware has become one of the most serious and costly types of cybercrime in recent years. It threatens digital infrastructure in critical sectors like banking, healthcare, and government services. Unlike traditional malware that usually takes time to encrypt files and systems, ransomware acts quickly. Ransomware locks down important data and demands extremely high ransoms. Once encryption starts, existing defenses like antivirus software, signature-based intrusion detection, and simple backups leave organizations with almost no options for real-time recovery. Response times to incidents are further delayed by the lack of automated containment and secure forensic logging. This lack of measures extends the attack window and increases the damage. To address these problems, we introduce ChainTrap, a defense framework that combines entropy- and behavior-based detection with stealthy decoy interaction, automated containment, and blockchain-backed secure logging. Unlike previous methods that treat these defenses separately, ChainTrap brings together detection, deception, and response into a system that reacts quickly to stop attacks while generating reliable forensic evidence. This work shows how ChainTrap improves ransomware defense beyond just passive detection to a practical, forensically sound security framework.

title: Fine Tuning Large Language Models for Accurate Fake News Detection
authors: Kannan N, Renuka N, Arun Antony V, Sangamithra G, Tanushree J, Vasikaran S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: In the current digital age, false information spreads quickly via social media and other online channels, making it challenging to tell real news from fake. This study introduces a framework for detecting fake news that uses Llama 3 for explanation-based classification in conjunction with conventional machine learning models. Using TF-IDF vectorization and SMOTE for improved balance and feature representation, we experimented with LSTM, SVM, Naive Bayes, Decision Trees, CNN, and Logistic Regression. Because of its capacity for sequential learning, Decision Tree demonstrated impressive results, and Llama 3 produced explanations in natural language to elucidate predictions. A speech-to-text module was also developed, enabling users to speak news statements that are converted into text and analyzed for authenticity.In addition to identifying false information, the framework offers context-aware reasoning, which enhances usability and trust in practical applications.

title: Application of Multi-Expert Hybrid Inference Architecture in Power Knowledge-Enhanced Multimodal Large Models
authors: Xiya Li, Ying Li, Zhuyi Rao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This paper aims to address the issues of insufficient accuracy, lack of professionalism, and poor interpretability of general-purpose multimodal large models in power-related tasks. A multi-expert hybrid inference architecture is proposed. This approach builds a multimodal base model integrated with a power grid knowledge graph and injects domain-specific knowledge through two-stage strategies: instruction fine-tuning and alignment pretraining. LoRA technology is then used to parallelly fine-tune three specialized expert modules (insulator fault diagnosis, operation ticket compliance verification, and power grid topology analysis). Finally, a deterministic gating network based on task semantic parsing is designed for intelligent task scheduling. Experimental results show that the proposed architecture performs excellently on a self-constructed dataset, with F1 scores of 94.3% and 91.8% for equipment status diagnosis and operation procedure verification tasks, respectively, which is a 15-20% improvement over the general-purpose multimodal model BLIP-2. The gating network achieves an expert selection accuracy of 98.2%, with inference speed 1.8 times faster than the traditional MLP gating. Ablation experiments indicate a significant performance drop in corresponding tasks when specific experts are removed. This study validates the effectiveness of the multi-expert hybrid architecture in power multimodal tasks, providing a reliable solution for the intelligent operation and maintenance of power systems.

title: Analyzing the Impact of Strategic Bidding on the Reserve Capacity via a Bi-Level Model
authors: Yun Xu, Yunxiao Bai, Yunyong Zhang, Jiqun Guo, Kaijun Xie, Peng Wang, Xuelin Wang, Rusheng Zhao
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources necessitates adequate reserve capacity to maintain power balance. However, in market clearing, power companies with flexible resources may submit strategic bids to maximize profits, potentially compromising system reserves. This paper examines the effects of such strategic behavior by modeling the market as a bi-level problem. The upper level represents a strategic company aiming to maximize profit, while the lower level simulates the system operator clearing the market based on submitted offers. To enable duality-based solution methods, we approximate unit commitments with a continuous reserve capacity calculation. Case studies indicate that, in an imperfectly competitive market, more units are incentivized to operate, enhancing system reserves. However, some units go online mainly for profit, ultimately raising electricity costs for consumers. These findings highlight the importance of market design in managing the trade-off between reserve adequacy and economic efficiency in the presence of strategic bidding behavior.

title: A Meteorological Data Correction Method for Transmission Line Ice Shedding Early Warning
authors: Hongfei Wang, Yuzhi Shi, Zepeng Sun, Kunpeng Ji, Bin Zhao, Peng Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Ice shedding galloping of transmission lines is a critical hazard threatening the safe and stable operation of power grids, and its accurate early warning is highly dependent on precise data of the micro-meteorological environment where the lines are located. In practical operation and maintenance, regional weather forecast data are mostly used but fail to reflect the actual meteorological conditions of complex terrains (e.g., mountains and hills) along transmission lines. This paper proposes a meteorological data correction method for icing shedding galloping early warning of transmission lines. By establishing correction models for air temperature vertical lapse rate, wind speed combined with terrain aspect, and humidity with altitude, the accuracy of meteorological data under complex terrains is effectively improved. Case verification results indicate that the corrected data are highly consistent with the on-site micrometeorological monitoring results. Meanwhile, this study finds that "abrupt temperature rise" is the primary inducement of icing shedding. Based on this, a quantitative definition method for "icing shedding days" is proposed, with the average and maximum values of solar radiation intensity as the criteria. Additionally, the physical process by which solar radiation promotes icing shedding through mechanisms such as thermal effect and thermal expansion/contraction is clarified. It provides a practical early warning scheme for icing shedding galloping of in-service transmission lines lacking micro-meteorological monitoring conditions, and has significant application value for improving the power grid's ice prevention and disaster resistance.

title: A Comparative Study of Rule-Based and Machine Learning-Based Methods for Authentication Anomaly Detection
authors: Lejla Muratović, Bećir Isaković
abstract: This research presents a comparative study of rule-based and machine learning-based approaches for detecting anomalous authentication activities. Rule-based detectors are evaluated against an unsupervised anomaly detector trained on normal user behavior, using the LANL dataset expanded with realistic synthetic attacks. Thresholds used by all detectors are calibrated on an evaluation set to meet fixed false-positive budgets. Results are reported using eventlevel and burst-level metrics. The results show that rule-based approaches perform strongly on high-rate attacks, while machine learning approaches are effective for low-rate, stealthy activity.

title:
Design and Implementation of Logarithmic Floating Point Multiplier for MAC
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Vijaya.P, Kullayamma.I
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This paper presents a novel logarithmic floating point multiplier (LFM) for the basic multiplier in the multiply-accumulate (MAC) circuit for the error-tolerant applications. The LFM method converts the general floating point (FP) numbers to logarithmic form processing multiplication to be simulate basic addition operation in the logarithmic design which performs anti logarithmic operation for reverse function of multiplication. By using proposed design we can reduce power & area when compared with the full-precision multipliers (FPM), and also speed has been increased with high accuracy for several DSP and neural network applications. The proposed LFM is synthesized in cadence using Verilog HDL and implemented in FPGA with 45nm technology, around 35-45% area has been reduced and 45% lower power consumed. These designs are well suited for signal & image processing applications to reduce the area and power consumption.

title: Power Grid Resilience Enhancement via Computing Task Migration of Data Centers
authors: Meng Hou, Xi Zhang, Heng Hu
abstract: With the increasing frequency of the occurrence of extreme climate events, the enhancement of power system resilience is attracting world-wide attention. Inter-connected data centers are characterized by tremendous temporal and spatial flexibilities that can effectively support the resilient operation of power systems, particularly in the context of fast development of artificial intelligence where the requirement of computing power is exponentially increasing. Therefore, this paper proposes a resilience-oriented computing power migration framework between inter-connected data centers featuring a dual-phase strategy that integrates preemptive disaster modeling with coordination optimisation mechanisms. Specifically, a dynamic computing task migration strategy is designed to prioritize migrating critical tasks to secure data center. Secondly, coordination across data centers achieves synergistic minimization of grid load curtailment through differentiated electricity procurement and storage-mediated resilience reserves. Finally, simulations demonstrate a 32.7% reduction in critical load shortage and a 28.4% improvement in renewable energy utilization compared to traditional inter-connected multi-energy microgrids schemes, providing an innovative approach for power grid resilience enhancement.

title: Robust and Adaptive Compliant Deep Reinforcement Learning with Vision-force Integration for Embodied Multiple Peg-in-hole Assembly_supp2-3675608.mp4
authors: Huaguang Shi
abstract: Robotic multiple peg-in-hole assembly is a typical contact-rich manipulation task in embodied intelligence, with applications from housework to consumer electronics production. However, this task involves handling strong geometric coupling, in which existing methods lack sufficient compliance and adaptability to pose deviations and environmental uncertainty, thereby increasing the risk of jamming and task failure. To address these issues, this paper proposes a framework based on deep reinforcement learning with vision-force integration. A refined Data-regularized Q module enhances visual feature robustness under target pose uncertainty by using synchronized augmentation for stacked frames with a temporal-consistency regularization. Meanwhile, a hybrid reward mechanism accelerates policy convergence by adaptively weighting external task rewards and intrinsic curiosity rewards to balance exploration and exploitation, providing denser learning signals and preventing premature convergence to local optima. Variable impedance control restores compliance through real-time stiffness adjustment for safe and compliant physical interaction. Experiments on diverse electrical socket configurations demonstrate reliable sim-to-real transfer, robust performance under challenging perturbations, and moderated contact forces, highlighting the practical potential of the proposed approach for autonomous robotic assembly.

title: DenseRefrception: A Densely Connected Perception Framework for Passive Retrieval of Ocean Refractive Environments via GPS Scattered Signals
authors: Han-Jie Ji, Jin-Peng Zhang, Li-Xin Guo, Yi-Wen Wei, Jia-Qi Leng, Yan Zhang, Qing-Liang Li, Xiang-Ming Guo, Yu-Sheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate ocean ducting environments sensing is crucial for radio communication. Conventionally, ducting profile inversion relied heavily on radar sea returns. Though feasible, this active approach suffers from operational drawback, which severely impedes practical deployment. Global positioning system (GPS) has revolutionized ocean monitoring through its inherent global coverage, all-weather capability, and high precision. GPS signals scattered from the sea surface provide unique yet underutilized information on atmospheric environments. Although duct-induced modulations encode key ducting parameters, direct extraction from these signals poses intractable nonlinear inverse problems due to complex signal-refraction interactions. Leveraging the unparalleled capability of deep learning to approximate highly complex, nonlinear mappings, this study presents a Densely Connected Perception Framework for Atmospheric Ducting Retrieval via GPS Scattered Signals (DenseRefrception). The core breakthrough resides in constructing a sophisticated architecture. This framework innovatively integrates Dense Connectivity to extract discriminative features from scattered GPS signal power, while incorporating multi-head attention and fully-connected networks to establish high-dimensional nonlinear mappings. Through systematic training of a unified multi-dimensional duct parameter learning mechanism, it precisely constructs highly nonlinear functional relationships. By transforming passively collected GPS signals into actionable refractivity intelligence, the DenseRefrception represents a shift towards persistent and clandestine monitoring of the oceanic environment, significantly enhancing situational awareness for maritime platforms.

title: Graph Condensation via Homophily Node Refining and Fine-Grained Distribution Matching_supp1-3672916.pdf
authors: Yongqiang Tang
abstract: The remarkable success of GNNs has provoked the challenge of high computational and memory overhead when training with large-scale graphs. As a promising solution, graph condensation is committed to constructing synthetic graphs with significantly smaller size, which are expected to preserve the essential characteristics of the original ones. During this process, a core problem is how to accurately portray and align the data distribution structures between the original graph space and the synthetic graph space. A mainstream idea in existing research is matching the class distributions between the two spaces. Unfortunately, they generally overlook two key issues: 1) heterophilic nodes in original graphs may render the chaotic class distribution patterns; 2) coarse-grained matching of the overall class centroid between original and synthetic spaces is insufficient for data with complex subcategory distributions. In this paper, we propose a novel Graph Condensation method via homophily node Refinement and fine-grained class Distribution matching (GCRD). Given the original large-scale graph, we first distinguish the nodes into advantageous homophilic nodes and detrimental heterophilic nodes, followed by adaptively assigning node weights to refine the generated class distribution patterns of the original graphs. Furthermore, with the refined class distribution patterns, we propose a fine-grained distribution matching objective to more delicately align the local distribution structure of subclasses within each class. The rigorous theoretical analysis confirms the effectiveness of our proposal in precisely learning the class information. Extensive experiments demonstrate our state-of-the-art classification and cross-architecture generalization performance against various baselines.

title: Onboard ROV Detection of Explosives and Narcotics Using Pulsed Fast Neutron Beams
authors: D. Sudac, V. Valkovic, K. Nad, M. Uroic, J. Batur, A. Cufar, Z. Orlic, J. Obhodas
abstract: This paper presents the Concealed Material Identification Module (CMIM), a novel system for real-time, underwater detection and identification of explosives and narcotics. CMIM is a Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV) attachable module that utilizes a pulsed neutron generator for neutron activation analysis. It has been developed as part of the EU Horizon UnderSec project, which focuses on a multi-layered strategy for underwater threat detection. The system determines the elemental composition of potential threats by analysing (1) prompt inelastic scattering gamma rays during pulses, (2) prompt radiative capture gamma rays between pulses, and (3) delayed deactivation gamma rays, either between pulses, after the decrease of the capture gamma-ray signal, or after irradiation. CMIM specifically targets the high nitrogen content characteristic of many explosives and narcotics. The high penetrating power of fast neutrons enables identification of these hazardous materials, even when they are immersed in water, concealed or aged. This research details the CMIM's design and methodology, followed by an analysis of laboratory experiments conducted in a water basin to simulate realistic operational conditions. The results clearly show that the system is capable of detecting and distinguishing various concealed explosives and narcotic substances based on their unique elemental composition. The findings underscore CMIM's significant potential as a tool for rapid in-situ assessment of underwater and hull-borne threats in maritime security operations. In the next phase of the project, the module will be tested in a real operational environment.

title: Integration of Day-Ahead HEMS Planning with MPC for Real-Time Operation of Smart Homes with PV and V2G
authors: Rodrigo Fiorotti, Jussara Farias Fardin, Helder Roberto Oliveira Rocha, Augusto César Rueda-Medina, Carlos Roberto Coutinho, Tiago Zanotelli, Sergio Bruno
abstract: This paper developed a new Home Energy Management System (HEMS) tool that integrates multi-objective day-ahead optimization with a real-time HEMS operation. The approach begins by applying the Long Short-Term Memory neural network to perform day-ahead predictions. With these results, a day-ahead multi-objective HEMS optimization aimed at minimizing energy costs and enhancing user comfort is solved by the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm III, integrating an optimal Load Scheduling (LS) and Electric Vehicle (EV) energy management. Finally, with the LS defined by the NSGA-III, the real-time SH operation using a Model Predictive Controller (MPC) is the main contribution of the paper, which utilizes the flexibility of the EV battery via a bidirectional charger to address prediction errors and minimize energy costs. The integration of multi-objective day-ahead optimization with real time HEMS operation results in a substantial cost reduction of 19.91% compared to the baseline scenario. Furthermore, the annual case study demonstrates an additional 1.78% cost reduction when this integrated approach is compared to the use of day-ahead optimization alone. Therefore, the inclusion of the proposed MPC is justified by its ability to generate further savings without necessitating additional investments or compromising user comfort.

title: Investigation of Electrical Injection Locking in Semiconductor Frequency Combs to Improve Frequency Stability Performance
authors: M. Faye, T. Steshchenko, F. Du-Burck, V. Roncin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: In this paper, we study electrical injection locking for semiconductor frequency combs stabilization. Mode-locked laser diodes (MLLD) are compact frequency combs capable of linking the microwave range to the optical frequency range. Controlling the degrees of freedom of these monolithic devices is essential to give them sufficient stability for their use in embedded metrology systems for high-precision measurements. Electrical injection locking is a simple method which is particularly well suited for the stabilization and the control of the comb repetition frequency (ƒ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rep</sub>). We then show that Adler's approach, used to describe the injection of electronic oscillators, can also be used to model phase-locked multimode optical oscillators. It effectively describes the frequency and noise behavior of these semiconductor lasers when they are injected by an electrical signal close to their repetition frequency. This approach allows us to study an original self-injection locking technique, which consists of detecting the MLLD output signal in a fast photodiode and feeding back the resulting beat at the repetition rate to the MLLD's electrical modulation input. By controlling the self-injection delay, it is then possible to drive the repetition rate ƒ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rep</sub> without external RF source. Analysis of the self-injection configuration shows that the spectral purity of the oscillator can be significantly improved and leads, in any case, to a more efficient optical mode locking and therefore to a more stable frequency comb.

title: Beyond Imaging: A 256-GHz Superheterodyne FMCW Radar System for 3-D Imaging and Micro-Doppler-Based Sound Reconstruction
authors: Ali Ghazizadeh Ghalati, Aditya Varma Muppala, Ali Mosallaei, Morteza Tavakoli Taba, Behzad Yektakhah, Kamal Sarabandi, Ehsan Afshari
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a state-of-the-art frequency-modulated continuous wave (FMCW) imaging radar system capable of high-resolution diffraction-limited 3-D imaging and micro-Doppler-based speech reconstruction. The system employs a phase-locked loop (PLL)-based heterodyne chirp generation unit and transmitter–receiver (TX–RX) chips fabricated in 65-nm CMOS. Operating with a 24-GHz bandwidth centered at 256GHz, the radar achieves a 6.25-mm range resolution and sub-millimeter cross-range resolution in 3-D imaging. Phase stability and long-term coherence are verified through inverse synthetic aperture radar (ISAR) measurements, demonstrating cross-range resolution near the theoretical diffraction limit (i.e., <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\approx \lambda /2$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). Over-the-air audio and speech reconstruction is demonstrated by capturing the micro-Doppler signature of a speaker on an aluminum membrane. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first integrated CMOS imaging radar front-end capable of coherent superheterodyne FMCW operation and micro-Doppler sound reconstruction above 200 GHz.

title: ModSolAgent: Automated Finite Element Code Generation for Abaqus via LLM-Based Agent
authors: Zidi Li, Hongwei Ge, Guozhi Tang, Yuxuan Liu
abstract: Finite element simulation and solution process represents a critical component in engineering analysis. While large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in general-purpose code generation from textual descriptions, their application to generating structured and specialized finite element simulation scripts presents unique challenges. A key challenge is AI-generated hallucination, as these tasks require precise intent analysis, complex planning and reasoning, and strict adherence to logical consistency during execution. This often results in outputs that seem convincing but are ultimately erroneous. To address this challenge, we propose ModSolAgent, an LLM-based agent that generates Python scripts for Abaqus software to perform finite element modeling and solving tasks. ModSolAgent ensures the accuracy and logical coherence of code generation by leveraging a structured reasoning instruction, dynamic retrieval guidance template, and iterative verification generation. Experimental results demonstrate that LLMs augmented by ModSolAgent achieve an 83.3% success rate on real-world finite element simulation tasks, effectively meeting most Abaqus scripting requirements while significantly outperforming baseline models. To further enhance accessibility, we construct the AbqInstruct dataset by distilling knowledge from ModSolAgent to fine-tune the lightweight open-source models. Experiments show that fine-tuning on AbqInstruct leads to substantial performance improvements, with the lightweight model achieving proficiency across most finite element modeling and solving tasks. This work establishes a paradigm for integrating LLMs with specialized engineering software and providing novel insights for other structured, domain-specific code generation scenarios in industrial applications.

title: N -to-1 Knowledge Transfer in Reinforcement Learning via Adaptive Q -function Selection
authors: Marko Ruman, Tatiana V. Guy
abstract: This paper tackles multi-source (N-to-1) knowledge transfer in reinforcement learning (RL), where an agent must adaptively solve a new task by leveraging a library of pre-learned skills. We consider a setting where these skills are represented as multiple Q-functions and corresponding environment models, without any explicit labels to guide the selection. To address this scenario, we introduce a theoretically grounded method that dynamically selects the most suitable Q-function at each learning stage. Instead of relying on noisy, short-term signals, our approach makes a farsighted choice by simulating the long-term performance of each skill while simultaneously evaluating the trustworthiness of its underlyingworld model. This allows the agent to intelligently transition from relying on transferred knowledge to using its own newly acquired policy. The proposed method is evaluated in three scenarios: (1) selection among multiple Q-functions to solve a fixed RL task, (2) adaptation in a dynamically changing environment, and (3) switching from a partially learned Q-function to a newly learned one. In all cases, our method accelerates learning and demonstrates robust adaptation, confirming its effectiveness for scalable multi-source transfer in RL.

title: Deep Learning Methods for Super-Resolution of Biomedical images: A brief Survey, Methodology, Application and Challenge
authors: Yiliu Hang, Qiong Zhang, Zhaopeng Hu, Xiaojing Jiang
abstract: Deep learning–based super-resolution reconstruction has become a rapidly growing research topic in radiology, nuclear medicine, and biomedical imaging, driven by the increasing demand for high-resolution image under hardware, radiation-dose, and acquisition-time constraints. Despite substantial progress and a rapidly expanding body of literature, existing studies remain fragmented, with limited systematic analysis of methodological principles, experimental reproducibility, and unresolved challenges across different imaging modalities. This survey provides a comprehensive and structured review of deep learning methods for biomedical image super-resolution. First, we summarize commonly used datasets, imaging modalities, data characteristics, and preprocessing pipelines, highlighting their influence on model design and evaluation. Second, from a methodological perspective, we categorize deep learning–based super-resolution approaches into convolutional neural networks, adversarial learning frameworks, and diffusion-based models, and analyze their underlying principles and representative architectures. Third, we reproduce and experimentally evaluate several classic super-resolution methods to identify their performance bottle-necks, generalization limitations, and modality-specific failures, and discuss corresponding improvement strategies. Finally, we outline open challenges and future research directions, including data scarcity, cross-modality generalization, uncertainty modeling, and clinical reliability. Overall, this survey aims to bridge the gap between rapidly evolving deep learning techniques and their practical deployment in biomedical image super-resolution, providing researchers and clinicians with a unified reference and actionable insights.

title: Towards Large-Scale and Robust Indoor Positioning: Deep Learning-Augmented VLP/INS Fusion with Efficient Anchor Calibration
authors: Xiaoxiang Cao, Xuan Wang, Tengfei Yu, Zhenghua Zhang, Jingxue Bi, Yue Yu, Yulin Hu
abstract: Visible Light Positioning (VLP) has emerged as a promising indoor localization technology owing to its high accuracy, low power consumption, and lighting compatibility. The Received Signal Strength (RSS)-based multi-anchor VLP pre-serves these advantages while having drawn considerable research attention due to its simple implementation and high reliability. However, its large-scale deployment encounters challenges at every stage: inefficient anchor calibration, limited model-based ranging performance, and robustness reduction from undetected gross errors. To address these issues, we propose a VLP and inertial navigation system fusion framework comprising an anchor position estimation module, a distance estimation module, and a fusion positioning module. For anchor calibration, a LiDAR-inertial odometry-based calibration scheme enhanced by a twolayer optimization strategy is introduced, which provides prior knowledge of anchor positions for the whole system. To improve the performance of the RSS-based ranging method, a hybrid Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise (CEEMDAN)-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network with an embedded distance quality assessor is developed, achieving over 50% higher accuracy than model-based baselines. It delivers precise distance estimates and their validity labels for measurement updates in subsequent fusion positioning. Additionally, a two-stage error detection mechanism filters low quality observations by combining network-generated usability labels with prior-state estimates. The system consistently attains decimeter-level positioning across various trajectories, meeting the needs of diverse Internet of Things applications.

title: Usability, Technicality, Interoperability (UTI), a Comprehensive Assessment Framework for Digital Platforms
authors: Hadise Rojhan, Francesco Bellotti, Matteo Fresta, Luca Lazzaroni, Riccardo Berta
abstract: Cross-organizational digital platforms are being increasingly used in various domains to connect multiple systems and stakeholders for data sharing and joint operations. However, state-of-the-art platform assessment frameworks have gaps in terms of completeness and depth of analysis. We propose the Usability, Technicality, Interoperability (UTI) framework, which integrates such dimensions into a single, comprehensive model. Each dimension is assessed through a bouquet of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), combining qualitative and quantitative metrics (13 in total). Comparison with expert-defined numerical thresholds allows identifying strengths and weaknesses of the analyzed platform, which is key for choice, utilization and maintenance. To support traceability and reuse of results, UTI implements a lightweight RESTful system architecture able to receive KPIs from heterogeneous sources, store them in a non-relational database and answer queries. We applied UTI to 4 leading-edge, open-source digital platforms (Measurify, Arduino Cloud, ThingsBoard, NextCloud). The assessment (with 30 end-users) involved usability testing, user questionnaires, automated tools, and developer feedback. Results show that UTI is able to spot capabilities and limitations of a wide range of digital platforms, both within and beyond the IoT context, demonstrating its adaptability across domains and use cases. Compared to 2 state-of-the-art frameworks, UTI is able to achieve a significant advancement, particularly across the usability and technicality dimensions, which it covers with more numerous and focused KPIs. Overall, the experimental results confirm UTI’s effectiveness in enabling holistic assessment, supporting informed adoption and development decisions, and guiding stakeholders in ensuring reliability, functionality, and continuous improvement.

title: A DBT-GAN Based on Multi-Scale Spatio-Temporal Features for Virtual Reality Motion Sickness Prediction
authors: Lingyun Wang, Ke Su, Liang Tian
abstract: Existing EEG-based virtual reality motion sickness (VRMS) prediction methods often struggle to capture comprehensive spatio-temporal dependencies and maintain cross-individual generalization. This study proposes a dual-branch Transformer–GAN（DBT-GAN） framework that integrates local CNN–LSTM representations with global Transformer dependencies and introduces a tri-attentive mechanism across temporal, spatial, and frequency dimensions. The GAN generator alleviates data scarcity through realistic EEG synthesis, while the discriminator improves data integrity via anomaly detection, forming a closed-loop quality control system. Evaluations on public and cross-domain datasets confirm that the proposed model achieves state-of-the-art predictive accuracy, superior generalization, and enhanced robustness under data imbalance. Furthermore, the model’s learned feature patterns exhibit strong consistency with questionnaire-based susceptibility assessments, demonstrating both predictive reliability and physiological interpretability. These findings highlight the framework’s potential for practical applications in real-time VR motion-sickness monitoring and personalized intervention.

title: STABLE-PCCT: A Spectral Transformer Architecture with Bayesian Learning and Edge Preservation for Photon-Counting CT Image Reconstruction
authors: Bahareh Morovati, Shuo Han, Changsheng Fang, Li Zhou, Dayang Wang, Shuyi Fan, Yu Shi, Hengyong Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: Photon-counting computed tomography (PCCT) provides energy-resolved imaging with high spectral resolution, enabling advanced material decomposition and tissue characterization. However, photon starvation and spectral distortion, especially in low-dose settings, introduce severe noise and cross-channel artifacts, significantly degrading image quality. To address these challenges, we propose a Spectral Transformer Architecture with Bayesian Learning and Edge Preservation for PCCT (STABLE-PCCT). It is a novel deep learning framework that integrates depthwise separable convolutions (DSConv), squeeze-and-excitation (SE) blocks, and a residual Transformer module as a learned prior for Bayesian reconstruction. Each spectral channel is independently processed through lightweight convolutional encoders and SE modules to extract fine spatial features and enhance channel-specific saliency. The resulting feature representations are fused and refined using a Transformer block by modeling global intra-channel dependencies and contextual relationships. A fully connected layer path generates a high-fidelity spectral output. Additionally, anisotropic diffusion filtering (ADF) is applied in the image domain to suppress noise while preserving edges and texture details. Experimental results on simulated and real extremity MARS PCCT datasets demonstrate that STABLE-PCCT outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines in preserving anatomical structures, reducing noise, and improving spectral fidelity. The proposed framework offers an efficient and scalable solution for denoising and reconstruction in low-dose PCCT and other spectral imaging applications.

title: Enhancing Magnetic Localization Accuracy via Directional Noise Analysis and Multi-Layer Adaptive Filtering
authors: Botao Wang, Yuanhao Xu, Kaichao Yang, Jiacheng Zhang, Rentai Liu
abstract: Magnetic localization provides a non-contact approach for structural deformation monitoring, but its accuracy is highly susceptible to environmental magnetic noise. This study investigates the directional sensitivity of magnetic localization to noise and proposes a multi-layer adaptive denoising framework to enhance tracking accuracy under complex conditions. Three-factor full-factorial analysis indicates that magnetic moment orientation is the dominant factor affecting trajectory inversion error, with F-values exceeding 275 in all three coordinate directions (p<0.001), where the F-value denotes the variance ratio in ANOVA used to quantify the relative influence of each factor, while sensor plane orientation and motion direction exhibit secondary effects and interactions. The effectiveness of the proposed denoising method was validated through structural deformation experiments. For unidirectional deformation, it achieved an average noise reduction ratio of approximately 65% across 75 sensor channels. Under the same deformation condition, the root mean square error (RMSE) is reduced from about 0.33 mm to 0.14 mm in the horizontal direction and from 0.35 mm to 0.18 mm in the vertical direction, while trajectory smoothness is markedly enhanced, as reflected by a significant reduction in the Jerk metric, which quantifies high-frequency trajectory fluctuations. Comparable performance improvements are observed under discontinuous and cyclic deformation scenarios, with RMSE reductions on the order of 40-50%. Numerical simulations further clarify the influence of sampling rate and deformation frequency on denoising effectiveness, while experiments with ferromagnetic interference highlight the method’s limitations in correcting low-frequency systematic distortions.

title: Modeling Continuous Weak Temporal Trends for Video-based Micro-Expression Recognition
authors: Qiuyan Zheng, Zhiming Zhou, Yue Zhao, Baoqing Zhu, Qiaoling Han
abstract: As spontaneous facial movements, micro-expressions (MEs) are subtle, uncontrollable, which can more accurately reveal genuine human emotions. Due to the short duration and low intensity of MEs, micro-expression recognition (MER) faces the challenge of modeling facial dynamics over time. Most existing methods focus on discrete key-frame sampling or local temporal modeling, which may disrupt the temporal structure and overlook the continuous and weak nature of ME dynamics. To this end, we propose a video-based MER framework that explicitly models continuous weak temporal trends in ME videos. A multi-timescale feature extraction module is first designed to preserve and capture weak temporal patterns across low-intensity ME frames. These features are then aggregated via hierarchical temporal modeling to progressively encode continuous temporal dependencies without mutual interference. Finally, a multi-head self-attention mechanism models the relationship between spatial and temporal continuous patterns. Compared to state-of-the-art video-based MER methods across three public databases, our method achieves superior performance with UF1/UAR scores of 0.8622/0.8614. Additionally, without spotting apex frames manually, our method attains performance comparable to state-of-theart apex-frame-based MER methods. These results demonstrate that modeling continuous emotion dynamics over time benefits MER.

title: TeDri:Teacher-Driven Region Knowledge Distillation
authors: Zherui Zhang, Changwei Wang, Rongtao Xu, Wenhao Xu, Xingtian Pei, Shibiao Xu, Wenbo Xu, Li Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Knowledge distillation as a practical tool to enhance the performance of small-capacity student networks on downstream tasks comes at the cost of a lengthy distillation process due to the online inference of teacher networks, especially when there is a large capacity gap between them. Therefore, in this paper, we propose a fast distillation framework called TeDri based on region images by offline saving relevant regional information and its teacher guidance. Specifically, first, to alleviate the lack of diversity caused by the fixed augmentation path in region images, we propose Teacher-driven MixUp strategies with mild intensity and advocate binding the mixing factor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lambda$</tex-math></inline-formula> with teacher guidance confidence, where more confident category representations dominate the MixUp process. Furthermore, recognizing the need to evaluate these randomly cropped regions, and we propose region contrastive learning, encourage the student network to mimic the region partitioning behavior of the teacher, promoting a comprehensive understanding of global semantic content from multiple local perspectives. Finally, we introduce region mutual learning, employing spatial constraints among regions to require the student network towards consistent content interpretation across localized regions. Experiments on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet-1 K validate the effectiveness of the proposed TeDri, achieving competitive performance while significantly reducing training time.

title: Enhancing Cloud Data Confidentiality and Fault Tolerance Through Secret Sharing Schemes
authors: C. Sushama, K. Koushik, Ganipudi Rohith, Keerthipati Sai Yaswin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This paper presents a secure and reliable framework for cloud-based data outsourcing that addresses confidentiality risks and the lack of trust in external service providers. The proposed approach combines encryption with a secret sharing mechanism, where each data file is first encrypted and then split into multiple independent shares. These shares are stored across three separate cloud servers, ensuring that no single server can reconstruct the original data. This distributed storage strategy removes single points of failure and strengthens protection against unauthorized access. The framework is cloud-compatible, fault-tolerant, and well suited for safeguarding sensitive outsourced data. Though, the current framework does not support the secure key-word search and the partial data retrieval because of the inherent limitations in secret sharing and are denoted as future work.

title: Polyimide-Based Microstrip Antenna for Real-time Arterial Pulsation Detection and Blood Pressure Monitoring
authors: R.D.S.Terese Sheeba, D.Judson, J.Merin Joshiba
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Blood pressure elevation is a common risk factor for arrhythmias, stroke and heart failure. The traditional method may cause discomfort to users and does not provide data about continuous variations in BP during daily activity. A flexible polyimide microstrip antenna for the non-invasive detection of arterial pulsations at 2.4 GHz is proposed in this study. The antenna design is flexible and skin-compatible for wearable use, with a dual-hole structure and partial ground plane optimized for stable radiation and low return loss. ANSYS HFSS simulations were used to investigate the interaction of electromagnetic waves with a multilayer human tissue model, including skin, fat, muscle, and arteries. The findings indicate a direct correlation between changes in blood pressure and the measurable changes in return loss and resonant frequency caused by arterial expansion and contraction. The optimized antenna had a return loss, which means that it was very sensitive and impedance was well matched. The simulation findings were confirmed by experiments with a phantom of the wrist and a human subject, which demonstrated the existence of S11 variations that were detected and associated with the cardiac cycle. The present research shows that real-time, non-invasive, and continuous BP monitoring can be achieved with the help of the antenna, and it might be integrated into wearable IoT medical devices. Further development can be directed towards miniaturization, multi-frequency sensing, and machine learning-based automatic blood pressure monitoring. Altogether, this study offers an easy, convenient, and effective method of constant cardiovascular monitoring.

title: Low-Power Comparator Using Time-Variant Biasing for Σ∆ ADCs
authors: Mohammad Rizwan, Vivek Raghuwanshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This work introduces a two-stage dynamic comparator optimized for fast operation with low power consumption, suitable for high-resolution Σ∆ ADCs. The first stage employs a dynamic preamplifier with time-variant biasing and embedded StrongARM-inspired positive feedback to enhance output swing while minimizing static power. The regenerative latch stage eliminates the conventional tail current source and integrates auxiliary input devices to accelerate regeneration. Simulation results demonstrate a clock-to-Q delay of 42.46 ps at V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">cm</inf> = 0.3 V and V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">id</inf> = 1 mV, with a power consumption of 1.37 µW to 0.43 µW in the 0-30 mV Vcm range. The input-referred noise is 4.643 nV. Due to these features, the proposed comparator delivers an effective solution for operating at high speeds with fine resolution in Σ∆ ADC systems.

title: Open-Circuit Fault Detection in Single-Phase Inverter Using Wavelet-Based Multi-Resolution Analysis
authors: Ravikrishna S, Jeyashree A, Kamali S, Narmada K S, Sneha V
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This paper presents an efficient and cost-effective method for detecting open-circuit faults (OCFs) in single-phase H-bridge inverters. Such inverters are widely used in residential, renewable energy, and industrial applications for DC–AC power conversion. Since open-circuit faults in semiconductor switches account for nearly 38% of inverter-related failures, the proposed technique provides a practical and reliable diagnostic solution. The method employs a single Hall-effect current sensor along with Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT)-based feature extraction and Random Forest (RF) classification. The measured current signal is processed using a five-level Daubechies-4 (db4) wavelet decomposition to capture time–frequency characteristics. Statistical features extracted from the wavelet coefficients are used for accurate fault classification. Simulation results demonstrate a classification accuracy of 92%, while an Arduino-based hardware prototype achieves an accuracy of 87%. The integration of db4 wavelet analysis, Random Forest classification, and single-sensor measurement ensures high diagnostic accuracy, low computational complexity, and suitability for real-time operation.

title: Research on Key Standards for Collaborative Interaction Between Buildings and Electric Vehicles
authors: Jiong Zhu, Difan Qiu, Yaohua Hu, Chengsi Xu, Yutao Qiu
abstract: To support the energy system transformation under the "dual carbon" goals, this paper focuses on the key standard research of building and electric vehicle synergy. By analyzing the synergy scenarios of building loads, renewable energy, energy storage systems, and electric vehicles, it reveals the key paths to energy efficiency. Secondly, this paper systematically reviews the policy frameworks and technical pilot progress in the vehicle to building field in China, the United States, and Europe. It further analyzes the existing building-electric vehicle synergy standard systems both domestically and internationally, pointing out the lack of standards in China regarding communication protocols, safety regulations, energy management, and data security. Finally, it proposes recommendations for improving the core standards for building-electric vehicle synergy in China.

title: Integrated Diagnostic and Predictive System for Coloboma Using Deep Learning
authors: Sophia P, Keerthanaa J, Abirami D, Divya Sri Dharshini B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Coloboma is a congenital eye condition that can lead to partial or severe vision problems, depending on which eye structures are affected. Traditional diagnostic on which eye structures are affected. Traditional diagnostic methods like fundus photography and optical coherence tomography are effective but need skilled operators and often take a lot time. With the growth of artificial intelligence and deep learning, automated diagnostic systems have become reliable tools to help doctors. This paper presents a combined diagnostic and predictive system for detecting coloboma using deep learning. The approach uses the InceptionV3 model, which was fine-tuned on a dataset of fundus images that included 902 images of coloboma and 377 normal images for training, and 205 coloboma images and 95 normal images for testing. We applied data augmentation techniques to correct the imbalance and improve how well the system generalizes. The system reached about 90% accuracy, 92% sensitivity and 91% specificity. The trained model was turned into a web application. This system shows promise in supporting in ophthalmologists by offering reliable, understandable, and easy-to-use diagnostic help.

title: Advanced Evaluation of Manuscript using Deep Intelligent Learning Model
authors: K. Koteswara Rao, D. Komali, Kondragunta Rama Krishnaiah
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Presently, many researchers, scholars, and students are seeking to publish their manuscripts in high-quality, peer-reviewed journals. Currently, the evaluation of manuscripts and books has become more complex due to a manual process that takes longer to analyze them. In this work, an Advanced Deep Intelligent Learning Model (DILM) is used to evaluate an automated manuscript. The DILM introduced the Deep Discourse Coherence Analyzer (DDCA), an advanced grammatical analysis tool for quality measurement of manuscripts. The proposed DILM first segments the input data using document structure segmentation (DSS), which divides the document into section-wise segments. The EquNormalization is a pre-processing technique that removes missing values in text and equations, yielding more effective text. A feature extraction technique, such as contextual embeddings, is used to capture significant features from the meaning and context of the given input document. Finally, the multi-criteria decision layer is introduced to classify the document based on the manuscript's quality. In this work, the main aim is to show whether the manuscript is accepted or not based on the quality score. The results show that the proposed approach obtains high performance.

title: Bridging the Basilisk Astrodynamics Framework with ROS 2 for Modular Spacecraft Simulation and Hardware Integration
authors: E. Krantz, N. N. Chan, G. Tibert, H. Mao, C. Fuglesang
abstract: Integrating high-fidelity spacecraft simulators with modular robotics frameworks remains a challenge for autonomy development. This paper presents a lightweight, open-source communication bridge between the Basilisk astro-dynamics simulator and the Robot Operating System 2 (ROS 2), enabling real-time, bidirectional data exchange for spacecraft control. The bridge requires no changes to Basilisk’s core and integrates seamlessly with ROS 2 nodes. We demonstrate its use in a leader-follower formation flying scenario using nonlinear model predictive control, deployed identically in both simulation and on the ATMOS planar microgravity testbed. This setup supports rapid development, hardware-in-the-loop testing, and seamless transition from simulation to hardware. The bridge offers a flexible and scalable platform for modular spacecraft autonomy and reproducible research workflows.

title: Enhanced AI-Powered Smart Precision Agriculture Industries 5.0 Using Deep Feature Engineering
authors: Murugesan J, Iyapparaja M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Precision agriculture aims to optimize crop yield and resource utilization through data-driven insights. However, high-dimensional datasets often hinder accurate predictions due to improper feature selection, leading to overfitting, reduced precision and recall rates, and an increased false positive rate. This essay offers a fresh method for address these challenges in the context of agricultural supply chain and production data augmented with seasonal scores. The method begins with Z-score normalization to pre-process the dataset, ensuring consistent scaling across features. Subsequently, Seasonal Support Impact Rate (SSIR) and On-Demand Supply Chain Impact Rate (ODSCIR) are estimated using a Decisive Vector Tree, providing a quantitative measure of feature importance based on seasonal and demand factors. To mitigate the curse of dimensionality, a Spider Swarm Particle Swarm Optimization (SSPSO) algorithm is employed for efficient feature selection, prioritizing features with high seasonal support. Finally, a Deep Scaled Convnet Hyper Capsuled GAN network (DSCHS-GAN) is leveraged to forecast product demand, providing farmers with cultivation recommendations designed to maximize yield. Experimental results show that, in comparison to conventional methods, the suggested system greatly enhances prediction performance, attaining greater precision, recall, F1-measure, and total detection accuracy.

title: Reconfigurable Row-Decoder Design for Adaptive Latency and Energy Optimization in Dram Systems
authors: Bojja Manju Veera Prasad, G Rajender, RadhaKrishna Karne, Niranjan Reddy Kallem, A Venkatalakshmi, A Padmapriya
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Dynamic Random-Access Memory (DRAM) continues to be a critical parameter in high-performance computing due to its latency. This work presents a novel, cost-effective DRAM architecture that enables dynamic reconfiguration of the row decoder to flexibly switch between normal and low-latency operating modes. In the proposed design, two neighboring DRAM cells are logically coupled to form a complementary pair used by the differential-sensing principle, reducing both row-to-column delay (tRCD) and row access strobe (tRAS) without altering the core array or sense-amplifier circuitry. The reconfigurable first-stage decoder is located outside the critical array region, ensuring negligible area overhead and preserving process reliability. FPGA-based simulation results demonstrate reductions of 25.7 % in tRCD and 23.2 % in tRAS over a standard LPDDR4 baseline, provides up to 8.5 % system-level performance improvement across SPEC2006 benchmarks. The proposed scheme further lowers refresh latency and command-bus occupancy, improving energy efficiency while maintaining full JEDEC compliance. Overall, the architecture delivers a robust, flexible, and economically viable solution for next-generation DRAM systems requiring reduced latency and enhanced throughput.

title: Spectral Graph Analysis Approaches for Quantifying and Enhancing Robustness in Large-Scale Communication and IoT Networks
authors: S. Maflin Shaby, Monali G. Dhote, Nurilla Mahamatov, Rajeshwari M, Yadavalli S S Sriramam, R S Sabeenian
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The rapid growth of large-scale IoT networks introduces significant challenges in maintaining robustness, connectivity, and efficient communication. Existing approaches, including genetic algorithms, edge swap mechanisms, and neural network models, often suffer from high computational cost, limited scalability, or insufficient resilience under node and link failures. This study proposes a Spectral Graph Analysis (SGA)–based framework to quantify and enhance network robustness by leveraging spectral metrics such as algebraic connectivity, spectral radius, and eigenvector centrality. The proposed method identifies critical nodes and optimizes the network topology, reducing average delay to 16.9 ms, increasing throughput to 93.6 Mbps, and improving packet delivery ratio to 98.6%, resulting in an overall robustness improvement of 26% compared to baseline configurations. The framework is implemented in Python using NetworkX and NumPy on a publicly available IoT dataset from Kaggle. Results demonstrate that SGA provides a scalable, resilient, and mathematically grounded approach, offering a significant improvement in operational performance and robustness for large-scale IoT networks.

title: Research on Multi-Energy Storage Planning Model for Provincial Power Grids Based on Wavelet Packet Decomposition
authors: Zhanxing Xu, Na Zhong, Qiang Liu, Lingjun Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This study proposes a novel multi-energy storage planning model for provincial power systems with high renewable penetration. Utilizing wavelet packet decomposition, the method allocates power-type and energy-type storage to manage high- and low-frequency net load fluctuations, respectively. An optimization model minimizes lifecycle costs while incorporating multiple operational constraints. A case study of the certain province demonstrates that the approach enables rational capacity configuration, enhances renewable integration, and reduces reliance on coal-powered backup, offering a technically and economically viable solution for regional grid planning.

title: An Interactive Holographic System for Real-Time 3D Architectural Visualization Using SolidWorks and Gesture Control
authors: Umang Duwani, Janya Mali, Shreyas Mahendra, Swedle Machado, Priyanka Gonsalves, Stevina Correia
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Holography enables the creation of three- dimensional images through light interference, offering immersive visual experiences without the need for specialized viewing equipment. While holograms have found widespread use in entertainment and advertising, their potential in the construction and architectural sectors remains largely untapped. This study introduces an innovative, mobile-free workflow that utilizes Blender software in conjunction with holographic fan projectors to transform conventional 2D architectural blueprints into dynamic, interactive 3D holograms. In contrast to existing approaches that rely on static visuals or pre- rendered models, the proposed system supports real-time, gesture-based interaction, allowing architects and stakeholders to intuitively explore and manipulate designs during review sessions. The integration of hand-tracking technology and on-the-fly 2D-to-3D conversion significantly enhances spatial comprehension and streamlines the decision-making process in architectural planning. The implementation of the system demonstrates seamless coordination between hardware and software components, while results from initial evaluations indicate increased user engagement, faster design iteration, and improved collaborative outcomes.The system performs real-time 2D-to-3D conversion and gesture-based rendering with an average latency of 92 milliseconds, representing a forty-five percent reduction compared to conventional interactive 3D visualization setups.

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Personalized Fashion: Body Shape Recognition, Seasonal Color Palette Matching, and Contextual Outfit Suggestions
authors: Harsh Koladkar, Rutuja More, Anshuta Jagtap, Mustqeem Masuldar, Shafaque Fatma Syed
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: As the world continues to advance in technology, strides have also been made in the custom-tailored fashion domain. This paper presents a smart fashion recommendation system that integrates body shape detection, seasonal color analysis, and event-based styling to offer personalized clothing suggestions. Previous models, which addressed these factors separately, failed to provide comprehensive recommendations. Our web-based solution unifies all the factors using user-uploaded data, such as images or body measurements, as well as contextual event data to produce dynamic and personalized styling recommendations. The platform is built with a React.js frontend, Python-Django backend, and MongoDB database. It employs machine learning models such as Random Forest and hybrid filtering to classify body shapes, identify seasonal color palettes, and predict outfits. Evaluation results show up to 89% accuracy in classification, a 40% decrease in outfit selection time, and a 78% user satisfaction rate, demonstrating the framework’s ability to enhance personalization, user decision-making, and engagement in digital fashion applications.

title: An Intelligent Troubleshooting Support System for Incident Resolution in Telecommunication Networks
authors: Anastasiia Dmytrus, Michal Greguš, Mykola Brych, Marian Seliuchenko
abstract: Modern telecommunication networks are increasingly complex, distributed, and knowledge-intensive, making manual problem resolution time-consuming and inefficient. Engineers often rely on fragmented documentation and heterogeneous information sources, leading to prolonged resolution times and limited reuse of operational knowledge. This paper presents a demonstrative study of an Intelligent Troubleshooting Support System (ITSS) designed to improve problem-solving efficiency in distributed telecommunication environments. The system integrates a structured knowledge base with Retrieval-Augmented Generation architecture and Large Language Models to enable semantic search, guided root cause analysis, and automated documentation. A proof-ofconcept implementation was developed and evaluated in a controlled simulator-based environment using predefined network fault scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that the ITSS significantly reduces time spent on knowledgeintensive troubleshooting stages. The findings indicate that ITSS substantially enhances operational efficiency while preserving human control over technical decision-making.

title: Integrating Security Early A Shift-Left Model for DevSecOps in Modern Software Pipelines
authors: Avdhesh Kumar Bhardwaj
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The hustle in software development nowadays forces everyone to prioritize the rush of getting things done and the ongoing incorporation of DevOps practices. Because of this, security in the software development life cycle gets pushed to the end. This way of doing things means security holes can be found only in the most expensive, risky older systems. This is where the shift left approach comes in. It tries to get security practiced in all of the development life cycle practices. This means getting security in all areas of development: design, code, test, and deploy. This way shift left security is practiced, holes in security can be found and fixed before they become problematic. This paper provides some of the first steps in using DevSecOps practices and goes into continuous security automation, the early detection of holes in security, and the shared responsibility model from security, development, and operations. This approach is focused on modern CI/CD frameworks, contact security from within the process and get more secure quicker.

title: Quantum-Inspired Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization under Non-Stationary Market Conditions
authors: Praveen Kumar Mannepalli, Parcha Kalyani, Shital Shende, Alka Jaswal, N. Srinivasa Rajneesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This paper had explored a quantum-inspired reinforcement learning (QIRL) approach for portfolio optimization in nonstationary market conditions, where traditional strategies often fail to adapt effectively. A synthetic market environment with regime shifts and volatility clustering had been simulated to test adaptability. The proposed QIRL model had integrated quantum-inspired layers with reinforcement learning to enhance exploration and robustness. Experimental results had shown that QIRL achieved higher cumulative returns, reduced maximum drawdowns, and more stable Sharpe ratios compared to PPO, DQN, and Markowitz baselines. Ablation studies had confirmed the critical role of quantum components. While results are promising, further validation with real financial data is still required. Overall, QIRL had offered a potential pathway towards more resilient decision-making in dynamic financial environments.

title: Development of Multi-Layered Wheels for Lunar Rover
authors: Taichi Haraguchi, Kimitaka Watanabe, Kazuto Tanaka, Masataka Kawaguchi
abstract: To enhance the running performance of lunar rovers on regolith, we have proposed the application of multi-layered wheel designs in our studies. The design builds upon a previously developed three-blade wheel that demonstrated strong running performance but suffered from vertical vibrations, due to cyclic variation between the wheel axle and the ground.In this study, we design and construct wheels by vertically layering multiple sets of blades, with each set phase-shifted relative to the others. Experiments conducted in a running test field using lunar soil simulant reveal that the proposed design maintains high running performance while significantly reducing vertical vibrations compared to one-layered wheels.

title: Screening and Coordinated Optimization of Key Control Parameters for Wind Power Grid-Connected Transient Voltage Stability Based on Sobol Method
authors: Zuohong Li, Zirui Huang, Zhaobin Du, Sicheng Shan
abstract: With the increasing penetration of wind power, transient voltage stability has become a critical challenge for power system operation. This paper proposes a Sobol-based global sensitivity analysis method for screening and coordinated optimization of key control parameters in doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG) systems, addressing the high-dimensional complexity of the control parameter space. First, Sobol sensitivity indices are calculated to quantify the influence of each control parameter on transient voltage stability, enabling the identification of key parameters and effectively reducing the complexity of subsequent optimization. Then, a parameter stability domain is constructed to perform coordinated optimization of the selected key parameters, balancing voltage stability margin and dynamic performance. Finally, simulation studies conducted on the IEEE 9-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method can significantly enhance transient voltage stability, validating its effectiveness and applicability in wind power integrated systems.

title: AI-Enabled Autonomous Data Preprocessing: A Scalable Architecture for Intelligent Machine Learning Pipeline Management
authors: Amit Kumar Padhy, Nandagopal Seshagiri, Vinay Soni, Arun Kumar Elengovan, Gajendra Babu Thokala, Madhushree Kumari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: An artificial intelligence-based pre-processing system, developed for the machine learning domain, is used to predict what data analysis procedure will provide a higher quality result when using machine learning or deep learning algorithms. In addition to utilizing state-of-the-art deep learning architectures to fully automate preprocessing steps such as missing value imputation, outlier detection, dimensionality reduction and feature selection, this study utilizes the following: a) Reinforcement Learning-Based Feature Selection b) Variational Auto-Encoders (VAE), c) Adversarial De-Biasing Networks d) Classical Scoring Techniques. Benchmarks indicate that an AI-first approach to preprocessing outperforms traditional preprocessing methods on three key metrics: speed, equity, and scalability. In addition, results show a marked improvement in the performance of the model at very low computational overhead. This study illustrates the need for artificial intelligence to be integrated into preprocessing workflows, with the ultimate goal of creating robust, fair and highly-performing machine learning models.

title: UPI Scam Transaction Detector using Machine Learning
authors: S.Sarumathi, Dhiviyadharsan A, Gowrinath V
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The UPI Scam Transaction Detector is the identification fraudster neighboring Unified Payments Interface (UPI) transactions using machine learning techniques getting to the next level. The system employs three predictive models: CatBoost with PCA, XGBoost with PCA, and an Ensemble Learning approach that integrates both models to present accurate, robust, and scalable scam detection. The process is initiated with UPI transactional datasets pre-processing, including the reduction of dimensionality through Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and afterward, the training and assessing of the individual and ensemble models. Stream-lit based interactive web application is the place where the trained models are deployed, and users can predict a single transaction or process a batch of predictions with the help of CSV files. Users can visualize their understanding with the help of various tools such as probability distributions, model agreement charts, and radar charts that show model performance and output. The results of the experiment indicate that the model was able to predict exceptionally accurately, with the ensemble model winning the race by the yard in AUC, precision, recall, and F1- score metrics. Real-time applicability, user-friendly interaction, and scalability are among the points characters that make the system ready for financials institutions and payment gateways' deployment to effectively UPI scam transaction detection and prevention. The system suggests high predictive accuracy and operational efficiency and acts as a scalable and user- friendly solution for real-time scam detection in UPI- based financial platforms.
