title: An Early Detection of Breast Cancer using Histopathology Images Through Vectorised Topological Features Analysis and Deep Learning Classification
authors: G.P.Susanna Wesley, Mo'Ath Alluwaici, P. Vishwanath, K Srinivas Rao, R V S S Nagini Y, Devipriyan S M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Breast cancer, a common disease in women globally, is caused by abnormal cell growth in the breast, forming tumors with potential fatality if not detected early. Breast cancer, a major global cause of death, necessitates the development of early-stage diagnostic tests. In this article, the authors show the Enhanced Breast Cancer Histopathology Vectorized Detection (EBCHVD) model that combines deep learning with topological data analysis in order to differentiate breast tissue samples as benign or malignant. For its detailed images, the model takes the Break His 400× histopathology dataset and applies preprocessing techniques followed by a meta-learning ensemble made up of different Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to capture diverse image representations. the performance analysis is benign-malignant of f24 ≈ 0.45, f30 ≈ 0.12, loss of 0.70, accuracy of 0.65, confusion matrix of 86.5%, and ROC curve of 81% calculations.

title: Contextual Multi Modal Cross Attention Fusion for Night Time and Occluded Drone Surveillance Sets
authors: B. Tarakeswara Rao, Balajee Maram, Sai Babu Veesam, R S M Lakshmi Patibandla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Military, law enforcement, border, industrial and wildfire-prone sectors need drone night monitoring because visibility decreases after sunset. Traditional RGB imaging or shallow thermal stream fusion detection methods work well in daylight but not in dimly lit and congested environments. RGB-IR fusion has largely been studied as end-of-process averaging, although occlusion, illumination change and contextual comprehension are unknown. How to show that anything can be seen partially disguised or a noise set silhouette? Close these gaps with a sequential pipeline, although it may be too complicated. A Geo-Contextual Semantic Field Estimator with map, weather and time priors estimate item locations. Cross-Spectral Radiance Harmonizers align radiance statistics to minimize false edge distraction. Occlusion-Aware Deformable Cross-Attention Fusion separated features into visible and occluded slots and explicitly reasoned about features behind walls or tree branches. TMCC sanctions drift and examines counterfactual frames ("What if the occluder were not there?") to ensure time consistency. To end the study, the Coverage-Aware Active Surveil Planner uses many drones to account for uncertainty without overlapping coverage. Initial results show over six-point mAP increases, 12% recall gains under heavy occlusion and over one-third lower redundant flight coverages, which could affect surveillance reliability. The performance of this layered complexity in bigger systems is unknown, but hostile events may improve it.

title: Confidence-Aware Transient Stability Assessment Using A Large Language Model and Conformal Prediction
authors: Genghong Lu, Jie Wang, Siqi Bu, Dongqin Feng
abstract: Real-time transient stability assessment plays an important role in securing power system. To satisfy real-time requirements and conduct fast transient stability assessment, advanced deep learning approaches are widely adopted. However, most existing advanced learning methods lack information on the confidence in their corresponding assessment results. The ability to quantify how confident the model is on its outputs upon the given data set can better support operators’ decision-making, particularly in the presence of uncertainties. This paper develops a novel transient stability assessment approach using a large language model and conformal prediction. The developed approach cannot only predict the transient stability status given multimodal data from power systems but also provide prediction sets with guaranteed coverage probabilities to improve the explainability of the learning model. The effectiveness of the developed model is tested using the IEEE 39 Bus System.

title: Research on improved linear active disturbance rejection control for AC-DC converter
authors: Jianwei Liang, Yuqian Zhao, Yu Wu, Shuxin Pan, Jiaming Tian
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved linear active disturbance rejection control (LADRC) strategy combined with fuzzy logic for a two-stage AC-DC converter. The Fuzzy-LADRC controller dynamically adjusts compensation factors to enhance system robustness and dynamic response. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves faster response, lower steady-state error, and stronger anti-interference capability compared to traditional PID and LADRC methods.

title: Deep Transfer Learning Framework for Multiclass Driver Behavioral States Recognition: A Computer Vision Approach
authors: Deanne Cameren P. Evangelista
abstract: Driver behavior monitoring is essential for intelligent transportation systems, as unsafe behaviors such as distraction, drowsiness, and mobile phone use significantly increase accident risk. This work presents a deep transfer learning-based computer vision framework for multi-class driver behavior recognition from in-cabin images, targeting four classes: neutral driving, distraction, microsleep, and cellphone use. A pretrained convolutional model is fine-tuned using an annotated dataset collected under diverse vehicles and lighting conditions, with preprocessing and data augmentation applied to improve robustness. Performance is evaluated using mAP@50, mAP@50-95, precision-recall, F1-confidence analysis, and confusion matrices. The proposed framework achieves high accuracy and strong generalization, with an overall mAP@50 of 0.992 and mAP@50-95 of 0.978, demonstrating minimal class confusion and reliable confidence calibration.

title: Hybrid CNN With Transfer Learning for Advanced Dermatological Analysis
authors: Sk. Khaleelullah, A.V.N.Pravallika, Bakuna Amulya, Arrabothu Sheerisha Reddy, Godhala Harika Reddy, B.V. Chowdary
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Dermatological diseases are among the most common health issues worldwide, and early detection is vital for effective treatment. This study proposes a hybrid CNN framework with transfer learning for advanced dermatological image analysis. By combining pre-trained models such as ResNet and Efficient Net, the system enhances feature extraction and classification performance. Confidence thresholding is employed to ensure high reliability predictions, while Bayesian CNNs provide uncertainty estimation. To preserve data privacy, federated learning enables collaborative training without sharing patient information. Additionally, explainable AI (XAI) techniques highlight critical image regions influencing the model’s decisions, improving clinical interpretability. The proposed framework enhances diagnostic accuracy, reliability, and transparency, making it a promising AI-driven decision-support tool for dermatology.

title: High-Voltage Cable Main Insulation Surface Defect Detection Based on RANSAC and AABB
authors: Chen Luo, Xiaoping Xu, Hangxing Lu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: As the weak link in XLPE cable lines, the main insulation of cable joints is prone to surface defects such as pits and protrusions during the accessory assembly process due to processes like grinding, crimping, and cleaning. These defects directly cause electric field distortion, leading to partial discharge and even insulation breakdown. This paper proposes a complete technical solution based on 3D point cloud processing. Firstly, the RANSAC algorithm is used to fit the reference cylinder model, accurately locating the central axis of the cable's main insulation. An improved least squares distance optimization algorithm is then employed to calculate the closest distance from points to the axis, and defect candidate points with excessive distance deviations are screened out. The DBSCAN density clustering algorithm is introduced to segment the candidate point cloud, enabling adaptive identification of defect regions with different shapes. In the defect quantification stage, the AABB (Axis-Aligned Bounding Box) and PCA (Principal Component Analysis) algorithms are combined to optimize dimension calculation. The direction of the point cloud is corrected through principal component decomposition, which reduces the impact of noise, data missing, and target rotation on the quantification results. Finally, the area and volume of defects are calculated using length, width, and height parameters. Experimental results show that this solution can effectively identify surface defects on the main insulation of high-voltage cables, meet the practical needs of on-site detection, and provide reliable technical support for the efficient evaluation and maintenance of the insulation state of high-voltage cables.

title: Vision-Driven Dynamic Traffic Light System for Smart Cities using Edge AI
authors: Konda Hari Krishna, Avatala Sai Chetana Reddy, Chinthakani Mohana Priya, Chennuru Tejesh, Amanchi Rishi Srinivas, G.Rajesh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion remains a persistent and complex challenge across metropolitan areas, leading to increased travel delays, excessive fuel consumption, and heightened safety risks. To mitigate these issues, this work proposes a vision-driven dynamic traffic signal control system that leverages real-time visual data to optimize signal timing and improve overall traffic flow within smart city environments. The proposed system employs advanced artificial intelligence techniques to enable adaptive traffic signal control without requiring additional hardware installations at each intersection. The system utilizes the YOLO (You Only Look Once) object detection framework to analyze real-time video streams from existing traffic surveillance cameras and estimate vehicle density. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is employed to classify detected vehicles into categories such as cars, buses, trucks, motorcycles, and emergency vehicles, enabling priority-based traffic management. Data security and user privacy are preserved through encryption and anonymization mechanisms applied during data transmission and storage. The proposed framework is scalable, cost-effective, and adaptable for deployment across diverse smart city environments, using vision and artificial intelligence to enable intelligent traffic management without extensive additional hardware.

title: Random Forest Classifier for Resume Parsing and Job Categorization
authors: Rohan Joshi, Rishi Mishra, Jay Darje, Tejas Bhovad, Saguna Ingle, Savita R. Bhosale
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Resume parsing has become essential in modern recruitment systems, requiring efficient extraction and classification of candidate information from diverse document formats. This paper presents a comprehensive implementation of a Random Forest (RF) classifier combined with TF-IDF feature extraction for resume parsing and job categorization. This Flaskbased web application demonstrates exceptional performance in categorizing resume content into 25 predefined job categories with an overall accuracy of 93.7% and macro-averaged F1score of 0.934. The system processes PDF resume files using advanced text preprocessing techniques and extracts key information including contact details, skills, experience, and education. This approach evaluates this approach against multiple baseline algorithms including Support Vector Machines, Naive Bayes, and Logistic Regression, demonstrating superior performance across all metrics. The implementation utilizes pre-trained models optimized through grid search hyperparameter tuning for both resume categorization and job recommendation tasks. This comprehensive evaluation on a dataset of 2,847 resumes shows precision values ranging from 0.82 to 0.98 across different job categories, making it highly suitable for deployment in automated recruitment systems. The system includes advanced features such as skill extraction using NLP techniques, experience level classification, and intelligent job matching algorithms.

title: Human-systems integration through the lenses of human factors ergonomics and systems engineering: from cooperation to collaboration
authors: Emilie Perreau, Yaroslav Menshinin, Romain Pinquié
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: Human-systems integration involves system views and human views on product development. Each of those views has roots in its own discipline: Systems Engineering (SE) and Human Factors and Ergonomics (HFE), respectively. Traditionally, these disciplines interact in a cooperative manner throughout the product lifecycle, meaning that different tasks for a shared objective are realized non-synchronously. However, this process of integration through cooperation of disciplines may require more time and effort to embed the other discipline’s perspectives, which increases the risk of misalignment.This paper analyses the areas of overlap and complementarity of SE and HFE through the transition from cooperation to collaboration. In this paradigm, the disciplines would be tightly integrated through the proposed approach rather than added to each other. The approach is enabled by the development of a meta-model to explore the interconnections of disciplines’ entities, identify their overlapping nature, and clarify the added value of collaboration.The key result of the paper is the integration of the core entities emerging from each discipline into a collaborative environment, rather than considering them through cooperative lenses. The value of the proposed work is that the definition of core entities supports the allocation of key responsibilities and roles for the HFE practitioners and systems engineers, including the establishment of key interfaces between the roles and their personal or joint outputs.

title: ReguQuery: An Agentic Framework For Automated Regulatory Compliance Analysis Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Darrian Bharucha, Nandika Palanisamy, Mohini Reddy
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The financial sector of India works under a very hectic and always evolving regulatory setup. There are several organizations, like the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI), Reserve Bank of India (RBI), and Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI), public authorities that are in charge of regulation. For financial institutions, manual compliance verification of documents is an expensive, slow, and error-prone process with huge operational risks. This paper presents ReguQuery, the AI framework with the ability to automatically audit compliance issues. ReguQuery utilises a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline for creating professional knowledge graphs from regulatory texts. A smart agent employs these knowledge graphs to conduct context-based analysis on financial documents uploaded by the users. The system can provide a numerical compliance score, highlight particular non-compliant sections, and suggest solutions, thus changing the regulatory risk management from simple compliance to a continuous stream of active business benefits. That was our study. We explain ReguQuery’s design, clarify its methodology, and show its comparison with services that have been created in the area of legality and financial technology.

title: Efficient Task scheduling and resource allocation using an AI-based Sugeno fuzzy inference scheme for a Video Conferencing system
authors: Ashis Kumar Mohapatra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The dedication is using an AI-based Sugeno fuzzy inference system that effectively plans the tasks and distributes the resources within a video conferencing system. It bestows the advantage of modeling uncertainties and dynamic conditions and optimizes performance, eliminating delays. The fuzzy system further allows real-time and intelligent decision-making in order to have seamless and quality communication. The available video conferencing systems commonly have issues with dynamic resource allocation and task scheduling, especially regarding latency and lower user experience. Conventional techniques are not flexible to real-time network change and dynamic workload, leading to poor performance. The first is that Z-score standardization standardizes incoming data, the network latency, CPU load, and bandwidth to the same scale, which removes bias based on different measurement units. As a next step, the standardized numeric inputs are transformed into fuzzy values by the Triangular Membership Function-Based Fuzzification Algorithm (TMF-Fuzz) that approximates them into linguistic terms Low, Medium, and High, using triangular membership functions. The Sugeno-Type Fuzzy Inference System (FIS) then computes these fuzzy inputs using a rule base, and the result is a crisp output that can be used to make resource allocation decisions according to predetermined rules of inference. Lastly, the Fuzzy Label Assignment effectively categorizes these outputs into the most suitable labels in order to give priority to tasks. The Sugeno fuzzy inference scheme that is AI-driven enhances video conferencing by intelligently scheduling tasks and resources with the best optimization of performance parameters. It reduces latency, improves throughput, adapts to variations in the network, minimizes packet loss, supports scalability, and improves audio-video quality. These components combine to provide easy and highly dependable video conferencing.

title: Attention-Based Transfer Learning for Non-IID Federated Human Activity Recognition Using Inertial Sensors
authors: Vaibhav Soni, Mayank Gupta, Himanshu Yadav, R. K. Pateriya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) enables health-care, fitness, and smart-environment services but must balance accuracy with privacy and on-device efficiency. Prior federated learning (FL) methods often degrade under non-IID client data and lack effective transfer of global knowledge to compact client models. We propose a hybrid FL approach that initializes client models from a centrally trained, attention-augmented BiLSTM with multi-head self-attention (MHA) and applies a lightweight feature-adaptation layer, models aggregate with FedAvg while keeping data local. We evaluate on mHealth, BandX, UCI-HAR, and WISDM using dataset-provided splits under a fixed 15-round FL protocol with evaluation based on Accuracy, Recall, Precision, and F1-score. The approach achieves 0.7479 Accuracy on UCI-HAR (+13.7 pp vs. FedAvg at 0.6107, +12.0 pp vs. FedMA at 0.6184), yields small but consistent gains on WISDM (0.6189 vs. 0.6147) and BandX (0.7048 vs. 0.7039), and accepts a privacy–accuracy trade-off on mHealth (0.9308 vs. centralized 0.9852). These results indicate that transfer-initialized, attention-based models narrow the FL–centralized gap for non-IID HAR while preserving data locality, supporting privacy-aware deployment on edge devices. Transfer learning (TL) improves client adaptation and alleviates non-IID performance degradation through the utilization of common pretrained weights.

title: A Hybrid CNN With Bagging- Hard Voting Ensemble Technique for Diabetic Retinopathy Diagnosis
authors: Vishnesh Reddy Patlolla, Mohd Abdul Hameed
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a major cause of vision loss in diabetes patients, early detection is crucial for timely clinical intervention. Consequently, several Deep Learning with AI methods have been implemented to automatically detect anomalies in DR from distinct phases, from photographs of the retina. In this study, we propose a deep learning-based framework that integrates a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) feature extractor with a two-stage ensemble classification model to accurately identify DR severity stages from fundus images. The hybrid CNN architecture combines DenseNet121 and ResNet50V2, pretrained on ImageNet, to capture both fine-grained retinal textures and global structural features. An attention mechanism is incorporated to emphasize lesion-relevant areas. The resulting high-dimensional features are reduced using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) to enhance class separability. A soft-voting classifier comprising Gaussian Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, and SVM is used as the base learner, which is further wrapped in a bagging ensemble to improve generalization. The proposed framework is evaluated on the Messidor-2 and APTOS2019 dataset. It achieved an accuracy of 99.71 %, F1-score of 99.75 %, AUC of 99.99 %, and a Cohen's Kappa score of 99.52 % on Messidor-2 and an accuracy of 98.24 % on the APTOS2019 dataset, outperforming baseline stacking and bagging classifiers. The results demonstrate the potential of combining hybrid deep features with ensemble learning to develop scalable and clinically reliable DR screening tools. The implementation of the proposed model is available at https://github.com/Vishnesh-Reddy/Hybrid_CNN_2_stage_ensemble/blob/main/diabetic-retinopathy_2_stage_ensemble.ipynb

title: Image Super-Resolution Using ESRGAN Model
authors: G. Uday Kiran, B. Priyanka, N. Nikhil, P Jethin, N. Sai Priya
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Reconstructing high-resolution images from their low-resolution counterparts is the main goal of Image Super-Resolution, a fundamental task in computer vision. Traditional interpolation techniques, like bicubic interpolation, frequently produce outputs that are blurry and devoid of perceptual quality and high-frequency details. This study investigates the application of the Enhanced Super Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (ESRGAN), a deep learning architecture that greatly enhances the visual fidelity and texture restoration of upscaled images, to overcome these drawbacks. Through a meticulously crafted generator network that integrates Residual-in-Residual Dense Blocks (RRDB), the ESRGAN model improves image quality and permits deep feature learning without the need for batch normalization layers. This is combined with a relativistic discriminator, which learns to differentiate between generated and real images in a relative sense, resulting in sharper outputs. In order to guarantee that the generated images closely resemble human visual perception, the model also incorporates perceptual loss calculated from a pretrained VGG19 network. The DF2K dataset, a commonly used benchmark that blends DIV2K and Flickr2K photos, was used for the study. Bicubic down-sampling was used to simulate low-resolution inputs, and LR-HR image pairs were used for both training and evaluation.

title: MPA-DNN: Projection-Aware Unsupervised Learning for Multi-period DC-OPF
authors: Yeomoon Kim, Minsoo Kim, Jip Kim
abstract: Ensuring both feasibility and efficiency in optimal power flow (OPF) operations has become increasingly important in modern power systems with high penetrations of renewable energy and energy storage. While deep neural networks (DNNs) have emerged as promising fast surrogates for OPF solvers, they often fail to satisfy critical operational constraints, especially those involving inter-temporal coupling, such as generator ramping limits and energy storage operations. To deal with these issues, we propose a Multi-Period Projection-Aware Deep Neural Network (MPA-DNN) that incorporates a projection layer for multi-period dispatch into the network. By doing so, our model enforces physical feasibility through the projection, enabling end-to-end learning of constraint-compliant dispatch trajectories without relying on labeled data. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves near-optimal performance while strictly satisfying all constraints in varying load conditions.

title: Efficient Implementation of Convolutional Neural Networks in FPGA Using approximate ripple carry adders
authors: Butchi Babu Sarnala, Siva Ramakrishna Pillutla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are an enhanced model of artificial neural networks for effective feature extraction and pattern recognition in image processing and object detection tasks. However, a huge computational complexity poses a challenge for resource- constrained and high-speed applications. Arithmetic units, particularly adders, are fundamental elements that are essential in the performance of the CNN accelerators. Ripple carry adders (RCAs) are utilized in CNN architectures to process small-sized image datasets. First, this work proposes a hardware-efficient approximate ripple carry adder (AxRCA) that achieves hardware reduction and less delay by using approximate computing. Secondly, the proposed approximate ripple carry adder (AxRCA) is integrated into the processing element of the convolution layer in the sparse CNN architecture to minimize the complexity of computations and resource utilization. The design of the proposed AxRCA achieves a 3.51% reduction in hardware resources and a 16.22% reduction in propagation delay, while convolutional operation achieves a 18.33% reduction in hardware resources and a 31.86% reduction in propagation delay. Furthermore, the sparse CNN architecture is implemented using the proposed AxRCA, and the comparisons show that the proposed design is suitable for real-time, resource-constrained computer vision applications.

title: AI-Powered Intelligent Writing Assistants for Enhancing ESL Composition Skills through Novel Deep Learning Frameworks
authors: Sonny Boy V. Villegas, Kristopher Rebale Lopez
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Although ESL learners are skilled in grammar, textual and discourse skills, they tend to have a problem with the overall coherence and structural writing problems. The existing AI-driven systems, such as Automated Writing Evaluation and large language models, mostly offer surface-level corrections and cannot fix structural and semantic problems. To address the above drawbacks, Multi-Task Hierarchical Transformer (MTH-Transformer) came into being, incorporating hierarchical attention with multi-task learning to optimize grammar, coherence and discourse simultaneously. The model is implemented in Python and is trained on a curated ESL writing dataset and uses tokenization, embeddings, and hierarchical attention to produce context-sensitive and adaptive feedback. Experimental analysis showed better performance over the available tools with an average grammar accuracy of 94.3, a F1-score of 92, a coherence of 88.2, a BLEU score of 88.8 and a semantic similarity of 0.91. These findings confirm MTH-Transformer as a viable framework of providing all-encompassing ESL writing assistance that can deliver practical implications to AI-assisted language learning and in intelligent and automated educational technologies as well as intelligent, pedagogically informed writing enhancement solutions.

title: A Comparative Study on Different Methods of Helmet Detection to Improve ATM Security
authors: S. Bharaninayagi, Lavanya M, Raghavardhini T, Sathiya Priya B, Theepshika S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The need of intelligent surveillance for Automated Teller Machines (ATMs) has increased with the sophistication of security threats. Real-time monitoring that can spot suspicious activity and unusual circumstances is now made possible by developments in edge computing, computer vision, and Internet of Things technologies. In order to improve ATM security, this assessment examines current methods that include automated access-control techniques, human analysis, and multi-object identification. It investigates how well existing vision models can identify firearms, forced entrance attempts, and facial coverings in various lighting and environmental conditions. IoT-based alerting mechanisms that provide quick action by automatically notifying security teams and financial authorities are also covered in this paper. This survey identifies future research directions for safe, real-time, edge-enabled ATM surveillance systems by examining strengths, limits, and deployment issues.

title: Fusion of Multi-Scale Wavelet, LBP, GLCM Features for SEM Image Classification using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Yuganesan R, Rajkumar S, Gowseelan C, Varun Manikam S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: In General, Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) images are used to strengthen the analysis of surfaces and structures of materials at microscopic scales. The automated classification of these images helps researchers efficiently manage datasets and identify materials faster. This study examined a classic machine learning approach to classify SEM images with handcrafted features for automated classification. The dataset used comprises of 18,577 SEM images which were collected at CNR-IOM (Trieste, Italy). These images fall into ten categories, includes Biological, Fibres, Nanowires, Particles, etc. The images contained no scientific metadata, and the images were divided into training and testing sets. Feature extraction was performed using Wavelet Transform, Local Binary Pattern (LBP), and Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) to obtain texture and structural patterns. Four classifiers namely Random Forest, Decision Tree, K-Nearest Neighbors (K-NN), and Support Vector Machine are trained and evaluated. To evaluate the performance of the machine learning methods like accuracy, precision, and recall metrics are used. From the experimental observed that the Random Forest classifier achieved a mean cross-validation accuracy of 67.88% and a maximum test accuracy of 77.42%. Among the classifiers, SVM is the least effective compared to K-NN but lower than all the others. The results suggested that texture-based handcrafted features when combined with classical machine learning methods can provide a simple yet effective solution for SEM image classification.

title: Research on Incremental Self-Evolution Architecture of Time-Series Large Models in Complex Power Operation and Maintena Scenarios
authors: Xiya Li, Renyu Li, Yun Zheng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This study aims to address the challenges of concept drift and catastrophic forgetting faced by time-series models in complex power operation and maintenance (O&M) scenarios, and proposes an incremental self-evolution architecture. The method extracts power data features through a multi-scale temporal convolution and attention mechanism hybrid encoder. It adopts a dynamic replay strategy based on uncertainty weighting to balance learning of new and old knowledge, and designs an autonomous triggering mechanism based on the CUSUM algorithm for adaptive updating. Experimental results show that the proposed architecture achieves an average accuracy of 83.2% after continuously learning 5 tasks, with a forgetting rate of only 9.8%, significantly outperforming the comparison methods (static fine-tuning 42%, EWC 65%, iCaRL 72%). Ablation experiments confirm the necessity of each component: replacing the encoder reduces the accuracy to 77.5%, and simplifying the replay strategy drops it to 75.1%. The conclusion demonstrates that this architecture effectively enhances the continuous learning ability of power O&M systems and provides key technical support for intelligent O&M.

title: Coordinated tuning of multiple power oscillation damping controllers of HVDC links: Application to a large-scale power system
authors: Luis Rouco, Lukas Sigrist, Philippe Carpentier, Gilles Torresan
abstract: This paper uses eigenvalue sensitivities to address the coordinated design of multiple power oscillation damping (POD) controllers of an HVDC-VSC link. Namely, POD controllers modulating the active and reactive power of each pole’s converter are designed. This paper shows the feasibility of applying such an approach to a large-scale power system and the challenges encountered. Moreover, the coordinated design method based on eigenvalue sensitivities has been extended to design POD controllers valid for several operating conditions. In particular, N-1 conditions in the parallel AC lines are considered. The performance of the proposed controller designs is checked in the time and frequency domains.

title: Design and Development of a Modular Bucket Drum Excavator for Lunar ISRU
authors: Simon Giel, James Hurrell, Shreya Santra, Ashutosh Mishra, Kentaro Uno, Kazuya Yoshida
abstract: In-Situ Resource Utilization (ISRU) is one of the key technologies for enabling sustainable access to the Moon. The ability to excavate lunar regolith is the first step in making lunar resources accessible and usable. This work presents the development of a bucket drum for the modular robotic system MoonBot, as part of the Japanese Moonshot program. A 3D-printed prototype made of PLA was manufactured to evaluate its efficiency through a series of sandbox tests. The resulting tool weighs 4.8 kg and has a volume of 14.06 L. It is capable of continuous excavation at a rate of $777.54 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{h}$ with a normalized energy consumption of $0.022 \mathrm{~Wh} / \mathrm{kg}$. In batch operation, the excavation rate is $172.02 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{h}$ with a normalized energy consumption of 0.86 Wh per kilogram of excavated material. The obtained results demonstrate the successful implementation of the concept. A key advantage of the developed tool is its compatibility with the modular MoonBot robotic platform, which enables flexible and efficient mission planning. Further improvements may include the integration of sensors and an autonomous control system to enhance the excavation process.

title: DAIR-FedMoE: Hierarchical MoE for Federated Encrypted Traffic Classification under Compound Drift
authors: Shamaila Fardous, Kashif Sharif, Fan Li, Ali Asghar Manjotho, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Federated learning (FL) offers a decentralized, privacy-preserving framework for encrypted traffic classification (ETC), enabling network management and security. However, real-world deployment of federated ETC faces compound client specific feature, concept, and label drift, which degrades model performance. Existing ETC methods under FL settings typically address these drift types in isolation or partial combinations, overlooking their entanglement. Moreover, multiple-global model and personalized FL approaches are computational and communication expensive. To fill this gap, we propose DAIR FedMoE, a Drift-Adaptive, Imbalance-Aware, RL-Managed Federated Mixture-of-Experts framework to simultaneously handle the drift triad with single-global model while minimizing the computational and communication overhead. DAIR-FedMoE in tegrates a GShard Transformer with a hierarchical Mixture of-Experts (MoE) layer that routes encrypsted flows to either stable or drift-specialist experts based on per-client drift scores. Within each expert, entropy-guided loss reweighting empha sizes low-confidence classes to address dynamic label imbalance. Additionally, a reinforcement learning-based policy dynamically manages the expert pool by spawning, pruning, and merging experts, enabling efficient adaptation to evolving traffic patterns. Experiments on federated splits of ISCX-VPN, ISCX-Tor, VNAT, and USTC-TFC2016 show that DAIR-FedMoE achieves superior macro-F1, minority-class recall, and drift-recovery speed compared to state-of-the-art baselines, while preserving privacy and communication efficiency. The source code is available at https://github.com/dairfedmoe/DairFM.

title: 2025 International Conference on Space Robotics (iSpaRo)
authors: N/A

title: Capacity-Latency Tradeoffs in CXL Memory Expander at Hyperscale
authors: Haneul Park, Grant Ayers, Nam Sung Kim, Philip Levis, Brian Morris
abstract: DRAM dominates the cost of hyperscale datacenter servers today and AI-driven memory supply shortages are pushing costs even higher. CXL memory expanders are a new technology to reduce server costs by reusing legacy DDR DRAM modules from older servers. Memory expanders further save cost through inline compression that increases the effective capacity. This paper investigates two critical design points of a memory expander: the compression algorithm and the compression block size. Both design points introduce tradeoffs between latency and capacity that involve complex interactions with the memory expander architecture, access patterns to the device, and the entropy of stored data. Current commercial devices use simple, fast compression and a 4kB block as the default. Using a compressibility benchmark and the latest traces of cold memory accesses from a hyperscaler, we find that using a different compression algorithm and block size can simultaneously increase expander capacity and reduce access latency. The key insight is counter-intuitive: using a slower, more complex compression algorithm improves latency because the reduction in DRAM traffic more than offsets the additional decompression time.

title: A Strategic Framework for Supplier Performance Assessment and Anomaly Detection in High-Tech Supply Chains: Integrating Shared-Resource DEA with Machine Learning_supp1-3675293.docx
authors: Sheng-Wei Lin
abstract: Evaluating supplier performance in high-tech supply chains is challenging because suppliers must allocate shared resources across operational and sustainability-related activities while maintaining resilience under uncertainty. This study develops an integrated decision-support framework that combines two-stage shared-resource data envelopment analysis (DEA) with machine learning for supplier assessment, anomaly detection, and efficiency prediction. Using NVIDIA's semiconductor supply chain as the empirical setting, the framework explicitly models resource allocation between operational and ESG objectives, thereby addressing the measurement distortion that arises when conventional DEA assumes independent resource use across stages. The proposed approach further incorporates multimodel anomaly detection to identify suppliers with abnormal performance patterns and employs predictive analytics to support forward-looking monitoring. Research rigor is strengthened through transparent data construction, comparative model benchmarking, time ordered validation, and multi-method analytical triangulation. The findings show that the proposed framework produces more reliable and more discriminating supplier assessments than conventional efficiency-evaluation approaches, while also offering earlier identification of performance risks. For engineering management, the framework provides a practical basis for balancing operational and ESG priorities, prioritizing supplier development efforts, and allocating managerial attention to suppliers requiring early intervention.

title: Learnable Residual Denoiser Enhanced Deep Sensor Fusion for Smartphone Navigation under GNSS Outages
authors: V Vishnukumar, M V Satheesh Kumar, Sony Udayan, K N Madhusoodanan
abstract: This study introduces an advanced sensor-fusion approach to improve localization robustness during Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) outages on smartphones. We developed a hybrid deep learning architecture combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) that learns spatio-temporal patterns in inertial data to generate pseudo-GNSS positions. To improve training stability and noise robustness, we evaluate two Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) denoising strategies before the CNN layers: (a) a Hierarchical Denoising Pipeline (HDP) and (b) a Learnable Residual Denoiser Network (LRDN). Evaluations on real-world vehicular logs under 30 seconds and 60 seconds outages demonstrate a clear performance hierarchy. The HDP-integrated CNN-GRU consistently outperforms conventional fusion and baseline deep-learning approaches by reducing error metrics and improving trajectory smoothness. The LRDN-integrated CNN-GRU, based on a learnable residual denoiser, achieves the highest gains, reducing RMSE by nearly 74% compared to the plain CNN-GRU, up to 85% compared to baseline models including standalone Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) fusion, and 52% over the HDP-enhanced CNN-GRU. This work investigates the integration feasibility of the proposed deep-learning-aided INS-GNSS navigation framework on smartphone-based sensing and processing platforms, addressing both sensor-level noise suppression and temporal sequence modeling within a unified architecture. The results demonstrate computational feasibility within the limits of commodity smartphones, confirming the practical relevance of the approach for edge-deployable navigation in GNSS-challenged environments.

title: A novel control strategy to maintain ZVS under component variability in resonant Modular Multi-Active Bridge converters
authors: Rupert L. Power, Udaya K. Madawala
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: A significant challenge for multiport resonant DC/DC converters is the need for multiple resonant tanks to operate at the same switching frequency, leading to loss of Zero Voltage Switching (ZVS) and increased reactive power at some ports when the resonant components are not perfectly tuned. No existing work addresses the issues caused by component variations without introducing actively-tuned components into the system. This paper proposes a model based on Generalized State Space Averaging (GSSA) for a resonant Modular Multi-Active Bridge (MMAB) converter to establish the relationship between port-to-port voltage phase angles and ZVS behavior of the converter. This model is also used to propose a novel method to control the switching currents, to achieve ZVS and minimize reactive power under varying resonant component values. A small-signal transfer function is developed from the GSSA model and used to characterize the system at high frequency, to enable design of the controller, which balances dynamic performance with system stability. The GSSA model and the proposed control strategy are verified through simulations and with a 6kW experimental prototype, demonstrating a peak efficiency of 98% which is maintained even as one of the resonant component values is increased or reduced by 10%.

title: A Combined Aliasing-Free Outphasing Transmitter
authors: Muhammad Fahim Ul Haque, Tahir Malik, Hameeza Ahmed, Hashim Raza Khan, Kamran Arshad, Khaled Assaleh, Ted Johansson
abstract: This paper presents a mobile communication transmitter architecture that exploits bandlimited pulse-width modulation (BL-PWM) combined with outphasing to eliminate aliasing and image distortions. The proposed combined outphasing-aliasing free PWM transmitter (COAF-PWMT) shows very high linearity and high power efficiency as it can use switch-mode power amplifiers (SMPAs) or linear power amplifiers operating at peak power. The transmitter is implemented using two 130 nm CMOS class-D power amplifiers and experimentally validated with both 1.5 MHz LTE signals and 10 MHz 5G NR signals at a carrier frequency of 700 MHz. Experimental results for the 1.5 MHz LTE signal show that the COAF-PWMT achieves an ACLR of 54.3 dBc and an average efficiency of 15.7%. The architecture improves ACLR by 22.6 dB and power efficiency by 4.4% compared to the aliasing-free PWM transmitter (AF-PWMT). It also provides a 14.3 dB ACLR and 2.2% efficiency boost over the Gibbs-phenomenon-reduced aliasing-free PWM transmitter (GR-AFPWMT), and a 15.0 dB ACLR improvement over the modified aliasing-free PWM transmitter (MAF-PWMT). Compared to the aliasing-compensated PWM transmitter (AC-PWMT), the COAF-PWMT increases ACLR by 19.5 dB with similar efficiency. For the 10 MHz 5G NR signal, the COAF-PWMT reaches an ACLR of 42 dB, which is 6 dB better than the GR-AFPWMT, and improves efficiency by 1.6%. These results show the COAF-PWMT’s promise for highly linear and efficient RF transmitter design in future wireless systems.

title: Deep Domain Adaptation with Channel Attention for Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis of Charging Piles Operating under Dynamic Working Conditions
authors: Quanxue Guan, Xue Hu, Yu Han, Jiabei Hu, Xiaojun Tan, Qin Wang
abstract: Open-circuit faults (OCFs) in charging converter modules can lead to performance degradation and system failures. Traditional deep-learning-based fault diagnosis methods typically require large amounts of labeled data, which is costly and time-consuming for two-stage power converters in practice. To this end, this paper proposes a deep domain-adaptation network with a channel attention mechanism to diagnose the OCFs of charging piles operating under dynamic operating conditions. Multi-channel features are first extracted from the three-phase input currents, the output voltage, and the resonant capacitor voltage. To overcome the lack of labeled data in varying working conditions, multi-kernel maximum mean discrepancy (MK-MMD) is employed to align feature distributions between the labeled source domains and the unlabeled target domains. This enables the proposed classifier model to generalize across different operating conditions in the target domain. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves over 99% diagnostic accuracy in the source domain and above 90% in the target domain within 11 ms, outperforming the existing baseline methods. It effectively identifies OCFs in both the Vienna rectifier and LLC converter stages. The proposed method also exhibits strong robustness to noise, load changes, and unbalanced grid conditions. By eliminating the need for extensive labeled datasets and adapting to unknown operating states, the proposed approach offers a practical and efficient solution for real-time fault diagnosis in charging piles, including multiple simultaneous faults that undermine the reliability of charging systems.

title: Spatial-Temporal Generative AI for Traffic Flow Estimation With Sparse Data of Connected Vehicles
authors: Jianzhe Xue, Yunting Xu, Dongcheng Yuan, Caoyi Zha, Hongyang Du, Haibo Zhou, Dusit Niyato
abstract: Traffic flow estimation (TFE) is crucial for intelligent transportation systems, which traditionally rely on extensive road sensors at significant cost. Sparse mobile crowdsensing (MCS) enables a cost-effective alternative by utilizing sparsely distributed probe vehicle data (PVD) collected from connected vehicles. However, according to the central limit theorem, the initial TFE results derived from sparse PVD by calculating the average vehicle speed for each road segment turn out to be inaccurate. To this end, this paper proposes a novel sparse MCS TFE approach and improves its accuracy with the spatial-temporal generative artificial intelligence (GAI) framework. Within this framework, the conditional encoder mines spatial-temporal correlations in the initial TFE results, and the generative decoder generates accurate TFE outputs, achieving similar results to those employing complete and sufficient PVD. Additionally, we discuss the design of the spatial-temporal neural network that serves as the backbone of the conditional encoder for effectively capturing spatial-temporal correlations. The effectiveness of the proposed TFE approach is demonstrated through evaluations based on real-world connected vehicle data. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed TFE approach and highlight the effectiveness of the spatial-temporal GAI framework in improving estimation accuracy.

title: Design and Evaluation of an AI-Based Conversational Agent for Adaptive Feedback in Educational Robotics
authors: Martina Morano, Dirk Ifenthaler, Lorenzo Cesaretti, Francesco Di Nardo, Laura Screpanti, David Scaradozzi
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly adopted in education to deliver structured, real-time feedback, especially in complex, collaborative learning scenarios. Large Language Models (LLMs), in particular, have enabled the integration of chatbots into a wide range of educational settings. This study presents the design and implementation of an AI-empowered chatbot, based on the Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet, within educational robotics activities, aiming to explore its potential as a valid support system for real-time, contextualised feedback delivery. The system was deployed in a field study involving 80 students (grades 5–7), organised into 26 collaborative groups. Three hypotheses were evaluated: whether chatbot interaction (i) correlates with students' perceived usefulness (PU), (ii) supports more efficient programming behaviour, and (iii) is related to task success. While the number of attempts did not significantly decrease, chatbot interaction showed mixed associations with success, highlighting that usage frequency alone does not predict effectiveness. Students also consistently rated the system as highly useful (PU=4.04 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.46). Additionally, the chatbot infrastructure enables continuous tracking of student activity, opening the way for advanced student modelling and the development of digital twins within educational systems. This work contributes to the field of intelligent tutoring by demonstrating that LLM-based agents, when pedagogically aligned, can improve both perceived feedback quality and learner engagement in collaborative, task-based learning environments. Future research should refine feedback strategies and evaluate system performance across more homogeneous learner groups to improve modelling accuracy and scalability.

title: Shortened Current Injection for Sensorless Switched Reluctance Motor Drives Based on High-Sensitivity-Region-Optimized Position Observer
authors: Zifeng Chen, Xijian Lin, Dehui Luo, Molong Duan, Shun Cai, Dianxun Xiao
abstract: Conventional pulse injection-based phase-locked loop enables full-cycle position estimation for low-speed sensorless switched reluctance motor (SRM) control without magnetic data but faces high injection current and low accuracy with its performance limitations related to inductance sensitivity characteristics. In particular, in the high-sensitivity region, where the rate of change of inductance with position is high, even a slight inductance disturbance can lead to an unexpected position estimation error. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an improved position observer considering the high sensitivity region of phase inductance. By introducing the optimized heterodyne method, the use of high-sensitivity low-inductance regions for position estimation is avoided, thereby enhancing position estimation accuracy. Furthermore, the proposed method can estimate the full-cycle position with a shorter injection interval, reducing the injection current amplitude. Finally, experiments conducted on a three-phase 750 W SRM setup validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed scheme. Compared with the conventional scheme, this scheme reduces the peak value of the injected current by up to 63% and improves the estimation accuracy by 50%, thereby demonstrating desirable estimation performance while reducing power losses.

title: Zero-sum Game Optimized Control With Augmented Time-synchronized Property
authors: Yuxiang Zhang, Dongyu Li, Shuzhi Sam Ge, Tong Heng Lee
abstract: This paper proposes and rigorously develops a reinforcement learning(RL)-based optimized control strategy with notable time-synchronized stability (TSS) properties for zero-sum differential games. The proposed method addresses the challenge of approximating the time-synchronized Nash equilibrium solutions in nonlinear systems governed by the Hamilton-Jacobi-Isaacs (HJI) equation. By incorporating a norm-normalized sign function into the learning framework, the system ensures all state-variables converge simultaneously, improving robustness and energy efficiency. The RL-based optimization iteratively refines the control policies while maintaining system stability underpinned rigorously by Lyapunov-based analysis. To demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, a motion control problem for an autonomous vehicle system is simulated; comparing the results with alternative existing fixed-time sliding control (FTC) and time-synchronized optimized control methods. The results illustrate that the proposed control strategy enhances convergence speed, smoothness, and disturbance rejection, making it well-suited for the requirements of real-world high-precision control applications.

title: TSFA: A Two-Stage Feature Alignment Method for Unsupervised Open-Set Domain Adaptation in Time-Series Classification
authors: Qingchen Wang, Dazhong Ma, Lishan Zhang, Zhiqiang Tian, Shaoyi Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: The unsupervised open-set domain adaptation (UOSDA) in computer vision is widely studied. However, designing UOSDA algorithms for time series remains challenging due to the complex nonstationary property of data and distribution shifts across different operating conditions, which heighten the risk of negative transfer. To address this, a novel two-stage feature alignment (TSFA) method for UOSDA in time-series classification is proposed. First, a time–frequency feature extractor is designed to effectively learn domain-invariant and discriminative interclass representations. Subsequently, a two-stage multigranularity feature alignment framework is proposed. Specifically, global alignment is achieved by minimizing the similarity distribution entropy (SDE), which encourages similar samples from both domains to converge in the feature space, thereby reducing intraclass distances of all target samples. Then, local alignment is performed via self-supervised learning guided by target pseudolabels, enhancing interclass discriminability of target common samples. Furthermore, an optimal similarity assignment matrix (OSAM) is designed to obtain more accurate pseudolabels for common samples, while an adaptive decision boundary is introduced to effectively reject private samples based on target-domain statistics. Finally, extensive experimental results on three real-world datasets indicate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Architecture-Dependent Radiation Response and Hardening of Bandgap References
authors: Yang Xiao, Ping Luo, Jieka Feng, Hao Wang, Zijun Yuan, Yening Qi, Tianyi He, Bo Zhang
abstract: We investigate the TID (total ionizing dose) and SEE (single-event effects) responses of voltage-mode and current-mode bandgap references fabricated in a 0.18 μm BCD (Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS) process, using both simulation and experiment. The <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">60</sup>Co source irradiation experiment agrees well with the simulation results, showing that the voltage-mode reference has superior TID tolerance with only up to 0.05% variation under 150 krad(Si), whereas the current-mode reference exhibits a variation up to 6.41%. The current-mode bandgap reference is more sensitive to TID because amplifier offset voltage and BJT degradation are amplified at the output due to CTAT/PTAT generation paths and bias-network structures. Both the double-exponential current source simulations and the back-side laser irradiation experiments indicate that the bandgap core acts as a dominant sensitive node in both architectures, where a fall-and-rise single-event transient (SET) can be triggered. The laser injection point is located by a back-side InGaAs infrared imaging system to verify the sensitive region. As the laser energy increases from 600 pJ to 1.33 nJ, both the undershoot magnitude and duration increase. Finally, an anti-single-event bandgap reference circuit based on slope detection is proposed. Simulation results demonstrate that the slope-detection and switching blocks respond rapidly under the evaluated conditions. This corrective action limits the output voltage drop an average value of 12.6 mV across all simulated conditions. The proposed circuit provides an early-response hardening approach suitable for integration in standard BCD processes.

title: Proton-Induced Displacement Damage in AlGaInAs on InP Multi-Quantum-Well Continuous-Wave Laser Diodes
authors: Colin L. Bryant, Di Huang, Kellen P. Arnold, Hannah M. Dattilo, Daniel M. Fleetwood, Ronald D. Schrimpf, En Xia Zhang, Phoenix M. Harris, Michael W. McCurdy, James M. Trippe, Michael L. Alles, Dennis R. Ball, Sharon M. Weiss, Peter J. Delfyett, Robert A. Reed
abstract: AlGaInAs/InP multi-quantum-well continuous-wave Fabry Perot laser diodes are exposed to 1.8 MeV protons at fluences up to 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</sup> p/cm². The threshold current increases with fluence, with maximum shift over 180%. Minimal change in slope efficiency is observed. A substantial redshift in emission wavelength is observed at all fluence levels, while the wavelength free spectral range remains constant. The wavelength shift persists even when measured at the same forward current for pre- and post-irradiation conditions, indicating that effects of heating and plasma from the forward current are a second order effect. The series resistance was not significantly affected by radiation exposure. Damage factor analysis reveals a value more than 100 times higher than that of previously studied lasers of these types, indicating high sensitivity to displacement events. For this laser, the dominant mechanism for the radiation-induced wavelength shift is likely a change in group refractive index and gain bandwidth due to radiation-induced defect levels within the bandgap, with minimal contributions from heating. System level and hardness-assurance implications are discussed.

title: A Novel Trans-Inverse Ultra-High Step-Up Quadratic DC-DC Converter Based on Three-Winding Coupled-Inductor
authors: Mingfa Yang, Yihan Liu, Haibin Li, Yin Chen, Hongkai Zheng, Tao Jin
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: A novel non-isolated high-gain DC-DC converter for renewable energy generation systems is proposed in this paper. The converter integrates Y-source network, voltage multiplier cell, and quadratic boost structure, significantly enhancing its voltage step-up capability. The Y-source configuration introduces a trans-inverse factor into the voltage gain expression, enabling high voltage gain to be achieved with a low total turns ratio of the coupled inductor and a moderate duty cycle. This helps reduce the size of the coupled inductor and minimizes winding losses. In addition, the converter features low voltage stress on the power MOSFETs, current splitting between the coupled inductor and an independent input inductor at the input side, and leakage energy recycled by the regenerative clamping circuit, all of which contribute to improved conversion efficiency. The common ground between the input and output, along with continuous input current, further ensures the converter's suitability for renewable energy applications. This paper presents a detailed steady-state analysis, parameter design, and loss evaluation of the proposed converter, and compares its performance with that of existing high-gain topologies. Finally, a prototype is built based on the design principles, and experimental results validate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed converter.

title: Small Signal Stability Analysis of More Electric Aircraft Based on Prony Method
authors: Xingkun Luo, Chang Cai, Yitong Wang, Xinran Zhang, Chihua Meng, Qingxuan Han
abstract: Facing stringent environmental requirements, the aviation industry is rapidly transitioning to More Electric Aircraft (MEA), replacing hydraulic and pneumatic systems with electrical power to address reliability and efficiency limitations. However, this shift introduces significant stability challenges in the MEA power system due to increased loads, particularly Electro-Mechanical Actuators (EMAs) and pulse-power loads. This paper proposes a data-driven stability analysis and control framework for the MEA high-voltage DC power system to mitigate voltage oscillations and instability caused by these loads. The approach conducts parametric sensitivity studies to establish quantitative relationships between critical control parameters and stability, enabling optimized controller design. It further analyzes flight-condition-dependent variations in system parameters, facilitating online mitigation of resulting fluctuations. The framework enhances system resilience against load-induced disturbances and operational uncertainties.

title: F2-YOLO: A Lightweight YOLO Framework for Forest Fire and Smoke Detection
authors: Neela Lohitha Susmitha Reddy Udumula, Bhavana Paturi, Balvindersingh Bondili, Srikanth Lukka
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Forest fires are now increasing in recent years with the impact of climate change. Early detection of the smoke is of prime importance for preventing the fast growth of the fire. New advancements in the YOLO-based models for the object detection of YOLOv7 to YOLOv12 models have significantly enhanced the real-time detection of the objects. However, YOLOv12 exhibits limited sensitivity to remote and small-scale fire and smoke regions, as it does not fully capture high-resolution spatial details. To resolve this problem this paper introduces F2-YOLO signifies Fire and Fume-detection, a lightweight and version of YOLOv12, specifically designed for early detection of forest fire and smoke. In this improved network, F2-YOLO integrates three key parts: a Lightweight Deep Adaptive Module (LDAM) responsible for efficient feature sharpening, a Multi-Level Feature Fusion (MFF) module for enhancing features related to small objects, and a Small-Object Detection Head (SODH) for superior localization accuracy. When tested using the ADSF and Wildfire datasets, F2-YOLO is shown to have a mAP@50 of 98.5% and mAP@50-95 of 73.92%, which is greater than other YOLO models.

title: AI-Native Observability and Self-Evolving Diagnostics: Generative Troubleshooting, Semantic Correlation, and Embedded Guidance for Large-Scale Cloud Systems
authors: Sharan Babu Paramasivam Murugesan, Dhivya Ganesan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Modern microservices architectures are too complex for traditional threshold-based monitoring, necessitating a shift toward AI-native observability. This paper presents a new methodology for generative artificial intelligence based self-evolving diagnostics that go well beyond passive alerting. By combining semantic correlation engines with generative troubleshooting models, the system reasons about the motivations of system anomalies rather than simply indicating metric deviations. The study uses a unique data set of 416 separate cyber event logs, generated from a simulated hyperscale cloud environment. The proposed observability pipeline is validated through an experimental validation implemented with a custom-built software package written in Python and using the TensorFlow library for DL applications and the Scikit-learn for clustering. The key process is a two-tier neural model that maps unstructured log data over structured metric streams into human-interpretable action recommendations. The proposed system reduces mean time to resolution by 59% and achieves a strong success rate in predicting cascading failures before they affect service availability. The results demonstrate that embedding generative guidance within the observability stack, a.k.a. explanatory reporting, enables the system to evolve its own diagnostic capabilities by learning from prior resolutions, allowing it to handle novel failures without manual rule setting or reconfiguration.

title: Bridging Knowledge Gaps in Internal Auditing and Cybersecurity: Transitioning from CAATTs to AI-Driven Solutions
authors: Katarina Topolko Herceg
abstract: Within the rapidly advancing technological environment, the intersection of internal auditing and cybersecurity has become increasingly critical. By applying a literature review, this research critically evaluates established frameworks to identify existing knowledge gaps and establish a basis for further development. The paper focuses on the technological transition from traditional Computer-Assisted Audit Tools and Techniques (CAATTs) to modern Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven internal auditing solutions. By analyzing recent academic literature (period 2021-2026), the study explores how AI enhances cyber threat detection within accounting information systems and identifies the primary challenges practitioners encounter during implementation. The findings aim to bridge the current gap in cybersecurity-related accounting literature and provide a theoretical foundation for redefining frameworks in the era of AI. Ultimately, this paper results in a synthesized theoretical foundation for future research. The identified future directions serve to guide both academics and practitioners in the evolving domain of automated auditing, providing a framework amidst the intensive development of AI technologies and the rapid digital transformation of the internal auditing.

title: Research on Dynamic Matching of Energy Supply and Demand in Phase Change Material-Thermoelectric Power Generation Coupling System for Long-Endurance Marine Monitoring Buoys
authors: Yutong Qiu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: This paper addresses the core issue of the spatiotemporal mismatch between energy supply and demand in phase change thermal storage/release and thermoelectric power generation for long-endurance marine monitoring buoys. It conducts research on the integrated design and intelligent dynamic matching and control of the coupled system. A closed-loop modular architecture of "acquisition-storage-conversion-control" is proposed. The thermal conductivity of the phase change material is enhanced by a honeycomb grid and copper fins. A corrugated heat exchange structure and retractable heat dissipation fins are used to optimize heat transfer efficiency. An improved particle swarm optimization algorithm is combined to complete multi-objective structural optimization. A dynamic model of multi-field coupling between heat, electricity, and structure is constructed, and a supply and demand matching algorithm based on model predictive control is designed, incorporating load priority allocation and anti-interference strategies. Experimental verification shows that the system parameters are only 1.18M, the energy transfer efficiency reaches 78.6%, the dynamic matching degree is maintained at 1.03-1.18, the voltage fluctuation is ≤4.8%, and the endurance can reach 92 days. It achieves a high-efficiency trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, has good engineering applicability, and provides a new solution for long-endurance buoy power supply.

title: SegFormer-SAC Framework for Safe Landing and Adaptive Exploration in Hazardous Terrains
authors: K. Nikitha, G. Rasmi Sree, Ch. Sanjith, P. Vijai Bhasker, G. Vallathan, G. Sreelakshmi
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Autonomous planetary exploration and hazardous terrain navigation require reliable frameworks for safe landing and adaptive decision-making in uncertain environments. Conventional approaches, including rulebased planning and classical reinforcement learning, provide baseline capabilities but often lack adaptability, generalization, and precision. This research proposes a SegFormer-based Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) framework that integrates semantic segmentation with continuous-control reinforcement learning to enhance hazard perception and policy adaptation. By generating risk and suitability maps from terrain data, the system enables accurate landing site evaluation and efficient navigation. Experimental evaluation demonstrates improved navigation efficiency, energy utilization, and resilience against unknown hazards. Investigational results show a 0.5 % increase in hazard detection, a 0.3 % reduction in false alarms, and a 0.5 % improvement in mission success. The framework reduces latency, improves energy and trajectory efficiency, and enhances robustness, ensuring superior adaptability, reliability, and safety over existing methods.

title: Energy Consumption Prediction using Ensemble Learning and Streamlit Application
authors: Priyanka Anup Ujjainkar, Tabassum H Khan, Smita Nirkhi, Sanskar Mudpalliwar, Shivang Chavan, Kailash Kumar Baraskar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This project introduces a user-friendly web application for predicting household energy usage using ensemble learning techniques. Built with Streamlit, the tool enables users to upload their own energy datasets, explore usage patterns, and receive real-time predictions with just a few clicks. To ensure accurate forecasting, the system includes essential preprocessing steps such as managing missing values, encoding categorical variables, and extracting relevant date and time features. The ensemble model used in the application delivers strong predictive performance, achieving an overall accuracy of 95.8%. It also maintains a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 12.5 kWh and a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) that falls within acceptable limits. These results indicate the model is well-suited for real-world applications in household energy forecasting. Feature analysis showed that the most influential factors affecting energy consumption were the number of appliances, average daily usage hours, and occupancy levels—factors that reflect common trends in actual energy use. In addition to delivering predictions, the system offers automated suggestions to help users improve model performance. These include collecting more historical usage data, enhancing feature engineering methods, and experimenting with hyperparameter tuning. The platform’s interface is simple and intuitive, making it suitable for both technical users and those without a background in data science. The overall findings suggest that ensemble learning can play a valuable role in promoting smarter and more efficient energy use at the household level. Looking ahead, future improvements may include incorporating real-time IoT data, developing more advanced hybrid models, and expanding the tool’s capabilities to support time-series forecasting for improved adaptability.

title: Multiclass Intrusion Detection in IIoT Environments Using Attention Mechanisms and Optimized LSTM Networks
authors: Bighnaraj Naik, Geetanjali Bhoi, Manohar Mishra, Rajat Kumar Sahu, Subhashree Mohapatra, Monalisa Biswal
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) networks has improved automation and operational efficiency but has also increased vulnerability to sophisticated cyberattacks. Traditional intrusion detection systems often fail to detect evolving threats due to their static design and limited adaptability. This paper proposes an optimized Attention-based Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model for multiclass attack detection in IoT environments. The approach leverages temporal sequence learning with an attention mechanism to focus on critical features, enhancing detection accuracy across 19 distinct attack types. To improve model generalization, hyperparameters such as the number of LSTM units and dropout rate are optimized using a randomized search strategy. Experiments conducted on the X-IIoTID dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance compared to traditional machine learning models and conventional LSTM architectures, achieving an F1-score, precision, recall, and accuracy exceeding 0.995. These results confirm the effectiveness of integrating attention mechanisms and automated hyperparameter tuning for robust IIoT intrusion detection, making it well-suited for real-world, dynamic industrial environments.

title: Geometric Approaches to 6-DoF Pose Estimation for Autonomous Spacecraft Docking
authors: Mansi Patel, Komal Prajapati, Raj Hakani
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Precise six-degree-of-freedom (6-DoF) pose estimation is essential for autonomous spacecraft docking, where even minor errors can compromise mission safety. Classical attitude determination algorithms such as QUEST, FOAM, and ESOQ are effective for rotational estimation, but are insufficient for full pose recovery due to the absence of translation estimation. The evolution of Perspective-n-Point (PnP) formulations has enabled complete pose estimation through geometric correspondences; however, these methods rely heavily on ideal imaging conditions, which are rarely available in space environments, especially under thermal imaging. This paper presents a comprehensive review of geometry-driven 6-DoF pose estimation methods for autonomous docking, emphasizing purely geometric approaches without reliance on deep learning. A baseline framework grounded in classical attitude solvers and PnP formulations is discussed and existing research gaps—particularly under thermal sensing constraints—are identified. The study establishes a solid foundation for future real-time, interpretable, and thermally adaptive spacecraft docking systems.

title: FinFlow: Enhancing Portfolio Performance Through Risk Optimization
authors: Ruchit Sheth, Shreya Khandekar, Vedica Mrudul, Monali Sankhe, Monika Mangla, Pravin Hole
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The growing complexity of financial markets has made personal asset management a major challenge for individual investors. While robo-advisors and digital platforms aim to simplify investment decisions, they often use fixed allocation rules and miss important factors like behavioral risk, long-term stability, and market sentiment. This paper presents FinFlow, a Personal Asset Management System that combines machine learning, sentiment analysis, and financial optimization into one framework. The system is delivered through a web-based interface, offering investors personalized recommendations and an interactive dashboard. Evaluation using simulated user profiles and historical market data shows that FinFlow creates allocations that match investor preferences, reduces volatility through diversification, and increases responsiveness to sentiment-driven changes. The findings show the potential of AI-driven systems to fill the gap between the tools used by institutions and the needs of retail investors.

title: Does Trade sail in tandem with Blockchain technology: cognizance from global pilot programs
authors: N V Ganesh Srinivasan, K Ashok Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: We saw technology has hasten the world in many areas and the financial industry has not been left out in the cold in embracing any new initiatives since the dawn of the internet. Blockchain has emerged as a one of the key technologies among contemporary digital innovations, where many organizations and financial institutions have invested and explored the technology wave and consent to the underlying potential. However, many blockchain adoptions by financial institutions or any corporates are progressing at a measured pace significantly due to the need for scaling up of technology components, regulators approvals, high stakes in public money and ascertaining trust among the stakeholders. A press release was made by A.P. Moller -Maersk and IBM during Nov 2022, announced the decision to withdraw the TradeLens platform, a blockchain enabled initiative designed for global trade and shipping solutions highlighting the challenges, a key trigger for this research and outlook on potential solutions. Furthermore, to achieve sustainable success, any products should focus on customer first approach viable for use rather focusing more on being technology driven.This paper aims to review the status quo of the blockchain pilot projects initiated by financial institutions in the trade finance domain and analyse the reasons for delay in adoption compared to the technology paradigms such as APIs (Application programming Interface), Cloud computing and microservices. Further this paper analyses the technical landscape of the blockchain, core projects implemented and associated metrics of the projects. The study adopts a product design approach focused on key transformations required to enhance the existing components, an aspect which has never been attempted in earlier studies.

title: Evaluating Robustness and Adaptability in Learning-Based Mission Planning for Active Debris Removal
authors: Agni Bandyopadhyay, Günther Waxenegger-Wilfing
abstract: Autonomous mission planning for Active Debris Removal (ADR) must balance efficiency, adaptability, and strict feasibility constraints on fuel and mission duration. This work compares three planners for the constrained multi-debris rendezvous problem in Low Earth Orbit: a nominal Masked Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) policy trained under fixed mission parameters, a domain-randomized Masked PPO policy trained across varying mission constraints for improved robustness, and a plain Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) baseline. Evaluations are conducted in a high-fidelity orbital simulation with refueling, realistic transfer dynamics, and randomized debris fields across 300 test cases in nominal, reduced fuel, and reduced mission time scenarios.
