Results show that nominal PPO achieves top performance when conditions match training but degrades sharply under distributional shift, while domain-randomized PPO exhibits improved adaptability with only moderate loss in nominal performance. MCTS consistently handles constraint changes best due to online replanning but incurs orders-of-magnitude higher computation time. The findings underline a trade-off between the speed of learned policies and the adaptability of search-based methods, and suggest that combining training-time diversity with online planning could be a promising path for future resilient ADR mission planners.

title: Novel UVM-Based Verification of a Short BCH Encoder and Decoder for Radiation-Tolerant Memory Arrays
authors: Jamuna S, Hannan Ashrafi, T Mohammad Nisar, Suraj Malagar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: As circuit complexity continues to grow with modern VLSI design, verification needs increase in rigor and necessity. The Universal Verification Methodology (UVM) provides a systematic, coverage-driven approach for validating digital systems. In environments where radiation is more intense, such as aerospace, single-event upsets can cause data corruption in the system’s storage, thus requiring a fault-tolerant regime which safeguards data with Error Correction Codes (ECC). This report details a shortened BCH(562,512,5) encoder–decoder system architecture appropriate for these types of fault-tolerant memory applications. The encoder is based on a serial LFSR, while the decoder utilizes an inversionless Berlekamp–Massey–Chien approach optimized for deterministic latency and reduced complexity. The researcher developed a verification environment based on UVM that verified claims through constrained-random testing, fault injection, and automated coverage collection. The system has reached 100% statement, branch, FSM, and toggle coverage which affirms complete functional verification. The proposed architecture provides 562 clock cycles of decoding latency and can correct five random bit errors, making it a reliable and efficient choice for radiation-tolerant memory applications in advanced VLSI systems.

title: DGFS – Deep Neural Network and Gated Recurrent Unit Based Feature Stacking for Attack Detection on User Behavior
authors: Prasanthi Kadirimangalam, Pratap Pachipulusu, P.Venkata Krishna, V.Saritha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: In the contemporary online world, it is essential to protect networks and systems from cyber-attacks. Most traditional methods cannot detect complex attacks because they are based on known criteria or signatures, and there is a need to develop with better strategies that will keep up with growing threats. In this study, an innovative technique is proposed that integrates deep learning methods such as Deep Neural Networks (DNN) and Gated recurrent unit (GRU) are employed to analyze user behavior patterns for detecting attacks. The proposed methodology integrates the feature stacking approach, in which the forecasts of numerous deep learning models are combined with other extracted features to enhance the overall detection properties. Feature stacking enables the integration of several sources of information and the use of distinct models' complementary capabilities, resulting in increased accuracy and adaptability in threat detection. Attack detection based on user behavior analysis is a proactive approach to Cybersecurity that detects irregularities and dangerous acts by analyzing system users' behavior. Assessed on CERT insider dataset, DGFS shows high accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score indicating its effectiveness over the current methods.

title: Novel Prediction Model for Air Quality And Health Risk Prediction
authors: V.Asha, A.Kalaivani, N S Sukanya, Arpana Prasad, Amulya M N, Amruth Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Current measures for assessing air quality cannot alert the general public of dangerous conditions in sufficient time. Air quality data is complicated to analyze in a timely fashion. A new modeling approach called XGBoost-L combines two of the leading-edge methods available to allow a better understanding of these complex datasets over time and shows how the relationship between air pollutants and their effects on human health occurs. Through our testing, we showed that our models performed better than current air quality monitoring systems using datasets from OpenAQ, EuroControl's ERA5 Weather Database and World Health Organization's reports on health consequences of various pollutants such as PM2.5 and NO₂. Our analyses demonstrated average accuracies of 0.9827, average precisions of 0.9828 and the average recall of 0.9827. Our research also utilized SHAP as an additional method of increasing the transparency of how our model classifies environmental factors, therefore the association of risks to human health with the classified environmental features. By merging LSTM and XGBoost methods together, we have developed a base of scientific evidence to support public health professionals when they are making decisions on issues such as air quality.

title: A Data-Driven Resilience Assessment for Integrated Energy Systems
authors: Peng Wang, Xue Li, Tao Jiang, Rufeng Zhang, Changjiang Wang
abstract: In recent years, the frequent occurrence of extreme natural disasters has posed substantial risks to integrated electricity-gas-heat energy systems (IES). Accurate resilience assessment of such systems requires evaluating a vast number of operating states using optimal energy flow (OEF), which are computationally demanding. To address this issue, this paper presents a data-driven method to accelerate resilience assessment. Specifically, a disturbance increment method is first applied to reduce failure scenario dimensionality, enabling the enumeration of only low-order failure cases. Subsequently, a Transformer-based neural network is employed as a surrogate model for the conventional OEF solver, effectively eliminating the need for full energy flow calculations. The proposed approach is validated using a modified Berry Island case study, demonstrating its accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Optimizing Sustainability in Deep Learning for Real-Time Banana Leaf Disease Detection using Densenet-201 and Alexnet Inception Network
authors: Gayathri Devi Krishnamoorthy, Sakthisudhan K, Surendiran B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Bananas are a fruit crop grown globally and account for an important part of the global production of fruits. It is imperative to detect such diseases early and correctly so as to counteract their effects and enhance banana production. Here, this research introduces a deep learning methodology AlexNet-Inception network and DenseNet-201 model for the dentification and categorization of banana leaf. This approach has considered five classes of banana leaf diseases: Black Sigatoka, Fusarium Wilt, and Banana Bract Mosaic Virus (BBrMV), Banana Bunchy Top Virus (BBTV) and healthy leaves gathered from different platforms. The AlexNet-Inception network and DenseNet-201 model were trained with different learning rates and with Quasi-Hyperbolic Adam with decoupled Weight decay (QHAdamW) and ADAM optimizer. The hyperparameter tuning concludes that AlexNet-Inception model when trained with QHAdamW optimizer for a learning rate 0.0001 produces enhanced validation accuracy of 96% with corresponding validation loss of 0.21. The system was validated with parameters that produced results with 96% accuracy, indicating that the suggested model has successfully predicted and diagnosed banana diseases. This study is intended to help farmers of banana detect diseases at an early stage, eventually helping to enhance banana production as well as agricultural productivity.

title: On Scaling Artifacts in Embedding Post-Processing: A Case Study of the Context Isolation Framework
authors: Rahul Yadav, Radhika Goriparthi, Renu Ashish Chaturvedi
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Post-processing methods are often proposed to address anisotropy in large language model embeddings for downstream tasks. In this paper, we investigate the effects of a deep, non-linear geometric transformation, which we implement as the Context Isolation Framework (CIF), to serve as a case study. The CIF applies a fixed, non-trained transformation, using a randomly initialized neural architecture whose weights are frozen after seeding. Although initial experiments on the three diverse datasets: 20 Newsgroups, Amazon Reviews, STS-B and multiple baselines, suggested remarkable performance gains, including a 168.1% increase in Adjusted Rand Index (ARI) and a 383.7% increase in inter-category Euclidean distance, further analysis revealed these improvements to be a scaling artifact. Applying L2 normalization as a control, we demonstrate that the performance gains disappear completely, with the ARI improvement dropping to a statistically insignificant 0.15%. This indicates that the transformation primarily inflates vector norms, instead of improving the angular structure of the embedding space. Our findings highlight a critical pitfall in evaluating embedding transformations and underscore the necessity of robust, normalized evaluation measures to distinguish genuine geometric improvements. This result offers a direction for future research on post-processing methods, ensuring that the reported improvements reflect meaningful geometric restructuring rather than misleading artifacts.

title: Voice-Signal based Prediction of Parkinson’s Disease with Machine Learning Models
authors: Swati Sharma, Harsh Taneja, Vivek Tomar, Sangeeta Arora, Jyotir Moy Chatterjee, Vedant Desai
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: One of the chronic diseases, Parkinson’s disease (PD), is a neurological disorder associated with ageing. It is one of the most prevalent diseases among the elderly. The symptoms of Parkinson’s disease, such as essential tremor, are visible with ageing, which is very challenging to diagnose. In this study, machine learning (ML) models are considered for sickness detection, in which we basically take the sound signal digits and graphs as an input for the models. The data set used is from the UCI MLR (Machine Learning Repository), in which 195 voice recordings from 31 individuals are considered. For the purpose, six ML models were implemented that include RF (random forest), SVM (support vector machine), KNN (K-nearest neighbour), DT (decision tree), NB (naïve bayes) and XGBoost. Parameters are chosen using GridSearchCV, and hyperparameter optimization is done using the synthetic minority sampling approach. These methods are employed to reduce overfitting and boost model performance. Among the different models used, the RF, SVM, and K-NN achieved the highest accuracy of 96.6%, RF achieves the highest F1-score of 0.962, RF and XGB show the highest recall of 0.962, and SVM and KNN achieve the best precision of 1.000. The proposed machine learning framework demonstrates high predictive accuracy for Parkinson’s disease using voice signal data. These findings will be helpful in the field of healthcare diagnostic systems for the early detection of this disease, PD, which can significantly improve patient outcomes and health.

title: How Online-Offline Integration Drives Adoption of Fashion Commerce Among Gen Z
authors: Laurencia Lynn, Michael S. Reyvinda, Maria Angelina, Yohannes Kurniawan
abstract: This study examines how physical experience and the integration of online and offline information shape the adoption of online-to-offline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{O} 2 \mathrm{O})$</tex> fashion commerce among Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Z$</tex> consumers in the Jabodetabek region of Indonesia. Data were gathered through purposive sampling from 401 respondents who had recently used online-to-offline platforms. The proposed model was analysed using partial least squares structural equation modelling (PLS-SEM). The results indicate that physical experience enhances perceived benefits while simultaneously increasing perceived risk, as consumers become more aware of potential quality discrepancies during in-store evaluation. Furthermore, the integration of online and offline information provides consistent and accurate information throughout the purchasing process, thereby increasing perceived benefit and perceived usefulness. Perceived risk is found to negatively influence purchase intention, whereas perceived benefit positively affects perceived usefulness. Among all constructs, perceived usefulness exerts the strongest positive effect on purchase intention. In line with these results, Generation Z consumers actively compare prices across channels and choose the option that offers the greatest financial benefit because they are highly value-oriented and price-sensitive.

title: Implementation of a Zero Trust Edge Blockchain Framework for IoT Security: Integrating 6G Connectivity and LoRaWAN
authors: Josephine R, Zafar Ali Khan. N, Devi. S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The Internet of Things is currently transforming into massive heterogeneous networks that require ultra high speed and low-power wireless communication between a large number of sensors and actuators. Consequently, classic perimeter oriented security has become ineffective. To address this issue, this research paper proposes Quantum Resilient Zero Trust Edge AI Blockchain, a secure IoT system design that combines edge AI, federated and swarm intelligence, post quantum cryptography, and a permissioned blockchain that works alongside zero trust enforcement practice. Edge nodes enable sixth generation wireless and Wi Fi seven integrated with LoRaWAN and incorporate a zero-trust engine that applies a hybrid convolutional neural network classifier and transformer classifier at the edge for intrusion detection, and its model updating is synchronized via swarm learning based on proof of authentication blockchain ledger. Experiments on MU IoT dataset demonstrate accuracy of ninety-nine-point one percent, macro one of ninety-eight-point seven percent, sub millisecond inference latency, and average finality of one point three seconds on the ledger. Swarm learning is shown to enhance resilience against non iid and poisoning attacks for IoT networks, and post-quantum cryptography secures model updates and IoT identity transactions on this system.

title: Strategic Bidding and Robust Pricing for Load Aggregators in Joint Electricity-Reserve Markets
authors: Jialu Li, Yixue Fan, Bingkai Huang, Rui An, Yuxiong Huang, Zhaohong Bie
abstract: With load-side entities such as load aggregators (LAs) progressively integrated into modern power market trading systems, they provide enhanced operational flexibility through market transactions and demand response mechanisms. This study proposes a two-stage optimization framework for LAs to enhance power system flexibility with increasing renewable integration. In Stage 1, LAs strategically bid in day-ahead energy and reserve markets via a bilevel model: the upper-level maximizes profits through multi-segment bidding, while the lower-level clears markets for optimal dispatch and locational marginal prices (LMPs), enabling LAs to provide upward/downward reserves via flexible load coordination. Post-market-clearing, Stage 2 employs a robust pricing model where LAs optimize end-users incentive prices using an uncertainty budget (Ω) to ensure contractual fulfillment under worst-case response deviations. Case studies on an IEEE 9-bus system demonstrate that LAs balance peak supply-demand via reserve markets, while robust pricing secures profits across scenarios. This framework boosts LA revenue and demand response capabilities, offering a novel load-side solution for grid flexibility.

title: A Mental Health Chatbot with Bitmoji-based Emotional Avatars for Enhanced Empathetic Communication
authors: T. Rajkumar, A. Kavitha, Sarath C, Satheesh M, Yokesh G D
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Young adults face increasing rates of mental health issues, which require immediate help through supportive environments that combat discrimination.This paper talks about the design and concept of an AI-powered mental health chatbot. It facilitates fun and emotionally intelligent communication by using Bitmoji style emotional avatars. These avatars exhibit empathy and emotional feedback with pleasant facial expressions and gestures. The system relies on Natural Language Understanding (NLU) and emotion detection to analyse user input and understand what their current state of emotion is. By integrating conversational AI with avatar-based emotional responses, the chatbot provides a more human-like, comforting and personalized experience. This approach lowers barriers that are experienced in traditional mental health interactions and encourages open communication and further emotional connection. This system demonstrates the possibility of integration of affective computing and visual communication in a digital mental health solutions to facilitate the positive emotional health, increasing engagement and building user trust with expressive visual signals with empathetic interaction.

title: A New Era in Mobile User Prediction: Federated Synthetic Data Meets Transformer-GNNs
authors: V. Vishnu Priya, Nisanth G, Mithun V, Sugavaneshwaran P, Yaswanth P S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Mobile usage data provide valuable insight into individual behavioral patterns, including screen engagement, application preferences, and daily activity rhythms. This paper proposes a privacy-preserving hybrid learning framework that integrates Transformer networks with Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to predict mobile user behavior effectively. Temporal dependencies in app usage and screen time are modeled using self-attention mechanisms, while relational patterns among users and applications are captured through graph-based learning. To ensure user data confidentiality, the framework is trained using a federated learning strategy that eliminates the need for centralized raw data storage. Challenges such as class imbalance and cold-start scenarios are mitigated through synthetic data augmentation using CTGAN and TimeGAN, enabling improved representation of rare behavioral patterns. The proposed model is evaluated on a comprehensive mobile usage dataset comprising screen time statistics, application diversity, battery consumption, and demographic attributes. Experimental results demonstrate a classification accuracy of 87.2%, outperforming LSTM-based, Transformer-only, and GNN-only baselines by 3–8%. Beyond predictive performance, the framework categorizes users into interpretable behavioral profiles, facilitating practical applications such as digital wellbeing alerts, personalized usage recommendations, and productivity-oriented interventions. The study highlights the potential of hybrid, privacy-aware AI systems to support responsible and user-centric mobile behavior analysis.

title: Smart Care Scheduler : AI driven healthcare appointment system with disease prediction
authors: Janhavi Baikerikar, Nilesh Ghavate, Vaishali Kavathekar, Afzal Siddique, Mrudul Arkadi, Abhay Singh, Sumit Sawant
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Today we face major challenges due to delayed diagnosis and inefficient doctor scheduling, thus contributing to critical health risks and prolonged treatment times. Our proposed Smart Care Scheduler uses artificial intelligence to predict life threatening neurological, cardiovascular, and hepatic conditions at an early stage, ensuring proactive intervention and timely medical care. By using intelligent scheduling algorithms, the system connects patients with top-rated doctors based on real-time availability, specialization, and geographical proximity in an efficient manner. Traditional hospital appointment systems many times result in long wait times, scheduling conflicts, and sometimes over or under utilization of resources, leading to patient dissatisfaction and increased operational inefficiencies. The solution proposed by us assesses patient needs and ensures that critical appointments are allocated without any delay. Our proposed system uses machine learning models trained on extensive medical data-sets to identify early indicators of severe health conditions, thereby enhancing diagnostic accuracy and reducing preventable complications. Furthermore, the Smart Care Scheduler proposed by us enhances hospital workflow by streamlining doctor schedules, optimizing appointment slots, and minimizing idle time. Its patient interface provides real-time appointment updates, automated reminders and intelligent rescheduling options, ensuring a patient centric experience. Healthcare providers also benefit from improved resource allocation, reduced administrative burden and better decision making capabilities of our system. By integrating AI-driven predictive analytics with smart appointment scheduling, our work aims to improve hospital management systems.

title: Assessment of Critical Clearing Time for GFM-based Converters Considering Current Saturation
authors: Ruiyuan Zeng, RuishengDiao, Junyi Chen, Fangyuan Sun
abstract: Loss of synchronization of grid-forming (GFM) based-renewable energy sources (RESs) may occur during large transient processes due to their complex virtual power angle characteristics caused by the current limiter. Since the current desaturation process is neglected in conventional critical clearing time (CCT) estimation, the stability assessment results may become overly optimistic. This paper examines the reliability of the conventional method for CCT identification for the GFM-RES system, considering the effects of inconsistencies in control switching conditions. Based on the theoretical analysis, a novel criterion is proposed to improve the robustness of the CCT estimation. The correctness of the criterion is verified through high-fidelity electromagnetic transient simulations.

title: Real-Time Pricing and Adaptive Optimization for EV Charging Demand Response
authors: Jiazhi Guo, He Wang, Bian Jing, Huanan Yu, Yao Wang
abstract: The flexible energy storage characteristics of electric vehicles (EVs) make them an important object for power grid demand response. However, against the backdrop of the large-scale integration of EVs into the grid, an important challenge currently faced is how to enhance the accuracy of the guidance plan in regulating the energy of the power grid while satisfying the interests of both the EVs and the grid. In response to this problem, this paper proposes a real-time electricity price and adaptive optimization strategy for the charging demand response of EVs. Firstly, by using the real-time feedback of the EVs information to be planned and the characteristics of EV charging and discharging (C&D) behavior under this electricity price change trend, the real-time electricity price is calculated with the goal of maximizing the comprehensive income of the power grid. Then, through an adaptive variable horizon scale rolling horizon method (AVHS-RHM) proposed in this paper, with the goal of minimizing the C&D cost for users, the C&D plan for EVs is formulated. Finally, the simulation results show that, compared with the commonly used time-of-use (TOU) price, the proposed strategy reduces the peak-valley load difference by 17.6%, effectively improving the accuracy of EVs participating in the energy regulation of the power grid.

title: Exploration of T-Type D-STATCOM using Cuttlefish Optimization
authors: Siva Krishna. G, Y.Rajitha, R.Bharath Singh, P. Likith Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The current research explores the adjustment of the proportional and integral (PI) gain of T-Type converter D-STATCOM using the Cuttlefish Optimisation Technique (COT) to enhance power quality. There is a need for additional processing time and a decrease in effectiveness with a present PI controller (PIC). COT is able to deal with nonlinear challenges because it has a rapid computation rate, a small number of tuning variables, and rapid resolution. In order to achieve the targets of improving the measurement of dc voltage, an online COT learned PIC has been proposed. According to the D-STATCOM topology, the hysteresis current control strategy is the one that is utilised for the consistent switching task. The extraction of the harmonics currents generated by loads is accomplished through the utilisation of an instantaneous real and reactive power approach. The performance of the COT trained PIC is superior to that of the normal PIC with regard of enhanced power quality. The work that is being proposed is capable of being carried out within the framework of the Matlab/Simulink application platform.

title: Ensemble-Based Lung Cancer Risk Prediction with SHAP Explainability
authors: Parinitha Varshini S, Perarivazhagan R, Dharshini N, Siva Vishnu C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Lung cancer is provided to be the most fatal disease worldwide, whose hold suffocated the highest population, most notably being based on the very early diagnosis of the disease by the majority of the patients. The conventional screening tests make it difficult and expensive to reach lung cancer in certain regions. This research suggested a machine learning system by considering clinical and life-style information to forecast lung cancer susceptibility. These datasets were obtained in relation to 1157 patient records that contained sufficient clinical and behavioural details. We trained and tested several ML models of logistic regression, random forest, extra trees, gradient boosting, support vector machines, XGBoost, and LightGBM by cross-validation. We employ the stacking ensemble to harvest their strengths of predictions. The complete system attained 95.69% accuracy together with excellent precision, recall, and ROC-AUC values. This illustrates its potential for performing an efficient lung cancer risk assessment. The research, therefore, incorporates SHAP-based explainability whereby interpretable results are delivered for medical decision-making.This paper highlights that hope to reduce delay in diagnosis and enhance the patients outcomes and to find the risk of the disease at early stage and future works include validation using real hospital data.

title: Deep Learning-Based American Sign Language Recognition System with Multi-Scale CNN and LSTM Architecture
authors: Chris Jones M, Kavya A, Infant Diksha J, Akila M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Sign language is a significant communication mode among the deaf and hard-of-hearing people. The conventional sign language recognition systems usually suffer from drawbacks such as isolated gesture recognition, lack of contextual understanding, and no real-time spoken output capability. This paper, however, contributes an innovative system that converts the American Sign Language (ASL) alphabet into an entire word vocabulary by employing an integrated approach that brings together gesture recognition in real-time, sequential sentence construction, and oral exercises resulting from the text synthesized. MediaPipe is the library used for the hand landmarks detection that is very reliable and is obtained from video streams that are live, thereby giving exact spatial feature extraction. A particular Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network with self-attention layers is being trained on an ASL dataset to accomplish this work so that accurate sequential gesture interpretation gets done with the help of contextual awareness that gets enhanced. The hybrid framework that is proposed employs the combination of multi-scale CNNs and BiLSTMs to recognize signs translated into words, make meaningful sentences, and transform them into speech in real time. Those evaluations conducted in the experiment have shown that the system is able to achieve 98.57% accuracy, 0.99 precision, 0.99 recall, and 0.99 F1-score which is a very high performance compared to that of the traditional CNN, MobileNetV2, and Vision Transformer models. The success of the results supports the claims about the system's robustness, efficiency, and ability to provide easy interaction between sign language users and the rest of the community through a simple, real-time, and user-friendly interface.

title: Recurrent Augmented Lagrangian Neural Network for Security-Constrained Economic Dispatch
authors: Jiayu Han, Guofeng Wang, Chenyuan Teng, Youbing Zhang, Lei Yan, Zuyi Li
abstract: This paper proposes a novel self-supervised REcurrent Augmented Lagrangian Model (REALM) for addressing security-constrained economic dispatch (SCED) problems. Distinct from conventional feed-forward architectures that process SCED holistically, REALM operates as a sequence model and it effectively captures temporal dependencies in input data and handles time-coupled ramping up/down constraints through backpropagation. What’s more, augmented Lagrangian loss function helps REALM converge fast. Experimental validation on the IEEE 57-bus test system demonstrates REALM's superior performance, achieving significantly higher solution feasibility and optimality compared to state-of-the-art approaches. Furthermore, it can achieve the balance between the feasibility and optimality by adjusting different penalty updating methods and different embedded constraints.

title: A Lightweight Hybrid Vision Transformer and CNN Approach for Image Inpainting
authors: S. Ananthakrishnan, D. Manivannan, E. Ponmani, K. S. Suresh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This article presents a novel efficient computational approach. Utilizing the complementing capabilities of Vision Transformers (ViTs) and high-quality Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), this hybrid image inpainting architecture restores damaged portions of deployment-ready images. On devices with very few resources, this paradigm has a highly remarkable exception.. Sparse architecture of 3.4 million lightweights. Despite its compactness, the model, trained on a synthetic dataset using free masked data which occupy 0-20 percent of the image always generate powerful inpainting results. This document highlights the possibilities of optimized hybrid designs to give a good compromise between inpainting fidelity and computational performance, an important advance towards accessibility and useful image restoration solutions.

title: Design and Analysis of 16-bit Vedic Multiplier using Peres Reversible Logic Gate
authors: D Jyothish, S Nagaraj, E Kishore Kumar, G Hitesh Kumar, A Rosireddy, C Samhitha Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This paper focuses on implementation of 16-bit Vedic multiplier based on Peres reversible logic gates and analyzes its performance metrics of computation power, efficiency,speed and area optimization. The implemented high-speed multiplier architecture follows Vedic mathematics, specifically Urdhva Tiryakbhyam method. In addition,application of reversible logic gates (Peres gate in particular) contributes to lowering power requirements and heat generation due to the lack of information loss. This work proposes design of 16-bit multiplier based on the combination of Vedic multiplication and reversible logic techniques. An evaluation of the performance of the multipliers was conducted using xilinx and ModelSim. The given performance results show that the suggested design offers positive enhancements in gate count, delay, and power relative to traditional irreversible multipliers, thereby increasing the design’s applicability for low power, high-speed digital systems.The application of Peres reversible gates enhances the 16x16 Vedic Multiplier’s performance. Area decreases by 1.20%, power reduces by 13.64%, and PDP improves by 13.49% with no change in delay. This shows the system is more efficient with respect to energy and power.

title: Analysis of Transient Voltage Oscillation Mechanism and Instability Characteristics Incorporating Renewable Energy Control-Switching Dynamics
authors: Yihan Xiao, Tong Wang, Jieyi Chen, Zixuan Fan, Ansi Wang
abstract: Due to the nonlinear switching dynamics of renewable energy sources (RES), repeated low voltage ride-through (LVRT) behavior induced transient voltage oscillation phenomena has emerged in recent years, posing a substantial threat to the security and stability of modern power systems. To address this issue, the dynamic response trajectories of system corresponding to voltage instability phenomenon are accurately characterized. Moreover, analyzing the impacts of dominant parameters of transient voltage oscillation severity, Finally, simulations conducted on a provincial renewable energy station system in China verify the validity and effectiveness of the proposed conclusion.

title: Battery Sorting of Liquid Metal Batteries based on the Phase Transition Feature and Autoencoder framework
authors: Lingrong Pang, Lei Fan, Yong Zhu, Zhengwei Guo, Yang Teng, Mingwei Wang, Xiayu Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Liquid metal batteries (LMBs) are increasingly regarded as a promising alternative to conventional lithium-ion batteries for long-duration energy storage, due to the exceptionally long theoretical cycle life, and low manufacturing cost. However, their stable power output, together with atypical features such as low operating voltage and broad voltage plateaus, presents substantial challenges for cell sorting and the assembly of highly consistent battery packs. Using experimentally measured incomplete discharge data from a batch of liquid metal batteries, this study reveals that, in addition to conventional variations in capacity and internal resistance, the voltage plateau arrival time— reflecting liquid–solid phase transition kinetics—is a critical determinant of cell consistency. Accordingly, an autoencoder-based method integrated with an adaptive thresholding strategy is developed, achieving a precision of 0.875, thereby enabling high-accuracy and high-efficiency sorting of liquid metal batteries.

title: Dynamic Q-Learning Optimization for Anomaly-Aware Intelligent Healthcare Access Security Framework
authors: G. Sivagamidevi, C. Pabitha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The demand for intelligent, flexible, and safe access control systems has grown due to the quick expansion of cloud- based healthcare applications. Unauthorized access behaviors and developing abnormalities are often missed by traditional rule-based security frameworks. An intelligent healthcare access security system with adaptive learning and real-time anomaly detection capabilities is proposed in this research using a DQ-NAC (Deep Q-Network with Adaptive Clustering). To dynamically modify authorizations, spot suspicious activity, and provide secure access choices, the platform combines behavioral data analysis and reinforcement learning. Compared to traditional access monitoring techniques, experimental testing shows major improvements in anomaly detection accuracy, reaction speed, and flexibility. The results demonstrate Q-Learning’s efficacy as a lightweight, self-sufficient security optimizer for upcoming healthcare systems.

title: A Two-Stage Scenario Clustering Method for Power System Operation Risk
authors: Yan Li, Ran Sun, Hongzuo Guo, Sha Yang, Mengjiao Wu, Zhenhua Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Since operational risks are rarely considered in power system scenario generation, we propose a two-stage scenario clustering method oriented by operational risk. In the initial clustering stage, wind-PV-multi-load scenarios are generated using the Kendall Correlation Coefficient and the k-medoids algorithm. In the final clustering stage, the temporal and spatial distribution differences of loads are considered by reallocating the clustered loads. The Gaussian Mixture Model is then employed to cluster scenarios based on risk. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can obtain more representative risk-clustered scenarios and enhance the defensive capability against operational risks.

title: Explainable Vision Transformer–CNN Fusion Network for Brain Tumor Detection and Classification using MRI with Grad-CAM Visualization
authors: Vishal, Shiva Mehta, Vinay Kukreja
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is an essential part of the early diagnosis and management of treatment through accurate and explainable brain-tumor classification. The paper is a hybrid Vision Transformer-Convolutional Neural Network (ViT-CNN) architecture, which features both the local feature extraction power of CNNs and the global attention of Vision Transformers, with the interpretation of Grad-CAM visualization. The model was trained and tested on the Br35H and Figshare MRI datasets which had four types of tumors- glioma, meningioma, pituitary and no tumor. The experimental data show that the proposed ViT-CNN achieved an excellent accuracy of 98.72, precision of 98.23, recall of 98.41, F1-score of 98.31, and the mean AUC of 0.991, which is superior to the baseline models such as ResNet-50 (96.1 percent), DenseNet-121 (96.5 percent), and pure ViT (97.1 percent). The model was also stable and had a standard deviation average of less than 0.25 per cent across five folds and a Cohen k of 0.982, which is almost near perfect in terms of consistency in predicting. ROC analysis showed per-class AUCs of 0.992 (glioma), 0.988 (meningioma), 0.994 (pituitary), and 0.990 (no tumor). The smooth convergence of the training and validation curves were confirmed with a small generalization gap (< 0.02). Grad-CAM images were able to localize tumor regions based on model attention to improve clinical interpretability. The proposed ViT-CNN has 39.8 M parameters, 6.7 GFLOPs, and inference time of 8.3 ms/image, which is an accurate, efficient, and explainable diagnostic framework, thus being much more useful in real-time and reliable medical image analysis.

title: Prediction of Osteoporosis using Traditional and Deep Learning Feature Extraction and Feature Fusion and ML Classification
authors: Aditya A S, Harshit R, Kamalakannan J
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Osteoporosis is a progressive bone disorder which increases the risk of fractures and it impacts significantly the quality of life. Early detection of this particular disease is very crucial, as there are many cases where it significantly goes unnoticed and undiagnosed until its complications are severe. It is a long-term public health issue as it is symptomless until older ages, and it results in fractures and further permanent disability. Its standard diagnostic method which includes measurement of bone mineral density (BMD), are not necessarily available or affordable, particularly in underprivileged communities. Moreover, the accessible screening tools may not integrate diverse risk factors, including genetic susceptibility, diet, and lifestyle, which can result in delayed or missed diagnosis. The plan is to use various ML algorithms, which includes Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, Random Forest and Ensemble Model for its early early detection of this disease. With the enhanced early diagnosis, this project facilitates enhanced preventive care and planning for treatment, facilitating the diagnosis of osteoporosis as easier and more effective.

title: Feature Evaluation of Nvidia R555-R560 Open-Source GPU Driver Transition
authors: Nelson Lungu, Bibhuti Bhusan Dash, Utpal Chandra De, Simon Tembo, Satyendr Singh, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: There are significant effects on security, performance, and feature compatibility when Nvidia's proprietary GPU drivers are replaced by open-source alternatives in the R555-R560 series. In order to improve the security posture of open-source GPU drivers while preserving peak performance and complete feature support, this article provides a thorough approach in this article. Mitigating side-channel vulnerabilities that may occur during GPU usage is the main priority. The suggested method includes: (i) a multilayered security architecture for the systematic identification and mitigation of critical vulnerabilities; (ii) the smooth integration of sophisticated cryptographic modules with low computational overhead; and (iii) hardware-assisted preprocessors for runtime detection and monitoring of possible side-channel activity. According to experimental findings, the solution delivers strong defence against a variety of attack vectors with little performance impact on a number of benchmarks and real-world workloads. The study's conclusions are useful to developers hoping to transform the current vendor-locked, high-performance GPU architectures into open, privacy-centric designs that are in line with security and trust by design, as well as end users looking for secure GPU software stacks based on free and open-source software (FOSS) components. There is an improvement of 3.33% in throughput (GFLOPS), 8% in the latncy, 6% in the power consumption in watts, 4 % in memory bandwidth in GB/s and 4.17% in frame rate (FPS).

title: Study on the Layout of Charging Facilities in Airport Movement Area Based on Standard Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Jian Zhang, Chengcheng Wu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: With the continuous construction of "dual-carbon airports" and the steady advancement of the "oil-to-electricity" transformation at airports, the number of new energy special vehicles within airports has been increasing, which has also brought about the demand for charging pile construction. To improve the comprehensive economic benefits of charging facilities in the movement area and scientifically conduct the site selection of charging facilities, this paper establishes a charging facility site selection model with the goal of minimizing comprehensive costs, including the construction costs of charging facilities and the charging costs of electric special vehicles. It also proposes a solution method based on the Standard Particle Swarm Optimization (SPSO) Algorithm. Through a case study of a specific airport, the model is simulated and verified, which proves the scientificity and feasibility of the proposed model.

title: Automated Preprocessing and Semi-Supervised Deep Learning Segmentation Framework With Dynamic Patching for High-Resolution Satellite Imagery
authors: Tejas Dhule, Prashant Khobragade, Mrunalee Dhone
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: High-Resolution satellites pictures analyses includes numerical and arithmetical restrictions, the labelled datasets are limited, and requires hardware flexibility. Traditional subdivision approaches rely heavily on extensive manually annotated data and require specialized computing resources, limiting their practical deployment in resource – constrained environments. This research introduces a novel automated preprocessing and semi -supervised deep learning segmentation. Framework with dynamic patching capabilities for high resolution satellite imagery. The suggested approach combines automated preprocessing with Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and saturation amplification utilizing a lightweight Mobile Net-U-Net architecture that was learned using semi – supervised learning. The new dynamic patching method automatically changes the amount of patches based on the resources available on the system. This lets standard computer hardware process validation on benchmark datasets shows that it works better than state–of–the –art fully supervised methods, with 89.2% segmentation accuracy, 0.84 F1score, and 0.78 mean Intersection over Union (mIoU). It also needs 67% less computing power. The approach uses only 15% labelled data and a lot of anonymous samples to do strong land cover classification through consistency based semi-supervised learning. Results show that satellite image analysis applications for environmental monitoring, disaster response, and urban planning have become much easier to use, faster to compute, and more flexible to deploy.

title: Symmetric Image-Text Tuning with Entropy-Guided Fusion for Online Continual Learning in Non-Stationary Visual Streams
authors: Leyuan Wang, Liuyu Xiang, Yujie Wei, Yiwei Ru, Yunlong Wang, Zhaofeng He
abstract: Online continual learning studies how models learn from continuous and non-stationary data streams. In this paper, we observe that CLIP models exhibit an asymmetric image–text interaction under online continual learning. Specifically, text features of previously seen classes may introduce unfavorable supervision when paired with visual features of newly observed data, leading to catastrophic forgetting. To alleviate this issue, we propose a simple yet effective symmetric image-text tuning (SIT) strategy that removes such asymmetric text supervision during online learning. We further introduce an entropy-guided fusion (EGF) mechanism that adaptively combines predictions from the pretrained and finetuned branches based on their relative uncertainty. This design allows the model to recover pretrained knowledge when the finetuned branch becomes unreliable, while still preserving plasticity on recently observed classes when confidence is high. In addition, we present MiD-Blurry, an online continual learning benchmark that combines multiple class distribution patterns to better reflect realistic data streams with blurred temporal boundaries. Extensive experiments on standard continual learning benchmarks and the MiD-Blurry setting evaluate inference-at-any-time performance and generalization to future data. The results show that the proposed approach maintains a practical balance between adapting to new data and preserving previously learned information in realistic online learning scenarios.

title: Highly Noise-Robust Customization of SSB Format and Reception Scheme for 5G NR Sidelink-Based 60 GHz WPAN Systems
authors: Satoshi Uemori, Ryogo Okura, Yusuke Koda, Hiroshi Harada
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: This study proposes a customized sidelink synchronization signal block (SSB) and corresponding reception designs tailored for 60 GHz wireless personal area network (WPAN) systems. To enable high-data-rate transmissions among proximate fifth-generation (5G) New Radio (NR) devices, renewed 60 GHz WPAN systems based on 5G NR sidelink communications have been conceptualized to ensure compatibility with widely deployed 5G mobile communication systems. For highly reliable 60 GHz WPAN operation, continuous inter-node synchronization is required even under severely low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions below −10 dB. In this context, a noise-robust physical sidelink broadcast channel (PSBCH) tailored for 60 GHz mmWave WPANs has recently been proposed to maintain synchronization with a network coordinator under severe 60 GHz channel conditions. However, such a noise-robust PSBCH necessitates an equally robust orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) symbol timing synchronization scheme. Accordingly, this study proposes a sidelink synchronization signal format and a corresponding symbol timing synchronization procedure specifically designed for reliable detection and demodulation of noise-robust PSBCH payloads in sidelink-based 60 GHz WPANs. The proposed design achieves accurate signal detection and symbol timing estimation for the customized PSBCH even at an SNR as low as −17 dB. Furthermore, to enhance the noise robustness of PSBCH demodulation, the demodulation reference signal (DMRS) is restructured by assigning identical pilot symbols to repeated subcarrier indices. This DMRS mapping enables subcarrier-domain averaging, thereby significantly mitigating noise-induced pilot contamination in low-SNR regimes. Link-level simulations based on a recently developed indoor 60 GHz WPAN multipath channel model demonstrate that the proposed SSB format and reception design enable reliable control information broadcasting at SNRs down to −11 dB, while achieving demodulation performance comparable to that obtained with ideal symbol timing synchronization.

title: Adaptive BPSK/QPSK Switching for FSO Links by Real-Time Scintillation Index Monitoring
authors: Yining Xu, Chengze Ming, Tongling He, Zhi Liu, Haifeng Yao, Keyan Dong, Shiming Gao
abstract: An adaptive BPSK/QPSK switching scheme is proposed and experimentally demonstrated based on scintillation index (SI) monitoring using a field-programmable gate array (FPGA) to mitigate random and time-varying atmospheric channel conditions in free-space optical (FSO) communication links. The SI is real-time detected at the receiver and fed back to the transmitter. The modulation format is switched between BPSK and QPSK according to the feedback signal with a dual-threshold hysteresis mechanism. A rotating phase plate (RPP) and a variable optical attenuator (VOA) are used to simulate the dynamic atmospheric channel, which is equivalent to a 12.5-km near-ground horizontal weak-to-moderate atmospheric environment with relatively good visibility. The effective throughput is defined to evaluate the achievable link capacity. This capacity is significantly enhanced across varying weather conditions by employing format switching, yielding an improvement of up to 132% compared to fixed modulation formats on sunny days.

title: High-Performance Cross-Subject Decoding of Multiclass Rhythmic Motor Imagery Using EEG Data from 100 Subjects
authors: Yuxuan Wei, Ximing Mai, Yang Li, Ruijie Luo, Ruijia Cheng, Jianjun Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Objective: Effective cross-subject decoding is essential for reducing calibration time and enhancing the practical usability of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs). However, large inter-subject variability in EEG features poses a major challenge, particularly for motor imagery (MI) paradigms. Recent studies have shown that rhythmic MI can induce steady-state movement-related rhythms (SSMRR), which provide more structured electrophysiological features than conventional sensorimotor rhythms (SMR) and may offer a promising basis for efficient cross-subject decoding. Methods: In this study, we comprehensively explored ways to achieve high-performance cross-subject decoding based on the rhythmic MI paradigm from both model and data perspectives. Results: We achieved an encouraging cross-subject four-class decoding accuracy of 72.94%±13.80% using a streamlined multilayer perceptron (MLP)-based network on a self-collected dataset comprising 100 BCI-naïve participants. From a model perspective, networks composed of simple MLP-based functional modules can achieve results comparable to, or even superior to, those of several state-of-the-art (SOTA) models. From a data perspective, increasing the training set size substantially improves cross-subject decoding performance (from 61.78% to 72.94%). Moreover, we revealed a strong positive correlation between EEG feature consistency and cross-subject decoding accuracy, providing a physiological explanation for why enlarging the training data scale enhances cross-subject generalization. Finally, we explored strategies for selecting high-quality training data. We found that feature-consistency-based selection serves as a more reliable criterion than within-subject decoding accuracy. Significance: Overall, our study provides novel insights into cross-subject EEG decoding from the perspectives of model design, data scale and quality. The code is available in https://github.com/SJTUwyxuan/RhythmicMI-CrossSubject.

title: A Scalable Semi-Grant-Free Access Scheme in Cell-Free Massive MIMO System
authors: Chenyu Zhang, Yutong Wu, Jie Wang, Jiamin Li, Pengcheng Zhu, Dongming Wang, Xiaohu You
abstract: Semi-grant-free (SGF) access is regarded as a promising technology for next-generation networks, effectively easing the tension between massive access caused by user growth and limited communication resources. However, how to achieve efficient resource utilization through effective pairing of grant-based (GB) users and grant-free (GF) users in SGF access mechanism while ensuring system reliability is a critical challenge for enhancing overall network performance. This paper proposes a scalable SGF access scheme suitable for cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) systems. Unlike traditional SGF schemes under centralized cellular systems, this scheme first groups access points (APs) into edge distributed unit (EDU)-centric clusters, then fully leverages the macro-diversity gain and signal power sparsity in the cell-free architecture, and employs a genetic algorithm (GA) to achieve efficient pairing between GB users and GF users. This approach ensures the quality of service (QoS) for GB users while sharing their resources with GF users, optimizing the efficiency of communication resource utilization. Subsequently, closed-form expressions for the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) and outage probability of GB users and GF users under perfect and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC) are derived to evaluate the system reliability. Finally, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms the other scheduling schemes in terms of outage probability and resource utilization performance, particularly under massive access.

title: Federated Split Learning with Non-IID Data: Convergence Analysis and Resource Allocation
authors: Huiqing Ao, Hui Tian, Wanli Ni, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: The growing architectural complexity of artificial intelligence models poses significant obstacles to the realization of pervasive intelligence on resource-constrained Internet of Things devices. Federated split learning (FSL) has emerged as a promising solution that enhances training efficiency by combining the benefits of model splitting and parallel computing. However, existing FSL encounters degraded model performance and training inefficiency due to data and device heterogeneity in wireless networks. To address these issues, we propose a gradient-compensation FSL (GCFSL) scheme that leverages complementary gradient information across devices. We derive the convergence upper bound by considering the impact of data heterogeneity and split layer selection of deep neural networks. To minimize the long-term training latency while guaranteeing learning performance in resource-constrained wireless networks, we formulate a two-timescale optimization problem. By decomposing the problem into short-term and long-term subproblems, we design iterative optimization algorithms to solve them with low computational complexities. Numerical results demonstrate that our GCFSL scheme achieves superior model accuracy with low communication overhead while attaining fast convergence under both independent and identically distributed (IID) and non-IID settings, outperforming state-of-the-art benchmarks. Moreover, the proposed algorithm can achieve a low training latency compared with its non-optimized counterpart.

title: A Robust MPCC Strategy With Improved Super-Twisting Observer-Based Non-Singular Integral Terminal Sliding-Mode Speed Controller for IPMSM
authors: Yang Zhang, Sicheng Li, Sheng Huang, Chenhui Liu, Wei Zhang, Mai Zhang, Ping Yang, Bing Luo
abstract: In this paper, a robust predictive current control strategy with improved super-twisting observer-based non-singular integral terminal sliding mode speed controller (ISTO-NSITSMSC-RPCC) for permanent magnet synchronous motor is proposed to improve the system anti-disturbance performance, parameter robustness and speed tracking accuracy. First, a current prediction error compensation-based robust predictive current control strategy for current-loop is proposed. The prediction error compensation factor is limited by setting a threshold and feed-forward compensation of current prediction. Then, a novel extended ultra-local model for speed-loop is designed, the model dynamic variables are extended into linear and nonlinear components. In addition, the non-singular integral terminal sliding-mode surface (NSITSMS) is proposed and the improved super-twisting observer is designed on basis of NSITSMS. The unknown components of disturbances are observed and compensated in real-time, compared with the existing advanced strategies, the ISTO-NSITSMSC-RPCC reduces the A-phase current total harmonic distortion by approximately 32.0% and 7.5%, and reduces the average electromagnetic torque ripple by 12.2% and 5.2%, respectively, under parameter mismatch conditions. Finally, the correctness and effectiveness of the ISTO-NSITSMSC-RPCC are verified by experimental results.

title: KICGPTv2: Large Language Model with Knowledge in Context for Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Yanbin Wei, Qiushi Huang, James T. Kwok, Yu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) is an essential task aimed at mitigating the issue of incompleteness in knowledge graphs, thereby enhancing their utility for various downstream applications. Existing KGC models predominantly fall into two categories: structure-based and semantic-based approaches. Structure-based methods often encounter challenges with long-tail entities due to the scarcity of structural information and imbalanced entity distributions. Conversely, semantic-based methods, while addressing those limitations, necessitate extensive training of language models and specific finetuning for each knowledge graph, thus constraining their practical efficiency. To alleviate those limitations in both approaches, in this paper, we propose KICGPTv2, an innovative framework that synergizes a large language model (LLM) with traditional KGC methods. This integration effectively mitigates the long-tail entity problem without incurring significant additional training overhead. Central to the KICGPTv2 model is a novel in-context learning strategy, termed Knowledge Prompt, which encodes structural knowledge into demonstrations to effectively guide the LLM. Comprehensive evaluations on various KGC tasks, including link prediction, relation prediction, and triple classification, underscore the efficacy of the KICGPTv2 model, highlighting its ability to achieve competitive performance with reduced training demands and without the need for finetuning

title: A Technique for the Characterization of the X-Ray-Induced Leakage Path in a Linear Voltage Regulator
authors: Vladimir D. Vukić
abstract: This paper presents a procedure for leakage path characterization in a power integrated circuit: LM2990T-5.0. Tested devices were irradiated in two radiation environments created by a high-energy X-ray generator. The SPICE computer simulation tool and the presented technique were used to estimate experimental data on output voltage and several parameters of the parasitic metal–oxide–semiconductor (MOS) structure. The drain–source (leakage) resistance and its accompanying drain current were also estimated, as well as the threshold voltage as a function of the absorbed total ionizing dose. Simulated values of drain current, along with the calculated drain-source and gate-source voltages of the parasitic MOS field-effect transistor (MOSFET), enabled the determination of the gate-channel aspect ratio of this device. Consequently, the oxide trap density was calculated as a function of the total ionizing dose and the surface potential of the leakage path. Data on the potential across the field oxide above the leakage path and the total accumulated space charge per unit area were used to verify the acceptor impurity concentration in the semiconductor area of the leakage path. The voltage regulators examined in the 300-kVp bremsstrahlung environment failed after absorbing lower doses of ionizing radiation than those exposed to the 83-kVp photon spectrum. An analogy with a radiation-sensitive FET (RADFET) was drawn, regarding variations in the oxide-trapped charge density along the leakage path. Consequently, the ideality factor of a leakage path was defined as a measure of the influence of the radiation-induced trapped charge on such a parasitic MOS capacitor structure.

title: ATF-MUSE: A Lightweight U-Net-Based Model with Adaptive Time-Frequency Transformer for Speech Enhancement
authors: Yongsheng Yu, Zhuoran Cai, Haowei Jiang
abstract: Speech enhancement techniques based on deep learning have demonstrated substantial improvements over traditional signal processing methods, particularly under complex non-stationary noise conditions. In this paper, we propose a novel lightweight speech enhancement model, ATF-MUSE, which is designed to more effectively model the complex interactions between temporal- and frequency-domain features, while dynamically adjusting attention weights to enhance adaptability to varying noise environments. Specifically, we introduce an ATF-Transformer module that alternately models dependencies along the temporal and frequency dimensions, thereby capturing both the global spectral patterns and long-term temporal relationships of speech signals. Moreover, we propose an Adaptive Multi-Head Attention (AMHA) mechanism that dynamically generates feature-dependent attention weights, significantly improving the model’s robustness against non-stationary noise. We evaluate the proposed ATF-MUSE model on the widely used VoiceBank-DEMAND dataset, and experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms several state-of-the-art approaches across multiple standard evaluation metrics, including PESQ, MOS, and STOI.

title: Multi Layer Jamming Detection and Classification in 6G-Enabled Satellite-Air-Ground Integrated Networks Using Machine Learning
authors: Shikhar Verma, Tiago Koketsu Rodrigues, Nei Kato, Masayuki Ariyoshi, Yohei Hasegawa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Future 6G networks aim to ensure ultra-reliable, high-throughput communication not only in dense urban environments but also across remote, rural, and disaster-prone regions. To support such ubiquitous connectivity, the Satellite-Air-Ground Integrated Network (SAGIN) has emerged as a promising multilayer architecture, integrating Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites, High Altitude Platform Stations (HAPS), Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAV), and terrestrial networks. However, the heterogeneous and dynamic nature of SAGIN significantly expands its attack surface, with multi layer jamming attacks posing a severe threat to communication reliability and network security. These jamming attacks can originate from space, air, or ground layers and may simultaneously target multiple communication domains, disrupting both data and control links. Despite their critical impact, research on the characteristics and correlations of multi layer jamming remains scarce, largely due to the lack of integrated platforms and publicly available datasets. Additionally, varying conditions across layers—such as mobility, power constraints, and spectrum usage—make real-time jamming classification extremely challenging. Traditional threshold-based approaches often result in high false alarm rates and poor generalization. To address these issues, we proposed a novel random forest based supervised machine learning classification model capable of real-time detection, source and target identification, and jamming type classification in SAGIN. To train the proposed random forest algorithm, we propose a novel synthetic dataset generation framework that models coordinated jamming scenarios under diverse SAGIN conditions. Moreover, this paper presents the first in-depth analysis of multi layer jamming characteristics and inter-layer correlations. Comprehensive evaluation results—comprising statistical analysis, correlation studies, confusion matrices, performance metrics, robustness assessment, and benchmarking against state-of-the-art machine learning models—validate the effectiveness, robustness, and generalization capability of the proposed framework.

title: AI-Powered Vision Sensing System for Real-Time Inspection of Particles in Intravenous Bags Through Multi-Object Detection-Tracking Configurations
authors: Ulises Tronco Jurado, Uriel Martinez-Hernandez
abstract: This research introduces a novel vision-based sensing system for automated inspection of particles in intravenous (IV) bags, addressing pharmaceutical quality control challenges. We present a camera-based sensing platform with an Arducam 64MP autofocus camera (OV64A40 sensor) on a custom electromechanical system, enhanced with a dual-stream transformer architecture that extends detection through continuous spatial-temporal tracking. The vision system shows an enhanced accuracy of 94.3% for detection of microscopic contaminants (0.1-5 mm), with achieved 100% F1-score for critical 0.1-1 mm particles. Transformer-based tracking achieves zero ID switches versus 4-7 in conventional DeepSORT and ByteTrack methods. The optimised architecture processes at 3.53-19.85 FPS, demonstrating feasibility for resource-constrained deployment. This advancement can significantly reduce human error, offer manufacturers flexible high-precision or high-speed configurations, and enhance production efficiency in pharmaceutical quality control.

title: IConSCEPT 2025 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)

title: Visual SLAM based Autonomous Lawn Mower
authors: Pubesh Kumaar, Ezhil R, Srinithi A, Yazhini R, Peeyush K P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The operation of the conventional lawn mowers is labor-intensive and time consuming. The main aim of proposed Visual SLAM Based Autonomous Lawn Mower is to integrate Visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) for accurate path planning and obstacle avoidance for user friendly operation of the Lawn Mower. The proposed system is also integrated with geofencing which allows the users to configure the area of operation of the Lawn Mower. The remote control as well as monitoring of the system is implemented through a web application.

title: DiffTile: High-Resolution Periodic Pattern Generation Via Splice, Focus and Enhance
authors: Xicheng Lan, Chenyu Niu, Shenghan Xu, Wenshuo Gao, Luyao Zhang, Jiaying Liu, Shuai Yang
abstract: High-resolution periodic patterns are widely used in the printing and dyeing industry. However, the manual design of such periodic patterns has considerable time and manpower costs. Recent advances in powerful image generation models, which are represented by latent diffusion models (LDMs), provide an automated solution. However, owing to the inherent randomness of the generation process and the computational complexity of LDMs, generating strictly periodic and highresolution patterns is still challenging. In this paper, a novel text-driven framework for high-resolution periodic pattern generation is proposed. For periodic pattern generation, we propose a training-free Splice-and-Focus mechanism, which constrains latent features and modifies the attention mechanism to generate natural and strictly periodic patterns. On this basis, we provide two types of generation modes: direct periodic pattern generation and periodization of arbitrary existing patterns. For high-resolution pattern generation, we propose a ReLife pipeline that integrates superresolution and image detail enhancement to improve pattern resolution while removing generative artifacts and distortions. Furthermore, for highly nonstationary patterns, we propose a compositional generation framework to design complicated patterns interactively. The experimental results show that our framework can generate high-quality patterns that meet the strict standards of the printing and dyeing industry.

title: Integration of a Human Controller Into a Digital Twin Prototype of a Chainsaw
authors: Helena Käppeler, Stefan C. Schlanderer, Edmund Marth, Gerd Bramerdorfer
abstract: In the development of resource-efficient and user-centered power tools, it is essential to identify a set of operating points corresponding to specific work processes, as performed under real-world field conditions. For power tools like chainsaws, the operating point is influenced by the applied load, which results from the interaction between the machine, the work piece, and — most critically — the user’s behavior. To effectively support the development through a digital twin (DT) prototype, it is vital to incorporate the human factor into the digital model. Current research lacks straightforward methods for modeling user behavior. Most existing literature focuses either on the DT of the product itself or on complex, large-scale approaches to modeling human behavior. This article explores the integration of a human controller (HC) framework into the DT environment for power tools. The objective is to generate added value in the early stages of the product life cycle with minimal effort, while also enabling a more detailed representation of a human twin. As a case study, the use of a chainsaw during the processing of a felled tree is examined. Relevant human behavioral characteristics are identified through empirical measurements. The functionality of the integrated HC is then verified within the DT prototype.

title: Design and FPGA Verification of a High-Speed Configurable 3D NAND Flash Controller
authors: Qiang Yan, Junshe An, Zhenxing Dong, Bing Han
abstract: The growth in data volume requires high performance and flexible storage systems that capable of fully leveraging the potential of advanced TLC and QLC 3D NAND Flash memory through sophisticated control methodologies. We present the design and FPGA-based implementation of a high-speed, configurable NAND Flash controller compliant with the ONFI 4.1 standard. To mitigate inherent latency limitations, the architecture utilizes a 32-bit parallel bus across four NAND chips and an 8-stage LUN pipelining mechanism. A key feature is its native support for multi-mode operation, enabling dynamic management of TLC/QLC chips in their standard high-capacity modes or a high-reliability SLC mode through a host AXI interface. Experimental results shows that the design achieves a sustained write throughput of up to 1.507GB/s in TLC chip of TLC mode with pipeline efficiency exceeding 94.2%, demonstrating a significant performance advancement.

title: QoS-Aware Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic CPU Pinning of Co-located Cloud Workloads
authors: Xi Tao, Dongji Lu, Weipeng Cao, Jiongjiong Gu, Zhiyuan Cai, Chuanfei Xu, Liang-Jie Zhang, Zhong Ming
abstract: In cloud computing, static resource configurations create a trade-off: tenants overprovision to avoid resource starvation, causing inefficiency and cost, while providers suffer low utilization despite high allocations. To improve efficiency, providers often use overcommitted environments where multiple workloads share hosts, but this leads to interference and potential Quality-of-Service (QoS) violations. This paper introduces a realtime dynamic control framework that mitigates interference by adaptively pinning workloads to CPU groups. Using deep reinforcement learning (DRL) with the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm, an intelligent agent continuously adjusts CPU pinning based on real-time feedback to maintain Service- Level-Agreement (SLA) compliance. Experiments under two optimization objectives—overall-performance-first and priorityperformance- first—show that the proposed approach improves overall QoS by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\approx$</tex-math></inline-formula>25% compared with static pinning. When prioritization is enabled, high-priority workloads gain significant performance improvements while lower-priority ones remain within SLA limits. These results demonstrate that a DRL-based CPU-pinning strategy effectively manages resource contention in overcommitted clouds, enhancing utilization while upholding tenant SLAs.

title: NPC-VMD: Noise-Power Constrained Variational Mode Decomposition for Robust Vision-Based Vibration Measurement
authors: Wenjie Lin, Yuyong Xiong, Zhike Peng, Shaohan Chen, Yingjie Gou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Vision-based measurement technology plays a pivotal role in fields such as structural health monitoring and aerospace engineering. However, mainstream techniques—including Digital Image Correlation, optical flow methods, and phase-based approaches—are susceptible to external environmental noise, and conventional filtering methods struggle to adequately address this issue. This paper proposes a Noise-Power Constrained Variational Mode Decomposition (NPC-VMD) method. By introducing prior noise power constraints and employing an SNR-weighted mechanism during center frequency updating, the method significantly enhances the noise robustness and accuracy of mode decomposition. Furthermore, a universal noise power calibration method suitable for vision systems is presented, which can effectively estimate background noise energy. Based on these innovations, a comprehensive experimental framework for vision-based measurement is established. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that, compared to classical VMD, NPC-VMD achieves cleaner mode separation and more accurate frequency-domain extraction even under strong noise conditions, providing a reliable solution for vision-based vibration measurement in complex environments.

title: Temperature Characteristics of High Frequency Transformers with Different Nanocrystalline Core Shapes Under Full-Load Condition
authors: Shihao Cheng, Xiaofan Zhang, Zihan Tian, Chenbo Wang, Siying Deng, Xinyu Guo, Xinbo Cao, Yu Han, Jing Wang, Haisen Zhao
abstract: High-frequency transformers (HFTs) are essential components of the power electronic equipment for electric energy conversion in smart grids. The local hotspots in the nanocrystalline core are a major challenge that hinders the development of HFTs toward large capacity and high power density. Although various core shapes are widely applied, their temperature distribution characteristics are not systematically investigated. This study develops a 3-dimentional (3-D) magnetic-thermal-fluid coupling model which accounts for the anisotropic thermal conductivity of nanocrystalline cores and the temperature-dependent conductivity of windings. The temperature distributions of the HFTs with U-core type, U-shell type, triangular, and toroidal cores under full-load condition are investigated with this multi-physics model. Furthermore, the 20-kVA prototypes with these four core shapes are designed and manufactured, and experiments are performed for validation. The results show that the U-shell core exhibits the highest operating temperature, in the range of 72°C-126°C, and the toroidal core shows the lowest temperature, in the range of 53°C-80°C, while the U-core type and triangular cores operate in the ranges of 51°C-90°C and 61°C-90°C, respectively. For the U-core type, U-shell type and triangular cores which feature cornered structures, a temperature difference of 8°C-15°C between inner and outer surfaces is observed. The achievements provide useful guidance for the core selection and thermal optimization of large-capacity HFTs.

title: CacheBlend: Fast Large Language Model Serving for RAG with Cached Knowledge Fusion
authors: Jiayi Yao, Hanchen Li, Yuhan Liu, Siddhant Ray, Yihua Cheng, Qizheng Zhang, Kuntai Du, Shan Lu, Junchen Jiang
abstract: None

title: ICAIQSA 2025 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)

title: Autonomous Planning In-space Assembly Reinforcement-learning free-flYer (APIARY) International Space Station Astrobee Testing
authors: Samantha Chapin, Kenneth Stewart, Roxana Leontie, Carl Glen Henshaw
abstract: The US Naval Research Laboratory’s (NRL’s) Autonomous Planning In-space Assembly Reinforcement-learning free-flYer (APIARY) experiment pioneers the use of reinforcement learning (RL) for control of free-flying robots in the zero-gravity (zero-G) environment of space. On Tuesday, May 27th 2025 the APIARY team conducted the first ever, to our knowledge, RL control of a free-flyer in space using the NASA Astrobee robot on-board the International Space Station (ISS). A robust 6-degrees of freedom (DOF) control policy was trained using an actor-critic Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) network within the NVIDIA Isaac Lab simulation environment, randomizing over goal poses and mass distributions to enhance robustness. This paper details the simulation testing, ground testing, and flight validation of this experiment. This on-orbit demonstration validates the transformative potential of RL for improving robotic autonomy, enabling rapid development and deployment (in minutes to hours) of tailored behaviors for space exploration, logistics, and real-time mission needs.

title: Cooperative Operations Between the Kalman Planetary Rover and an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle for Planetary Exploration
authors: Tymoteusz Glazar
abstract: Planetary exploration is often constrained by limited situational awareness and slow detection of potential hazards, especially when relying on a single ground platform and limited to its onboard sensors.
