This paper presents a concept of cooperative operations between a Planetary Rover and an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle, based on an example of the Student Planetary Rover Kalman and the Companion UAV. The proposed framework outlines an adaptable architecture for integrated air–ground cooperation, focusing on communication interfaces, autonomy sharing, and mission planning for analogue and future extraterrestrial applications.

title: AI-Powered Diagnosis of Pomegranate Diseases in Maharashtra: A Comprehensive Review of Machine and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Seema Murkar, Manoj Varshney
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Agriculture plays a vital role in India’s economy, with Maharashtra being a key contributor to the cultivation of pomegranates, a high-value crop. However, pomegranate yields are frequently impacted by diseases such as bacterial blight and fungal infections, which threaten farmers’ livelihoods and the state’s agricultural output. In this study, we examine the applications of deep learning techniques to predict pomegranate diseases accurately, enabling timely interventions. Leveraging insights from existing literature on crop disease prediction and artificial intelligence (AI) applications in agriculture, this study highlights the implementation of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transfer learning techniques for precise disease detection. A The global analysis of analogous Artificial Intelligence applications reveals how these methods can boost agricultural output levels. Our methodology uses Maharashtra’s specific data sources while optimizing predictive models according to the distinct environmental characteristics throughout the state. The system aims to detect diseases before manual inspection thresholds by minimizing farmers’ dependence on hands-on evaluation to reduce product waste while maximizing resource potential. The findings of this study advocate for deploying AI-driven solutions as part of sustainable farming practices.

title: Automated Detection and Classification of Chronic Eye Diseases Using Deep Learning
authors: Shilpi Wadhwa, Navdeep Singh
publish: 2025 10th International Conference on Research in Intelligent Computing in Engineering (RICE)
abstract: Chronic eye diseases constitute a major global health burden, with millions experiencing preventable vision loss due to limited access to specialized diagnostic care, particularly in resource-constrained regions. This study develops an advanced deep learning framework for automated detection and classification of multiple chronic eye diseases using Optical Coherence Tomography imaging. We comprehensively evaluate ten state-of-the-art architectures, including traditional CNNs, Vision Transformers, and hybrid models, across accuracy, precision, recall, training efficiency, and parameter efficiency metrics. Results demonstrate that ConvNeXt-Base achieves superior diagnostic performance (accuracy: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>, precision: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4 \%}$</tex>, recall: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2 \%}$</tex>), while lightweight architectures such as Attention-Enhanced CNN and EfficientNet variants exhibit optimal computational efficiency. The analysis reveals a fundamental trade-off between predictive accuracy and resource utilization, with training times ranging from 0.26 to 0.83 hours. These findings provide evidencebased guidance for context-specific model deployment: highperformance transformers for tertiary care facilities and efficient CNNs for telemedicine and mobile screening programs. The automated framework enables scalable, equitable disease screening in underserved populations, facilitating early detection critical for preventing irreversible vision loss and advancing global efforts toward accessible, quality ophthalmic care.

title: An Analysis of Deep Learning and Multimodal Deep Learning Methods for Crime Prediction from Social Media Data
authors: Banumathi P, Manoharan S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Crime prediction aims to identify and reduce future criminal activities, assisting law enforcement agencies in preventing crime before it occurs. Social media has emerged as a widely used platform where individuals share their opinions and emotions; perspectives and feedback about crimes are very useful data for crime predictions. Nevertheless, challenges such as data imbalance of various crime types, noisy or incomplete information and the dynamic nature of criminal behavior make crime prediction complex. These issues were successfully solved and efficient crime prediction was achieved by many DL-based models in the literature. Deep Learning (DL) models can handle large amounts of data and automatically identify relevant features for finding crime patterns with high prediction rates. The literature also analysed various multimodal DL-based crime forecasting models. Multimodal DL models incorporate features from text, photos, and videos to predict crime. The combination of varied sources enables more extensive contextual knowledge for crime prediction. This paper analyzed the strengths and weaknesses of various DL models. Then, compare the performance metrics achieved by those methods. The analysis of various DL models provides a broad knowledge about the data types and various operations involved in DL models. Finally, the study provides a few suggestions for future work in DL models for crime prediction. This study enables researchers to identify existing gaps and gives them a road map for identifying innovative solutions for DL-based crime detection models.

title: Efficient Ads Ranking Using Distilled Large Language Models and Transformer Architectures
authors: Arpita Vasant Shah, Sargam Menghani, Shuxian Yu, Patrick Jordan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Science and Applications in Industry and Society (CAISAIS)
abstract: Traditional user-to-ads recommendation systems rely heavily on click-based signals to train ranking models, however these signals are often sparse and noisy, leading to suboptimal relevance. To address this, we developed LLM-Ranker, a proprietary solution that leverages large language models (LLMs) to reason over complex user behaviors and infer nuanced preferences. This approach yielded significant gains in user engagement over production baselines, achieving +68.6% increase in View Click-Through Rate (ViewCTR). However, the high inference cost of LLMs limited this solution’s viability for real-time production use. In this work, we propose a scalable framework that retains LLM-Ranker’s effectiveness while enabling production-grade deployment. We introduce two efficient models trained on LLM-generated labels: (1) Distill-Ranker, a distilled version based on LLaMA-2-7B, and (2) Transformer-Ranker, a lightweight architecture optimized for low-latency inference. Offline experiments show both models closely match LLM-Ranker’s performance (Mean Reciprocal Rank at 10, MRR@10, of 0.92) while reducing inference latency by up to 190x. Our contributions with this work are (1) scalable LLM-based ranking solutions and (2) the effective use of LLMs as label generators to overcome data sparsity in traditional click-based training approaches.

title: Small-Signal Stability Analysis of Grid-Tied VSC With Two Typical Negative-Sequence Control Under Unbalanced Grid Conditions
authors: Zefei Wu, Jianhang Zhu, Jiabing Hu, Guojie Zhao, Buyang Du, Zeren Guo, Shicong Ma, Jianbo Guo
abstract: Voltage source converters (VSCs) are essential for integrating renewable energy into the grid. Their small-signal dynamics under unbalanced grid conditions have garnered significant attention. A typical approach is negative sequence (NS) control using a proportional-integral (PI) controller in the double synchronous reference frame (DSRF), known as DSRF control. Alternatively, the same control objective can be achieved using a PIR controller in the positive SRF, where a resonant term is inserted to track the NS component at twice the fundamental frequency. While the small-signal modeling and stability analysis of grid-tied VSC with DSRF control have been extensively studied, research on PIR control remains limited, and a comparative analysis of the two approaches is still lacking. To fill this gap, this paper develops a linear time-periodic (LTP) model of a grid-tied VSC with PIR control and presents a comprehensive comparison of the two typical NS control schemes using Floquet theory.

title: Artificial General Intelligence: Current Progress, Safety Considerations, and Ethical Imperatives
authors: Nayan Potdukhe, Utkarsha Pacharney, Mrinal Mankar, Atharva Waghale, Harshraj N. Gadbail, Nakul Dube
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Artificial general intelligence (AGI) describes AI systems made to be as flexible and adaptive as humans, capable of handling any intellectual task that a human being is capable of. Presenting the historical development, current technological advancements, and well-known model architectures, this review summarises the current status of AGI research. Although current systems still display limited expertise rather than fluid, human-like generalisation, the analysis discusses how large-scale machine learning and cognitive architectures converge to create more generalised capabilities. Creating hybrid architectures, neurosymbolic techniques, and embodied Agent AI that processes multimodal data are critical technological trends. The alignment problem, accountability, privacy, and workforce impacts are crucial ethical and societal issues that have received much attention. The review emphasises the general agreement regarding the necessity of strong safety procedures, proactive governance, and frameworks that guarantee human oversight. The study concludes by outlining potential application areas, such as healthcare and climate modelling, while recognising the high ambiguity in expert projections for AGI timelines. It concludes that a foundation of interdisciplinary cooperation and cautious stewardship to balance innovation and societal well-being is necessary to achieve beneficial AGI.

title: Dynamic Risk-Economic Equilibrium Strategy for Virtual Power Plant Operation via Probabilistic Feasible Regions
authors: Anni Hu, Tiance Zhang, Gengyin Li, Ming Zhou
abstract: Under high renewable energy penetration, virtual power plants (VPPs) face significant computational burdens due to large-scale stochastic data exchange. The probabilistic feasible region (PFR), defined as a confidence-bounded set of power exchanges under high-dimensional uncertainty, has emerged as a lightweight and efficient tool for information transmission. However, selecting an appropriate confidence level remains challenging due to the trade-off between risk and economy. To address this challenge, a confidence level PFR framework is proposed, to mitigate the conflicts between load-shedding risk and economic benefits in the coordination between VPPs and power system operators (PSOs). Through a PFR equivalent projection method, the modeling complexity induced by high-dimensional uncertainties is effectively reduced. Furthermore, a confidence–risk–revenue dynamic equilibrium model is established within a variational inequality framework, enabling the adaptive determination of the optimal time-varying confidence level to balance the strategic interactions between PSOs and VPPs. The proposed method facilitates accurate and efficient information exchange. Case studies based on modified IEEE 33-bus distribution network and 30-bus transmission network demonstrate the effectiveness and computational efficiency of the proposed approach.

title: Distribution Network Reconfiguration with Time Period Partitioning Based on Fused Information Entropy
authors: Zhengfeng He
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To improve the operational economy and voltage quality of distribution networks with new energy sources, and address traditional reconfiguration’s failure to fully consider load time-period characteristics and multi-dimensional costs, this study proposes a reconfiguration model integrating information entropy-based equivalent load partitioning, and demand response impacts. Targeting minimal total operational costs (network loss, curtailment, switch operation), it is transformed into a mixed-integer second-order cone programming problem via the big M method and second-order cone relaxation, solved with YALMIP/GUROBI. Simulations on the modified IEEE 33-bus system show the proposed strategy reduces total cost by 20.41% and increases average node voltage by 2.48%, providing theoretical/technical support for optimal operation.

title: Frequency-Constrained Planning of Energy Storage with Adaptive Frequency Control Strategy
authors: Weiyu Li, Haiwang Zhong, Yiliu He, Baorong Zhou, Siyu Lu, Yingjun Zhuo
abstract: High penetration of renewable energy sources (RESs) poses major challenges to frequency security, highlighting the need for coordinated planning of frequency support and flexibility resources. This paper proposes a frequency-constrained planning model for battery energy storage systems (BESSs) that incorporates adaptive frequency control strategy. A BESS frequency response model with virtual inertia and droop control is integrated into system frequency response model. The nonlinear frequency nadir constraint is linearized using a piecewise-linear method. To enable dynamic and flexible frequency support, BESS frequency control parameters are treated as decision variables within a frequency-constrained unit commitment model. The proposed framework co-optimizes BESS sizing by minimizing both operational and investment costs. A case study based on the modified RTS-GMLC test system demonstrates that the proposed model effectively enhances frequency security, improves BESS operational flexibility, and reduces overall system costs.

title: Plant Disease Classification using YOLOv8 Algorithm
authors: J. Mythili, R Gopalakrishnan, B Saravanan, S Sridhar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Tomato leaf diseases remain a major concern in agricultural production, as delayed diagnosis can lead to reduced yield and quality. Manual inspection methods are often inefficient and difficult to apply on a large scale, especially in time-sensitive farming environments. To overcome these challenges, this study introduces a real-time tomato leaf disease detection system based on the YOLOv8 object detection framework. The proposed approach enables automated analysis of leaf images by detecting visible disease symptoms and identifying the corresponding disease categories in a single processing step. Experimental evaluation shows that the system delivers reliable detection performance and achieves improved accuracy, precision, and recall when compared with commonly used deep learning models such as AlexNet, LSTM, and BiGRU. To support practical usage, the trained model is deployed through a web-based interface that provides rapid diagnostic results and visual feedback, assisting farmers and agricultural practitioners in making timely disease management decisions.

title: Machine Vision based Chili Species Recognition
authors: Md. Ataur Rahman, Zaed Bin Monir Atib, Md Shamim Hossen, Saddam Hossen Sourov, Md Abdul Alim, Md. Tarek Habib
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive exploration of automated chili species classification using deep learning. We cover the full pipeline—data collection, preprocessing, model selection, and experimental analysis. A dataset of chili pepper images spanning multiple species was manually curated and augmented to increase diversity and size, then split 80/20 into training and test sets. Three state-of-the-art CNN backbones, InceptionV3, MobileNetV2, and Xception, were employed with transfer learning, initializing from large-scale image recognition weights. Each model was fine-tuned and optimized for accurate chili classification. Experiments yielded strong results, with the best configuration achieving 93% test accuracy. For each species, we report precision, recall, and F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>-score, showing consistent performance across classes and validating both the model choices and the augmentation strategy. Beyond the technical results, the work has practical implications for agriculture, biodiversity research, and plant breeding programs. An automated chili species classification system can streamline field operations, enable precise species identification, and support monitoring of plant diversity and conservation efforts. Overall, our findings demonstrate that modern deep learning, coupled with careful dataset design, provides an effective and scalable approach to fine-grained plant identification. The approach is lightweight enough for deployment on modest hardware and can be extended to related crops with minimal additional labeling effort.

title: Serverless Cloud Cost Optimizer
authors: Aryan Dubey, Vivek Chindalia, Soham Patil, Sareen Deore
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Cloud computing has reshaped modern application deployment by enabling serverless execution models that automate scalability and infrastructure management. Despite these advantages, the abstraction often obscures resource inefficiencies, idle components, and limited cost visibility across distributed cloud services, leading to avoidable operational expenditure. This paper presents a Serverless Monitoring Dashboard, a lightweight and centralized system designed to provide real-time observability into cloud performance, usage, and cost behaviour. The platform aggregates key operational metrics such as invocation frequency, CPU utilization, storage activity, and service-level spending and visualizes them through a unified interface to support efficient monitoring and analysis. By integrating real-time data with persistent historical records, the proposed system improves transparency, reduces manual oversight, and enables data-driven optimization of cloud resources. Experimental evaluation demonstrates enhanced operational visibility and measurable reductions in cloud expenditure, highlighting the system’s effectiveness in promoting efficient and sustainable serverless cloud management.

title: AI-Enabled Zero-Trust Security for Omnichannel CRM Platforms
authors: Sitaram Srivatsavai, Karthik Bodducherla, Vijaya Sai Munduru
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This paper explores the implementation of ZeroTrust Architecture (ZTA) and AI to help augment cyber-handshakes in omnichannel CRM frameworks. As these businesses become more reliant on systems connected to run the customer experience across multiple channels, the attack surface for possible penetration swells. This study introduces a novel security model using continuous authentication, least-privilege access model and AI-based anomaly detection it manages to reduce unauthorized data exfiltration threats. For the experiment it uses a synthetic dataset with 457 data samples which model various user login behaviors, transaction sequences and API access logs. Simulation in Python, predictive modeling using TensorFlow and visualization with Tableau were used to investigate the effectiveness of the proposed controls. Results show that ZTA by itself combined with listen/watch real-time AI monitoring is an excellent deterrent, significantly cutting down false positives and speeding up response to incidents. CRM and similar platforms whose defenses have traditionally been perimeter, and static-based benefit greatly from a more dynamic, identity-aware security, it makes them much more resilient. The results offer actionable advice for IT architects and security compliance officers who want to secure their customer data ecosystems against modern cyber threats, without jeopardizing a frictionless user experience.

title: Designing Adaptive Lightweight Encryption Protocols for Secure Multi-Device Communication in Resource-Constrained IoT Architectures
authors: Vishakha D Bhandarkar, Subhasis Patra, Naveen Malik, Al-Hussein Maysir Majid, D. Jyothi Preshiya, B Kiran Bala
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The proliferation of IoT devices presents security considerations due to the limited resources available to provide efficient communication. The purpose of this research is to come up with a secure and practical encryption architecture specific to the multi-device IoT systems. As compared to the static approaches, the presented way adjusts the encryption according to the parameters of the device in real-time. The ALiGKM framework combines adaptive and lightweight security with scalable group key management to tackle the changing resource constraints in the system on the one hand and on the other hand to provide inter-device communication security. Using the UNSW-NB15 dataset, the framework was able to score an accuracy of 98.67% with minimal encryption time, memory, CPU and energy consumption. The findings validate that ALiGKM is a strong and effective implementation of the secure access of resource-restricted IoT environments with low overheads and customization.

title: Inertia Estimation for Stability-Constrained Economic Dispatch of IBR Penetrated Grid
authors: Prasant Basnet, Xin Fang
abstract: The increasing adoption of inverter-based resources (IBRs) in power systems has prompted the need for controllable virtual inertia provision to stabilize frequency. Therefore, it is imperative to conduct a comprehensive analysis and estimation of inertia in the IBR-penetrated power system to ensure adequate dispatchable inertia capacity in real time. This paper presents a comparative analysis of system identification methods for estimating inertia of inverter-based resources (IBRs) under continuous, real-time operating conditions. In addition, a stability-constrained dispatch framework has been adopted to optimize the virtual inertia provision of IBRs. Meanwhile, an inertia estimation is performed after each dispatch period to validate the virtual inertia provided by IBRs. Inertia estimation approaches are validated using the IEEE-39 bus system. The results demonstrate higher accuracy in inertia estimation when using the least-squares method with the exact measurement window size. Consequently, this technique is integral to procure adaptive virtual inertia from IBRs in the stability-constrained economic dispatch framework.

title: AI-Based Pollen Detection for Montenegrin Honey Authenticity Verification
authors: Andrea Milačić, Elvis Taruh, Nadja Raičević, Stevan Čakić, Sanja Savković, Aleksandra Martinović, Tomo Popović
abstract: Melissopalynology, the microscopic analysis of pollen in honey, remains the reference method for determining botanical origin, but it is highly time-consuming and strongly dependent on expert knowledge. The PollenTrace project addresses the need for faster, scalable analytical solutions by developing a large-scale pollen microscopy dataset and an AI-assisted detection pipeline. The project encompasses 1,100 biological samples (600 honey, 500 plant-source) collected over five years across Montenegro, from which a target dataset of over 33,000 high-resolution microscopy images is being constructed. Using standardized imaging protocols (Olympus BX43, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\times 400$</tex> magnification) and the Roboflow annotation platform, over 150 images have been annotated to date, with 600 targeted by project completion. For proof-of-concept validation, the initial subset of annotated images (expanded three times via augmentation) was used to train a YOLOv11 detection model, achieving 84 % precision, 88 % recall, 97% mAP©50, and 84% mAP © 50-95 for chestnut pollen detection. These preliminary results demonstrate pipeline feasibility and establish a foundation for comprehensive model development as annotation coverage expands.

title: Gabor-ViT: A Parameter-Efficient Multi-Stream Vision Transformer for Remote Sensing Scene Classification
authors: Vikrant Rajendra Shinde, Akansha Chaudhuri, Avinash Chandra, Hemprasad Yashwant Patil, Vinaya Shinde, B. Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Remote Sensing Scene Classification (RSSC) is a pivotal challenge in Earth observation; however, the complete retraining of massive foundation models like Vision Transformers (ViTs) is frequently computationally impractical. To address these limitations, we propose Gabor-ViT, a novel and parameter-efficient multi-stream architecture that combines classical Gabor filters with a modern ViT backbone. Our method enriches the input to a frozen ViT by generating parallel data streams—one with the original RGB image and two derived from a Gabor filter bank—to explicitly capture textural and directional features. For adaptation, we employ (IA)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, a lightweight parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) technique that modifies a minimal subset of parameters by rescaling internal activations. We evaluate our approach on benchmark dataset using a stratified 80:20 traintest split. Gabor-ViT achieves a competitive accuracy of 97.45% on the AID dataset. These results demonstrate that Gabor-ViT provides an effective and computationally efficient solution for RSSC, achieving high performance while drastically reducing the number of trainable parameters.

title: A Unified RF-DETR Framework Integrating Super Resolution and Temporal Fusion for Small Object Recognition in Traffic Scenes
authors: A. Mary Jenifer, R. Balamanigandan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Smart transportation and self-driving navigation systems still have a challenging time finding and sorting minor traffic signs effectively when the road is busy. This study discusses the Unified RF-DETR Framework, which uses a transformer-based detection network to improve recognition performance for small and faraway objects in traffic scenes by combining super-resolution improvement and temporal feature fusion. We use the DETR (Detection Transformer) model as the main way to identify things to work around the problems with anchor-based approaches. It uses global self-attention to identify signs accurately, even if they are buried or in varied lighting conditions. Therefore three lightweight DL models are MobileNetV3, SqueezeNet and ViT-Tiny which are used for the classification of the identified regions. This strategy tries to find out the best tactic that can be adopted varying in terms of accuracy and speed during design. Experiments on standard traffic sign datasets demonstrate that our approach can efficiently improve the detection and recognition accuracy especially for small-sized low-resolution signs. Comparative results also show that ViT-Tiny has more competitive object classification accuracy and MobileNetV3 achieves more efficient real-time inference. These results demonstrate that the integration of transformer-based detection with super-resolution and temporal fusion is very successful.

title: Automated IoT-Based Pill Dispenser with Real-Time Remote Monitoring for Dementia patients
authors: C.S. Anita, Bhuvanesh Ravi, Chandu K, Girija S D, Hasika R, Hindhuja R, Kumari Selvi C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Medication adherence is a major challenge for individuals with dementia due to memory-related difficulties. This project presents an IoT-enabled smart pill dispenser integrated with a mobile application for caregivers. The dispenser automates pill delivery at scheduled times, detects whether the medication has been taken, and provides reminders or alerts if a dose is missed. Caregivers can remotely monitor intake history, receive real-time notifications, and dynamically update medication schedules through the mobile app. By combining automated dispensing with caregiver supervision, the system reduces the risk of missed or double doses, ensures timely medication intake, and offers greater safety and reassurance in dementia care.

title: A Game-Theoretic Approach for Users of Edge Computing: QoS-Aware and Cost-Efficient
authors: Prachi Bangade, Sumit Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: In edge computing, numerous independent users, driven by self-interest, collectively shape the system’s behavior. From the user’s perspective, the main priority is obtaining excellent service at the lowest possible price, whereas the system’s goal is to effectively allocate users to make the best use of its server resources. This letter introduces the Edge Server Selection Protocol (ESSP). ESSP effectively balances the interplay between user cost and Quality of Service (QoS), ensuring an equitable load distribution across servers in a competitive, multi-provider environment. ESSP enables users to optimize their individual objectives and rapidly attain a pure Nash equilibrium (PNE). The protocol breaks the problem down into smaller subproblems using distance-2 graph coloring, which speeds up convergence. We confirm that at least one PNE exists and assess its efficacy.

title: Graftiee: Machine Learning-Based Prediction System for Optimized Plant Grafting
authors: Geetha Rajakumari. R, Dhanalakshmi. R, Anwar Raja. A, Sathishkumar V E
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Graftiee is an application that designed for farmers to assist farmers in predicting the feasibility and profitability of grafting two plants. By analyzing factors such as climate, soil type, genomes compatibility, growth duration, and plant cost, the system provides actionable recommendations for successful grafting. The application is trained on of 100,000 rows of data for that used the Random Forest algorithm, to predict the grafting success rates, growth, and expected profit. When the predicted success rate is below 60 percent, Graftiee suggests alternative plant combinations. Users can also save recommendations as PDF files for future reference. Its empowers farmers to take decisions to maximize land use, minimize resource consumption, and increase profit.

title: Automated Inspection of Photovoltaic cells using Deep Learning Algorithm
authors: Arun Antony V, Karthikeyan T A, Kannan N, Sujeeth Kumar M S, Suhirdha K S, Sujith B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: The increasing global demand for renewable energy has increased the need for dependable and efficient inspection of photovoltaic (PV) cells. Solar deployment at scale requires automation, as manual inspection is labour-exhaustive, slow, and has the risk for human error. In this paper, we present an automated inspection framework for PV cells based on deep learning algorithms with the goal of reducing human involvement and to increase the efficiency of the inspection process. Our framework is built on the inspections performed in the base paper for the PV cells and even with the lesser computational cost and account for comparable accuracy when assessing the inspection characteristics of the PV cells, while minimizing the computational complexity of the algorithm. To ensure reliable defect classification, we utilize EfficientNet-based models with extensive data augmentation techniques and optimal training methods. Our process achieves high classification accuracy with decreased computational costs, which enables scalability and cost-effectiveness in PV cell inspection, as shown by experiments against the ELPV dataset. Additionally, using electroluminescence (EL) imaging creates a perfect identification of manufacturing defects in PV cells to detect microcracks, broken regions and inactive areas that cannot be seen with the naked eye. This reduces false negatives and reliably identifies defective cells. The lightweight aspect of the proposed model provides practicality for integration in production lines to help manufacturers automate quality control processes, reduce labor expense and visual inspections and ensure systemic product reliability.

title: Ensemble Learning for Financial Crisis Prediction: An AI Framework for Industry Application
authors: Aditya Sameer Tawade, Shwet Banktesh Tiwari, Mahek Yusuf Shah, Adarsh Manoj Pandey, Janisa Pereira
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: A downturn in the economy, if detected early and prevented from fully maturing, is a process that has gained enormous respect worldwide in this increasingly volatile global financial ecosystem. This paper implements an AI-based Financial Crisis Prediction System architecture through ensemble learning techniques to analyze and detect patterns in a detailed benchmark of GDP growth, inflation rate, interest rate, unemployment rate, public debt levels, and stock market indices for predicting the probability attached to upcoming financial crises. The preprocessed historical datasets perform imputation, scaling, and normalization so that robustness is ensured on consolidated datasets enriched with crisis labels. Predictive feature engineering creates new economic ratios with higher predictive value in eliminating redundancy. Random Forest and XGBoost come under state-of-the-art machine learning paradigms capable of learning any highly complex nonlinear relationship between financial indicators and crisis events. A Voting Classifier with a soft voting strategy combining them achieves 94.1% prediction accuracy, higher than individual models. The system integrates a next-generation modern interactive dashboard built on Next.js in real-time visualizing important financial indicators, model outputs, and predictions enabling analysts, investors, and policymakers to get timely insights hence informed decision-making processes as well as economic governance.

title: Development of Bucket Drum Excavation System with Reaction Force Control for In-Situ Resource Utilization
authors: Tomoyasu Nakano, Takuya Omura, Genya Ishigami
abstract: This paper presents the development and experimental evaluation of a bucket drum excavation system for InSitu Resource Utilization (ISRU). A custom-designed bucket drum, equipped with a vertical lift mechanism for reaction force control during excavation, has been developed and mounted on a mobile robotic platform. Experiments under varying target reaction force conditions demonstrated the controller’s suitability and effectiveness. Additionally, excavation performance was compared under two scenarios: when the robot was stationary and while it was in motion. Results showed that the excavation by bucket drum in robot motion was more energy efficient since the bucket drum can excavate fresh and uncompacted soil. These findings highlight the potential of reaction force control for the bucket drum mounted on mobile robotic platforms.

title: Sign Language Recognition Using Computer Vision and Machine Learning
authors: Gokulnath Naidu, Ritikesh Alhate, Aryan Bhatjoshi, Akash Chaudhari, Abhishek Shiwalkar
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: Sign Language Recognition (SLR) continues to be an important tool for supporting communication between deaf or hard-of-hearing individuals and people who do not understand sign language. In this work, we develop a vision-based system that combines conventional image processing with deep learning to recognize both static and moving gestures. Instead of relying only on static hand images or wearable sensors, the proposed approach uses hand segmentation, landmark extraction, and a joint CNN-RNN model to capture both the shape of the hand and its motion over time. Testing was carried out on a custom dataset containing ASL alphabets and dynamic gestures. With the expanded dataset, the system achieved an overall accuracy of about 98%, showing a clear improvement over initial CNN-only trials, which struggled with similar hand shapes when trained on limited data. The model runs at 15.48 FPS, allowing smooth real-time gesture interpretation. These results suggest that integrating temporal modeling with carefully designed preprocessing steps offers a noticeable performance gain compared to typical static-framebased methods. The system shows strong potential for use in assistive communication tools, educational platforms, and realtime translation applications.

title: Research on an Iterative Algorithm for Power System Carbon Emission Flow Based on Node-Neighborhood Characteristics and Green Electricity Traceability
authors: Hong Li, Lumin Li, Tao Wang, Jiangtao Guo, Xiang Gao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Accurate and real-time carbon measurement is an important foundation for supporting carbon reduction and the operation of the carbon market in order to achieve the "dual carbon" goal. This study proposes an iterative algorithm for carbon emission flow based on the proximity characteristics of power system nodes to address the problem that traditional methods for calculating carbon emission flow in power systems are difficult to meet the precise measurement needs of regional carbon emission sources. By constructing a mathematical model of node carbon potential, the feasibility of using node carbon potential to calculate local carbon emission flow was analyzed, and the synergistic mechanism of adjacent node carbon potential was explored. The simulation results using the IEEE 14 node system as an example show that the iterative algorithm can effectively improve the accuracy of calculations. On this basis, the study further adopts the "carbon emission flow model" to describe the distribution of carbon flow in the power grid through carbon emission intensity, thereby achieving real-time tracking and accurate characterization of green electricity trajectories in transmission and distribution lines of various voltage levels. This work provides a theoretical basis for the subsequent development of distributed carbon measurement systems.

title: Deep Physics-Informed Extreme Learning Machines for Orbit Determination
authors: Fabian Dallinger, Thomas Andert, Benedikt Aigner, Benjamin Haser
abstract: Accurate and robust Orbit Determination (OD) is foundational for numerous space robotics applications, including autonomous docking, planetary rover navigation, and aerial probe control. Traditional approaches such as Weighted Least Squares and the Extended Kalman Filter are widely used due to their statistical reliability, although they can suffer from sensitivity to poor choices of the initial conditions. Recent advances in Machine Learning, particularly Physics-Informed models, offer promising alternatives [1], [2], [3], [4], [5]. Building on earlier work applying Physics-Informed Extreme Learning Machines (PIELMs) to OD [6], [7], a similar framework is introduced here that incorporates orbital dynamics directly into the learning process. This approach is further extended with a Deep PIELM variant featuring autoencoder-based hidden layers [8] to enhance representational capacity and robustness. Comparative results demonstrate the benefits of these deeper architectures in improving OD performance while keeping training times low, positioning them as effective complements to classical techniques.

title: Sensitivity Analysis for Parameterization of Generic Wind Generator Models in Power Systems with High Renewable Penetration
authors: Giovanna S. Mayese, Tatiane C. C. Fernandes, Luis Otávio De A. Dias, Rodrigo A. Ramos, Eduardo N. Asada, Taisa Dornelas A. Calvette
abstract: The rapid global expansion of renewable energy sources is significantly altering the dynamic behavior of modern power systems. As converter-interfaced generators become more prevalent, challenges such as reduced system inertia have gained prominence. In systems with high wind penetration, a key challenge lies in the dynamic representation of wind generation due to restricted access to detailed manufacturer models. As an alternative, standardized models, such as the WECC renewable models, are commonly used. This paper proposes a sensitivity-based methodology parameterize the Type 3 second generation model, aiming to identify the most influential parameters for dynamic behavior. The proposed approach applies trajectory sensitivity analysis to identify the main parameters, followed by estimation of this set using the Differential Evolution algorithm. Results show that generic models, once properly tuned, can reproduce key dynamic responses, although some limitations still remain.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Various Mppt Techniques Coupled With DC-DC Converters Feed With BLDC Motor in Photovoltaic Systems
authors: S. Rajarajan, R. Dhanasekar, S. Dhanushkumar, D. Somesh, G. Aswin, S. Santhoshkumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: As energy needs grow steadily worldwide, the demand for non-polluting and sustainable energy resources is becoming more important. Solar power is an effective renewable solution since it is plentiful, pollution -free, and sustainable over the long run. Improving system efficiency requires efficient power conversion techniques and suitable DC-DC converter units. This paper performs a comparative performance study of the Perturb and Observe (P&O) and Incremental Conductance (INC) MPPT algorithms applied to two DC-DC converter topologies, Quadratic Boost Converter (QBC) and Conventional Boost Converter in a PV based system supplying a BLDC motor. MATLAB/Simulink is used to simulate the proposed system with a BLDC motor load, and performance parameters such as efficiency, voltage ripple, MPPT response time and motor speed stability are analyzed.

title: Accurate Detection of Fetal and Maternal Heartbeats from Abdominal ECG for Clinical Monitoring
authors: Niyan Joseph Savio Marchon
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The precise extraction of the fetal QRS (FQRS) complex, derived from the mother’s abdominal electrocardiogram (aECG), has become a critical clinical requirement for evaluating fetal wellness and distress. By calculating the Fetal Heart Rate (FHR) from the FQRS complex, clinicians can identify signs of fetal hypoxia, distress, and various other related conditions. First, we pre-process the signal in this research paper to mitigate baseline wandering and power line interference. In the subsequent step, we detail our proposed technique for extracting maternal QRS (MQRS) and FQRS from the various PhysioNet aECG databases. We optimized and fine-tuned the computed maternal and FQRS templates individually for every record from the aECG dataset. Our proposed and yet simple technique demonstrated correct evaluation and estimation of the fetal ECG (FECG) signal across most records in the PhysioNet abdominal and direct FECG database (adfecgdb) that provided FQRS annotations. The outcomes are confirmed using the direct scalp ECG data from the PhysioNet FECG database. Our proposed method achieved highly reliable performance across both events. MQRS identification showed near-perfect results (Sensitivity 100%; Precision/F1 95–100%). FQRS detection was robust, demonstrating exceptional precision (98–100%) and a high true positive rate (Sensitivity 86–98%), resulting in a strong F1 score (91–99%), thus signifying that our straightforward algorithm locates the maternal and fetal R-peak indices with considerable proficiency.

title: Gamified IoT-based STEM Education for Girls: A Machine Learning Approach to Enhancing Engagement and Retention through Data-Driven Insights
authors: Arpan Bhattacharya, Raj Sinha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: This research explores the integration of gamified Internet of Things (IoT)-based platforms into STEM education for girls by using machine learning (ML) techniques to enhance engagement and retention. The platform employs IoT sensors to offer young female learners interactive and immersive learning experiences tailored to their needs. The study employs reinforcement learning algorithms to tailor gamification approaches based on individual participant's performance metrics, the number of times they engaged with the content, the effectiveness rating for the same, or time spent in active engagements. Real-time feedback using IoT devices motivates a learner and enhances skill accomplishment through a friendly and enjoyable educational environment. Research quantified the strength of the platform by calculating key metrics related to model predictiveness. The RMSE found was 0.1327, and it can be stated that such an error reflects low prediction error. MAE is 0.0923, indicating average magnitude error in the prediction. The value of R-squared or R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is 0.7810, which means around 78.1% of the variance in race effectiveness can be explained by this model. These results represent the capability of the model to accurately predict engagement and retention outcomes. From extensive data analysis, we found patterns of considerable influence on dropout rates and participation levels that resulted in the formulation of interventions targeted at building confidence and sustaining interest in STEM disciplines among girls. This research contributes to the understanding of factors affecting female engagement in STEM and provides a framework for future initiatives aimed at increasing female participation in these critical fields. This approach will thereby empower young girls through experiential learning, resulting in a long-term fascination with STEM fields and mitigation of the gender gap with respect to these fields of study. The results of such research highlight the potential in gamified IoT-based educational interventions as transformative tools enhancing girls' learning in these areas of STEM, enabling a more inclusive and varied future in science and technology fields.

title: Energy Storage Optimal Configuration Strategy to Enhance the Renewable Energy Hosting Capacity of Distribution Network
authors: Penghui Qin, Wenping Qin, Zhilong Zhu, Ruipeng Lu, Jie Wang, Yunfeng Peng
abstract: The large-scale integration of renewable energy imposes higher requirements on the new energy hosting capacity of distribution networks. Effectively evaluating and enhancing this hosting capacity has become a critical issue in the development of modern power systems. To address this, this paper proposes a strategy for improving the new energy hosting capacity of distribution networks by coordinating the short-term operating costs and long-term investment costs of energy storage systems. First, a comprehensive life-cycle model for energy storage is established, coordinating long-term investment costs with short-term operating costs. Then, to enhance the new energy hosting capacity, an evaluation model for distribution network hosting capacity is developed, incorporating active management techniques. Finally, a two-stage optimal configuration model for energy storage is constructed, aimed at enhancing the new energy hosting capacity of the distribution network. This model achieves the optimal economic configuration of energy storage while synergistically improving hosting capacity. Case study results demonstrate that the proposed energy storage optimization method maximizes the enhancement of the new energy hosting capacity of the distribution network while ensuring economic benefits, achieving a holistic optimization and improvement of the distribution network's renewable energy absorption capability.

title: AI Legal Argument Generator
authors: Sagar Janokar, Aaryan Shelar, Arya Shedge, Rohan Sharma, Rishabh Pundir, Atharva Shinde
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Lawyers encounter difficulties in studying previous cases and developing compelling points and legal arguments under tight time constraints. To help with this difficulty, an AI-powered legal assistant is introduced, that automates two legal reasoning tasks: retrieval of comparable previous cases and provision of tailored and case-specific recommendations about legal reasoning. It does so in a multi-step way: it first collects judgments, then scrapes them from the Bombay High Court, organizes and structures the large language model (LLM)-assisted data pairs, stores the processed data in a NoSQL database, converts the process data into vector format for semantically similar cases retrieval. In the retrieval stage, the identified case law is processed with GPT-type models for context, and this processing produces case-specific, tailored strategies that address typical pitfalls to avoid, clauses to consider, and good methods of arguing. It is concluded with a discussion of our implementation, further indicating how automating phases of legal workflows can substantially improve-by reducing overall time spent on research, and increasing accuracy in legal reasoning. The paper identifies some limits outside of the simultaneously expanding LLP data set (of cases): limitations in the jurisdiction model to reflect (current) case law for strictly supervisory or binding authority and future directions in real time, multilingual and by simulation.

title: A comprehensive analysis of YOLO models for pomegranate (Punica granatum) fruit disease detection and classification
authors: V Divya, B Pakruddin, C N Manasa, Tanuja Meti
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The pomegranate, sometimes referred to as the “jewel of fruits,” is significant globally due to its nutritional and economic worth. But diseases like Alternaria, Bacterial Blight, Cercospora, and Anthracnose can drastically reduce yield and quality, posing a threat to its production. Conventional disease detection is based on visual inspections done by hand. This approach is subjective, slow, and prone to mistakes. In order to address this issue, this study employs YOLOv8 models for deep learning-based object detection. YOLOv8n (nano), YOLOv8s (small), YOLOv8m (medium), YOLOv81 (large), and YOLOv8x (extra-large) are among the models used to automatically identify pomegranate fruit problems. Healthy, Alternaria, Anthracnose, Bacterial Blight, and Cercospora are the five categories that make up the dataset used in this study. The resolution of every photograph was adjusted to $416 \times 416$. Depending on the model, batches of 8 or 16 were used for training across 50 epochs. With a mAP@50 of 98.0%, YOLOv8m (medium) outperformed the other evaluated models. The next highest percentages were 97.7% for YOLOv8n (nano), 97.6% for YOLOv8x (extra-large), 96.8% for YOLOv81 (large), and 96.6% for YOLOv8s (small). These findings show that YOLOv8, particularly the medium version, is useful for precisely identifying and categorizing pomegranate diseases in real time. Early disease identification made possible by this method enables prompt intervention by farmers and agricultural specialists. In the end, this promotes better production management, lowers losses, and enhances crop health.

title: Traditional Image Classification Using Handcrafted Features and SVM
authors: Karthik Reddy, Sarabjot Singh, Sparsh Chakraborty, Amutha S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This paper presents an image classification frame work that leverages traditional handcrafted feature extraction techniques combined with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier to distinguish between cat and dog images. The method starts with turning raw images into grayscale and using bilateral filtering. Next, different feature descriptors are extracted, such as Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG), Local Binary Patterns (LBP), Haralick features derived from Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrices, Gabor filters, Hu moments, and region based morphological properties. Each feature set is individually evaluated, and the most informative features are selected using the SelectKBest algorithm. The selected features are then used to train an SVM classifier, with hyper-parameter optimization performed using GridSearchCV. Experimental results demonstrate that while LBP features achieve the highest individual accuracy of 62.50%, the optimized SVM model using the combined features achieves an improved classification accuracy of 63.82%. The proposed pipeline effectively demonstrates how handcrafted features, when carefully combined and optimized, can offer competitive results in image classification tasks.

title: Weld Defect Detection using Image Processing and Ensemble Learning
authors: R S Jaidev, Kaavyaa Aravind, Rayhan Muhammed, Gauree Nandan V, Padmavathi S, Sujee R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Weld defect detection is vital to ensure the structural integrity and reliability of welded joints. However, variations in lighting, surface texture and defect geometry make accurate classification challenging. This paper presents an automated weld defect detection framework that integrates image processing and ensemble deep learning. The preprocessing pipeline applies geometric transformation, adaptive or median filtering to enhance defect visibility and standardize weld image orientation. For classification, a bootstrapped ensemble of DenseNet121 models is employed to improve generalization and reduce variance compared to single model approaches. Experimental results in a benchmark weld image dataset demonstrate that the proposed ensemble achieves 97.8% accuracy, outperforming ResNet50 (86.9%), EfficientNetB0 (90.9%), and single DenseNet121 (87.3%) models. The framework exhibits strong robustness and consistent precision, recall, and F1-scores across all weld categories — Good, Bad, and Defect. Highlighting its potential for industrial weld quality inspection.

title: Golden Jackal Optimization-Driven SHE Control on 13-Level SCMLI for Renewable Applications
authors: Ankit Singh, Vibhu Jately, Peeyush Kala, Jyoti Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: This study introduces a switched capacitor based multilevel inverter. The main goal is to reduce the component count, multiple dc source to achieve the 13-level waveform. The proposed topology utilized on one dc source, three capacitor with the capability of voltage boosting to twice and self-balance by common grounding without use of external circuit for charging or balancing while generating voltage level. To mitigation of lower harmonics, a selective harmonic elimination (SHE) technique used in this topology, to find the optimal switching angles are using a metaheuristic Golden Jackal Optimization (GJO) algorithm. This modulation technique effectively mitigates lower-order harmonics and improves waveform quality low to high modulation index by using GJO. For the real-world application, the proposed inverter is confirmed via a laboratory scale prototype, tested under constant and dynamic operating conditions, which works well under changes in modulation index and load transitions. Experimental results shows that the inverter achieves low total harmonic distortion (THD), robust dynamic response, and high efficiency, showcasing its suitability for the integration of renewable energy sources and applications involving low-voltage direct current.

title: A Novel Telugu Dataset for Mental Health Sentiment Analysis Using Transformer Models and Explainable AI
authors: Tharun Balaji P, Kolla Lokesh, Bhavya Sainath U, Brahma Datta D, Sreelakshmi K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: High rates of mental disorders such as depression, anxiety and stressors are a major concern and therefore the necessity to come up with diagnostic tools. These conditions are vital to identify in Indian languages like Telugu yet difficult due to the linguistic complexity of the language and the non-availability of annotated data. As a means to fill this gap, we introduce a new framework for mental health detection in Telugu text using sentiment analysis with the help of recent achievements in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and deep learning. To construct an annotated dataset of more than 53,000 statements, we translated various English mental health datasets into Telugu, which comprised seven categories: Depression, Anxiety, Stress, Suicidal, Bipolar, Personality Disorder, and Normal. We have compared a number of multilingual transformer models and fine-tuned them with Telugu-specific preprocessing methods. Moreover, we combined explainable AI techniques to provide more interpretable models. Our experiments have discovered that IndicBERTv2 became the winning model with a 78% accuracy. Besides addressing the shortage of annotated materials in local languages, our project would offer a background of early diagnosis and intervention strategies, which would make a more positive change in the mental health outcomes of underrepresented groups.

title: Machine Learning-Based Obesity Detection Using WBANs for Continuous Health Monitoring
authors: Suvedhaa A, Dhurga Suruthiga K, Vikneswari R, Pabitha B, V.Vani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Obesity is one of the fastest-growing health risks today, especially in countries like India, where lifestyle changes and poor physical activity are becoming common. Obesity causes diseases such as diabetes, heart disease, and high blood pressure. Regular health check-ups sometimes miss early symptoms. There is a need for smarter, continuous monitoring systems. This work proposes a health monitoring solution that uses Wireless Body Area Networks (WBANs) with machine learning (ML) techniques to identify obesity levels and detect irregular health patterns. A Real-world obesity dataset was used, containing personal and habit features like age, weight, height, food habits, physical activity, and family history. These were used to imitate data that wearable sensors may collect. Several ML models were trained and tested-including Support Vector Machine (SVM), K- Nearest Neighbours (KNN). Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Logistic Regression (LR), and XGBoost - as well as a hybrid SVM+KNN model. The models were evaluated using various performance metrics. Among them, XGBoost gave the best overall accuracy of around 96%, followed by Random Forest and SVM. The hybrid model offered stable and reliable results in borderline cases. By integrating wearable sensors along with ML algorithms, the proposed system supports the detection of obesity-related risks beforehand and provides continuous health status, especially useful in remote and mobile healthcare settings.

title: Research on Frequency Support Control Strategy of Distributed Energy Storage Units in Microgrids
authors: Mingtao He, Mingyu Yan
abstract: When a microgrid operates in islanded mode, fluctuations in renewable energy generation and load can lead to frequency deviations or even system collapse. Distributed energy storage units (DESUs), characterized by small capacities, large quantities, rapid response can effectively mitigate these fluctuations. This paper proposes a coordinated control strategy for DESUs considering state-of-charge (SoC) differences. The strategy can achieve fast frequency support without steady-state errors in microgrids. Moreover, the proposed strategy ensures rational power distribution among DESUs, promoting convergence of their SoC values. In this paper, an islanded microgrid simulation model has been established in MATLAB/Simulink. A clear comparison is made between the simulation results of the traditional control strategy and the one proposed in this paper. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the control strategy proposed in this paper, and highlighting its significant contribution to the stable operation of microgrids.

title: AI for Applications in Public Safety: A Comprehensive Review of Models, Risks and Validation Techniques
authors: Anuj Arora, Jimmy Singla
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: With its ability to facilitate early identification of risks, sophisticated monitoring, optimal response to emergencies and decision based on data, artificial intelligence (AI) has become a revolutionary tool for public safety systems. A massive amount of diverse information collected by wireless communications, spatial devices, sensors and surveillance cameras can be processed by artificial intelligence based public safety platforms using modern data analytics, deep learning and machine learning. The adoption of such models is still difficult with major improvements because of problems with governance, adaptability, accessibility, privacy, bias and quality of data. Therefore, this work provides a comprehensive review of AI for applications in public safety. Moreover, the different existing public safety systems based on AI are also compared with each other to examine which AI approach is outperforming. Furthermore, this paper also detects potential risks in public safety AI based systems and explanation of validation processes in order to ensure the required reliability of these platforms.

title: A Fast Calculation Method for Assessing Line Fault Outage Factor in Cascading Failures
authors: Pengyi Fan, Tao Yu, Zhenning Pan, Ziyao Wang, Yufeng Wu, Wencong Xiao
abstract: Cascading failures are a significant cause of large-scale blackouts in power grids. Efficiently identifying the outage areas caused by branch faults during the propagation of cascading failures and calculating the load loss values is of great significance for taking targeted emergency measures. This paper proposes an efficient method for assessing the line fault outage factor (LFOF). By solving the pseudoinverse of the network admittance matrix at each stage of cascading failures, the virtual power transfer distribution factor (v-PTDF) and the virtual line outage distribution factor (v-LODF) are obtained. The virtual power distribution is then derived from these two factors, and the outage areas and line fault outage factor (LFOF) can be identified based on the nodal deviations under the virtual power distribution. Compared with the traditional network reconfiguration method based on mixed-integer programming, the proposed method improves computational efficiency while ensuring accuracy. Tests on power grids of different sizes have verified the efficiency and accuracy of the proposed method.

title: Exploring the Feasible Regulation Region of Building HVAC Systems within Hygro-Thermal Comfort
authors: Taoyi Qi, Hongxun Hui, Yonghua Song
abstract: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) systems are widely recognized as promising demand response (DR) resources for enhancing grid flexibility. Despite extensive research on evaluating the regulation capacity of HVAC systems, the role of humidity in air dynamic description and occupant comfort assessment during DR has been overlooked. This paper addresses these gaps by exploring the feasible regulation region of HVAC systems while meeting occupant hygro-thermal comfort. Firstly, a comprehensive model of HVAC systems is developed to describe the hygro-thermal air dynamics and equipment characteristics. Subsequently, the hygro-thermal comfort area is defined to establish permissible temperature and humidity boundaries. Based on key constraints, the hygro-thermal air dynamics are categorized into 3 distinct types. Moreover, a novel criterion is proposed to identify these dynamics without requiring full knowledge of boundary conditions. On this basis, the maximum regulation capacities are quantitatively evaluated to explore the feasible regulation region throughout the day. Finally, numerical case studies are conducted to validate the proposed approach. The results demonstrate that humidity plays a crucial role in the operating power and the feasible regulation region of HVAC systems. Incorporating humidity improves hygro-thermal comfort, thereby encouraging more occupant participation in DR programs.

title: Effective IMU Sensing and Servo Actuation Delay Estimation for Inertially Stabilized Platform
authors: Yi Chiew Han
abstract: Inertially Stabilized Platforms (ISPs) rely on motion sensors and actuators to maintain output stability, but sensor measurement, signal processing, and actuator response introduce combined delay, causing compensating motion to lag behind disturbances and impairing stabilization performance. Although numerous studies model IMU behavior, servo dynamics, and stabilization control strategies, very few works directly quantify the effective delay in an ISP. This work provides a systematic experimental method to measure this delay using a combined IMU-servo setup and an optical motion-tracking reference. Three experiments were implemented: (1) baseline stabilization with direct servo commands to establish ideal zero sensor and processor delay performance and verify the absence of additional system delays; (2) active stabilization using real-time IMU measurements, which showed significant degradation compared to baseline, with significantly increased peak-to-peak displacement and prolonged deviations above 5°; and (3) predictive stabilization using future angle estimation, which restored near-baseline stability and allowed estimation of the effective sensor-actuator delay. The results demonstrate that even modest delays of tens of milliseconds can lead to severe instability, and that estimation of effective delay provides essential insight into hardware limitations and guides the design of both stabilization algorithms and low-latency ISP system.

title: Exploiting Beam-Split Effect in IRS-Aided Systems via Opportunistic OFDMA: Design and Analysis
authors: L. Yashvanth, Chandra R. Murthy
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges caused by employing phased-array-based intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRSs) for wideband beamforming applications at mmWave frequencies. When a large IRS is used in conjunction with a wideband signal, a phenomenon known as the beam-split (B-SP) effect arises wherein the IRS fails to focus its reflected energy toward a single user equipment (UE) across the entire signal bandwidth, resulting in a dramatic reduction in array gain and throughput. While B-SP is commonly regarded as a performance-limiting impairment, in this paper, we take an optimistic viewpoint and exploit the B-SP to improve system performance using orthogonal frequency division multiple access (OFDMA). We first show that, under the B-SP effect, the IRS can inherently exhibit a multi-directional beamforming behavior, directing its energy toward different spatial angles at different frequency components. Capitalizing on this property, we then propose an opportunistic OFDMA scheme wherein the IRS phase shifts are randomly configured, and multiple UEs are opportunistically scheduled over different subcarriers (SCs) using a max-rate scheduling policy. We rigorously prove that, when the number of UEs is large, we can almost surely procure the optimal array gain on all SCs (possibly at different UEs) by exploiting the B-SP effect. Subsequently, we characterize a sufficient condition on the number of UEs to witness near-optimal gains in practical systems. Then, we demonstrate that the proposed OFDMA scheme offers multi-user diversity benefits, resulting in further throughput enhancements beyond those achieved through optimal beamforming. Finally, we also show how our idea extends to near-field propagation scenarios. Numerical simulations reveal that the OFDMA scheme outperforms the existing IRS-aided wideband beamforming solutions at low cost and complexity.

title: Development of an All-solid-state Silver Ion-selective Electrode based on the Polypyrrole/Multi-walled Carbon Nanotubes for Thrombin Detection
authors: Dong Jiang, Feng Tu, Qingjiang Wang
abstract: All-solid-state ion-selective electrodes have been rapidly developed in recent years due to their advantages of simple fabrication, fast response, and ease of miniaturization and array formation. However, issues, including low ion-electron conversion efficiency at the interface between the electrode substrate and the selective membrane, small double-layer capacitance, and the formation of a water layer may lead to poor potential stability. This work developed an all-solid-state A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> ion-selective electrode (A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>-ISE) utilizing a polypyrrole/multi-walled carbon nanotube (PPY-MWCNTs) nanocomposite as the solid contact layer. By combining the excellent conductivity of polypyrrole with the high capacitance of carbon nanotubes, the developed A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>-ISE effectively enhanced ion-electron transfer while suppressing water layer formation, thereby exhibiting outstanding potential stability. It exhibited a linear response to A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> in the concentration range of 1.0×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> to 1.0×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> mol/L, with a slope of 48.0±1.7 mV/dec and a detection limit of 7.1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-7</sup> mol/L. Relying on the readily oxidizable nature of silver nanoparticles (A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub> NP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</sub>), an indirect detection strategy has been proposed for thrombin. Through the specific recognition between thrombin and its two aptamers (Aptamer 15 and 29), a sandwich-type nanocomplex (Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>-A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">pt</sub> 15/thrombin/A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"/>pt 29-A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub>@S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>) was constructed. Following magnetic separation, A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub> N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ps</sub> within the nanocomplex were oxidized by nitric acid to release Ag+. The prepared PPY-MWCNTs/A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>-ISE then quantitatively detected Ag+ via open-circuit potential measurement, thereby enabling the detection of thrombin. Benefiting from the signal amplification effect of A<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub>@S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, this electrode exhibited a detection sensitivity of 24.1 mV/dec and a detection limit of 4.2 UN/mL for thrombin, showing a high potential for application to other biomarkers.

title: Miniaturized Carbon-Cloth/TiO Nanofiber-Based Electrochemical Sensor for Simultaneous Detection of Dopamine and Insulin_supp1-3673354.docx
authors: Sanket Goel
abstract: A plug-and-play, structurally sound electrochemical device is necessary for the simultaneous detection of dopamine and insulin to facilitate real-time health monitoring and optimal diabetes treatment. This research investigates the application of CC/TiO₂ nanofiber-based substrates as a sensitive surface for both. The well-separated and simultaneous detection of dopamine and insulin is enabled by their distinct electrochemical oxidation mechanisms and differential surface interactions on the CC/TiO₂ nanofiber substrate, where the small dopamine molecule undergoes rapid diffusion-controlled electron transfer, while the larger insulin molecule exhibits adsorption-assisted kinetics at higher oxidation potentials. Herein, the response of biosensors to different scan rates and increased analyte concentrations has been analyzed using electrochemical techniques Cyclic Voltammetry (CV) and Chronoamperometry (CA). The results showed a linear increase in current with increasing dopamine and insulin concentrations, confirming high sensitivity and highly efficient electrocatalytic activity. The modified CC/TiO₂ substrates showed superior performance compared to bare carbon cloth, confirming their enhanced detection capabilities. The successful modification of the substrate is confirmed by implementing a detailed characterization that was carried out besides various techniques, such as FTIR, XPS, and morphological studies, as well as results obtained from the electrochemical measurements using K₃Fe(CN)₆ supporting electrolyte. This sensor could be a potential candidate in biomolecule detection and personalized healthcare monitoring due to its biocompatibility, low cost, and high sensitivity.

title: Game-based LLM Inference Task Offloading for Edge Computing System
authors: Shoulu Hou, Min Gan, Wei Ni, Zhongyi Zhai, Xiulei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs), with their powerful capabilities, are fundamentally transforming society. Cloud-based LLM deployment has drawbacks, including latency, lack of offline functionality, and high long-term costs. Edge computing-based LLM solutions address these issues by offloading inference tasks to edge servers. This paper formulates and optimizes an LLM inference framework that jointly accounts for inference time and predictive quality. Specifically, to address the natural tendency of selfish users for personal utility maximization, we develop a Game-theoretic Offloading Algorithm (GOALIT) to optimize LLM inference offloading. The approach enables distributed users to iteratively adjust their strategies and converge to a Nash equilibrium. Compared to the optimal tree-based search (OT-GAH), the proposed approach yields a 27% increase in token throughput and shortens inference latency by 20%, while maintaining lower perplexity under dynamic system loads. These findings confirm the effectiveness of our approach in resource-constrained edge environments.

title: Fault Detection and Diagnosis in Cyber Physical Systems using Hybrid Deep Learning Approach
authors: K.K. Smitha, Nitin Vetcha, Manju S Nair, K.S. Bijukumar, BS Manoj
abstract: Cyber-physical systems (CPS) integrate computational algorithms and physical systems, facilitating coherent communication between the digital and physical world. Ensuring the reliability and efficiency of CPS is crucial across various domains, including space missions, manufacturing, transportation, and energy systems. One critical aspect is fault detection and diagnosis (FDD), which involves promptly identifying and addressing abnormalities or faults in the system. Fault detection in complex electromechanical systems remains a significant challenge due to non linear dynamics, diverse operating conditions and limited labeled data. Traditional model based methods, such as Bond Graph approaches, provides physics-informed insights but struggle with scalability, while deep learning based methods often lacks interpretability and robustness. To address these challenges we proposes a robust Hybrid Deep Learning Approach (HyDeLA), a hybrid architecture that integrate Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) units and an attention mechanism, combined with residual signals derived from bond graph modeling. The methodology employs residual signal augmentation, sliding window segmentation, Keras tuner-based hyperparameter optimization to adapt the model to specific datasets. Experimentation validation on two different CPS - DC Motor and Proton Exchange Membrane (PEM) Electrolyzer- demonstrates that HyDeLA achieves superior fault detection accuracy of 99.47% and 95.42% respectively, outperforming recent CNN-LSTM, VAE-GAN, Bond Graph CNN methods, even with a minimum number of labeled fault samples. In addition to this, the proposed model provides improved temporal-spatial feature capture and robustness under varying conditions.
