These results highlight HyDeLA’s capability to bridge physics-based modeling and deep learning, offering a reliable and generalizable framework for fault detection in safety critical cyber-physical systems.

title: Adaptive Rotor Position Error Compensation Scheme for PMSM Sensorless Drives Based on Low-Frequency Sinusoidal Signal Injection
authors: Shuo Chen, Xiaoyue Wang, Xiang Wu, Chao Li, Wen Ding, Yongxiang Xu, Jibin Zou, Dexu Lv, Yan Chen
abstract: Model-based sensorless control of permanent magnet synchronous machines (PMSMs) is susceptible to significant position deviations caused by inverter nonlinearity, parameter mismatch and digital delay, which are challenging to compensate for accurately. To address this issue, an adaptive position error compensation scheme based on low-frequency sinusoidal injection is proposed. By injecting a low-frequency sinusoidal signal into the estimated position, disturbance components containing position error information are excited and extracted using proposed signal demodulation method. A cost function is designed based on these disturbance components, and the gradient descent optimization (GDO) method is adopted to minimize it, thereby searching for the optimal compensation value. The proposed scheme can achieve the total position error compensation without requiring accurate knowledge of inverter nonlinearity or motor parameters. The effectiveness of proposed scheme is verified by experimental results.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: Blind detection scheme for pilot-free short packets: joint capture, synchronization and demodulation
authors: Menglei Xiu, Gaoqi Dou, Hao Wang, Congying Li
abstract: We propose a blind detection scheme for pilot-free short packets that eliminates the pilot structure, significantly enhancing information transmission efficiency. To thoroughly evaluate the proposed pilot-free short packets transmission scheme, we analyze the packet capture, synchronization, and demodulation processes separately and jointly, highlighting that the synchronization process is a major limitation to pilot-free short packets transmission performance. By incorporating cyclic redundancy check (CRC) to optimize the pilot-free short packets structure and leveraging CRC-based soft decision techniques to assist in synchronization and demodulation, the system’s joint blind detection performance is significantly improved. Both simulation and experimental results demonstrate that, given the same time and bandwidth resources, the proposed pilot-free short packets blind detection scheme outperforms the widely used LoRa transmission scheme. At the cost of some spatial complexity, it achieves superior demodulation performance in AWGN, rayleigh fading, doppler frequency offset and multi-path channels. This scheme offers a promising alternative for large-scale machine-to-machine communication in Internet of Things (IoT) applications.

title: Runtime Feature Compression for Adaptive Keyword Spotting on Embedded Systems
authors: Valentino Peluso, Andrea Calimera, Enrico Macii, Paolo Montuschi
abstract: Voice user interfaces rely on keyword spotting (KWS) to detect wake-word commands, enabling low-power devices to switch from drowsy to active states and initiate more complex tasks. In embedded systems, KWS combines handcrafted acoustic features extraction with lightweight neural network classifiers to achieve accurate detection within strict resource constraints. Adapting KWS to time-varying energy budgets requires optimization strategies that operate at runtime. Most existing approaches adjust the complexity of the neural model but overlook that a substantial amount of latency, and thus energy consumption, is due to feature extraction, which remains unaffected by model scaling. This work introduces Runtime Feature Compression (RFC), a dynamic rescaling strategy that modulates the workload of the entire KWS pipeline. RFC promotes the hop-length parameter of the Short-Time Fourier Transform as a runtime control knob to adjust the number of time frames in speech features, allowing a single model to operate across multiple latency modes. To support this flexibility, we introduce two training-time techniques: HopAugment, a data augmentation scheme that exposes the model to variable hop lengths during training, and Masked Layers, which preserve consistent activation statistics during training and inference under compressed feature settings. Evaluations on four KWS datasets using the TC-ResNet model family show that RFC outperforms model scaling techniques, offering a wider range of latency-accuracy trade-offs. RFC achieves up to 31.8% lower latency without accuracy degradation, or up to 0.30% higher accuracy within equivalent latency bounds. That proves RFC improves adaptability in energy-constrained IoT speech interfaces. A set of ablation studies further demonstrates the robustness of RFC by evaluating the role of its training components, batching strategies, ability to preserve accuracy with a shared weight set, scalability across operating modes, and applicability to different model architectures.

title: STABLE: Efficient Hybrid Nearest Neighbor Search via Magnitude-Uniformity and Cardinality-Robustness
authors: Qianyun Yang, Zhiwei Chen, Yupeng Hu, Zixu Li, Zhiheng Fu, Liqiang Nie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering

title: PCIP: Toward Real-Time Pedestrian Crossing Intention Prediction With Adaptive Fusion and Temporal Dynamics Encoding
authors: Rongrong Ni, Hao Liu, Chuan Hu, Hai Wang, Biao Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Pedestrian crossing intention prediction is critical for autonomous vehicles. Analyzing pedestrian motion characteristics provides a preemptive decision-making basis for the planning module. The prediction accuracy directly affects the timeliness of collision avoidance mechanisms in vehicles, especially in mixed traffic scenarios, which is crucial for the safety and reliability of autonomous driving systems. Existing methods struggle to jointly achieve accuracy and real-time performance due to motion-unaware representations and inflexible fusion, which introduce redundancy and latency on embedded platforms. To tackle this problem, we propose PCIP, which leverages adaptive multimodal fusion to achieve efficient and robust crossing intention prediction. PCIP proposes three key contributions: 1) The dynamic selection of the quasi-polar coordinate system accurately characterizes the relative motion trends between pedestrians and vehicles through adaptive multi-pole selection. 2) The Koopman-based CI-TDE module linearizes the spatiotemporal dynamics of skeletal actions, significantly enhancing the expressive capability of temporal features that describe pedestrian motions. 3) A multi-scale dynamic weighting strategy based on distance and angle effectively suppresses noisy modalities and enhances pivotal features. Experiments show that PCIP achieves intention classification accuracies of 89% and 93% on the JAAD and PIE datasets, respectively, and achieves a real-time inference speed of 0.5ms per frame on an embedded platform (NVIDIA Jetson AGX). This study provides a lightweight and robust solution for pedestrian intention prediction in complex traffic scenarios.

title: Out-of-Sight Embodied Agents: Multimodal Tracking, Sensor Fusion, and Trajectory Forecasting
authors: Haichao Zhang, Yi Xu, Yun Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Trajectory prediction is a fundamental problem in computer vision, vision-language-action models, world models, and autonomous systems, with broad impact on applications including autonomous driving, robotics, and surveillance. Most existing approaches assume observations are complete and relatively clean, and thus do not adequately address out-ofsight agents or the intrinsic noise in sensing modalities (e.g., sensor measurements) caused by restricted camera coverage, occlusions, and the lack of ground-truth denoised trajectories. These factors introduce substantial safety concerns and reduce the robustness of trajectory prediction in practical deployments. In this extended study, we introduce major improvements to Out-of-Sight Trajectory (OST), a new task aimed at predicting noise-free visual trajectories of out-of-sight objects from noisy sensor observations. Based on our prior work, we expand the setting of Out-of-Sight Trajectory Prediction (OOSTraj) from pedestrians to both pedestrians and vehicles, thereby increasing its relevance to autonomous driving, robotics, and surveillance scenarios. Our improved Vision-Positioning Denoising Module utilizes camera calibration to construct a vision-position correspondence, mitigating the absence of direct visual cues while enabling effective unsupervised denoising of noisy sensor signals. Extensive experiments on the Vi-Fi and JRDB datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art results for both trajectory denoising and trajectory prediction, with clear gains over prior baselines. We further provide comparisons against classical denoising techniques, including Kalman filtering, and adapt recent trajectory prediction models to this setting, establishing a stronger and more comprehensive benchmark. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to incorporate vision-positioning projection to denoise noisy sensor trajectories of out-of-sight agents, opening new directions for future research in this area. The code and preprocessed datasets are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Hai-chao-Zhang/OST</uri>.

title: SM-HAD: Spectrum Mamba for Hyperspectral Anomaly Detection
authors: Tanishq Rachamalla, Aryan Das, Swalpa Kumar Roy, Swagatam Das, Lorenzo Bruzzone
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral anomaly detection (HAD) is vital in remote sensing and surveillance, where the goal is to identify rare or unknown targets without prior labels. However, HAD remains challenging due to: (1) high spectral dimensionality, (2) complex spectral–spatial dependencies, (3) diverse anomaly scales, and (4) the need for computationally efficient inference. To address these challenges, we propose SM-HAD, a unified encoder–decoder framework that integrates three complementary modules. First, the Ortho Spectrum Fourier Block (OSFB) mitigates spectral redundancy by projecting features into the frequency domain and selectively enhancing informative spectral modes. Second, the Masked Vanilla Attention Block (MVAB) explicitly preserves local spatial relations through a context-aware masking mechanism, improving fine-grained anomaly localization. Third, the Residual Mamba Block (RMB) leverages state-space modeling to capture long-range spatial dependencies with linear complexity, enabling scalable inference. Extensive experiments and ablation studies on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that SM-HAD consistently outperforms 18 State-of-the-Art (SoTA) methods, achieving competitive accuracy while requiring fewer parameters and lower FLOPs. These properties make SM-HAD highly suitable for real-time and resource-constrained applications. The source code is available at https://github.com/Tanishq251/SM-HAD.

title: Online and Safe Multi-Agent RL for Massive Random Access in Tactical Flying Ad-Hoc Networks
authors: Jimin Jeon, Jaeha Ahn, Min Lee, Youngbin You, Heejung Yu, Howon Lee
abstract: In massive random access-based tactical flying adhoc networks (FANETs), the rapid mobility of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) leads to highly dynamic topological changes that frequently cause collisions of control and data packets and ultimately degrade network performance. To address the collision problem, this study proposes an online safe multi-agent reinforcement learning (OSMAR)-based massive random access method, incorporating an artificial Q-adjustment (AQA) mechanism to optimally allocate transmission slots to multiple UAVs within each frame. Specifically, a safe reinforcement learning (RL) technique is employed to design the action selection policy, which balances exploration and exploitation by considering long-term rewards and risks based on a Boltzmann distribution. With this, a blacklist mechanism is incorporated to prevent UAVs from choosing the time slots with high collision risk. Also, the AQA mechanism, composed of an artificial Q-decrement (AQD) that lowers the Q-values of slots already used by other UAVs and an artificial Q-initialization (AQI) that resets the Q-values of idle slots, leverages overheard channel status information to accelerate convergence and enhance adaptation under dynamic network conditions. Extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed OSMAR method outperforms existing approaches in terms of collision probability, while also enhancing fairness and maintaining robustness under various network conditions.

title: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society

title: DVFL-CPN: A Decentralized and Verifiable Federated Learning Approach for Computing Power Network
authors: Boyang Wang, Xiaolong Xu
abstract: The computing power network (CPN) interconnects dispersed heterogeneous computing nodes through a network and achieve rational allocation and efficient utilization of computing power through unified algorithms. Meanwhile, with its decentralized nature, it serves as an ideal platform for decentralized federated learning. However, the mutual distrust among computing providers, computing users, and between computing providers and computing users leads to untrustworthy models in federated learning, and decentralized federated learning also suffers from privacy issues such as data leakage, hindering its application in CPN. Therefore, to address the aforementioned problems, we propose a decentralized and verifiable federated learning approach for computing power network, termed DVFL-CPN. Specifically, we first propose a single-masking encryption algorithm based on pseudorandom numbers, primarily to protect the local models trained by each computing node. Then, we propose a verification algorithm based on homomorphic hashing to verify the aggregated results of computing nodes. Subsequently, we propose a robust aggregation algorithm, enabling online nodes to complete model aggregation and decryption even when some computing nodes go offline. We also deploy a blockchain to record the operation results of each node. Finally, we conduct experiments on real-world datasets. Compared to state-of-the-art decentralized and verifiable federated learning methods, our method achieves a 13.5% improvement in accuracy and demonstrates robustness against offline nodes. Additionally, our method outperforms existing methods in terms of computational efficiency and communication efficiency by approximately 3×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> and 1.6×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> times, respectively.

title: Compound Permanent Magnet Machine: Opportunity and Challenge to be Next-generation Powerful Electric Drive
authors: Zaixin Song, Yongtao Liang, Shuangchun Xie, Ching Chuen Chan, Christopher H. T. Lee
abstract: Permanent magnet (PM) machine designs need to be upgraded for higher electric drive output requirements. Recent vigorous development of motor technology has suggested some novel compound PM machine (CPMM) topologies, which have become alternative solutions in these applications. Not only do these CPMMs increase the power density and torque density, but also possess flexibility and adjustability in performance. According to most recent publication records, the research on CPMM targets various electric drive applications like electric propulsion, robot actuation, heavy machinery, etc., and is dedicated to suitable, feasible CPMM designs addressing pragmatic issues. This review article aims to expound the emerging CPMMs in view of topology, performance feature, relevant technology, and application potential. This article recategorizes the technical routes of achieving CPMMs into four patterns, namely by adding parts, sharing parts, additional field sources, and complementarity. The following discussion will contain quantitative and comparative survey in operational principles and performance features. Therefore, this article tends to fill in the blank of current and future comprehensive research on CPMMs in order to make a milestone and reference value.

title: EBFT: Simplifying BFT Consensus Through Egalitarianism_supp1-3673924.pdf
authors: Jianyu Niu
abstract: We present EBFT, an egalitarian framework of BFT consensus for permissioned blockchains. The key innovation in EBFT is egalitarian block generation: instead of relying on a fixed leader, nodes randomly and non-interactively propose blocks, which makes the system resilient to attacks targeting the leader. By combining the longest-chain rule from Nakamoto consensus with quorum voting from classical BFT, EBFT achieves deterministic safety with a design as simple as Nakamoto consensus. To demonstrate its practicality, we build three protocols: EBFT-SYN for synchronous networks, EBFTPSYN for partially synchronous networks, and EBFT-TURBO, a variant that incorporates Bitcoin-NG style decoupling to demonstrate the framework's extensibility towards high performance. We implement EBFT atop the Go version of Bitcoin, btcd. We implemented EBFT-SYN, EBFT-PSYN and EBFT-TURBO in about 920 LoCs in total. This indicates that EBFT can be built on top of existing blockchains with relatively little effort. We evaluate these protocols on EC2 instances with up to 256 nodes. Our evaluation shows that EBFT-SYN (resp. EBFT-PSYN) achieves a latency of 6 (resp 1) seconds. EBFT-TURBO can process up to 3.2k transactions per second and has a latency of 8 seconds.

title: An Optimized Deep Learning Approach for Intrusion Detection: AE-DBN Hybrid Model with Dingo Feature Selection on CSE-CIC- IDS2018
authors: Srinivas Cheekati, Ramya Vani Rayala, Chandrakanth Reddy Borra, G K Madhura
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) enables interconnected, always-on smart devices. Attacks on network communications made possible by these interconnected devices provide a problem for cybersecurity systems. Threats to system and user operations from these types of assaults have persisted. So, one of the most popular ways to keep these vulnerabilities protected from cyberattacks is with Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS). Traditional intrusion detection systems are made more difficult by the ever-changing and multi-faceted nature of the threats that affect IoT networks. The need for more sophisticated intrusion detection systems (IDS) to detect and prevent cyber-attacks has grown in response to the sophistication of network security threats. Our paper presents a deep learning model for network attack detection that combines Autoencoder (AE) and Deep Belief Network (DBN), together with an optimised feature selection method based on the Dingo Optimiser (DO). The CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset, an extensive dataset for intrusion detection research, is used to test the model. In this method, DBN is used for its strong classification skills, and AE is used for efficient feature extraction and dimensionality reduction. To reduce computational complexity and improve model performance, the Dingo Optimiser is used to choose the most relevant features. The experimental results show that our strategy greatly improves the accuracy of attack detection while reducing the rates of false alarms. Accuracy, precision, recall, $\mathbf{F 1}$-score, and detection rate are all areas where our hybrid method outperforms traditional ML and DL models. This study’s results show that intrusion detection systems that use a mix of deep learning classification, unsupervised feature learning, and metaheuristic optimisation perform better. Cybersecurity applications can benefit from this model’s potential for real-time deployment and resistance to adversarial attacks, and it offers a viable approach for enhancing network security.

title: Comparative Analysis of Adaptive ICI Cancellation in Backscattered OFDM Using Unused Subcarriers
authors: Ashwath Vinodkumar, Rahul Bhargav Tallada, Ramesh R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Backscattered Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) enables ultra-low-power communication in IoT applications by allowing passive devices to reflect and modulate ambient or dedicated RF signals. However, due to the hard limiting behavior and low-resolution impedance states of backscatter tags often constrained to just two levels these systems are highly susceptible to Inter-Carrier Interference (ICI), which causes significant signal distortion and degrades Bit Error Rate (BER) performance. This paper proposes a novel ICI cancellation technique termed Adaptive Unused-Subcarrier ICI Cancellation (AUSIC), which exploits unused subcarriers in an OFDM system to construct a time domain interference suppression signal that actively cancels the ICI generated during signal quantization. Unlike traditional ICI self-cancellation techniques that rely on mirroring or redundancy within subcarrier allocation, the proposed method offers a more flexible and effective interference reduction mechanism. The approach is validated through simulations across multiple OFDM sizes (N = 16, 32, 64, 128, 256), demonstrating consistent improvements. The results show that thSe proposed method significantly lowers the BER floor compared to both systems without ICI cancellation and those employing conventional self-cancellation methods. Moreover, it achieves a notable improvement in Signal-to-Interference Ratio (SIR), especially under high Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) conditions. This improvement enables more efficient subcarrier usage, supports higher data rates, and facilitates the integration of OFDM-based backscatter systems into high density and interference prone IoT environments without increasing receiver complexity or power consumption.

title: The Identification of Load Model Parameters with Photovoltaic Generation Considering LVRT Control
authors: Haonan Fang, Xinran Zhang, Ying Wang, Chao Lu
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy on the load side of power systems has changed the load dynamic properties. To better model the dynamics of the load side, a load model considering renewables and its identification method are necessary. Therefore, we propose an identification method for the generalized load model. This method aims to improve the identifiability of the complex load model by combining ambient signals and large disturbances. As the foundation, we first introduce the generalized load model structure which includes the photovoltaic generation model considering low-voltage ride through control. Then, the likelihood profile method for the parameter identifiability analysis and the two-stage identification framework are presented. Finally, the simulation is conducted in IEEE 39 bus system to verify the method effectiveness. The results indicate that the likelihood profile method can reveal the parameter dependency and find the appropriate group of the parameters to be identified, while the identification method can follow the dynamics of the generalized load well.

title: Data Trustee Framework in Logistics
authors: Michael Koch, Jessica Chwalek, Benjamin Gaunitz, André Ludwig, Bogdan Franczyk
abstract: The transport sector includes data exchange across organizational boundaries. However, once uploaded, the data are under the control of existing platforms, which offer only limited usage and access controls for this data. For this purpose, the Transit data trustee platform was developed to enforce fine-grained usage control at the attribute level, supported by a dedicated access rights management service. The centrally stored data are only shared for the purposes of the data owner. Inside the platform, modules such as attributelevel usage control, central logging, geo-fencing, and a modular and multi-layered data trustee architecture ensure the proper use of the data.

title: A Comprehensive Review of the BCI Techniques
authors: Rajapriyan S K, Kishore V, Shiny R M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) provide a direct avenue of converting neural action into control language, offering communication and interaction without the action of the muscle. Despite 25 years of advances, affected on practice by calibration overhead, the variability of neural signals, hardware concerns, and sensitivity to the user, the practical use of BCIs is still constrained. Emerging technologies with machine learning, deep learning, and edge computing have presented new vistas of getting around such difficulties. The paper provides an overview and contextualization of the current state of BCI research, specifically focusing on non-invasive EEG-based applications, hybrid systems, and learning approaches such as big data, transfer learning, adaptive models and system-level solutions that reduce calibration time and enhance robustness and usability by combining wearable hardware and processing the data in real time. The primary contribution is a synthesized view in BCI practicality through algorithmic, hardware, and application-inspired progress. Results in the current literature reveal that calibration-free or calibration-sparse BCIs, multimodal integration, and effective on-device intelligence have the potential to enhance accuracy, stability, and user experience significantly, moving towards the assumption that BCIs can be used more often assisting in the clinical and assistive environment as well.

title: Designing for Accessibility: A Systematic Literature Review on User Experience Research for Visually Impaired Users
authors: Khadisha Reswara Hippy, Mohammad Genta Pramesti Linduaji, Fredy Jingga
abstract: This article presents a systematic literature review (SLR) aimed at enhancing User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) accessibility for visually impaired users. With approximately 2.2 billion people globally suffering from vision impairment, there is an urgent need to shift application design towards inclusivity. From an initial pool of 1,898 candidate studies, 33 were selected for in-depth analysis to address two key research questions. The study identifies key factors that significantly improve accessibility and examines the most common barriers persisting in current digital designs. These findings offer insights into the essential components and strategies necessary for creating truly accessible digital environments.

title: Research on Difference Analysis and Construction of International Standard System for Smart Metering
authors: Chao Lv, Xingde Huang, Zhongxing Wu, Lian Liu, Hanwen Qiao, Min Hua, Pengyue Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: As a core supporting technology for the construction of new power systems and the digital transformation of energy, smart metering's standardization level directly determines the efficiency of global energy resource optimization and allocation and the quality of cross-border cooperation. Currently, China has formed a complete independent industrial chain and built the world's largest electricity information collection system, but faces issues such as insufficient international standard discourse power and lagging layout of cutting-edge technical standards. Firstly, this paper systematically sorts out the differences between domestic and foreign technologies and standard systems, and constructs the Intelligent Measurement Differential Quantization Collaborative Model (IM-DQCM). Through the integration of subjective and objective weights using the AHP-Entropy Weight Method, grey relational analysis, and fuzzy evaluation, the core domestic-foreign differences and the priority of shortcomings are clarified. Secondly, combined with the quantitative analysis results, a five-level international standard system framework for smart metering is constructed, and key areas for standard formulation and revision are proposed. Finally, from the perspectives of demand alignment, implementation paths, and safeguard measures, landing strategies are put forward. The research results provide theoretical support and paths for China's smart metering standards to "go global", helping to enhance China's participation and influence in global energy governance.

title: FedRS: Federated Learning-based Remote Sensing Image Classification in Telecommunication Nodes
authors: Dhruvil Dave, Abhay Chovatiya, Aditi, Khushbu Joshi, Trupesh Patel, Mitesh Patel
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Satellites capture earth’s surface data without any physical contact, popularly known as remote sensing data. The classification of remote sensing data plays a significant role in extracting meaningful information. This paper proposes deep learning-based federated learning for accurate object detection from the EuroSAT dataset. This dataset consists of ten classes, various segments of an earth. We used efficient deep learning models such as DenseNet-121, MobileNetV2, ResNet-50, ResNet-101, and ResNet-152 on EuroSAT that classifies it. The federated learning approach provides privacy preservation due to the decentralized environmental setup. DenseNet-121 shows the highest nearly ninety-five percent of an accuracy that exhibits the automatic feature extraction capabilities of deep learning models.

title: Artificial Intelligence as the Shield of Cybersecurity
authors: Manish Rai, Hemlata Parmar, Satyam Chaturvedi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: The rising complexity and frequency of cyberattacks have compelled the implementation of advanced technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) to improve cybersecurity protocols. Artificial Intelligence (AI) ’s role has increased with each passing time. This paper examines the transformative impact of AI on danger detection, prevention, and reaction through the use of Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL). This study’s significant applications encompass intrusion detection, malware analysis, and access management underpinned by sophisticated neural networks. The capability of AI to surpass conventional methods is demonstrated by its proficiency in identifying intricate dangers, reducing false positives, and evaluating unstructured data for new risks. The results highlight AI’s potential to transform cybersecurity by fostering innovation and tackling vulnerabilities and ethical issues. This review summarizes findings from twenty research articles, emphasizing AI’s increasing implementation and efficacy across several sectors, especially in banking, IT, and IoT security.

title: A Comprehensive Survey of Deepfake Identification & Localization Methods in Images and Videos
authors: Manisha P Sonawane, Uttara Gogate
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: With the growing modification and misuse of AIgenerated media, deepfake detection is an important field of study. It is rapidly impacted the public trust and human rights. In digital forensics deepfake detection and identification of media is extremely vital and in demand. Techniques such as deep learning, frequency domain analysis, physiological and biological signal analysis, temporal inconsistency analysis, artifact detection, and model fingerprinting are used to distinguish authentic and manipulated multimedia content. This survey highlights the methodologies used and evaluation parameters to improve accuracy, localization, and computational efficiency.

title: Free-Flying Crew Cooperative Robots on the ISS: A Joint Review of Astrobee, CIMON, and Int-Ball Operations
authors: Seiko Piotr Yamaguchi, Andres Mora Vargas, Till Eisenberg, Christian Rogon, Tatsuya Yamamoto, Shona Inoue, Christoph Kössl, Brian Coltin, Trey Smith, Jose V. Benavides
abstract: Intra-vehicular free-flying robots are anticipated to support various work in human spaceflight while working side-by-side with astronauts. Such example of robots includes NASA’s Astrobee, DLR’s CIMON, and JAXA’s Int-Ball, which are deployed on the International Space Station. This paper presents the first joint analyses of these robot’s shared experiences, co-authored by their development and operation team members. Despite the different origins and design philosophies, the development and operations of these platforms encountered various convergences. Hence, this paper presents a detailed overview of these robots, presenting their objectives, design, and onboard operations. Hence, joint lessons learned across the lifecycle are presented, from design to on-orbit operations. These lessons learned are anticipated to serve for future development and research as design recommendations.

title: ScanDroidX: A Mobile-Native Security Tool with MITRE ATT&CK Mobile Integration for Pre-Deployment Web Application Assessment
authors: Alisha Anthony, Andrew Daniel, Rishabh Tiwari, Dhanashree Huddedar
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: The mobile web security issue is now an urgent one, given the immense usage of smartphones as the dominant service means of accessing online services. Companies that deal with mobile-first web pages should have comprehensive predeployment security testing to avoid breaches of data information, session hijacking, and phishing attacks that emerge as a result of the disregard of vulnerabilities peculiar to the mobile environment. The current security tools do not typically provide a way to apply the MITRE ATT&CK Mobile framework directly, nor do they support a lightweight and mobile-native auditing tool with a rich threat intelligence context, such as ScanDroidX, which can be used to conduct a complete vulnerability analysis of mobile websites and static files. It uses the method of the static analysis to determine the areas of weakness and matches each of the identified weaknesses with the relevant MITRE ATT&CK Mobile techniques offering actionable threat intelligence. Among such a multi-format analysis engine with support to JavaScript, HTML, APK, and EXE scanning, automatic generation of PDF reports, and a convenient Android interface. The linkage of vulnerability detection and adversary behavior mapping makes ScanDroidX a better resource in pre-deployment testing and add to mobile cybersecurity preparedness in organizations of any size.

title: Waveform Matching Based Single-Ended Protection Scheme for Multi-Terminal MMC-HVDC System
authors: Yang Dai, Ke Jia, Tianshu Bi, Shengyao Zhang, Kexin Liu
abstract: MMC-HVDC technology is powerful in addressing the integration of large-scale renewable energy, and its line protection is key to ensuring system safety and stability. Single-ended protection of the line does not require communication and can better meet the time requirements of MMC-HVDC transmission systems. However, the performance of existing Single-ended protection in MMC-HVDC transmission systems is significantly affected by subsequent traveling waves (STW), resulting in a certain range of protection dead zones. To address this, based on fully considering the impact of STWs on the initial traveling wave (TW), the differences between internal and external fault TWs are explored, and a fault identification criterion based on waveform matching is proposed. This criterion can measure the impact of STWs by combining matching errors with matching waveform characteristics, ultimately achieving full-line rapid protection. A Multi-Terminal MMC-HVDC System is built based on PSCAD/EMTDC. Simulation results verify the feasibility of the proposed method, which has the advantages of a long protection range, strong tolerance to transition resistance, and strong anti-noise interference capability.

title: High-impedance faults simulation and location method of distribution cable based on self generated wavelet and arc model
authors: Youxiang Yan, Tong Wu, Hong Chen, Jingyi Chen, Zhenjia Li, Zhixiong Lin, Zhihong Sun
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Accurate localization of high-impedance faults in distribution cables is crucial for ensuring the reliability of distribution network operation. Conventional wavelet-based methods suffer from limitations in the selection of wavelet basis functions, which compromises the accuracy and stability of fault location. To address this issue, this paper introduces a novel high-impedance fault location algorithm for distribution cables based on data-driven wavelets. First, using the Mayr arc model and a distributed parameter cable model, a high-impedance fault simulation is built in ATP-EMTP to analyze the propagation characteristics of voltage traveling waves during fault occurrence, thereby clarifying the relationship between the characteristic frequency of the fault and its location. Furthermore, a method for constructing an adaptive mother wavelet is proposed, which generates a matched wavelet based on the actual fault waveform. By applying continuous wavelet transform with this customized wavelet, the characteristic frequency components are effectively extracted, enabling accurate fault location estimation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves an average relative localization error of only 0.168% (approximately 1.76 m ) in a 1050 m cable, outperforming traditional wavelet techniques. It also exhibits strong noise immunity and high computational efficiency. This approach offers a highly accurate and adaptive solution for locating high-impedance faults in distribution cables.

title: Research on Multi time Scale Balancing Capability of New Energy Storage in High Proportion New Energy Grid
authors: Ding Li, Minghua Jiang, Dezhen Kong, Yuxuan Wang, Qian Wu, Wangcheng Long
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: To address the challenge of grid balance caused by the high proportion of new energy grid integration, this article focuses on the research of the multi time scale balance capability of new energy storage. Firstly, the power and frequency balance challenges of high proportion new energy grids at the second, minute, and hour time scales were analyzed; Secondly, the technical characteristics of different types of new energy storage were elaborated, and their functional positioning at various time scales was clarified; The core has constructed a dynamic model of the energy storage body and a multi time scale balance function model, covering scenarios such as frequency regulation, hill climbing control, and energy time shift; Design four types of simulation scenarios based on IEEE 30 node retrofit system, and verify the effectiveness of the model through indicators such as frequency deviation standard deviation and wind and solar curtailment rate. Research has shown that comprehensive energy storage and layered control strategies with multi time scale collaborative service capabilities can effectively improve the balance performance of the power grid and the level of new energy consumption, providing technical support for the safe and stable operation of high proportion new energy power grids.

title: Research on Anti-Mechanical Vibration Performance of Rydberg Atom Electric Field Sensing System
authors: Wenxin Peng, Songnong Li, Yanling Sun, Xiaodong Xia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Rydberg atom electric field sensing technology shows great potential for power system frequency electric field measurement but faces mechanical vibration interference when moving from laboratory to on-site applications. To address this bottleneck, a compact Rydberg atom optical path system was constructed: the cesium atomic vapor cell for measurement was fiber-connected to enable flexible fixation of the sensing unit, and key optical components were configured to optimize the Electromagnetically Induced Transparency (EIT) effect. Experiments were conducted to analyze the effects of vibrations applied in directions parallel and perpendicular to the optical path on EIT Spectral, both with and without DC electric field dressing. Experimental observations reveal distinct impacts of vibrations along the two directions, and in both cases the intensity of vibration-induced effects scales with increasing frequency and amplitude. Despite periodic baseline fluctuations observed under vibrations in either direction, no frequency shifts of the EIT Spectral were detected across all experiments. This confirms the excellent anti-vibration performance of the proposed Rydberg cesium atom electric field sensing system, providing theoretical support for its on-site application in power systems.

title: LIME Explanation Integration for Real-Time Collision Detection using Deep Learning
authors: Dhruv R. Kothari, Harsh S. Jain, Satishkumar L. Varma, Kushal T. Mehta, Dev Bhanushali, Vinaya Sawant
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: We present a deployable, compact hybrid pipeline for real-time collision risk estimation that combines data-driven, interpretable Time-to-Collision (TTC) estimation with fast deep-vision object detection. The pipeline makes use of DenseNet121 to create compact visual feature vectors for every object it detects and YOLOv5 for frame-level object detection. To estimate TTC as a continuous value, these features are normalized and run through a lightweight ensemble regressor. Experiments show strong prediction accuracy and high object-detection robustness, indicating useful margins for timely warnings at realistic urban speeds. The model was trained and tested on KITTIderived sequences, and its performance was evaluated using standard regression and safety-critical metrics as well as detection accuracy. To enhance transparency and facilitate validation for safety-critical deployment, LIME-based local explanations are incorporated to emphasize image regions most relevant to a single TTC prediction, unveiling if the model pays attention to semantically relevant signals. The introduced modular architecture trades off representation capability, computational cost, and interpretability, rendering it well-suited for edge or in-vehicle ADAS deployments.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Driven Solutions for QoS Enhancement in 6G Networks with LCR in Complex Fading and Interference Scenarios
authors: Deepak Upadhyay, Nookala Venu, Nitin Thapliyal, Love Kumar, Abhishek Pandey, Deepa Jaiswal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: This research has studied how artificial intelligence may be useful in improving QoS of 6G networks as fading and interference get more complex and less predictable. The use of level crossing rate (LCR) to model the variation of the channel, and a deep Q-Network (DQN) to learn an adaptive control system is central to the work. This research utilized simulations to generate synthetic data to include variations in signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), mobility, and interference to assess the performance of AI systems. The results were that compared to both static and supervised learning models, the DQN provided superior performance regarding outage, latency, jitter, and fairness, and demonstrated that energy consumption was more stable, and that reward-based learning converged faster with LCR features. Therefore, this research suggests that LCR-driven AI could provide one or more methods for developing intelligent and reliable 6G systems.

title: Computer Vision Based Damage Detection and Prognosis for Concrete Infrastructure
authors: Badri Narayanan V T, Mohamed Mujammil Y S, Sumanth Arige, Sreehari Veeramachaneni, Sumedha Moharana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Concrete structures are susceptible to defects like cracks, spallation, efflorescence, exposed bars, and corrosion stains that can lead to loss of structural integrity. The conventional approach depends on manual inceptions, which are time-consuming and prone to errors. This paper utilizes the deep learning for the detection and localization of defects on concrete surfaces with the CNN model. It also takes advantage of high-resolution annotated datasets to improve detection precision on various defect classes. The outcomes enhance accuracy in detecting smaller and less separable defects than traditional approaches. This contributes to an efficient and scalable structural health monitoring solution and introduces a deep learning-based method for automatic defect detection and localization on concrete surfaces with the proposed model. The high-resolution annotated data improved detection performance over a variety of defect types. Through application of an optimized model design and training methods, the system obtains better accuracy in identifying smaller and less distinct defects. This work points out that how deep learning helps to auto-mate structural health monitoring and decrease reliance on human-based monitoring through improved scalability. The findings also show that this work surpasses traditional defect classification methods by offering enhanced localization and classification accuracy. Future improvements will include the addition of hybrid models and the development of detection abilities for enhanced robustness.

title: Multi-level Collaborative Business Analysis and Interaction Scheme Design for Digital Converter Station Equipment Operation and Maintenance
authors: Siyuan Liu, Qing Wang, Xiaojun Shen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: Under the "cloud–edge–device" collaborative framework, this paper first analyzes the multi-level collaboration requirements of equipment operation and maintenance (O&M) services in digital converter stations. It further categorizes O&M services into two major types: real-time operation monitoring and non-real-time optimization operations. The characteristics of service algorithms, real-time requirements, and computing power demands are then examined across three levels: device, station, and power grid. Subsequently, an object-oriented data model based on a middle-platform architecture is constructed, which strengthens the topological associations among "geographical region – equipment asset – measurement point – measurement type," thereby supporting digital twin applications and condition analysis. Finally, a unified interaction framework-based cloud-edge business interaction scheme is proposed, focusing on key technologies such as unified interaction interfaces, information models, permission management, API integration, data access engines, and message transmission engines. This provides a systematic solution for multi-level collaboration in the operation and maintenance of digital converter station equipment.

title: A Data-Driven Framework for Career Alignment Based on Personality Attributes and Market Trends Using Expert-Weighted Supervised Learning and Generative AI
authors: Ayushi Gupta, Shruti Gabhane, Om Mohod, Somit Karadbhajane, Yash Shelke, Kavita Gudadhe
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The authors introduce an AI-based career guidance system in the paper which is capable of giving personalized advice. Their work makes use of machine learning (ML) and large language model (LLM) as the two main pillars. The first step consisted of handing out Google Forms for responding, then the data was passed through the domain-specific ML models for analysis to spot the professionals who were most suitable for it. After that, the system provided in-depth reports with practical tips. Depending on their semi-supervised model, specialists rated the significance of different traits. The scores obtained from the weighting served as a basis for the training of our models. The system did not stop there; it was constantly scraping the web for job market data which was then stored in a MongoDB database and results were delivered via automated email using Nodemailer. There were seven specialized ML models in place that provided predictions for the suitability scores in Technology, Healthcare, Business, Law, Media, Sports, and Creative occupations. The LLM came up with suggestions that referred to job titles and skills, potential business opportunities, project ideas, and future trends. Our system was made easier to access by deploying it with Cloudflare Tunnel, and a simple yet user-friendly interface was created with Jinja2 templates. The experimental evaluation showed that the system was capable of delivering high-quality results for several career areas.

title: Robust Face Recognition for Smart Surveillance Using MRFE–GLCM and LTP Variants
authors: Sikhinam Nagamani, N. Renugadevi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Surveillance systems play a vital role in the safety and protection of people, although they are often faced with problems such as inadequate lighting, occlusions, and pose variations. Conventional face recognition methods typically fail in this kind of setting and deep learning algorithms require significant computational resources, which is incompatible with real time use on a compact IoT device. We present a lightweight texture-feature-based smart surveillance framework in this paper, combining Max-Regional Fuzzy Encoding (MRFE) and the Gray-Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) statistics and the variations of Local Ternary Pattern (LTP). The MRFE-GLCM component is complementary to enhance strength, as it combines fuzzy regional encoding with global texture encoding, whereas LTP variants are resilient to illumination and noise variations. These characteristics are merged into a small form and categorized with the help of SVM, KNN, and Decision Tree classifiers. It is experimented on benchmark datasets (ORL, YALE, Extended Yale B) that the proposed hybrid has better accuracy than traditional LBP variants and CNN-LBP hybrids, with low computational complexity. Based on a light weight structure, the framework can be used in real time smart surveillance applications, such as secure access control, smart monitoring, and IoT-based public safety systems.The proposed framework should be computationally lean and efficient.Extended performance analysis confirms the robustness and suitability of the model for real-time IoT-based surveillance.

title: Fault Classification and Location in Transmission Lines using Attention Driven Dual-Stage Deep Architecture Network (ADDA-Net)
authors: V.Rajesh Kumar, Jenipa. R, S.Ravindran, P.Vetrivel, S.Udaiyakumar, Mahesh K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Reliable fault detection, classification, and location are critical for ensuring the stability and resilience of modern power transmission networks. Conventional signal processing and machine learning approaches face challenges in handling noisy measurements, high-dimensional data, and fault conditions with overlapping features. In response to these challenges, this work introduces an Attention-Driven Dual-Stage Deep Architecture Network (ADDA-Net) designed for transmission line fault classification and location estimation. The architecture employs convolutional layers to extract discriminative features, while two attention modules are incorporated to emphasize both temporal variations and spatial dependencies within the signals. Finally, fully connected layers are utilized to perform fault type classification and to estimate the corresponding fault location. A dataset comprising simulated fault scenarios, including single-line-to-ground, double-line-to-ground, double-line, and three-phase faults, was used for evaluation. Results demonstrate that ADDA-Net achieves superior accuracy in both classification (99.2%) and location estimation (mean error <0.5 km), outperforming CNN, SVM, and traditional classifiers. The proposed framework provides a robust and scalable solution for intelligent protection of transmission networks.

title: AI-Driven Tutorial Code Learning System: Personalized Programming Education Through Adaptive Instruction and Gamification
authors: Nashwa Sharafudheen, Rohini V, Meenakshi Malviya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Existing programming education faces critical challenges, such as lack of personalization, restricted feedback tools, and scalability limitations that hinder efficient learning outcomes. This paper presents an AI-powered tutorial code learning system to transform programming education through personalized and adaptive instruction. The system integrates advanced components, including learner modeling, intelligent content recommendation, error analysis, adaptive evaluation, gamification, learning analytics, integration frameworks, quality assurance, security, and scalability layers. To evaluate the system, the study employs a mixed-methods research approach, incorporating embedded case studies and a randomized controlled trial (RCT). Rigorous data collection methods, system measure validation, undergraduate program participant selection, quality assurance protocols, statistical analysis, and ethical considerations are utilized in this work. The architecture demonstrates potential for scalable and globally accessible programming education, addressing traditional challenges through personalized learning protocols. Unlike traditional platforms offering static content and limited feedback, this AI-powered system acts as a personalized tutor, providing active problem-solving and continuous learner engagement. The adaptive system delivers optimal learning paths based on individual student needs and has the potential to transform programming education delivery and outcomes.

title: Enhanced Sensitivity and Specificity in Early Cancer Detection using Photonic Crystal Biosensors and GAN-Optimized Signal Processing
authors: Nancy Chitra Thilaga N, Esakki Muthu K, Selvendran S, Sivanantha Raja A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Early and reliable detection of cancer biomarkers is critical yet challenging with conventional methods. Photonic crystal (PhC) biosensors enable sensitive, label-free detection by monitoring resonance shifts due to biomolecular binding [1] [2]. However, real-world signals are degraded by fabrication noise and environmental fluctuations. We propose a hybrid PhC – GAN framework: a 2D slab-type PhC cavity (optimized lattice, FDTD- simulated in COMSOL 6.0) provides a high - Q resonant response, while a GAN (continuous conditional GAN) learns from experimental/simulated spectra to generate synthetic samples and denoise measurements [3] [4]. A CNN classifier trained on GAN- enhanced spectra then distinguishes true biomarker binding from artifacts. Compared to the PhC sensor alone, our hybrid approach yields approximately 20– 30% higher sensitivity and 15–20% higher specificity, with false positives reduced by ~30%. These improvements demonstrate that GAN-based data augmentation and denoising significantly enhance PhC biosensor performance, paving the way for low-cost, non-invasive early cancer diagnostics.

title: A Multi-Stage and Multi-Type Holomorphic Embedding Method Framework for Identifying Convergence Issues in Power Flow Problems
authors: Yongjian Luo, Chengxi Liu, Zaiyu He, Jiangbei Han, Pengfei Tang
abstract: Power flow analysis is a fundamental task in power system operation and planning. Traditional iterative methods, such as the Newton-Raphson (NR) method, often suffer from convergence issues, especially under stressed operating conditions. In recent years, a non-iterative approach known as the Holomorphic Embedding Method (HEM) has been proposed as a promising alternative. This paper presents a multi-stage and multi-type HEM framework for diagnosing convergence issues in power flow calculations. Firstly, new types of HEM are developed, including a reactive-power-based HEM (Q-HEM) and a lossless HEM (WL-HEM). Then, these formulations are integrated into a structured power flow computation framework, which incorporates convergence detection techniques based on power mismatch detection and abnormal voltage buses identification. Finally, the proposed method is validated on large-scale test systems with 6468 and 13659 buses. Comparative results show that the proposed approach effectively identifies the causes of convergence failure.

title: A Deep Learning Paradigm for Automated Disease Detection in Smart Healthcare Systems
authors: Kiran Puttegowda, Mahesh R, Chandra Shekar P, Ranjan Kumar H S, Ashwini T S, Thejaswini R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Healthcare for people is one of society’s most important issues. To ensure that patients receive the care they require as quickly as possible, it seeks out the best possible and reliable disease diagnosis. Medical system diagnoses are frequently used for rapid disease identification, corrective care, and risk-free treatment of established disorders. Technology is developing even as risks and problems increase. We introduce a fresh paradigm for disease detection in this paper. We will construct a deep learning model that will recover different biomedical data from dispersed and homogeneous sensors. The outcomes will demonstrate the advantages of utilising deep learning technologies in the field of artificial intelligence of medical devices to identify diseases in the process of making healthcare decisions. We will employ several deep learning architectures. One of the most challenging issues in human medicine is the early detection of chronic disorders. In most cases, patients are not aware of the issue until their symptoms start to appear. One of the popular methods for identifying and determining these conditions and diseases, which aids doctors in making the correct diagnosis, is deep learning.

title: Integrating Reinforcement Learning and Cognitive AI for Low-Latency and Resilient High-Speed Network Optimization
authors: Guru Kesava Dasu Gopisetty, Balajee Maram, Manikonda Srinivasa Sesha Sai
publish: 2026 6th Biennial International Conference on Nascent Technologies in Engineering (ICNTE)
abstract: High-speed networks are handling a growing amount of information that requires adaptive optimization at timelines of a few milliseconds in order to satisfy ultrareliable low-latency communication bassoses (URLLC). Classical rule-based orchestration and static optimization tend to fail in the face of partial observerability and dynamic workloads resulting in tail-latency failures and sluggish response to disruption. The proposed hybrid framework combines the reinforcement-based learned behavior with cognitive AI reasoning and enables resilience in intent driven control of networks. We formalize the problem as an optimization of p95/p99 latency, mean time to recovery and control overhead under firm capacity and SLA constraints. We apply the use of multi-agent policies edge deployment combined with the coordination of dialectical inference to support real-time adaptation. Experimental evaluations on a variety of topologies and workloads report consistently reductions of 30+ percent in p99 tail latency and faster recovery over heuristic baselines. The system is published with reproduced artifacts, such as code, datasets, and containerized implementations, in order to achieve a sense of transparency and to have a verifiable progress.

title: Intelligent Coordinated Diagnosis of Primary and Secondary Systems in Market Oriented Distribution Networks
authors: Boyu Wang, Yong Wei, Linlin Zhao, Baoquan Su
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
abstract: As distributed energy resources, virtual power plants, and portfolios of source, load, and storage scale across distribution networks, operating regimes and dispatch practices have shifted markedly. The coupling between primary assets and secondary systems has intensified, raising the requirement for coordinated diagnosis that covers operating states, protection behavior, and communication links. Building on standardized feeder templates, coordination between protection and distribution automation, and coordinated communications, this study integrates artificial intelligence into a unified diagnostic workflow that addresses tasks relevant to planning and operation. These tasks are hosting capacity assessment for distributed interconnection, rapid screening of nodal voltage risk under load conditions, analysis of secondary protection carrying capacity and reliability, health assessment of secondary auxiliary systems, and reliability diagnosis for protection related communications. Evaluation on representative historical playback scenarios and shadow trials indicates consistent gains in diagnostic accuracy, reduced response time from end to end, and improved scalability when compared with rule based and physics only baselines. The resulting guidance supports planning and retrofit decisions and informs day to day operation in market oriented distribution environments.

title: Enhancing Cryptocurrency Price Prediction Using Hybrid LSTM-Attention Mechanism and Reinforcement Learning for Trading Optimization
authors: Anurag Agnihotri, K. Selvakumarasamy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are extremely volatile, and as such, predicting the actual price of digital assets and developing a trading strategy is exceedingly difficult. The problem of accurately predicting the price of cryptocurrency and developing an effective trading strategy is examined in this study. A hybrid model that combines Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with Attention networks to predict price accurately is proposed, and, furthermore, a Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent is used to dynamically make trading decisions. By using deep learning to extract a market trend and reinforcement learning trading patterns, the model is designed to maximize profits and enhance prediction performance. The model is verified by applying it to Bitcoin and Ethereum data. Compared to knowledge-driven price prediction models, we demonstrate better accuracy, and efficient trading returns. The method provides a fully automated trading system capable of responding to cryptocurrency price changes in real-time while generating optimal returns. Most critically, the hybrid RL and deep learning model is robust to dynamic financial environments.

title: Autonomous AI Agents in Scientific Discovery – How Agentic AI Accelerates Research in Drug Design, Materials Science, or Climate Modeling
authors: Rupesh Kohli, Vishwajit K Barbudhe, Vijay M. Rakhade, Trushna M Bhanarkar, Nandini S. Patil, Shreya S. Deshmukh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: You know, science is getting a serious upgrade thanks to these new agentic AI systems. We’re not talking about the old-school, "let’s analyze some data and hope for the best" kind of stuff—now AI can actually plan, learn, and run experiments on its own, all in this wild sort of feedback loop where it designs, tests, learns, and just keeps getting better. With this closed-loop thing, you mash up automation with hands-on experimentation, and suddenly your AI is tossing out high-value experiments, speeding up the whole process, and even taking some of the grunt work off scientists’ plates. That’s a big deal in areas like drug development, material science, and even climate prediction—stuff that used to take ages, now? Way faster, less mind-numbing for people, and actually exploring smarter options. The paper runs through the current tech stack— platforms, system architecture, all the little bits like perception, planning, reinforcement learning, symbolic reasoning, plus tossing humans into the mix when needed. It digs into how these systems work across different fields, sketches out how you’d roll out one of these setups no matter the industry, and doesn’t shy away from the messy parts (yeah, I’m talking about trust issues, bias, safety hiccups, reproducibility headaches). Then there’s some crystal ball gazing about what all this means for science, both now and down the road. Bottom line? Using things like policy optimization, these agentic AI setups can shrink development cycles without turning your research into a rush job. Science just got way more interesting.

title: Concepts of a Robotic Arm System for Lunar Pressurized Rover and its Dust Mitigation Challenges
authors: N. Tanishima, S. P. Yamaguchi, M. Wada, Y. Yamasaki, A. Otsuka, N. Fujioka
abstract: As one of the Artemis systems, Pressurized Rover is being developed by Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA). Since this system has $\mathbf{1 1}$ months of uncrewed phase in a year, robotic arm system is installed to perform exploration tasks efficiently. Many robotic arms have been developed and operated in space development, but a robotic arm to operate on the Moon presents unique challenges. In this paper, requirements and concept of robotic arm system for the Pressurized Rover are introduced. Unique challenge such as regolith dust mitigation is described, and candidate technologies to mitigate the issue are presented and discussed.

title: Adaptive Science Operations in Deep Space Missions Using Offline Belief State Planning
authors: Grace Ra Kim, Hailey Warner, Duncan Eddy, Evan Astle, Zachary Booth, Edward Balaban, Mykel J. Kochenderfer
abstract: Deep space missions face extreme communication delays and environmental uncertainty that prevent real-time ground operations. To support autonomous science operations in communication-constrained environments, we present a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP) framework that adaptively sequences spacecraft science instruments. We integrate a Bayesian network into the POMDP observation space to manage the high-dimensional and uncertain measurements typical of astrobiology missions. This network compactly encodes dependencies among measurements and improves the interpretability and computational tractability of science data. Instrument operation policies are computed offline, allowing resource-aware plans to be generated and thoroughly validated prior to launch. We use the Enceladus Orbilander’s proposed Life Detection Suite (LDS) as a case study, demonstrating how Bayesian network structure and reward shaping influence system performance. We compare our method against the mission’s baseline Concept of Operations (ConOps), evaluating both misclassification rates and performance in off-nominal sample accumulation scenarios. Our approach reduces sample identification errors by nearly $40 \%$.

title: Design and Implementation of a Deep Learning–Driven ECG Signal Classification System for Cardiac Arrhythmia Detection
authors: A. Gnana Rooba, R. Prem Ananth, J. Relin Francis Raj, P. Bharathi, J. Nirmal Jothi, N Soundiraraj
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Image Processing and Capsule Networks (ICIPCN)
abstract: Automated electrocardiogram (ECG) analysis represents a significant engineering problem due to the need for reliable signal interpretation under noisy conditions, limited labeled data, and high inter-patient variability. From an engineering perspective, the development of robust computational models with scalable technological capabilities is essential for next-generation cardiac monitoring systems. This work presents a deep learning–based engineering framework for automated arrhythmia detection that combines unsupervised feature learning with supervised classification. A convolutional denoising autoencoder is employed to learn compact and noise-resilient representations directly from raw ECG signals, reducing dependence on handcrafted features and enabling systematic Scientific research in ECG signal processing. The learned representations are subsequently integrated with a hybrid classifier composed of bidirectional long short-term memory networks and an attention mechanism to model temporal dependencies and morphological variations in cardiac rhythms. The proposed framework was implemented using a publicly available benchmark ECG dataset and evaluated under a patient-wise experimental protocol to ensure realistic performance assessment. The system demonstrates strong generalization capability and improved classification accuracy compared to conventional end-to-end supervised deep learning models. From an engineering systems viewpoint, the integration of attention-based interpretability supports deployment in real-time decision-support platforms built on modern ICT infrastructure and scalable computing Infrastructure. The results highlight the applicability of advanced deep neural architectures in ECG signal classification tasks and emphasize the role of algorithmic optimization and model design in improving diagnostic reliability. Overall, the proposed approach reflects meaningful Innovation in ECG signal analysis by unifying unsupervised representation learning, temporal modeling, and explainable artificial intelligence within a single engineering framework suitable for embedded and wearable health monitoring applications.

title: AIMock: An AI-Powered Mock Interview Platform
authors: Rashmi K. Dixit, Darshna Bhasme, Anjali Sonavane, Manasi R. Patil, Palak Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The aim of this project is to create an AI-based Mock Interview Platform, which should allow students to train, enhance the skills in interviewing within an interesting and interactive setting. The system permit to enable the students to choose the field, which they are personally interested in (e.g. Web Development, Java, Python, and so on) subsequently, domain questions are fetched dynamically via Google Gemini AI. The user is engaged with system through talking out their answers and the camera keeps on recording. facial expression and body language-to get a feel of a live interview.Upon completion of the interview, the system gives an elaborate feedback of the student who has had an interview results, in terms of whether the responses are correct or not, areas of improvement etc. Implying more appropriate replies. One of the most distinguishable characteristics of this project is that it is dynamic and the number of the questions-all the mock interviews sessions presents different questions, so the students have a chance to answer various questions to redo interviews that be long to the same field without redundancy. This increases the performance of practice makes confidence with the real world interviews. Mock interview based on the combination of AI, speech input, and real time camera analysis is designed to be as realistic and strictly personalized as possible.

title: AI-Driven Dynamic Microsegmentation for East-West Traffic in Cloud and Data Center Networks
authors: Spas Georgiev, Monika Velkova, Kamelia Nikolova
abstract: Eastwest traffic in cloud and data center networks has grown significantly due to microservices, containerization, and elastic workload scaling. While microsegmentation is a core component of Zero Trust architectures, existing approaches rely on static, manually defined policies that struggle to adapt to dynamic environments. This paper proposes an AI-driven dynamic microsegmentation framework that leverages machine learning to model workload communication behavior and automatically adapt segmentation policies based on observed eastwest traffic patterns. Network telemetry and workload metadata are analyzed to infer normal service interactions and identify anomalous behavior indicative of potential lateral movement. The inferred behavior is translated into fine-grained microsegmentation policies enforced through a software-defined policy engine with safeguards to prevent application disruption.

title: QR Based Smart Parking Navigation System for Vehicle Localization and Route Guidance
authors: Lakshanya A S, Lipika P, Padmashree G S, Kayalvizhi S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computation, Electronics, Power and Telecommunication (IConSCEPT)
abstract: Citizens can struggle to localize their vehicle especially given the multimodal dimensions of large multi-level parking garages and limitations of GPS. This project would play a role in offering an affordable, self-scaling, and user friendly approach to localize a user's vehicle .This paper proposes a societal Cyber-Physical System (CPS) based mobile application that utilizes a graph-based algorithm to efficiently solve pathfinding and minimize a traveller’s time through QR codes used as physical anchors. The user scans a QR code at their parking spot to save the vehicle’s location. To start navigation later, the user needs to scan a QR code from any other spot within the parking area. After that, the app calculates the route back to the parked vehicle. The mobile application guides the user back using either a 2D map or an AR overlay .Intermediate QR scans are not needed for regular navigation. However, they can be used optionally to adjust the user’s position if there is drift or uncertainty. This modular societal CPS allows adjustments and flexibility to re-use for purposes such as campuses, shopping centers, airports, and other infrastructures. Future growth enhancements would include cloud computing, hybrid localization, and predictive routing. This new approach reduces search time, increases usability, and supports smart mobility in urban ecosystems.

title: Exploring SysML v2 for Variability Modeling and Digital Continuity in Model-Based Systems Engineering
authors: Mohamed Seddik
publish: 2026 International Workshop on Systems Engineering Research
abstract: SysML v2 introduces formal semantics, improved expressiveness, and standardized APIs, offering new opportunities for Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) and Product Line Engineering. This paper explores its potential to support variability modeling and digital continuity across product families, including alignment with ISO/IEC 26580. We examine limitations of SysML v1 in modeling variability and illustrate how SysML v2 concepts address these gaps through a comparative automotive product line example. Rather than proposing a complete framework, this work identifies challenges and opportunities, including formalization of variability semantics, integration with feature modeling approaches, and leveraging SysML v2 APIs for automated variant generation, laying the foundation for next-generation MBSE practices.

title: Electric Vehicle Charging Behavior Prediction Using Deep Learning Methods
authors: Rahul Rajendra Bibave, Mithilesh Singh, P. William
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
abstract: The rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) is transforming transportation and electricity consumption patterns, creating both opportunities and challenges for grid operators. Predicting EV charging behavior when, where, and how much energy EV users will draw is essential for demand forecasting, infrastructure planning, demand response, and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) services. Deep learning methods, with their capacity to learn complex temporal, spatial, and user- behavioral patterns from large datasets, have emerged as powerful tools for charging behavior prediction. This paper surveys deep learning approaches applied to EV charging prediction, including recurrent architectures (RNN, LSTM, GRU), convolutional models for spatio-temporal feature extraction, attention mechanisms, graph neural networks that capture network and mobility topology, and hybrid models that fuse external features (weather, calendar, traffic) with historical charging logs. We discuss feature engineering, data sources (smart meter, charging station logs, GPS/telemetry), preprocessing challenges, and evaluation metrics relevant to forecasting sessions, energy demand, and plug-in times. The review highlights strengths and limitations: deep models offer improved accuracy and the ability to incorporate heterogeneous inputs, but they demand careful handling of data sparsity, privacy, interpretability, and deployment constraints. Finally, we identify research gaps and recommend directions transfer learning, federated learning for privacy-preserving prediction, explainable AI for stakeholder trust, and real-time lightweight models for edge deployment that will support scalable, reliable EV integration into future smart grids.

title: A Transformer-Based Sentiment and Topic Analysis of Public Perception on Pertamina Gas Station Services
authors: Matthew Wirasana Sulistyono, Tuga Mauritsius
abstract: Public perception of gas station service quality can be effectively analyzed through user-generated content on social media platforms such as Twitter/X. This study investigates transformer-based sentiment analysis to evaluate public sentiment toward gas station services using Indonesian Twitter data, which is characterized by informal language and class imbalance. IndoBERT and IndoBERTweet are fine-tuned and compared with random oversampling, class weighting, and back translation applied to address class imbalance. The results show that both models achieve comparable performance, while IndoBERTweet demonstrates more stable results and improved macro-averaged F1-scores, particularly when combined with back translation. BERTopic is further applied to sentiment predictions to identify dominant service-related themes across sentiment categories. Overall, the proposed framework effectively integrates sentiment classification, imbalance handling, and topic modeling to analyze public perceptions of gas station services from social media data.

title: Elevating Recruitment Efficiency: The Role of MERN in Creating Career Bridge's Intuitive Job Platform
authors: Nitesh Ghodichor, Vaishali Patil, Sharda Chhabria
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Quantum Computation-Based Sensor Application (ICAIQSA)
